PT P
PN KR2019021821-A; KR2088772-B1
TI Bio-solid fuel in the form of wood pellet, wood chip or block used for coal-fired power generation, comprises kenaf as main component.
AU CHO D H
   SHIN M H
   SHIN M
AE KENAF CO LTD (KENA-Non-standard)
   SJ GLOBAL CO LTD (SJGL-Non-standard)
GA 201923873N
AB    NOVELTY - A bio-solid fuel comprises kenaf as a main component.
   USE - Bio-solid fuel in the form of wood pellet, wood chip or block (all claimed) is used for coal-fired power generation.
   ADVANTAGE - The bio-solid fuel exhibits high calorific value, fine dust reduction effect, and carbon dioxide emission reduction effect. Since kenaf is an energy crop, it can contribute to environmental preservation, and does not cause damage to usual farmland and forest land. Since kenaf has short growth period and high production per unit area, it can solve the environmental problems, energy problems, and raw material supply problems of usual wood biomass burning fuel.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) manufacture of kenaf wood pellet, which involves removing foreign material of kenaf, pulverizing the kenaf, drying the pulverized kenaf powder at a temperature of 30-40 degrees C for 2-3 days to adjust water content of the kenaf powder to 7 wt.% or less, and compression-molding the dried kenaf powder;
   (2) kenaf wood pellet;
   (3) manufacture of kenaf wood chip, which involves drying kenaf at temperature of 30-40 degrees C for 2-3 days to adjust water content of the kenaf powder to 7 wt.% or less, and pulverizing the dried kenaf to a length of 5-30 mm and a width of 2-10 mm; and
   (4) kenaf wood chip.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Properties: The bio-solid fuel has a calorific value of 4600-4700 kcal/kg on a dry material basis, and a calorific value of 3900-4000 kcal/kg on a moisture content basis. Preferred Process: The bio-solid fuel in the form of pellet is obtained by kneading process, single extrusion process, and pelletizing process.
DC H09 (Fuel products not of petroleum origin - excluding coal handling, preparation or mining, but including coking, briquetting, peat processing synthesis, gas production, coal gasification. Combustion improvement additives for coal, peat and other nonhydrocarbon based fuels are included in this Section together with coal liquefaction and desulphurisation.)
MC H09-F01; H09-F03
IP C10L-005/36; C10L-005/44
PD KR2019021821-A   06 Mar 2019   C10L-005/44   201934   Pages: 7   
   KR2088772-B1   13 Mar 2020   C10L-005/44   202025

PN CN109395477-A
TI Portable gas cleaning device, has shell formed with inlet through-hole and outlet through-hole, sensor connected to actuator to sense generate gas detection value, and gas cleaning unit is arranged to actuating sensing module to clean gas.
AU MO H
   XUE D
   CHEN S
   MO L
   LIN J
   HAN Y
   HUANG Q
   CHEN X
AE MICROJET TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (MIJT-C)
GA 201923461Q
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a shell formed with an inlet through-hole and an outlet through-hole. An actuating sensing module is fixed at an inner side of the shell. An actuator drives external gas into an inner part of the shell by the inlet through-hole and the outlet through-hole for forming internal air flow in the shell. A sensor is connected to the actuator to sense generate a gas detection value. A gas cleaning unit is arranged to the actuating sensing module to clean the gas. The sensor comprises an oxygen sensor, a carbon monoxide sensor, a carbon dioxide sensor, an ozone sensor, a particle sensor, a volatile organic compound sensor, an optical sensor and a graphene sensor. The actuator is an electric actuator, a magnetic actuator, a thermal actuator, a piezoelectric actuator, and a fluid actuator. The fluid actuator is a micro-electric mechanic system pump or a piezoelectric actuated pump.
   USE - Portable gas cleaning device.
   ADVANTAGE - The device senses the internal air flow and generates a gas detection value so as to generate a gas detection result, controls the gas cleaning unit to adjust clean operation strength, and performs surrounding environment air quality monitoring process so as to automatically perform air-cleaning actions on the surrounding environment.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a sectional view of a portable gas cleaning device.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. PT P
PN CN109390638-A
TI Start-stop lead-acid battery for vehicle, has electrolyte that is injected into pole group and electrolyte that is adsorbed on alkali glass fiber (AGM) separator, such that separator is AGM separator.
AU HUANG Z
   HUANG L
   MA L
   JIANG C
AE ZHEJIANG PINGHU HUALONG IND CO LTD (ZHEJ-Non-standard)
GA 201921175U
AB    NOVELTY - The battery has a battery case that is provided with multiple cavities. The cavities are provided with a battery pole group. The battery pole group is alternately arranged by multiple positive electrode plates and negative electrode plates separated by a separator. The pole groups of adjacent batteries are connected in series by a battery bus bar. The bus bar is fixed with a terminal. Multiple pole groups are connected in series with each other in the cavity of the battery case, and are connected end to end to the terminal. An electrolyte is injected into the pole group. The electrolyte is adsorbed on an AGM separator, such that the separator is an AGM separator.
   USE - Start-stop lead-acid battery for vehicle.
   ADVANTAGE - The battery capacity and rate discharge capability are improved. The carbon-dioxide (CO2) emissions are reduced. The requirements of the car start and stop battery are satisfied. The height of the battery is appropriately increased to avoid or reduce the delamination of the electrolyte. The void ratio is improved, a smooth oxygen diffusion path is formed, oxygen recombination efficiency is improved, water loss during battery use and separator wetting shrinkage are reduced. The rebound resilience of the separator is improved. The labor and time are saved. The support force of the separator to the plate is increased to prevent the active material from falling off.
DC L03 (Electro-(in)organic - chemical features of conductors, resistors, magnets, capacitors and switches, electric discharge lamps, semiconductor and other materials, batteries, accumulators and thermoelectric devices, including fuel cells, magnetic recording media, radiation emission devices, liquid crystals and basic electric elements. Growing of single crystals of semiconductors and their doping are included, but semiconductor devices, where the manufacture is not claimed are excluded. Electrography, electrophotography, magnetography, electrolysis, electrophoresis, power plant, X-ray and plasma-techniques, ion exchange resins, polyelectrolytes, electroplating, metal electrodeposition, electroforming, anodising, electrolytic cleaning, cathodic protection and electrolytic or electrothermic production or refining of metals are all covered elsewhere (Sections G, J, K and M).); X16 (Electrochemical Storage)
MC L03-E01C; X16-B01B; X16-F02
IP H01M-010/12; H01M-010/14

PN CN109364315-A; CN209286303-U
TI Double chamber membrane oxygenator with variable temperature, has variable greenhouse which is composed of temperature change film and plugging layer arranged at top and bottom of temperature change film.
AU WEI X
AE JIANGSU MEISIKANG MEDICAL TECHNOLOGY CO (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 201920949S
AB    NOVELTY - The oxygenator has upper mandrel (7) and the lower mandrel sidewall space which are communicated by a connecting shaft (9). The upper mandrel and the lower mandrel sidewall space are both in communication with the oxygen pressure film gap. The upper cover (1), the intermediate connecting cover (3) and the lower cover (2) are connected with a gas nozzle. The top and bottom of the first oxygenation chamber are respectively connected to the upper cover and the intermediate connection cover. The top and bottom of the second oxygenation chamber are respectively connected to the intermediate connection cover and the lower cover. The variable greenhouse is composed of a temperature change film, a plugging layer arranged at the top and bottom of the temperature change film, and is connected with a water nozzle and a blood inlet, and the temperature change film gap is in communication with the oxygen pressure film gap. The oxygenation chamber is connected to a bleeding nozzle.
   USE - Double chamber membrane oxygenator with variable temperature.
   ADVANTAGE - The double chamber membrane oxygenator adjusts the blood temperature before the gas exchange in the blood, and effectively adjusts the carbon dioxide and oxygen content in the blood according to the demand.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a perspective view of a variable temperature dual chamber membrane oxygenator.
   Upper cover (1)
   Lower cover (2)
   Intermediate connecting cover (3)
   Upper mandrel (7)
   Connecting shaft (9)

PN KR1950107-B1; WO2019050106-A1; EP3680368-A1; EP3680368-A4
TI Manufacturing method of hollow fiber composite, involves heat treating precursor to convert to catalyst comprising graphitic carbon nitride (g-C3N4), and heat-treating polymer fibers to form inner hollow section.
AU LEE C S
   KANG S
   JANG J
   LEE S
AE UNIV HANYANG IND COOP FOUND (UHYG-C)
   UNIV HANYANG IND COOP FOUND (UHYG-C)
GA 201918693H
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves preparing (S110) a polymer fiber. The precursor comprising nitrogen on the polymeric fibers is provided (S120). The polymer fibers provided with the precursor are heat treated (S130). The precursor is heat treated (S140) to convert to a catalyst comprising g-C3N4. The polymer fibers are heat-treated to form (S150) the inner hollow section. The adhesive force between the catalyst and the polymer fibers is improved so that the catalyst fixes the outer wall of the polymer fibers.
   USE - Manufacturing method of hollow fiber composite (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The surface area of polymer fiber is increased and the content of the catalyst can be improved. The light can be used as the material returning the contaminant like the carbon dioxide and can be used through the composite material, used for the radiation plate including the electrode material, in which the ionosorption modulus is improved the gas sensor, in which the gas adsorption rate is improved the energy reservoir, the aircraft vehicle etc. the conducting polymer composite, the photoelectrochemistry water splitting material etc.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart illustrating the manufacturing process of the hollow fiber composite. (Drawing includes non-English language text)
   Step for preparing polymer fiber (S110)
   Step for providing precursor (S120)
   Step for heat treating polymer fiber (S130)
   Step for heat treating precursor (S140)
   Step for forming inner hollow section (S150)
DC A94 (Semi-finished materials - fibres, films, foams.); F01 (Threads and fibres - natural or artificial; spinning - including the production of mineral and carbon fibres (D01).); F02 (Yarns - mechanical finishing of yarns or ropes; warping or beaming (D02, D07).)

PN KR1943345-B1
TI Brayton cycle based electric power generation            system for use in atomic power plant, has recirculation            compressor integrated with main compressor such that            working fluid is heat-exchanged in fuel economizer            while being supplied to reheater.
AU PARK S H
   RHIM D R
   KIM J I
   SHIN J U
   YOON M K
   KO H J
AE INST ADVANCED ENG (ADVG-C)
   KOREA SOUTH EAST POWER CO LTD (KEPC-C)
GA 2019123229
AB    NOVELTY - The system (100) has a heat exchanger unit                arranged with a reheater section (112) and a fuel                economizer section (113). A recuperator (130) is                equipped with a branch pipe (163) branched from a                connection pipe (162), where the connection pipe                connects the recuperator and a refrigerator. A                recirculation compressor (151) supplies working                fluid to the refrigerator. The recirculation                compressor is integrated with a main compressor                (150) such that the working fluid is heat-exchanged                in the fuel economizer section while being supplied                to the reheater section, and the working fluid                includes carbon dioxide in a supercritical                state.
   USE - Brayton cycle based electric power generation                system for use in an atomic power plant.
   ADVANTAGE - The recirculation compressor is integrated                with the main compressor such that working fluid is                heat-exchanged in the fuel economizer section while                being supplied to the reheater section so as to                improve heat transfer efficiency of the turbine                without phase change loss in an effective manner,                so that economic benefits about the system can be                improved.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a                Brayton cycle based electric power generation                system.
   Brayton cycle based electric power generation                system (100)
   Reheater section (112)
   Fuel economizer section (113)
   Recuperator (130)
   Main compressor (150)
   Recirculation compressor (151)
   Connection pipe (162)
   Branch pipe (163)

PN CN109270217-A
TI Washroom smell detector, has pressure detector and gas sensor whose end are connected with input end of single chip microcomputer chip, where output end of single chip microcomputer chip is connected with control end of air pump.
AU YANG F
   XIE L
   ZHANG W
AE JIANGSU SITCORES ELECTRONIC TECHNOLOGY (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 201911796L
AB    NOVELTY - The detector has a pressure detector (3) and a gas sensor (4) i.e. carbon dioxide concentration detector formed with a gas collecting chamber (1). The pressure detector and an output end of the gas sensor are connected with an input end of a single chip microcomputer chip (5). An output end of the single chip microcomputer chip is connected with a control end of an air pump (2) and control valve (7). The air pump is connected to the gas collection chamber through an air pipe (6). The control valve is placed in the air pump and the gas collection chamber. The single chip microcomputer chip controls the control valve and the air pump.
   USE - Washroom smell detector.
   ADVANTAGE - The detector is simple in structure, and has high practicability, and detects odor gas in the washroom by collecting composition of certain fixed gas in the air in the washroom and analyzing total increase of the odor gas component in the washroom according to proportion of volume fraction.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a washroom smell detector gas monitoring method.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a side view of a washroom smell detector.
   Gas collecting chamber (1)
   Air pump (2)
   Pressure detector (3)
   Gas sensor (4)
   Single chip microcomputer chip (5)
   Air pipe (6)
   Control valve (7)

PN CN109252840-A; CN109252840-B
TI Foamed gel-based thin layer viscous oil reservoir            recovery rate improving device, has intermediate            container filled with water, where liquid outlet end of            intermediate container is fixed with input end of            back-pressure valve.
AU ZHOU W
   ZHAO S
   CHEN S
   DONG M
AE UNIV CHANGAN (UCHA-C)
GA 2019104035
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a first intermediate container                filled with water. A second intermediate container                is filled with oil. The containers are mounted on a                second pipeline among multiple pipelines for                filling jelly foaming solution in a third                intermediate container. A fourth intermediate                container is filled with carbon dioxide gas. Liquid                inlet ends of the first container, the second                container and the third container are provided with                a first plunger pump. A liquid inlet end of the                fourth intermediate container is provided with a                second plunger pump. Liquid outlet ends of the                first container and the second container are                connected with an input end of a sand pipe. A                liquid outlet end of the fourth intermediate                container is fixed with an input end of a                back-pressure valve.
   USE - Foamed gel-based thin layer viscous oil                reservoir recovery rate improving device.
   ADVANTAGE - The device reduces difficulty of entering of                air into a jelly foam generator and improves                recovery rate of an oil reservoir.

PN CN109233918-A
TI Low carbon gasoline comprises hydrocracking light oil, 95# gasoline, crude alcohol, methyl tertiary butyl ether, petroleum ether, and exhaust gas cleaning agent.
AU CHANG W
AE CHANG W (CHAN-Individual)
GA 201912575Y
AB    NOVELTY - Low carbon gasoline comprises 10-25 pts. wt. hydrocracking light oil, 35-60 pts. wt. 95# gasoline, 2-6 pts. wt. crude alcohol, 10-20 pts. wt. methyl tertiary butyl ether, 5-10 pts. wt. petroleum ether, and 10-20 pts. wt. exhaust gas cleaning agent.
   USE - Used as low carbon gasoline.
   ADVANTAGE - The gasoline: has bright color, excellent appearance and power performance, and complete fuel without generating carbon; reduces the emission of carbon dioxide, carbon monoxide, and sulfur dioxide; supplies enough oxygen to completely burn, and increasing heat generation; increases the power of internal combustion engine of the vehicle; and is environmentally friendly.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing low carbon gasoline comprising (i) placing hydrocracking light oil in a stirring tank, vacuumizing the inside of stirring tank for 10 hours, then adding crude alcohol, stirring while adding, and aging for 30 minutes to obtain first oil liquid, (ii) adding 95# gasoline into stirring tank, mixing with first oil, stirring for 15 minutes, and then aging for 30 minutes to obtain second oil liquid, (ii) separating methyl tertiary butyl ether, and petroleum ether for ten minutes, then sequentially adding to the second oil liquid, stirring for 15 minutes, and aging for 30 minutes to obtain third oil, and (iv) adding the exhaust gas cleaning agent to the third oil liquid, and vacuum stirring for 40 minutes to obtain the finished product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The carbon gasoline comprises 23 pts. wt. hydrocracking light oil, 44 pts. wt. 95# gasoline, 6 pts. wt. crude alcohol, 16 pts. wt. methyl tertiary butyl ether, 8 pts. wt. petroleum ether, and 16 pts. wt. exhaust gas cleaning agent. Preferred Components: exhaust gas cleaning agent comprises 30-40 pts. wt. polyether synthetic oil, 10-15 pts. wt. naphthenic oil, 5-8 pts. wt. vulcanized cottonseed oil, 4-6 pts. wt. sodium silicate, 5-8 pts. wt. polyamine, and 2-6 pts. wt. polyoxyethylene polyoxypropylene polyether. Preferred Method: The exhaust gas cleaning agent is prepared by (a) taking polyether synthetic oil, and naphthenic oil, mixing, and stirring for 30 minutes, (b) ultrasonically mixing the vulcanized cottonseed oil, sodium silicate, and polyamine, and stirring for 20 minutes, (c) adding the mixed liquid obtained in the step (a) to the mixed liquid of step (b), and stirring for 30 minutes, and (iv) adding polyoxyethylene polyoxypropylene polyether to the mixed liquid obtained in the step (iii), and stirring for 10 minutes to obtain the product.
DC H06 (Gaseous and liquid fuels - including pollution control. Chemical aspects of catalytic exhaust systems for cars are included as well as liquid or gaseous fuels of non-petroleum origin e.g. methanol or ethanol-based fuels. Combustion improvement additives for liquid fuels are included (C10L).)

PN CN109234040-A
TI Natural plant detergent useful for products and/or product additives with bactericidal and cleansing effects, comprises e.g. Saponaria officinalis extract, Sapindus extract, citronella volatile oil, grapefruit volatile oil and seaweed glue.
AU YANG D
AE YANG D (YANG-Individual)
GA 201913270X
AB    NOVELTY - Natural plant detergent comprises 20-30 pts. wt. Saponaria officinalis extract, 10-20 pts. wt. Sapindus extract, 0.2-0.3 pts. wt. citronella volatile oil, 0.1-0.5 pts. wt. grapefruit volatile oil, 0.2-0.3 pts. wt. seaweed glue, 50-70 pts. wt. lemongrass pure dew, 0.5-1 pts. wt. citric acid and 0.1-0.25 pts. wt. cinnamon volatile oil.
   USE - The natural plant detergent is useful for products and/or product additives with bactericidal and cleansing effects (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The detergent: is rich in plant saponins, natural, and safe and convenient to use and mild to the skin; does not produce toxic side effects; has a strong cleaning effect; softens the hard water; and replaces chemical petroleum surfactant.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing natural plant detergent, comprising (i) washing Saponaria officinalis, 10-20 pts. wt. Sapindus with warm water, drying at 40-50 degrees C, pulverizing and extracting with supercritical carbon dioxide for many times to obtain Saponaria officinalis and Sapindus extract; (ii) drying citronella, pulverizing, refining by steam extraction to obtain citronella pure dew and volatile oil, separating pure dew and the volatile oil and using; and (iii) emulsifying and aging of each raw material component according to a preset ratio until disappearing foam.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Composition: The natural plant detergent preferably comprises 20 pts. wt. Saponaria officinalis extract, 10 pts. wt. Sapindus extract, 0.25 pts. wt. citronella volatile oil, 0.4 pts. wt. grapefruit volatile oil, 0.25 pts. wt. seaweed glue, 68 pts. wt. lemongrass pure dew, 1 pts. wt. citric acid and 0.1 pts. wt. cinnamon volatile oil. The natural plant detergent further comprises 5-10 pts. wt. tea tree extract, 3-6 pts. wt. Gynostemma pentaphyllum extract, 2-5 pts. wt. horseradish tree leaf extract and 1-2 pts. wt. cashew nut oil, preferably 6 pts. wt. tea tree extract, 5 pts. wt. Gynostemma pentaphyllum extract, 2 pts. wt. horseradish tree leaf extract and 1 pts. wt. cashew nut oil. Preferred Components: The Saponaria officinalis extract comprises saxosaponin, and Sapindus extract comprises Sapindus saponin. Preferred Method: The step (i) comprises using supercritical carbon dioxide for extraction three times, specifically comprises (a) using anhydrous ethanol as a solvent, controlling carbon dioxide flow rate at 30 liter/h, extraction pressure is 20 MPa, the temperature is 50 degrees C for 2 hours to obtain crude product of Saponaria officinalis and Sapindus; (b) maintaining carbon dioxide flow rate at 30 liter/hour, increasing the pressure to 27.5 MPa at 55 degrees C for 3 h and extracting further Saponaria officinalis and Sapindus; and (c) maintaining carbon dioxide flow rate at 30 liter/hour, increasing the pressure to 35 MPa at 60 degrees C for 4 hours to obtain Saponaria officinalis and Sapindus extract, preferably, the mass ratio of the powder to the absolute ethanol is 1:(2-4), the warm water refers to water at 20-25 degrees C, and pulverizing dried raw material to 80-100 meshes. The step (ii) comprises pulverizing dried lemongrass to 80-100 meshes, and adding deionized water for steam extraction, where ratio of the lemongrass powder and deionized water is 1:(2-4); and emulsification process in step (iii) is carried out in a high shear homogenizing emulsification pot and the emulsification for 20 minutes. The method comprises drying tea tree, Gynostemma pentaphyllum and horseradish tree leaf, pulverizing, soaking in saline solution, decocting, extracting, filtering and collecting respectively, to obtain tea tree extract, Gynostemma pentaphyllum and horseradish tree leaf extract, where, in the brine solution is 10-15% sodium chloride, soaking for 20-50 minutes and boiling specifically for boiling first for 5-8 minutes and heating under sim condition for 60-100 minutes.

PN US2019020079-A1; EP3432411-A1; CN109273803-A; KR2019008728-A; EP3432411-B1; US11038226-B2; KR2461577-B1
TI Secondary battery module for use as power source            for motor drives in hybrid electric vehicles, has            insulation sheets provided between cells and including            aerogel for blocking heat transfer between cells, and            housing fixing cells and sheets.
AU LEE J H
   HAN M S
   PARK M K
   YOON S J
   LEE J
   HAN M
   PARK M
   YOON S
   SEOKJOONYOON
AE SAMSUNG SDI CO LTD (SMSU-C)
   SAMSUNG SDI CO LTD (SMSU-C)
   SAMSUNG SDI CO LTD (SMSU-C)
GA 201906123Y
AB    NOVELTY - The module (100) has battery cells (10)                aligned along a direction. Insulation sheets (20)                are provided between the battery cells. The                insulation sheets include aerogel for blocking heat                transfer between the battery cells. Housing fixes                the battery cells and the insulation sheets.                Aerogel particles in the insulation sheets comprise                carbon dioxide, and have size ranging from 10                micrometer to 100 micrometer. The aerogel particles                include nano-sized pores. Each insulation sheet has                thickness of 0.3 mm. An adhesion tape is provided                between the battery cells and the insulation                sheets.
   USE - Secondary battery module for use as a power                source for motor drives in hybrid electric                vehicles.
   ADVANTAGE - The insulation sheets include extremely small                pores holding air layers such that the module can                reduce weight of the aerogel insulation sheet and                demonstrate excellent insulating performance using                lightweight heat insulating material. The module                maximizes heat insulating efficiency by allowing                thickness of the adhesion tape having high thermal                conductivity to be smaller than that of the                insulation sheet. The insulation sheet prevents or                suppresses high-temperature heat generated in a                battery cell from being transferred to the                adjoining battery cell.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a perspective view of a                secondary battery module.
   Battery cells (10)
   Positive electrode terminal (11)
   Negative electrode terminal (12)
   Insulation sheets (20)
   Secondary battery module (100)

TI Composite container used for accommodating content            liquid e.g. food, has vapor deposition film that is            formed in inner surface of container main portion which            is expanded integrally with plastics element by blow            molding.
AU SUGA Y
   MIYAWAKI T
AE DAINIPPON PRINTING CO LTD (NIPQ-C)
   DAINIPPON PRINTING CO LTD (NIPQ-C)
GA 201906227J
AB    NOVELTY - The container (10A) has a container main                portion (10) that is made of a plastic material and                formed with an opening portion (11), a trunk                portion (20) and a bottom portion (30). A plastic                bottle (40) is provided to be closely contacted                with an outer side of one portion of the trunk                portion of the container main portion and the outer                side of portion of the bottom portion without                bonding. The container main portion and the                plastics element are expanded integrally by blow                molding. A vapor deposition film (21) is formed in                the inner surface of the container main                portion.
   USE - Composite container used for accommodating                content liquid such as food and beverage.
   ADVANTAGE - Since the vapor deposition film is formed in                the inner surface of the container main portion,                the penetrating of gas such as oxygen gas, water                vapor and carbon dioxide gas into the container                main portion is prevented by the vapor deposition                film. Thereby the quality degradation of the                contents is prevented. The manufacturing cost of                the container is suppressed.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a                manufacturing method of composite container.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a partial vertical                sectional view of the composite container.

PT P
PN CN109103467-A; CN109103467-B
TI Graphene-based metal catalyst for use as catalyst for e.g. hydrogenation reaction, comprises electrochemically stripped graphene and metal nanoparticles, where stripped graphene and metal nanoparticles are distributed on surface of graphene.
AU YUE W
   YUAN X
AE UNIV BEIJING NORMAL (UYBN-C)
GA 201902955B
AB    NOVELTY - A graphene-based metal catalyst comprises electrochemically stripped graphene and metal nanoparticles, where the electrochemically stripped graphene and metal nanoparticles are distributed on the surface of graphene.
   USE - Graphene-based metal catalyst for use as catalyst for hydrogenation reaction, dehydrogenation reaction and carbon dioxide conversion reaction (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The graphene-based metal catalyst has improved mechanical performance, conductivity and catalytic performance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a method for preparing graphene metal catalyst, which involves weighing 0.5-1.5 g ammonium sulfate, dissolving in 100 mL water as electrolyte, using platinum wire as negative electrode and graphite foil as positive electrode, connecting with DC power source for electrolysis and filtering to obtain a suspension, allowing the suspension for suction filteration, washing with deionized water and ethanol for more than 3 times and drying to obtain dried product, mixing the dried product with N,N-dimethylformamide, sonicating and discarding the bottom precipitate after standing, filtering the upper dispersion with a PTFE filter and drying to obtain electrochemically stripped graphene, followed by ultrasonically dispersing the electrochemically stripped graphene in a polar aprotic solvent, adding metal precursor, stirring at room temperature for 2 hours, adding reducing agent to the solution under the protection of nitrogen stream, heating at 70-90 degrees C, stirring for 0.5-1 hour, washing the mixture by centrifugation and drying to obtain the finished product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The metal nanoparticle comprises platinum, palladium, gold, silver, iron, cobalt, nickel, copper, tin, zinc and alloy of two metals. The metal precursor comprises chloride, nitrate, sulfate, acetate and alkoxide. The reducing agent comprises sodium citrate, sodium borohydride, potassium borohydride, lithium aluminum hydride, hydrazine hydrate, vitamin C and ascorbic acid.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The polar aprotic solvent comprises acetonitrile, N,N-dimethylformamide, dimethyl sulfoxide, hexamethylphosphoric triamide and 1,3-dimethyl-2-imidazolidinone.

GA 2018996501

An object in the inside of the pickling machine which is subjected to high pressure permeation treatment in a high pressure environment is formed in the pickling machine. The pickling machine is provided with a low pressure space. The pickling machine is provided with a pressure reducing device to remove the gas in the space inside the curing machine to reduce the air pressure in the space inside the curing machine. The low pressure environment in the curing machine is formed to remove odor or mold and virus from the inside of the curing machine.

PN CN108823415-A; CN108823415-B
TI Preparation of stainless steel super-fine polishing wax involves baking high-purity aluminum hydroxide, obtaining alumina powder, pulverizing, adding stearic acid to fine powder and blending with lubricant and gloss additive.
AU CHEN X
   ZHAO P
AE UNIV GUIZHOU (UYGZ-C)
GA 2018918817
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of stainless steel super-fine polishing wax involves passing carbon dioxide gas into high-purity sodium metaaluminate solution, filtering resultant white precipitate, obtaining aluminum hydroxide, adding aluminum hydroxide to hydrochloric acid, washing the mixture using water until the filtrate is neutral, obtaining high-purity aluminum hydroxide, baking high-purity aluminum hydroxide, obtaining alumina powder, pulverizing alumina powder, obtaining high-purity alumina fine powder, adding stearic acid to alumina fine powder, and blending with lubricant and gloss additive.
   USE - Preparation of stainless steel super-fine polishing wax.
   ADVANTAGE - The method efficiently and economically provides stainless steel super-fine polishing wax having high polishing quality, by simple method.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The high-purity sodium aluminate solution is obtained by mixing and grinding phosphogypsum, red mud, additives and modifiers into raw meal, delivering into kiln for roasting, producing clinkers, subjecting clinkers to water-milling dissolution at the liquid-solid ratio of 4-6:1, performing solid-liquid separation, obtaining solution (B), subjecting resultant residue to flotation, separating sulfide, placing sulfide in oxygen-rich environment of 30-50%, baking at 800-1200 degrees C for 3-5 hours, catalyzing flue gas generated by calcination by vanadium pentoxide, absorbing with concentrated sulfuric acid, obtaining sulfuric acid, adding solution (B) to calcium oxide, stirring at constant temperature and filtering.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The red mud is red mud produced by Bayer process for producing alumina. The additive is sodium carbonate, sodium sulfate or caustic soda. The modifier is anthracite, carbon or coal gangue. Phosphogypsum and red mud are mixed in the ratio of 1:0.8-1.6. The addition ratio of additive is 10-25% with respect to molecular ratio of total content of sodium oxide, alumina and iron oxide in the raw material.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The stainless steel ultrafine polishing wax comprises 60-65 pts. wt. alumina fine powder, 20-25 pts. wt. stearic acid, 5-10 pts. wt. lubricant, and 5-7 pts. wt. gloss additive. The lubricant comprises 85-90 pts. wt. oleic acid and 10-15 pts. wt. triethanolamine. The gloss additive contains 80-85 pts. wt. rosin and 15-20 pts. wt. petroleum sulfonate. Preferred Property: The grain diameter of high-purity aluminum oxide powder is 200-220 nm.

PN CN108641586-A
TI Nano-paint includes polymethylphenyl silicone resin, elastic acrylic resin, refractory aggregate, nano titanium dioxide, nano diatomite, silk fiber, nano talc powder, gelatin, dispersant, hydrophobic agent, and film-forming agent.
AU WANG Y
AE ANHUI QIJIA TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 201881293B
AB    NOVELTY - A nano-paint comprises 20-30 pts. wt. polymethylphenyl silicone resin, 15-18 pts. wt. elastic acrylic resin, 5-7 pts. wt. refractory aggregate, 2-7 pts. wt. nano titanium dioxide, 6-10 pts. wt. nano diatomite, 8-12 pts. wt. silk fiber, 8-12 pts. wt. calcined aluminum powder, 10-15 of nano talc powder, 3-8 pts. wt. SBA-15 molecular sieve, 25-35 pts. wt. softened water, 2-5 pts. wt. gelatin, 2-7 pts. wt. dispersant, 2-6 pts. wt. hydrophobic agent, and 1-3 pts. wt. film-forming agent.
   USE - A nano-paint.
   ADVANTAGE - The paint is aging resistant, high temperature resistant, waterproof, water-resistant, and is environment-friendly. It has room humidity regulating, antibacterial, bactericidal, and UV radiation effect. The paint can purify indoor air, absorb carbon dioxide, and absorb indoor furniture decorating residual formaldehyde.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of the nano-paint, comprising:
   (A) ball-milling softened water in the ball mill, polymethylphenyl silicone resin, and elastic acrylic resin at 150 revolutions/minute (rpm) to obtain a slurry;
   (B) adding refractory aggregate, nano titanium dioxide, nano diatomite, silk fiber, calcined aluminum powder, nano talc powder, SBA-15 molecular sieve, dispersant, hydrophobic agent, and film-forming agent, and ball-milling at 400 rpm for 12 hours; and
   (C) adding gelatin, and ball-milling at 50 rpm for 4 hours.

   ADVANTAGE - The integrated manual override arrangement prevents fluid flow from the foot pedal to the slave cylinder if the electronic clutch actuator element electronically operates the slave cylinder and permits fluid flow from the foot pedal to the slave cylinder if the electronic clutch actuator element manually operates the slave cylinder, thus reducing carbon dioxide ejection.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a method for actuating a clutch in a transmission of a vehicle.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a system for actuating a clutch.

AU JONES R D
AE JONES R D (JONE-Individual)
GA 201848515J
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves compounding a first                material suitable as a hybrid rocket fuel and a                second energetic and pyrophoric nano-scale metallic                material according to a predetermined mixture ratio                to form a third material. The third material is                served as feedstock material for use in an additive                manufacturing apparatus (10). The additive                manufacturing apparatus is operated by using the                feedstock material to fabricate a fuel grain                comprising fused stacked layers of solidified fuel                grain material (12). An inner circumferential                surface of each ring-shaped bead is provided to                define projections, such that as a ring-shaped bead                forming the boundary wall ablates due to combustion                in the combustion port. An inner circumferential                wall of an adjacent ring-shaped bead is provided to                define projections presents to form the boundary                wall of the combustion port. The heavier-than-air                inert gas comprises argon, carbon dioxide,                nitrogen, or nitrogen dioxide.
   USE - Method of making fuel grain for use in hybrid                rocket engine.
   ADVANTAGE - The solid fuel grain causes increased                regression rate and corresponding increased thrust                impulse without the decreased fuel volumes                associated with multi-port designs.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for constructing rocket motor.

   XU L
   ZHANG W
   XIA X
   XU J
   FAN D
AE QINGDAO KEHAI BIOLOGICAL CO LTD (QING-Non-standard)
GA 201814971K
AB    NOVELTY - High density culturing of euglenophyta comprises (a) pouring euglenophyta culture medium into a conical flask, sterilizing, cooling, inoculating the algae seed into a euglenophyta culture medium, culturing, (b) placing the inoculated euglenophyta culture medium into a removable medium configured tank, sterilizing and cooling, transferring into a plurality of plastic film bioreactor, inoculating euglena algae on each plastic film bioreactor, culturing, and (c) adding cooled euglenophyta culture medium into each plastic film bioreactor and culturing.
   USE - The method is useful for culturing euglenophyta.
   ADVANTAGE - The method fixes maximum carbon dioxide and increases chlorophyll content in euglena. The euglena cell concentration reaches 3 million/ml and increases the dry weight.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - High density culturing of euglenophyta comprises (a) expanding seed cultivation, pouring euglenophyta culture medium into a conical flask, sterilizing, inoculating the algae seed into a sterilized and cooled euglenophyta culture medium, culturing using LED light source irradiation, (b) placing the inoculated euglenophyta culture medium in a removable configured tank, sterilizing and cooling to room temperature, transferring cooled euglenophyta culture medium into a multiple plastic film bioreactor through a sterilized sealed pipeline, inoculating euglena algae grown to the exponential growth phase on each plastic film bioreactor, culturing using LED light source irradiation, and (c) expanding culture by adding continuously cooled euglenophyta culture medium obtained in step (b) into each plastic film bioreactor, cultivating for certain volume, culturing for 2-4 days.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: In the step (a), (b) and (c), euglenophyta culture medium comprises 0.5-3 g peptone, 0.8-3 g yeast powder, 0.02-0.1 g monopotassium phosphate, 0.05-0.2 g magnesium sulfate, 0.03-0.1 g calcium chloride, 1 ml iron-ethylenediaminetetraacetic acid mother liquor, 1 ml microelement mother liquor and 1 ml vitamin mother liquor, pH of the euglenophyta culture medium is 3-7. Preferred Method: In the step (a), seed inoculating amount is 10%, LED light source irradiation culturing is carried out for 3-4 days, the LED light intensity is 1500-3000 lx and temperature is 22-28 degrees C. In the step (b), inoculating 30% euglena algae grown to the exponential growth phase, LED light source irradiation culturing for 2-4 days. In the step (a), intermittent shaking is carried out with interval of 4 hours. In the step (b) and (c) the plastic film biological reactor bottom ventilation ratio is 0.2-1:1 vol./vol.m. In the step (b) and (c), LED light source irradiation intensity is 1500-3000 lx, the culturing temperature is 22-28 degrees C, the carbon dioxide content is 0.02-1%. In the step (c), adding 6 l cooled euglenophyta culture medium obtained in step (b) into the plastic film bioreactor, culturing, the culturing conditions is same as in the step (b), after culturing, adding 30 l cooled euglenophyta culture medium obtained in step (b) into the plastic film bioreactor.

   USE - The method is useful for synthesizing cyclohexanone by using carbon dioxide to accelerate hydrogenation of aromatic compound.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables simple, safe, economical and large-scale continuous synthesis of cyclohexanone in an environmentally-friendly manner, and effectively uses greenhouse gas carbon dioxide to change waste into valuable.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The catalyst comprises a noble metal which is chosen from one or more of palladium, platinum, gold or ruthenium, and further comprises a carrier chosen from one or more of carbon materials, titanium dioxide, zinc oxide, gamma -aluminum oxide, silicon dioxide, magnesium oxide, molecular sieves, molybdenum trioxide, graphitic carbon nitride or molybdenum carbide. The catalyst is chosen from one or more of palladium/carbon, palladium/graphitic carbon nitride, palladium/P25 or palladium/ gamma -aluminum oxide. The molar ratio of carbon dioxide and hydrogen is 100:1-1:100, preferably 1:3.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The reacting step is carried out at a temperature of -20 degrees C to 200 degrees C under a pressure of 0.01-12 MPa for 0.001-200 hours. Preferred Components: The mass ratio of aromatic compound and catalyst is 100:1-1:100. The aromatic compound is an aromatic compound capable of producing hydrogen bonds with carbonic acid. The aromatic compound is chosen from one or more of an aromatic nitro compound, an aromatic nitro compound substituted product, an aniline or an aniline compound substituted product, preferably anisole or benzoic acid.

PN CN112174546-A
TI Cement production device useful for recovering            carbon dioxide, comprises lime rotary kiln,            purification tank whose one side is installed with            intake pipe, which is connected with exhaust end of            kiln, and carbon dioxide treatment equipment.
AU DA C
   PENG Z
   YI C
AE TIBET CHANGDU GAOZHENG BUILDING MATERIAL (TIBE-Non-standard)
GA 202106422Q
AB    NOVELTY - A cement production device comprises a lime                rotary kiln, a purification tank, and a carbon                dioxide treatment equipment. The tank is provided                with cavities (A,B,C). An intake pipe is installed                on one side of the tank, is communicated with the                cavity (A), and is connected with an exhaust end of                the kiln. A coarse filter plate and a fine filter                plate are fixedly installed in the cavity (A) by                bolts. A rotary shaft is provided in the cavity                (A). Four arc-shaped plates are fixedly installed                on the shaft, and are arranged circularly at equal                intervals. Cavity (A) is provided with a vertical                shaft (A) between the coarse and fine filter                plates. A cam is installed at the bottom end of the                shaft (A). A metal block (A) is fixedly installed                on the sides of the coarse and fine filter plates                close to each other. Two sliding rods (A) are                fixedly installed on the side of the coarse filter                plate close to the fine filter plate. Sliding                sleeves on the rods (A) are provided with same                sliding plate (A).
   USE - The cement production device is useful for                recovering carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The cement production device: can effectively                filter and purify the dust particles carried in the                gas produced by lime calcination; can perform                desulfurization and denitration purification                treatment on the gas, so that the gas filtration                and purification effect is excellent; improves the                purity and quality of carbon dioxide recovery; and                effectively recovers and utilizes the waste heat in                the gas, so that the waste heat recovery effect in                the gas is excellent, and the recovery efficiency                is high.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A cement production device comprises a lime                rotary kiln, a purification tank, and a carbon                dioxide treatment equipment. The tank is provided                with cavities (A,B,C). An intake pipe is fixedly                installed on one side of the tank, is communicated                with the cavity (A), and is fixedly connected with                an exhaust end of the kiln. A coarse filter plate                and a fine filter plate are fixedly installed in                the cavity (A) by bolts. A rotary shaft is provided                in the cavity (A). Four arc-shaped plates are                fixedly installed on the shaft, and are arranged                circularly at equal intervals. Cavity (A) is                provided with a vertical shaft (A) between the                coarse and fine filter plates. A cam is fixedly                installed at the bottom end of the shaft (A). A                metal block (A) is fixedly installed on the sides                of the coarse and fine filter plates close to each                other. Two sliding rods (A) are fixedly installed                on the side of the coarse filter plate close to the                fine filter plate. Sliding sleeves on the sliding                rods (A) are provided with same sliding plate (A).                Two sliding rods (B) are fixedly installed on the                side of the fine filter plate close to the coarse                filter plate. The sliding sleeves on the sliding                rods (B) are provided with same sliding plate (B).                A metal block (B) is fixedly installed on the sides                of the sliding plates (A) and (B) far away from                each other. The metal block (B) on the sliding                plate (A) is in contact with the corresponding                metal block (A). The sliding rods (A) and (B) are                close to the vertical shaft (A), and are fixedly                installed with a baffle. Two springs are fixedly                installed on the sides of the sliding plates (A)                and (B) close to each other. The four springs are                sleeved on the two sliding rods (A) and the two                sliding rods (B). One end of the four springs close                to the shaft (A) is fixedly connected to one side                of the corresponding baffle. Two circular holes (A)                are opened on the inner wall of the top of the                cavity (A). The top ends of the rotary shaft and                the vertical shaft (A) are penetrated into the                corresponding circular holes (A), and are both                fixedly sleeved with gears, which are meshed. A                vertical shaft (B) is provided in the cavity (B).                Two mixing blades (A) are fixedly sleeved on the                vertical shaft (B). The cavities (B) and (C) are                provided with the circular hole (B) on the inner                wall of one side close to each other. The bottom                end of the shaft (B) is provided to penetrate the                circular hole (B), and is fixedly installed with                the mixing blade (B). A circular hole (C) is opened                on the inner wall of the top of the cavity (B). The                top end of the shaft (B) is provided to penetrate                the circular hole (C). The vertical shafts (A) and                (B) are fixedly sleeved with a sprocket located                above the purification tank. A chain is wound on                the two sprockets. An air pipe (A) is fixedly                installed in the cavity (B), and is communicated                with the cavity (A). A fan is fixedly installed on                the side of the tank away from the air pipe. The                suction end of the fan is fixedly connected to one                end of the suction pipe. The other end of the                suction pipe is fixedly installed on one side of                the tank, and is communicated with the cavity (B).                The discharge end of the fan is fixedly connected                to one end of an exhaust pipe. The other end of the                exhaust pipe is provided to extend into the cavity                (C). A U-shaped coil is fixedly installed in the                cavity (C). One end of the exhaust pipe located in                the cavity (C) is fixedly connected to one end of                the U-shaped coil pipe. The other end of the coil                pipe is fixedly connected with an air pipe (B). The                end of the air pipe (B) away from the coil pipe is                extended to the outside of the tank, and is fixedly                connected with the air inlet pipe of the treatment                equipment.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Device: A protective cover is                  fixedly installed on the top of the tank. The                  gears, the sprockets and the chain are all                  located in the cover. A sewage pipe is fixedly                  installed on the side of the tank away from the                  air inlet pipe. Water supply pipe, and water                  outlet pipe and the sewage pipe are connected to                  the cavity (B). The water supply pipe and the                  water outlet pipe are both connected with the                  cavity (C). The top of the end                  cap is provided to extend beyond the filling                  hole.

PT P
PN CN112758930-A; CN112758930-B
TI Modifying activated carbon comprises performing            first heat treatment, mixing modified activated carbon            I with polyimine, performing second heat treatment,            mixing modified activated carbon II with siloxane and            performing third heat treatment.
AU CHANG C
   YANG Y
   XU D
   GAO T
   SUN Z
   XU Y
AE CHN ENERGY GROUP NEW ENERGY TECHNOLOGY (CHNE-Non-standard)
GA 202154283V
AB    NOVELTY - Modifying activated carbon comprises (i)                performing first heat treatment to the activated                carbon to obtain the modified activated carbon I;                (ii) mixing the modified activated carbon I with                the polyimine and performing second heat treatment                to obtain the modified activated carbon II; and                (iii) mixing the modified activated carbon II with                siloxane and performing third heat treatment to                obtain the modified activated carbon.
   USE - The method id useful for modifying activated                carbon.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple; has convenient                operation; and improves the carbon dioxide                adsorption capacity, water resistance and abrasion                resistance of the modified activated carbon.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                modified activated carbon.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In step (i), activated                  carbon is shell activated carbon, coconut shell                  activated carbon, peach shell activated carbon,                  walnut shell activated carbon and/or jujube shell                  activated carbon. In the step (ii), the polyimine                  is polyethyleneimine and/or polyetherimide,                  preferably polyethyleneimine. The mass ratio of                  the modified activated carbon I and the polyimine                  is 1:0.01-1.2, preferably 1: 0.2-0.4. In the step                  (iii), the siloxane is polydimethylsiloxane,                  cyclic methyl siloxane, amino siloxane, and/or                  polymethylphenylsiloxane, preferably dimethyl                  silicone polymer. The mass ratio of the modified                  activated carbon II and the siloxane is                  1:0.001-0.3, preferably 1:0.005-0.01. Preferred                  Method: In the step (i), the treatment                  temperature of first heat treatment is 550-750                  degrees C, preferably 600-700 degrees C and the                  processing time is 1-3 hours, preferably 1.5-2.5                  hours. In the step (ii), the treatment                  temperature of second heat treatment is 140-200                  degrees C, preferably 160-180 degrees C and the                  heating time is 1-3 hours, preferably 1.5-2                  hours. In the step (ii), the treatment                  temperature of third heat treatment is 370-450                  degrees C, preferably 390-410 degrees C and the                  heating time is 1-3 hours, preferably 1.5-2.5                  hours.
DC A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.)

PT P
PN CN112110468-A
TI Recycling phosphogypsum by preprocessing phosphogypsum to obtain primary powder, leaching with ammonium chloride solution under stirring while introducing micro-nanobubbles, separating, and post-processing obtained leaching solution.
AU ZHOU C
   SHEN J
   YU Y
   HU C
   WANG L
AE FUQUAN ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION CITY DEV CO LTD (FUQU-Non-standard)
GA 202102211G
AB    NOVELTY - Recycling phosphogypsum comprises drying phosphogypsum, crushing, repeatedly washing with water, washing with weak acid solution, and drying to obtain primary powder; adding primary powder to leaching device, adding ammonium chloride solution, leaching under stirring while introducing micro-nanobubbles at bottom of leaching device, subjecting material liquid to liquid-solid separation, and collecting leaching solution; and dropping ammonia water to leaching solution, introducing carbon dioxide small bubbles, stirring, standing and precipitating, filtering, washing solid, and drying to obtain calcium carbonate.
   USE - The method is for recycling phosphogypsum.
   ADVANTAGE - The method recovers calcium source from phosphogypsum and has simple operation and no secondary pollution to the environment.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The ammonium chloride solution has concentration of 1-2 mol/L. The ratio of ammonium chloride solution to primary powder is 10-15:1. The micro-nanobubbles have average diameter of 20-30 nm. The ratio of ammonia water to phosphogypsum is 10-20:1. The carbon dioxide small bubbles have average diameter of 1-3 mu m. Preferred Method: The leaching step is performed at 55-65 degrees C for 1-2 hours. The micro-nanobubbles are added at 20-30 mL/minute. The carbon dioxide small bubbles are added at 45-55 mL/minute. Stirring is performed at 30 degrees C and 300-500 revolutions/minute for 1-2 hours.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The weak acid solution is 3-5 wt.% citric acid solution.

   USE - The formulation is useful: as mosquito attractant trapping and/or killing mosquitoes, where the mosquitoes are Culex mosquitoes; and as part of a mosquito trapping device (all claimed). No biological data given.
   ADVANTAGE - The formulation: mimics the relevant odor; and is suitable for storage and/or transport.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) attracting and optionally trapping and/or killing mosquitoes, comprising providing the mosquito attractant formulation;
   (2) a combination product or kit-of-parts comprising the formulation, and a source of gaseous carbon dioxide; and
   (3) a device for attracting and optionally trapping and/or killing mosquitoes, comprising the mosquito attractant.

The formulation consists essentially of each of 3-octanone, heptanal, benzaldehyde, sulcatone, octanal, nonanal and decanal. The formulation is in the form of liquid, a solution in a solvent, gas or a liquified gas. Preferred Components: The formulation is provided in conjunction with a solid or semi-solid carrier, where the carrier comprises biodegradable porous plastic granules or wax. The product or kit or device further comprises a mosquito control agent. Preferred Method: The method comprises mixing the formulation with gaseous carbon dioxide.

   MECHANISM OF ACTION - None given.

PN CN110950328-A
TI Preparation of doped graphene powder involves electrifying and heating mixture of magnesium powder, magnesium oxide powder and doping agent, carrying out self-propagation synthesis and mixing with dilute acid solution.
AU LI C
   ZHANG X
   MA Y
AE INST ELECTRICAL ENG CHINESE ACAD SCI (CADG-C)
GA 202028298Q
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of doped graphene powder involves mixing magnesium powder, magnesium oxide powder and doping agent, placing the mixed powder in a sealed container, introducing carbon dioxide gas into sealed container, electrifying and heating, carrying out self-propagating synthesis to obtain the black powder, mixing the black powder with dilute acid solution, leaving still and drying.
   USE - Doped graphene powder.
   ADVANTAGE - The doped graphene powder has large specific area and pore rate, and is prepared by a simple and easy process in high yield using a wide source of raw materials. The preparation process does not cause pollution and effectively prevents the agglomeration of the powder.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The mass ratio of magnesium powder and magnesium oxide powder is 1:4-10. The dopant is calcium metaborate, calcium sulfate, calcium phosphate, melamine, thiourea and/or urea. The dopant is in powder form with average particle size of 1-100 mu m. The mass ratio of magnesium powder and dopant is 1:0.1-2. The electrification is carried out applying DC power to two ends of metal coil made of tungsten, nickel or chromium. The dilute acid is 0.1-1 mol/L hydrochloric acid solution or nitric acid solution. Preferred Properties: The doped graphene powder has average pore diameter of 2-50 nm and BET specific surface area of 100-500 m2/g.

   ADVANTAGE - (I): has excellent thermal stability and excellent processability; and exhibits typical aggregation-induced luminescence properties due to the introduction of aggregation-induced luminescent groups.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Polycyclic carbonate compound of formula (I) are new.
   n = 1-200;
   R1, R2 = organic groups including triphenylamine-4,4'-diyl, propane-2,2-diyldibenzene-4,4'diyl, dimethyldiphenylsilane-4,4'-diyl, cyclic compounds of formula (II)-(III);
   m, k, h = 1-20; and
   X = N, O, P, S or Si.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparation of (I).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The catalyst is palladium(II) acetate, palladium(II) pivalate, palladium(II) trifluoroacetate, tris(dibenzylideneacetone)dipalladium(0), bis(tri-tert-butylphosphine)palladium(0), dichlorobis(triphenylphosphine)palladium(II), palladium(II) dichloride, silver tungstate, silver iodide, silver nitrate, silver fluoroborate, silver chloride, silver bromide, silver oxide, silver acetate, cuprous chloride, cuprous bromide, cuprous iodide, cuprous cyanide and/or cuprous oxide, the catalyst is 1-40 mol.% of the difunctional acetylenic monomer. The base is cesium carbonate, potassium carbonate, sodium carbonate, potassium hydroxide, sodium hydroxide, cesium fluoride, potassium fluoride, sodium fluoride, cesium hydroxide, potassium t-butoxide, sodium t-butoxide, lithium tert-butoxide, 1,5,7-triazabicyclo(4.4.0)dec-5-ene and/or cesium acetate, and the amount of base is 100-1000% of molar quantity of the bifunctional acetylenic monomer.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The organic solvent is tetrahydrofuran, dichloromethane, chloroform, toluene, 1,4-dioxane, methyl sulfoxide, N,N-dimethylformamide, N,N-dimethylacetamide, acetonitrile, ethanol, N-methylpyrrolidone, dimethyl carbonate, diethyl carbonate, ethylene carbonate, carbonic acid and/or propylene esters. The concentration of bifunctional acetylenic monomer and halogenated monomer in organic solvent is 0.05-5 mol/l. The precipitating agent is methanol or n-hexane.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preparation (Claimed): Preparation of (I) comprises carrying out polymerization of bifunctional acetylenic monomer of formula (II), carbon dioxide and halogenated monomer of formula (III) under the action of catalyst and a base in organic solvent under carbon dioxide, and separating the reaction product to obtain final product.A = CI, Br or I.Preferred Method: The polymerization temperature is 0-200 degrees C and the time is 0.25-72 hours. The separation comprises adding the product into precipitating agent for precipitation, and collecting the precipitate and drying to obtain (I).

PN RU2677374-C1
TI Method of endoscopic treatment of destructive pancreatitis comprises, major duodenal papilla using angiographic guide located in channel of endoscopic instrument.
AU KULMINSKII A V
   KULMINSKII M A
   BASYROV R T
   BEDA A S
AE BASYROV R T (BASY-Individual)
   KULMINSKII A V (KULM-Individual)
GA 201907386D
AB    NOVELTY - Invention relates to medicine and may be applicable for endoscopic treatment of destructive pancreatitis. Endoscope is guided through the mouth into the patient's duodenum. Endoscopic instrument - catheter is used in the endoscope for ERCPG (endoscopic retrograde cholangiopancreatography). Catheter for ERCPG is cannulated at the mouth of the major duodenal papilla using an angiographic guide located in the channel of the endoscopic instrument. Remove the angiographic guide. Carry out insufflation of carbon dioxide under the pressure of 400mm of water column in a volume of 300cubic meters. See the main pancreatic duct.
   USE - Medicine.
   ADVANTAGE - Method allows to reduce the activity of digestive enzymes secreted from the pancreas.

PT P
PN CN108928979-A; CN108928979-B
TI Treating waste liquid from production of ethyl chloride comprises hydrolyzing ethyl chloride waste liquid by adding ammonia, oxidizing waste liquid produced by hydrolysis under air and evaporating oxidized solution to extract ammonium salt.
AU SHEN J
   WU L
AE HANGZHOU SHENRUI WATER WORKS CO LTD (HANG-Non-standard)
GA 201899967N
AB    NOVELTY - Process for treating a waste liquid from the production of ethyl chloride, comprises (i) hydrolyzing the ethyl chloride waste liquid by adding ammonia, (ii) oxidizing the waste liquid produced by the hydrolysis under air, and (iii) evaporating the oxidized solution to extract an ammonium salt.
   USE - The process is useful for treating waste liquid from the production of ethyl chloride.
   ADVANTAGE - The process degrades the organic matter in the waste liquid, degrades malodorous thiol and thioether into carbon dioxide, water and sulfate, and discharges water without a foul smell. The extracted ammonium salt can be used for chemical fertilizer production.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ENVIRONMENT - Preferred Method: The waste liquid in step (i) is diluted 2-21 times before hydrolysis, where the hydrolysis is carried out at 95-245 degrees C and at a pH of 8-12 under sealed condition. The waste liquid produced by the hydrolysis in step (ii) is oxidized at 160-300 degrees C under a pressure of 6-9.9 MPa using a catalyst. The air supply amount (Nm3/hour) or the amount of waste water (m3/hour) in step (ii) is 40-400 times. The step (iii) involves performing multi-effect evaporation or mechanical vapor recompression for evaporation to extract salt.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The ammonia used in step (i) is industrial ammonia or ammonia gas. The catalyst is a copper-silver bimetallic nanoparticle. The ammonium salt is ammonium hydrogen phosphate, ammonium sulfate and/or ammonium chloride.
DC C04 (Fertilisers - including urea and phosphoric acid production. Also soil modifiers and plant growth media. Chemical aspects of compost production.); D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).)

PN EP3296009-A1; WO2018054928-A1; EP3296009-B1; IN201947010881-A; KR2019051053-A; CN109789373-A; US2019368399-A1; JP2019537500-W; US10961886-B2; IN368707-B; JP7008692-B2; CN109789373-B
TI Particulate filter used for removing nitrogen oxide from exhaust gas, comprises wall flow filter comprising channels extending in parallel between ends of filter, and selective catalytically reduced-active material containing palladium.
AU SCHULER A
   DORNHAUS F
AE UMICORE & CO AG KG (UMIC-C)
   UMICORE & CO AG KG (UMIC-C)
   UMICORE & CO AG KG (UMIC-C)
   UMICORE & CO AG KG (UMIC-C)
GA 201821841A
AB    NOVELTY - A particulate filter comprises a wall flow filter, and a selective catalytically reduced-active material. The filter comprises channels extending in parallel between ends of the filter, alternately gas-tightly sealed at one of the ends separated by porous walls. The active material is in the form of a coating in the porous walls of the filter, and contains palladium.
   USE - Particulate filter is used for removing nitrogen oxide from exhaust gas of combustion engine.
   ADVANTAGE - The particulate filter has improved low temperature activity and nitrogen oxide removal rate, and reduced emission of carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: Palladium is homogeneously distributed in the active material, and is supported on a carrier comprising alumina, silica, magnesia and/or titania. The content of palladium is 10-1000 ppm. The active material contains vanadium-containing or vanadium-free mixed oxides, and small-pore zeolite which is exchanged with copper and/or iron, preferably copper-exchanged chabazite-type zeolite and palladium, a copper-exchanged zeolite of the chabazite type and palladium, and/or a support material supported palladium. The small-pore zeolite is AEI-type zeolite, CHA-type zeolite, erionite-type zeolite, Levyne-type zeolite and KFI-type zeolite. Preferred Property: The carried has a BET surface area of 30-250 m2/g, determined according to DIN 66132.

PN CN111635797-A; CN212451329-U
TI Device for production and carbon dioxide recovery of biological natural gas, comprises circulating tank which top portion is provided with storage airbag, and biomass is crushed by pulverizer.
AU LIU J
AE HEBEI HONEST EXPRESS YANGPU NEW ENERGY (HEBE-Non-standard)
GA 202088059N
AB    NOVELTY - Bio-natural gas production and carbon dioxide recovery device comprises bio-natural gas production device, a desulfurization treatment device, a methane gas purification and recovery device. The biological natural gas production device includes a pulverizer (1), a fermentation tank (2), a sump (3), a circulation tank (5), where top of the circulating tank is provided with a storage airbag, biomass is crushed by the pulverizer and transported to the fermentation tank for fermentation. The bottom of the fermentation tank is connected to the sump through a pipe, and the outlet of the sump is connected to the circulation tank. The bottom of the circulation tank is connected with the fermentation tank. The outlet of the storage airbag is connected to the double-membrane gas tank (6) for biogas storage through a pipe.
   USE - Device for production and carbon dioxide recovery of bio-natural gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The bio-natural gas production and carbon dioxide recovery device has an ingenious concept, compact and reasonable layout, uses renewable biomass, livestock manure, and organic waste as raw materials to prepare combustible gas biogas rich in methane, and purify biogas for desulfurization, decarbonization, produces high-calorific, high-value gas, namely bio-natural gas, recycles by-product carbon dioxide, increase use of gas and reduced environmental pollution.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for production of bio-natural gas and carbon dioxide recovery, which involves:
   (A) crushing crop straw materials into 2-8mm scraps by the pulverizer, transporting organic biomass materials containing crop straw scraps, livestock manure, and domestic garbage directly to the fermentation tank;
   (B) transporting biogas slurry in the circulation tank to the biogas heating heat exchanger by the circulation pump and heating, then entering top of the fermentation tank and spraying on the biomass materials through the liquid distribution pipe, carrying out medium-temperature fermentation at 30-40 degrees C;
   (C) flowing down biogas slurry at the bottom of the fermentation tank into the sump, transporting to the circulation tank by the biogas slurry pump, and recycling after heating;
   (D) outputting biogas produced by fermentation in the fermentation tank from the top into the storage airbag and transporting through the pipeline to the double membrane gas tank for storage;
   (E) pressurizing into the desulfurization tank after the biogas stored in the double membrane gas tank is separated from the gas and liquid to remove the free water and using desulfurized gas directly as fuel for local use or for biogas power generation facilities for biomass power generation;
   (F) entering desulfurized gas to carbon dioxide adsorption regeneration tank, adsorbing carbon dioxide and water vapor, meeting the national standard natural gas heating value requirements, using bio-natural gas by users, opening the valve on the pipeline connected with the methane storage tank, and closing the valve on the pipeline connected with the carbon dioxide purification and recovery device;
   (G) performing decompression regeneration to regenerate carbon dioxide gas as a by-product when the carbon dioxide adsorption regeneration tank is saturated with adsorption, passing carbon dioxide gas passes the carbon dioxide purifier, providing connecting pipe between the condenser and the liquid carbon dioxide storage tank with an expansion tank and an ice press; and
   (H) passing liquid carbon dioxide through the expansion tank and the ice press to make solid carbon dioxide and storing in a solid carbon dioxide storage tank.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows schematic view of device for production and carbon dioxide recovery of biological natural gas.
   Pulverizer (1)
   Fermentation tank (2)
   Sump (3)
   Circulation tank (5)
   Double-membrane gas tank (6)

AU SHI M
   MIN X
   LI Q
   WU G
   CHAI L
   WANG Q
AE UNIV CENT SOUTH (UYCS-C)
GA 202225867C
AB    NOVELTY - Purifying and recycling device comprises a recycling cavity assembly, a dust removing assembly, a compression buffer gas tank assembly, carbon dioxide purifying and recycling sub-system and carbon dioxide circulation sub-system. The recycling cavity assembly comprises partitions. Between the top of the partition and the indirect heating dolomite calcining furnace and the outer wall of the blanking chamber is surrounded to form an exhaust chamber. The feeding port of the dolomite calcining furnace, the blanking port of the blanking chamber and the exhaust port of the dolomite calcining furnace are connected with the exhaust chamber, which is connected with the dust removing assembly. The compression buffer gas tank assembly comprises a first air outlet and a second air outlet. The carbon dioxide cycle sub-system comprises a gas pipeline, a carbon dioxide water steam recycling pipe, a gas-liquid separator, a first recycling pipeline and a second recycling pipeline.
   USE - The device is used for purifying, separating and recycling dolomite calcined carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - Primary calcination, carbonization, pyrolysis and secondary calcination in the dolomite calcium-magnesium separation process are organically combined with the purification and recycling device, and thus the purification and recycling of carbon dioxide in the dolomite deep processing are completed. Through setting of the recycling cavity assembly, the feeding port and the exhaust chamber form a closed exhaust system, so as to construct a primary calcining section of carbon dioxide purifying and recycling passage, the exhaust system can avoid the flue gas contact from the outside, so that the flue gas self-generated to the collection is in a relatively independent environment, ensuring the initial purity of the carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Purifying and recycling device comprises a recycling cavity assembly, a dust removing assembly, a compression buffer gas tank assembly, carbon dioxide purifying and recycling sub-system and carbon dioxide circulation sub-system. The recycling cavity assembly comprises partitions. Between the top of the partition and the indirect heating dolomite calcining furnace and the outer wall of the blanking chamber is surrounded to form an exhaust chamber. The feeding port of the dolomite calcining furnace, the blanking port of the blanking chamber and the exhaust port of the dolomite calcining furnace are connected with the exhaust chamber, which is connected with the dust removing assembly through an exhaust pipe. The air inlet of the compression buffer gas tank assembly is connected with the outlet of the dust removing assembly, and the carbon dioxide (CO2) gas is compressed and stored. The compression buffer gas tank assembly comprises a first air outlet and a second air outlet. The carbon dioxide purifying and recycling sub-system is connected with the first air outlet. The carbon dioxide cycle sub-system comprises a gas pipeline, a carbon dioxide water steam recycling pipe, a gas-liquid separator, a first recycling pipeline and a second recycling pipeline. The gas conveying pipeline is connected with the second air outlet and the carbonization system of dolomite calcined calcium-magnesium separation technique. The carbon dioxide water steam recycling pipe is connected with the smoke gas outlet of the heavy magnesium water pyrolysis reactor of the dolomite calcined calcium-magnesium separation process and the air inlet of the gas-liquid separator. The first recycling pipeline is connected with the air outlet of the gas-liquid separator and the air inlet of the compression buffer gas tank assembly. The second recycling pipeline is connected with the smoke gas outlet of the magnesium carbonate calcining furnace of the dolomite calcined calcium-magnesium separating technique and the air inlet of the dust removing component.
