TI Performing decarbonization from a centralized            carbon dioxide emission source based on wet-process            regeneration of carbon dioxide capture material            involves condensing the flue gas from the centralized            carbon dioxide emission source by an alkaline solution,            and heating.
AU CHEN X
   ZHU L
   LI F
   WANG K
AE LINHE CLIMATE TECHNOLOGY BEIJING CO LTD (LINH-Non-standard)
GA 2022772038
AB    NOVELTY - Performing decarbonization from a centralized                carbon dioxide emission source based on wet-process                regeneration of carbon dioxide capture material                involves condensing the flue gas from the                centralized carbon dioxide (CO2) emission source by                an alkaline solution, heating to not lower than                15&#8451; or higher than the saturation                temperature of the flue gas, and passing into                various groups of parallel carbon capture units                equipped with wet-process regenerated CO2capture                materials, and drying the wet-process-regenerated                CO2capture materials. The wet regenerated                CO2capture material is used after drying to carry                out CO2adsorption, and then carried out                CO2desorption to the above-mentioned wet                regenerated CO2capture material after CO2adsorption                by unit of water spraying.
   USE - Method for performing decarbonization from a                centralized carbon dioxide emission source based on                wet-process regeneration of carbon dioxide capture                material
   ADVANTAGE - The method adopts specific steps, conditions                and parameters to achieve overall better                interaction, can reduce material, equipment and                operating costs, improve product gas purity, and                has broad application prospects.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Performing decarbonization from a centralized                carbon dioxide emission source based on wet-process                regeneration of carbon dioxide capture material                involves condensing the flue gas from the                centralized carbon dioxide (CO2) emission source by                an alkaline solution, heating to not lower than                15&#8451; or higher than the saturation                temperature of the flue gas, and passing into                various groups of parallel carbon capture units                equipped with wet-process regenerated CO2capture                materials, and drying the wet-process-regenerated                CO2capture materials. The wet regenerated                CO2capture material is used after drying to carry                out CO2adsorption, and then carried out                CO2desorption to the above-mentioned wet                regenerated CO2capture material after CO2adsorption                by unit of water spraying. A vacuum pump is used to                pump the carbon monoxide (CO) into a buffer device,                and then dehumidifying and compressing in turn to                obtain CO gas storage. The various groups of                parallel carbon capture units equipped with wet                regenerated CO2 capture materials performed                continuous gas production or intermittent gas                production to achieve decarbonization from a                centralized carbon dioxide emission source.

The                upper surface of the test block is provided with a                mounting hole for the discharge end of the carbon                dioxide breaker. The pressure data collection                element is set in the test block and is used to                collect the burst pressure of the carbon dioxide                fracturing device after the energy is discharged.                The electronic terminal of the pressure data                collection element is electrically connected with a                data processing module (30).
   USE - Carbon dioxide cracker blasting pressure and                effect experimental system.

The sytem is convenient                to carry out experiments on the burst pressure and                effect of the carbon dioxide cracker.

PN CN107998685-A
TI Extracting Chinese medicinal herb involves cleaning Chinese herbal medicine, drying, crushing to powder, softening, spraying warm water of Chinese herbal medicine, performing supercritical carbon dioxide fluid extraction and adding modifier.
AU 
AE CHANGSHA WUDAO IND DESIGN CO LTD (CHAN-Non-standard)
GA 201838634W
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting Chinese medicinal herb involves cleaning Chinese herbal medicine, drying, crushing to powder with granularity of 100 micrometers-1 millimeters, softening for 2-3 hours at 30-60 degrees C, spraying 30-50 wt.% warm water of Chinese herbal medicine, performing supercritical carbon dioxide fluid extraction, adding modifier in carbon dioxide fluid, where modifier is mixture of methanol, lecithin and stearic acid, adding 8-16 wt.% carbon dioxide fluid carrying agent, controlling extracting temperature at 80-100 degrees C, where pressure is 12-18 megaPascals and time is 1-1.5 hours.
   USE - Method for extracting Chinese medicinal herb.
   ADVANTAGE - The method needs low flow carbon dioxide in extraction process, which enables to realize efficient extraction, improve extraction yield and save energy.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Extracting Chinese medicinal herb involves cleaning Chinese herbal medicine, drying, crushing to powder with granularity of 100 micrometers-1 millimeters, softening for 2-3 hours at 30-60 degrees C, spraying 30-50 wt.% warm water of Chinese herbal medicine, performing supercritical carbon dioxide fluid extraction, adding modifier in carbon dioxide fluid, where modifier is mixture of methanol, lecithin and stearic acid, adding 8-16 wt.% carbon dioxide fluid carrying agent, controlling extracting temperature at 80-100 degrees C, where pressure is 12-18 megaPascals and time is 1-1.5 hours and separating extract, where carrying agent is ethyl acetate. The extraction of Chinese medicinal herb further involves adding modifying agent of carbon dioxide fluid with 30-50 wt.% carbon dioxide fluid-carrying agent, extracting, controlling extracting temperature to 90-110 degrees C, where pressure is 25-30 megaPascals and time is 1.5-2 hours, separating extract, forwarding rest portion to next, where second lifting carrying agent is acetone, adding third carrying agent with modifying agent of carbon dioxide fluid, adding 60-70 wt.% carbon dioxide fluid, extracting at 100-120 degrees C for 1-2 hours, where pressure is 25-30 megaapascal, separating extract from herb and purifying, where third lifting carrying agent is mixture of ethanol and acetonitrile.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Compositions: The dosage of modifier is 1.2-2.3 wt.%. The weight proportion of methanol, lecithin and stearic acid is (1-2):(2-3):(1-2). The mixing weight ratio of ethanol and acetonitrile is 6:1. Preferred Conditions: The temperature of warm water is 30-60 degrees C. The flow rate of carbon dioxide is 10-12 l/hour.

PN CN113731353-A
TI Preparation of carbon dioxide adsorbent material used in fixed bed reactor, involves mixing carbon dioxide-adsorbed silicon dioxide-loaded amine material, aluminum source, ZSM-5 seed crystal, sodium metasilicate nonahydrate, and ammonium chloride, reacting, washing and drying.
AU LI C
   ZHANG C
AE HARBIN INST TECHNOLOGY SHENZHEN GRADUATE (HAIT-C)
GA 2021E52947
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of carbon dioxide adsorbent material involves mixing 5 g/L silicon dioxide powder with deionized water, ultrasonically dispersing to obtain dispersion, adding amine to dispersion, stirring for 0.5-1.5 hours, freeze-drying to obtain silicon dioxide-loaded amine material, vacuum degassing at 150degrees Celsius for 12 hours, subjecting to carbon dioxide adsorption at 40degrees Celsius under pressure of 0-50 bar, mixing silicon dioxide-loaded amine material after saturated adsorption of carbon dioxide with aluminum source, ZSM-5 seed crystal, sodium metasilicate nonahydrate, and ammonium chloride, adding to closed reactor, heating and reacting, washing and drying.
   USE - Preparation of carbon dioxide adsorbent material used in fixed bed reactor (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The preparation method is easy, safe and environmentally-friendly, and does not use any organic solvent during reaction, and realizes precise and uniform modification of the surface of the three-dimensional pores of the amine and produces adsorbent material having similar advantages of existing both aluminum-based and silicon-based material.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for application method of the carbon dioxide adsorbent material, which involves feeding the carbon dioxide adsorbent into fixed bed reactor, vacuum degassing at 150degrees Celsius under pressure of 0.013 bar and performing carbon dioxide adsorption at 30-60degrees Celsius under pressure of 0-50 bar.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The silicon dioxide powder is precipitation method silicon dioxide or gas phase method silicon dioxide. The aluminum source is aluminum salt or aluminum oxides.Preferred Composition: The amount of ZSM-5 seed crystal added is 1-20 %mass of the mass of silicon dioxide. The mass ratio of silicon dioxide to aluminum oxide of aluminum source is 25 to infinity.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The amine is one or more of tetraethylenepentamine, triethylenetetramine, diethanolamine, polyethylenimine, tripropylamine, triisopropanolamine, formamide, p-phenylenediamine, and phthalimide.Preferred Composition: The amount of loaded amine is 0.2-50 %mass of the mass of silicon dioxide in stable dispersion.

GA 202262742C

   USE - The photocatalyst is used for reducing carbon dioxide by light irradiation, for producing methane (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The photocatalyst is capable of efficiently reducing carbon dioxide by light irradiation. The method achieves a high methane production rate by light irradiation. The photocatalyst is possible to form a carbon-neutral cycle in which the carbon dioxide is returned to the fuel against the irreversible generation of carbon dioxide associated with the use of the existing fossil fuel. The photocatalyst can contribute to solving global warming and energy problems. The photocatalyst provides higher catalytic activity, and is capable of converting carbon dioxide into methane. The photocatalyst has high catalytic activity and high selectivity for methane in the photoreduction reaction of carbon dioxide. The photocatalyst stably converts carbon dioxide into useful methane as a fuel for a long period of time.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for producing a methane, involving contacting the photocatalyst with a mixed gas containing carbon dioxide and hydrogen gas, and irradiating the photocatalyst with light containing either one or both of UV light and visible light to generate methane.

Preferred Composition: The amount of metallic nickel supported is 1-35 %mass, preferably 7-18 %mass in terms of nickel atoms in the photocatalyst. TI Method for freezing and removing carbon dioxide gas in air in closed space by cooling energy liquid oxygen, involves determining circulating air volume and concentration difference between inlet and outlet of freezing device.
AU PENG W
   MAO Z
   WANG Y
   CAO T
AE CHINA SHIP DEV & DESIGN CENT (CSHI-C)
GA 202032869M
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves obtaining cooling energy by using return cooling air and cooling energy by liquid oxygen. Air is passed into a freezing device, where the air is passed through a deep cooling device. Carbon dioxide contained in the freezing device is frozen into dry ice for removing carbon dioxide gas in the air. Target cooling energy is obtained for freezing the carbon dioxide contained in the freezing device into the dry ice. Circulating air volume and concentration difference between an inlet and an outlet of the freezing device are determined according to incoming air of a water removal device.
   USE - Method for freezing and removing carbon dioxide gas in air in closed space by cooling energy liquid oxygen.

PT P
PN CN113649056-A
TI Photocatalytic carbon dioxide reduction catalyst useful for reducing carbon dioxide to carbon monoxide under photocatalysis.
AU ZHU B
   XU Q
   QIN Y
AE UNIV GUANGXI NORMAL (UNGT-C)
GA 2021D5461L
AB    NOVELTY - Photocatalytic carbon dioxide reduction catalyst, is claimed. The reduction catalyst is nickel-aluminum-layered double hydroxide (LDH)/porous carbon nitride foam three-dimensional heterojunction composite material.
   USE - The catalyst is useful for reducing carbon dioxide to carbon monoxide under photocatalysis (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst: improves the photocatalytic performance of the composite material; and reaches CO yield to 159.62 micromol/g; and improves the yield of the photocatalytic carbon dioxide reduction reaction.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing photocatalytic carbon dioxide reduction catalyst, comprising (i) preparing three-dimensional network structure of melamine porous carbon nitride foam, (ii) preparing aqueous precursor solution of hydrotalcite, and (iii) placing porous carbon nitride foam three-dimensional network structure into the hydrotalcite precursor aqueous solution and reacting to obtain porous carbon nitride foam/hydrotalcite three-dimensional heterojunction material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The specific process of step (i) includes heating the melamine sponge to 600degrees Celsius for 100 minutes at heating rate of 10degrees Celsius/minutes in a nitrogen atmosphere, and then naturally cooling to room temperature, alternately washing with deionized water and ethanol, and drying to obtain porous carbon nitride foam rich in graphitic carbon nitride, and melamine porous carbon nitride foam presents a smooth three-dimensional network structure. The specific process of step (ii) includes dissolving 0.872-3.488 g nickel(II) nitrate hexahydrate, 0.375-1.5 g aluminum nitrate hexahydrate, 2.402 g urea and 0.593 g ammonium fluoride in 70 ml deionized water, and stirring to obtain homogeneous aqueous solution of the hydrotalcite precursor. The specific process of step (iii) includes immersing melamine porous carbon nitride foam three-dimensional network structure in the reaction solution of the autoclave and ultrasonically treating for 30 minutes, sealing, placing in a 120degrees Celsius oven for hydrothermal treatment for 8 hours, finishing reaction, and naturally cooling to room temperature, taking out, washing with deionized water and absolute ethanol for 3 times for removing the impurities adsorbed on the surface of the composite material, and drying in an oven at 60degrees Celsius to obtain a porous carbon nitride foam/hydrotalcite three-dimensional heterojunction material. The specific process of reduction includes placing the reduction catalyst in the reactor, heating water bath to obtain steam, bringing the mixed gas of steam and carbon dioxide into the reactor by passing carbon dioxide gas into the reactor, purging for specific period of time, and then adding light to the outside of the reactor, sampling 1 ml gas products from the reaction chamber for every hour, and then using gas chromatograph equipped with FID and TCD detectors for analysis. The method comprises before introducing the mixed gas, first passing carbon dioxide gas for purge 1.5 hours for removing the air in the reactor, where the aeration rate is 10 ml/minutes, and the reactor is transparent quartz reactor, using 300W xenon lamp for simulating sunlight, the distance between the xenon lamp and the catalytic reduction material is 8 cm, volume of the reactor is 200 ml, mixed gas is equilibrated in the atmosphere for 30 minutes, and ensuring complete adsorption of gas molecules.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

PN US2021317587-A1
TI Reduction of carbon dioxide used in production of            ethylene, involves contacting carbon dioxide with            cathode housed in flow-through electrolysis cell by            flowing electrolyte through cathode comprising            hierarchical nanoporous metal.
AU VEDHARATHINAM V
   STADERMANN M
   QI Z
   LIANG S
   BIENER J
   BIENER M M
AE LAWRENCE LIVERMORE NAT SECURITY LLC (USGO-C)
GA 2021B8244M
AB    NOVELTY - Reduction of carbon dioxide involves                contacting carbon dioxide with a cathode housed in                a flow-through electrolysis cell by flowing the                electrolyte through the cathode. The cathode                comprises a hierarchical nanoporous metal. The                flow-through electrolysis cell comprises an anode                and an ion-exchange membrane. The anode comprises a                metallic mesh. The carbon dioxide is dissolved in                an electrolyte.
   USE - Reduction of carbon dioxide used in production                of ethylene as feedstock for producing plastics,                surfactants, detergents and polymer.
   ADVANTAGE - The method utilizes hierarchical nanoporous                metal with improved stability against                electrochemical potential.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                anion exchange membrane in the cell.
   1Electrolyte-in line
   2 Endcap
   3,5Gaskets
   4Frame
   6Reservoir
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The method further                  involves collecting a reduction product                  comprising hydrocarbon, an aldehyde, an alcohol,                  a ketone, and/or carboxylic acid or reduction                  product comprising ethylene and/or methane, and                  monitoring the reduction product via gas                  chromatography mass spectrometry.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The method preferably                  involves flowing electrolyte by applying a                  pressure gradient of 0.1-10 atm across the                  cathode through the cathode at a velocity of less                  than 1 cm/second.Preferred Components: The hierarchical                  nanoporous metal comprises one or more of copper,                  platinum, silver, gold, nickel, iron, and zinc.                  The hierarchical nanoporous metal is hierarchical                  nanoporous copper. The hierarchical nanoporous                  metal comprises nanopores with an average                  diameter of 10-500 nm and macropores with an                  average diameter of about 500-1&#215;106nm.                  The metallic mesh comprises one or more of                  platinum, palladium, carbon and boron-doped                  carbon/diamond. The hierarchical nanoporous metal                  is a catalytic metal for reducing reactant which                  contacts the hierarchical nanoporous metal.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ELECTRICAL POWER AND ENERGY - Preferred Components: The cathode is between                  an electrolyte-in line (1) and an electrolyte-out                  line of the flow-through electrolysis cell. The                  cathode comprises a face (F1) and an opposite                  facing face (F2). The flow-through electrolysis                  cell further comprises an electrolytic fluid                  input (I1) proximal to the face (F1) and an                  electrolytic fluid input (O1) proximal to the                  face (F2). AU JIN F

   ZHONG H
AE UNIV SHANGHAI JIAOTONG (USJT-C)
GA 201929482R
AB    NOVELTY - The method for hydrothermally reducing carbon dioxide involves adding iron powder, cobalt powder and carbon dioxide/bicarbonate source to hydrothermal reaction kettle at mass ratio of 1:(0.1-10):(0.5-10), adding water, controlling filling rate of hydrothermal reaction kettle at 10-80%, adjusting pH to 8-12, reacting at 150-400 degrees C for 0.5-12 hours and controlling pressure of 1-42 MPa.
   USE - Method for hydrothermally reducing carbon dioxide for producing long-chain alkane.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables hydrothermally reducing carbon dioxide ensuring increased purity and faster production rate of long-chain alkanes by simple and efficient process.

The cobalt powder is recycled. Preferred Process: The cobalt powder is reshaped into a honeycomb cobalt nanosheet in the reaction and formed into active center for the formation of long-chain alkane.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Components: The iron powder is nano iron, commercial iron powder, metal alloy powder containing iron or powder formed by breaking iron-containing domestic waste. Preferred Properties: The particle diameter of iron powder is 100-400 mesh. The particle diameter of cobalt powder is 100-200 mesh.

   BACKOV R

GA 201885688Q
AB    NOVELTY - Photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide                involves contacting a feed containing carbon                dioxide and sacrificial compound(s) with a                photocatalyst in the form of a porous monolith                having a bulk density of less than or equal to 0.25                g/ml, and irradiating the photocatalyst with                irradiation source(s) producing wavelength(s) less                than the forbidden band width of the photocatalyst,                at a temperature of -10 degrees C to 200 degrees C                and at a pressure of 0.01-70 MPa.
   USE - Photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide                into valuable product e.g. fuel and industrial                chemical.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables stable, economical,                efficient and environmentally-friendly                photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The photocatalyst                  comprises 5-70 wt.% semiconductor(s) as a                  monolith, and inorganic phase(s) containing                  silica or alumina not absorbing photons of energy                  higher than 4 eV. The semiconductor is chosen                  from titanium oxide, zinc oxide, copper(I) oxide,                  copper(II) oxide, cerium(III) oxide, cerium                  dioxide, indium(III) oxide, silicon carbide, zinc                  sulfide, and indium sulfide. Preferred                  Properties: The photocatalyst has a mesoporous                  volume of 0.01-1 ml/g, and a pore diameter of                  more than 0.2 nm to less than or equal to 50 nm.                  The photocatalyst has a type I macroporous volume                  of 0.1-3 ml/g, and a pore diameter of more than                  50 nm to less than or equal to 1000 nm, and a                  type II macroporous volume of 0.1-8 ml/g, and a                  pore diameter of more than 1 mu m to less than or                  equal to 10 mu m, and a macroporous volume of                  less than 0.5 ml/g, and a pore diameter of more                  than 10 mu m. The photocatalyst has bulk density                  of preferably less than 0.19 g/ml, and a specific                  surface area of 10-1000 m2/g.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The photocatalytic                  reduction of carbon dioxide is performed in a gas                  phase, and the sacrificial compound is a gaseous                  compound chosen from water, ammonia, hydrogen,                  methane, and alcohol. The photocatalytic                  reduction of carbon dioxide is performed in a                  liquid phase, and the sacrificial compound is a                  soluble liquid or solid compound chosen from                  water, ammonia, alcohol, aldehyde, and amine. The                  irradiation source is an artificial or natural                  irradiation source.

PT P
PN CN108404981-A; CN108404981-B
TI Producing hydrogen by reducing carbon dioxide with alkali metal hydride involves placing alkali metal hydride in ball mill tank under argon atmosphere, extracting argon gas from ball mill tank, and charging high purity carbon dioxide.
AU LI H
   WANG Y
   HAN B
AE UNIV JINAN SHANDONG (UNJN-C)
GA 201866846Q
AB    NOVELTY - Producing hydrogen by reducing carbon dioxide with alkali metal hydride involves placing an alkali metal hydride in a ball mill tank under an argon atmosphere, extracting argon gas from the ball mill tank, and charging high purity carbon dioxide gas at room temperature, and using ball mill for the ball milling reaction to obtain final product.
   USE - Method for producing hydrogen by reducing carbon dioxide with alkali metal hydride.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to produce hydrogen by reducing carbon dioxide with alkali metal hydride in simple manner, which has good catalyst stability, degradable and environment-friendly, is easy to recycle, has mild reaction condition, high catalytic activity, and requires small dosage.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The alkali metal hydride is selected from sodium hydride or potassium hydride. The hydrogen produced is free of ammonia and COx. Preferred Conditions: The ball mill tank is a stainless steel ball mill tank and the ball mill medium is 30 steel balls. The mass ratio of steel balls to alkali metal hydride during the ball milling reaction is 90:1. The ball mill has a rotation speed of 450 rpm, a ball milling reaction time of 0.5-2 hours, and carbo dioxide gas pressure of 0.25 MPa. The molar ratio of alkali metal hydride to carbon dioxide gas is 4:1.

PT P
PN DE102020000937-A1
TI Method for producing industrial product involves providing gas mixture as feed gas containing oxygen and carbon dioxide to industrial process for producing industrial product by oxidation, using carbon dioxide electrolysis.
AU HENTSCHEL B
   PESCHEL A
   SCHODEL N
AE LINDE GMBH (LIND-Non-standard)
GA 2021954520
AB    NOVELTY - Method (100) for producing an industrial product (P) involves providing a gas mixture as feed gas (F) containing oxygen and carbon dioxide to an industrial process (I) for producing the industrial product by oxidation reaction, using a carbon dioxide electrolysis (E).
   USE - Method for producing industrial product.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for plant for the production of industrial product (P), which consists of unit for performing industrial process, and unit for converting oxygen in feed gas, unit for carbon dioxide electrolysis.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view explaining production of industrial product.
   Production method (100)
   Anode gas (A)
   Carbon dioxide electrolysis (E)
   Feed gas (F)
   Industrial process (I)
   Industrial product (P)
   Exhaust gas (X)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: A portion of an anode gas (A) of the carbon dioxide electrolysis, which is unchanged in terms of its relative contents of oxygen and carbon dioxide, is provided as feed gas. The partial amount of the anode gas (A) or the entire anode gas (A) which is unchanged in its material composition is provided as the feed gas (F). The carbon dioxide electrolysis (E) is carried out as high-temperature electrolysis using a solid oxide electrolysis cell, or low-temperature electrolysis using a proton exchange membrane and/or a diaphragm and/or an electrolyte salt in aqueous solution. The feed gas (F) has ratio of carbon dioxide to oxygen of 1:2-2:1 or 4:1-2:1 or less. The industrial process (I) is selected from a steel mill process in which the oxygen in a blast furnace is used to convert coke, an oxyfuel process in which the oxygen is used for the oxygen-assisted combustion of a fuel, a biogas processing process in which oxygen is used to convert hydrogen sulfide, a synthesis process in which the feed gas (F) is used as a mixture of an oxidizing gas and an inert gas, a partial oxidation or autothermal reforming process in which the feed gas (F) is used as an oxygen supplier, a Claus process for the production of elemental sulfur, and an ammonia oxidation process. The feed gas (F) runs through the industrial process (I) with at least partial conversion of the oxygen to further carbon dioxide, to produce an exhaust gas (X). The exhaust gas (X) as an electrolysis feed gas (A) is returned to the carbon dioxide electrolysis (E).
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J03 (Electrochemical processes and electrophoresis - including ozone production, brine electrolysis, water electrolysis, production of chemical compounds and non-metallic elements, but excluding batteries or other means of producing power and the treatment of metals (C25B).); X25 (Industrial Electric Equipment)

AE KUNSHAN JINHONG CARBON CO LTD (KUNS-Non-standard)

The utility model firstly passes through the desulfurization mechanism, dealkylation mechanism, drying tower for primary purification, then using 2 rectifying tower in series, one for removing the heavy component, the other one for removing light component, finally obtaining more than 99.999 % of high-purity carbon dioxide, can reach the GB quality standard.

PT P
PN CN111603906-A; CN111603906-B
TI Preparing carbon dioxide magnesium-based adsorbent by grinding magnesite into powder, and calcining, adding calcined product into potassium permanganate aqueous solution, stirring, and drying.
AU WU H
   CHEN B
AE UNIV WUHAN TECHNOLOGY (UYWL-C)
   UNIV WUHAN TECHNOLOGY (UYWL-C)
GA 202087339K
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing carbon dioxide magnesium-based adsorbent involves (a) grinding magnesite into powder, and calcining at a certain temperature for a certain period of time to obtain a powder with magnesium oxide as the main component, (b) adding the calcined product obtained in step (a) into the potassium permanganate aqueous solution, and stirring until the mixture is uniform, and (c) drying the mixture obtained in step (b) at a certain temperature to obtain a carbon dioxide magnesium-based adsorbent.
   USE - The method is used for preparing carbon dioxide magnesium-based adsorbent (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide magnesium-based adsorbent is recycled for many times and has high stability, high acid gas adsorption capacity and carbon dioxide adsorption effect about 4 times that of the unmodified magnesite adsorbent.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: In the step (b), the mass concentration fraction of the potassium permanganate aqueous solution is 10-30%. Preferred Method: In the step (c), the obtained carbon dioxide magnesium-based adsorbent is further subjected to grinding treatment to obtain a carbon dioxide magnesium-based adsorbent, and the particle size is 0.1-0.3 mm. Preferred Condition: In the step (a), the mass fraction of magnesium carbonate in the magnesite is 80% or more, and the magnesite powder is calcined at 500-700 degrees C for 20-30 minutes. In the step (b), the mixing temperature is at room temperature, and the mass ratio of magnesium oxide to potassium permanganate in the calcined product is (100-200):1. In the step (c), the mixture is dried at 110-150 degrees C, and the drying time is 1-2 hours.
DC J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. PN CN110819936-A; CN110819936-B
TI Anti-corrosion soft nitriding process of ammonia-nitrogen-carbon dioxide atmosphere, comprises e.g. placing components into a well furnace, filling the gas with ammonia, nitrogen and carbon dioxide, and reducing the temperature.
AU CHEN G
   YU R
   LIANG C
   JIAO G
   DENG J
AE GUANGZHOU ELECTRICAL & MECHANICAL IND (GUAN-Non-standard)
   GUANGZHOU GUANGZHI ELECTROMECHANICAL IND (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 202018109L
AB    NOVELTY - Anti-corrosion soft nitriding process of ammonia-nitrogen-carbon dioxide atmosphere, comprises (i) placing components into a well furnace for pre-heat treatment, (ii) filling the gas with 45-55 wt.% ammonia, 40-50 wt.% nitrogen and 4.5-5.5 wt.% carbon dioxide, and (iii) reducing the temperature after the soft nitriding treatment, and stopping the carbon dioxide, ammonia and nitrogen during the temperature reduction process.
   USE - The method is useful for anti-corrosion soft nitriding process of ammonia-nitrogen-carbon dioxide atmosphere.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves the hardness and corrosion resistance of the component, reduces the amount of deformation of parts, and improves the hardness, corrosion resistance, and appearance of parts.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (ii), the temperature of the soft nitriding treatment is 550-600 degrees C, and the time of the soft nitriding treatment is 4-8 hours. In the step (iii), the soft nitriding treatment immediately after stopping introducing carbon dioxide, and enters the cooling treatment stage when the temperature is reduced to 400-450 degrees C, immediately stopping filling ammonia gas, when the temperature is reduced to 150-200 degrees C and immediately stopping filling nitrogen. In the step (iii), when the carbon dioxide filling is stopped immediately after the soft nitriding treatment is completed, the nitrogen filling amount remains unchanged, the ammonia gas decreased from 45-55% of the total of the three gases to 40-50% of the total of the three gases. In the step (i), the pre-heating process is divided into three stages: the first stage: heating the temperature to 300-350 degrees C, the second stage is the temperature of the first stage heating to 400-450 degrees C, and the third stage is the temperature of the second stage heating to the soft nitriding treatment temperature. In the step (i), the first stage of heating to 300-350 degrees C and keeping the temperature for 2-6 hours. In the step (i), the second stage heating to 400-450 degrees C and keeping the temperature for 2-6 hours. In the step (i), during the second and third stages, the amount of ammonia gas is 40-50% of the sum of the three gases in step (ii). In the step (i), (ii) and (iii), further includes the treatment of the exhaust gas from the well.
DC M13 (Coating material with metals, diffusion processes, enamelling and vitreous coatings - including coating from liquid metal or solution, spraying, cementation, cathodic sputtering, enamelling and oilfree lubricant coatings, but not coatings for the production of semiconductors (C23C, D).)

PN EP4075408-A1; US2022335822-A1; FR3122011-A1
TI Method for determining quantity of e.g. carbon            dioxide emitted by bus on stretch of slope, involves            constructing learning base from macroscopic data of            road network, and constructing polluting emissions            model by automatic learning method.
AU DE NUNZIO G
   LARAKI M
   OTHMAN B
   THIBAULT L
AE IFP ENERGIES NOUVELLES (INSF-C)
   IFP ENERGIES NOUVELLES (INSF-C)
   IFP ENERGIES NOUVELLES (INSF-C)
GA 2022D7863T
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves constructing a learning                base from macroscopic data (MAC) of a learning road                network and measured and simulated traffic data                (TRA) on the network. A polluting emission model                (MFE) is constructed by an automatic learning                method (APP) trained on the constructed learning                base. A quantity of a pollutant (Q) emitted by a                vehicle on a road network considered by applying                the model of pollutant emissions to the macroscopic                data of a strand of the network considered and to                the simulated traffic data of the considered                network is determined. The traffic data is measured                by a sensor placed on a section of the road                network.
   USE - Method for determining quantity of pollutant                e.g. gaseous compound or particles such as nitrogen                oxides, carbon dioxide, carbon monoxide, fine                particles and unburned hydrocarbons, emitted by a                motorized vehicle e.g. motor vehicle, two-wheeler,                heavy goods vehicle, coach and bus, on stretch of a                road network e.g. slope and road layout.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables effectively determining the                quantity of pollutants emitted by the vehicle on                the stretch of the road network by taking into                account impact of congestion, signaling and                topography of the road network. The method enables                reducing calculation time and calculation memory                and facilitating implementation of the model in an                efficient manner.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic block diagram                of a method for determining quantity of                pollutants.
   APPAutomatic learning method
   MACMacroscopic data
   MFEPolluting emission model
   QQuantity of pollutant
   TRAMeasured and simulated traffic data

PN DE202021004080-U1
TI Ventilation system for local and needs-based            improvement of air quality in room, has controller to            determine carbon dioxide (CO2) concentration of air in            area, and determined CO2 concentrations each with            predetermined CO2, control air supply line to conduct            ionized air into area.
AU 
AE MARQUARDT GMBH (MRQU-C)
GA 2022B6432L
AB    NOVELTY - The ventilation system has an ionizer for                ionizing air, one carbon dioxide (CO2) sensor                (20-24) that is configured for determining a CO2                concentration in an area (10-14) of a room (1) and                one air supply line (30-34) through which one                ionizer ionized air is conducted into the                respective area of the room. A controller is                designed to determine a CO2 concentration of the                air in the area using the CO2 sensor. The                determined CO2 concentrations each with a                predetermined CO2, compare the limit value and                control the ionizer to ionize the air in a                predetermined manner and control the air supply                line to conduct the ionized air into the                area.
   USE - Ventilation system for local and needs-based                improvement of air quality in room.
   ADVANTAGE - The ions in the air can attach to other                portions in the air and neutralize them, thus                improving the air quality. The air quality in a                room e.g. a large room can be improved in a                targeted manner and depending on the actual air                quality. The air supply lines are distributed over                the room in a uniform grid. The ionizer can be                adjusted as required so that the ionization                performance can be increased or reduced depending                on the room air quality or the CO2                concentration.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawings show the schematic view of the                first room with a single area, and a second space                divided into four equal areas.
   1Room
   10-14Areas of a room
   20-24CO2 sensors
   30-34Air supply lines

PN US2022280918-A1; WO2022187558-A1
TI Preparing composite membrane involves forming            mixture comprising preset amounts of fluoropolymer and            sorbent, forming phase inversion coating composition            having preset mass ratio of nonsolvent and solvent,            coating composiiton on substrate, forming porous            fluoropolymer film with dispersed sorbent.
AU MULDOON P F
   BAKER J S
   ZHU L
   HOPKINSON D P
AE BATTELLE MEMORIAL INST (BATT-C)
   BATTELLE MEMORIAL INST (BATT-C)
GA 2022B5650H
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of sorbent polymer composite                membrane, involves: 1) mixing a dissolved                fluoropolymer and a sorbent in an organic solvent                to form a mixture, the amount of fluoropolymer and                sorbent in the mixture, is 5 wt.% or more; 2)                forming a phase inversion coating composition by                adding a nonsolvent to the mixture, the mass ratio                of nonsolvent and solvent in the coating                composition, is 0.2 or less; 3) applying a film of                the coating composition, on a substrate, with a                casting knife; 4) vaporizing the solvent from the                film at temperature above 150&#176;C, to                increase the ratio of nonsolvent/solvent in the                mixture, so that the fluoropolymer precipitates                from the solvent; and 5) forming a porous                fluoropolymer film with dispersed sorbent.
   USE - Method is used for preparing sorbent polymer                composite membrane used for carbon dioxide                absorption.
   ADVANTAGE - The sorbent polymer composite membrane                prepared using cost-effective materials, has                ability to function as free-standing films for CO2                capture.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for the                sorbent polymer composite membrane which comprises                a porous fluoropolymer matrix,                polytetrafluoroethylene (PTFE) filler, a solid                sorbent dispersed in the porous membrane, and                optionally a fabric layer formed in the membrane or                adhered to the membrane. The membrane has a surface                with water contact angle more than 100&#176;,                preferably 101 -131&#176;. The air, nitrogen                and/or carbon dioxide permeance of the membrane, is                more than 10000 gas permeance unit (GPU) (1                GPU=7.501&#215;10-12 m 3(STP) m -2s -1pa                -1).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Process: The Preparation of                  sorbent polymer composite membrane, further                  involves drying the porous fluoropolymer film at                  an elevated temperature above 30&#176;C,                  preferably 30-100&#176;C for removing the                  solvent and nonsolvent.Preferred Composition: In step 1 of the                  process, the prepared mixture comprises at least                  7 wt.% or 8-10 wt.% fluoropolymer and                  sorbent.The mixture comprises at least 4 wt. % or                  8-10 wt.% fluoropolymer.The coating composition prepared in step 2                  of the process, has nonsolvent/solvent (for                  example water/acetone) mass ratio 0.2 or less or                  0.024-0.100.The amount of fluoropolymer and sorbent used                  are adjusted so that the sorbent in the resulting                  membrane, is 15-75 wt. percent.Preferred Conditions: In step 4 of the                  process, vaporizing is performed at temperature                  below 150&#176;C or at                  10-30&#176;C.Preferred Components: The substrate used in                  the process, is a wet fabric. The coating                  composition is impregnated and adhered to the                  fabric. The sorbent used in the process,                  comprises a zeolite, activated carbon, molecular                  organic framework (MOF), amine grafted or                  impregnated silica, amine functionalized MOF or                  amine impregnated polymer. The MOF used,                  comprises (RTM: zirconium-based metal organic                  framework) UiO-66, MOF-808 and/or magnesium                  dioxybenzenedicarboxylate (Mg 2 (dobdc),                  preferably amine functionalized MOF.Preferred Properties: The sorbent polymer                  composite membrane used, has thickness 20-200                  &#956;m. The sorbent polymer composite                  membrane has carbon dioxide (CO2) adsorption                  capacity above 0.2 mmol CO2 per gram adsorbents,                  at CO2 partial pressure 0.1 bar, or above 2 mmol                  CO2 per gram adsorbents at CO2 partial pressure                  1.0 bar. The sorbent polymer composite membrane                  has reversible CO2 adsorption capacity after                  thermal regeneration of adsorbents, after steam                  exposure for 10 seconds to 10 minutes or exactly                  one minute, at temperature above 80&#176;C,                  preferably at 100&#176;C for more than 10                  times or 100 times or more than 1000 times.

AB    NOVELTY - The device has a sampling system for placing                sand sample, where the sand sample falls into a                sample bearing mold system. The sample bearing mold                system comprises a cylindrical lower base and an                upper base fixed on a three-axis instrument. An end                of the three-axis instrument is sleeved on an outer                wall of the upper base and fixed by a lower rubber                ring of a rubber film. AB    NOVELTY - Processing niobium oxide gated tube by                supercritical fluid comprises placing the                nickel-oxide gated pipe in the reaction cavity,                adding water to the reaction chamber, introducing                supercritical carbon dioxide fluid into the                reactor, controlling the pressure in the reactor to                2800-3200psi, the temperature is 380-424 K, and the                reaction time is 1-2 hours, and controlling the                temperature of the reactor at 380-424K, the                pressure is 300-380&#8451;, and water is added                to the reactor.
   USE - Method for processing niobium oxide gated tube                by supercritical fluid, which is used in transition                metal oxide material e.g. vanadium dioxide, niobium                dioxide in semiconductor device.
   ADVANTAGE - The method uses supercritical fluid processing                niobium oxide gating tube, which successfully                reduces the OFF state current of the device,                greatly improves the gating ratio, and provides                powerful support for the further integration and                application of device. The method provides a device                loss threshold value characteristics. The device                does not have a threshold value conversion                performance, exhibits the performance of the 1S1R                and when the temperature rises to 424K, the voltage                scanning curve of the gated tube device is very                disorderly, and the consistency of device becomes                very poor. Thus, it can be seen that pure elevated                reaction temperature does not generate positive                effect on the device performance.

   ZHANG J
AE HUBEI CHANGE PHARM CO LTD (HUBE-Non-standard)
GA 2022A50327
AB    NOVELTY - Nattokinase enteric-coated micro-capsule                comprises an inner core layer material and enteric                outer-layer coating material coated on the surface                of the inner core layer material, where the inner                core layer material comprises 3-5 pts. wt.                deacetylated chitin, 1-3 pts. wt. hydroxypropyl                methyl cellulose, 0.8-1.2 pts. wt. microcrystalline                cellulose, 1-3 pts. wt. lactitol and 4-8 pts. wt.                nattokinase
   USE - The nattokinase enteric-coated micro-capsule                is useful for preparing medicine for preventing                and/or treating thrombus (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The nattokinase enteric-coated micro-capsule                prolongs release time of nattokinase in the inner                layer material due to cross-linking of chitosan and                hydroxypropyl methylcellulose, gradually releases                its content and improves utilization rate of                nattokinase.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for method                for preparing the nattokinase enteric-coated                micro-capsule, which involves (a) dissolving                chitosan, hydroxypropyl methyl cellulose,                microcrystalline cellulose, lactitol and                nattokinase in water, emulsifying, homogenizing and                spray-drying to obtain an inner core layer                material, (b) mixing sodium alginate, triethyl                citrate, polyethylene glycol and water, stirring                and fully dissolving to obtain enteric outer-layer                coating material and (c) adding inner core layer                material and enteric outer-layer coating material                into a static reaction kettle, then introducing                carbon dioxide gas, controlling pressure and                temperature in static reaction kettle to fluidize                the carbon dioxide, continuously heating and                increasing the pressure and turning on stirring rod                for coating to obtain the micro-capsule.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Components: The enteric                  outer-layer coating material comprises 4-8 pts.                  wt. sodium alginate, 1-2 pts. wt. triethyl                  citrate, 0.5-0.8 pts. wt. polyethylene glycol and                  80-120 pts. wt. water.Preferred Method: The step (a) involves                  emulsifying and homogenizing 1-2 times at 15-20                  MPa. The step (a) involves spray drying at feed                  liquid temperature of 30-40&#176;C, inlet air                  temperature of 50-80&#176;C and outlet air                  temperature of 40-50&#176;C. The step (b)                  involves stirring for 2-3 hours. The step (c)                  involves heating at 34-36&#176;C and                  increasing the pressure to 18-22 MPa and                  performing coating reaction for 25-30 minutes.                  The step (c) further involves adding triethyl                  citrate into the static reaction kettle.
   ACTIVITY - Anticoagulant; Thrombolytic.

PN CN114849652-A
TI Activated carbon encapsulated imidazole metal            organic framework complex material useful in gas            adsorption separation.
AU JIA M
   WANG S
   ZHANG Z
   XUE M
AE UNIV SUN YAT-SEN (UYSY-C)
GA 2022A61472
AB    NOVELTY - Activated carbon encapsulated imidazole metal                organic framework complex material, is claimed. The                general formula of the complex material is                AC_x.MOF_y, where, AC is activated carbon, MOF is                metal organic framework material absorbed with                imidazole ligand, x: y is 50-200, the mass ratio of                the AC is 80-90 wt.%, the mass ratio of MOF is                10-20 wt.%, the AC comprises coconut shell carbon,                apricot shell carbon, walnut shell carbon,                anthracite active carbon, and/or bamboo charcoal,                the AC is the formed activated carbon, and the                shape of the AC comprises granular, columnar, and                spherical.
   USE - The activated carbon encapsulated imidazole                metal organic framework complex material is useful                in gas adsorption separation (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The complex carbon material forms a porous                microcrystalline MOF, the smaller pore size can                significantly increase the content of micropores,                significantly increase the specific surface area,                and increase the types of functional groups of the                complex material, thus improving the selectivity                and adsorption capacity of the complex carbon                material.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) preparing activated carbon encapsulated                imidazole metal-organic framework complex material                comprising (i) adsorbing metal ion or molten metal                salt on the surface and internal pores of activated                carbon, and then reacting in ligand imidazole                ligand solution to generate activated carbon                encapsulating imidazole metal organic framework                complex material, at last, removing the solvent                molecules in the complex material pore canal by                activation to obtain the product; and
   (2) use of activated carbon encapsulated                imidazole metal organic framework complex material                in gas adsorption separation, where the gas                adsorption separation comprises adsorbing carbon                dioxide, adsorbing nitrogen, adsorbing ethane, and                selectively separating carbon                dioxide/nitrogen.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method specifically                  comprises (i) soaking the activated carbon in the                  metal salt solution or liquid molten metal salt,                  absorbing metal ion or molten metal salt on the                  surface of the activated carbon and the internal                  pore canal, preparing activated carbon rich in                  metal ion or liquid metal molten salt in the                  surface and internal pore canal, (ii) soaking the                  activated carbon in the imidazole ligand                  solution, after fully reacting, preparing into                  activated carbon packaging imidazole metal                  organic framework complex material, (iii) drying                  the activated carbon encapsulated imidazole metal                  organic framework complex material, then placing                  under vacuum at 80-200&#8451; for 8-16 hours                  to remove the solvent molecule in the pore canal                  to obtain the final activated carbon encapsulated                  imidazole metal organic framework complex                  material. The metal ions absorbed in the                  activated carbon are converted into metal oxide                  after finishing step (i), then reacting the                  imidazole ligand to prepare the activated carbon                  encapsulated imidazole metal organic framework                  complex material. The step (ii) is prepared into                  active carbon encapsulated imidazole metal                  organic framework complex material, and then                  repeating the operation of the complex material                  for multiple steps (i) and (ii), so as to improve                  the loading amount of imidazole ligand and metal                  organic framework material. The reaction                  temperature in the imidazole ligand solution is                  110-150&#8451; and the reaction time is 4-24                  hours.Preferred Component: The imidazole ligand                  comprises imidazole, methylimidazole and                  benzotriazole. The metal salt comprises zinc                  nitrate hexahydrate, zinc acetate dihydrate, and                  mixture of zinc chloride and sodium formate. The                  concentration of the metal ion in the metal salt                  solution is 100-400 g/l, and the concentration of                  the imidazole ligand solution is 100-400                  g/l.

AB    NOVELTY - A gas separation membrane comprises a                cellulosic matrix, and a polymer of intrinsic                microporosity (PIM) comprising chains coupled by a                heat-treating under vacuum.
   USE - Gas separation membrane used for removing acid                gas from natural gas feedstock to form sweetened                natural gas (claimed). Uses include but are not                limited to carbon dioxide, hydrogen sulfide,                nitrogen, helium, water, mercaptans and heavy                hydrocarbons.
   ADVANTAGE - The gas separation membrane has high free                volume polymers, and high selectivity and                permeability for carbon dioxide and hydrogen                sulfide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) forming the gas separation membrane, which                involves forming a cellulosic polymer solution,                forming a polymer of intrinsic microporosity (PIM)                solution, blending the polymer solution of the                cellulosic polymer with the polymer solution of the                PIM to form a mixed polymer solution, forming a                dense film from the mixed polymer solution, drying                the dense film, and heat-treating the dense film                under heat and vacuum to couple the PIM chains;                and
   (2) removing an acid gas from a natural gas                feedstock to form a sweetened natural gas, which                involves flowing the natural gas feedstock over a                membrane and isolating the acid gas in a permeate                from the membrane, and producing the sweetened                natural gas in a retentate from the membrane.

   Feed gas tank (502)
   Oven (504)

   Vacuum pump (510)

Preferred                  Process : The method involves dissolving a                  cellulosic polymer in tetrahydrofuran to form the                  cellulosic polymer solution and dissolving PIM-1                  in tetrahydrofuran to form polymer of intrinsic                  microporosity.

AB    NOVELTY - High-quality silicon nitride dielectric                passivation layer processing based on supercritical                fluid comprises (a) placing the substrate sample                with the silicon nitride dielectric passivation                layer vertically into the reaction vessel; (b)                filling deionized water into the reaction vessel                and sealing the reaction vessel; (c) controlling                pressure, and filling carbon dioxide or nitrous                oxide supercritical gas into the reaction vessel;                (d) subjecting the reaction vessel filled with                carbon dioxide or nitrous oxide supercritical gas                to temperature increase and pressure increase                treatment, maintaining the pressure, where the                inside of the reaction vessel is in a supercritical                state; (e) maintaining the supercritical state                treatment, depressurizing the reaction vessel, and                maintaining the supercritical treatment condition                of 20-22 MPa; and (f) subjecting the reaction                vessel to cooling treatment, and taking out when                the pressure is reduced to atmospheric                pressure.

PN CN114369289-A
TI Preparing nanocomposite powder of graphene-calcium carbonate, comprises digesting quicklime, filtering and removing residue, introducing carbon dioxide mixed gas, controlling reaction temperature, adding crystal form control agent, separating, adding stearic acid rare earth and reacting.
AU REN D
   WANG H
   WANG S
   WANG X
   LI L
   ZHENG Z
   QI X
   CAI X
AE HEZHOU XUBAO MINING IND INVESTMENT CO (HEZH-Non-standard)
GA 2022654447
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing nano-composite powder of graphene-calcium carbonate involves (a) stirring and digesting quicklime at 60-90degrees Celsius for 40-50 minutes, filtering and removing residue to prepare calcium hydroxide slurry, (b) mixing the slurry with graphene to obtain a mixed material, adding a surfactant dimethyl sulfoxide, raising the temperature to 60-80degrees Celsius, maintaining for 3-4 hours to reduce the temperature, (c) introducing carbon dioxide mixed gas, controlling the reaction temperature at 20-25degrees Celsius under the ultrasonic condition, reacting at constant temperature, (d) adding a crystal form control agent at 1/2-1/3 of the total time of the carbonization reaction, stopping the reaction when the pH value is 7, (e) peeling, separating, adding stearic acid rare earth, stirring and reacting for 30-50 minutes, filtering and drying.
   USE - Preparation method of graphene-calcium carbonate nano composite powder used for filling base material. Uses include but are not limited to rubber, plastic, paper making, coating, ink and medicine industries.
   ADVANTAGE - The method prepares graphene-calcium carbonate nano-composite powder with high activation degree and low oil absorption value. The powder is used for filling the base material, it can reduce the cost and improve the environment-friendly property of the material.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for nanocomposite powder of graphene-calcium carbonate, prepared as mentioned above.

PN CN113916926-A
TI Method for evaluating pore throat blockage characteristic under dense reservoir carbon dioxide drive asphaltene deposition, involves comparing and analyzing hole throat blocking rate with ratio of difference of saturated water.
AU ZHOU Z
   WANG X
   ZHANG Y
   TIAN Z
   GU L
   HUANG X
AE UNIV XIAN SHIYOU (UXSY-C)
GA 202215079T
AB    NOVELTY - Evaluating pore throat blockage characteristic comprises selecting porosity, permeability, movable fluid saturation, moving fluid porosity and displacement pressure, median pressure, sorting coefficient, hole throat radius, maximum mercury saturation, and pore type dense reservoir evaluation parameter according to the scanning electron microscope, high-pressure mercury, casting sheet and nuclear magnetic resonance experiment test. The 3-type reservoir is selected with a representative rock sample for displacement experiment according to the compact reservoir classification evaluation standard. The three-type rock cores are vacuumed from two ends of the holder by using a molecular vacuum pump. The temperature of the core holder is raised to the reservoir temperature, pressurized the rock core, and then saturated the stratum crude oil in the three types of rock cores in a constant speed mode until the outlet end does not discharge water.
   USE - Method for evaluating pore throat blockage characteristic under the effect of dense reservoir carbon dioxide drive asphaltene deposition. Can also be used in evaluation of pore-throat blockage feature under different pore structure dense reservoir CO2 drive asphaltite deposition.
   ADVANTAGE - The method can accurately evaluate the pore throat blockage degree of different micro-pore structure type reservoir, supplementing the compact light oil reservoir CO2 development mechanism, and provides a certain reference and guidance for mine parameter optimization.

PN CN113416273-A; CN113416273-B
TI Hydrogel electrolyte used for manufacturing e.g. aqueous lithium-ion batteries, comprises hydrogel obtained by free radical polymerization of polymer monomer, crosslinking agent and initiator in solvent, and inorganic salt electrolyte solution permeated and adsorbed in hydrogel.
AU OU X
   ZHOU Y
   YAN F
AE UNIV DONGHUA (UYDG-C)
GA 2021B22503
AB    NOVELTY - A degradable and low temperature resistant hydrogel electrolyte comprises a degradable, low-temperature-resistant hydrogel, and an inorganic salt electrolyte solution permeated and adsorbed in the degradable, low-temperature-resistant hydrogel. The degradable and low-temperature resistant hydrogel is obtained by free radical polymerization of polymer monomers, crosslinking agents, and initiators in a solvent. The polymer monomers comprise potassium acrylate and acrylamide. The crosslinking agent is a cyclic carbonate derived from carbon dioxide.
   USE - Degradable and low temperature resistant hydrogel electrolyte used for manufacturing aqueous lithium-ion batteries, zinc ion batteries, zinc air batteries and supercapacitors (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The hydrogel electrolyte is prepared by simple, efficient, green and environmentally-friendly method using volatile organic compounds-free raw material and products.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of degradable and low temperature resistant hydrogel electrolyte, which involves (i) preparing acrylic acid into aqueous solution and adding potassium hydroxide to adjust the pH to obtain potassium acrylate aqueous solution, (ii) adding carbon dioxide-derived cyclic carbonate and acrylamide to the potassium acrylate solution to obtain a uniformly mixed precursor solution, (iii) preparing initiator into aqueous solution and adding to the precursor solution, (iv) casting obtained the solution in a mold and sealing for a certain period of time to obtain a degradable and low temperature resistant hydrogel, and (v) drying the degradable, low-temperature-resistant hydrogel and absorbing inorganic salt electrolyte solution.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The initiator is ammonium persulfate. The inorganic salt is chosen from zinc sulfate, zinc nitrate, zinc chloride, zinc bis(trifluoromethanesulfonimide), manganese sulfate, manganese nitrate, manganese chloride, sodium sulfate, lithium nitrate, lithium chloride, lithium hexafluorophosphate and lithium bistrifluoromethanesulfonimide. Preferred Property: The potassium acrylate aqueous solution has pH of 7-14. Preferred Composition: The content of the initiator used in the method 1-5 wt.% of mass of polymer monomer.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The carbon dioxide-derived cyclic carbonate is chosen from small molecule cyclic carbonates, polymer-based cyclic carbonates and bio-based cyclic carbonates. The small molecule cyclic carbonate is chosen from compounds of formulae (I) and (II). The polymer-based cyclic carbonate is chosen from compounds of formulae (III) and (IV). The bio-based cyclic carbonate is chosen from compounds of formulae (V) and (VI). The polymer monomers comprise potassium acrylate and acrylamide.n = positive number.Preferred Composition: The molar ratio of carbon dioxide-derived cyclic carbonate to acrylamide is 1:8-2:1. The hydrogel electrolyte comprises 0.2-4.2 mol/L inorganic salt electrolyte solution. The mass ratio of inorganic salt electrolyte solution and degradable and low-temperature resistant hydrogel after freeze-drying is 0.9-5.4.

PT P
PN CN113399853-A
TI Welding method of steel structure used in building construction, involves performing mixed welding of gas shielded welding and electroslag welding on weld bead until slag tank is formed, and performing electroslag welding on weld bead from position where slag tank is formed up to top of weld bead.
AU SHAN J
   YANG Z
   XU B
   PEI X
   ZHANG H
   KUANG A
AE HANGXIAO STEEL STRUCTURE CO LTD ZHEJIANG (HANG-Non-standard)
GA 2021B0811L
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves splicing a wing plate, a lining plate, a clapboard and a web plate to form a welding channel. The gas shielded welding is performed at the bottom of the weld bead for a preset time. The bottom of the welding channel is upward. The mixed welding of gas shielded welding and electroslag welding on the weld bead is performed until a stable molten slag tank is formed. The electroslag welding is performed on the weld bead from the position where the stable slag tank is formed up to the top of the weld bead. The protective gas of the gas shielded welding is carbon dioxide, argon or mixed gas of carbon dioxide and argon.
   USE - Welding method of steel structure used in building construction.
   ADVANTAGE - The good quality of the whole welding seam is ensured. The welding process is easy to automatic production, reducing manual mounting arc cap operation amount, saving the labor and material cost, optimizing the processing flow of the electroslag welding, shortening the processing technique time, saving the device cost. The welding seam of the starting end of the electroslag welding is not used for cutting, reducing the welding seam filling amount.

PT P
PN CN112756005-A
TI Preparing monoatomic silver-supported nitrogen-doped titanium dioxide catalyst useful in catalytic oxidation reaction of formaldehyde, comprises e.g. dissolving titanium source in organic solvent, and dissolving in mixed solvent.
AU GUAN C
   GUO W
   LANG J
   WU H
   ZHAO G
   HUANG Z
AE BEIJING HYPERION TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (BEIJ-Non-standard)
GA 2021541948
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing monoatomic silver-supported nitrogen-doped titanium dioxide catalyst, comprises (i) dissolving the titanium source in an organic solvent to obtain solution a, (ii) dissolving the silver source and nitrogen source in a mixed solvent of water, acid, and organic solvent to obtain solution B, (iii) dripping the solution B into the solution A under the action of an ultrasonic instrument, after the dripping is complete, continuously sonicating for 1-5 hours to form a stable gel, (iv) drying the gel in a constant temperature drying oven to obtain a dry gel, grinding into powder, and placing in a hydrogen atmosphere furnace for high-temperature roasting.
   USE - The catalyst is useful in catalytic oxidation reaction of formaldehyde (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is simple and controllable; and prepares product which can achieve high-efficiency removal of formaldehyde under normal temperature and light/non-light conditions, and convert formaldehyde into completely non-toxic carbon dioxide and water. The catalyst: does not decay the ability to catalyze the degradation of formaldehyde after long-term operation; and has excellent stability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) monoatomic silver-supported nitrogen-doped titanium dioxide catalyst, prepared by the above-mentioned method; and
   (2) use method of the monoatomic silver-supported nitrogen-doped titanium dioxide catalyst in the catalytic oxidation reaction of formaldehyde, comprising converting formaldehyde into carbon dioxide and water under normal temperature and light/no light conditions.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the ratio of the amount of the titanium source and the organic solvent substance is (0.01-1):1. In the step (ii), the mass ratio of the water, acid, and organic solvent is (0.1-10):(0.1-1):1. In the step (iii), the solution B is added dropwise to solution A to control the amount ratio of nitrogen source, silver source and titanium source to (0.1-10):(0.01-1):1. In the step (i), the titanium source is tetramethyl titanate, tetraethyl titanate, tetrapropyl titanate, tetrabutyl titanate, tetraisopropyl titanate, tetraisobutyl titanate, titanium tetrachloride, and/or titanium sulfate. In the step (ii), the silver source is silver nitrate, silver acetate, and/or silver acetylacetonate, the nitrogen source is ammonium carbonate, urea, triethylamine and/or ammonium acetate, and the acid is glacial acetic acid, hydrochloric acid and/or nitric acid. Preferred Method: In the step (iii), the ultrasonic temperature is 0-25 degrees C, the ultrasonic frequency is 50-100 Hz, and the dropping rate is 1-10 ml/minute. In the step (iv), the drying is carried out at 50-100 degrees C for 6-24 hours, and the roasting is carried out at 300-500 degrees C for 0.5-6 hours.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the steps (i) and (ii), the organic solvent is methanol, ethanol, isopropanol, ethylene glycol and/or glycerol.
DC E17 (Other aliphatics.); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)
MC E10-D01D; E11-P; E11-Q02C; J04-E04A; J04-E11; N02-E03; N06-E01; N06-F; N07-L02D

AB    NOVELTY - A refrigeration cycle device comprises a                refrigeration circuit containing a compressor (1),                an outdoor heat exchanger (3), an indoor heat                exchanger (5) and an expansion valve (4), and a                refrigerant sealed in the circuit. The refrigerant                contains difluoromethane (R32), trifluoroethylene                (HFO1123) and refrigerant containing carbon dioxide                (R744). A composition diagram showing ratio of                three components by triangular coordinates contains                a straight line (X) linking point (A) indicating                ratio of the three components as 46 %mass, 54 %mass                and 0 %mass and point (B) indicating ratio of three                components as 46 %mass, 37.2 %mass and 16.8 %mass,                a straight line (Y) linking point (A) and point (C)                indicating ratio of three components as 0 %mass,                100 %mass and 0 %mass, a straight line (Z) linking                points (C) and point (D) indicating ratio of the                three components as 0 %mass, 85.7 %mass and 14.3                %mass, and a curve linking points (B) and (D). The                ratio of the three components is greater than 0                %mass.
   USE - Refrigeration cycle device for air conditioner                (claimed). Uses include but are not limited to                commercial air conditioner, household air                conditioner, car air conditioner, heat pump for                vending machine, refrigerator, marine transporter,                chiller unit, turborefrigerator, floor heater, snow                melting system, etc.

The refrigerant further contains                  trifluoroiodomethane, and                  2,3,3,3-tetrafluoropropene (R1234 yf). Preferred                  Composition: The refrigerant contains 8-20 %mass                  difluoromethane (R32) and mixed refrigerant                  containing carbon dioxide (R744) (in total) with                  respect to total amount of the refrigerant. The                  ratio of trifluoroethylene (HFO1123) is 50-70                  %mass, and the ratio of trifluoro iodomethane is                  10-30 %mass. The total ratio of mixed refrigerant                  containing carbon dioxide (R744) and                  2,3,3,3-tetrafluoropropene (R1234 yf) is 8-20                  %mass. The ratio of mixed refrigerant containing                  carbon dioxide (R744) and                  2,3,3,3-tetrafluoropropene (R1234 yf) is greater                  than 0.65.

PN GB2587579-A; WO2021089024-A1; GB2587579-B
TI Method for continuous detection of multi-channel            gas samples, involves entering gas sample to sample            detection channel through multi-channel gas path            automatic switching unit and, generating interval gas            by interval gas supply device.
AU LI X
   XU M J
   YIN Z
AE OGRANDS INNOVATION INC (OGRA-Non-standard)
   UNIV JINAN (UJIN-C)
GA 2021351980
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves entering a gas sample to                the sample detection channel (400) through the                multi-channel gas path automatic switching unit                (30). The interval gas supply device (60) generates                interval gas. The multi-channel gas path automatic                switching unit switches to next gas sample, and the                step is repeated for the next gas sample, otherwise                stop. The content ratio of carbon dioxide in the                interval gas is no less than the content ratio of                carbon dioxide in the gas sample.
   USE - Method for continuous detection of                multi-channel gas samples.
   ADVANTAGE - The current condition and contamination of the                sample channel can hardly be observed in real time                so the experimental adjustment cannot be made to                ensure the efficiency of the experiment. The                purpose of the interval gas is to delay the                diffusion of gas samples during continuous                detection of multiple gas samples, so that there is                no interference between neighboring samples as far                as possible. Both the interval gas and samples are                detected online in real time, so the degree of                diffusion between them can be observed through the                analysis results of the instrument, so that the                target test results can be better judged during the                data analysis process.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a multi-channel sampling                and detection device with particle filters.
   Gas Inlet diversion unit (10)
   Multi-channel gas path automatic switching                unit (30)
   Gas detection device (40)
   Interval gas supply device (60)
   Sample detection channel (400)

TI Tracheal tube attachment for a tracheal device, such as a tracheostomy tube, comprises a first end that is used to attach to an air hose, and a second end is used to attach to the tracheal device, and the diameter of the first end is dimensioned to fit within the air hose.
AU GAMMON C
AE GAMMON C (GAMM-Individual)
GA 202127131N
AB    NOVELTY - The tracheal tube attachment (110) comprises a first end that is used to attach to an air hose (112), and a second end is used to attach to the tracheal device (102). A central section has multiple radial openings, and a collar (140) is used to fit around a portion of the central section to cover, partially cover or leave uncovered radial openings. The diameter of the first end is dimensioned to fit within the air hose, and the diameter of the second end is dimensioned to fit around a cap (106) of the tracheal device. The collar completely covers the radial openings to maximize an amount of oxygen received through the tracheal tube attachment and minimizing an amount of carbon dioxide vented through the radial openings.
   USE - Tracheal tube attachment for a tracheal device, such as a tracheostomy tube.
   ADVANTAGE - The collar and radial openings are used to control the amount of oxygen received through the oxygen tube, as well as exhaling carbon dioxide to ambient. The tracheal tube attachment provides for a relatively unobstructed turbulent free flow of gas with a minimal effort by the patient, and further omits the need for a mask.
