TI Method for enhancing carbon dioxide (CO2)            adsorption performance of shale and synergistically            storing nitrogen dioxide (NO2), involves using chemical            adsorption and chemical absorption action of shale to            stably seal and store NO2.
AU XU Y
   HU Z
   ZHANG D
AE UNIV KUNMING SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UKST-C)
GA 202123706X
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves using nitrogen-containing                nitrogen-containing fuel gas or NO2 to enhance the                adsorption performance of shale CO2. The chemical                adsorption and chemical absorption action of the                shale are used to NO2 and nitrogen oxide (NO) to                stably seal and store NO2. The molar ratio of NO2                to CO2 is 1:100-10:100. The adsorption temperature                is 40-80 degrees C, and the adsorption equilibrium                pressure is 10-30 MPa. The shale is sea-phase shale                with different maturity.
   USE - Method for enhancing carbon dioxide (CO2)                adsorption performance of shale and synergistically                storing nitrogen dioxide (NO2).
   ADVANTAGE - The method uses NO2 to improve the adsorption                performance of the shale to CO2, and reaches the                purpose of reducing NO2. The generated nitric acid                dissolves the mineral components such as carbonate                in the shale and expose the shale portion pores so                as to enhance the CO2 adsorption performance of the                shale. The shale reservoir environment is anoxic                environment, the generated NO2 does not oxidized to                generate NO2. The shale has strong chemical                adsorption effect to the NO so as to realize                long-term stable sequent of the shale to NO2. The                method seals the NO2 in the process of sealing                greenhouse gas CO2, thus, providing an effective                way for reducing the coal gas denitration process                cost.

TI Non-dispersive infrared gas analyzer for determining gas components in measurement gas, has gas filter with optical thickness, by which saturation absorption is achieved in one absorption band and no absorption is present in other band.
AU HOERNER T
AE SIEMENS AG (SIEI-C)
GA 202114280U
AB    NOVELTY - The gas analyzer (1) has an infrared radiation source (2), a modulation device (8), a measuring chamber (5) receiving the measuring gas (7), a comparison chamber (6) and two consecutive optopneumatic detectors (10,11) containing different gas components. A gas filter (12) is arranged between the infrared radiation source and the detectors. The gas filter contains the gas component present in the high concentration in the measurement gas. The gas filter is set with an optical thickness, by which saturation absorption is achieved in the region of an absorption band of the gas component and no saturation absorption is present in other absorption band.
   USE - Non-dispersive infrared (NDIR) gas analyzer for determining two gas components such as carbon dioxide, in measurement gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The gas filter causes pre-absorption of the infrared radiation in the area of the absorption band of the high concentration gas component used for the measurement, around 2700 nm in the case of carbon dioxide, even if no saturation absorption occurs. Any unnecessary weakening of the intensity of the radiation through the window of the detectors is avoided. The gas filter in the form of separate component is not required.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the non-dispersive infrared gas analyzer.

PT P
PN KR2021001239-A; KR2219540-B1
TI Preparing blue titania composite wrapped with graphene on which platinum nanoparticles are photo-deposited used in selective photo-conversion process from carbon dioxide to methane and ethane, involves preparing blue titania nanoparticles.
AU IN S I
   SORCARSAURAV
AE DAEGU GYEONGBUK INST SCI & TECHNOLOGY (DAEG-C)
GA 202104985W
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing blue titania composite wrapped with graphene on which platinum nanoparticles are photo-deposited involves preparing blue titania nanoparticles, preparing blue titania nanoparticles wrapped with graphene by mixing the blue titania nanoparticles with a graphene oxide solution and then subjecting to vacuum heat treatment, preparing a suspension by mixing a solution containing blue titania nanoparticles wrapped with graphene and a platinum (Pt) precursor, performing a photodeposition process of the suspension, filtering, washing, and drying to obtain crystals of blue titania composite wrapped with graphene on which platinum nanoparticles are photo-deposited.
   USE - Method for preparing blue titania composite wrapped with graphene on which platinum nanoparticles are photo-deposited used as photocatalyst in a selective photo-conversion process from carbon dioxide (CO2) to methane (CH4) and ethane (C2H6) and for removing CO2 (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method has excellent conversion rate and high industrial use, excellent stability and high reusability, C2H6 is obtained in high yield, and controls composition in the complex, and adjusts the time of optical conversion.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a method for selectively converting CO2 and water vapor into CH4 and C2H6, which involves reacting CO2 and water vapor in a gas phase using photo-deposited graphene-wrapped blue titania complex as a photocatalyst, where gas phase reaction is a selective photo-conversion method of CO2; and
   (2) a method for removing CO2, which involves using the selective light conversion method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The blue tinania nanoparticles are titania (TiO2). The platinum precursor is chloroplatinic acid (H2PtCl6), platinum chloride potassium chloride (K2PtCl6) and cisplatin (Pt(NH3)2Cl2). Preferred Process: The blue tinania nanoparticles are prepared by mixing the nanoparticles and the reducing agent, heating the mixture at a temperature of 280-420 degrees C for 20-40 minutes under an inert gas. The heated mixture was washed with a solvent, dried at a temperature of 70-110 degrees C for 9-15 hours to obtain blue titania nanoparticles. The mixing is carried out by adding 1-10 pts. wt. platinum precursor to the 100 pts. wt. solution and then stirring under closed system and dark conditions. Preferred Composition: The graphene oxide solution contains 35-200 pts. wt. graphene oxide based on 100 pts. wt. of the solvent, where solvent is in distilled water, ethylene glycol (C2H4(OH)2) and ethanol (C2H5OH). The solution containing blue titania nanoparticles wrapped with graphene comprises 0.25-2.50 wt.% blue titania nanoparticles wrapped with graphene and a mixed solvent of the balance. The blue titania wrapped with graphene comprises 0.2-3.0 wt.% platinum nanoparticles of the total weight, and 0.2-3.0 wt.% graphene based of the total weight of platinum nanoparticles. Preferred Conditions: The vacuum heat treatment is performed at 210-250 degrees C for 60-120 minutes. The mixed solvent comprises water and ethanol in a volume ratio of 1:0.2-0.6. The blue titania has an average particle diameter of 18-27 nm, and platinum nanoparticles has average particle diameter of 1.0-5.0 nm. The conversion of CO2 and water vapor into CH4 and C2H6 was carried under artificial sunlight of air mass (AM) 1.5 G for 6 hours. The selectivity ratio for CH4 and C2H6 is 70.0-77.5:22.5-30.0.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

The condensing mirror is used to focus the laser light reflected by the reflector (5) to increase the energy density, and then irradiate the light exit of the stage (3) through the through hole on the base to the bottom of the heating stage to heat the material (1) to be measured on the heating stage. The base is provided with a fluid channel for heat dissipation, and the surface of the base case is provided with a water inlet and a water outlet is connected to the fluid channel. The output port of the water cooler (8) is connected with the water inlet on the surface of the base through a pipe (7), and the input port is connected with the water outlet on the surface of the base through the pipe, so as to dissipate heat from the base.
   USE - Heating device for measuring spectral characteristics of high-temperature material.
   ADVANTAGE - The laser is used to heat various types and sizes of materials to be measured, which can effectively reduce the rate of heat dissipation of the materials to be measured and the environmental temperature rise.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the heating device for measuring spectral characteristics of high-temperature material.
   Material (1)
   Stage (3)
   Carbon dioxide laser (4)
   Reflector (5)
   Pipe (7)
   Water cooler (8)

AB    NOVELTY - Synthesizing natural gas comprises subjecting raw material gas at 180-300 degrees C to first methanation reaction to obtain first reaction gas at 400-600 degrees C, cooling first reaction gas to 180-300 degrees C for first time to obtain a second reaction gas of 250-400 degrees C through a second methanation reaction, and the feed gas includes 45-70 wt.% hydrogen, 15-35 wt.% methane, 1-15 wt.% carbon monoxide and 1-15 wt.% carbon dioxide.
   USE - The method is useful for synthesizing natural gas.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for device for synthesizing natural gas comprising a first reactor having a first feed port and a first discharge port. The first cooler is set on the first discharge port. The heat exchanger has a second feeding port, a third feeding port, a second discharging port and a third discharging port. The second feeding port is connected to the first discharging port so that at least part of the fluid cooled by the first cooler flows to the heat exchanger. The first separator has a fourth feeding port and a fourth discharging port, the fourth feeding port is connected to the second discharging port. The fourth discharging port is connected to the third feeding port, the second reactor is provided with a fifth feeding port, and the fifth feeding port is connected with the third discharging port.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows schematic representation of the device for synthesizing natural gas.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: After the first reaction gas is cooling to 180-300 degrees C, mixing partially cooled first reaction gas with raw material gas to performing first methanation reaction, the remaining cooled first reaction gas undergoes a second methanation reaction, and the flow ratio of the first reaction gas and the raw material gas mixed with raw material gas is 1-3:1. Before the second methanation reaction, the first reaction gas needs to undergo a first water separation treatment, the first water separation treatment includes cooling first reaction gas to 20-60 degrees C for second time, separating water, and heating to 170-290 degrees C. The first reaction gas after the second cooling is heating to 170-290 degrees C through heat exchange with first reaction gas before the second cooling. Subjecting second reaction gas to a second water separation treatment, the second water separation treatment includes cooling the second reaction gas to 20-60 degrees C for third time and separating water to obtain product gas. The device for synthesizing natural gas includes a recycle compressor, the recycle compressor has a sixth inlet and a sixth outlet. The sixth inlet is connected to the first outlet, the sixth discharge port is connected to the first feed port so that part of the fluid cooled by the first cooler flows to the first feed port through the circulating compressor. The device for synthesizing natural gas includes a second cooler arranged at the second outlet. The second reactor has a fifth discharge port, the synthetic natural gas equipment includes a third cooler arranged at the fifth discharge port. The synthetic natural gas device includes a second separator has a seventh inlet, and the seventh inlet is connected to the fifth outlet so that it passes through the third cooler, and the cooled fluid flows to the second separator.

TI Preparing long-life adsorbent useful for the treatment of industrial waste gas containing carbon dioxide, comprises adding low molecular weight polyethyleneimine aqueous solution and porogen B into reaction flask, stirring and reacting.
AU JIANG T
AE CHENGDU QIQIXIAOSHU TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (CHEN-Non-standard)
GA 2020745945
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing long-life adsorbent comprises (i) adding divinylbenzene, initiator, porogen A and acetic acid into the reaction flask, stirring continuously at a speed of 10-20 revolutions per minute, then passing nitrogen, heating to 70 degrees C, reacting at constant temperature for 4-5 hours to produce low molecular weight polydivinylbenzene; (ii) stopping heating, cooling to room temperature naturally, adding low molecular weight polyethyleneimine aqueous solution and porogen B into the reaction flask, increasing the stirring speed to 60-90 revolutions per minute, stirring for 20-40 minutes, and irradiating with gamma rays, initiating the reaction, stopping irradiation after 6-8 hours to generate polydivinylbenzene/polyethyleneimine graft copolymer microspheres; (iii) filtering, washing with ethanol and deionized water in turn, and then vacuum drying at 60 degrees C for 12-24 hours.
   USE - The adsorbent is useful for the treatment of industrial waste gas containing carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The adsorbent contains a large amount of hydrophobic polydivinylbenzene, which can can prevent the loss of polyethyleneimine, shorten the service life of adsorbent, prevent the structure of the adsorbent from changing, and prevent the aperture and the specific surface area from reducing so as to reduce the adsorption capacity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for long-life adsorbent, which is a low molecular weight polydivinylbenzene and a low molecular weight poly cross-linked network porous microsphere formed by graft copolymerization of ethyleneimine.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The content of the para-isomer and the meta-isomer in the divinylbenzene is 80%. The initiator is azobisisobutyronitrile or azobisisoheptonitrile. The porogen A is toluene. The concentration of the low-molecular-weight polyethyleneimine aqueous solution is 15-20 wt.%, and the amine is a branched polyethyleneimine with a number average molecular weight of 500-800. The porogen B is dichloromethane. The mass ratio of divinylbenzene, initiator, porogen A, acetic acid, polyethyleneimine aqueous solution and porogen B is 6-8:1-1.5:10-12:100:100-120:5-8. The gamma -ray irradiation dose is 1.5x 103-3x 103 Gy.

PN CN111442965-A
TI Diagnosing purple cabbage harmed by soil potassium-calcium-magnesium nutrient imbalance has mixing, air-drying soil samples, crushing, adding carbon dioxide-free pure water, stirring and obtaining e.g. magnesium ion concentration.
AU QI S
   GAO X
   GUO S
   ZHAO H
   XU G
AE SUQIAN AGRIC TECHNOLOGY INTEGRATED SERVI (SUQI-Non-standard)
GA 202071663S
AB    NOVELTY - Diagnosing purple cabbage harmed by soil potassium-calcium-magnesium nutrient imbalance comprises (a) performing layered soil sampling, collecting 10 sample points in the soil sampling area in an S shape, taking soil at each sample point, mixing, air-drying soil samples to obtain air-dried soil, (b) crushing the air-dried soil to 20 mesh, taking 10.0 g air-dried soil of 20 mesh, placing in a plastic bottle, adding 50ml carbon dioxide-free pure water, stirring vigorously for 90 seconds, allowing to stand for 30 minutes, filtering to obtain the test solution, (b1) determining potassium by flame spectrophotometry in the liquid to be tested, measuring calcium ions and magnesium ions by flame atomic absorption spectrophotometry, obtaining soil aqueous-soluble potassium, calcium and magnesium ion concentration through the measurement and (c) comparing the concentration of potassium, calcium, and magnesium ions in the soil measured in step (b) to determine whether high or low.
   USE - The method is useful for diagnosing purple cabbage harmed by soil potassium-calcium-magnesium nutrient imbalance.
   ADVANTAGE - The method ensures that the degree of damage to the soil potassium-calcium-magnesium nutrient imbalance is judged by diagnosing the amount of the main constituent ions that cause the soil nutrient imbalance and provides a set of complete, scientific, reasonable and feasible diagnosis method.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow chart of the diagnosis method of purple cabbage harmed by soil potassium-calcium-magnesium nutrient imbalance (Drawing includes non-English language text).

TI Electric power construction fire protection device, has dry powder fire extinguisher and carbon dioxide fire extinguisher connected with hose, where end of hose is connected with nozzle and two sides of bottom of outer frame are connected with moving device.
AU HE C
AE POWERCHINA HUBEI ENG CO LTD (CNPW-C)
GA 2020709850
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model claims a power construction electric fireproof protection device, comprising an outer frame, two sides of the inner wall of the outer frame is slidingly connected with a screw rod; two sides of the screw rod surface are in threaded connection with a moving block; the top of the moving block is fixedly connected with a base; the top of the base is rotatably connected with a top seat through a connecting rod; the top of the top seat is fixedly connected with a moving plate; the two sides of the top part of the moving plate are respectively fixedly connected with a dry powder fire extinguisher and a carbon dioxide fire extinguisher. PT P
PN CN111410214-A; CN111410214-B
TI System useful for preparing soda ash and sulfur using industrial by-product sodium sulfate, comprises feeding unit, reaction unit and discharging unit, and solid feeding unit and gas feeding unit are independently connected to feeding port.
AU YANG G
   WANG Y
   AN X
AE INST PROCESS ENG CHINESE ACAD SCI (CAGC-C)
GA 202067923L
AB    NOVELTY - System comprises includes a feeding unit, a reaction unit and a discharging unit. The feeding unit includes a solid feeding unit and a gas feeding unit that are independently connected to the feeding port of the reaction unit. The discharging unit includes a soda ash discharging unit and a sulfur discharging unit respectively connected to the discharging port of the reaction unit.
   USE - The system is useful for preparing soda ash and sulfur using industrial by-product sodium sulfate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: utilizes superheated steam, carbon dioxide, coke powder and sodium sulfate, adopts hydrogen sulfide and sulfur dioxide as raw materials; and realizes the resource utilization of industrial by-product sodium sulfate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing soda ash and sulfur using industrial by-product sodium sulfate comprising adopting the system to prepare soda ash and sulfur using industrial by-product sodium sulfate as raw materials, feeding the industrial by-product sodium sulfate and coke powder into the reaction unit from the solid feed unit, feeding carbon dioxide and superheated steam into the reaction unit from the gas feed unit, reacting the raw materials in the reaction unit to generate solid products and gas products, crystallizing the solid products out of the soda ash discharging unit, and reacting the gas products to obtain sulfur through the sulfur discharging unit.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the system structure for preparing soda ash and sulfur using industrial by-product sodium sulfate (Drawing includes non-English language text).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The solid feeding unit includes a raw material drying device and a granulating device connected in sequence along the feeding direction, the industrial by-product sodium sulfate is dehydrated by the raw material drying device and then enters the granulation device, mixed with coke powder in the granulation device, and then sent to the reaction unit, the granulation device is a disc granulator, the gas feeding unit includes a superheating device, a mixing buffer device and a natural gas pure oxygen combustion device, the hybrid buffer device and the natural gas pure oxygen combustion device are respectively connected to the overheating device, the water vapor and carbon dioxide enter the superheating device after being mixed by the mixing buffer device, the mixed gas of water vapor and carbon dioxide contacts with the flue gas produced by the combustion of natural gas in a pure oxygen environment to form superheated gas including superheated steam and carbon dioxide, the overheating device is filled with thermal storage ceramics; preferably, the overheating device is a thermal storage superheater. The reaction unit includes a feeding device and a reaction device connected to each other, the inlet of the feeding device is respectively connected to the outlet of the overheating device and the outlet of the granulating device, the outlet of the feeding device is connected to the inlet of the reaction device, the reaction device includes a calciner and a combustion chamber jacket sleeved on the periphery of the calciner, and the discharge port of the feeding device is connected to the inlet of the calciner, the combustion chamber jacket is provided with a gas nozzle, and natural gas is injected into the combustion chamber jacket from the gas nozzle, the combustion-supporting fan is externally connected to the gas nozzle, the both ends of the calciner are respectively provided with a gas discharge port and a solid discharge port, the gas discharge port is connected to a sulfur discharge unit, and the solid discharge port is connected to a soda ash discharge unit, the gas discharge port is located at one end of the calciner near the feeding device, the solid discharge port is located at the end of the calciner away from the feeding device, a flue gas outlet is provided on the combustion chamber jacket, and the flue gas outlet is connected to a raw material drying device, and the flue gas produced by the combustion of natural gas in the jacket of the combustion chamber is discharged into the raw material drying device through the flue gas outlet. The soda ash discharging unit includes a water immersion device and a crystallization device connected in sequence along the discharging direction, the discharge direction, an air cooling device is provided at the front end of the water immersion device, the solid product discharged from the reaction device is cooled by the air cooling device and then enters the water immersion device to be dissolved in water, the connecting pipeline between the water immersion device and the crystallization device is sequentially provided with a discharge pump and a filter device along the discharge direction, the filtering device is used to filter out silicon slag in the solution, the discharging direction, the crystallization device is connected to the separation device and the product drying device in sequence, the separation device is a centrifuge, the sulfur discharging unit includes a Claus reaction device, the Claus reaction device includes a Claus furnace and a Claus converter that are sequentially connected along the discharge direction, the discharge direction, a dust removal module is provided at the front end of the Claus furnace, the gas product discharged from the reaction device enters the Claus reaction device after being dedusted by the dust removal module, the dust removal module includes an electric dust removal device and a bag dust removal device connected in sequence along the discharge direction, and the outlet of the Claus converter is connected to a cooling device. Preferred Method: The method specifically comprises (I) drying the industrial by-product sodium sulfate, dehydrating by the raw material drying device, introducing the granulation device, and mixing with coke powder in the granulation device, and granulating to obtain solid raw material particle, and feeding the solid raw material particles into the calciner by the feeding device, (II) mixing The water vapor and carbon dioxide in the mixing buffer device in proportion, passing into the superheating device, contacting and mixing with the flue gas produced by the combustion of natural gas in a pure oxygen environment, forming a superheated gas including superheated steam and carbon dioxide, and conveying the superheated gas to calciner by the feeding device, (III) feeding the solid raw material particles and the superheated gas into the calciner in parallel through the feeding device, injecting the natural gas into the jacket of the combustion chamber, burning to produce flue gas under the action of combustion-supporting wind, performing indirect heating of solid raw material particles and superheated gas, and reacting to produce solid products and gas products, (IV) cooling the solid product generated, introducing the water immersion device, adding water to dissolve, filtering the solution to remove the silicon residue, introducing the crystallization device, evaporating, crystallizing, precipitating soda ash, and drying to obtains soda ash product, dedusting the gas product generated, introducing the Claus reaction device , reacting, and gradually cooling the reaction product to obtain sulfur products. In the step (I), drying the industrial by-product sodium sulfate, dehydrating at 150-200 degrees C, where the drying and dehydration time is 60-90 minutes, the mass ratio of the dehydrated sodium sulfate to the coke powder is 10: (1.5-2), the fixed carbon content in the coke powder is greater than or equal to 80 wt.%, the silica content in the coke powder is less than or equal to 8 wt.%; and the particle size of the solid raw material particles is 10-30 mm. In the step (II), the standard volume ratio of water vapor to carbon dioxide is (2-5):1, and the temperature of the superheated gas is 600-650 degrees C. In the step (III), introducing the flue gas discharged from the combustion chamber jacket to the raw material drying device for secondary use, drying the industrial by-product sodium sulfate and dehydrating, where the temperature of the flue gas discharged from the jacket of the combustion chamber is 300-400 degrees C, the reaction temperature of the solid raw material particles and the superheated gas in the reaction device is 650-750 degrees C, the reaction time of the solid raw material particles and the superheated gas in the reaction device is 240-270 minutes, the solid product includes soda ash and silicon slag, the temperature of the gas product is 400-500 degrees C, the gas products include water, carbon dioxide, hydrogen sulfide and sulfur dioxide, the volume ratio of water in the gas product is 30-35%, the volume ratio of carbon dioxide in the gas product is 30-35%, the volume ratio of H2S in the gas product is 20-25%, and the volume ratio of sulfur dioxide in the gas product is 7-12%. In the step (IV), the solid product is cooled to 150-200 degrees C, the ratio of the mass of water added to the solid product in the water immersion device is (5-10):1, the dissolution temperature in the water immersion device is 70-90 degrees C, the water immersion device uses low-pressure steam as a heat source to indirectly heat the contents, the steam pressure of the low-pressure steam is 0.4-0.5 MPa, the steam temperature of the low-pressure steam is 140-150 degrees C, and the mass fraction of sodium carbonate in the solution obtained after adding water to dissolve is 15-20 wt.%.

PN WO2020124187-A1; BR102018076909-A2; AR117483-A1; US2022062860-A1
TI Preparing nanostructured material for the absorption of carbon dioxide, involves solubilizing the metal hydroxide in acetyl acetone in the proportion of metal hydroxide for a portion of acetyl acetone, and transferring solution to an oven.
AU BELCHIOR J C
   ARAUJO M H
   CAMILO MOURA F C
   SILVEIRA VIEIRA S
   CARVALHO MONTEIRO DE CASTRO M
   CAFFARO VICENTINI P
   NEVES LOUREIRO L
   CARVALHO MONTEIRO DE CASTR
   NEVES L L
   CAFFARO V P
   CARVALHO M D C M
   SILVEIRA V S
   CAMILO M F C
AE UNIV FEDERAL MINAS GERAIS (UFMG-C)
   PETROBRAS PETROLEO BRASIL SA (PETB-C)
   FUNDACAO AMPARO A PESQUISA MINAS GERAIS (AMPA-Non-standard)
   FCA FIAT CHRYSLER AUTOMOVEIS BRASIL LTDA (FCAF-Non-standard)
   FAPESP FUNDACAO AMPARO PESQUISA ESTADO (FAPE-Non-standard)
   FCA FIAT CHRYSLER AUTOMOVEIS BRASIL LTDA (FCAF-Non-standard)
   PETROBRAS PETROLEO BRASIL SA (PETB-C)
   UNIV FEDERAL MINAS GERAIS (UFMG-C)
GA 202057298U
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing nanostructured material involves solubilizing the metal hydroxide with purity between 95 and 99.99% in acetyl acetone with purity between 97 and 99%, in the proportion of 0.5-5 pts. wt. metal hydroxide for a portion of acetyl acetone, preferably 1:1, and shaking for 15-20 minutes. The solution is transferred to an oven at a temperature of 90-120 degrees C, preferably 105 degrees C, for a period of 18-36 hours, until the material remains at constant mass, and formed the metal acetylacetonate. The obtained compound is transferred to the polystyrene dissolved in organic solvent, preferably in the proportion of 1 g of the compound in 20 ml of the organic solvent. The obtained compound is transferred to a reactor and pressurized in an inert atmosphere, between 5 and 15 bar. The reactor is heated with a heating rate of 5-10 degrees C/minute to a temperature in the range of 500-600 degrees C, and maintainec at that temperature for a period of 3-8 hours. The system is cooled to a temperature of 20-30 degrees C.
   USE - Method for preparing nanostructured material used for the absorption of carbon dioxide (CO2) in temperatures between 100 and 600 degrees C (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare nanostructured material, that improves regeneration of the material due to the introduction of salts through the formation of species of nitro or magnesium nitrate, which increases the critical thickness of the product layers and attenuated the degradation of nanoclusters over the repeated cycles of sorption/desorption.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The metal hydroxide is selected from the group comprising alkali and/or alkaline earth metals. The metal acetylacetonate is selected from the group comprising calcium, magnesium, sodium and beryllium acetylacetonate. The organic solvent is ethyl acetate or acetone. The inert atmosphere contains nitrogen or argon. A nanostructured material comprises a mesoporous support and alkali and/or alkaline earth metals. The mesoporous support is impregnated with soluble and calcined calcium, magnesium, sodium or beryllium salts. The mesoporous support is based on carbon, activated or not, obtained from any carbon source, such as alcohols, sugars, cellulose, aliphatic and aromatic hydrocarbons, impregnated or doped with calcium, magnesium, sodium or beryllium.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

AU WANG X
   ZHAO G

   ZHAO Q
AE LANZHOU CHEM & PHYSICS INST CHINESE ACAD (CLCP-C)
   QINGDAO RESOURCE CHEM & NEW MATERIALS (QING-Non-standard)
GA 202041369P
AB    NOVELTY - A sealing grease comprises 50-70 wt.% base oil, 10-15 wt.% alkylbenzene sulfonic acid, 10-15 wt.% calcium hydroxide, 1-4 wt.% boric acid, 5-12 wt.% dodecyl hydroxystearic acid, 1-3 wt.% phase conversion agent and 0.5-1 wt.% antioxidant.
   USE - Sealing grease for sliding channel of sintering machine.
   ADVANTAGE - The grease has excellent high temperature resistance, excellent lubricity and extreme pressure abrasion resistance, excellent sealing performance, and excellent colloidal stability and pumpability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of the sealing grease, which involves (1) adding base oil, alkylbenzene sulfonic acid, calcium hydroxide, boric acid, dodecyl hydroxystearic acid, phase conversion agent and water into reaction kettle, increasing the temperature to 50-60 degrees C, using homogenizer to stir at high speed, reacting for 1 hour, (2) cooling to 41-46 degrees C, introducing carbon dioxide gas for carbonation reaction, controlling the flow of carbon dioxide, reacting for 1-4 hours until the material reaches certain consistency, obtaining a material in a semi-fluid state, (3) turning on the air induction system under the continuous stirring of the mechanical stirring paddle, increase the temperature to 81-90 degrees C to distill out the phase conversion agent, recovering the distilled solvent, ensuring the system does not boil violently, heat-preserving at 90 degrees C for 1 hour, (4) stirring and heating to 160-180 degrees C, refining for 10-15 minutes, (5) cooling to 110 degrees C through the cooling system, adding antioxidants, natural cooling, grinding and homogenizing on a three-roll mill.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The phase conversion agent is chosen from one or more of methanol, ethanol, isopropanol, and isobutanol. The antioxidant is chosen from one or more of diphenylamine, dioctyldiphenylamine, and di-tert-butyl-p-cresol.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Component: The base oil is chosen from one or more of mineral oil, poly alpha -olefin oil, and ester oil.

TI Separation of acidic gas components from gas            mixture used as detergent in gas scrubbing process,            involves gas washing, contacting obtained detergent            with heated compressed recycle gas, transferring heat            to loaded detergent, and cooling.
AU SCHMIDT S
   GUBRINSKI A
AE AIR LIQUIDE SA (AIRL-C)
   AIR LIQUIDE SA (AIRL-C)
GA 202012693F
AB    NOVELTY - Separation of acidic gas components from a gas                mixture involves gas washing with a physically                active detergent, in which a recycle gas drawn off                from at least one regeneration stage of the                physically active detergent is fed to a compressor                to obtain a heated compressed recycle gas, indirect                contacting the detergent withdrawn from a wash                column and loaded with acidic gas components with                the heated compressed recycle gas, transferring                heat from the heated compressed recycle gas to the                loaded detergent, and cooling the heated compressed                recycle gas and heating the loaded detergent to                obtain a cooled compressed recycle gas and a heated                loaded detergent.
   USE - Separation of acidic gas components from gas                mixture used as detergent in gas scrubbing process                for separating hydrogen sulfide and/or carbon                dioxide from raw synthesis gas comprising hydrogen,                carbon monoxide, hydrogen sulfide and carbon                dioxide and optionally at least one element chosen                from carbonyl sulfide, hydrogen cyanide and thiols                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables energy-saving separation of                acidic gas components from gas mixture. The method                is capable of performing gas scrubbing process by                an effective and/or economical method with respect                to return of compressed recycle gases to the                scrubbing column.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for device                (1) for separating acidic gas components from gas                mixture.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                device for separating acidic gas components from                gas mixture.
   Device (1)
   Heat exchanger (101)
   Compressor (102)
   Line (104)
   Column (107)

PN CN110725955-A; CN210830483-U
TI Supercritical carbon dioxide (CO2) special safety sealing structure for protecting bearing box, has static ring that moves to side far from moving ring until returning to original position, when pressure of CO2 on leakage side disappears.
AU TANG D
   BAO X
   YU H
   ZHANG S
   LI T
AE CHENGDU YITONG SEAL CO LTD (CHEN-Non-standard)
GA 202010505S
AB    NOVELTY - The structure comprises a sealing cavity (4) which is connected to a bearing box (10). A dry gas sealing structure (2) is sleeved on a main shaft (1). A static ring (11) and a movable ring (12) are sleeved on main shaft. A labyrinth seal structure is provided between the static ring and the main shaft. The static ring is connected to the sealing cavity and the moving ring is connected to the main shaft. An automatic reset mechanism is used for automatic reset of static ring. The static ring moves toward the moving ring under the pressure of CO2 until it comes into contact with the moving ring, when a large amount of CO2 leaks from the leakage side of dry gas seal structure and at the same time a seal is formed between the static ring and the moving ring. The static ring moves to the side far from the moving ring through the automatic reset mechanism until it returns to original position, when the pressure of CO2 on the leakage side disappears.
   USE - Supercritical carbon dioxide (CO2) special safety sealing structure for protecting bearing box.
   ADVANTAGE - The supercritical CO2 special safety sealing structure is featured with good sealing performance, which effectively protects the safety of the bearing box and increases the reliability of the entire unit.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a cross-sectional view of the supercritical CO2 special safety sealing structure for protecting bearing box.
   Main shaft (1)
   Dry gas sealing structure (2)
   Sealing cavity (4)
   Bearing box (10)
   Static ring (11)

PN CA3049568-A1
TI Method of upgrading heavy oil feedstock, involves feeding heavy oil feedstock and light hydrocarbon feedstock into reaction vessel to incorporate light hydrocarbon feedstock into heavy oil feedstock to produce mixed effluent.
AU BENHAM K
   ARCHER B M
   BENHAM N D
AE SUNCOR ENERGY INC (SNCR-C)
GA 202006515G
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves generating a high hydrogen-content light hydrocarbon feedstock in a thermal processor. The heavy oil feedstock and light hydrocarbon feedstock is fed into a reaction vessel to incorporate the light hydrocarbon feedstock into the heavy oil feedstock to produce a mixed effluent. The thermal processor is an upgrader. The coker comprises operating a fractionator tower to separate out the light hydrocarbon feedstock. The light hydrocarbon feedstock from the fractionator tower is fed into the reaction vessel.
   USE - Method of upgrading heavy oil feedstock for slurry-phase hydrocracking unit or integrated thermal processing reactor.
   ADVANTAGE - The direct incorporation reduces the overall carbon dioxide production that is typically associated with operating heavy-oil processes by reducing the reliance on sources of hydrogen that are associated with the production of carbon dioxide. The mitigation of the coking reactions in turn allows for increased asphaltene conversion, stability of the cracked products at elevated operating temperatures, and incorporation of higher hydrogen content feeds to the reaction system products. The significant volumetric boost to the cracked hydrocarbon-products is provided by increasing the mass of both the carbon and hydrogen incorporated into the cracked hydrocarbon-product.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a system for upgrading a heavy oil feedstock; and
   (2) a reactor unit for upgrading a hydrocarbon-feedstock.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a delayed coker and fractionators system.
   Atmospheric distillation towe (12)
   Vacuum distillation tower (14)
   Heater (20)
   Coker drum (21)
   Fractional tower (22)

PN WO2019223016-A1
TI Blue anthraquinone reactive disperse dye useful for dyeing natural fiber in supercritical carbon dioxide fluid dyeing and dyeing processing of blended fabric of natural fiber.
AU LONG J
   FAN Y
AE UNIV SOOCHOW ZHANGJIAGANG IND TECHNOLOGI (ZJGI-C)
   UNIV SOOCHOW (USWZ-C)
GA 2019998008
AB    NOVELTY - Blue anthraquinone reactive disperse dye (I), is claimed.
   USE - (I) is useful for dyeing natural fiber in supercritical carbon dioxide fluid dyeing and dyeing processing of blended fabric of natural fiber.
   ADVANTAGE - (I) has good dyeing effect for fiber, preferably cotton, wool and silk, fastness indexes and strong applicability. The method is simple and economical.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Blue anthraquinone reactive disperse dye of formula (I), is claimed.
   n = greater than or equal to 2; and
   R = reactive active group.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for producing blue anthraquinone reactive disperse dye (I), comprising (a) adding 1,4-dihydroxyanthraquinone and 1,4-diaminoanthraquinone leucochromite in molar ratio of 1:1.5-1:3 into a reaction container, dissolving aniline and greater than or equal to C diamine in the isobutanol solution, and adding the obtained product into the reaction container to obtain a reaction solution, where the molar ratio of 1,4-dihydroxyanthraquinone and aniline is 1:2-1:7.5, (b) stirring the reaction solution at 55-90 degrees C under nitrogen protection for 2 hours, raising temperature to 108 degrees C, and stirring for 3 hours, reducing the temperature to 95 degrees C, adding copper acetate and piperidine catalyst, and raising the temperature to 108 degrees C, where the molar ratio of 1,4-dihydroxyanthraquinone and copper acetate is 1:1.5-1:6, (3) passing oxygen to oxidize the reaction at 108 degrees C for 2-5 hours, cooling to room temperature, separating, and purifying to obtain a blue solid as a precursor for anthraquinone reactive disperse dye, (4) reacting the precursor with a reactive active group (R) to obtain (I).
TF Preferred Definitions:
   R = dichlorotriazine, monochlorotriazine, 2-bromoacrylamide, vinyl sulfone or their derivatives.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PT P
PN US2019337863-A1; WO2019213461-A1
TI Producing organic ammonia, comprises e.g. heating organic waste material in anaerobic digester device to drive biological anaerobic digestion of organic waste material and yield digested organic waste composition.
AU STEMWEDEL T
AE CALIFORNIA ORGANIC FERTILIZERS INC (CALO-Non-standard)
GA 201993752P
AB    NOVELTY - Producing (M1) organic ammonia, comprises: heating an organic waste material in an anaerobic digester device to drive a biological anaerobic digestion of the organic waste material and yield a digested organic waste composition; separating solid portions of the organic waste composition from an effluent of the digested organic waste composition; heating the effluent to evolve carbon dioxide-laden gas from the effluent; transferring the heated effluent to a steam stripping apparatus to extract ammonia from the heated effluent in a steam/ammonia solution; and condensing the steam/ammonia solution to yield an aqueous ammonia product derived from effluent without chemical reactions.
   USE - The method is useful for producing organic ammonia, which is used in liquid organic nitrogenous fertilizer composition (claimed) for organic farming. No biological data given.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) producing (M2) organic ammonia, comprising heating a nitrogen-rich effluent to evolve carbon dioxide-laden gas from the effluent, transferring the heated effluent to a steam stripping process to extract ammonia from the heated effluent in a steam/ammonia solution, and condensing the steam/ammonia solution to yield an aqueous ammonia product derived from the effluent without utilizing chemical reactions;
   (2) a liquid organic nitrogenous fertilizer composition (C1) comprising an aqueous solution of (i) the aqueous ammonia product of method (M1) or (M2), and (ii) an organic acid in a concentration of 6-50%; and
   (3) a liquid organic nitrogenous fertilizer composition (C2) comprising an aqueous solution including a biologically produced ammonia in a concentration of 3-25%.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Method: The methods (M1) and (M1) further comprise: transferring the carbon dioxide-laden gas to a packed column to cool the carbon dioxide-laden gas and dissolve ammonia present in the gas in a cooling fluid; adding a basic chemical agent to the effluent before or during the step of heating the effluent to increase the pH of the effluent; and transferring the cooling fluid and ammonia dissolved in it to the steam stripping process to extract the dissolved ammonia. In the methods (M1) and (M2), the step of heating the effluent drives the chemical equilibrium of ammonium bicarbonate toward the production of ammonia and carbon dioxide. Preferred Composition: The compositions (C1) and (C2) comprise 10-20 wt.% of the organically produced ammonia. The composition (C2) further comprises: humic acids as a chelation agent for the ammonia; and organic acid. The composition (C2) comprises 3-8% of the humic acids. The organic acid is a triprotic acid.
   ACTIVITY - Fertilizer.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - None given.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC C04 (Fertilisers - including urea and phosphoric acid production. Also soil modifiers and plant growth media. Chemical aspects of compost production.); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.)

PT P
PN WO2019191034-A1; CA3095128-A1; AU2019242630-A1; KR2020127038-A; US2021008529-A1; CN112218716-A; IN202017042289-A; EP3774038-A1; JP2021519204-W; US11406971-B2
TI Preparing confined metallic nanocatalyst within a            mesoporous material for catalyzing alkene ammoxidation            reactions, involves impregnating organic compound,            comprising multidentate ligand capable of forming            coordination bonds with metal ion.
AU LUZ MINGUEZ I
   SOUKRI M
   LAIL M
   CARPENTER J R
   PARVATHIKAR S
   CARPENTER M
   LUZ M I
   LUZMINGUEZ I
AE RES TRIANGLE INST (RTRI-C)
   RES TRIANGLE INST (RTRI-C)
   RES TRIANGLE INST (RTRI-C)
   RES TRIANGLE INST (RTRI-C)
GA 201983281P
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing a confined metallic nanocatalyst                within a mesoporous material (MPM) involves                impregnating at least one or more organic compound,                comprising one or more multidentate ligand(s)                (Ax(L-x)) capable of forming coordination bonds                with at least one metal ion, on the mesoporous                material to form a first intermediate                ((Ax(L-x))/MPM), exposing the first intermediate                ((Ax(L-x)/MPM)) to an acid in gas phase to form a                second intermediate ((HX(L-x)/MPM)), adding to the                second intermediate ((Hx(L-x)/MPM)) a solvent                solution of one or more metal ions (M1+y, M2+y,                M3+y) so as to form coordination bonds with the one                or more multidentate ligand(s) forming a metal                organic framework (MOF) precursor confined within a                mesoporous material (MOF/MPM), and treating the                precursor (MOF/MPM) under controlled transformation                conditions to form the metallic nanocatalyst                confined within the mesoporous material.
   USE - Method of preparing a confined metallic                nanocatalyst within a mesoporous material for                catalyzing alkene ammoxidation reactions, alkene                epoxidation, ammonia synthesis, carboxylation                reactions, carbon dioxide methanation reactions,                conversion of carbon dioxide to fuel, direct                methanol synthesis from methane, dry-methane                reforming, electrocatalytic ammonia oxidation,                electrocatalytic oxygen reduction reactions,                Fischer-Tropsch synthesis, hydro-/dehydrogenation                of liquid organic hydrogen carriers, hydrotreating                and hydroprocessing esterification reactions,                methanol synthesis from syngas, reverse water-gas                shift reactions, or water-gas shift reactions (all                claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare confined                metallic nanocatalyst within a mesoporous material                which is stable under high temperature conditions,                has unique and highly tunable features, and                superior catalytic activity.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The method further                  involves contacting the precursor (MOF/MPM) with                  one or more organic compounds (Z) to make a                  second multidentate ligand capable of forming                  coordination bonds (Z/MOF /MPM) and adding a                  solvent solution of one or more additional metal                  ion to form a modified MOF precursor with                  additional metals confined within the mesoporous                  material (MOF/MPM). The controlled transformation                  conditions cause greater than 90% of the carbon                  in the MOF to be released from the MOF/MPM. The                  controlled transformation conditions cause 50                  plus minus 10% of the carbon in the MOF to be                  released from the MOF/MPM. The treatment under                  controlled transformation conditions is pyrolysis                  at a temperature of about 300-1000 degrees C in                  an inert gas atmosphere, calcination at a                  temperature of about 300-600 degrees C in an                  atmosphere containing oxygen gas and reduction                  with hydrogen at a temperature of about 25-300                  degrees C. The confined nanocatalyst is                  monometallic (M1). The confined nanocatalyst is                  bimetallic (M1+M2). The confined nanocatalyst                  within the mesoporous material has 3 or more                  metals and has a diameter of less than 10 nm. The                  mesoporous material has a surface area of about                  100-1000 m2/g. The confined nanocatalyst confined                  within mesoporous material is further reacted                  with additional organometallic metal complexes or                  metal salts with polymers, organometallic ligand                  precursors, nitrogen-containing organic                  compounds, phosphorus-containing organic                  compounds, sulfur-containing organic compounds,                  boron-containing organic compounds, halide salts,                  organic halides, or metal atoms added via atomic                  layer deposition or chemical vapor deposition.                  Preferred Components: The chelating ligand                  comprises a metal binding site for complexing a                  second metal ion. The mesoporous material is a                  mesoporous metal oxide, a mesoporous silica, a                  mesoporous carbon, a mesoporous polymer, a                  mesoporous silicoalumina (zeolite), a mesoporous                  organosilica, or a mesoporous aluminophosphate.                  The mesoporous metal oxide is aluminum oxide,                  cerium oxide, titanium oxide, zirconium oxide, or                  magnesium oxide. The metal ions (M1+y, M2+y,                  M3+y) are selected from the group consisting of                  aluminum (Al), gold (Au), cerium (Ce), cobalt                  (Co), iron (Fe), iridium (Ir), molybdenum (Mo),                  nickel (Ni), palladium (Pd), rhodium (Rh),                  ruthenium (Ru), titanium (Ti), vanadium (V) and                  zirconium (Zr) or combinations. The multidentate                  ligand for the MOF is selected from the group                  consisting of, terephthalate,                  benzene-l,3,5-tricarboxylate, 2,5-dioxibenzene                  dicarboxylate, biphenyl-4, 4 -dicarboxylate,                  imidazolate, pyrimidine-azolate, triazolate,                  tetrazolate, derivatives or combinations. The MOF                  is selected from HKUST-l, M2(dobpdc), MIL-100,                  MIL-101, MIL-53, MOF-74, NU-1000, porous                  coordination network (PCN)-222, PCN-224, UiO-66,                  UiO-67, ZIF (Zeolitic Imidazolate Framework)-8,                  ZIFs, or derivatives. The mesoporous material is                  selected from the group consisting of, MCM-41,                  SBA-15, or commercially available silica. The                  catalyst further comprises an added metal                  promotor.
DC E17 (Other aliphatics.); E35; ; H04 (Petroleum processing - including treating, cracking, reforming, gasoline preparation - biosynthesis based on hydrocarbon feedstocks is included (C10G).); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.)

TI System for co-conversion of greenhouse gases and biochar by pulsating jet plasma, has cyclone gas output port that is connected to exhaust pipe, and sampling tube that is taken up on exhaust pipe to collect gas.
AU ZHANG H
   LI X
   LI L
   YAN J
   CEN K
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG (UYZH-C)
GA 2019400191
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a pulse jet plasma reactor (1) that is provided with outer electrode, inner electrode, carbon dioxide primary air inlet, base, carbon dioxide secondary air inlet, quartz cover and air distribution plate. The top of the quartz cover is connected to the cyclone through a pipeline. The solids outlet of the cyclone separator (5) is connected to the return port of the quartz cover through a pipeline. A return valve (6) is arranged on the pipeline. The cyclone gas output port is connected to an exhaust pipe, where outlet of the exhaust pipe acts as a gas outlet (7). The sampling tube (8) is taken up on the exhaust pipe to collect the gas and to pass the gas into the online infrared analyzer (9).
   USE - System for co-conversion of greenhouse gases and biochar by pulsating jet plasma.
   ADVANTAGE - The system has simple process, high energy density, low investment and operating costs. The high-efficiency conversion of CO2 under the condition of no catalyst and atmospheric pressure is realized.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for synergistically transforming greenhouse gases and biochars using system.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows the schematic view of the system for co-conversion of greenhouse gases and biochar by pulsating jet plasma.
   Pulse jet plasma reactor (1)
   Cyclone separator (5)
   Return valve (6)
   Gas outlet (7)
   Sampling tube (8)
   Online infrared analyzer (9)

TI Orally administrable composition used to treat gastroesophageal diseases, comprises preparation of slippery elm bark, alginic acid or alginate, compound producing release of carbon dioxide, and alginic acid crosslinking agent.
AU COLL R
   GARDIER S
AE LAB ARKOPHARMA (ARKO-Non-standard)
   LAB ARKOPHARMA (ARKO-Non-standard)
   LAB ARKOPHARMA (ARKO-Non-standard)
   LAB ARKOPHARMA (ARKO-Non-standard)
GA 201930679A
AB    NOVELTY - Orally administrable composition for a human or animal individual, adapted to form, upon ingestion, a gelled raft floating on the surface of the gastric contents of the individual, comprises: (a) a preparation based on slippery elm bark (Ulmus rubra), preferably based on the internal bark of slippery elm; (b) alginic acid or alginate; (c) at least one compound producing a release of carbon dioxide in acidic medium, including (c1) at least one bicarbonate salt, preferably at least one alkali metal bicarbonate including sodium bicarbonate and/or potassium bicarbonate, and/or (c2) at least one carbonate salt, preferably at least one divalent or trivalent alkaline earth metal cation carbonate including calcium carbonate and/or magnesium carbonate; (d) optionally at least one alginic acid crosslinking agent, preferably at least one divalent or trivalent metal cation source, or at least one alkaline earth metal salt, including calcium salt and/or magnesium salt.
   USE - The composition is useful for: treating or preventing gastroesophageal diseases, preferably gastroesophageal reflux disease and/or its symptoms including acid reflux, heartburn, difficult digestion, indigestion, heartburn-gastritis, dyspepsia and peptide ulcers; prolonged or targeted delivery of active ingredient(s) into the stomach and/or the gastroesophageal junction of the individual; and local relief of irritated gastroesophageal mucosa (all claimed). Test details are described but no results given.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for tablet including chewable tablet, pellet, sachet, hermetic, stick, preferably hermetic, solution, oral suspension, gel or syrup, comprising the composition.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The composition comprises: 5-600 mg of elm bark powder, preferably of the slippery elm bark; 50-1500 mg sodium alginate; 50-1000 mg sodium bicarbonate; and 50-500 mg calcium carbonate. Preferred Components: The component (c) comprises (c1) and/or (c2). The active ingredient (d) when (c2) is absent, or (c2) is present but is different from at least one divalent or trivalent metal cation carbonate. The component (b) is an alkali or alkaline earth metal salt of alginate, preferably an alkali metal salt of alginate including sodium alginate and/or potassium alginate. The mass ratio components (a)/(b) is 0.003-12. The mass ratio components (a)/(c1) is 0.005-12. The mass ratio components (a)/(c2) is 0.003-12. The slippery elm bark preparation is a slippery elm bark powder, an aqueous extract of slippery elm bark and/or an hydroglycerin extract of slippery elm bark, preferably an elm bark powder and/or an aqueous extract of slippery elm bark, where the slippery elm bark is the inner bark of slippery elm.
   ACTIVITY - Antiinflammatory.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - None given.
   EXAMPLE - Typical composition comprised of: 267 mg sodium bicarbonate; 250 mg elm red powder (inner bark); 160 mg calcium carbonate; 166 mg sodium alginate; 34 mg spearmint mint powder aroma; 772.1 mg sorbitol; 3.3 mg Stevia; 34 mg mono-, di- and triesters of behenic acid; and 13.6 mg magnesium stearate.

PN US2019070551-A1
TI Device for separating carbon dioxide from flue            gas, has gas inlet manifold comprising inlets adjacent            to bottom portion of ascending gas channels, and fluid            manifold comprising outlets adjacent to bottom portion            of descending fluid channels.
AU BAXTER L
   KNIGHT H
   PILLING B
   DAVIS N
AE BAXTER L (BAXT-Individual)
   KNIGHT H (KNIG-Individual)
   PILLING B (PILL-Individual)
   DAVIS N (DAVI-Individual)
GA 2019217894
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a vessel comprising a top                portion and a bottom portion, where the top portion                comprises a gas outlet (110), a fluid inlet (108),                and a direct-contact heat exchanger, and the bottom                portion comprises an indirect-contact heat                exchanger, a gas inlet manifold, and a fluid outlet                manifold (120). The indirect-contact heat exchanger                is aligned vertically within the vessel and                comprises parallel heat exchange surfaces, where                plenums between the heat exchange surfaces comprise                alternating, adjacent ascending gas channels and                descend fluid channels. The gas inlet manifold                comprises inlets adjacent to the bottom portion of                each of the ascending gas channels, where the fluid                outlet manifold comprises outlets adjacent to the                bottom portion of each of the descending fluid                channels.
   USE - Device for separating a vapor component e.g.                carbon dioxide, nitrogen oxide, sulfur dioxide,                nitrogen dioxide, sulfur trioxide, hydrogen                sulfide, hydrogen cyanide, water, mercury, and                hydrocarbons, from gas e.g. flue gas, producer gas,                natural gas, steam reforming gas, hydrocarbons,                light gas, refinery off-gas, organic solvents,                steam, and ammonia (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The device combines benefits of                indirect-contact heat exchanger (ICHE) and                direct-contact exchanger (DCE) while minimizing and                eliminating difficulties of each is needed.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a                method for separating a vapor component from                gas.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a cross-sectional view of a                combined DCE and ICHE for removing a vapor                component from carrier gas.
   ICHE (106)
   Fluid inlet (108)
   Gas outlet (110)
   Fluid outlet manifold (120)
   DCE (128)
   Plates (130)
   Fluid (144)
   Bubbles (154)

PT P
PN CN109188507-A; CN109188507-B
TI Carbon dioxide gun based-mine seismic advanced            detection method, involves transmitting collected            vibration signals by collecting host to computer, and            combining on-site geological data by computer for            comprehensive analysis.
AU WANG B
   HAO J
   HUANG L
   LIU S
   ZHOU F
   ZHENG F
   XIE J
   GAO B
AE UNIV CHINA MINING & TECHNOLOGY BEIJING (UYMB-C)
GA 201909137U
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves using multiple jet holes                to face the same CO2 gun (2) as a seismic source                for mine seismic detection. The drilling holes (3)                are drilled along the pre-mining direction in the                middle of roadway (4), and multiple predetermined                positions are determined at equal intervals in the                borehole. Multiple six-component detectors are                arranged side by side in the range of 100 meters                from the side of roadway. The CO2 gun is passed                through detonating line and signal triggering box                in parallel on the low-voltage initiator, and the                signal triggering box is connected to the                collecting host (7). The acquisition host is turned                on, and the CO2 gun is used to transmit a vibration                signal to the pre-mining direction through                low-pressure initiator. The collected vibration                signals are transmitted by collecting host to                computer, the on-site geological data is combined                by computer for comprehensive analysis, and the                geological anomaly structure is predicted in front                of coal lane.
   USE - Carbon dioxide (CO2) gun based-mine seismic                advanced detection method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has low pressure initiation, source                orientation, and is energy controllable and                reusable. The mine seismic advance detection can be                carried out during the coal body fracturing                operation of the CO2 gun, so that the mine seismic                advanced detection can be carried out without                affecting the construction of the working                face.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a layout view of the                carbondioxide gun based-mine seismic advanced                detection method.
   Coal seam (1)
   CO2 gun (2)
   Drilling holes (3)
   Roadway (4)
   Collecting host (7)

MC S03-C01A; S03-C01B; T01-J13

PN WO2019010394-A1; US2019039682-A1; EP3649040-A1; US10926830-B2; EP3649040-A4
TI Suspension linkage system for use in a two-wheel vehicle, such as bicycle, comprises shock that has first and second mounting axes, which is operably connected to linkage bodies, where portion of link body is positioned within swingarm body.
AU ZAWISTOWSKI P
AE YETI CYCLING LLC (YETI-Non-standard)
   YETI CYCLING LLC (YETI-Non-standard)
   YETI CYCLING LLC (YETI-Non-standard)
GA 201904655S
AB    NOVELTY - The suspension linkage system comprises a shock (302) that has first and second mounting axes, which is operably connected to two linkage bodies. The shock is positioned to provide linear resistance between the two linkage bodies that has relative motion with respect to one another. A shock extension assembly includes extension bodies (38). The first and second extension body mounting axes are perpendicular with respect to one another. Each of multiple extension bodies are coupled to opposing mounting surfaces of the shock body (36). The first extension body axis is positioned in front of the seat tube (103). The portion of the link body (33) is positioned within the swingarm body. The shock extension assembly couples to an accessory, such as inflation device, a carbon dioxide cartridge, a carbon dioxide inflator valve, a tube, tire, patch-kit, or a spare component of the vehicle.
   USE - Suspension linkage system for use in a two-wheel vehicle, such as a bicycle. Can also be used in electric bicycle, or motorcycle.
   ADVANTAGE - The suspension linkage system ensures to provide the vehicle with a dynamic response to acceleration and deceleration, and also allow larger shocks or dampers to fit within the assembly without interfering with the linkage, and also enhances the shock capability, easier assembly and disassembly of the shock, and room for accessories, such as, tools and spare parts.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the shock or damper with the suspension linkage assembly.
   Link body (33)
   Shock body (36)
   Extension body (38)
   Seat tube (103)
   Shock (302)

PN CN108887545-A
TI Vegetable beverage useful for beautifying, comprises rose, Chrysanthemum, Lophatherum gracile, lotus leaf, erythritol, mogrosides, acesulfame potassium, anhydrous citric acid, rose flavor, lychee flavor, carbon dioxide and water.
AU ZHANG J
   WANG Y
   QIN H
   ZHANG C
   LI S
   HUAN F
   ZHOU J
   HU Q
AE UNIV NANJING MEDICAL (UYNM-C)
GA 201897289K
AB    NOVELTY - Vegetable beverage comprises (i) main material composition comprising 1-5 pts. wt. rose, 1-5 pts. wt. Chrysanthemum, 5-15 pts. wt. Lophatherum gracile and 2-8 pts. wt. lotus leaf, (ii) 150-260 g/l main material extract, (iii) 34-50 g/l excipient comprising 36-44 pts. wt. erythritol, 0.06-0.26 pt. wt. mogrosides, 0.06-0.26 pt. wt. acesulfame potassium, 0.7-2.3 pts. wt. anhydrous citric acid, 0.1-0.4 pt. wt. rose flavor and 0.1-0.4 pt. wt. lychee flavor, (iv) gaseous component (6.5-7.5 g/l) comprises carbon dioxide, and (v) water, where the weight ratio of main material extract and main material composition is 5-20:1.
   USE - The vegetable beverage is useful for beautifying.
   ADVANTAGE - The vegetable beverage is sugarless and has good stability, and harmonious and pleasant flavor.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for method for preparing vegetable beverage which involves (a) boiling and extracting the above mentioned main ingredient composition using water, filtering to remove solid matter, leaving to stand for preset time, centrifuging and filtering to obtain liquid as main material extract, where the weight ratio of main material extract and main material composition is 5-20:1, filling and sterilizing the material, (b) taking main material extract, excipient and water, uniformly stirring to obtain primary product beverage, filling and sterilizing the material, and (c) pressurizing the steam component into primary product beverage, and filling the completed beverage product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Composition: The beverage comprises (i) main material composition comprising 2-4 pts. wt. rose, 2-4 pts. wt. Chrysanthemum, 8-12 pts. wt. L.gracile and 4-6 pts. wt. lotus leaf, (ii) 170-230 g/l main material extract, (iii) excipient (39-45 g/l) comprising 38-42 pts. wt. erythritol, 0.11-0.21 pt. wt. mogrosides, 0.11-0.21 pt. wt. acesulfame potassium, 1.2-1.8 pts. wt. anhydrous citric acid, 0.15-0.35 pt. wt. rose flavor and 0.15-0.35 pt. wt. lychee flavor. Preferred Method: The step (a) involves boiling at least twice, by adding 10-30 times of water each time, extracting for at least 50 minutes, filtering to remove solid matter using at least 200 mesh filter cloth, centrifuging and filtering using ceramic membrane having a pore diameter of less than or equal to 0.8 mu m, settling at 2-6 degrees C for 24-72 minutes, and centrifuging at 2000 revolutions per minute for at least 10 minutes, recording the volume of main material extract as V before filling, concentrating the main material extract, where the volume of obtained concentrate is at least 1/4 of the volume of main material extract V, filling and sterilizing the concentrate, and storing the material at 2-4 degrees C for 72 hours. The step (b) involves diluting the concentrated solution to obtain main material extract of volume V, and taking main material, excipient and water to prepare primary product beverage.

TI Ring-shaped chicken product prepared by using minced chicken meat, water, water retaining agent, spices, filling agent, salt, and quality improver comprising sodium hydrogen carbonate, sodium citrate, edible salt, and maltodextrin.
AU FU F
   ZHOU H
   MA R
   ZHANG R
AE FUJIAN SUNNER FOOD CO LTD (FUJI-Non-standard)
GA 2018888369
AB    NOVELTY - Ring-shaped chicken product is prepared by using 46-60 pts. wt. minced chicken meat, 2-5 pts. wt. water, 3-8 pts. wt. water retaining agent, 0.2-0.3 pts. wt. spices, 20.2-25.3 pts. wt. filling agent, 1-2 pts. wt. salt, 0.2-0.5 pts. wt. quality improving agent, where the quality improving agent is prepared by using sodium hydrogen carbonate, sodium carbonate, sodium citrate, edible salt, and maltodextrin in a weight ratio of 8:1.8:0.4:18:20.
   USE - The ring-shaped chicken product is useful in fried products.
   ADVANTAGE - The chicken product maintains original taste of chicken meat, has good water retention effect, and reduces weight loss rate after frying by 2% as compared to existing product.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for method for preparing chicken product, which involves weighing the above-mentioned raw materials, grinding chicken meat and adding into a carbon dioxide mixer, add salt, quality improving agent, adding remaining raw materials except filler, stirring for 6-15 minutes, add filler, continuously stirring for 6-15 minutes, mixing, adding dry ice into the carbon dioxide mixer and mixing for 10-12 minutes, maintaining under vacuum condition for -0.08 MPa, adding into molding device, pressing into a ring-shaped product, wrapping and drying at 180 degrees C for 26-30 seconds, and quick-freezing and packing product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Product: The chicken product further comprises 6-8 pts. wt. vegetable. Preferred Components: The minced chicken meat comprises 18-25 pts. wt. chicken breast meat, 8-10 pts. wt. chicken leg meat and 20-25 pts. wt. chicken skin. The filling agent comprises 9-11 pts. wt. chicken, 9-11 pts. wt. water, 0.09-0.11 pts. wt. salt, and 2.11-3.19 pts. wt. soy protein. The water retaining agent comprises starch, soy protein and maltodextrin in a ratio of 6:6:1.

PN WO2018175349-A1; US2020290742-A1; US11465763-B2
TI Hybrid-electric aircraft, has range extending            generator coupled to distribution bus, where carbon            dioxide equivalent well-to-wake greenhouse gas            emissions of series hybrid powertrain is in specific            value.
AU KUMAR A A
   KNAPP B M
AE ZUNUM AERO INC (ZUNU-Non-standard)
   ZUNUM AERO INC (ZUNU-Non-standard)
   ZUNUM AERO INC (ZUNU-Non-standard)
GA 201875494P
AB    NOVELTY - The aircraft has a series hybrid powertrain                for powering a hybrid-electric aircraft for                medium-haul flight with a range of 1,000 miles,                where the series hybrid powertrain comprises a set                of energy storage units with an onboard stored                energy of 1,500 kWh. A distribution bus is coupled                to the set of energy storage units. A range                extending generator is coupled to the distribution                bus to generate onboard generated power of 7.5 MW.                A degree of hybridization for the series hybrid                powertrain given by a ratio of energy expended from                the set of energy storage units to a total amount                of energy consumed by the three electric propulsors                during the medium-haul flight with a range of 1,000                miles is 25%. A carbon dioxide equivalent (CChe)                well-to-wake greenhouse gas (GHG) emissions of the                series hybrid powertrain are less than 0.25                lbs/available seat mile (ASM).
   USE - Hybrid-electric aircraft.
   ADVANTAGE - The aircraft allows a variable pitch fan to                provide high efficiency across a wide range of                speeds and altitudes in regional operations and a                ducted fan to provide high static thrust and low                noise for operation out of community and regional                airports.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a                method for operating a hybrid-electric                aircraft.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic block diagram                of a hybrid powertrain architecture with two gas                turbine generators for a hybrid-electric                aircraft.

TI Sports footwear for indoor sports, has cushioning insert which is inserted into predetermined position inside uni-brush so as to be directly under insole, and upper portion which is coupled with uni-brush to cover upper portion of foot.
AU LUNDBERG J H
AE LUNDBERG J H (LUND-Individual)
GA 2018765401
AB    NOVELTY - The footwear (100) has a uni-brush (200) which is prepared with a blown-foam rubber having several uniformly dispersed micropores. An insole (400) is arranged on a top of the uni-brush and made of a foamed thermoplastic polyurethane such as expanded thermoplastipolyurethane. A cushioning insert (300) which is made of a blowthermoplasticpolyurethane is manufactured by using nitrogen or carbon dioxide as a blowing agent and is inserted into a predetermined position inside the uni-brush so as to be directly under the insole. An upper portion (600) is coupled with the uni-brush to cover an upper portion of a foot.
   USE - Sports footwear for indoor sports.
   ADVANTAGE - The uni-brush has a structure in which a midsole and a bottom sole are integrated, and is made of a material of a blown-foam-rubber to increase lightness and elasticity, and is used for molding. The elasticity of the uni-brush is increased by inserting a cushioning insert made of a blown-thermoplasticallyholethane manufactured by using nitrogen or carbon dioxide as a foaming agent at a predetermined position of the uni-brush. The three-layered cushioning structure includes a cushioning insert made of blown-foam-rubber and a cushioning insert made of blownthermoplasticpolyurethane and an insole made of expanded thermoplastipolyurethane.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows an exploded perspective view of the sports footwear.
   Sports footwear (100)
   Uni-brush (200)
   Cushioning insert (300)
   Insole (400)
   Upper portion (600)

PT P
PN CN108467047-A; CN108467047-B
TI Lithium iron silicate preparation involves dissolving sodium silicate in water to obtain sodium silicate solution, and then adding battery grade ferrous sulfate crystal to hydrazine hydrate solution to prepare ferrous sulfate solution.
AU FANG J
AE FANG J (FANG-Individual)
GA 201870077Y
AB    NOVELTY - Lithium iron silicate preparation involves dissolving sodium silicate in water to obtain sodium silicate solution, adding battery grade ferrous sulfate crystal to hydrazine hydrate solution to prepare ferrous sulfate solution having a concentration of 1-1.5 mol/l, and then adding lithium silicate solution, a ferrous sulfate solution and an aqueous ammonia solution to the reaction vessel at 20-35 degrees C with stirring for 15-30 minutes to prepare aqueous ammonia solution. The obtained slurry is heated at 100-120 degrees C in oxygen atmosphere under stirring and then filtered and washed to obtain ferrosilicon mixture. The obtained ferrosilicon mixture is added to pure water slurry, and then mixed with lithium carbonate, nano titanium dioxide and manganese acetate with stirring and dispersing followed by carbon dioxide introduction to give hydrogen carbonate.
   USE - Method for preparing lithium iron silicate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method prepares the lithium iron silicate in a simple, cost-effective and eco-friendly manner with stabilized structure during charging and discharging by coating of carbon and doping of manganese and titanium, and prolonged cycle life.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The carbon dioxide and sand are placed into sand mill to a particle size of 150-200 nm, centrifuged to obtain dry material, and then pulverized by airflow to obtain pulverized material. The pulverized material is placed in a crucible and sintered under an inert atmosphere at 610-625 degrees C for 18-20 hours, and then cooled for 4-6 hours to obtain the lithium iron silicate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The sodium silicate, ferrous sulfate crystal, lithium carbonate, nano-titanium dioxide, and the manganese acetate are provided in a mol ratio of 1:1:1.02-1.03:0.01-0.02:0.1-0.2. The inert atmosphere is nitrogen or carbon dioxide.
