PT P
PN CN108380216-A; CN108380216-B
TI Cobalt-based catalyst preparation involves taking soluble cobalt salt, soluble aluminum salt and water to form mixed solution, adding precipitating agent to mixed solution, and then heating to obtain cobalt aluminum hydrotalcite.
AU WANG L
   XIAO F
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG (UYZH-C)
GA 201864339C
AB    NOVELTY - Cobalt-based catalyst preparation involves taking soluble cobalt salt, a soluble aluminum salt and water to form a mixed solution, adding precipitating agent to the mixed solution, and then heating under stirring at 100-110 degrees C for 6-12 hours to obtain cobalt aluminum hydrotalcite. The obtained cobalt aluminum hydrotalcite is dried at 90-110 degrees C for 8-16 hours, and then calcined at 300-600 degrees C for 3-6 hours to obtain cobalt aluminum mixed oxide. The obtained cobalt aluminum mixed oxide is subjected to a reduction reaction in a mixed gas atmosphere of hydrogen and argon at 300-650 degrees C for 2-6 hours. The obtained cobalt aluminum mixed oxide is naturally cooled at room temperature to obtain the cobalt-based catalyst.
   USE - Method for preparing cobalt-based catalyst for catalyzing carbon dioxide to ethanol.
   ADVANTAGE - The method prepares the cobalt-based catalyst in a simple, cost-effective and eco-friendly manner without influencing uncertain factors in the preparation process on the structure and performance of the catalyst.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for hydrogenating carbon dioxide to ethanol, which involves:
   (A) placing 5-50 mg catalyst and 0.5-5 ml solvent in 20-100 ml reaction vessel at 100-240 degrees C with stirring for 1-24 hours to obtain main product;
   (B) mixing 0.1-1.0 g catalyst with quartz sand in a mass ratio of 1-5; and
   (C) filling the mixture into quartz reaction tube at 100-240 degrees C.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The precipitating agent is selected from urea, sodium hydroxide, potassium hydroxide, sodium carbonate, potassium carbonate, sodium hydrogencarbonate, potassium hydrogencarbonate, ammonia water or oxalic acid. The soluble cobalt is selected from cobalt nitrate, cobalt acetate, cobalt sulfate or cobalt chloride. The soluble aluminum salt is selected from aluminum nitrate, aluminum acetate, aluminum sulfate or aluminum chloride.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The solvent is selected from water, cyclohexane, toluene, ethylbenzene, dichloromethane, chloroform, dimethyl sulfoxide, acetonitrile or acetone.

PN US2018224173-A1; CA2994114-A1; EP3361203-A1; CN108413658-A; US10254023-B2; EP3361203-B1
TI System for cooling area proximate to load using carbon dioxide, has pipe for removing heat from refrigerant as refrigerant is flowed through refrigerant tubes, where load utilizes refrigerant from heat exchanger to cool space proximate load.
AU DELVENTURA R
AE HEATCRAFT REFRIGERATION PROD LLC (HEAT-Non-standard)
   HEATCRAFT REFRIGERATION PROD LLC (HEAT-Non-standard)
   HEATCRAFT REFRIGERATION PROD LLC (HEAT-Non-standard)
GA 2018663915
AB    NOVELTY - The system has refrigerant tubes (204) that are extended through a frame (202). A high side heat exchanger removes heat from a refrigerant as the refrigerant flows through the refrigerant tubes. Heat pipes contact the frame, where a heat pipe of the heat pipes is directly coupled to the frame. The heat pipe removes the heat from the refrigerant as the refrigerant is flowed through the refrigerant tubes. A load is configured to utilize the refrigerant from the high side heat exchanger to cool space proximate the load, where the high side heat exchanger comprises a gas cooler, a fluid cooler and a condenser. One of the heat pipes transfers the heat from a refrigerant tube to the frame.
   USE - System for cooling an area proximate to a load using refrigerant i.e. carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The system replaces anchor pipes by the heat pipes of same size and geometry to increase efficiency of high side heat exchangers, thus improving overall efficiency of the system. The high side heat exchanger maintains same or similar physical space to increase heat rejection efficiency and efficiency of the system.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a method for cooling an area proximate to a load using refrigerant.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a perspective view of a high side heat exchanger.
   Frame (202)
   Refrigerant tube (204)
   Anchor tube (206)
   Fan (208)
   Area of high side heat exchanger (210)

PN CN108339357-A
TI MTO catalyst regeneration device comprises oxygen-containing gas supplying device which is provided with oxygencontaining gas supplying port in which oxygen containing gas is oxygen, and pressure-regulated gas.
AU WANG H
   WU G
   GAO W
   LIU Y
   PAN H
AE CHN ENERGY INVESTMENT GROUP CO LTD (SHGR-C)
   EERDUOSI COAL-TO-LIQUID BRANCH CHINA SHE (SHGR-C)
   SHENHUA XINJIANG CHEM CO LTD (SHGR-C)
GA 201864777V
AB    NOVELTY - MTO catalyst regeneration device comprises oxygen-containing gas supplying device which is provided with oxygencontaining gas supplying port in which oxygen containing gas is oxygen, or mixed gas of oxygen and pressure-regulated gas, and pressure regulated gas is carbon dioxide and water steam regenerator, wherei regenerator is provided with catalyst to regenerated inlet, oxygen-containing gas inlet, first regenerated catalyst outlet and smoke gas outlet, where oxygen-containing gas inlet and oxygencontaining gas supplying inlet are connected. The purifying device is provided with purifying device inlet, second regenerated catalyst outlet and primary purified gas outlet, and purifying device inlet and smoke gas outlet are connected with washing device which is provided with primary purified gas inlet and purified gas outlet, and primary purified gas inlet is communicated with primary purified gas outlet.
   USE - MTO catalyst regeneration device.
   ADVANTAGE - The MTO catalyst regeneration device has low regeneration gas volume, simple to operate, cost-effective and suitabile for industrialization.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for performing MTO catalyst regeneration, which involves:
   (A) incompletely burning catalyst to regenerated with an oxygen-containing gas to obtain first section regeneration catalyst and smoke gas;
   (B) purifying smoke gas to obtain purified gas and second section regeneration catalyst; and
   (C) removing carbon di-oxide in the purified gas to obtain carbon mono-oxide product gas, where oxygen-containing gas is oxygen, or mixed gas is oxygen and pressure-regulated gas, and pressure-regulated gas is carbon dioxide or water steam, where weight ratio of carried coke on catalyst to regenerated and oxygen in oxygen-containing gas is 0.34-0.44: 1.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Condition: The carrying coke amount in catalyst to be regenerated is 4-9 wt.%, preferably 5-7 wt.%. the apparent pressure of the incomplete burning process is 0.05-0.9 mega Pascal. The temperature is 500-700 degrees C, preferably, apparent pressure is 0.06-0.15 mega Pascal, temperature is 550-680 degrees C. The temperature of the flue gas is reduced to 150-300 degrees C. The solid content of purified gas is 10-100 milligram per cubic meter, and volume percent water vapor in purge gas is 10-30 wt.%. Preferred Process: The purifying process involves performing flue gas waste heat recovery, and removing solid particles in the flue gas and partial steam to obtain primary purified gas. The primary purified gas is washed to obtain purified gas.

TI Capnoxygen mask for capnoxygen system for            performing carbon dioxide sampling operation, has nasal            breath-sampling element for reducing dilution of            exhaled breath, and breath sampling port for receiving            breath samples.
AU KERTSER M
   EMTANIS A A I
AE ORIDION MEDICAL 1987 LTD (ORID-Non-standard)
   ORIDION MEDICAL 1987 LTD (ORID-Non-standard)
GA 201858001D
AB    NOVELTY - The mask (100) has a nasal breath-sampling                element for reducing dilution of exhaled breath by                delivered oxygen. A breath sampling port (120)                receives breath samples, sampled by the                breath-sampling element. The nasal breath-sampling                element is molded on or attached to an internal                side of the oxygen mask. The oral sampling element                samples exhaled breath from a subject's mouth (104)                when in use. The nasal breath-sampling element                comprises two nasal prongs (110) fitted within the                mask to be positioned within or below a subject's                nostrils when in use. The nasal prongs are                connected to an adjustment handle that is located                at an outer side of the oxygen mask, where movement                of the adjustment handle adjusts a position of the                nasal prongs within the oxygen mask.
   USE - Capnoxygen mask for a capnoxygen system                (claimed) for performing carbon dioxide sampling                and oxygen delivery operations.
   ADVANTAGE - The mask provides a single integrated unit,                which is comfortable to wear than bulky                simultaneous wearing of an oxygen mask for oxygen                delivery and a stand-alone breath sampling cannula                for breath monitoring. The breath sampling cannula                is aligned with a patient's nose for reducing                dilution and increasing patient comfort when worn                in conjunction with oxygen masks.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for the                following:
   (1) a breath sampling cannula
   (2) a capnoxygen system.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a side perspective view of                a capnoxygen mask with nasal prongs.
   Capnoxygen mask (100)
   Subject's mouth (104)
   Nasal prongs (110)
   Breath sampling port (120)
   Nose-bridge (122)

PT P
PN CN108033522-A; CN108033522-B
TI Electrocatalytic coupling advanced oxidation system useful in the field of sewage treatment, comprises three-dimensional hexagonal star-shaped cobalt(II, III) oxide and nano-sheets of flower-shaped cupric oxide as anode and cathode material.
AU DONG W
   XING Q
   LIU S
   ZOU J
   CHEN Y
   ZHU M
   LUO S
   LI P
   YANG Y
   YANG J
AE UNIV NANCHANG HANGKONG (UYNH-C)
GA 201839864T
AB    NOVELTY - Electrocatalytic coupling advanced oxidation system comprises three-dimensional hexagonal star-shaped cobalt(II, III) oxide and nano-sheets of flower-shaped cupric oxide as anode and cathode materials.
   USE - The system is useful in the field of sewage treatment (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The system: has excellent catalytic effect, reaches removal rate of p-nitrophenol to over 98%, and yields of methanol and ethanol to 49.145 and 20.475 micromoles/liter/hour.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for
   (1) preparing the electrocatalytic coupling advanced oxidation system, comprising combining advanced oxidation techniques with electrocatalysis at the anode, activating cobalt(II, III) oxide electric anode to the persulfate for generating sulfate radicals and hydroxy radicals, and generating hydroxy radicals by electrocatalysis, and completely mineralizing the organic pollutants into carbon dioxide and water;
   (2) use of electrocatalytic coupling advanced oxidation system, comprising stacking three-dimensional hexagonal star-shaped cobalt(II, III) oxide and nano-sheets as flower-shaped cupric oxide as anode and cathode material, adding 0.1 mol/l sodium sulfate solution into the anode chamber, adding 0.1 mol/l potassium bicarbonate solution into the cathode compartment, adding 0.5 g/liter of persulfate and 10 mg/liter p-nitrophenol solution into the anolyte, performing electrolysis for electrolysis and advancing oxidation coupling reactions; and
   (3) use of electrocatalytic coupled advanced oxidation system in the field of sewage treatment, comprising removing organic pollutants from wastewater, mineralizing at the anode to obtain carbon dioxide, introducing into the cathode through the exhaust pipe, and reducing to hydrocarbons on the cathode.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method comprises introducing the carbon dioxide produced by the complete mineralization of organic pollutants at the anode into the cathode through the exhaust pipe, and reducing into a hydrocarbon on the cupric oxide cathode. The volume of the 0.1 mol/liter sodium sulfate solution added in the anode chamber and the 0.1 mol/liter potassium hydrogencarbonate solution added in the cathode chamber are both 60 ml. The synthesis method of three-dimensional hexagonal star-shaped cobalt(II, III) oxide material comprises dissolving 0.5-1 g cobalt nitrate in a 40 ml mixed solution of ethylene glycol and water, and stirring, completely dissolving the solution, adding 0.5-1.5 g urea stirring, adding 30-50 mg cetyltrimethylammonium bromide and 1-2 g ammonium fluoride, stirring at room temperature for 30-45 minutes to obtain uniform, transparent, and colorless solution, transferring the solution into a 100 ml polytetrafluoroethylene reactor lining, placing piece of cleaned conductive glass in a obliquely in the reaction kettle, where the conductive surface of the conductive glass faces up, transferring the reaction kettle into a drying oven for reacting at 100-120 degrees C, reacting on the conductive glass for 10-12 hours to obtain purple precursor, placing the conductive glass into the muffle furnace, calcining at 350-550 degrees C for 2-4 hours, and cooling to room temperature to obtain three-dimensional hexagonal star shaped cobalt(II, III) oxide. The synthesis method of nano-sheet stacked flower-shaped cupric oxide material comprises taking 5-6 g sodium hydroxide, sequentially dissolving the 1-1.2 g ammonium persulfate and 0.2-0.4 g sodium tungstate in a 30-40 ml deionized water, stirring at room temperature for 30-45 minutes to obtain uniform, and transparent colorless solution, adding 0.4-0.6 g sodium lauryl sulfate into the above mentioned solution to obtain uniform white solution, dissolving the sodium lauryl sulfate, transferring into a polytetrafluoroethylene liner stainless steel autoclave, immersing the cleaned copper mesh in the above mentioned solution, when the autoclave is closed, reacting at 100-150 degrees C for 24 hours, cooling to room temperature, taking out the copper net, and washing with deionized water to obtain black nanosheet-stacked flower-shaped cupric oxide.

TI System for performing combined power production            and hydrogen production, has flow components which are            configured for passage of streams between power            production unit and hydrogen production unit.
AU ALLAM R J
   RAFATI N
   NAVID R
AE 8 RIVERS CAPITAL LLC (ERIV-C)
   8 RIVERS CAPITAL LLC (ERIV-C)
GA 201835938E
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a power production unit which                is provided to expand pressurized carbon dioxide                for power production. The hydrogen production unit                is arranged, in which a hydrocarbon fuel is                partially oxidized to produce a synthesis gas from                which hydrogen is separated. The flow components                are configured for passage of streams between the                power production unit and the hydrogen production                unit. A combustor is configured to receive a                hydrocarbon fuel and oxygen and output a heated                stream comprising pressurized carbon dioxide.
   USE - System for performing combined power                production and hydrogen production.
   ADVANTAGE - The turbine inlet temperature and flow rate                can be increased to provide a significant                additional power output from the turbine. The steam                and hydrocarbon or carbonaceous fuel can be heated                to the typical temperature of about 400 degrees C                to about 550 degrees C, while the turbine exhaust                can be cooled to typical temperature of about 700                degrees C to about 800 degrees C before entering                the recuperative heat exchanger. The significant                quantity of low grade heat available from the                cooling stream is ideally suited to provide the low                temperature level heat that can be added to the                power production cycle to augment the recuperated                heat from the turbine exhaust and thus achieve a                high efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) a method for combined power production and                hydrogen production; and
   (2) a system for power production.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                structure of hydrogen production facility including                elements for integration with power production                system and process.
   Partial oxidation reactor (201)
   Heat exchanger (210)
   Crude hydrogen product stream (230)
   Water cooler (235)
   Saturated steam Stream (250)

PT P
PN CN107988482-A
TI Comprehensive utilization of fluorine and aluminum in fluorocarbon cerium ore involves subjecting fluorocarbon cerium ore to oxidation roasting, hydrochloric acid leaching, alkaline transfer, and hydrochloric acid leaching.
AU 
AE SICHUAN YINGCHUANG ENVIRONMENTAL TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SICH-Non-standard)
GA 2018386610
AB    NOVELTY - Comprehensive utilization of fluorine and aluminum in fluorocarbon cerium ore involves subjecting fluorocarbon cerium ore to oxidation roasting, hydrochloric acid leaching, alkaline transfer, and hydrochloric acid leaching to obtain value-adjusted slag; and subjecting bismuth cerium ore to acid cleaning to remove calcium, hydrochloric acid leaching, alkaline decomposition, neutralizing, and purifying to obtain conditioned slag.
   USE - Comprehensive utilization of fluorine and aluminum in fluorocarbon cerium ore.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves utilization ratio of fluorine element in raw ore, eliminates fluorine wastewater discharge, reduces carbon dioxide emission, and realizes comprehensive utilization of resources.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Comprehensive utilization of fluorine and aluminum in fluorocarbon cerium ore comprises subjecting fluorocarbon cerium ore to oxidation roasting, hydrochloric acid leaching, alkaline transfer, and hydrochloric acid leaching to obtain value-adjusted slag; and subjecting bismuth cerium ore to acid cleaning to remove calcium, hydrochloric acid leaching, alkaline decomposition, neutralizing, and purifying to obtain conditioned slag. It preferably involves dissolving the bauxite or aluminum-containing solid waste in sodium hydroxide solution to form sodium aluminate solution; adding value-adjusted slag to sodium hydroxide solution for alkali conversion to adjust slag alkali conversion wastewater; adding value-adjusted slag to sodium hydroxide solution for alkali conversion to obtain binary slag alkali transfer wastewater; mixing slag alkali converted wastewater and binary slag alkali transfer wastewater; further mixing with sodium aluminate solution; precipitating with carbon dioxide; washing precipitate; and drying to obtain aluminum fluoride product.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of a process for comprehensive utilization of fluorine and aluminum in fluorocarbon cerium ore (Drawing includes non-English language text).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The slag has aluminum content of 3-10% after fluorocarbon antimony ore is subjected to acid washing and calcium-hydrochloric acid leaching-alkali decomposition-neutralization and purification. The molar ratio of aluminum in sodium solution is 3:1. The slag alkali conversion wastewater has fluorine content of 10 g/l. The carbon dioxide precipitation has end point pH of 8-12. Preferred Method: The method preferably involves dissolving bauxite or aluminum-containing solid waste in sodium hydroxide solution to adjust alkaline residue concentration to 5-40 g/l. The alkali conversion is performed for 4-10 hours using 60-150 g/l sodium hydroxide solution, preferably for 6 hours using 90 g/l sodium hydroxide solution.
DC M25 (Production and refining of metals other than iron - including ore treatment, extraction, working up scrap, obtaining specific metals, control testing methods (C22B).)
MC M25-A02; M25-E; M25-G01; M25-G05; M25-G07; M25-G21

PN CN107881146-A
TI Separating and purifying myocardial cells, by            centrifuging heart tissue using myocardial buffer,            adding type II collagenase, culturing, adding to            density gradient solution and inoculating in plate            containing coating liquid.
AU DONG N
   LIU B
   LI F
   SHANG X
   SHI F
   QIU X
   SHI J
   CHEN S
   WU L
   LIU J
   LIU Y
   HU H
   HONG H
   LI H
   SUN Y
   QIAO W
   ZHANG C
   WANG Y
   LI G
   ZHU P
   LIU M
   DAI J
   BAI P
   JIANG Y
AE HUST TONGJI MEDICAL COLLEGE UNION HOSPIT (UYHZ-C)
GA 201827987A
AB    NOVELTY - Separating and purifying myocardial cells, by                adding coating liquid to six-well plate,                incubating, adding myocardial buffer solution to                Petri dish, adding cut heart tissue in a centrifuge                tube containing a myocardial buffer, adding type II                collagenase, culturing, filtering, centrifuging,                resuspending in myocardial buffer, slowly adding                the cell suspension to density gradient solution,                centrifuging, centrifuging middle layer,                resuspending in 10% streptomycin free fetal bovine                serum solution, inoculating in six-well plate and                incubating in 5% carbon dioxide incubator.
   USE - The method is used for separating and                purifying myocardial cells.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for separating and purifying myocardial                cells, involves adding coating liquid to six-well                plate, incubating at 37 degrees C, adding                myocardial buffer solution to Petri dish, placing                heart in the Petri dish, adding the cut heart                tissue in a centrifuge tube containing a myocardial                buffer, adding 0.05% type II collagenase to a                centrifuge tube containing heart tissue, culturing                in shaker, removing supernatant, adding 0.1% type                II collagenase, culturing in shaker, recovering                supernatant, mixing in a centrifuge tube with                serum, filtering, centrifuging, resuspending in                myocardial buffer, adding the cell suspension to a                centrifuge tube, slowly adding the cell suspension                to density gradient solution, centrifuging,                aspirating top layer, then middle layer, taking the                middle layer in a centrifuge tube, centrifuging,                resuspending in 10% streptomycin free fetal bovine                serum solution, inoculating in six-well plate and                incubating in 5% carbon dioxide incubator at 37                degrees C.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Components: The coating liquid is                  one of laminin coating liquid, polylysine coating                  liquid and gelatin coating liquid. The myocardial                  buffer solution comprises 7.012-8.007 pts. wt.                  sodium chloride, 0.395-0.403 pt. wt. potassium                  chloride, 0.123-0.222 pt. wt. magnesium sulfate                  heptahydrate, 0.973-1.046 pt. wt. glucose,                  0.06-0.077 pt. wt. potassium dihydrogen                  phosphate, 0.042-0.071 pt. wt. disodium hydrogen                  phosphate, 4.766-5.958 pts. wt.                  4-hydroxyethylpiperazine ethane sulfonic acid,                  ultrapure water and sodium hydroxide and has pH                  of 7.5. The density gradient solution comprises a                  high density liquid and a low density liquid in                  volume ratio of 4:5. The high density liquid                  comprises ultrapure water, Percoll solution and                  buffer in volume ratio of 1:3:1. The low density                  liquid comprises ultrapure water, Percoll liquid                  and buffer solution in volume ratio of 2:2:1. The                  buffer solution comprises 35.064-40.032 pts. wt.                  sodium chloride, 1.975-2.013 pts. wt. potassium                  chloride, 0.615-1.107 pts. wt. magnesium sulfate                  heptahydrate, 4.865-5.226 pts. wt. glucose,                  0.301-0.384 pt. wt. potassium dihydrogen                  phosphate, 0.213-0.365 pt. wt. disodium hydrogen                  phosphate and 23.83-29.788 pts. wt.                  4-hydroxyethylpiperazine ethanesulfonic acid.                  Preferred Method: The method further involves                  culturing purified cardiomyocytes in a cell                  culture medium, preferably 10% fetal calf                  serum.

PN US2018085548-A1
TI Handheld air scrubber device for removing e.g. carbon dioxide from air, has alignment unit aligning and positioning air treatment device within inhalation channel, and sealing unit sealing air outlet valve against outlet valve stop.
AU COX S
   WOLF G C
AE COX S (COXS-Individual)
   WOLF G C (WOLF-Individual)
GA 2018245401
AB    NOVELTY - The device (100) has an air outlet valve opened upon exhalation to direct air flow entering a housing through a ventilation port (5) to exit through an exhaust port and closed upon inhalation through the ventilation port into the housing to inhibit introduction of air into the housing from the exhaust port. An alignment unit aligns and positions an air treatment device within an inhalation channel. A first sealing unit seals an inlet valve against an inlet valve stop. A second sealing unit seals the outlet valve against an outlet valve stop.
   USE - Handheld air scrubber device for removing undesired constituents e.g. carbon dioxide, carbon monoxide, water, nitrous oxide, sulfur oxide, volatile organic compounds, acids, bases and chemical and biological toxins (all claimed), from air.
   ADVANTAGE - The device prevents the exhaled air from entering the air treatment chamber and traversing the adsorbent cartridge so as to avoid premature depletion of an adsorbent by unnecessary exposure to the constituents of the exhaled air. The device optimizes a thickness of the adsorbent cartridge to ensure a tight fit within the air treatment chamber so as to ensure sufficient air flow through the adsorbent cartridge rather than around the adsorbent cartridge. The device maintains absorbent sheets properly aligned to maintain filter ventilation channels open and unobstructed and to minimize a pressure differential across the cartridge.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a perspective view of a handheld air scrubber.
   Ventilation port (5)
   Lateral casings (40, 45)
   Housing base (43)
   Casing joint (47)
   Handheld air scrubber device (100)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - The device utilizes an adsorbent selected from a group consisting of lithium hydroxide and calcium hydroxide.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - The housing and a removable filter cartridge housing are made of polypropylene and thermoplastic elastomer.

AB    NOVELTY - The method involves joining the first and                second sectors by barrier by forming a surface                equal to each cell. The                distribution of gases at the same time in input and                recovery of produced gases is improved.

PN CN107828582-A
TI Preparation of radix codonopsitis lanceolatea sparkling wine used for, e.g. tonifying stomach by crushing, e.g. radix codonopsitis lanceolatea and medlar, boiling with ethanol, filtering, fermenting twice, stirring, and refrigerating.
AU WANG S
AE NANLING RADIX POLYGONATI OFFICINALIS (NANL-Non-standard)
GA 201824325U
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of radix codonopsitis lanceolatea sparkling wine includes crushing radix codonopsitis lanceolatea, Salvia miltiorrhiza, prepared rhizome of rehmannia, medlar and Gynura, sieving, soaking in ethanol, boiling, filtering, and collecting medicine filtrate; adding domesticated wine yeast, fermenting, adding sulfur dioxide to stop fermentation, adding to sealed fermentation device, adding sucrose and xylitol, fermenting, stirring, refrigerating, adding soya gel, diatomite filtering, and performing cold stabilization treatment; and cooling and injecting carbon dioxide.
   USE - Method for preparation of radix codonopsitis lanceolatea sparkling wine used for tonifying stomach and promoting fluid, regulating blood fat, promoting memory, and relieving fatigue.
   ADVANTAGE - The sparkling wine is tender and delicious, and has rich red color and nutrient contents, mellow and sweet taste, and easy to be absorbed nutritious components.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of radix codonopsitis lanceolatea sparkling wine comprises (A) crushing radix codonopsitis lanceolatea, Salvia miltiorrhiza, prepared rhizome of rehmannia, medlar and Gynura, sieving through 60 mesh sieve, adding to boiling container, adding 1-2 times its weight 95% ethanol, soaking for 50 minutes, boiling for 1.5 hours, filtering, and collecting medicine filtrate; (B) adding 310-350 ppm domesticated wine yeast, fermenting, adding 65 ppm sulfur dioxide to stop fermentation when residual sugar content of fermentation broth is 48-55 g/L, adding 100% fermentation broth to sealed fermentation device, adding 25 g/L sucrose and 5.5 wt.% xylitol, fermenting, stirring, refrigerating at 8-12 degrees C for 2-4 months, adding 450 ppm soya gel, diatomite filtering, and performing cold stabilization treatment; and (C) cooling to - 7 degrees C, injecting carbon dioxide, and subjecting to pressurized aseptic filling.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The fermentation is done at 8-12 degrees C for 18-28 days.
   ACTIVITY - Gastrointestinal-Gen.; Antilipemic; Nootropic; CNS-Gen.; Muscular-Gen.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. General compositions.); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).)

PN CN107624861-A
TI Thawing frozen meat by allowing frozen meat to stand under static condition, spraying calcium oxide, stewing soaked frozen meat, adjusting pH, ultrasonically vibrating, adding e.g. ascorbic acid, electrifying, and removing thawed meat.
AU YINFU X
   FANG L
   LIU Q
AE YINFU X (YINF-Individual)
GA 201810256M
AB    NOVELTY - Method for thawing frozen meat involves (a) allowing the frozen meat to stand under static condition, and spraying calcium oxide on the frozen meat, (b) stewing the frozen meat after soaking in soaking solution, adjusting pH to neutral, then performing ultrasonic vibration, and collecting filtrate, (c) placing the pre-frozen meat in a container containing ammonium nitrate solution, then adding ascorbic acid, and filling carbon dioxide into the container, and (d) electrifying the mixture, increasing temperature, and then removing thawed meat.
   USE - The method is useful for thawing frozen meat.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to form antibacterial film on the surface of thawed meat which is rich in nutrients.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for thawing frozen meat involves (a) allowing the frozen meat to stand under static condition at 0-2 degrees C for 1-2 hours, uniformly spraying calcium oxide on the frozen meat, and continuously allowing to stand under static condition for 30-40 minutes, (b) stewing the frozen meat after soaking in soaking solution, adjusting the pH to neutral using hydrochloric acid, then performing ultrasonic vibration, filtering, collecting filtrate, and obtaining pre-thawed frozen meat, (c) placing the pre-frozen meat in a container containing ammonium nitrate solution, allowing to stand under static condition at 2-4 degrees C for 40-50 minutes, then adding ascorbic acid, and filling carbon dioxide into the container, and (d) electrifying the mixture in the container, then naturally increasing the temperature to 6-9 degrees C, allowing to stand under static condition, and then removing thawed meat.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The step (a) involves uniformly spraying 60-80 g/kg calcium oxide on the frozen meat. The soaking solution is prepared by mixing grinding cactus in mill at a speed of 800 revolutions/minute (rpm), adding ethanol solution, where ground cactus are ethanol solution in a mass ratio of 1:3, collecting ground material, subjecting the ground materials to supercritical carbon dioxide extraction, filtering and collecting extract, distilling extract, recovering ethanol, and collecting distilled residue. The mass ratio of pre-thawed frozen meat and ammonium nitrate solution in step (c) is 1:2-4. The step (a) involves adding 3-5 %mass ascorbic acid.
DC D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).)
MC D03-A01
IP A23B-004/07

PN CN107555461-A
TI Preparation of plastic nano-calcium carbonate involves calcining limestone into calcium oxide, placing into slurry container, adding sodium bicarbonate and fatty acid amide, aging, adding dispersant, stirring, drying, crushing and sieving.
AU LI W
   ZHANG C
   OU X
AE GUANGXI HUAYANG MINERAL RESOURCES CO LTD (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 2018048253
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of plastic nano-calcium carbonate involves calcining limestone into calcium oxide through calcination apparatus, placing calcium oxide into slurry container to obtain suspension with 12-15% of calcium hydroxide, adding sodium bicarbonate and fatty acid amide into suspension, and aging for 1-2 hours; adding calcium carbonate slurry with 0.1-1 wt.% dispersant, stirring and settling for 1-2 hours to obtain dispersed nano calcium carbonate slurry; and filter pressing dispersed nano calcium carbonate slurry, drying, crushing and sieving to obtain product.
   USE - Method for preparing plastic nano-calcium carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has improved plastic processability and compatibility, reinforcing and toughening effect, and high dispersion and high extrusion effect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of plastic nano-calcium carbonate comprises calcining limestone into calcium oxide through calcination apparatus, placing calcium oxide into slurry container to obtain suspension with 12-15% of calcium hydroxide, adding sodium bicarbonate and fatty acid amide into suspension, and aging for 1-2 hours; adding suspension of calcium bicarbonate into stearic acid as co-active agent, placing mixture into reaction kettle, mixing carbon dioxide and nitrogen and passing into reaction kettle, carrying out carbonization to adjust slurry pH into 10-11, adding sodium bicarbonate, and re-opening mixed gas of carbon dioxide and nitrogen to adjust slurry pH into 6.5-7 to obtain nano-calcium carbonate slurry; adding calcium carbonate slurry with 0.1-1 wt.% dispersant, stirring and settling for 1-2 hours to obtain dispersed nano calcium carbonate slurry; and filter pressing dispersed nano calcium carbonate slurry, drying, crushing and sieving to obtain product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The dispersant comprises 1-7 pts. wt. adipic acid, 3-8 pts. wt. sodium dodecyl benzene sulfonate, 2-9 pts. wt. hydroxyethyl methacrylate and 15-20 pts. wt. polyethylene glycol. Preferred Method: The preparation of plastic nano-calcium carbonate comprises aging at 50-60 degrees C.

PN CN115156240-A
TI Method for realizing resource utilization of            desulfurization ash used for e.g. coal-fired power            generation, involves performing solid-liquid separation            on sulfuric acid slurry to obtain solid calcium            carbonate and ammonium sulfate solution.
AU PAN M
   YU K
   CHEN X
   FENG B
   CHEN Z
AE GUANGDONG TONGCHUANG KEXIN ENVIRONMENTAL (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 2022D0120G
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves mixing desulfurized ash                and water after wet sieving to obtain first                desulfured ash slurry. Oxidant is added to the                first desulfured ash slurry. Stirring and reacting                process is performed obtain a second desulfuralized                ash silt. Excess ammonium carbonate is added to the                second sulfurified ash silurry. A mixture of oxygen                and carbon dioxide is introduced, stirred and                reacted until a pH value of the reaction system is                reduced to 7-8 to obtain third desulfurate ash                sillurry. Ammonium and carbon dioxide generated by                decomposition of ammonial carbonate in a reaction                process is collected and recycled in the reactor.                Solid-liquid separation is performed on sulfuric                acid slurry to obtain solid calcium carbonate and                ammonium sulfate solution.
   USE - Method for realizing resource utilization of                desulfurization ash used for coal-fired power                generation, steel sintering, industrial boiler,                petrochemical industry, flue gas desulfurization                process.
   ADVANTAGE - The desulfurization ash resource utilization                method overcomes the defect that the recovery                efficiency of the desulfurized ash in the prior art                is not high, or the purity of the product finally                generated. The conversion rate of calcium and                sulphur element in the ash can reach more than 97%                and the high-efficiency recycling resource                utilization of the ash is realized.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow diagram illustrating                a method for realizing resource utilization of                desulfurization ash used for coal-fired power                generation, steel sintering, industrial boiler,                petrochemical industry and flue gas desulfurization                process. GA 2022815280
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a main housing cooperatively                engaging with an exhalation valve of an oxygen                mask. An optical transmitter housing is disposed on                the main housing, and houses an optical transmitter                element e.g. tunable laser. The transmitter element                is optically aligned with an optical receiver                element such that a signal path between the                transmitter element and the receiver element                transects an opening in the housing such that                exhaled air from a user of the mask passes through                the signal path as the air is pushed through the                valve. A temperature sensor measures temperature of                the system.
   USE - In-situ sensor system for measuring oxygen,                carbon dioxide and water vapor levels of a user                i.e. pilot, in an aircraft.
   ADVANTAGE - The mask systems with embedded sensors can                accurately measure, in real time, the amount of CO2                and/or water vapor that is being produced by the                pilots. This measurement can serve as a benchmark                in determining if a pilot is getting close having a                physiological event while piloting an aircraft. The                masks can serve to provide pilots with the proper                oxygen mixture in order to prevent the hypoxia that                can result from various maneuvers. Hypoxia can pose                a dangerous threat to pilots because it can cause                physiological episodes often associated with                syncope and conditions that may result in                catastrophic events. Despite the long use of masks                by pilots, there continue to be issues with                physiological events with unknown causes.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a pilot                oxygen mask system comprising: an in-situ sensor                system.

AU ZHAO C
AE ANHUI SHANGKA TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 2022638214
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing gas diffusion electrode involves coating conductive polymer dispersion liquid on the surface of the current collecting layer raw material, and drying to obtain a current collecting layer. A hydrophobic and breathable layer raw material is coated on one side of the current collecting layer, and dried to obtain a hydrophobic and breathable layer. A catalyst layer raw material is coated on the other side of the flow collecting layer, and dried to obtain a catalyst layer. The composite layer material consisting of the hydrophobic breathable layer, the current collecting layer and the catalytic layer is subjected to hot pressing treatment at 100-150degrees Celsius, sintered at 200-400degrees Celsius for 1-3 hours (h) to obtain the gas diffusion electrode.
   USE - Method for preparing gas diffusion electrode used in metal-air battery and for electrochemical oxygen producing process (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves the interlayer sealing degree of the gas diffusion electrode, to enhance the stability of the electrode in the strong corrosive environment and high-current density operation.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for an electrochemical oxygen producing process, which involves using water and carbon dioxide as reaction material and performing electrolysis on the anode to produce oxygen, while carbon dioxide undergoes an electrochemical reaction under the action of a cathode catalyst, where cathode is the gas diffusion electrode.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The conductive polymer in the conductive polymer dispersion liquid comprises at least one of polyaniline, polypyrrole and polyacetylene. The carbon material comprises at least one of conductive carbon black, carbon nanotubes, and acetylene black. The solvent comprises at least one of water, absolute ethyl alcohol, n-propanol and isopropanol. The hydrophobic polymer comprises at least one of polytetrafluoroethylene, polyvinylidene fluoride, polytrifluoroethylene and tetrafluoroethylene-perfluoroalkoxy vinyl ether copolymer.Preferred Conditions: The mass of the conductive polymer loaded on the current collecting layer is 1-2.5 mg/cm2. The carbon material is crushed, sieved and uniformly mixed with a solvent to obtain a mixed solution. The mixed solution is pulped to obtain carbon material slurry with the particle size of less than 15 micrometers. The hydrophobic polymer and a pore-forming agent are added into the carbon material slurry, pulped for 2-5 hours at 12000-20000 rpm to obtain slurry with the particle size of less than 15 microm. The slurry is baked at 50-90degrees Celsius to form paste which forms the hydrophobic breathable layer raw material. The catalyst and a carbon material are crushed, sieved and uniformly mixed with a solvent to obtain a first solution. The first solution is pulped to obtain mixed slurry with the particle size of less than 15 microm. The hydrophobic polymer and a pore-forming agent are added into the mixed slurry, pulped for 2-5 hours at 12000-20000 rpm to obtain catalyst layer slurry with the particle size of less than 15 microm. The catalyst layer slurry is baked at 50-90degrees Celsius to form paste to obtain the catalyst layer raw material.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The material of the current collecting layer is a metal wire mesh or foam metal. The pore-forming agent comprises at least one of sodium sulfate, ammonium oxalate, ammonium bicarbonate and lithium carbonate. The catalyst comprises a manganese dioxide catalyst.

The species from the genus Bacillus comprises Bacillus subtilis.

TI Foam material used in vibration plate for            loudspeaker, formed by foaming and curing main resin            comprising cyanate ester resin, chemical foaming agent            and curing agent, and chemical foaming agent undergoes            chemical reaction when heated to generate nitrogen and            carbon dioxide to enter foaming state.

   LING F
   LI M
   XIE B
AE GOERTEK INC (QDGR-C)
GA 2021C4467U
AB    NOVELTY - Foam material is formed by foaming and curing                raw materials comprising main resin, chemical                foaming agent and curing agent, where the main                resin comprises cyanate ester resin and/or                bismaleimide resin, and the chemical foaming agent                undergoes a chemical reaction when heated to                generate nitrogen and/or carbon dioxide so that the                raw material enters a foaming state.
   USE - The foam material is useful in vibration plate                for loudspeaker (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The foam material has excellent adhesion                property, low density, high modulus, low water                absorption rate and excellent heat resistance, and                is suitable to replace traditional                polymethacrylimide foam material. The vibrating                plate has high strength, high-frequency performance                and excellent reliability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following: (1) a vibration plate comprising foam                material layer, where the foam material layer                adopts the foam material; (2) a loudspeaker                comprising a vibrating diaphragm and a vibrating                plate for connecting and supporting the vibrating                diaphragm, where the vibrating plate is the                above-mentioned vibrating plate, and the vibrating                plate and the sound-producing vibrating diaphragm                are bonded by glue film or glue; and (3) a                preparation method of the foam material, involving                mixing the raw materials, performing a first stage                of heating to perform a foaming reaction,                proceeding to a second stage of heating to perform                curing reaction, continuing to a third stage of                heating, heat-preserving under stable temperature,                and cooling.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The chemical foaming                  agent comprises N-nitroso compound, azo compound,                  hydrazide compound and/or carbonate compound,                  preferably azobisisobutyronitrile, where the                  total weight of chemical foaming agent is 5-40                  wt.%, preferably 12-20 wt.%. The curing agent                  comprises epoxy resin, where the amount of the                  curing agent is 10-35 wt.%. The raw material of                  the foam material further comprises a catalyst, a                  accelerator and/or hollow glass microspheres. The                  catalyst comprises cobalt acetylacetonate, where                  the amount of the catalyst is 1-10 wt.%. The                  accelerator comprises nonylphenol, where the                  amount of the accelerator is 1-5 wt.%.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Properties: The foam material has                  a density of 0.35-0.8 g/cm3 and a modulus of 8-40                  GPa. The thickness of the vibration plate is                  0.04-0.25 mm.Preferred Components: The vibrating plate                  further comprises at least one substrate layer,                  where the substrate layer is arranged on the                  outer surface of the foam material layer. The                  substrate layer adopts aluminum foil, engineering                  plastic, carbon fiber and/or aramid fiber                  composite material.Preferred Conditions: The end temperature of                  the first stage of heating is 140-150degrees                  Celsius. The end temperature of the second stage                  heating is 150-180degrees Celsius. The end                  temperature of the third stage heating is                  180-200degrees Celsius. The heating rate of the                  first stage heating is 0.8-1degrees                  Celsius/minute. The heating rate of the second                  stage heating is 2-3degrees Celsius/minute. The                  heat preservation time after the temperature rise                  in the third stage is 2-3 hours. TI Grain storage mildew in-situ lossless online real-time detection method for detecting grain storage in grain storage stack using electronic nose technology and carbon dioxide concentration detection technology, involves establishing grain stored mildew information real time sensing model.
AU CHEN X
   XIE T
   ZHAO X
   YU C
   TIAN H
AE UNIV BEIJING TECHNOLOGY & BUSINESS (UYTB-C)
GA 2021C26352
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves designing a probe installed in a grain stack. Dielectric constant information of grain around the probe is measured in situ in a real time manner. Impedance conversion processing is performed to dielectrical constant feature information. A multi-information parameter is analyzed for coupling mildew gas concentration parameter information on the probe based on main component analysis. Main characteristic is extracted for constructing a multivariate variable model and outputting a main parameter variable of gas concentration according to obtained mildew air concentration parameters information. A grain stored mildew information real time sensing model is established.
   USE - Grain storage mildew in-situ lossless online real-time detection method for detecting grain storage in a grain storage stack using an electronic nose technology and carbon dioxide concentration detection technology.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables obtaining grain stack internal mildew information, accurately detecting and tracking grain mildew condition, which can avoid error caused by mildew measurement based on grain grain on grain stack surface, thus improving precision of grain storage mildew Information measurement. The method allows a microprocessor to perform impedance conversion processing to the dielectric feature information to obtain the gas concentration parameter information of surrounding mildew grain around the probe so as to obtain gas concentration main parameter variable of gas concentration at the probe.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a grain storage mildew in-situ lossless online real-time detection device.

PN CN113372470-A; CN113372470-B
TI Preparing hydrolysis-resistant polyvinyl alcohol            polymer useful for preparing hydrolysis resistant            polyvinyl alcohol fiber, comprises e.g. copolymerizing            dodecyl vinyl ether, vinyl formate and vinyl acetate            and carrying out alcoholysis.
AU LI W
   WANG Y
   HE J
   ZOU L
   SUN W
   SHANG S
   SHENG J
   DU Y
AE UNIV DONGHUA (UYDG-C)
GA 2021A7999M
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing hydrolysis-resistant polyvinyl                alcohol (PVA) polymer comprises copolymerizing                dodecyl vinyl ether, vinyl formate and vinyl                acetate as monomer to prepare polyvinyl alcohol                prepolymer and carrying out alcoholysis of the                polyvinyl alcohol prepolymer. The water content of                the hydrolysis-resistant polyvinyl alcohol polymer                is 0.5-1.5 wt.%.
   USE - The hydrolysis-resistant polyvinyl alcohol                polymer is useful for preparing hydrolysis                resistant polyvinyl alcohol fiber (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: prepares polyvinyl alcohol (PVA)                polymer and PVA fiber with good hydrolysis                resistance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                application of hydrolysis-resistant PVA polymer to                prepare hydrolysis resistant polyvinyl alcohol                (PVA) fiber, comprising (a) formulating the                hydrolysis-resistant PVA polymer into a high solid                content spinning solution, spinning the high solid                content spinning solution through a dry and wet                method to obtain a hydrolysis resistant PVA fiber                and the solid content of the high solid content                spinning solution is 28-32 wt.% and preparing                hydrolysis-resistant PVA polymer into a spinning                solution with high solid content comprises adding                the hydrolysis-resistant PVA polymer into the                dissolving kettle containing dimethylformamide and                water, heating up in stages to swell, passing                carbon dioxide gas into the dissolving kettle to                form a certain pressure until the                hydrolysis-resistant PVA polymer is completely                dissolved, and discharging carbon dioxide gas at a                high speed to obtain spinning solution with high                solid content and the softening coefficient of the                hydrolysis-resistant PVA fiber is 0.85-0.95.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The mol ratio of                  dodecyl vinyl ether, vinyl formate and vinyl                  acetate is 3-5:2-3:2-4. The mass ratio of                  dimethylformamide to water in the preparation of                  hydrolysis resistant polyvinyl alcohol (PVA)                  fiber is 7-8:2-3.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The polymerization                  degree of the hydrolysis-resistant PVA polymer is                  3200-3600, and the alcoholysis degree is 98-99%.                  The size of the hydrolysis-resistant PVA fiber is                  16-22 dtex, the breaking strength is 12.3-13.4                  cN/dtex, the elastic modulus is 310-322cN/dtex                  the elongation at break is 1.8-3.6%. Preferred                  Method: The specific preparation process of the                  polyvinyl alcohol pre-polymer comprises taking                  dodecyl vinyl ether, vinyl formate and vinyl                  acetate as monomer, taking water as solvent,                  p-toluenesulfonic acid as catalyst, adding the                  monomer, solvent and catalyst into the reactor                  until the monomer and catalyst are dissolved, and                  reacting for 4-5 hours under a nitrogen                  atmosphere at 30-35&#176; C to obtain                  polyvinyl alcohol (PVA) prepolymer, the added                  amount of p-toluenesulfonic acid is 2.5-3.5 wt.%                  of the total mass of dodecyl vinyl ether, vinyl                  formate and vinyl acetate and at mass ratio of                  dodecyl vinyl ether, vinyl formate and vinyl                  acetate to water is 1:8-10 and the specific                  process of preparing hydrolysis-resistant                  polyvinyl alcohol (PVA) comprises adding                  100-120g/liter sodium hydroxide/methanol into the                  PVA prepolymer/methanol solution for alcoholysis,                  rotating, evaporating and filtering process and                  drying for 8-10 hours at 50-55&#176; C to                  obtain hydrolysis-resistant PVA polymer, where                  the adding rate of the sodium hydroxide/methanol                  solution is 1-2 liter/min and the alcoholysis                  temperature is 40-45&#176; C and alcoholysis                  time is 10-20 minutes. The heating in stages in                  the preparation of hydrolysis resistant polyvinyl                  alcohol (PVA) fiber comprises heating firstly to                  35-40&#176; C from room temperature and                  maintaining for 10-20 minutes, heating to                  45-50&#176; C and maintaining for 20-30                  minutes. The pressure formed by introducing                  carbondioxide gas into the dissolution kettle in                  the preparation of hydrolysis resistant polyvinyl                  alcohol (PVA) fiber is 1.2-1.6 MPa, where the                  hydrolysis- resistant PVA polymer is completely                  dissolved and placing the dissolving kettle                  stably for 50-80 minutes at 50-60&#176; C                  before discharging carbon dioxide gas and the                  carbon dioxide gas discharging speed is 15-20                  liter/min. The technical flow of dry-wet spinning                  in the preparation of hydrolysis resistant                  polyvinyl alcohol (PVA) fiber comprises extruding                  PVA high solid content spinning liquid by                  spinneret hole to form spinning fine flow,                  passing spinning fine flow into coagulation bath                  through air layer, primary stretching, three                  times extraction and carrying out three times hot                  stretching, the technical parameters of the                  dry-wet spinning are as follows spinneret hole                  aperture is 0.45-0.55 mm, spinneret hole                  extrusion rate is 4.5-4.8 ml/min, the air layer                  height is 10-12 cm, the coagulating bath                  temperature is -6 to -3&#176; C, the                  solidifying bath solidifying time is 1.5-2                  minutes, the times of the first stretching is                  2.6-3 times, the temperature of the first                  extraction is 68-70&#176; C and the time is                  6-8 hours, the temperature of the second                  extraction is 72-75&#176; C and the time is                  8-10 hours, the temperature of the third                  extraction is 75-80&#176; C and the time is                  10-12 hours, the temperature of the first heat                  stretching is 185-195&#176; C, the stretching                  times is 2-2.5 times, the temperature of the                  second heat stretching is 195-205&#176; C and                  the stretching times is 2.5-3 times and the                  temperature of the third heat stretching is                  205-210&#176; C and the stretching times is                  3-3.5 times.

TI Catalyst useful for oxidizing low-carbon alkane to olefins by carbon dioxide, comprises cerium-zirconium solid solution as carrier, and loads cerium and auxiliary agent on carrier.
AU JING F
   LI Y
   LUO S
AE UNIV SICHUAN (USCU-C)
GA 202178486Y
AB    NOVELTY - Catalyst comprises cerium-zirconium solid solution as a carrier, and loads 8-15% cerium and less than 10% auxiliary agent on the carrier. The auxiliary agent is lithium, sodium, potassium, magnesium, calcium, and/or strontium.
   USE - The catalyst is useful for oxidizing low-carbon alkane to olefins by carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst adopts a specific cerium-zirconium solid solution as a carrier and introduces an auxiliary agent, which obviously improves the selectivity of the target product olefin and the ability to resist carbon deposition, has good resistance to carbon deposition, and improved catalytic stability. The method is simple and economical, and has good industrial application prospects.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Catalyst comprises cerium-zirconium solid solution as a carrier, and load 8-15% cerium and less than 10% auxiliary agent on the carrier. The molecular formula of the CeZr solid solution is CexZr(1-x)O2, and the auxiliary agent is lithium, sodium, potassium, magnesium, calcium, and/or strontium.
   x = 0.1-0.9.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for: a method for preparing the catalyst, comprising mixing cerium salt, zirconium salt, urea and water, refluxing for 20-30 hours, separating solid and liquid, taking the precipitate, washing, drying, and roasting at 400-600 degrees C to obtain cerium-zirconium solid solution; (ii) mixing chromium salt, auxiliary salt and water to form an impregnation solution, impregnating cerium-zirconium solid solution in the impregnation solution, removing, drying, and roasting at 500-700 degrees C.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The content of cerium is 9-11%, preferably 10% and the content of auxiliary agent is 0.6-10%, preferably 5%. The low-carbon alkane is ethane, propane or butane. The value of x is 0.3-0.7, preferably 0.7. The cerium salt is cerium nitrate. The zirconium salt is zirconium nitrate. The chromium salt is chromium nitrate. The lithium salt is lithium nitrate. The sodium salt is sodium nitrate. The potassium salt is potassium nitrate. The magnesium salt is magnesium nitrate. The calcium salt is calcium nitrate. The strontium salt is strontium nitrate. Preferred Method: The carrier is prepared by a uniform precipitation method, and cerium and auxiliary agent are loaded on the carrier by the incipient wetness dipping method. In the step (i), the reflux reaction is carried out for 24 hours; and roasted at 500 degrees C. In the step (ii), the roasting is carried out at 600 degrees C.

PN CN112520694-A
TI Biomass fuel gas purification treatment process by roasting biomass, removing moisture contained in biomass and heating drying equipment, superimposing dried biomass body, implementing tar removal treating on biomass, and gasifying.
AU DONG Y
   DENG X
   JIANG J
   YING H
   DAI W
   GONG J
   SUN K
   XU J
   XU Y
   SUN Y
AE DONGGUAN BAIDA NEW ENERGY CO LTD (DONG-Non-standard)
GA 202131494P
AB    NOVELTY - Biomass fuel gas purification treatment process involves (a) roasting the biomass, removing the moisture contained in the biomass and heating the drying equipment to dry the biomass and retaining the biomass itself, (b) superimposing the dried biomass body and reorganizing by unit of a pressing mechanism, and centrally storing the superimposed biomass by unit of a storage box, (c) implementing tar removal treating on the stored biomass, and (d) gasifying the biomass in a gasifier to obtain hydrogen, and using the synthesis gas for catalytic conversion, converting the carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide in the synthesis into hydrogen through the water-gas shift reaction, purifying and separating to obtain high-purity hydrogen.
   USE - As biomass fuel gas purification treatment process.
   ADVANTAGE - The process realizes a distributed gas, heat and power cogeneration system, which is an important component of the future clean energy supply, realizes regional gas and heating supply, and achieves the reduction of enterprise energy consumption and energy costs, pollutant emissions and carbon dioxide emissions, alleviates the conflict between regional energy supply and demand, and reduces the tar content of biomass gas through the development of tar catalytic conversion catalysts, converts the large molecules of hydrocarbons in tar into useful gases such as small molecules of hydrocarbons, reduces the generation of tar from the source, and improves the efficiency of gasification and the calorific value of gasification products to achieve great purification effects.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The process involves: placing catalytic conversion of the synthesis gas in the gasification furnace for synchronous operation, and the same proportion of synthesis gas and the gasified biomass in the gasification furnace are input for mixed catalytic treatment; dedusting and decoking the hydrogen gas produced by the gasification of biomass, and modulating the biomass in a gas modulator, and finally using purified equipment to produce hydrogen gas that meets the requirements of hydrogen combustion batteries; testing the hydrogen for hydrogen combustion quality through device debugging and cold and hot state operation detection. The water-gas shift reaction involves subjecting carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide to hydrogen production treatment by a water-gas reactor, and adding a catalyst to eliminate excess carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide. Preferred Components: The hydrogen obtained by the separator is purified, and the purity of the hydrogen is not greater than 99%. The catalyst is specifically a ceria-based water shift catalyst. Preferred Conditions: The processing time of the biomass gasification in the gasification furnace is 60 minutes, and the same amount of biomass body is put into the gasification furnace every 3 minutes.

Combustion improvement additives for coal, peat and other nonhydrocarbon based fuels are included in this Section together with coal liquefaction and desulphurisation.); H06 (Gaseous and liquid fuels - including pollution control. PN CN112410566-A
TI Processing zinc-containing dust by pre-reducing microwave sintering comprises e.g. mixing zinc-containing dust and coal powder, adding flux into ingredients, mixing and pelletizing materials to obtain internal carbon pellets.
AU HU B
   YE H
   LIU C
   XIE Z
AE ZHONGYE CHANGTIAN INT ENG CO LTD (CMEG-C)
GA 202127493S
AB    NOVELTY - Processing zinc-containing dust by pre-reducing microwave sintering comprises (i) mixing zinc-containing dust and coal powder into the ingredients, adding flux into the ingredients, mixing and pelletizing the prepared materials to obtain internal carbon pellets; (ii) passing the internal carbon pellets into the microwave heating zone (i), and pre-reducing by microwave heating to vaporize the zinc in the internal carbon pellets; (iii) passing the internal carbon pellets after pre-reduction dezincification into the roasting zone to undergo roasting and crystallization, passing through the slow cooling zone and the cooling zone to cool to obtain the blast furnace raw material; and (iv) passing the carbon dioxide produced in the roasting process through the microwave heating zone, reacting the carbon dioxide with carbon in the internal carbon pellets to generate carbon monoxide and discharging the zinc vaporized together with the carbon monoxide tail gas.
   USE - The method is useful for processing zinc-containing dust by pre-reducing microwave sintering.
   ADVANTAGE - The method combines the pre-reduction sintering technology with the microwave heating process, realizes separation of zinc, sodium, potassium and other elements and iron, at the same time, generates high purity carbon monoxide gas, and obtains the high quality of blast furnace raw material.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for device.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows the schematic representation of flow chart of processing zinc-containing dust by pre-reducing microwave sintering. (Drawing includes non-English language text).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method of processing zinc-containing dust by pre-reducing microwave sintering further comprises (v) recovering the exhausted tail gas by zinc to obtain a high carbon monoxide tail gas; and (vi) sending part of the high carbon monoxide tail gas into the reburning chamber for combustion, combining part with carbon monoxide/hydrogen to form coal gas, and sending into the roasting zone to provide a heat source for the roasting zone. The step (iii), further comprises blowing air or oxygen from the cooling zone to provide oxygen for the combustion in the roasting zone.
DC M24 (Metallurgy of iron and steel - including manufacture and processing, treatment of steel melts and changing the physical properties of iron and steel, control/testing methods, blast furnaces and converters.    USE - The method is useful for making pots eminently suitable for raising nursery plants.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is capable of making pots eminently suitable for raising nursery plants which are biodegradable, highly effective in performance, environmentally safe, economically viable and most suitable for good and healthy growth of seedling plants. The pot is better substitute for polythene bags and plastic pots which are commonly used for raising nursery plants which at the same time give rise to deterioration in quality of soil where planted. The pot has good strength to absorb shock up to 25 kg during transportation and thus has no risk in handling in planting. The pot affords slow and steady biodegradation, builds up microbial population, develops carbon pools and gives rise to appreciable carbon stock in soil with less emission of carbon dioxide to environment. The pot has good manurial value and thus saves the expenditures on manure, fertilizer and water for a long time. The pot is eco-friendly, free from pollutants, safe and 100 % organic in nature suitable for organically grown nursery of seedlings of vegetables and horticultural crops.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - AGRICULTURE - Preferred Components: The pots can afford permeability to water at the level of 0.1-2cm hr-1 depending upon the size of pot developed through the process. The pot has 65-70% water retention capacity. Preferred Product: The pot has good porosity to allow air and water to reach plant rhizosphere and regulate soil temperature, which encourage the vertical and horizontal root expansion. The pot can afford permeability to water at the level of at least 0.1 cm.hour-1 and 2 cm.hour1 depending upon the size of pot developed through the process. The pot has water retention capacity of 65-70%. The pot is made up of cow dung, cow urine, Bel fruit pulp powder, waste pulse powder, molasses and neem cake which are eco-friendly and 100%renewable and organic which produce beneficial effects in soil.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PT P
PN CN111054346-A; CN111054346-B
TI High-carbon olefins synthesis catalyst comprises active metal component 1, active metal component structural additive and electronic additive.
AU ZHANG M
   MA D
   SU M
   XU Y
   HU W
   PENG M
   QIAO S
   ZHENG H
   ZHAO D
   YAN Z
   LI Q
   WANG T
AE SOUTHWEST CHEM IND RES & DESIGN INST (CNCC-C)
GA 202037155E
AB    NOVELTY - A high-carbon olefins synthesis catalyst comprises 10-80 wt.% active metal component 1, 10-80 wt.% active metal component 2, 1-5 wt.% structural additive and 1-5 wt.% electronic additive. The active metal component 1 is iron, cobalt, nickel and/or chromium. The second active metal component is copper, zinc and/or manganese. The structural additive is berrylium oxide, magnesium oxide, calcium oxide, strontium oxide, barium oxide, tungsten oxide and/or molybdenum oxide. The electronic additive is lithium oxide, sodium oxide, potassium oxide, rubidium oxide and/or cesium oxide.
   USE - High-carbon olefins synthesis catalyst.
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst has low methane and carbon dioxide selection, good carbon monoxide conversion rate and C5+ olefin selectivity, high stability, and low cost.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) preparation of the catalyst comprising mixing soluble salt solution of active metal component 1 and soluble salt solution of active metal component 2, stirring, adding soluble salt solution of structural additive and precipitant solution under oil bath condition, stirring, co-precipitating, aging, crystallizing, washing, filtering, adding filter cake to pore-forming agent solution, stirring to obtain slurry, spray granulation drying, and calcining to obtain catalyst precursor; and adding to soluble salt solution of electronic additive to carry out medium volume impregnation, drying, and calcining; and
   (2) preparation of the high-carbon olefins comprising subjecting catalyst to pre-reduction reaction at 200-400 degrees C and at reaction space velocity of 10000-100000/hour, adding syngas raw material comprising 15-25 wt.% carbon monoxide, 5-15 wt.% carbon dioxide, 50-80 hydrogen and balance argon, and performing F-T reaction at 1-4 MPa.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The soluble salt of active metal component 1 is iron nitrate, cobalt nitrate, nickel nitrate, lanthanum nitrate and/ro chromium nitrate. The soluble salt of active metal component 2 is copper nitrate, zinc nitrate and/or manganese nitrate. The soluble salt of structural additive is beryllium nitrate, magnesium nitrate, calciumnitrate, strontium nitrate, barium nitrate, ammonium metatungstate and/or ammonium heptamolybdate. The precipitant is sodium carbonate, potassium carbonate, sodium bicarbonate, potassium bicarbonate, and/or water ammonia. The soluble salt of electronic additive is lithium carbonate, sodium carbonate, potassium carbonate, rubidium carbonate, sodium bicarbonate, potassium bicarbonate, sodium aluminate and/or potassium aluminate. Preferred Method: The co-precipitation is done at oil bath temperature of 60-120 degrees C while stirring at speed of 100-800 revolutions/minute. The aging is done at 60-120 degrees C while stirring at speed of 100-800 revolutions/minute for 2-24 hours. The crystallization is done at 100-200 degrees C for 12-72 hours. The spray granulation drying is done at 150-280 degrees C. The calcining is done at 300-800 degrees C for 3-18 hours under air atmosphere. The drying is done at 80-120 degrees C for 5-20 hours. The calcining is done at 300-800 degrees C for 3-24 hours. The pre-reduction reaction is done by adding catalyst to quartz straight lined fixed bed reactor, and reacting with 5-20 wt.% hydrogen and balance nitrogen as reducing gas at reduction temperature of 200-400 degrees C, airspeed of 2000-10000/hour and pressure of 0-2 MPa for 2-10 hours.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The pore-forming agent is polyvinyl butyral, polyvinyl alcohol, polyethylene glycol, polymethyl methacrylate, ammonium bicarbonate and/or urea.

TI Gas analyzing apparatus for analyzing            concentration of carbon dioxide in sample gas            containing waste gas of internal combustion engine, has            span gas flow path that supplies span gas used for            calibration of gas analyzer to main flow path.
AU YOSHIMURA T
AE HORIBA LTD (HORB-C)
   HORIBA LTD (HORB-C)
GA 2020249125
AB    NOVELTY - The apparatus (100) has a gas analyzer (1)                which analyzes a measuring object component                contained in sample gas. One end of a main flow                path (2) is connected to the gas analyzer, and                provided to introduce sample gas into the gas                analyzer. A dehumidifier (22) is provided in the                main flow path, and provided to cool and dehumidify                the sample gas. A span gas flow path (4) is                provided to supply span gas used for the                calibration of the gas analyzer to the main flow                path, and set with one end connected to the                dehumidifier or upstream side of main flow path. A                zero gas flow path (3) is provided to supply water                to the dehumidifier. The span gas is humidified by                the water applied by the zero gas flow path, when                passing through the dehumidifier.
   USE - Gas analyzing apparatus for analyzing                concentration of carbon dioxide in sample gas                containing waste gas of internal combustion engine                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The gas analyzer is calibrated in shorter                time. The necessary to provide the humidifier in                the span gas flow path is eliminated so that the                structure of the gas analyzing apparatus is                simplified and the manufacturing cost the gas                analyzing apparatus is reduced.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for                calibration method of gas analyzing                apparatus.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a circuit diagram of the                gas analyzing apparatus. (Drawing includes                non-English language text)
   Gas analyzer (1)
   Main flow path (2)
   Zero gas flow path (3)
   Span gas flow path (4)
   Dehumidifier (22)
   Gas analyzing apparatus (100)
