PT P
PN CN110911694-A; CN110911694-B
TI Preparation of electrocatalyst in gas diffusion            electrode for catalyzing e.g. oxygen reduction            reaction, involves mixing metal phthalocyanine            dispersion with carbon nanomaterial dispersion,            ultrasonically processing and post-treating.
AU LIANG Y
   WANG Y
   ZHANG X
AE UNIV SOUTHERN SCI & TECHNOLOGY (USOU-C)
GA 202024368W
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing an electrocatalyst involves: (1)                mixing metal phthalocyanine molecules with a                solvent to obtain a metal phthalocyanine                dispersion, and mixing carbon nanomaterials with a                solvent to obtain a carbon nanomaterial dispersion;                (2) mixing the metal phthalocyanine dispersion with                the carbon nanomaterial dispersion, followed by                ultrasonically processing and stirring to obtain a                product; and (3) post-treating the product.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing an                electrocatalyst, which is useful in a gas diffusion                electrode for catalyzing carbon dioxide reduction                reaction and/or catalyzing oxygen reduction                reaction (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables simple preparation of the                electrocatalyst with high efficiency and excellent                catalytic performance of oxygen reduction to                peroxide. The integration of the single-molecule                catalyst on the gas diffusion electrode achieves                high product selectivity and stability at high                currents in the carbon dioxide reduction                reaction.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following: (1) the electrocatalyst prepared by the                above method; (2) a gas diffusion electrode, which                comprises a matrix, and a catalyst component                including the electrocatalyst and a binder, which                includes ionic polymer Nafion (RTM: Sulfonated                tetrafluoroethylene based fluoropolymer-copolymer)                and PTFE; and (3) a method for preparing the gas                diffusion electrode, which involves (1) mixing the                electrocatalyst and the binder according to a                predetermined ratio to obtain a catalyst slurry,                (2) applying the catalyst slurry to a gas diffusion                electrode substrate, and (3) heating the product                obtained in step (2) in an inert gas atmosphere,                where the inert gas includes argon and/or nitrogen,                and the heating step is completed at 280-350                degrees C for 0.5-2 hours.

AB    NOVELTY - The device has a pressure-bearing device                jacket and a modular scaffolding system made of two                different types of webs arranged within the device                jacket. PN KR2059888-B1
TI Container type mushroom cultivation device, has terminal electrically connected to sensor group, and communication unit installed to allow communication between terminal, management server and facility control panel.
AU KIM J S
   PARKWANSUN
AE DAWOOLENG CO LTD (DAWO-Non-standard)
GA 2020031675
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a container (100) having an opening and closing door. A facility control panel (200) controls the fan, the air purifier, the cold fan and the hot air fan installed inside container. A sensor group (300) is installed inside the container to measure a mushroom growth environment, and includes a temperature sensor, a humidity sensor, a carbon dioxide sensor, and an illumination sensor. A terminal (400) is electrically connected to the sensor group, receives information of a sensor unit, transmits mushroom growth related information to a management server, and controls mushroom grower. A management server (500) is electrically connected to terminal to record and manage the mushroom growth state using mushroom growth related information, and control the growth environment by analyzing growth state and controlling facility control panel. A communication unit (600) is installed to allow communication between the terminal, the management server and the facility control panel.
   USE - Container type mushroom cultivation device.
   ADVANTAGE - The device has an effect of providing a mushroom cultivation apparatus, which is configured to cultivate high-quality mushrooms by controlling temperature, humidity, carbon dioxide, and illuminance using appropriate values using an internet network and various sensors.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a container type mushroom cultivation method.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of a container type mushroom cultivation device. (Drawing includes non-English language text)
   Container (100)
   Facility control panel (200)
   Sensor group (300)
   Terminal (400)
   Management server (500)
   Communication unit (600)

PN CN110474375-A; CN110474375-B
TI Self-organized micro-grid system for fuel cell, has production and consumption storage device that uses generated electric energy to supply power to inside and outside of microgrid system, and that simultaneously stores electric quantity.
AU ZHOU M
   LIU F
AE CHINA HYDROGEN ENERGY TECHNOLOGY CO (CHHY-Non-standard)
GA 201998875V
AB    NOVELTY - The self-organized micro-grid system has a control system (100) that is electrically connected to a capture device (200) and a production and consumption storage device (300). The control system controls the operation, coordination and cooperation of the functional modules in the self-organizing micro-grid system of the fuel cell in real time. The capture device collects and captures carbon dioxide, and processes the carbon dioxide. The production and consumption storage device generates electric energy by using a fuel cell system, and uses the generated electric energy to supply power to the inside and outside of the self-organizing microgrid system, and simultaneously store the electric quantity. The fuel cell in production and consumption storage device includes a methanol reforming hydrogen fuel tank. The catalytic effect of the catalyst chromium oxide-zinc oxide is utilized. The mixed gas of carbon monoxide and water vapor causes a chemical reaction to produce methanol.
   USE - Self-organized micro-grid system for fuel cell.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon cycle and zero carbon emissions in the entire ad hoc network are realized, and the high-efficiency, low-consumption, and environmental protection beneficial effects of the self-organized micro-grid system are realized.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the self-organizing micro-grid system for a fuel cell. (Drawing includes non-English language text)
   Control system (100)
   Capture device (200)
   Production and consumption storage device (300)

PN CN110452932-A
TI Method for preparing microalgal fuel by solar-driven biochemical thermochemical conversion, involves first subjecting separated algae residue to oil extraction and returning harvested culture solution.
AU LIAO Q
   FU J
   HUANG Y
   XIA A
   FU Q
   LI J
   ZHU X
AE UNIV CHONGQING (UYCQ-C)
GA 2019979971
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves first subjecting the separated algae residue to oil extraction. The fats and oils obtained from oil and fat extraction are put into a biomass bio-oil refining system (7) for producing biodiesel. The degreased algae residue is passed into a biomass fermentation gas production system (5). The anaerobic fermentation is performed to produce hydrogen mixed gas and biogas. The hydrogen mixed gas and biogas produced by anaerobic fermentation are passed into a sodium carbonate solution for decarbonization and purification to obtain pure hydrogen and methane gas. The sodium carbonate solution absorbs a lot of carbon dioxide which is converted into sodium bicarbonate solution. The sodium bicarbonate solution is passed into a microalgae high-density culture and harvesting system (2) for microalgae culture. The harvested culture solution is returned to the sodium carbonate solution for carbon dioxide absorption.
   USE - Method for preparing microalgal fuel by solar-driven biochemical thermochemical conversion.
   ADVANTAGE - The system has high production of microalgal biomass, easy operation, low energy consumption, low cost, and good recycling of biomass.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a system for preparing microalgal fuel by solar-driven biochemical thermochemical conversion.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of the system for preparing microalgal fuel by solar-driven biochemical thermochemical conversion. (Drawing includes non-English language text)
   Solar photovoltaic and storage system (1)
   Microalgae high-density culture and harvesting system (2)
   Solar hydrothermal pre-treatment system (3)
   Biomass fermentation gas production system (5)
   Biomass bio-oil refining system (7)

Combustion improvement additives for liquid fuels are included (C10L).)

TI Ion-conductive powder for producing molded article            for electrical storage device, comprises lithium-ion            conductive solid electrolyte, and has preset lithium            carbonate content per gram of electrolyte measured            based on carbon dioxide content.
AU YUKI T
   DAISUKE S
   HIDEAKI H
   HIDETOSHI M
   TAKEUCHI Y
   SHISHIHARA D
   HIKOSAKA H
   MIZUTANI H
AE NGK SPARK PLUG CO LTD (NITS-C)
   NGK SPARK PLUG CO LTD (NITS-C)
   NGK SPARK PLUG CO LTD (NITS-C)
   NGK SPARK PLUG CO LTD (NITS-C)
GA 201993782A
AB    NOVELTY - An ion-conductive powder (202) comprises a                lithium-ion conductive solid electrolyte which is                an ionic conductor having a garnet-type structure                or a structure similar to the garnet-type structure                comprising lithium, zirconium, lanthanum, and                oxygen. The ion-conductive powder has a lithium                carbonate content of less than 3 mg per 1 g                lithium-ion conductive solid electrolyte measured                based on the amount of carbon dioxide detected at                500 degrees C or more by temperature programmed                desorption-mass spectrometry.
   USE - Ion-conductive powder is used for producing                molded article e.g. solid electrolyte layer,                positive electrode, or negative electrode for                electrical storage device (all claimed) e.g.                all-solid-state lithium-ion secondary battery for                personal computer, mobile telephone, and electric                vehicle. Can also be used for lithium-air battery,                lithium flow battery, and solid capacitor.
   ADVANTAGE - The ion-conductive powder produces molded                article having improved lithium-ion conductivity,                thus improving electrical performance of the                electrical storage device, and can suppress                increase in particle interface resistance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for                electrical storage device (102), which comprises a                solid electrolyte layer (112), a positive electrode                (114), and a negative electrode (116), in which at                least one comprises the ion-conductive                powder.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic cross-sectional                view of the all-solid-state lithium-ion secondary                battery.
   All-solid-state lithium-ion secondary battery                (102)
   Solid electrolyte layer (112)
   Positive electrode (114)
   Negative electrode (116)
   Ion-conductive powder (202)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The ionic conductor                  comprises at least one element chosen from                  magnesium, aluminum, silicon, calcium, titanium,                  vanadium, gallium, strontium, yttrium, niobium,                  tin, antimony, barium, hafnium, tantalum,                  tungsten, bismuth, and lanthanoids, or magnesium                  and/or at least one element (A) chosen from                  calcium, strontium, and barium. The mol ratio of                  lithium to total content of lanthanum and element                  (A) is 1.33-3. The mol ratio of magnesium to                  total content of lanthanum and element (A) is                  0-0.5. The mol ratio of element (A) to total                  content of lanthanum and element (A) is 0-0.67.                  The ion-conductive powder further comprises a                  lithium halide and/or a complex hydride.                  Preferred Properties: The lithium-ion conductive                  solid electrolyte has a powder average particle                  diameter of 0.1 mu m or more, preferably 0.5-10                  mu m.

A heating component (103) is provided for heating contents of the pressure vessel to produce a supercritical fluid. The liquid solvent is composed of carbon dioxide, nitrous oxide, propane, butane, methane, ethane, n-pentane, n-hexane, n-heptane, acetonitrile, acetone, methanol, ethanol, isopropanol, carbon disulfide, ammonia, xenon, water, fluoroform, sulfur hexafluoride, monofluoromethane, dichloromethane, chloroform, chlorotrifluoromethane, chlorodifluoromethane, difluoromethane, benzene, cyclohexane, isobutene, hydrogen sulfide, and 2,2-dimethyl propane.

   ADVANTAGE - The portable supercritical carbon dioxide extraction apparatus is easily handled and carried by one individual and has mechanically passive components. The supercritical fluid extraction apparatus is lighter and smaller. The apparatus operation is simplified by omitting the supercritical fluid generator tank by using an extraction vessel to complete both supercritical fluid generating and extracting work.

AU OMALLEY J P
   MONTGOMERY D
AE GRIDTHINK INC (GRID-Non-standard)
   GRIDTHINK INC (GRID-Non-standard)
GA 2019712107
AB    NOVELTY - A Kolbe reactor system consists of several open cavity Kolbe reactor cells and a hydrocarbon chemical formulation comprising an initial formulation of 4-6 N 2-5C carboxylic acid and 2-4 M alkali 2-5C carboxylate, in which 2-5C carboxylate and carboxylic acid have the same carbon alkyl length.
   USE - Kolbe reactor system is used for storing energy and harvesting electrical power from renewable energy source (all claimed) e.g. wind and solar energy.
   ADVANTAGE - The Kolbe reactor system has high round trip electrical energy efficiency, and is economical.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) method for storing energy, which involves converting electric power into hydrocarbon chemicals and hydrogen by providing an open cavity Kolbe reactor of the Kolbe reactor system, introducing a formulation of 2-5C primary carboxylic acid at a total acid concentration of 3-6 N, and an alkali salt of the carboxylic acid, providing electrical power to the Kolbe reactor, and recovering a product stream containing 2-8C alkane, carbon dioxide, and hydrogen; and
   (2) method for harvesting electrical power from renewable energy source, which involves converting electric power into hydrocarbon chemicals and hydrogen by providing the open cavity Kolbe reactor, introducing a formulation of 2-5C primary carboxylic acid at a total acid concentration of 3-6 N, and a potassium salt of the carboxylic acid, providing electrical power to the Kolbe reactor, and recovering a product stream containing 2-8C alkane, carbon dioxide, and hydrogen.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Component: The Kolbe reactor system further consists of a continuously fed formulation comprising 4-6 N 2-5C carboxylic acid, in which the addition of 2-5C carboxylic acid maintains the initial formulation for a continuous process.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ELECTRICAL POWER AND ENERGY - Preferred Process: Method for storing energy further involves recovering the 2-8C alkane by a liquefaction process of compressing or cooling the gas effluent.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The 2-5C carboxylic acid is acetic acid. The alkali 2-5C carboxylate is potassium acetate. The concentration of acetic acid in the initial formulation is 6 N. The 2-8C alkane is ethane.

AB    NOVELTY - Hydrocarbon burner comprises heat exchanger has first heat exchanger inlet connected to inlet of hydrocarbon burner and first heat exchanger outlet connected to heater, second heat exchanger inlet connected to reactor outlet and second heat exchanger outlet connected to outlet of hydrocarbon burner. The reactor includes reactor inlet, reactor outlet, and catalyst mixture (142) disposed in reactor bed between reactor inlet and reactor outlet. The heater (130) connecting first heat exchanger outlet to reactor inlet. The reactor is low temperature reactor (140) configured to convert hydrocarbon to water and carbon dioxide.
   USE - Hydrocarbon burner for enclosed environment (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The hydrocarbon burner reduces potential poisoning atmosphere.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a method for preventing buildup of hydrocarbons in enclosed environment, which involves removing portion of air from environmental system, passing portion of air through regenerative heat exchanger, heating output of regenerative heat exchanger in heater, providing output of heater to reactor and converting hydrocarbons entrained in output into water and carbon dioxide in reactor, passing output of reactor through regenerative heat exchanger, transferring heat from output of reactor to portion of air, returning output of reactor to environmental system; and
   (2) a enclosed environmental system, which comprises sealed environment, air recirculation and filtration system connected to sealed environment and hydrocarbon burner connected to air recirculation and filtration system, hydrocarbon burner includes regenerative heat exchanger.

   Regenerative heat exchanger (120)
   Heater (130)
   Reactor (140)
   Catalyst mixture (142)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The method involves transferring heat from output of reactor to portion of air includes raising temperature of portion of air to 90% of desired temperature at input to reactor, removing portion of air from environmental system includes drawing air out of existing environmental system via fan disposed upstream of regenerative heat exchanger. The output of reactor returned to environmental system is approximately 20 degrees F above ambient temperature of environmental system. TI Composite duplex stainless steel/vanadium carbide for e.g., automotive industry, is produced by breaking and blending chips with carbon dioxide, high-energy grinding, recovering material, carrying out tension treatment, and sintering.
AU DE SALES PEREIRA MENDONCA
   SILVA G
   DE LOURDES NORONHA MOTTA
AE UNIV UNIFEI FEDERAL ITAJUBA (UYUN-Non-standard)
GA 201930429D
AB    NOVELTY - A composite duplex stainless steel/vanadium carbide is produced by high-energy milling which involves breaking and blending chips with carbon dioxide and without process control agent homogenized in a stainless steel container, high-energy grinding in a planetary mill with argon atmosphere to avoid oxidation of post-parameters and their defined values according to design of experiments (DOE), performing granulometric analysis and scanning electron microscopy, recovering material according to increased milling time and presence of martensitic phase, verified by X-ray diffraction, analyzing magnetic properties for characterization of ferromagnetic phase, carrying out tension treatment, comparing in a uniaxial press, where samples are made with 1 g material (stainless steel+carbide), performing green density measurement, sintering in inert atmosphere (vacuum) at 10-2 mbar and 1200 degrees C, cooling in water, and performing apparent density and porosity measurements on sintered composite.
   USE - A composite duplex stainless steel/vanadium carbide produced by high-energy milling for automotive, aeronautical, and oil industries.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is low cost due to the use of waste from the machining process and can be an alternative to recycling waste production that would be discarded in the environment. To obtain the lowest particle size value, a complete factorial design is used, varying the parameters of time, rotation, mass/sphere ratio parameters, and percentage of carbides added in the grinding process, which can generate a reduction in the number of experiments, time, and cost of the overall process.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Component: Powdered material comprises iron, nickel, chromium, manganese, molybdenum, and vanadium carbide.

TI Nitrogen-modified covalent triazine organic polymer visible light photocatalyst useful in reduction of carbon dioxide to methane.
AU BI J
   CHENG Z
   LI L
   WU L
AE UNIV FUZHOU (UFZU-C)
GA 2019114856
AB    NOVELTY - Nitrogen-modified covalent triazine organic polymer visible light photocatalyst, where the nitrogen element is introduced into the covalent triazine organic polymer by hydrothermal method to synthesize a visible light photocatalyst modified with nitrogen.
   USE - The photocatalyst is useful in reduction of carbon dioxide to methane (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst has good visible light response. The method is simple, convenient and environmentally friendly.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing nitrogen-modified covalent triazine organic polymer visible light photocatalyst, comprising (i) adding 40 ml trifluoromethanesulfonic acid slowly into 5.12 g terephthalonitrile under stirring at 0 degrees C, changing the oil bath and heating to 30 degrees C, allowing to stand for 3-5 days to obtain solid, pulverizing, washing with 100-160 ml dichloromethane, washing with aqueous ammonia for many times, and adding with 100-200 ml aqueous ammonia for 12 hours, washing the mixture with water to neutrality, finally centrifuging once with methanol, collecting the solid precipitate, refluxing with methanol at 80-100 degrees C for 24 hours, refluxing with dichloromethane at 60-80 degrees C for 24 hours, and drying at 80 degrees C for 12 hours under vacuum to obtain a covalent triazine organic polymer, and (ii) dissolving 200-400 mg covalent triazine organic polymer in 100 ml polytetrafluoroethylene reaction kettle, adding 30 ml deionized water, adding 1-3 ml hydrazine into the reaction kettle and stirring under a magnetic stirrer for 2 hours, placing the reaction kettle in an oven at 180 degrees C for 12 hours, cooling to room temperature, washing the reaction product, centrifuging many times with deionized water, and drying in an oven at 60 degrees C for 12 hours.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The modified concentration of nitrogen in the visible light photocatalyst is 1-1.6 wt.%.

TI Rotating an electro-magnetic field for synchronous            assistant laser welding method involves applying            alternating magnetic field to welding pool during laser            welding, where field lines acting on welding pool are            vertical to welding direction.
AU SHI Y
   YAN S
   LIU J
   LI Y
AE UNIV CHANGCHUN SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYCY-C)
GA 2019049097
AB    NOVELTY - Rotating an electro-magnetic field for                synchronous assistant laser welding method involves                applying an alternating magnetic field to welding                pool together during laser welding, where magnetic                field lines acting on welding pool are vertical to                welding direction, and direction of electric field                is parallel to direction of laser beam.
   USE - Method for rotating an electro-magnetic field                for synchronous assistant laser welding                method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to rotate an                electro-magnetic field for synchronous assistant                laser welding method, which realizes simple, rapid                and continuous adjustment of rotating magnetic                field and constant electric field, ensures                synchronous movement of electric and magnetic                fields with welding laser beam, sets a reasonable                power, focal length, defocusing amount, and                shielding gas flow rate, improves laser process                parameters and improves quality of laser welding                line formation.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a device                for rotating an electro-magnetic field for                synchronous assistant laser welding method, which                comprises carbon dioxide laser, laser reflection                system, machine tool moving system, machine tool                numerical control operating system, encoder,                locating and clamping fixture, electromagnetic                adjusting device, pneumatic valve manual switch,                helium gas and argon gas cylinder and power supply                unit, where laser beam emitted by the carbon                dioxide laser passes through the laser reflection                system, and focuses laser beam on upper surface of                workpiece by welding the laser head, where machine                tool numerical control operating system controls                the movement of the machine tool moving system, and                electromagnetic adjusting device is mounted on main                shaft of the machine tool moving system.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Conditions: The region with                  largest magnetic field strength acts on welding                  pool, magnetic field strength is 0-70mT, magnetic                  field rotation speed of alternating magnetic                  field is 0-600 revolutions per minute (rpm), and                  electric field voltage is 0-60V. The laser power                  P is 1500W, welding speed V is 600mm/minute,                  mixed gas of helium gas and argon gas is used as                  shielding gas, where helium gas flow rate is                  f1=10L/minute, argon gas flow rate is                  f1=15L/minute, and defocusing amount Delta f=0mm.                  The electromagnetic adjusting device comprises                  direct drive motor, magnetic field adjusting                  plate, neodymium iron boron permanent magnet, and                  neodymium iron boron permanent magnet fixed outer                  casing. The neodymium iron boron permanent magnet                  fixed outer casing is made of permalloy. The                  device comprises bottom plate and supporting                  column which is made of acrylic, insulating                  sleeve which is made of bakelite, and insulating                  bush and insulating cushion are made of                  marble.

PN CN108976947-A
TI Anti-wear ceramic coating comprises gas phase white carbon black, magnesium oxide, carbon dioxide, cupric oxide, volcanic rock, zircon, silicon dioxide, zinc stearate, nano aluminum oxide, zinc stearate, xylene, hydroxide and surfactant.
AU XIONG Z
AE FOSHAN GAOMING PRODUCTIVITY PROMOTION (FOSH-Non-standard)
GA 2018A29700
AB    NOVELTY - Anti-wear ceramic coating comprises 55-75 pts. wt. gas phase white carbon black, 40-45 pts. wt. magnesium oxide, 28-35 pts. wt. carbon dioxide, 16-20 pts. wt. cupric oxide, 16-19 pts. wt. volcanic rock, 11-16 pts. wt. zircon, 12-18 pts. wt. silicon dioxide, 4-8 pts. wt. zinc stearate, 3-5 pts. wt. nano aluminum oxide, 1-4 pts. wt. zinc stearate, 6-10 pts. wt. xylene, 5-10 pts. wt. hydroxide, 9-18 pts. wt. surfactant, 10-18 pts. wt. anti-aging agent, 26-30 pts. wt. melamine, 16-22 pts. wt. succinic anhydride, 30-39 pts. wt. polyacrylic acid and 80-105 pts. wt. de-ionized water.
   USE - Anti-wear ceramic coating.
   ADVANTAGE - The anti-wear ceramic coating has anti-abrasion effect and prolongs service life.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing anti-wear ceramic coating, which involves:
   (A) adding succinic anhydride, polyacrylic acid, de-ionized water, zirconite, nanometre aluminum oxide, zinc stearate into stirring container, heating at 93-95 degrees C, stirring at 68-72 revolution per minute for 23-25 minute;
   (B) adding sixcarbon, copper oxide, volcanic, zircon, silicondioxide, xylene, cesium hydroxide, modifying at 42-48 revolution per minute and stirring for 15-18 minute; and
   (C) adding gas-phase white carbon black, magnesium oxide,surfactant, antioxidant, melamine pouring, stirring at 26-29 revolution per minute for 30-35 minutes, placing into grinding machine, grinding for 6-10 hours at 55-57 degrees C to obtain final product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The surface active agent is sodium dodecyl benzene sulfonate. The anti-aging agent is N'-diphenyl-para-phenylene diamine. Preferred Compositions: The anti-wear ceramic coating comprises 65 pts. wt. gas phase white carbon black, 43 pts. wt. magnesium oxide, 30 pts. wt. carbon dioxide, 18 pts. wt. cupric oxide, 17 pts. wt. volcanic rock, 15 pts. wt. zircon, 16 pts. wt. silicon dioxide, 6 pts. wt. zinc stearate, 4 pts. wt. nano aluminum oxide, 3 pts. wt. zinc stearate, 8 pts. wt. xylene, 7 pts. wt. hydroxide, 12 pts. wt. surfactant, 14 pts. wt. anti-aging agent, 28 pts. wt. melamine, 20 pts. wt. succinic anhydride, 35 pts. wt. polyacrylic acid and 95 pts. wt. de-ionized water.

PN CN108799512-A; CN208831740-U; US2020072361-A1; US10989311-B2
TI High pressure valve, has pressing device for controlling opening or closing function of air inlet and air outlet, and limiting block for limiting intake of air inlet hole on pressing device, where pressing device is connected with air inlet.
AU TAN D
   WANG S
AE SHENZHEN CHANGZHI ELECTRONICS TECHNOLOGY (SHEN-Non-standard)
   SHENZHEN CHANGZHI ELECTRONICS TECHNOLOGY (SHEN-Non-standard)
   SHENZHEN CHANGZHI ELECTRONICS TECHNOLOGY (SHEN-Non-standard)
GA 201892617R
AB    NOVELTY - The valve has a valve main body provided with an air inlet and an air outlet and fixed with an inner side wall of a mounting hole. The valve main body is formed with an air inlet hole and an air outlet hole. A pressing device controls opening or closing function of the air inlet and the air outlet. The pressing device is connected with the air inlet. A limiting block limits intake of the air inlet hole on the pressing device. The pressing device is provided with a connecting pipe that is located at an upper end opening of the mounting hole. The air inlet hole is fixed with an end of the connecting pipe.
   USE - High pressure valve.
   ADVANTAGE - The valve is simple and smart in structural design, and has wide application range, and utilizes the limiting block to jack high pressure in the air inlet so as to block the air inlet hole of the pressing device, thus stopping outer jet when outer jet is not required, fixes the limiting block by the pressing device so as to form a gap between the limiting block and the air inlet hole, thus sending carbon dioxide into the air inlet and ejecting carbon dioxide from the air outlet if outer jet is required.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a side perspective view of a high pressure valve.

TI Method for measuring stress field variations during carbon-dioxide fracturing process, involves slicing cooled three-dimensional physical models, and processing stress fringe distribution to produce three-dimensional shear stress of points.
AU JU Y
   LIU P
   LIU H
   YANG Y
AE UNIV CHINA MINING & TECHNOLOGY BEIJING (UYMB-C)
   UNIV CHINA MINING & TECHNOLOGY BEIJING (UYMB-C)
GA 2018840185
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves performing carbon-dioxide experiments (S104) at corresponding target pressures with respect to three-dimensional physical models in different crack states. Three-dimensional physical models that underwent carbondi-oxide, fracturing experiments at corresponding target pressures are cooled (S105). Cooled three-dimensional physical models are sliced (S106) to produce three orthogonal plane two-dimensional slices of each three-dimensional physical model. Stress fringe distribution is processed (S107) to produce maximum three-dimensional shear stress of points in three dimensional physical models.
   USE - Method for measuring stress field variations during carbon-di-oxide fracturing process.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables utilizing multiple identical transparent three- dimensional physical models to implement the display and expression, thus ensuring accuracy and reliability of a measurement result and solving problem of low calculation precision and difficulty in ensuring accuracy and reliability found in an existing numerical simulation process.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow diagram illustrating a method for measuring stress field variations during carbondi-oxide fracturing process. '(Drawing includes non-English language text)'
   Step for printing on basis of three dimensional digital models in different crack states to produce three-dimensional physical models in different crack states (S103)
   Step for performing carbondi-oxide experiments (S104)
   Step for cooling three-dimensional physical models that underwent carbondi-oxide, fracturing experiments at corresponding target pressures (S105)
   Step for slicing cooled three-dimensional physical models to produce three orthogonal plane two-dimensional slices of each three-dimensional physical model (S106)
   Step for processing stress fringe distribution to produce maximum three-dimensional shear stress of points in three dimensional physical models (S107)

PT P
PN CN108558783-A; CN208562198-U
TI Processing melamine comprises adding liquid urea into a melamine fluidized bed reactor, reacting to form a gas mixture of melamine, ammonia and carbon dioxide gas and cooling the mixture gas in a hot gas cooler and passing through purifier.
AU CHENG K
   CHENG W
AE CHENG K (CHEN-Individual)
GA 201877982K
AB    NOVELTY - Processing melamine comprises (i) adding liquid urea as raw material into a melamine fluidized bed reactor, and reacting to form a gas mixture of melamine, ammonia and carbon dioxide gas; and (ii) cooling the mixture gas from the melamine reactor in a hot gas cooler, passing through the purifier, adding the melamine-containing process gas into the chiller, dehydrating the melamine to a solid phase, adding the chilled gas containing melamine into the combined high-efficiency separator, and separating the solid melamine and melamine reaction gases, and adding the melamine into the packaging system.
   USE - The device is useful for processing melamine.
   ADVANTAGE - The method does not discharge any melamine process tail gas outside the melamine production plant boundary zone, the melamine process tail gas realizes self-recycling inside the melamine process production device boundary area. The device is clean, environmentally friendly, and melamine process tail gas is efficient recycles all melamine process production equipment.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for device useful for processing melamine comprising a liquid urea which is efficiently synthesized by the urea conversion circulating liquid urine system unit is directly connected with the melamine fluidized bed reactor, which is connected with the hot gas cooler, which is connected with the purifier, and the purifier is connected with the chiller, where chiller is connected to a combined high efficiency separator, and the combined high efficiency separator is connected to the product packaging system and the combined high efficiency wash cooling tower.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The combined high-efficiency separator and combined high-efficiency washing and cooling tower are combined with high-efficiency washing and cooling tower, which are connected to melamine fluidized bed reactor through compressor. The one-way triamine tail gas is internally circulated to high-efficiency synthetic urea conversion circulating liquid urinary system unit and urea is efficiently synthesized. The conversion cycle liquid urine system unit is connected to the melamine fluidized bed reactor to form a closed circuit full cycle. The combined high efficiency wash cooling tower is connected to the compressor on a line to a chiller. The combined high efficiency wash cooling tower is connected to the heat exchanger via a medium solution pump and the heat exchanger is connected to a combined high efficiency wash chiller to form a cycle. The high-efficiency synthetic urea conversion circulating liquid urine system unit comprises a synthetic urea tower, a medium-pressure decomposer and a two-stage low-pressure decomposer, characterized in that the synthetic urea tower is connected with a medium-pressure decomposer, a medium-pressure decomposer and a second-stage low-pressure reactor. The decomposer is connected to the two-stage low-pressure decomposer, which is connected with the flash concentration, which is connected with the first-stage concentration device. The first-stage concentration device is connected with the second-stage concentration device, and the second-stage concentration device is connected to the melamine fluidized bed reactor to form a closed circuit full cycle. A section of the decomposer is connected to a section of the absorption device via a pipeline, and a section of the absorption device is connected to the high-temperature heater of methylammonium solution and the high-efficiency heater of liquid ammonia and the high-temperature heater of methylammonium solution and the high-efficiency heater of liquid ammonia are connected to the synthetic urea tower. The two-stage low-pressure decomposer is connected to the second-stage absorption device via a pipeline and the second-stage absorption device is connected to the synthetic urea tower through a section of the absorption device. The flash concentrating device, the first-stage concentrating device and the second-stage concentrating device are connected to a desorption hydrolysis device by a pipeline. The desorption and hydrolysis device is connected to the synthetic urea tower via the first and second absorption devices, and the other desorption and hydrolysis device is connected to the urea circulation pool through the pipeline for hydration to form a closed cycle of the closed wastewater. Preferred Method: In step (i) the liquid urea participates in a reaction has a temperature of 350-420C and pressure of 0.1-2.6 MPa and in step (ii) the mixture gas is passed through a hot gas cooler for cooling to 320-340 degrees C. In the step (ii) the melamine reaction gas separated by the high-efficiency separator with the mixed tail gas containing ammonia and carbon dioxide at a temperature of 200-230 degrees C, and the mixed tail gas enters the combined washing and cooling tower, and is cooled to 130-145 degrees C by washing, where the cooled gas is pressurized into the melamine reactor all the way, all the way through the high-efficiency synthesis of urea conversion circulating liquid urine unit to synthesize liquid urea and then into the melamine reactor to repeat the production of melamine products. In step (ii) the cooled gas is pressurized into the melamine reactor, partially enters the chiller for chilling, and the mixed exhaust gas of the step (ii) is a mixed gas of gaseous ammonia and carbon dioxide in a molar ratio of 2-2.5:1. The method uses using a tail gas containing ammonia, carbon dioxide and trace melamine according to synthesize liquid urea through a high-performance synthesis urea conversion circulating liquid system and then entering a melamine reactor to produce a melamine product. The method comprises washing the mixed exhaust gas cooled to 130-145 degrees C to directly or stepping up to 1.7-2.0 MPa, then entering the two-stage low-pressure system of the high-efficiency synthetic urea conversion circulating liquid urine system unit and reacting to generate a hydroxy group in a medium pressure system ammonium formate, after heating with a high-efficiency heater of methylammonium solution, the ammonium carbamate liquid formed by the reaction enters the synthetic urea tower and the urea synthesis liquid is from the synthetic urea tower, which is sequentially subjected to a medium-pressure decomposition, a two-stage low-pressure decomposition, and is synthesized and decomposed and absorbed. The urea column is subjected to primary concentration and secondary concentration to deliver qualified liquid urea to the melamine reactor. The high-temperature heater of the methylammonium solution has a temperature control of 130-189 degrees C and a pressure of 15-22 MPa. In the synthetic urea column, the temperature is controlled to be 186-189 degrees C, the pressure control is 15.5-20.0 MPa, the molecular ratio of ammonia/carbon dioxide is controlled to 3.5-4.6, the molecular ratio of water/carbon is less than or equal to 1.5 and the ammonium carbamate liquid is synthesized and the reaction time in the urea column is 50-70 minutes. The urea synthesis solution from the synthetic urea tower contains methylammonium and liquid ammonia which are not reacted to urea, and is subjected to a medium pressure decomposition by heating under reduced pressure, and the pressure in the intermediate pressure decomposer is controlled to be 1.6-1.75 MPa, and the urea synthesis liquid is used, where theammonia and carbon dioxide gas are decomposed into methylammonium and liquid ammonia enters a medium-pressure absorption system to form a concentrated ammonium carbamate solution, which is pressurized by a methyl ammonium pump to 15-22 MPa and returned to the synthetic urea tower via a high-temperature heater of methylammonium solution. The pressure decomposition liquid comes out from the one-point heater and enters the top of the second-stage low-pressure decomposer. The pressure in the second-stage low-pressure decomposer is 0.25-0.4 MPa, and flows through the packing layer of the two-stage low-pressure decomposer continuously into the two-point heater at the bottom, where the separated gas is continuously raised from the rectifying section of the packing layer through the hood, and the water vapor having a higher boiling point in the gas phase is continuously condensed by the solution flowing down the top of the column, and the undecomposed methylammonium in the urine is heated by the gas, where the continuous decomposition and gasification into ammonia and carbon dioxide into the second-stage low-pressure absorption system to produce dilute methyl ammonium solution and dilute aqueous ammonia, where the pressurized by pump to return a medium-pressure absorption system mixed with concentrated methyl ammonium solution and pressurized by the ammonium-methyl pump, urea synthesis column, where the secondary low pressure decomposer of the urea solution out of the urea aqueous solution is concentrated to achieve a melamine urea feed liquid urea concentration required by the system. The urea concentration system condenses and evaporates the urea aqueous solution to a surface liquid. The second surface liquid is discharged to the urea circulating water system by the desorption and hydrolysis equipment of the unit to the urea circulating water system for reuse of the urea circulating water; the desorbed gas returns to the second stage low pressure absorption system recovery.

TI Experimental device for preparing hydrocarbon, has catalyst chamber that is located in middle of reaction tube and illumination port which is arranged on both sides of temperature control device and is aligned with catalyst chamber.
AU WANG W
   ZHENG X
   WEI J
   DAN H
AE UNIV QINZHOU (UYQZ-C)
GA 2018683213
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a gas mixer (3) whose inlet is provided with the three passages for hydrogen, carbon dioxide and nitrogen. The passages are provided with a switch control valve (9) and a mass flow meter (10). The gas mixer outlet is provided with a switch control valve. A reaction device (5) is provided with a reaction tube, an illumination port and a catalyst chamber. The temperature control device (7) is arranged on both sides of the reaction tube. The catalyst chamber is located in the middle of the reaction tube. The illumination port is arranged on both sides of the temperature control device and is aligned with the catalyst chamber.
   USE - Experimental device for preparing hydrocarbon.
   ADVANTAGE - The structure of the experimental device is simplified. The experimental device is easy to install. The energy for the experimental device is saved. The environmental conditions are reduced. The experimental device is safe to use. The requirement of complicated design is eliminated. The catalyst containing the photosensitive material is placed in the catalyst chamber, thus activation energy is decreased and the conversion of the raw material is accelerated. The laboratory's research is significant for subsequent carbon dioxide and water preparation fuels.

GA 201865341K
AB    NOVELTY - Use of dopamine salt (I) in preparation of drug and pharmaceutical composition for treatment of chronic refractory heart failure, occlusive vascular disease and/or critical cases, is claimed.
   USE - The dopamine salt is useful for preparing drug or pharmaceutical composition in the form of injection for treating chronic refractory heart failure, occlusive vascular disease and/or critical cases (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The sodium or potassium salt of dopamine is safe, as it does not cause side effects and hyperchloremia in human body as compared to dopamine hydrochloride.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Use of dopamine salt of formula (I) in preparation of drug and pharmaceutical composition for treatment of chronic refractory heart failure, occlusive vascular disease and/or critical cases, is claimed.
   n = 1 or 2;and
   X = salt.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) preparation of dopamine salt (I), which involves reacting dopamine compound of formula (II) with an aqueous solution of a strong base. The base is sodium hydroxide or potassium hydroxide; and
   (2) preparation of injection solution of dopamine salt (I), which involves passing water for injection into carbon dioxide to make it saturated, adding 60-80 wt.% dopamine salt, 3-8 wt.% sodium sulfite and 47-54 wt.% D-glucose monohydrate, passing the saturated carbon dioxide gas, stirring and dissolving, adjusting pH of the resultant mixture with an acid to 3.2-4.3, adding water for injection, stirring uniformly, filtering, potting and sterilizing at 115-121 degrees C for 15-30 minutes.

TI Soybean blend oil comprises soybean, safflower seeds, wheat germ, and flaxseed, which has balanced proportion of omega-three and omega-six fatty acids, is rich in linolenic acid, has balanced nutrition and easy to be absorbed by human body.
AU LIN S
   XIE J
   LIN X
   YU J
AE GUANGDONG JINNIBAO TECHNOLOGY DEV CO LTD (GUAN-Non-standard)
   GUANGZHOU GINNIBABY EDIBLE OIL CO LTD (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 201844492X
AB    NOVELTY - Soybean blend oil comprises 40-50 wt.% soybean, 30-40 wt.% safflower seeds, 10-20 wt.% wheat germ, and 10-20 wt.% flaxseed.
   USE - Soybean blend oil.
   ADVANTAGE - The soybean blend oil has balanced proportion of omega-3 and omega-6 fatty acids, is rich in linolenic acid, has balanced nutrition and is easy to be absorbed by human body.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing soybean blend oil, which involves:
   (A) weighing the raw materials, crushing, mixing uniformly, adding into an extraction kettle of a multi-stage separation supercritical carbon dioxide extraction equipment;
   (B) carrying out extraction at a temperature of 35-60 degrees C, pressure of 20-40 mega pascal for 2.0-4.0 hours, passing the supercritical carbon dioxide and allowing the mixture to enter the separation vessel for vacuum separation, which is carried out at a temperature of 40-60 degrees C, pressure of 10-20 mega pascal (MPa) to obtain soybean blended oil crude oil;
   (C) adding the soy blend crude oil to a molecular distillation sample tank to adjust the molecular distillation process parameters, where the vacuum degree is 2-8 Pascal, thetemperature is 100-150 degrees C, speed is 100-300 rotations per minute, feed rate is 10-50mL per minute and condensing temperature is 30-50 degrees C; and
   (D) perfroming refining process to collect the blended oil to obtain the soybean blend oil.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Conditions: The separation further comprises a separation vessel II and a separation vessel III, where the separation vessel II has a temperature of 30-50 degrees C, a pressure of 4-8 MPa, a separation vessel III temperature of 30-40 degrees C and a pressure of 4-6MPa.

PN CN108034010-A
TI Preparing hydroxypropyl cyclodextrin comprises            e.g. mixing calcium hydroxide or calcium oxide, water            and cyclodextrin, adding cyclodextrin, stirring,            removing insoluble matter by filtration and drying            obtained liquid.
AU LIU G
   SHEN J
   LI Y
   CHEN J
AE SHANDONG BINZHOU ZHIYUAN BIOLOGICAL TECH (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 201839853S
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing hydroxypropyl cyclodextrin comprises                e.g. (i) mixing calcium hydroxide or calcium oxide,                water and cyclodextrin to obtain suspension, then                controlling solution temperature to 20-30 degrees                C, adding cyclodextrin of 1,2-propylene oxide,                stirring for 2-24 hours, (ii) adding reaction                solution obtained in step (i) with carbon dioxide                to obtain calcium carbonate precipitate, (iii)                removing insoluble matter by filtration from the                precipitated solution obtained in step (ii), and                (iv) drying liquid obtained in step (iii) to obtain                white amorphous powder hydroxypropyl                cyclodextrin.
   USE - The cyclodextrin is useful in food, medicine,                cosmetic and agriculture.
   ADVANTAGE - The cyclodextrin: has high-efficiency, short                preparation time, high yield and low cost; and is                environmentally friendly and suitable for                industrial production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing hydroxypropyl cyclodextrin comprises                (i) mixing calcium hydroxide or calcium oxide,                water and cyclodextrin in a mass ratio of                1.6:100:10-25 to obtain suspension, then                controlling solution temperature to 20-30 degrees                C, adding 20-36 wt.% cyclodextrin of 1,2-propylene                oxide, controlling temperature to 20-30 degrees C,                dropping for 8-36 hours, stirring for 2-24 hours to                obtain reaction solution, (ii) adding reaction                solution obtained in step (i) with carbon dioxide                to adjust pH to 6.5-8.5 to obtain calcium carbonate                precipitate, then allowing to stand resulting                solution for 24 hours to precipitate, (iii)                removing insoluble matter by filtration from the                precipitated solution obtained in step (ii), and                (iv) drying liquid obtained in step (iii) to obtain                white amorphous powder hydroxypropyl                cyclodextrin.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In step (iv), introducing                  drying method to spray dryer, controlling inlet                  air temperature to 180-210 degrees C; or removing                  moisture using rotary evaporator. In step (i),                  dropping process temperature is 23-25 degrees C.                  In step (ii), adjusting pH value to 7.2-7.5 using                  carbon dioxide. Preferred Components: In step                  (i), mass ratio between calcium hydroxide or                  calcium oxide, water, cyclodextrin and                  1,2-propylene oxide is 1.6:100:15:4.2. The                  cyclodextrin is alpha -cyclodextrin, beta                  -cyclodextrin or gamma -cyclodextrin.

PN CN107987511-A
TI Preparation of UV-cured formaldehyde removing polyester plastic film involves preparing mixture of e.g. polyurethane, formaldehyde and calcium stearate, adding e.g. titania, and extruding and molding mixture.
AU ZHOU Z
AE ZHOU Z (ZHOU-Individual)
GA 201836637P
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of UV-cured formaldehyde removing polyester plastic film involves mixing polyurethane, polypropylene fiber, formaldehyde, calcium stearate, calcium silicate and coupling agent in a reaction kettle, reacting, adding ground tea powder and titanium dioxide to the reaction furnace filled with oxygen, reducing the temperature to 120-150 degrees C and reacting, extruding, pelletizing and molding the mixture, placing the molding material in a UV chamber for 20-30 minutes, filling carbon dioxide gas, spraying 120 degrees C steam, and drying naturally.
   USE - Preparation of UV-cured formaldehyde removing polyester plastic film.
   ADVANTAGE - The process produces environmentally-friendly plastic film having good compressive performance and tensile performance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of UV-cured formaldehyde removing polyester plastic film involves mixing 5-10 pts. wt. polyurethane, 1-3 pts. wt. polypropylene fiber, 1-2 pts. wt. formaldehyde, 2-3 pts. wt. calcium stearate, 1-3 pts. wt. calcium silicate and 1-2 pts. wt. coupling agent in a reaction kettle, stirring and reacting at 250-280 degrees C, adding 1-3 pts. wt. ground tea powder and 1-2 pts. wt. titanium dioxide to the reaction furnace filled with oxygen, reducing the temperature to 120-150 degrees C and reacting the contents for 2 hours under pressure of 2 MPa, adding the mixture obtained into a twin-screw extruder, pelletizing and molding, placing the molding material in a UV chamber for 20-30 minutes, filling carbon dioxide gas, spraying 120 degrees C steam, and drying naturally.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The silane coupling agent is vinyl trimethoxy silane, ( gamma )-mercaptopropyltrimethoxysilane, vinyltrichlorosilane and/or isobutyltriethoxysilane.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Process: The powder is ground and sieved with 500 mesh sieve. The twin screw extruder temperature is 220-260 degrees C and rotation speed of the screw is 300 rpm. The UV wavelength is 505 nm. The pressure of the carbon dioxide gas is 1.5-2 MPa.

TI Preparing continuous and controllable shape of calcium carbonate crystal whisker involves weighing amount of calcium oxide, and adding into deionized water to obtain calcium hydroxide suspension, and heating to make it digestible.
AU WANG Z

   WANG S
   TIAN W
   DU P
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG (UYZH-C)
GA 201831340V
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing continuous and controllable shape of calcium carbonate crystal whisker involves weighing amount of calcium oxide, and adding into deionized water to obtain concentration of 0.1-0.3 mole per liter calcium hydroxide suspension, heating to make it digestible, weighing magnesium chloride as crystalline form control agent, adding water to dissolve, and adding magnesium chloride solution to digestion of calcium hydroxide suspension, stirring to make it uniformly, and using double pipe aeration unit filling carbon dioxide gas to bottom of reaction unit.
   USE - Method for preparing continuous and controllable shape of calcium carbonate crystal whisker.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare continuous and controllable shape of calcium carbonate crystal whisker which is a new type calcium carbonate whisker controlled by reaction conditions and used as filling material which has great advantage to improved chemical resistance and mechanical properties.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing continuous and controllable shape of calcium carbonate crystal whisker involves weighing amount of calcium oxide, and adding into deionized water to obtain concentration of 0.1-0.3 mole per liter calcium hydroxide suspension, heating to make it digestible, weighing magnesium chloride as crystalline form control agent, adding water to dissolve, and adding magnesium chloride solution to digestion of calcium hydroxide suspension, stirring to make it uniformly, using double pipe aeration unit filling carbon dioxide gas to bottom of reaction unit, carrying out carbonatation reaction, finishing reaction, filtering, washing obtained precipitate, and drying to obtain the sample.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Condition: The heating digestion temperature is 70-100 degrees C. The adding amount of magnesium chloride and magnesium calcium mole ratio is 1:1-2.5:1. The carbon dioxide gas flux is 60-150 millilite per minute. The double-pipe aeration device is three-mouth flask. The carbonation reaction time is 2-3 hours. The branch arm whisker sample obtained calcium carbonate is 2-16 pts. wt..

Preferred Components: The pigment is anthocyanin, brown soluble pigment, or capsanthin powder.

AU XU X
   YAN R
   ZHAO L
   SHEN J
AE UNIV SUN YAT-SEN (UYSY-C)
   UNIV SUN YAT-SEN (UYSY-C)
GA 201806145L
AB    NOVELTY - The separation and purification of capsaicin                and dihydrocapsaicin involves crushing pepper                fruit, sieving, and extracting using supercritical                carbon dioxide fluid to obtain pepper oil resin,                mixing the pepper oil resin and methanol-water                solution, ultrasonically processing, centrifuging,                and concentrating to obtain oil containing                capsaicin and dihydrocapsaicin, dissolving in                alkali solution, adjusting the pH value,                crystallizing, filtering, washing, and drying to                obtain capsaicinoid crystal, injecting the                capsaicinoid crystal to high speed counter-current                chromatography separation.
   USE - Separation and purification method of                capsaicin and dihydrocapsaicin.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables separation and purification                of capsaicin and dihydrocapsaicin with capsaicin                purity of greater than 97%, dihydrocapsaicin purity                of greater than 96%, extraction efficiency of more                than 93%, recovery rate of more than 70%, excellent                repeatability, and high product purity, by simple                and economical process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The separation and purification method of                capsaicin and dihydrocapsaicin involves (S1)                crushing pepper fruit, sieving, and extracting                using supercritical carbon dioxide fluid to obtain                pepper oil resin, (S2) mixing the pepper oil resin                in the step (S1) and methanol-water solution,                ultrasonically processing, centrifuging, and                concentrating to obtain oil containing capsaicin                and dihydrocapsaicin, (S3) dissolving oily                substance in alkali solution with pH value of                10-14, adjusting the pH value to 4-8 using acid,                crystallizing at 0-10 degrees C for 1-24 hours,                filtering, washing, and drying to obtain                capsaicinoid crystal, and (S4) continuously                injecting the capsaicinoid crystal obtained in step                (S3) to high speed counter-current chromatography                separation.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: In the step (S1), the                  extracting pressure is 10-50 MPa, the extraction                  temperature is 10-50 degrees C, and the                  extracting time is 30-150 minutes. In step (S4),                  the high-speed countercurrent chromatographic                  separation adopts head-tail elution mode with                  500-1000 rpm speed, 1-5 ml/minute mobile phase                  flow rate, and 280 nm detection wavelength.                  Preferred Composition: In the step (S1), the                  content of ethanol entrainer is 10-150 ml. In the                  step (S2), the concentration of methanol-water                  solution is 30-90 %mass. In the step (S2), the                  solid-liquid ratio of pepper oil resin in the                  step (S1) and methanol-water solution is 1                  g:(1-10) ml. In step (S3), the solid-liquid ratio                  of the oily substance to the sodium hydroxide                  solution is 1 g:(1-10) ml. Preferred Components:                  In step (S4), the solvent system for high-speed                  countercurrent chromatography comprises n-hexane,                  ethyl acetate, methanol and water in volume ratio                  of (1-10):(1-10):(1-5):(1-5).

PN CN107535590-A
TI Storing and preserving sweet persimmon comprises e.g. picking eight to eight mature sweet persimmon, removing insect and fruit, fermenting, pressing, diluting persimmon tannin solution and water and spraying on surface of sorted fruit.
AU XU D
AE LUOTIAN NATURE FOOD CO LTD (LUOT-Non-standard)
GA 2018029027
AB    NOVELTY - Storing and preserving sweet persimmon comprises e.g. (i) picking eight to eight mature sweet persimmon, removing insect and fruit, packaging soft persimmon food or persimmon food, (ii) removing fresh wounds, fermenting, pressing to obtain persimmon tannin liquid, diluting persimmon tannin solution and water, spraying on surface of sorted fruits, packaging, and (iii) configuring two cold stores at -18 to -1 to 0 degrees C, adjusting oxygen gas in two refrigerants to 2-5%, carbon dioxide 3-8% and relative humidity to 85-90% and packaging persimmon fruit.
   USE - The method is useful for storing and preserving sweet persimmon (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The sweet persimmon has smooth surface.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Storing and preserving sweet persimmon comprises (i) picking eight to eight mature sweet persimmon, removing insect and fruit, and packaging soft persimmon food or persimmon food supply, (ii) removing fresh wounds, fermenting, pressing to obtain persimmon tannin liquid, diluting persimmon tannin solution and water in a ratio of 1:100-150, spraying on the surface of sorted fruits, and packaging using 0.06 mm polyethylene film, and (iii) configuring two cold stores at -18 to -1 to 0 degrees C, adjusting oxygen gas in two refrigerants to 2-5%, carbon dioxide 3-8% and relative humidity to 85-90%, packaging persimmon fruit by 0.06 mm polyethylene film, freezing, placing at -18 degrees C cold storage, and then placing hard persimmon fruit supply at -1 to 0 degrees C cold storage.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Method: The method comprises placing sorted fruits on the grass having thickness of 8-15 cm, pendulum 5-7 layers, and spacing of each fruit should not be less than 0.5 cm. The refrigerator is provided with a blower for flowing indoor air.

PN CN114185378-A
TI Double-well hydraulic carbon dioxide compression            energy storage system, has double-well provided with            carbon dioxide storage well that is connected with            water storage well, where connection part of water            storage well is provided with water turbine.
AU BAI W
   JI H
   SONG X
   YAO M
   LU X
   FU K
   YU Z
   HAN W
AE HUANENG GROUP TECHNOLOGY INNOVATION CENT (CHHN-C)
   XIAN THERMAL POWER RES INST CO LTD (CHHN-C)
GA 202243886U
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a water storage well (3) whose                water inlet and water outlet are connected with a                water source through a water pipe. A double-well is                provided with a carbon dioxide storage well that is                connected with the water storage well. A connection                part of the water storage well is provided with a                water turbine. A lower portion of the carbon                dioxide storage well is provided with a liquid                level indicating device (9). A liquid level meter                (8) and the liquid level indicating device are                connected to a control system (7). A water level                meter is provided on the water source. A heat                exchanger (11) is provided in the carbon dioxide                storage well. The heat exchanger is communicated                with a heat storage system (12). A heat exchange                baffle plate (14) and a floating plate are fixed                under the heat exchanger.
   USE - Double-well hydraulic carbon dioxide                compression energy storage system.
   ADVANTAGE - The system uses potential energy of water and                carbon dioxide compression energy to realize energy                conversion so as to improve compression energy                storage efficiency, and reduce need of building                storage tank or dig volume of the hole. The system                saves the floor area and project cost of the                system. The heat storage system transfers the heat                to carbon dioxide liquid for absorbing heat and                gasifying, rapidly expanding, extruding the water                into the carbon dioxide storage well, making the                liquid return to the water storage well side, and                driving the water turbine to generate power.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a                double-well hydraulic carbon dioxide compression                energy storage system operating method.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a                double-well hydraulic carbon dioxide compression                energy storage system.

   Valve (6)
   Control system (7)
   Liquid level meter (8)
   Liquid level indicating device (9)
   Conveying channel (10)
   Heat exchanger (11)
   Heat storage system (12)
   Floating plate (13)
   Heat exchanger baffle plate (14)

TI Cover for carbon dioxide cylinder, has casing that            is comprised with cavity for receiving carbon dioxide            and configured to form cylinder, such that cover main            portion is picked up by empties machine for taking back            returnable bottles.
AU HERRMANN R
   HERMANN R
AE SODAPOP AUSTRIA GMBH (SODA-Non-standard)
   SODAPOP AUSTRIA GMBH (SODA-Non-standard)
   SODAPOP AUSTRIA GMBH (SODA-Non-standard)

AB    NOVELTY - The cover (1) has a casing (3) that is                comprised with a cavity for receiving carbon                dioxide and forming a cylinder, such that the cover                main portion is picked up by an empties machine for                taking back returnable bottles. A fixing device is                configured for fixing the carbon dioxide received                in the cavity of the casing. The fixing device is                comprised with on fixing hook (22), preferably two                fixing hooks, more preferably two fixing hooks, for                fixing carbon dioxide received in the cavity.
   USE - Cover for carbon dioxide cylinder e.g. gas                cartridges used in drinking water bubblers for                producing carbonated table water e.g. soda                water.

The casing is comprised with a guide                rail for guiding cylinder in the cavity in first                direction, thereby making easier to insert the                cylinder into the casing and position the cylinder                exactly in the cavity of the casing. The improper                return of empty cases or cases filled with other                objects is avoided. PN US2020360854-A1; WO2020236533-A1; CN113825559-A; KR2022011679-A; EP3946687-A1; US11325065-B2
TI Recovery of sulfur and carbon dioxide for enhanced oil recovery processes, involves introducing retentate stream comprising hydrogen sulfide to sulfur recovery unit, and processing to produce sulfur stream containing liquid sulfur.
AU VAIDYA M M
   DUVAL S A
   HAMAD F
   BAKER R
   MERKEL T
   LOKHANDWALA K
   BAHAMDAN A A
   AL-OTAIBI F D
   BAHAMMADAN A A
   FAISALDI A
AE SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)
   MEMBRANE TECHNOLOGY & RES INC (MEMB-Non-standard)
   SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)
   MEMBRANE TECHNOLOGY & RES INC (MEMB-Non-standard)
   MEMBRANE TECHNOLOGY & RES INC (MEMB-Non-standard)
   SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)
   MEMBRANE TECHNOLOGY & RES INC (MEMB-Non-standard)
   SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)
GA 2020B3330V
AB    NOVELTY - Recovery of sulfur and carbon dioxide from sour gas stream (603) involves providing a sour gas stream comprising hydrogen sulfide and carbon dioxide to a membrane separation unit, separating the hydrogen sulfide from the carbon dioxide in the membrane separation unit to obtain a retentate stream (621) comprising hydrogen sulfide and a permeate stream (622) comprising carbon dioxide, introducing the retentate stream to a sulfur recovery unit (640), processing the retentate stream in the sulfur recovery unit to produce a sulfur stream (642) comprising liquid sulfur and a tail gas stream (641), introducing the permeate stream (622) to an amine absorption unit (650) and processing the permeate stream in the amine absorption unit to produce an enriched carbon dioxide stream.
   USE - Recovery of sulfur and carbon dioxide from sour gas stream used for enhanced oil recovery processes and carbon dioxide sequestration processes.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables recovery of sulfur and carbon dioxide with excellent efficiency. Since the amount of carbon dioxide in the tail gas treatment process of the Claus plant is reduced, thereby reducing complexity and cost to operate of the tail gas treatment process. The removal of the contaminants from the feed to the amine absorption process can reduce and eliminate foaming and other operability issues in the amine absorption process. Since the combination of membrane separation unit and amine absorption process improves the Claus unit operability and efficiency, the sulfur recovery is improved, and the sulfur dioxide emission from the incinerator stack is minimized.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of recovery of sulfur and carbon dioxide from sour gas stream and system using two-stage permeate-in-series membrane stage configuration.
   Sour gas stream (603)
   Retentate stream (621)
   Permeate stream (622)
   Sulfur recovery unit (640)
   Tail gas stream (641)
   Sulfur stream (642)
   Selective amine absorption unit (650)
   Recovered hydrogen sulfide stream (652)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The retentate stream comprises 80-95 mol% hydrogen sulfide. Preferred Processes: The tail gas stream is recycled to the selective amine absorption unit. The recovery of sulfur and carbon dioxide from two sour gas stream involves introducing the sour gas stream (a1) comprising 10 mol% or more carbon dioxide and hydrogen sulfide and 10 mol% or less nitrogen to membrane separation unit and separating the sour gas stream (a1) to obtain retentate stream comprising hydrogen sulfide and permeate stream comprising carbon dioxide, introducing the permeate stream and the sour gas stream (a2) to the selective amine absorption unit and using an amine absorption process to obtain a recovered hydrogen sulfide stream (652) comprising hydrogen sulfide and an enriched carbon dioxide stream comprising carbon dioxide, recycling the recovered hydrogen sulfide stream to the membrane separation unit and introducing the retentate stream to the sulfur recovery unit and processing the retentate stream using the Claus process to obtain a sulfur stream comprising sulfur.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The membrane separation unit comprises a membrane which is a carbon dioxide-selective membrane. The membrane comprises perfluoropolymer. The membrane separation unit comprises two membrane stages in a retentate-in-series configuration and two membrane stages in a permeate-in-series configuration. Preferred Properties: The membrane has carbon dioxide-hydrogen sulfide selectivity of at least 10 and permeance of at least 500 gas permeation units (gpu).
