PN WO2020202023-A1; EP3946770-A1; BR112021019918-A2; US2022169582-A1
TI Waste processing plant for disposal of organic            material, has reaction conditions control system            downstream catalytic element which is adapted to cause            variations of temperature and pressure, according to            predetermined cycle in catalyst.
AU GAZZETTA A
AE LIFE BREATH SRL (LIFE-Non-standard)
   LIFE BREATH SRL (LIFE-Non-standard)
   LIFE BREATH SRL (LIFE-Non-standard)
GA 202098259N
AB    NOVELTY - The plant (10) has a reactor (12) that                comprises several electric heating elements adapted                to heat a volume inside the reactor to 800 degrees                C and to perform a sublimation reaction of organic                material to obtain a synthetic gas (GR). A                filtering assembly (14) is in fluid communication                with reactor for receiving from the latter                synthetic gas. The catalyst (58) of the methanation                assembly (18) comprises a first catalytic element                comprising a catalyst layer of stone wool and                nickel nanospheres. The second catalytic element                downstream the first catalytic element comprises                several steel micro-tubes, containing copper                micro-filaments. A reaction conditions control                system downstream the second catalytic element is                adapted to cause variations of temperature and                pressure, according to a predetermined cycle, in                the catalyst, in order to extract nitrogen (N2) and                carbon dioxide (C02) from the burnt gas (GC).
   USE - Waste processing plant used for disposal of                organic material for obtaining syngas.
   ADVANTAGE - The plant converts organic material into                syngas up to approximately 100 %, so that an amount                of process residues is produced that is equal to                the quantity of inert material that is initially                contained in the organic material supplied to the                reactor. The treatment plant not emits any solid,                liquid or gaseous polluting agents into the                external environment. The general efficiency of the                sublimation reaction is improved. The strong and                sudden decrease occurs in the temperature of the                syngas so that the formation of dioxins is                prevented. The waste of a process for treating                organic material is minimized. The water, nitrogen,                carbon, oxygen, methane, hydrogen, thermal energy                and electrical energy escape from a treatment plant                according to the invention, all in usable form. The                clear savings in terms of costs and ease of                management of the process are achieved.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The filtering assembly is adapted to filter                the synthetic gas to obtain a filtered gas (GF). A                motor-generator unit (16) comprises a heat engine                (16a) and an associated generator (16b), adapted to                produce electric energy by unit of the combustion                of the filtered gas provided by the filtering                assembly. The heat engine comprises an outlet (16c)                for discharge of a burnt gas produced by the                combustion of the filtered gas. A methanation                assembly comprises a catalyst in fluid                communication with outlet of the heat engine. The                catalyst is adapted to extract carbon dioxide (C02)                and nitrogen (N2) from the burnt gas. An                electrolyser (60) is adapted to be sup-plied with                water and to separate water into oxygen and                hydrogen (H2) by unit of a process of electrolysis.                A methanation reactor (62) is in fluid                communication with the catalyst for receiving                carbon dioxide from it, and in fluid communication                with the electrolyser for receiving hydrogen from                the latter. The methanation reactor is adapted to                produce methane (CH4) by unit of the Sabatier                reaction. A piping system (66) is arranged to                collect condensate water (CW) from the reactor                and/or from the filtering assembly. A purifier (68)                is arranged to receive the collected condensate                water and to purify it obtaining purified water. A                sensor is adapted to measure the composition of the                filtered gas downstream the filtering assembly. The                three-way valve (80) is arranged downstream the                filtering assembly, adapted to receive filtered gas                flowing out of the filtering assembly and to adjust                a flow rate supplied to the reactor and/or to the                motor-generator unit, as a function of the                measurement of sensor. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is                included for a processing method for disposal of                organic material involves inserting organic                material in a reactor, and heating a volume inside                the reactor by unit of electric heating elements to                800 degrees C to perform a sublimation reaction of                organic material and obtain a synthetic gas. The                synthetic gas is filtered to obtain a filtered gas                by unit of a filtering assembly in fluid                communication with reactor for receiving from the                latter synthetic gas. The electric energy is                produced by unit of the combustion of filtered gas                performed by a heat engine associated to a                generator. The heat engine comprises an outlet for                discharge of a burnt gas produced by the combustion                of the filtered gas. The carbon dioxide and                nitro-gen are extracted from the burnt gas by unit                of a catalyst in fluid communication with out-let                of the heat engine. The process of electrolysis of                water is performed by unit of an electrolyser to                obtain hydro-gen. The carbon dioxide extracted by                the catalyst and the hydrogen obtained by unit of                the electrolyser is supplied to a methanation                reactor to produce methane by unit of the Sabatier                reaction. The oxidising gas in turbulent regimen in                the reactor is inserted by unit of an oxidising gas                supply system, in fluid communication with the                reactor. The oxidising gas supply system is                supplied with external air by unit of an external                air conveying system. The oxidising gas supply                system is supplied with methane produced by the                methanation re-actor by unit of a methane conduit.                The collected condensate water is purified by unit                of a purifier to obtain purified water. The                purified water is supplied to a water reservoir                arranged to contain the purified water.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of a                treatment plant.
   Waste processing plant (10)
   Reactor (12)
   Filtering assembly (14)
   Motor-generator unit (16)
   Heat engine (16a)
   Associated generator (16b)
   Outlet (16c)
   Methanation assembly (18)
   Catalyst (58)
   Electrolyser (60)
   Methanation reactor (62)
   Piping system (66)
   Purifier (68)
   Three-way valve (80)
   Condensate water (CW)
   Burnt gas (GC)
   Filtered gas (GF)
   Synthetic gas (GR)

PN CN109929844-A; CN109929844-B
TI Glioma prognosis marker carboxypeptidase vitellogenic type (CPVL) inhibitor and use of inhibitor comprising e.g. plating glioma cell line in six-well plate, culturing in carbon dioxide cell incubator, overgrowing cells and extracting RNA.
AU LV K
   YANG H
   LI X
AE WANNAN MEDICAL COLLEGE FIRST AFFILIATED (WNMC-C)
GA 201958873G
AB    NOVELTY - Glioma prognosis marker carboxypeptidase vitellogenic type (CPVL) inhibitor.
   USE - Used as glioma prognosis marker carboxypeptidase vitellogenic type (CPVL) inhibitor.
   ADVANTAGE - The inhibitor verifies that CPVL gene participates in regulation of glioma cell apoptosis through the interferon- gamma signaling pathway, provides theoretical data and basis for proliferation and apoptosis of glioma cells.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for kit comprising inhibitor; and #use of glioma prognostic marker CPVL inhibitor comprising (i) plating glioma cell line in 6-well plate, culturing at 37 degrees C with 5% carbon dioxide cell incubator, overgrowing cells, extracting total RNA of each cell, applying real-time PCR to different gliomas, detecting of CPVL gene of cell, extracting total protein of each cell, detecting protein expressed by CPVL gene in different glioma cells by Western Blot technique, (ii) extracting total RNA of glioma specimens, detecting CPVL gene in human glioma tissues and normal brain tissues by real-time PCR, extracting total protein of tissue specimen, detecting protein expressed by CPVL gene of the glioma tissue by Western Blot, embedding glioma specimens in paraffin, freezing, sectioning, observing distribution of CPVL gene in glioma specimens by immunohistochemistry, further comparing correlation between CPVL gene expression distribution and glioma pathological stage classification, combining with clinical survival follow-up data, calculating directly or indirectly cumulative survival probability of 3 years as end point of glioma disease death, according to real-time PCR expression data of CPVL gene of each specimen, dividing specimens into high expression group and low expression group, plotting survival curve as x-axis with cumulative probability as y-axis, analyzing correlation between CPVL gene expression and clinical prognosis in patients with glioma, (iii) infecting sample glioma cells with a lentivirus infection of glioma cell line with high CPVL gene expression, (iv) trypsinizing cells in logarithmic growth phase, re-suspending complete medium into cell suspension and counting, inoculating three cell 6-well plates at gradient density of 100, 200, and 300 cells per well, and gently rotating, evenly dispersing cells, culturing for 14 days in 37 degrees C with 5% carbon dioxide cell incubator, observing that when macroscopic clones appeared in the culture dish, terminating culture, discarding supernatant, washing twice with phosphate buffered saline, adding pure methanol or 1:3 acetic acid/methanol 5 ml, fixing for 15 minutes, removing fixing solution, adding crystal violet staining solution for 15 minutes, slowly washing staining solution with running water, air drying, observing staining and cloning size, counting and calculating colony formation rate, (v) performing cell proliferation assay on infected glioma cells by MTT assay, expecting detection for 5 days, trypsinizing cells in logarithmic growth phase, re-suspending complete medium into cell suspension and counting, seeding six cells in 96-well plate at 2000 cells/well in slow virus group and CPVL slow virus group, where the culture system is 100 mu l/well, during the plating process, it is necessary to ensure that the number of cells added per well is the same, and using another 6 blank wells with only 100 mu l of complete medium as reference values, placing total of 5 plates at the beginning of the experiment, culturing in 37 degrees C with 5% carbon dioxide cell incubator, testing plate from the second day of culture by MTT every day, where MTT test method comprises adding 20 mu l of 5 mg/ml MTT to well 4 hours before the termination of the culture, without changing the solution, completely aspirating culture solution after 4 hours, taking care not to absorb the formazan particles at bottom of well plate, adding 100 mu l of dimethyl sulfoxide, dissolving formazan particles, shaking oscillator for 2-5 minutes, detecting OD value by microplate reader at 490/570 nm, measuring cell proliferation, using the Celigo cell counting method and expecting to be detected for 5 days, trypsinizing cells in logarithmic growth phase, re-suspending complete medium into cell suspension and counting, inoculating 6 wells into 96-well plates at 2000 cells/well in control slow virus group and CPVL slow virus group, where each group had 3 replicate wells, and the culture system is 100 mu l/well, during the plating process, ensuring that the number of cells added to each well is the same, placing in a 37 degrees C with 5% carbon dioxide incubator, reading Celigo test plate once a day from the second day after plating, continuously testing the plate for 5 days, accurately calculating number of green fluorescent cells in each scanning well by adjusting input parameters of analysis settings, statistically plotting data to plot 5-day cell proliferation curve, (vi) performing cell cycle assays on infected glioma cells using propidium iodide-fluorescence activated cell sorting method, detecting cell cycle, inoculating cells of two experimental groups with 3 wells in 6-well plate of cells, attaching cells to wall, starving for 14 hours, adding to complete medium for culture until cells entered logarithmic growth phase, trypsinizing cells, collecting cells by centrifugation, discarding supernatant, re-suspending with pre-cooled phosphate-buffered saline, centrifuging again, discarding supernatant for 2 times, adding pre-cooled 70% ethanol, fixing for 3 hours, collecting cells by centrifugation, washing cells once with 1 ml of phosphate-buffered saline, adding 500 mu l phosphate-buffered saline comprises 50 mu g/ml propidium iodide, 100 mu g/ml RNase A, 0.2% Triton X-100 (RTM: Polyethylene glycol p-(1,1,3,3-tetramethylbutyl), incubating at 4 degrees C for 30 minutes in dark, detecting by flow cytometry using standard procedures.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Components: The glioma cells are U87MG, U251 and LN382. Preferred Method: the use of glioma prognostic marker CPVL inhibitor comprising (vii) infecting apoptosis of glioma cells with detected by Annexin V-APC single staining, inoculating cells of two experimental groups with 3 wells in a 6-well plate of cells, where the cells are in good growth state, trypsinizing and centrifuging, washing with phosphate-buffered saline at room temperature (1500 revolutions/minute for 5 minutes), re-suspending cells with 500 mu l of binding buffer, adding 10 mu l of Annexin-V medium buffer and 1 mu l of Annexin V fluorescent antibody, incubating at room temperature for 15 minutes in the dark, centrifuging, washing with phosphate-buffered saline, resuspending cells in 500 mu l of binding buffer, analyzing by flow cytometry within 1 hours and analyzing and (viii) performing recovery experiment, verifying that CPVL gene regulatory mechanism silences the CPVL gene in glioma cells, using signal transducer and activator of transcription 1 (STAT1) gene, inhibiting expression of STAT1 gene, performing assays, using above the steps, recording cell proliferation data after the detection, silencing CPVL gene in U251 cells, using STAT1 inhibitor, inhibiting expression of STAT1 gene, adding nifuroxazide for experiment, using above the steps, recording cell proliferation data after the detection.
   ACTIVITY - Cytostatic.

PN CN114549228-A
TI Method for online calculating carbon emission of provincial power grid of wide area measuring system in thermal power plant, involves adding carbon dioxide emission to obtain carbon dioxide emission total amount of provincial power grid power generation facility.
AU FANG H
   ZHU S
   ZHOU J
   GONG X
   ZHAO X
   WANG R
   HUANG M
   ZHU X
   ZHANG Y
   FU A
   YANG S
   MA X
   CHEN Y
   HE Y
   DONG G
AE STATE GRID CORP CHINA (SGCC-C)
   STATE GRID CHONGQING ELECTRIC POWER CO (SGCC-C)
   ELECTRIC POWER RES INST STATE GRID CHONG (SGCC-C)
GA 202281854Y
AB    NOVELTY - The method enables judging(S1) whether a current time is an end time of one calculation period. A carbon emission data of the thermal power unit is read(S2) in a provincial power grid network collected by a wide area measuring system and a power exchange data of an interprovincial section. A carbon dioxide discharge amount of the thermal power unit in the provincial power grid network is calculated(S3) in an online. A carbon dioxide emission generated by the purchased electric power is calculated(S4) in the online. The carbon dioxide emission is added(S5) to obtain a carbon dioxide emission total amount of a provincial power grid power generation facility.
   USE - Method for online calculating carbon emission of a provincial power grid of a wide area measuring system in a thermal power plant.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables fully using a wide area measurement system based on a characteristic of an uniform time scale collection information for accurately obtaining the carbon dioxide emission of each carbon emission source in a same period of a power grid. The method enables realizing a strong operability, comprehensive consideration factors, reasonable solution and high engineering practical value.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow diagram illustrating a method for online calculating carbon emission of a provincial power grid of a wide area measuring system in a thermal power plant. (Drawing includes non-English language text).
   Judging whether current time is end time of one calculation period (S1)
   Reading carbon emission data of thermal power unit in provincial power grid network collected by wide area measuring system and power exchange data of interprovincial section (S2)
   Calculating carbon dioxide discharge amount of thermal power unit in provincial power grid network in online (S3)

PN CN111602766-A
TI Supercritical carbon dioxide extrusion to inactivate rice bran lipase, by adding carbon dioxide as foaming agent during extrusion, destroying cell structure, exposing grease, inactivating lipase, and storing heat-sensitive components.
AU WANG T
   ZHANG X
   YU C
   TANG H
   CHEN J
   YU D
   CHEN K
   CHEN Y
AE UNIV NORTHEAST AGRIC (UNEA-C)
GA 202087386V
AB    NOVELTY - Method for supercritical carbon dioxide extrusion to puffed inactivate rice bran lipase, involves adding a certain pressure of carbon dioxide as a foaming agent during extrusion, destroying the cell structure, completely exposing the grease, effectively inactivating the lipase, and allowing the heat-sensitive components to storage. The method includes (a) pre-processing by cleaning and screening a certain amount of rice bran, crushing and screening, (b) supercritical carbon dioxide extrusion expansion by putting the processed rice bran into the extrusion expansion machine, and adjusting the carbon dioxide pressure, so that the pressure in the barrel reaches 8-10 MPa, adjusting the barrel temperature at 60-100 degrees C, with the screw speed at 100-180 rpm, and the die hole diameter at 4-12 mm, and (c) determining nutrient composition by subjecting the supercritical carbon dioxide extruded rice bran to the determination of vitamin E and gamma -oryzanol.
   USE - Method for supercritical carbon dioxide extrusion to puffed inactivate rice bran lipase.
   ADVANTAGE - The method ensures high-temperature and short-term treatment process extrusion, which destroys the cell structure and fully exposes the oil, and high temperature and high shear force will cause a certain loss of heat-sensitive ingredients in the food. The method utilizes a supercritical fluid which is a solvent-free system, non-toxic, and harmless, and is low production cost. The density of supercritical carbon dioxide is changed in a wide range, so it has a strong ability to dissolve many substances, and protect heat-sensitive components and unstable natural products from being destroyed. The method effectively inactivates lipase activity in rice bran by supercritical carbon dioxide and stabilizes rice bran. The method well stores heat-sensitive components in the materials, and determines vitamin E content of 150.45 mg/kg and the gamma -sitosterol of 2.81 mg/g. The method optimizes the technical parameters of supercritical carbon dioxide extrusion and expansion, and finally obtains stable rice bran.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Conditions: In step (b), the carbon dioxide pressure is adjusted to 8.5-9.5 MPa, and supercritical carbon dioxide extrusion expansion is performed. In step (b), the temperature of the barrel is adjusted to 70-90 degrees C to perform supercritical carbon dioxide extrusion and expansion. In step (b), the screw speed is adjusted to 120-160 rpm to perform supercritical carbon dioxide extrusion expansion. In step (b), the die hole diameter is adjusted to 6-10 mm, and supercritical carbon dioxide extrusion is performed.
DC D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.)

PN WO2018226909-A1; AR112190-A1; CA3066580-A1; AU2018280161-A1; US2020156859-A1; AU2021282489-A1
TI Pressurized spray system used in aerosol paint product, comprises sealed container, and aqueous composition of solubilizer of primary, secondary or tertiary amine disposed within sealed container, and solubilized or gaseous carbon dioxide.
AU HALSTEAD J M
   ROBEY L E
AE SWIMC LLC (SHEW-C)
   HALSTEAD J M (HALS-Individual)
   ROBEY L E (ROBE-Individual)
   SWIMC LLC (SHEW-C)
   SWIMC LLC (SHEW-C)
GA 201899596V
AB    NOVELTY - Pressurized spray system comprises: a sealed container; and an aqueous composition disposed within the sealed container, where the composition comprises a solubilizer comprising at least one of a primary amine, a secondary amine or a tertiary amine; and solubilized carbon dioxide and gaseous carbon dioxide, where the solubilized carbon dioxide is in equilibrium with the gaseous carbon dioxide.
   USE - The system is useful in the aerosol paint product (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The system: matches or exceeds the performance of conventional systems while being both safer and environmentally friendly; operates by pressurizing a container with a propellant composition having a pressurized gas phase in equilibrium with a bulk liquid phase; utilizes the carbon dioxide that is relatively safe for exposure to humans, is non-flammable, has low odor, and is less environmentally damaging than chlorofluorocarbons or other more potent greenhouse gases e.g. methane; utilizes the solubilizer that enhances the solubility of carbon dioxide in water-based composition by using hydrogen bonding interactions to stabilize the dissolved carbon dioxide molecules, is a liquid over the entire expected operating temperatures of the system, thus preventing undesirable fluctuations in pressure and improving the lifespan of the system by preventing the loss of carbon dioxide when the solubilizer undesirably freezes or evaporates; and utilizes the rheology modifier that decreases the shear rate and improves the quality of the paint coating.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) forming the system, comprising adding the solubilizer to water to form a propellant mixture, sealing the propellant mixture inside a sealed container, and pressurizing the sealed container by adding carbon dioxide to the sealed container at a pressure of 100-140 psi; and
   (2) an aerosol paint product comprising the sealed container comprising a can, a valve cup with a valve assembly, a dip tube, and an actuator, and a propellant mixture and a water-based paint composition disposed within the sealed container, where the propellant mixture comprises water, the solubilizer and carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The system further comprises: a benefit agent comprising a water-based paint; at least one of a rheology modifier, a defoamer, a flow and leveling additive or a filler; 1-20 wt.% of a secondary propellant comprising dimethyl ether; and 0.03-1.5 wt.% of ammonium hydroxide. The sealed container comprises 1-10 wt.% of carbon dioxide. Preferred Method: The method further comprises: the step of including a benefit agent in the sealed container, where the benefit agent is a latex paint; and the step of adding at least one of a rheology modifier, a defoamer, a flow and leveling additive or a filler to the sealed container.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The solubilizer: comprises at least one of triethanolamine, diethanolamine or monoethanolamine; is a liquid at 10-30 degrees C; has a water solubility of at least 10 g/l at 20 degrees C; and is modified by a silane treatment agent. The aqueous composition comprises 2% or less of the solubilizer. The aqueous composition comprises 1-10 (preferably 2-3) wt.% of carbon dioxide. The aqueous composition comprises 5-50% of water.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The water-based paint comprises at least one of a styrene acrylic latex paint, vinyl acrylic latex paint, ethylene vinyl acetate latex paint or a polyurethane dispersion paint.

PN WO2018049419-A2; WO2018049419-A3; CN109715281-A; EP3509981-A2; US2019217256-A1; JP2019529107-W; EP3509981-A4; US11318427-B2; US2022203311-A1; JP7106546-B2; CN109715281-B; JP2022132413-A
TI Carbonation apparatus for carbonating mixed input            flow of pressurized and refrigerated carbon dioxide and            water molecules, has first micromesh net to define            outer surface that responds to flow of water and carbon            dioxide molecules.
AU FANTAPPIE G
   KAMALI F
   MACRELLINO D
   MIYAMOTO J
   MIYAMOT J
AE SUN DESERT CORP (SUND-Non-standard)
   SUN DESERT CORP (SUND-Non-standard)
   SUN DESERT CORP (SUND-Non-standard)
   DRINKSTATION INC (DRIN-Non-standard)
   PEPSICO INC (PEPS-C)
   PEPSICO INC (PEPS-C)
GA 2018216651
AB    NOVELTY - The apparatus has a first carbonation chamber                (31) and a first cartridge mounted within a central                chamber. The first micromesh net defines an outer                surface that responds to the flow of water                molecules and carbon dioxide molecules impacting                upon and passing through the first micromesh net by                generating a passive polarizing field. The passive                polarizing field is provided with a polarizing                influence on water molecules passing through the                first micromesh net to enhance bonding between the                water molecules and the carbon dioxide molecules                within the central chamber.
   USE - Carbonation apparatus for carbonating mixed                input flow of pressurized and refrigerated carbon                dioxide molecules and water molecules to form                carbonated drinking beverages such as soda or                sparkling water in food and beverage                industry.
   ADVANTAGE - The apparatus for the enhancement of                carbonation of water or other beverages by                utilizing low temperature and high pressure is                provided. The low-pressure area creates a venturi                effect, thus enhances the flow of the carbon                dioxide and water and facilitates the mixing of the                carbon dioxide and water molecules to initial                carbonation levels prior to entry into the                carbonation chambers. The enhanced mixture of the                water and carbon dioxide facilitates the                functionality of the carbonation chambers by                stabilizing the flow into the first carbonation                chamber, and avoids multiple laminar flows that can                mitigate the bonding of the water and carbon                dioxide molecules in the first carbonation chamber.                The passive polarization created as a consequence                of the interaction of the molecules and the net                enhances the dipole bonding between the water and                carbon dioxide molecules. The surface catalyst                helps to produce more carbonic acid and fewer free                carbon dioxide molecules, resulting in a                carbonation that persists for a much longer lasting                period of time.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a perspective view of the                carbonation apparatus.
   Input port (22, 26)
   Output port (24, 28)
   Carbonation chambers (31, 33)

PN WO2019065879-A1; JP2019066170-A; JP6665909-B2; CN111182784-A; SG11202002852-A1; EP3677114-A1; US2020282356-A1; EP3677114-A4; US11471822-B2
TI Air conditioning device for adjusting composition            of air inside storage case, has pressure regulator            which is configured to adjust pressure of air supplied            from air pump to one of first separation portion and            second separation portion.
AU NORITAKA K
   NAOHIRO T
   HIDENORI M
   KAMEI N
   TANAKA N
   MATSUI H
AE DAIKIN IND LTD (DAIK-C)
   DAIKIN KOGYO KK (DAIK-C)
   DAIKIN IND LTD (DAIK-C)
   DAIKIN IND LTD (DAIK-C)
   DAIKIN IND LTD (DAIK-C)
GA 201930702S
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a first separation portion (41)                which separates the supply air whose composition is                different from the outside air outside the outside                of a storage room (1) from the outside air by the                gas separation membrane. A first composition                control unit (40) is configured to supply the                inside of the storage room with air. A second                separation portion (61) is configured to separate                discharge air having a different composition from                the air in the storage room from the air in the                storage room by the gas separation membrane. A                second composition control unit (60) is configured                to discharge the discharge air to the outside of                the storage room. An air pump (36,37) is configured                to supply air to the first separation portion and                the second separation portion. A pressure regulator                (39,46,66) is configured to adjust the pressure of                air supplied from the air pump to one of the first                separation portion and the second separation                portion.
   USE - Air conditioning device for adjusting                composition of air inside storage case.
   ADVANTAGE - The pressure of air supplied from the air pump                to the gas separation membrane is adjusted by                pressure regulator. Thus, the pressure of the gas                passing through the gas separation membrane is                changed even if the gas flow rate of the air pump                is constant. The separation performance of the gas                separation membrane is adjusted to the required gas                components. The configuration in which the                separation performance is adjusted to the required                gas component is easily realized.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The pressure regulator is constituted by a                valve mechanism provided on the downstream side of                the gas separation membrane. The pressure regulator                is provided on the downstream side of the                separation membrane of the second composition                regulator. The pressure regulator is provided                downstream of the separation membrane of the first                composition control unit and downstream of the                separation membrane of the second composition                control unit. The pressure regulator is provided on                the air inlet side of the air pump. The pressure                regulator is provided on the outflow side of the                air from the air pump. An inverter is configured to                change the frequency of the current supplied to the                air pump to change the flow rate. The nitrogen,                oxygen and carbon dioxide are separated from the                air in the storage room. The low oxygen                concentration gas is provided with nitrogen                concentration higher than the air in the storage                and lower oxygen concentration and carbon dioxide                concentration. An adsorption section is provided                with an adsorbent capable of generating high oxygen                concentration gas with nitrogen concentration lower                than in the storage air and high oxygen                concentration and carbon dioxide concentration. A                concentration measuring device is configured to                measure the concentration of carbon dioxide and                oxygen in the storage. The pressure regulator is                configured to control the pressure of the air                supplied from the air pump so that the measured                value of the concentration meter approaches the                target value of carbon dioxide concentration and                the target value of oxygen concentration determined                in advance. A controller (110) is configured to                control the separation pressure of the gas                separation membrane under control. The controller                is provided with a carbon dioxide concentration in                the inside of the storage higher than a target                value. The first composition adjustment unit is                configured to generate a low oxygen concentration                gas having a lower oxygen concentration than the                air outside the storage. The separation pressure of                the gas separation membrane is reduced by the                second composition control unit to reduce the                discharge amount of gas out of the storage. The                second composition adjusting unit is configured to                supply the air after the composition adjustment                into the inside of the storage. The controller is                configured to control the first composition control                unit, when the carbon dioxide concentration of the                storage is lower than the target value and the                oxygen concentration of the storage is higher than                the target value. The low oxygen concentration gas                whose oxygen concentration is lower than the                outside air is generated. The separation pressure                of the gas separation membrane of the second                composition adjusting unit is increased to increase                the amount of air after composition adjustment                returned to the inside of the storage. The                controller is configured to operate the outside air                in the storage space when the carbon dioxide                concentration of the storage room is higher than                the target value and the oxygen concentration of                the storage room is lower than the target value by                raising the separation pressure of the gas                separation membrane of the second composition                control unit. An operation to discharge carbon                dioxide gas is provided with carbon dioxide                concentration higher than the air in the storage                room to the outside space or high oxygen having an                oxygen concentration higher than the outside air                generated by the first composition control unit.                The separation pressure of the gas separation                membrane of the second composition adjustment unit                is reduced to reduce the amount of air after                composition adjustment discharged to the outside of                the storage.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the air                conditioning device for adjusting composition of                air inside the storage case.
   Storage room (1)
   Air pump (36,37)
   Pressure regulator (39,46,66)
   Composition adjusting unit (40,60)
   Separation portions (41,61)
   Controller (110)

PT P
PN JP2020163246-A
TI Carbon dioxide capture device of hydrocarbon manufacturing apparatus, has control unit which controls supply of exhaust gas to recovery device, injection of purge gas, and decompression inside recovery device.
AU OGASAWARA K
   YAMAMOTO S
AE TOYOTA CHUO KENKYUSHO KK (TOYW-C)
GA 202098233G
AB    NOVELTY - The device (1) has a collector that houses an adsorbent (11a,12a,13a,14a) that adsorbs carbon dioxide. An exhaust gas supply unit supplies exhaust gas containing carbon dioxide to the collector. A purge gas injection unit injects purge gas inside the collector. A decompression pump (45) decompresses the inside of the collector. A control unit (55) controls the supply of the exhaust gas to a recovery device (11-14), the injection of the purge gas, and the decompression inside the recovery device. The control unit supplies the exhaust gas to the collector to adsorb carbon dioxide to the adsorbent, injects the purge gas, and extracts carbon dioxide adsorbed on the adsorbent from the inside of the collector. The inside of the collector is depressurized from the start or the middle of the desorption step to the end of the desorption step, and the injection amount of the purge gas is relatively reduced from the middle during the depressurization inside the collector.
   USE - Carbon dioxide capture device of hydrocarbon manufacturing apparatus (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The composition fluctuation of a mixed gas of carbon dioxide and purge gas taken out from a recovery device is suppressed.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The control unit decompresses the inside of the collector from the middle of the desorption step, and injects the purge gas before depressurizing the inside of the collector. A heating unit heats the purge gas before being injected from the purge gas injection unit, and the control unit controls whether or not to heat the purge gas to be injected into the collector. The heated purge gas is injected, when the injection amount of the purge gas is relatively reduced from the middle. The heating unit is a portion of the flow path through which the purge gas flows, and heats the purge gas by heat exchange with a heat medium that raises the temperature of the adsorbent. A concentration estimation unit estimates the carbon dioxide concentration of the mixed gas of the carbon dioxide discharged from the recovery device and the purge gas. The control unit uses the estimated carbon dioxide concentration to control the temperature of the purge gas injected into the collector in the desorption step. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a carbon dioxide capture method involves supplying exhaust gas containing carbon dioxide to the collector that houses an adsorbent capable of adsorbing carbon dioxide, and the adsorbent adsorbs carbon dioxide. The carbon dioxide adsorbed on the adsorbent from the inside of the collector is extracted by injecting purge gas inside the collector and depressurizing the inside of the collector. The inside of the collector is depressurized from the start or the middle of the desorption step to the end of the desorption step, and the injection amount of the purge gas is reduced from the middle during the depressurization inside the collector.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the methane production apparatus. (Drawing includes non-English language text)
   Carbon dioxide capture device (1)
   Recovery device (11-14)
   Adsorbent (11a,12a,13a,14a)
   Decompression pump (45)
   Control unit (55)
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

PT P
PN JP2020030893-A; JP6698763-B2
TI Liquefied carbon dioxide recovery type fuel cell power generation system, has compressor that is driven by electric power generated by fuel cell and compresses carbon dioxide gas separated by carbon dioxide gas separation unit.
AU KAWABATA Y
   MATSUZAKI Y
AE TOKYO GAS CO LTD (TOLG-C)
GA 202017257T
AB    NOVELTY - The system (10A) has a fuel cell (12,14) containing a carbon compound and supplied to the fuel electrode (12A,14A), and an oxidant gas containing oxygen and supplied to the air electrode, which generates power and discharges off-gas. A carbon dioxide gas separation unit (26) separates carbon dioxide gas from the off-gas. A waste heat input type absorption refrigerator (36) cools water using the off-gas as a driving heat source to generate cooling water. One of an electric turbo refrigerator is driven by electric power obtained by power generation of the fuel cell and generates cold heat. A compressor (68) is driven by electric power generated by the fuel cell and compresses the carbon dioxide gas separated by the carbon dioxide gas separation unit. A cooling device (70) cools the compressed carbon dioxide gas with at least one of the cooling water and the cold heat to generate liquefied carbon dioxide.
   USE - Liquefied carbon dioxide recovery type fuel cell power generation system for performing electric power generation using carbon compound fuel.
   ADVANTAGE - The system is capable of efficiently liquefying carbon dioxide gas separated from exhaust gas of a fuel cell and collecting it as liquefied carbon dioxide, which improves power generation efficiency. The system can drive efficiently an exhaust heat injection or throwing-in type or mold absorption refrigerator or adsorption-type refrigeration device, since the off gas is high temperature. The compressor can be driven with the electric power electric-power-generated by the fuel cell. The system is not accompanied by a transmission loss during an electric power generation, since there is no need of using the external or utility power from a commercial power source. The compressor can be driven efficiently, which continues at the time of the power failure of commercial power source. The highly efficient fuel cell power generation is continued, which collects or recovers liquefied carbon dioxides. The density or concentration fall of the fuel gas supplied to a second fuel electrode can be small, and can improve generating efficiency in a second fuel electrode, since the quantity of the water vapor or steam contained in a 1st fuel electrode off gas decreases. The energy losses are reduced, and are efficient at alternating current power. The cooling water circular flow paths are reduced, or the circulating flowing quantity of cooling water is made to increase. The quantity of the carbon dioxide gas which remains without liquefying inside the cooling device is indirectly measurable. The amount of air electrode off-gas flows branched to branch air electrode off-gas pipe or tube can be adjusted easily.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of the fuel cell power generation system. (Drawing includes non-English language text)
   Liquefied carbon dioxide recovery type fuel cell power generation system (10A)
   Fuel cell (12,14)
   Fuel electrode (12A,14A)
   Carbon dioxide gas separation unit (26)
   Waste heat input type absorption refrigerator (36)
   Compressor (68)
   Cooling device (70)
DC X16 (Electrochemical Storage)
MC X16-C01; X16-M
IP H01M-008/04; H01M-008/0662; H01M-008/12; H01M-008/249

PT P
PN US2020247733-A1; US11174208-B2
TI Chemical production of ethylene, hydrogen and methanol, comprises e.g. compressing reaction product stream to pressure, and separating carbon dioxide from reaction product stream in separation unit to provide upgraded stream having olefin.
AU BEAUCHAMP D
   LU X
   RAFATI M
AE 8 RIVERS CAPITAL LLC (ERIV-C)
GA 202074957T
AB    NOVELTY - Chemical production, comprises: providing at least one hydrocarbon and carbon dioxide into a reactor at a molar ratio so that the quantity of provided carbon dioxide is in excess of the stoichiometrically required quantity for complete reaction with the hydrocarbon; reacting the hydrocarbon with the carbon dioxide in the reactor in the presence of a catalyst to form a reaction product stream comprising at least one olefin, water and carbon dioxide; passing the reaction product stream through a primary heat exchanger to withdraw heat; removing water and optionally any further condensates present in the reaction product stream; compressing the reaction product stream to a pressure of at least 20 bar; and separating carbon dioxide from the reaction product stream in a separation unit to provide an upgraded stream comprising the olefin.
   USE - The method and system are useful for chemical production (claimed) of ethylene, hydrogen and methanol.
   ADVANTAGE - The method and system: reduce the energy requirement for the chemical production, prevent catalyst deactivation, consume (instead of producing) carbon dioxide and generate other valuable commodity materials e.g. hydrogen gas (e.g. via a water gas shift reaction) and/or methanol (e.g. via reverse water gas shift followed by methanol synthesis); provide a sustainable and environmentally friendly method and system for the production of ethylene, hydrogen and methanol, which are the three most fundamental building blocks for the chemical industry globally; enable industry to produce ethylene using less energy, while producing hydrogen, methanol, and even further chemical products; and consume carbon dioxide by essentially fixing it into compounds and ultimate use will not re-emit that carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a system comprising a catalytic reactor (310) configured for reacting the hydrocarbon with carbon dioxide at greater than or equal to 450 degrees C to form a reaction product stream including at least carbon dioxide and the olefin, a line configured for delivery of the hydrocarbon into the catalytic reactor, a line (303) configured for delivery of carbon dioxide into the catalytic reactor, a primary heat exchanger (362) configured to receive the reaction product stream from the catalytic reactor and withdraw heat, a gas-liquid separation unit (335) configured for removal of water and optionally other condensates from the reaction product stream, a compressor (361) configured for compressing the reaction product stream to a pressure of at least 20 bars, a carbon dioxide separation unit (configured for receiving the reaction product stream after at least one stage of compression and for separating at least a portion of the carbon dioxide from the reaction product stream to provide an upgraded stream comprising the one or more olefins, and a line configured for delivering at least a portion of the carbon dioxide separated from the reaction product stream in the carbon dioxide separation unit to the reactor while being heated with at least a portion of the heat withdrawn from the reaction product stream in the primary heat exchanger.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The figure shows a schematic view of the system.
   Line (303)
   Catalytic reactor (310)
   Gas-liquid separation unit (335)
   Compressor (361)
   Primary heat exchanger (362)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method further comprises heating at least a portion of the carbon dioxide separated from the reaction product stream using the heat withdrawn from the reaction product stream to form a stream of heated carbon dioxide and recycling the stream of heated carbon dioxide back into the reactor. Recycling the stream of heated carbon dioxide back into the reactor comprises at least one of injecting the stream of heated carbon dioxide directly into the reactor, injecting the stream of heated carbon dioxide into a carbon dioxide source, injecting the stream of heated carbon dioxide into a line delivering carbon dioxide from a carbon dioxide source to the reactor, injecting the stream of heated carbon dioxide into a source of the hydrocarbon, or injecting the stream of heated carbon dioxide into a line delivering the hydrocarbon from a source of the hydrocarbon to the reactor. At least a portion of the stream of heated carbon dioxide is passed through a line heater configured for transfer of heat from the stream of heated carbon dioxide to at least one stream being passed into the reactor. The reaction product stream is at 500-800 degrees C. The reaction product stream exiting the primary heat exchanger is at 200-400 degrees C. Removing water and optionally any further condensates present in the reaction product comprises passing the reaction product stream through a condensing unit. Heating at least a portion of the carbon dioxide separated from the reaction product stream using the heat withdrawn from the reaction product stream comprises passing the carbon dioxide through a secondary heat exchanger against a circulating stream that is heated in the primary heat exchanger using the heat withdrawn from the reaction product stream. A portion of the heat withdrawn from the reaction product stream in the primary heat exchanger is used for heating at least one of the reactor, a carbon dioxide source, a carbon dioxide line delivering carbon dioxide from a carbon dioxide source to the reactor, a source of the hydrocarbon, or a line delivering the hydrocarbon from a source of the hydrocarbon to the reactor. A portion of the heat withdrawn from the reaction product stream in the primary heat exchanger is used for heating one or both of a pressurized steam stream and a pressurized carbon dioxide stream for use in power generation in a closed loop or semi-open loop power production system, where a working stream is repeatedly compressed and expanded for power production. A portion of the heat withdrawn from the reaction product stream in the primary heat exchanger is used for heating a steam stream that is injected into the reactor. The method further comprises processing the upgraded stream comprising the olefin to provide at least one chemical product, where the processing comprises at least one of passing the upgraded stream comprising the olefin through an adsorber to absorb any water in the upgraded stream comprising the olefin, passing the upgraded stream comprising the olefin through a refrigeration unit to cool the upgraded stream comprising the one or more olefins to a temperature of less than or equal to 50 degrees C, passing the upgraded stream comprising the olefin through a de-methanizer unit, and passing the upgraded stream comprising the olefin through a de-ethanizer unit. The method comprises injecting steam into the reactor. Preferred Components: The reaction product stream comprises 10-60 mass% carbon dioxide, based on the total mass of the reaction product exiting the reactor. The hydrocarbon comprises at least one of ethane, propane or butane.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

TI Dry aging system using oxygen, useful to produce very good dry-aging meat, comprises e.g. a refrigerator chamber, at least one dry aging chamber, a vacuum pump for generating vacuum state inside of the dry aging chamber, and oxygen tank.
AU KANG W H
   KIM S M
AE KANG W H (KANG-Individual)
   KIM S M (KIMS-Individual)
   CHUN Y J (CHUN-Individual)
   KIM S M (KIMS-Individual)
   KIM S M (KIMS-Individual)
   CHUN Y (CHUN-Individual)
   KIM S (KIMS-Individual)
   KIM S M (KIMS-Individual)
   CHUN Y J (CHUN-Individual)
   YOUN J C (YOUN-Individual)
GA 201813228N
AB    NOVELTY - Dry aging system using oxygen comprises e.g. a refrigerator chamber (110), at least one dry aging chamber (120) mounted in the refrigerating chamber, a vacuum pump (130) for generating vacuum state inside of the dry aging chamber, an oxygen tank (140) for storing oxygen, a carbon dioxide tank (150) for storing carbon dioxide, a gas mixing device for mixing oxygen and carbon dioxide, a cooling pipe for cooling the mixed gas and supplying the cooled mixed gas into the inside of the dry aging chamber, and a humidity controller for supplying moisture into the dry aging chamber.
   USE - The dry aging system is useful to produce very good dry-aging meat.
   ADVANTAGE - The system reduces dryness, and is economical.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Dry aging system using oxygen comprises either: a refrigerator chamber (110), at least one dry aging chamber (120) mounted in the refrigerating chamber, a vacuum pump (130) for generating vacuum state inside of the dry aging chamber, an oxygen tank (140) for storing oxygen, a carbon dioxide tank (150) for storing carbon dioxide, a gas mixing device for mixing oxygen and carbon dioxide, a cooling pipe for cooling the mixed gas and supplying the cooled mixed gas into the inside of the dry aging chamber, provided in the refrigerating chamber, a humidity controller for supplying moisture into the dry aging chamber, and a control unit to supply the mixed gas into the dry-aging chamber using the gas mixing device for the re-set value after vacuuming in interior of the dry aging chamber by operating the vacuum pump when ratio of oxygen and carbon dioxide in each dry aging chamber is periodically detected and different from the set value, and supplying the ratio of oxygen to carbon dioxide to each dry aging chamber by setting ratio of oxygen to carbon dioxide, and separately controlling each of the one or more dry aging chambers; or a refrigerator chamber, at least one dry aging chamber, a vacuum pump, an oxygen tank, a carbon dioxide tank, a cooling pipe, a moisture regulator for supplying water into the at least one dry aging chamber, and a control unit. The system further comprises a nitrogen tank to supply nitrogen. The gas mixing apparatus mixes oxygen, carbon dioxide, and nitrogen. The control unit: supplies the mixed gas into dry aging chamber using the gas mixing device for the re-set value after vacuuming in interior of the dry aging chamber by operating the vacuum pump when ratio of oxygen, carbon dioxide and nitrogen in each dry aging chamber is periodically detected and different from the set value, and supplying predetermined ratio of oxygen, carbon dioxide, and nitrogen to each dry aging chamber; and increases humidity by controlling the humidity control device, and supplies the mixed gas by lowering nitrogen ratio and increasing oxygen ratio, and fixing the carbon dioxide ratio. The carbon dioxide ratio is fixed and the oxygen ratio is lowered, the nitrogen gas ratio is increased to supply the mixed gas, and the humidity control device is controlled to lower the humidity.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the dry aging system.
   Refrigerator chamber (110)
   Dry aging chamber (120)
   Vacuum pump (130)
   Oxygen tank (140)
   Carbon dioxide tank (150)

AE UNIV XIAMEN (UYXI-C)
GA 2020C7839F
AB    NOVELTY - A pyrrolidone-containing compound (I), is new. The compound (I) is prepared by copolymerizing 1-acryloxy-2-pyrrolidone, allyl ethyl ether and methyl 4-vinylbenzoate.
   USE - New pyrrolidone-containing compound used as carbon dioxide solubilizing crude oil viscosity reducing agent for increasing solubility of carbon dioxide in crude oil (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The compound (I) effectively increases solubility of carbon dioxide and reduces viscosity of the crude oil, and reduces minimum miscibility pressure of crude oil and carbon dioxide, thereby enhancing the fluidity of crude oil and improving recovery rate of crude oil.

   x = 20-300;
   y = 20-500;and
   z = 20-400.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) preparation of compound (I); and
   (2) application method of the compound (I) to increase solubility of carbon dioxide in crude oil, which involves injecting compound (I) to carbon dioxide through co-injection method, while reducing the viscosity of crude oil and the minimum miscible pressure of carbon dioxide and crude oil.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The initiator is at least one of azobisisobutyronitrile, azobisisoheptonitrile and dibenzoyl peroxide. The quinone polymerization inhibitor is at least one of tetrachlorobenzoquinone, p-benzoquinone and naphthoquinone.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preparation (claimed): Compound (I) is obtained by dissolving and mixing 1-acryloxy-2-pyrrolidone, allyl ethyl ether and methyl 4-vinylbenzoate in methanol, blowing in nitrogen to form protective atmosphere, adding methanol solution of initiator, stirring, heating to 40-60 degrees C, adding quinone polymerization inhibitor to terminate the polymerization reaction, cooling to room temperature, rotary evaporating to remove methanol to obtain crude product, dissolving crude product in acetone to remove unreacted monomers and drying. Preferred Property: The compound (I) has molecular weight of 1000-100000. Preferred Composition: The amount of compound (I) injected is 0.5-20 %mass of the total mass of carbon dioxide in crude oil. The mass ratio of 1-acryloxy-2-pyrrolidone, ally ethyl ether, methyl 4-vinylbenzoate and initiator is 1-5:5-10:20:0.05-0.2. The amount of quinone polymerization inhibitor is 0.05-0.2 wt.% to the total weight of three monomers. Preferred Condition: The heating reaction time is 0.5-2 hours. The polymerization reaction time is 10-60 minutes.

PN KR2254729-B1
TI Producing hydrogen and carbon dioxide using combustible waste comprises carrying out redox reaction of combustible waste in reactor, generating electricity, manufacturing brown gas by hydrolysis method and purifying hydrogen.
AU KIMKANGLYEN
   SONG Y H
   KIM J
AE CHANGJO E & E CO LTD (CHAN-Non-standard)
GA 202158899G
AB    NOVELTY - Producing hydrogen and carbon dioxide using combustible waste comprises (i) carrying out redox reaction of combustible waste in a reactor, (ii) generating electricity from the high-temperature gas by an organic rankine cycle power generation device, (iii) manufacturing brown gas by hydrolysis method and supplying it to the reaction furnace, (iv) purifying the gas that has passed through the evaporator of the organic rankine cycle power generator by a gas purifier, (v) filtering the gas purified by a bag filter, (vi) purifying hydrogen from the gas filtered, separating hydrogen and other gases by selectively removing a specific gas from a gas other than hydrogen, collecting carbon dioxide, supplying the collected carbon dioxide to the carbon dioxide compression tank for compressing, and returning the remaining gas to the gas purifier.
   USE - The method is useful for producing hydrogen and carbon dioxide using combustible waste.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: manufactures oxygen economically; can supply to the furnace; and prevents the production of the nitrogen oxide; and can sell carbon dioxide gas separately after doing compression.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart illustrating the method for producing hydrogen and carbon dioxide using combustible waste (Drawing includes non-English language text).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (i): the inflow of nitrogen is blocked by configuring the lower part of the reaction furnace in a closed type; a separation tank is provided in the lower part of the reaction furnace to separate and collect the melt and the residue; the concentrations of hydrogen, carbon monoxide, and carbon dioxide are measured in the reactor, respectively, and the amount of oxygen, hydrogen and steam are adjusted. When the amount of hydrogen inside the reactor is higher than the standard value, the amount of oxygen and hydrogen is reduced and the amount of steam is increased. When the amount of hydrogen in the reactor is lower than the reference value, the amount of oxygen and hydrogen is increased and the amount of steam is reduced. When the amount of carbon monoxide inside the reactor is higher than the reference value, the amount of oxygen and hydrogen is reduced and the amount of steam is increased. When the amount of carbon dioxide in the reactor is lower than the reference value, the amount of oxygen and hydrogen is increased and the amount of steam is reduced. In the step (ii): supplying water to the reaction furnace after supplying water to the organic Rankine cycle power generator to generate steam; supplying the electricity produced by the organic Rankine cycle power generation device to the brown gas generation module; and utilizing the steam produced in the organic rankine cycle power generation device for heating. In the step (iii): supplying the brown gas to the reaction furnace to be used as a heat source; and separating the oxygen and hydrogen, supplying oxygen to the reaction furnace to be used as a heat source, and supplying hydrogen to a hydrogen compression tank for compression. In the step (vi), supplying the separated hydrogen to a hydrogen compression tank and compressing it, and the compressed hydrogen is used for fuel cell power generation or internal combustion/external combustion engine power generation.

Combustion improvement additives for coal, peat and other nonhydrocarbon based fuels are included in this Section together with coal liquefaction and desulphurisation.); J03 (Electrochemical processes and electrophoresis - including ozone production, brine electrolysis, water electrolysis, production of chemical compounds and non-metallic elements, but excluding batteries or other means of producing power and the treatment of metals (C25B).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. PN CN112573555-A
TI Mineralizing and fixing carbon dioxide of calcium            carbide slag and preparing calcium carbonate comprises            e.g. reacting carbon dioxide containing gas and carbide            slag to obtain calcium bicarbonate, decomposing, and            returning carbon dioxide.
AU YU C
   WANG Q
   WEI W
AE WINTURE TECHNOLOGY BEIJING CO LTD (WINT-Non-standard)
GA 202137166C
AB    NOVELTY - Mineralizing and fixing carbon dioxide of                calcium carbide slag and preparing fine calcium                carbonate comprises (1) using the carbon dioxide                containing gas and slurry of carbide slag to react                in first reactor to generate calcium bicarbonate;                (2) conveying the aqueous solution containing                calcium bicarbonate obtained by solid-liquid                separation of reaction system obtained in step (1)                to second reactor, heating and decomposing to                obtain calcium carbonate precipitate, carbon                dioxide gas and water vapor; and (3) returning the                carbon dioxide gas obtained from reaction in step                (2) to the first reactor for reaction, separating                calcium carbonate in system after the reaction in                step (2), returning obtained clear liquid to the                first reactor to complete a cycle.
   USE - The method is useful for mineralizing and                fixing carbon dioxide of calcium carbide slag and                preparing fine calcium carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: can avoids the use of leaching                agents e.g. acid, alkali, ammonium chloride, etc.,                reduces material consumption, and avoids possible                introduction of impurities; avoids the use and                overflow of ammonia gas; has short process flow; is                easy to amplify, which is conducive to the                realization of large-scale industrial                applications.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (1), before                  performing the reaction, grinding the calcium                  carbide slag to 100-500 mesh, and then mixing it                  with water to form slurry; based on the mass of                  calcium oxide, the mass fraction of calcium                  carbide slag in the slurry is 1-8 wt.%. In the                  step (1), the carbon dioxide containing gas                  includes, but is not limited to, thermal power                  plant waste gas, coal chemical waste gas and                  cement plant waste gas; the volume content of                  carbon dioxide in the carbon dioxide containing                  gas is 5-100%. In the step (1), the molar ratio                  of the carbon dioxide containing gas to calcium                  carbide slag is 2-6:1, where the amount of the                  calcium oxide slag is calculated as calcium                  oxide; control the pH of the solution at 4-8.4.                  In the step (2), the temperature of the heating                  and decomposing is 50-100&#176; C; the method                  further comprises the step of adding a dispersant                  and a modifier to the system after step (2) to                  modify the calcium carbonate; the particle size                  of the modified calcium carbonate product is                  100-1000 nm. In the step (2), the solid-liquid                  separation is precipitation separation,                  filtration and/or centrifugation; in the step                  (3), the calcium carbonate is separated by                  precipitation separation, filtration and/or                  centrifugation. In the step (2), partially                  recycling the undissolved solid residue after                  solid-liquid separation to the first reactor with                  recycling ratio of 2-6:1; the circulation ratio                  refers to the ratio of the quality of the                  circulation to the mass of the discharge. In the                  step (1), recycling part of the unreacted carbon                  dioxide gas to the first reactor with recycling                  ratio of 1:1-7; the circulation ratio refers to                  the ratio of the circulating volume to the                  discharged volume.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The first reactor                  includes, but is not limited to, spray tower,                  bubble tower, bubble stirred tank, bubble tube                  reactor and airlift reactor; the second reactor                  includes, but is not limited to, an evaporation                  crystallizer, a spray tower, a slurry bed, and a                  heating reaction reactor.
DC E33 (Compounds of Be, Mg, Ca, Sr, Ba, Ra, Sc, Y, La, Ac, Al, lanthanides (Rare-earths), Th.)
MC E34-D03A
IP C01F-011/18

   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Polyolefin ester compounds of formula (I) are                new.
   n=1-200; and
   R and R =organic groups.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                preparation of (I).
                                                                                                                     
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation (Claimed): The preparation of                  (I), comprises performing polymerization of                  bifunctional acetylenic monomer of formula (II),                  carbon dioxide and the dihalide monomer of                  formula A-R2-A (III) in organic solvent through                  the combined action of a catalyst and basic                  compound under carbon dioxide environment, and                  separating reaction product to obtain (I).A=Cl, Br or I.Preferred Components: The catalyst is                  palladium acetate, palladium pivalate, palladium                  trifluoroacetate,                  tris(dibenzylideneacetone)dipalladium, bis                  (tri-tert-butylphosphine) palladium,                  tetra(triphenylphosphine)palladium,                  bis(triphenylphosphine)palladium dichloride,                  palladium dichloride, silver tungstate, silver                  iodide, silver nitrate, silver tetrafluoroborate,                  silver chloride, silver bromide, silver oxide,                  silver acetate, cuprous chloride, cuprous                  bromide, cuprous iodide, cuprous cyanide and/or                  cuprous oxide; basic compound is cesium                  carbonate, potassium carbonate, sodium carbonate,                  potassium hydroxide, sodium hydroxide, cesium                  fluoride, potassium fluoride, sodium fluoride,                  cesium hydroxide, potassium tert-butoxide, sodium                  tert-butoxide, lithium tert-butoxide,                  1,5,7-triazabicyclo[4.4.0]dec-5-ene,                  7-methyl-1,5,7-triazabicyclo[4.4.0]dec-5-ene,                  1,8-diazabicycloundec-7-ene, and/or cesium                  acetate. The organic solvent is tetrahydrofuran,                  dichloromethane, chloroform, toluene,                  1,4-dioxane, dimethyl sulfoxide,                  N,N-dimethylformamide, N,N-dimethylacetamide,                  acetonitrile, ethanol, N-methylpyrrolidone,                  dimethyl carbonate, diethyl carbonate, ethylene                  carbonate, and/or propylene carbonate. In the                  polymerization reaction, amount and concentration                  of bifunctional acetylenic monomer and the                  dihalide monomer in organic solvent are                  independently 0.05-5 mol/l; and amount of the                  catalyst is 1-40% the molar amount of the                  bifunctional acetylenic monomer. The amount of                  the base is 100-1000% the molar amount of the                  bifunctional acetylenic monomer. The                  precipitation agent is methanol or n-hexane.                  Preferred Method: The temperature of the                  polymerization reaction is 0-200&#176; C, and                  the time of the polymerization reaction is                  0.25-72 hours. The separation step comprises                  adding precipitant for precipitation, collecting                  precipitate, and drying to a constant weight to                  obtain final product.                                                                                                                              
   Preferred Definitions:
   R , R =diphenylmethane-4,4'-diyl,                oxydibenzene-4,4'-diyl, diphenylsulfane-4,4'-diyl,                dimethyldiphenylsilane-4,4'-diyl,                benzophenone-4,4'-diyl,                sulfonyldibenzene-4,4'-diyl, or                propane-2,2-diyldibenzene-4,4'-diyl.

PN CN108424478-A
TI Preparation of inulin involves degreasing inulin extract or inulin slurry, decolorizing using activated carbon, softening resin, concentrating, desalting resin, decolorizing, concentrating and spray drying.
AU LI Z
   CHEN D
   ZHENG C
   LIAN M
   LU B
   LIAN Y
AE XIAMEN NEWSEP TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (XIAM-Non-standard)
GA 201866723K
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of inulin involves degreasing inulin extract or inulin slurry using calcium hydroxide-carbon dioxide or calcium hydroxide-sulfur dioxide, decolorizing using activated carbon, softening resin, concentrating by nanofiltration, removing salt and monosaccharide, desalting resin, decolorizing, concentrating and spray drying.
   USE - Preparation of inulin.
   ADVANTAGE - The method efficiently provides inulin with reduced equipment and operation cost, and without reduced generation of wastewater.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for inulin preparation system.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Process: The nanofiltration concentration, and removing process of salt and monosaccharide, are increased to 25-30% by concentrating the liquid to solid matter. When the conductivity is 3000-5000 mu s/cm, it enters the resin desalination and decolorization. The desalting and decolorization of the resin includes sequentially using cationic resin, anion resin, cationic resin, anion resin, cationic resin, or anion resin, and processing. Preferred Composition: The inulin extract or inulin slurry is prepared using Jerusalem artichoke or chicory as raw materials. The cationic resin comprises strong acid type cationic resin, weak acid-type cationic resin, macroporous strong acid-type cationic resin or macroporous weak acid-type cationic resin. The anion resin includes strong base-type anion resin, weak base-type anion resin, macroporous strong base-type cationic resin or macroporous weak base-type anion resin. The resin regeneration solution after desalting and decolorizing the resin uses sulfuric acid and potassium hydroxide. The regenerant is then concentrated by electrodialysis or the membrane is concentrated to 15 %mass or more.
DC A11 (Polysaccharides; natural rubber; other natural polymers (only a restricted range of (modified) natural polymers are included. Thus starch would be excluded, but chemically modified starch included).)

AB    NOVELTY - Production of hydrogen from waste gas involves                removing tar from waste gas, purifying,                desulfurizing, compressing, dehydrating,                deoxidizing, detoxifying, performing two-stage                sulfur-tolerant shift reaction, separating                conversion gas, removing hydrocarbon, carbon                monoxide, carbon dioxide, methane, hydrogensulfide,                processing resultant gas and removing nitrogen to                obtain hydrogen.

PT P
PN CN107555490-A; CN107555490-B
TI Fast preparation of chloride ion intercalated cobalt aluminum hydrotalcite by mixing aluminum chloride hexahydrate and cobalt chloride hexahydrate, sodium hydroxide precipitator and deionized water, titration co-precipitating, and heating.
AU WU X
   CI C
   ZOU C
   FENG Y
   MENG H
   LI Z
AE UNIV TAIYUAN TECHNOLOGY (UYTL-C)
   UNIV TAIYUAN TECHNOLOGY (UYTL-C)
GA 201804824E
AB    NOVELTY - Fast preparation of chloride ion intercalated cobalt aluminum hydrotalcite comprises mixing aluminum chloride hexahydrate and cobalt chloride hexahydrate raw materials, sodium hydroxide precipitator and deionized water solvent, titration co-precipitating, hydrothermally heating, washing, filtering, and drying.
   USE - Method for fast preparation of chloride ion intercalated cobalt aluminum hydrotalcite (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method can effectively avoid formation of carbonate ions from dissolution of carbon dioxide in environment.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The fast preparation of chloride ion intercalated cobalt aluminum hydrotalcite is done by:(A) mixing buffer solution having pH of 4-5, 0.036-0.140 mol/L sodium acetate solution and 0.060-0.136 mol/L acetic acid solution to obtain acetic acid-sodium acetate buffer solution;(B) preparing 1 mol/L sodium hydroxide aqueous solution, and transferring into bottle vial;(C) adding acetic acid-sodium acetate buffer solution into reactor, water bath heating to 30-60 degrees C while stirring, dropping sodium hydroxide aqueous solution and mixed salt solution, and real-time monitoring pH using acidity meter while controlling titration speed; and stirring for 20-30 minutes, sealing precipitate and mother liquor into PTFE-lined stainless steel autoclave, and heating to 90-150 degrees C for 3-48 hours;(D) cooling turbid solution to room temperature, filtering through Buchner funnel, retaining filter cake product in the filter paper, and pumping filtrate into filter bottle;(E) adding filter cake product into a beaker, adding 100-200 mL deionized water, stirring, washing for 5-10 minutes, placing washing solution into Buchner funnel, suction filtering, retaining filter cake product, pumping filtrate to filter bottle, washing with deionized water, suction filtering until filtrate is neutralized; and(F) adding to watch glass, oven-drying at 80-100 degrees C for 12-24 hours.Preferred Components: The mixed salt solution has aluminum chloride content of 0.10-1 mol/L.
DC E31 (Compounds of V, Nb, Ta, Cr, Mo, W, Mn, Tc, Re, Fe, Ru, Os, Co, Rh, Ir, Ni, Pd, Pt, Pa, U and subsequent actinides.)

PN CN110424179-A; WO2021037033-A1; CN110424179-B
TI Solid waste modification for preparing papermaking filler, involves incinerating paper sludge, mixing paper sludge ash and calcium hydroxide solid, adding water and mixing and carrying out carbonization modification reaction.
AU FAN H
   SONG H
   LIU J
   ZHANG Y
   XIE K
AE UNIV SOUTH CHINA TECHNOLOGY (UYSC-C)
   UNIV SOUTH CHINA TECHNOLOGY (UYSC-C)
GA 201995527F
AB    NOVELTY - Solid waste modification involves incinerating paper sludge to obtain paper sludge ash, mixing paper sludge ash and calcium hydroxide solid, adding water, stirring and mixing, introducing carbon dioxide and air mixed gas for carbonization modification reaction.
   USE - Solid waste modification for preparing papermaking filler.
   ADVANTAGE - The method produces composite papermaking filler which satisfies filling performance of paper and ensures high value resource utilization of solid waste sludge.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The paper sludge comprises deinking sludge, sludge generated in pulp and papermaking process, sludge generated by pulp and papermaking wastewater treatment, and sludge produced by domestic wastewater treatment. The calcium hydroxide is obtained using calcium oxide and water. Preferred Composition: The calcium hydroxide accounts for 1.0-20.0% of total mass of paper sludge ash, calcium hydroxide and water. The ratio of carbon dioxide to air flow rate is 1:1-4. The mass ratio of paper sludge to calcium hydroxide is 1:1-5. Preferred Property: The flow rate of carbon dioxide is 0.10-1.00 L/minute. Preferred Conditions: The incineration reaction is carried out at 600-1000 degrees C for 1.5-6.5 hours. The carbonization modification reaction is stirred at 400-1200 rpm.
