TI Preparation of isophthalic acid comprises primary oxidation reacting m-xylene in presence of catalyst, oxidizing agent and solvent.
AU SUN W
   ZHAO L
   LV Q
   DONG J
AE UNIV EAST CHINA SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYEC-C)
GA 201834461N
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of isophthalic acid comprises primary oxidation reacting m-xylene in presence of a catalyst, oxidizing agent and solvent. The catalyst is cobalt-manganese-bromide catalyst. The oxidizing agent comprises 5-21 vol.% oxygen, 0.04-79 vol.% carbon dioxide and nitrogen. The primary oxidization ends when the oxygen partial pressure in the tail gas is 1.21-6%.
   USE - Method for preparing isophthalic acid.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has fast reaction speed, reduced combustion, and increased operation safety.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: Preparation of isophthalic acid further involves secondary oxidation reacting with the same condition and system of primary oxidation reaction, hydrogenation refining in platinum carbon catalyst at 180-270 degrees C and 0.8-3 MPa for 30-90 minutes, filtering, washing, and drying. Primary oxidation reaction is done at 150-190 degrees C and 0.45-2 MPa for 30-60 minutes. Preferred Components: The oxidizing agent further includes 0.0002-0.002 vol.% carbon monoxide. The oxidation system further includes 50-1500 ppm catalyst additive selected from rare earth metal acetate, alkali metal acetate and/or transition metal acetate. The rare earth metal acetate is cerium, neodymium, praseodymium, gadolinium, dysprosium, samarium and/or lanthanum. The alkali metal acetate is lithium, sodium, potassium, rubidium, cesium and/or francium. The transition metal acetate is hafnium, zirconium, iron and/or molybdenum. The catalyst is cobalt acetate, manganese acetate, sodium bromide and/or hydrobromic acid. The solvent is water and acetic acid.

PN CN107759436-A; CN107759436-B
TI Simulated moving bed system for preparing high purity methane by adsorptive separation of methane and nitrogen comprises e.g. multiple adsorbent filling tower provided with five gas input and output port and nitrogen outlet.
AU LI P
   YU J
   YANG Y
   QU D
   LU K
AE UNIV EAST CHINA SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYEC-C)
   UNIV EAST CHINA SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYEC-C)
GA 201819981B
AB    NOVELTY - Simulated moving bed system comprises e.g. multiple adsorbent filling tower provided with five gas input and output port, low quality methane raw material gas inlet, nitrogen outlet, carbon dioxide purge gas input port, high purity methane product gas output port and desorption of carbon dioxide recovery port. The five gas input output port is connected with tower divided into four areas namely methane adsorption area, methane concentration area, replacement area and carbon dioxide carbon dioxide vacuum desorption area, simulated moving bed tower adsorbent does not flow.
   USE - The system is useful for preparing high purity methane by adsorptive separation of methane and nitrogen (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The system: is economical and suitable for low quality methane concentration; and produces high quality methane product gas with 90% purity and 90% recovery.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Simulated moving bed system comprises multiple adsorbent filling tower provided with five gas input and output port, low quality methane raw material gas inlet, nitrogen outlet, carbon dioxide purge gas input port, high purity methane product gas output port and desorption of carbon dioxide recovery port. The five gas input output port is connected with the tower divided into four areas namely methane adsorption area, methane concentration area, replacement area and carbon dioxide carbon dioxide vacuum desorption area, simulated moving bed tower adsorbent does not flow, and a packed tower is synchronously switched and moved in the direction of gas flow through the gas input/output port for a set time interval, and is switched and moved continuously for many times to make the gas in the tower countercurrent to the adsorbent, low-quality methane feed gas enters from the low-quality methane feed gas input port in the methane adsorption area, and frequently switches through the gas input/output ports to perform low-concentration methane adsorption and methane exchange nitrogen concentrating towers in sequence in each packed column, internal methane and carbon dioxide displacement in the tower methane and carbon dioxide vacuum desorption operation, the simulated moving bed system continuously adsorbed methane from low concentration methane feed gas to obtain high purity methane product gas. The operating temperature of the simulated moving bed system is 10-60 degrees C, the operating pressure of the methane adsorption area, the methane enrichment area and the carbon dioxide replacement area is 1-6 atm, and the vacuum pressure at the carbon dioxide vacuum desorption area is 2-17 kPa. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for adsorbing and separating methane nitrogen to produce high-purity methane by a simulated moving bed comprising methane adsorption area, methane enrichment area and the carbon dioxide displacement area containing multiple packed columns connected each other in turn, through the valve switching, the adsorbent and the gas phase form countercurrent operation, carbon dioxide vacuum desorption area is operated independently by a single tower, require to maintain the same switching time, located after the carbon dioxide replacement area.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Method: The methane adsorption area is a separation area comprised of a low quality methane feed gas inlet and a nitrogen discharge outlet. The methane enrichment area is a separation area consisting of a low-quality methane feed gas inlet and a high-purity methane product gas outlet. The carbon dioxide replacement area is a separation area composed of a high-purity methane product gas outlet and a carbon dioxide displacement gas inlet. In the carbon dioxide vacuum desorption area, replacement of methane with carbon dioxide in the packed tower for vacuum adsorbent regeneration, desorption of carbon dioxide for recycling, desorption vacuum pressure is 3-15 kPa, adding a vacuum purge step, improve carbon dioxide desorption efficiency. The five gas input/output synchronous switching time intervals are 30-300 seconds. The filling tower number is 10~20 or more, the carbon dioxide vacuum desorption region comprises filling tower, methane adsorption area, methane concentration and carbon dioxide exchange each regions containing two to five or more packed tower. The adsorbent filling height of each packed tower is less than 1 m. The adsorbent is carbon-based adsorbent materials, zeolites, and high-silicon molecular sieves. The operating temperature of the simulated moving bed system is 10-50 degrees C, the operating pressure of the methane adsorption area, the methane concentration area and the carbon dioxide displacement area is 1.1-5 atm, and the vacuum pressure at the carbon dioxide vacuum desorption area is 3-15 kPa. The low-quality methane feed gas is mainly a mixture of methane and nitrogen, its methane content is 5-30 vol./vol.%, and its water vapor and carbon dioxide content is controlled to less than 1 vol./vol.%. The resulting high purity methane product gas has a methane content of more than 90%.

AU TOMISHIGE K
   TAMURA M
   SAKODA K
   YANASE M
AE TOSOH CORP (TOYJ-C)
   UNIV TOHOKU (TOHO-C)
GA 2021E7305Y
AB    NOVELTY - Method for producing a cyclic urea compound (II), involves (a) contacting a mixed fluid containing at least carbon dioxide, a liquid substance with a boiling point of 120 degrees C or higher at normal pressure, a gaseous substance at normal temperature and pressure other than carbon dioxide, and a diamine compound (I), with the metal oxide catalyst, while continuously supplying the mixed fluid to the metal oxide catalyst, and (b) reacting the carbon dioxide with the compound (I).
   USE - The method is used for producing a cyclic urea compound (II), where the cyclic urea compound (II) is 2-imidazolidinone (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method has excellent productivity, reduces the energy cost required for recovery of the compound (II), and effectively reduces and utilizes the emission of carbon dioxide emitted from a thermal power plant. The method is suitable for industrial production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for producing a cyclic urea compound of formula (II), involves (a) contacting a mixed fluid containing at least carbon dioxide, a liquid substance with a boiling point of 120 degrees C or higher at normal pressure, a gaseous substance at normal temperature and pressure other than carbon dioxide, and a diamine compound represented by the general formula (I), with the metal oxide catalyst, while continuously supplying the mixed fluid to the metal oxide catalyst, and (b) reacting the carbon dioxide with the compound (I).
   R1-R6 = H or 1-4C alkyl;and
   n = 0 or 1, where R1 in the formulae (I) and (II) are the same, and R2 to R6 and n are same.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The metal oxide catalyst is cerium (IV) oxide. Preferred Composition: The content of the liquid substance having a boiling point of 120 degrees C or higher at normal pressure is 1-50000 pts. wt. with respect to 100 pts. wt. of the compound (I). The content of the gaseous substance at normal temperature and pressure is 1-10000 pts. vol. with respect to 100 pts. vol. of carbon dioxide.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The distribution rate of the compound (I) is 0.005-0.02 mmol/minute with respect to 1 g of the metal oxide catalyst. The carbon dioxide is reacted with the compound (I) at 0-0.3 MPa (gauge pressure), and at 100-200 degrees C. Preferred Components: The compound (I) is ethylenediamine, and the compound (II) is 2-imidazolidinone.
   EXAMPLE - A cerium oxide (IV) (HS grade, calcined at 600 degrees C for 3 hours, Brunauer-Emmett-Teller (BET) surface area of 85 m2/g) was pressure-molded at 30 MPa and then crushed. Catalyst particles having 60 mesh through and 80 mesh up (catalyst particles having a particle size of 177-250 mu m) were obtained. The obtained catalyst particles 1 g were filled in the reaction tube, and the catalyst layer was formed in the reaction tube. The reaction tube was placed in a heating furnace and heated to 125 degrees C for maintenance. A carbon dioxide gas supplied at 5 ml (0.22 mmol)/minute from the carbon dioxide gas supply device, was merged with the flow path of nitrogen gas supplied from the nitrogen gas supply device at 10 ml (0.45 mmol)/minute. In the flow path of the mixed gas in which carbon dioxide gas and nitrogen gas were mixed, the solution of 6 g (0.10 mol) ethylenediamine, and 148.7 g (1.50 mol) N-methyl-2-pyrrolidone having normal pressure and boiling point of 202 degrees C was mixed. A mixed fluid was obtained by merging at a ratio of 0.010 ml/minute (0.0066 mmol/minute as ethylenediamine). The mixed fluid was injected into the reaction tube and circulated through the catalyst layer. The carbon dioxide and ethylenediamine were reacted in the reaction tube (in the presence of the catalyst layer) while maintaining the flow contact of the mixed fluid with the catalyst layer. The pressure in the reaction tube was 0 MPa in gauge pressure. 9 hours after the start of mixed fluid injection, the reaction product (liquid) from the reaction tube stored in the receiver was analyzed (quantified by the calibration curve method using a gas chromatograph), and 184 mg (2.14 mmol, yield 60% with respect to ethylenediamine) of 2-imidazolidinone was obtained.

AE CHANGZHOU BEIHUA AOLIAN ENVIRONMENTAL (CHAN-Non-standard)

AB    NOVELTY - Catalyst, prepared by precipitating by mixing                and stirring mixed metal salt solution of a copper                salt, a zinc salt and an aluminum salt with sodium                carbonate solution, aging, filtering, drying the                filter cake, placing in container, filling with                carbon dioxide gas for calcination, maintaining the                pressure at 2-4 MPa, after the completion of the                calcination, lowering the temperature and the                pressure to obtain catalyst precursor, and molding                to catalyst, is claimed.
   USE - The catalyst is useful for preparing methanol                by hydrogenation of carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst: has high activity and high                methanol selectivity more than 85%; and achieves                carbon dioxide conversion rate more than 25% at                lower reaction temperature 200 degrees C; The                method makes the crystal form of the catalyst more                easy to adsorb the activated carbon dioxide, thus                greatly improves the catalytic performance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) preparing catalyst comprising                precipitating by mixing and stirring mixed metal                salt solution of a copper salt, a zinc salt and an                aluminum salt with sodium carbonate solution,                aging, filtering, drying the filter cake, placing                in container, filling with carbon dioxide gas for                calcination, maintaining the pressure at 2-4 MPa,                after the completion of the calcination, lowering                the temperature and the pressure to obtain catalyst                precursor, and molding to obtain catalyst;                and
   (2) Use method of catalyst for preparing                methanol by hydrogenation of carbon dioxide                comprising introducing the catalyst into the                reactor at atmospheric pressure hydrogen before the                carbon dioxide hydrogenation reaction, in situ                reduction at 280-320 degrees C, introducing mixture                of carbon dioxide and hydrogen in the ratio of                3-3.5:1, and carrying out the carbon dioxide                hydrogenation reaction at 180-200 degrees C and                1.8-2.2 MPa.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the process,                  controlling the dropping rate of the sodium                  carbonate solution to maintain the pH of the                  precipitate system between 8-9. In the process,                  controlling the temperature at which                  precipitation is 70-80 degrees C. In the process,                  carrying out the aging by stirring for 2-2.5                  hours. The condition for drying the filter cake                  in an oven is 100-105 degrees C for 0.8-1 hour.                  The calcination time is 10-12 hours, and the                  calcination temperature is 600-700 degrees C. The                  preparation process further comprises                  precipitating by mixing mixed metal salt solution                  of a copper salt, a zinc salt and an aluminum                  salt with a sodium carbonate solution,                  controlling the drop rate of sodium carbonate,                  maintaining the pH of the precipitation system to                  8-9, controlling the temperature of the                  precipitation system at 70-80 degrees C, stirring                  the mixture for 2-2.5 hours for aging, filtering,                  and drying the filter cake, placing in a                  container, calcining with carbon dioxide gas at                  600-700 degrees C, during the calcination,                  maintaining the pressure at 2-4 MPa, carrying out                  the calcination for 10-12 hours, after the                  completion of the calcination, lowering the                  temperature and pressure to obtain catalyst                  precursor and molding to obtain a catalyst.                  Preferred Components: The molar ratio of copper,                  zinc, and aluminum is 1:0.5-1:2.5-3.

PN CN114618990-A
TI Method for making flat bottom surface of large            flat casting, involves erecting upper end surfaces of            two wooden strips by straight metal strip, and pouring            or spraying carbon dioxide on uppermost layer of carbon            dioxide hardened water glass sand pile.
AU CHEN B
   HUANG W
   CHEN G
   ZOU Z
   LAN M
   CHEN Z
   ZHENG J
AE FUJIAN XINGHANG HEAVY IND CO LTD (FUJI-Non-standard)
GA 2022827246
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves laying sand-type carbon                dioxide hardened water glass sand layer by layer in                the area between the two wooden strips (A2). The                carbon dioxide hardened water glass sand is higher                than the upper end surface of the wooden strip,                when laying the top layer of carbon dioxide                hardened water glass sand pile (A3). A straight                metal strip (A4) is used to erect the upper end                surfaces of the two wooden strips, and two people                hold the two ends of the metal bars respectively.                The carbon dioxide hardened water glass sand pile                that is higher than the upper end face of the                wooden strip is scraped off, and carbon dioxide is                poured or sprayed on the uppermost layer of carbon                dioxide hardened water glass sand pile. The surface                of the hardened carbon dioxide-hardened water glass                sand is a flat bottom surface when the layer is                hardened, and the flat bottom surface meets the                flatness requirement, and the inner core and the                outer mold of the casting are laid on the                surface.
   USE - Method for making flat bottom surface of large                flat casting.
   ADVANTAGE - The flat bottom surface of the large plane                castings has a reasonable design and simple                manufacturing process, which greatly reduces the                manufacturing cost of the mold and shortens the                manufacturing cycle of the mold.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a top view illustrating the                flat bottom surface manufacturing process.
   Rectangular pit (A1)
   Wooden strip (A2)
   Carbon dioxide hardening water-glass sand                pile (A3)
   Metal strip (A4)
   Laser level (A5)
DC M22 (Casting; powder metallurgy - including foundry moulding, moulding machines, patterns, moulds, cores and metal casting (B22).); P53 (Metal casting, powder metallurgy (B22).)

PN WO2018066787-A1; KR2018038658-A; KR1975451-B1
TI Hybrid-type generating station intake marine growth adhesion restraint apparatus for use in power plant, has chlorine-type germicide generating unit for producing chlorine-type germicide to bubble supplied from micro bubble generator.
AU MOON W H
   SEONG W G
   YOON J H
   KIM J W
   HEO J S
AE EK CO LTD (EKEK-Non-standard)
GA 2018283564
AB    NOVELTY - The apparatus has a carbon dioxide collector (20) for collecting carbon dioxide generated from a power plant chimney (10). A bubble trap (40) dissolves the carbon dioxide collected from the carbon dioxide collector in cooling seawater of a heat exchanger. A vacuum pump (50) transfers unreacted carbon dioxide, which is not dissolved in the bubble trap. A measuring unit (90) measures pH of cooling seawater. A chlorine-type germicide generating unit (100) produces chlorine-type germicide to bubble supplied from a micro bubble generator (80).
   USE - Hybrid-type generating station intake marine growth adhesion restraint apparatus for use in a power plant.
   ADVANTAGE - The apparatus reduces ecosystem destruction so as to dissolve the carbon dioxide injected in the cooling seawater in the bubble trap. The micro bubble generator re-dissolves the unreacted carbon dioxide and forms a minute bubble due to effective reaction structure so as to avoid environmental contamination through small amounts of carbon dioxide injection, so that deposit efficiency of a marine growth can be reduced by injecting the carbon dioxide and controlling the pH of the cooling seawater pH, thus improving performance of a heat exchanger pipe of a power plant.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a hybrid-type generating station intake marine growth adhesion restraint method.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of a hybrid-type generating station intake marine growth adhesion restraint apparatus. '(Drawing includes non-English language text)'
   Power plant chimney (10)
   Carbon dioxide collector (20)
   Bubble trap (40)
   Vacuum pump (50)
   Micro bubble generator (80)
   Measuring unit (90)
   Chlorine-type germicide generating unit (100)
DC Q42 (Hydraulic engineering, soil shifting and sewerage (E02, 3))

PN CN109650805-A
TI Preparing solid carbon filling paste using waste            concrete comprises e.g. dusting industrial waste gas            generated by dust filter, mixing de-dusted industrial            waste gas with and using crushing equipment for            preliminary crushing pretreatment.
AU WANG S
   JIANG H
   ZHU J
   LU L
   YIN H
   ZHAO Z
   SONG L
   ZHANG Z
   MA Q
   ZHU X
AE UNIV XUZHOU TECHNOLOGY (XUZH-C)
GA 2019393917
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing solid carbon filling paste using                waste concrete comprises e.g. dusting industrial                waste gas generated by a dust filter, mixing                de-dusted industrial waste gas with the normal                temperature air, mixing waste concrete and coal                gangue in a ratio of 0.5-6, using crushing                equipment for preliminary crushing pretreatment of                the mixture of waste concrete and coal gangue,                milling and sieving treatment to obtain coarse                aggregate, fine aggregate and fine powder, where                the whole process is in the industrial waste gas                atmosphere in the waste concrete carbonization                reaction occurs with the carbon dioxide in the                industrial waste gas, and collecting industrial                waste gas after carbonization reaction, placing                coarse aggregate, fine aggregate and fine powder in                a carbon fixation chamber, filling industrial waste                gas treated into the carbon fixation chamber, at                the same time, controlling humidity in the carbon                fixation chamber, continuously carbonizing with                carbon dioxide in industrial waste gas.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing solid                carbon filling paste using waste concrete.
   ADVANTAGE - The method fully utilizes carbonization                reaction of waste concrete and carbon dioxide and                hydration heat in the carbonization reaction,                reduces environmental damage of waste concrete and                greenhouse gas emission.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing solid carbon filling paste using                waste concrete comprises (i) dusting industrial                waste gas generated by a dust filter, mixing                de-dusted industrial waste gas with the normal                temperature air, and after mixed industrial exhaust                gas temperature is 35-45 degrees C, (ii) mixing                waste concrete and coal gangue in a ratio of 0.5-6,                using crushing equipment for preliminary crushing                pretreatment of the mixture of waste concrete and                coal gangue, milling and sieving treatment to                obtain coarse aggregate, fine aggregate and fine                powder, where the whole process is in the                industrial waste gas atmosphere after processing                step (i) in the waste concrete carbonization                reaction occurs with the carbon dioxide in the                industrial waste gas, and collecting industrial                waste gas after carbonization reaction, (iii)                placing coarse aggregate, fine aggregate and fine                powder in a carbon fixation chamber, filling                industrial waste gas treated into the carbon                fixation chamber, at the same time, controlling                humidity in the carbon fixation chamber to 45-55%,                continuously carbonizing coarse aggregate, fine                aggregate and fine powder with carbon dioxide in                industrial waste gas, collecting industrial waste                gas after carbonization reaction, where the coarse                aggregate, fine aggregate and fine powder are not                less than 48 hours in the carbon fixation chamber                to obtain carbon-fixed coarse aggregate,                carbon-fixed fine aggregate, and carbon-fixed fine                powder for filling the paste, (iv) fully mixing                collected industrial waste gas after the                carbonization reaction with the normal temperature                air and industrial waste gas of dust, where the                mixed industrial waste gas temperature is 35-45                degrees C, (v) mixing 35-50 pts. wt. carbon-fixed                fine powder, 15-20 pts. wt. biomass ash, 10-20 pts.                wt. slag powder, 8-20 pts. wt. fly ash , 1-2 pts.                wt. slaked lime, 1-2 pts. wt. calcium chloride                early strength agent, 0.6-1.5 pts. wt. naphthalene                sulfonate water reducing agent, 0.1-0.5 pts. wt.                nitro-lignin retarder, 0.3~0.8 pts. wt.                alkylbenzenesulfonate air entraining agent                and0.1-0.5 pts. wt. calcium aluminate expansion                agent, ball milling, and sieving through a 800 mesh                sieve to obtain a sieved paste additive, (vi)                according to the performance parameters required to                filling the paste comprises 34-48 pts. wt. solid                carbonaceous aggregate, 18-36 pts. wt. carbon-fixed                fine aggregate, 18-35 pts. wt. filling paste                additive, 6-16 pts. wt. cement, 6-12 pts. wt. phase                change material microcapsules and 10-22 pts. wt.                water, stirring and mixing to form a filling paste                mixture, during mixing, mixing filling paste,                continuously filling industrial waste gas after                treatment into mixing vessel until the end of the                mixing and stirring, then stopping, carbonizing                filling paste mixture with carbon dioxide in                industrial waste gas, and collecting industrial                waste gas after carbonization reaction to obtain a                filling paste mixture having a mass concentration                of 78-90% and having a amount of carbon dioxide                fixed, and (vii) transporting filling paste mixture                through filling pipeline conveying to the mine                filling place, during filling of the paste mixture,                filling industrial waste gas treated in step (iv)                through the feed port, further carbonizing filling                paste mixture with carbon dioxide in industrial                waste gas and finally to obtain carbon-filling                paste.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The maximum particle                  size of solid carbonaceous coarse aggregate is                  1/6-1/5 of the minimum pipe diameter in the                  filling paste conveying pipe. The phase change                  temperature of phase change material microcapsule                  is 30-40 degrees C. The wall material of phase                  change material microcapsule is an aqueous wall                  material. The core material of phase change                  material microcapsule is a mixture of                  n-octadecane and paraffin wax in a ratio of                  1-2.5.The cement is ordinary Portland cement.                  Preferred Composition: The specific mass each                  component in the filling paste additive are: 45                  pts. wt. carbon-fixed fine powder, 18 pts. wt.                  biomass ash, 16 pts. wt. slag powder, 14 pts. wt.                  fly ash, 1 pts. wt. slaked lime, 2 pts. wt.                  calcium chloride early strength agent, 0.9 pts.                  wt. naphthalene sulfonate water reducing agent,                  0.4 pts. wt. nitro-lignin retarder, 0.6 pts. wt.                  alkylbenzenesulfonate air entraining agent and                  0.3 pts. wt. calcium orthosilicate expansion                  agent.

PT P
PN CN109569695-A; CN109569695-B
TI Preparing core-shell catalyst for hydrogenation of carbon dioxide comprises e.g. Preparing aqueous solution of copper nitrate and zinc nitrate aqueous solution, mixing, stirring and preparing aqueous surfactant solution.
AU LI W
   HU X
   GUAN Q
AE UNIV NANKAI (UNAN-C)
GA 2019336153
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing core-shell catalyst for hydrogenation of carbon dioxide comprises e.g. (i) Preparing aqueous solution of copper nitrate and zinc nitrate aqueous solution, and mixing, stirring at room temperature to obtain mixed solution A, (ii) preparing aqueous surfactant solution, mixing with a prepared aqueous solution of precipitant, and stirring at room temperature to obtain mixed solution B in which an aqueous surfactant solution and a precipitate are prepared, (iii) slowly dripping the mixed solution A and the mixed solution B into a water-filled three-necked flask by a coprecipitation method under stirring, and drops during the addition, stopping heating, and cooling the mixture to room temperature, and repeating washing with deionized water, suction filtration until the filtrate is neutral, and drying the filter cake to obtain sample C, (iv) placing sample C in a tube furnace, raising temperature, and cooling.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing core-shell catalyst for hydrogenation of carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The core-shell catalyst: has excellent catalytic activity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing core-shell catalyst for hydrogenation of carbon dioxide comprises (i) Preparing 5% aqueous solution of copper nitrate and zinc nitrate aqueous solution having a mass fraction of 5%, and mixing the two at a mass ratio of 1:1, stirring at room temperature for at least 30 minutes to obtain mixed solution A, (ii) preparing 10 wt.% aqueous surfactant solution, mixing with a prepared aqueous solution of precipitant having a mass fraction of 5%, and stirring at room temperature for at least 1 hour to obtain mixed solution B in which an aqueous surfactant solution and a precipitate are prepared, where the mass ratio of the aqueous solution is 2:1, (iii) slowly dripping the mixed solution A and the mixed solution B into a water-filled three-necked flask by a coprecipitation method under stirring at 50-60 degrees C, where the mass of the water accounts for 20-60% of the mass of the mixed solution A, and drops during the addition, the pH of the solution in the three-necked flask was adjusted to between 7-8 by controlling the dropwise addition amount of the mixed solution B until the mixed solution A was added dropwise, stirring was continued for at least 2 hours, stopping heating, and cooling the mixture to room temperature, and repeating washing with deionized water, suction filtration until the filtrate is neutral, and drying the filter cake to obtain sample C, (iv) placing sample C in a tube furnace, raising temperature to 400 degrees C at 1?5 degrees C/minutes under an inert gas atmosphere, and keeping at this temperature for at least 2 hours, cooling to room temperature to obtain mixed metal oxide D, where the volumetric space velocity of the inert gas is 600-1200 hours, (v) uniformly mixing mixed metal oxide D and 2-methylimidazole by grinding, transferring to a hydrothermal reaction kettle, and reacting at 180-190 degrees C for 12-24 hours, cooling to room temperature, and grinding the sample, thoroughly washing with deionized water, suction filtration to obtain sample E, where the molar ratio of 2-methylimidazole to mixed metal oxide D is 1:1-5:1, and (vi) in a flowing inert gas atmosphere, heating sample E in a tube furnace at a temperature increase rate of 1-5 degrees C/minutes to 300-350 degrees C for at least 1 hour, followed by 5-10 degrees C/minutes, heating rate is raised to 500-800 degrees C and maintaining for at least 4 hours, and lowering to room temperature to obtain desired core-shell catalyst, where the inert gas has a volumetric space velocity of 600-1200/hours. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) core-shell catalyst for hydrogenation of carbon dioxide is prepared by the above mentioned method; and
   (2) using core-shell catalyst comprising (a) grinding the prepared core-shell catalyst, placing 0.4 g in a constant temperature zone of a fixed bed reactor, and raising the temperature to 300 -350 degrees C in a flowing hydrogen atmosphere for at least 2 hours, where the volumetric velocity of hydrogen gas 3000-9000/hours, (b) raising reaction temperature to 350-550 degrees C, introducing which carbon dioxide gas, where the volumetric velocity of carbon dioxide is 3000/hour, and the volume ratio of carbon dioxide to hydrogen is 1:1-1:5.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The precipitating agent comprises sodium carbonate, potassium carbonate, sodium hydroxide and/or potassium hydroxide. The surfactant is cetyltrimethylammonium bromide, sodium dodecylsulfonate, sodium dodecylbenzenesulfonate, ethylene glycol, polyethylene glycol, glycerin and/or polyvinylpyrrolidone.

PT P
PN CN114784292-A
TI Lithium-carbon dioxide battery anode material            useful for assembling battery without adding conductive            agent or agglomerant, comprises Brunauer-Emmett-Teller            specific surface area, aperture and nitrogen            content.
AU LIU L
   WANG K
   XIAO C
AE UNIV XIAN JIAOTONG (UYXJ-C)
GA 2022A3878M
AB    NOVELTY - Lithium-carbon dioxide battery anode material                comprises Brunauer-Emmett-Teller (BET) specific                surface area is 400-600m2g-1, the aperture is                5-100nm, and the nitrogen content is 2-8a t%.
   USE - The lithium-carbon dioxide battery anode                material is useful for assembling battery without                adding conductive agent or agglomerant.
   ADVANTAGE - The lithium-carbon dioxide battery anode                material has good circulation performance and high                energy efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                preparation method of lithium-carbon dioxide                battery anode material.

AU KANG Y S
   KANG M J
AE UNIV SOGANG RES FOUND (USOG-C)
GA 201813271W
AB    NOVELTY - A carbon dioxide reduction device comprises a photoanode containing bismuth vanadate, Nafion (RTM: sulfonated tetrafluoroethylene-based fluoropolymer-copolymer)/titanium dioxide hybrid cathode, a separation membrane provided between the photoanode and cathode, and an electrolyte located on both sides of the separation membrane. The device reduces carbon dioxide by irradiating light to the photoanode while supplying carbon dioxide into the electrolyte.
   USE - Device is used for reducing carbon dioxide for producing 1C fuel material, preferably methanol (all claimed).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: Bismuth vanadate is in the form of film having (040) crystal facets. The electrolyte comprises a mixture comprising acetonitrile and water. Preferred Process: Carbon dioxide supplied into the electrolyte is adsorbed on the Nafion (RTM: sulfonated tetrafluoroethylene-based fluoropolymer-copolymer)/titanium dioxide hybrid cathode, solar light or visible light is irradiated to the photoanode, and electrons generated in the photo-irradiated photoanode are supplied to hybrid cathode, to reduce carbon dioxide.

PT P
PN CN110357759-A; WO2021000456-A1; CN110357759-B
TI Realizing carbon dioxide methanation by placing hydrogen storage alloy hydride in ball mill tank under protective atmosphere, extracting argon gas, charging carbon dioxide-containing gas and preparing methane using ball mill reaction.
AU OUYANG L
   LIU F
   ZHONG D
   ZHU M
   WANG H
   LIU J
   ZENG M
AE UNIV SOUTH CHINA TECHNOLOGY (UYSC-C)
   UNIV SOUTH CHINA TECHNOLOGY (UYSC-C)
GA 2019888334
AB    NOVELTY - Method for realizing carbon dioxide methanation involves placing a hydrogen storage alloy hydride in a ball mill tank under a protective atmosphere, extracting argon gas, charging a carbon dioxide-containing gas and preparing a methane by a ball mill reaction at room temperature.
   USE - The method is useful for realizing methanation of carbon dioxide using a hydrogen storage alloy hydride.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has methane yield of 83.2%.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The hydrogen storage alloy hydride comprises at least one of lanthanum nickel hydride, zirconium nickel hydride, neodymium nickel hydride, praseodymium nickel hydride and samarium nickel hydride. The carbon dioxide-containing gas is pure carbon dioxide or a mixed gas containing carbon dioxide and a protective atmosphere. Preferred Method: The hydrogen storage alloy hydride is prepared by crushing a hydrogen storage alloy through a standard sieve of 200-500 mesh under a protective atmosphere, carrying out a hydrogen absorption activation reaction on the hydrogen storage alloy at 100-200 degrees C for 5-10 hours under a hydrogen atmosphere having a pressure of 1-4 MPa, and cooling to room temperature after completing the reaction. The step of ball mill is carried out at a rotation speed of 300-500 rpm for 1-60 hours. Preferred Composition: The molar ratio of hydrogen in the hydrogen hydride alloy hydride to the carbon dioxide gas in the ball mill tank is (1-15):1. The mass ratio of the ball milling medium to the hydrogen storage alloy hydride is (10-40):1.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

TI Method for conversion and utilization of carbon dioxide, involves taking coal as raw material, pyrolyzing raw material, utilizing semi-coke portion for power generation, followed by transforming carbon dioxide produced into carbon monoxide.

The raw material is pyrolyzed or vaporized partially in an upstream reactor, followed by utilizing solid semi-coke portion for combustion power generation, transforming the carbon dioxide produced into carbon monoxide by reacting with remaining coke, and forming a synthesis gas for chemical synthesis by the carbon monoxide and hydrogen produced in the upstream reactor.

PN CN114917723-A
TI Full temperature range pressure swing adsorption            process for recycling carbon dioxide from flue gas,            involves pre-processing exhaust gas containing carbon            dioxide, entering raw material gas into concentration            system with adsorption tower filled with e.g. activated            alumina, and performing adsorption.
AU ZHAN J
   WANG L
   ZHONG Y
   CHEN Y
AE SICHUAN TIANCAI TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SICH-Non-standard)
GA 2022B3730V
AB    NOVELTY - Full temperature range pressure swing                adsorption process for recycling carbon dioxide                from flue gas, involves pre-processing industrial                exhaust gas containing carbon dioxide as raw                material gas, cooling, removing oil, demisting,                blowing and/or compressing pre-treatment process,                removing impurities including solid particles,                green oil, 6C component and 6C or more hydrocarbon                and concentrating middle temperature PSA, entering                the raw material gas into middle-temperature PSA                concentration system, where the adsorption tower is                filled with one or more adsorbent, such as                activated alumina, silica gel, activated carbon,                molecular sieve and carbon molecular sieve,                performing adsorption to form closed adsorption and                desorption of circulating operation and flowing                carbon dioxide-rich concentrated gas out from                bottom of the adsorption tower, and subjecting                carbon dioxide enriched gas from medium-temperature                PSA concentration process to catalytic                hydrogenation or air oxidation.
   USE - Full temperature range pressure swing                adsorption process for recycling carbon dioxide                from flue gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The adsorption and desorption is easy to match                and balance the circulating operation in                middle-temperature pressure swing adsorption                process, the carbon dioxide is prevented from being                deeply absorbed and the impurity component with                strong polarity. The method for separating and                purifying carbon dioxide with low cost, which                solves the problem that the product purity and                yield are inversely proportional to existing                low-pressure or normal-pressure PSA process of the                product obtained from adsorption phase.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Full temperature range pressure swing                adsorption process for recycling carbon dioxide                from flue gas, involves pre-processing industrial                exhaust gas containing carbon dioxide as raw                material gas, cooling to 60-120&#8451;,                removing oil, demisting, blowing and/or compressing                pre-treatment process, removing impurities                including solid particles, green oil, 6C component                and 6C or more hydrocarbon, entering into next                process and concentrating middle temperature PSA,                entering the raw material gas after pretreatment                process into middle-temperature PSA concentration                system with operating temperature of                60-120&#8451; and operating pressure of normal                pressure to 2 MPa, where the system is a pressure                swing adsorption consisting of at least two or more                adsorption towers in series or in parallel,                adsorption towers are operated alternately to                ensure continuous entry of raw gas, one or more                adsorption towers are in adsorption state and rest                of the adsorption towers are in regeneration state,                and discharging non-adsorption phase gas from top                of the tower through bed as exhaust gas, where the                volume percentage of carbon dioxide is 1% or less,                the adsorption tower in the middle-temperature PSA                concentration system is filled with one or more                adsorbent combinations of activated alumina, silica                gel, activated carbon, molecular sieve and carbon                molecular sieve, the control and adjustment of                pressure change during PSA adsorption and                desorption cycle operation between the adsorption                towers are carried out through control system                composed of regulating valve and/or program control                valve and/or other valves arranged on connecting                pipeline, the desorption process includes                displacement, smooth discharge, equal pressure                drop, reverse discharge, vacuuming, flushing,                vacuuming and flushing, equalizing pressure rise                and/or final charging, and performing adsorption to                form closed adsorption and desorption of                circulating operation and flowing carbon                dioxide-rich concentrated gas out from bottom of                the adsorption tower with carbon dioxide                concentration of 92-99% and yield of 60-80% or                directly outputting as product gas, and subjecting                carbon dioxide enriched gas from medium-temperature                PSA concentration process to catalytic                hydrogenation or air oxidation to remove small                amount of carbon monoxide, ethane, second carbon                and light hydrocarbon organics above second carbon                in the carbon dioxide enriched gas, cooling, deep                drying and performing one-time adsorption                purification, entering obtained purified carbon                dioxide concentrated gas into carbon dioxide                low-temperature rectification or fractional                condensation or flash evaporation system, from                which carbon dioxide liquid product with purity of                99.99% or more is obtained and using generated hot                regeneration waste gas as flushing gas of                medium-temperature PSA concentration system and                other as regeneration gas after cooling.

PT P
PN EP4015450-A1; WO2022128993-A1
TI Upgrading hydrocarbon-containing feed gas to            methanol product stream and hydrogen product stream, by            providing hydrocarbon-containing feed gas to reforming            reactor, reforming, cooling, removing water from            synthesis gas stream, compressing, and feeding            methanol-rich stream to separation unit.
AU MORTENSEN P M
   NIELSEN C S
AE HALDOR TOPSOE AS (TOPS-C)
   TOPSOE AS (TOPS-C)
GA 2022812413
AB    NOVELTY - Method for upgrading a hydrocarbon-containing                feed gas to a methanol product stream and a                hydrogen product stream, involves (a) providing a                hydrocarbon-containing feed gas to a reforming                reactor, (b) reforming the hydrocarbon-containing                feed gas in the reforming reactor to provide a                first synthesis gas stream, (c) cooling the first                synthesis gas stream and/or the shifted synthesis                gas stream in a cooling unit to provide a second                synthesis gas stream, (d) removing water from the                second synthesis gas stream in a water removal unit                to provide a third synthesis gas stream, (e)                compressing the third synthesis gas stream in a                compressing unit to a first pressure, (f) feeding                at least part of the fourth synthesis gas stream                and/or at least part of the fifth synthesis gas                stream from step (e) to a methanol synthesis unit                to provide a methanol-rich stream, and (g) feeding                methanol-rich stream from step (f) to a separation                unit to provide a methanol product stream and a                hydrogen rich stream.
   USE - The method is used for upgrading                hydrocarbon-containing feed gas to methanol product                stream and hydrogen product stream.
   ADVANTAGE - None given.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for upgrading a hydrocarbon-containing                feed gas to a methanol product stream and a                hydrogen product stream, involves (a) providing a                hydrocarbon-containing feed gas to a reforming                reactor, (b) reforming the hydrocarbon-containing                feed gas in the reforming reactor to provide a                first synthesis gas stream, (b1) optionally feeding                at least part of the first synthesis gas stream                from step (b) to a water gas shift reactor to                provide shifted synthesis gas stream, (c) cooling                the first synthesis gas stream and/or the shifted                synthesis gas stream in a cooling unit to provide a                second synthesis gas stream, (d) removing water                from the second synthesis gas stream in a water                removal unit to provide a third synthesis gas                stream, (e) compressing the third synthesis gas                stream in a compressing unit to a first pressure,                where the first pressure is higher than the feed                pressure of the hydrocarbon feed gas to provide a                fourth synthesis gas stream, (e1) optionally                feeding at least part of the fourth synthesis gas                stream from step (e) to a carbon dioxide removal                unit to provide a carbon dioxide-rich stream and a                fifth synthesis gas stream, (f) feeding at least                part of the fourth synthesis gas stream and/or at                least part of the fifth synthesis gas stream from                step (e) to a methanol synthesis unit to provide a                methanol-rich stream, and (g) feeding at least part                of the methanol-rich stream from step (f) to a                separation unit to provide a methanol product                stream and a hydrogen rich stream. An INDEPENDENT                CLAIM is included for a system for upgrading a                hydrocarbon-containing feed gas to a methanol                product stream and a hydrogen product stream                comprising a hydrocarbon-containing feed gas                arranged to be fed to a reforming reactor,                reforming reactor arranged to reform the                hydrocarbon-containing feed gas to provide a first                synthesis gas stream from the reforming reactor,                optionally a water gas shift reactor arranged to                receive at least part of the first synthesis gas                stream from the reforming reactor and provide                shifted synthesis gas stream, a cooling unit                arranged to cool the first synthesis gas stream                and/or the shifted synthesis gas stream and provide                a second synthesis gas stream, a water removal unit                arranged to remove water from the second synthesis                gas stream, and provide a third synthesis gas                stream, a compressing unit arranged to compress the                third synthesis gas stream to a first pressure,                where the first pressure is higher than the feed                pressure of the hydrocarbon feed gas, and provide a                fourth synthesis gas stream, optionally a carbon                dioxide removal unit arranged to receive at least a                part of the fourth synthesis gas stream and to                provide a carbon dioxide-rich stream and a fifth                synthesis gas stream, a methanol synthesis unit                arranged to convert at least part of the fourth                synthesis gas stream and/or at least part of the                fifth synthesis gas stream to a methanol-rich                stream, a separation unit arranged to provide a                methanol product stream and a hydrogen rich stream                from at least part of the methanol-rich                stream.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The carbon dioxide                  removal unit is a cryogenic separation unit,                  where the increase in the amount of carbon                  dioxide removed in the cryogenic separation unit                  is achieved by decreasing the operating                  temperature in the cryogenic separation unit. The                  reforming reactor comprises an autothermal                  reformer. Preferred Method: The method further                  involves adjusting the molar ratio between the                  hydrogen product stream and the methanol product                  stream, adjusting the amount of carbon dioxide                  which is removed in the carbon dioxide removal                  unit, relative to the carbon dioxide content in                  the fourth synthesis gas stream, providing a                  carbon dioxide-containing feed to the reforming                  reactor, providing an oxygen-containing feed to                  the autothermal reformer, providing a                  hydrogen-containing feed upstream the methanol                  synthesis unit, preferably in admixture with the                  at least part of the fourth synthesis gas stream                  and/or at least part of the fifth synthesis gas                  stream. Preferred Conditions: A first part of the                  fourth synthesis gas stream from step (e) is fed                  to the carbon dioxide removal unit to provide the                  carbon dioxide-rich stream and the fifth                  synthesis gas stream, where at least part of the                  fifth synthesis gas stream is fed to the methanol                  synthesis unit together with a second part of the                  fourth synthesis gas stream in step (f). The                  molar ratio between the methanol product stream                  and the hydrogen product stream is changed by                  regulating the carbon dioxide-containing feed,                  the oxygen-containing feed, and/or the                  hydrogen-containing feed. The portion of the                  hydrogen rich stream from step (g) is compressed                  and returned to the methanol synthesis unit as a                  methanol loop recycle stream. Preferred Device:                  The reforming reactor comprises a tubular                  reformer, a convective reformer, an electrically                  heated reformer, and/or an autothermal reformer,                  preferably a tubular reformer placed in series                  with an autothermal reformer, or a combination of                  an electrically heated reformer placed in series                  with an autothermal reformer.

PT P
PN CN112439025-A
TI Preparing wheat-flavored radix rehmanniae oral liquid, by pulverizing e.g. dogwood and radix rehmanniae, extracting mixture, adding with ethanol aqueous solution, fermenting, adding with sodium benzoate, and filtering liquid.
AU ZHAO X
   CHEN Z
AE JIANGXI HEMING PHARM CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 202124502S
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing wheat-flavored radix rehmanniae oral liquid involves (a) pulverizing e.g. dogwood and radix rehmanniae, mixing them to obtain powder (A), and pulverizing tree peony bark and Schisandra berry to obtain powder (B), (b) adding water to powder (A), and performing ultra-high pressure extraction to obtain extract (A) and medicine residue (A), (c) performing supercritical carbon dioxide extraction of drug residue (A) to obtain extract (B) and drug residue (B), (d) mixing medicine residue (B) and ethanol aqueous solution to obtain extract solution (C) and medicine residue (C), (e) mixing medicine residue (C) with water and extracting to obtain extract (D) and medicine residue (D), (f) mixing powder (B) with compound enzyme, spraying with distilled water, fermenting and filtering to obtain extract (E) and medicine residue (E), (g) obtaining a clear paste, and (h) refrigerating the clear paste, adding sodium benzoate aqueous solution, and filtering oral liquid.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing wheat-flavored radix rehmanniae oral liquid.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for preparing wheat-flavored radix rehmanniae oral liquid involves (a) pulverizing dwarf lily turf root, dogwood, radix rehmanniae, tuckahoe, Alisma orientale and Chinese yam respectively, passing through an 80-100 mesh sieve, mixing them to obtain powder (A), pulverizing tree peony bark and Schisandra berry, and passing through an 80-100 mesh sieve to obtain powder (B) after mixing, (b) adding water to the powder (A), putting it in a plastic bag, sealing it, and placing in an ultra-high pressure extraction device for performing ultra-high pressure extraction to obtain extract (A) and medicine residue (A), (c) performing supercritical carbon dioxide extraction of the drug residue (A) to obtain extract (B) and drug residue (B), (d) mixing the medicine residue (B) and ethanol aqueous solution, and heating and refluxing for extraction for 30-50 minutes to obtain extract solution (C) and medicine residue (C), (e) mixing medicine residue (C) with water and extracting two times for 20-35 minutes (each) to obtain extract (D) and medicine residue (D), (f) mixing powder (B) with compound enzyme, spraying evenly with distilled water and sealing, fermenting at 35-40 degrees C for 3-5 days, adding with n-hexane, ultrasonically extracting mixture, and filtering to obtain extract (E) and medicine residue (E), (g) adding medicine residue (D) and medicine residue (E) to water, extracting twice for 1.5-2.5 hours (each time), combining the extracts i.e. extract (A), extract (B), extract (C), extract (D) and extract (E), adjusting the pH value to 4-5, passing through an 80-100 mesh screen for a concentration, performing alcohol precipitation, and concentrating the supernatant for a second time to a relative density of 1.05-1.1 to obtain a clear paste, and (h) refrigerating the clear paste at 2-10 degrees C for 24-30 hours, filtering it with 250-350 mesh filter material while it is cold, heating it and adding sucrose under stirring, heating to boiling, keeping for 15-25 minutes, filtering with 200-400 mesh filter material, adding sodium benzoate aqueous solution, adding purified water to make the volume constant, and filtering with 200-400 mesh filter material to obtain oral liquid. The ultra-high pressure extraction is performed by increasing pressure to 300-350 MPa within 10-15 minutes, holding the pressure for 5-10 minutes, relieving pressure to normal pressure, increasing the pressure to 300-350 MPa within 10-15 minutes, maintaining pressure for 5-10 minutes, releasing the pressure to normal pressure, filtering and centrifuging obtained materials to obtain extract (A) and medicine residue (A). The supercritical carbon dioxide extraction is performed with extraction temperature of 40-45 degrees C and extraction pressure of 18-23 MPa for 60-100 minutes using 80-90 vol.% ethanol aqueous solution as entrainer, where flow rate of carbon dioxide is 30-35 kg/hour. The ultrasonic extraction includes primary ultrasonic extraction, secondary ultrasonic extraction, and third ultrasonic extraction, where power of the primary ultrasonic extraction is 300-450 W and time is 10-18 minutes, the power of the second ultrasonic extraction is 500-600 W and the time is 10-22 minutes, and power of third ultrasonic extractions is 180-250 W and the time is 20-40 minutes.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The step (b) involves soaking powder (A) in water in a weight ratio of 1:7-10 for 2-3 hours, putting it into a plastic bag and sealing bag. The temperature of the ultra-high pressure extraction is 25-30 degrees C. The step (d) involves using drug residue (B) and ethanol aqueous solution in a weight-volume ratio of 1:10-15 g/1 ml, where ethanol aqueous solution comprises 80-85 vol.% ethanol. In step (e), solid-liquid weight ratio is 1:8-10 (in first extraction process), and solid-liquid weight ratio is 1:5-7 (in second extraction process). The step (f) involves using 0.5-0.9 wt.% compound enzyme with respect to weight of the powder (B), and using powder (B) and n-hexane in a weight ratio of 1:6-9. The step (g) involves maintaining solid-liquid weight ratio to 1:8-10. the process of primary concentration and secondary concentration, the temperature of concentration is 65-85 degrees C, the degree of vacuum is -0.02 to -0.06 MPa, and the temperature of the cleaning cream is 70-80 degrees C. In the primary concentration process, a double-effect decompression and energy-saving concentrator is used for concentration, and in the secondary concentration process, a single-effect decompression and energy-saving concentrator is used for concentration. The step (h) involves adding clear paste, sucrose and sodium benzoate in a weight ratio of 850-950:100-200:1-3. Preferred Component: The compound enzyme comprises mixture of cellulase, hemicellulase, and amylase in a weight ratio of 1-3:8-12:5-9.

Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. IP A61K-036/8968; A61K-009/08; A61P-001/08; A61P-001/14; A61P-011/00; A61P-011/04; A61P-011/14; A61P-013/12; A61P-027/16; A61P-003/10; A61P-043/00; A61P-007/04

PT P
PN CN111011138-A
TI Cultivating Daphne giraldii by subjecting seeds to post-netting carbon dioxide laser irradiation treatment, immersing in putrescine solution, sowing seeds, covering with humus soil, planting in mountainous area and top dressing fertilizer.
AU HAN Z
   WANG Y
   ZHANG D
   MA B
AE GANSU BUYUN AGRIC & ANIMAL HUSBANDRY TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (GANS-Non-standard)
GA 202034534Q
AB    NOVELTY - Method for cultivating Daphne giraldii involves selecting robust D.giraldii mother plant for seed collection, spreading the collected seeds, sun drying, peeling the seeds, subjecting the seeds to post-netting carbon dioxide laser irradiation treatment, immersing the seeds in putrescine solution, then immersing in iodine, allowing to stand in a ventilated and dry place, stratifying and germinating the dried seeds while spraying putrescine solution, tilling the soil, exposing to sun, applying ferrous sulfate, phosphogypsum, organic fertilizer, cotton stalk charcoal and bamboo vinegar, sowing the seeds, covering with humus soil, compacting with rolling wood, selecting a mountainous area with a gentle and sandy soil, improving the soil before afforestation, then planting plants, performing drip irrigation and top dressing complex water fertilizer during autumn.
   USE - The method is useful for cultivating D.giraldii.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves germination rate of D.giraldii seeds and survival rate of D.giraldii in sandy soil, is suitable for large scale artificial planting and increases yield and quality of D.giraldii.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for cultivating Daphne giraldii involves (a) selecting robust D.giraldii mother plant for seed collection, spreading the collected seeds, sun drying, peeling the seeds, subjecting the seeds to post-netting carbon dioxide laser irradiation treatment for 60 seconds, where the diameter of the spot after carbon dioxide laser beam expansion is 25 mass/mass%, irradiation distance is 30 cm and power density is 3 mW/mm2, immersing the seeds in 1 mmol/l putrescine solution for 8 hours at 3 degrees C under dark, then immersing in 0.5 weight/volume% iodine for 4 hours and allowing to stand in a ventilated and dry place, (b) (i) stratifying and germinating the dried seeds at 25 degrees C while spraying 10 degrees C 1 mmol/l putrescine solution once in every 12 hours to make the seeds completely wet and when the seed cracking rate reaches 30%, sowing the seeds, (ii) tilling the soil at a depth of 25-30 cm, exposing to sun for 10 days and applying 30-50 pts. wt./acre ferrous sulfate, 30-40 pts. wt./acre phosphogypsum, 100-300 pts. wt./acre organic fertilizer, 35-60 pts. wt./acre cotton stalk charcoal and 25-60 pts. wt./acre bamboo vinegar and (iii) sowing the seeds, covering with 2 cm humus soil, compacting with rolling wood, when the seedling height is greater than or equal to 50 cm and the ground diameter is greater than or equal to 0.8cm, performing afforestation, (c) selecting a mountainous area with a gentle and sandy soil with an elevation of 2500-3000 m and a slope of less than 15 degrees , improving the soil before afforestation, then planting at a row spacing of 1 mx 1 m, where 2 plants are planted per hole and the planting density is 1800-31000 plants/acre and (d) performing drip irrigation once in every 3-4 days and top dressing 100-300 kg/acre complex water fertilizer during autumn. The compound water fertilizer comprises 10-15 pts. wt. polyaspartic acid, 1-3 pts. wt. microbial inoculants, 12-19 pts. wt. sugar alcohol, 11-17 pts. wt. nano-potassium silicate, 11-12 pts. wt. L-methionine, 12-13 pts. wt. glycine, 10-14 pts. wt. proline, 1-2 pts. wt. cerium nitrate, and 0.5-3 pts. wt. lanthanum nitrate. The microbial inoculants comprises 1-2 pts. wt. Bacillus amyloliquefaciens, 1-2 pts. wt. B.mucilaginous, and 0.5-1.5 pts. wt. Actinomycetes and 2-3 pts. wt. Trichoderma. The organic fertilizer comprises 20-40 pts. wt. potassium fulvate, 100-120 pts. wt. Potosia brevitarsis feces, 60-90 pts. wt. mealworm insect feces, 90-150 pts. wt. sugarcane filter mud, 20-30 pts. wt. burdock root waste, 70-100 pts. wt. cow dung and 80-120 pts. wt. sheep dung. The soil improvement involves turning the mountain at a depth of 20-30 cm, applying 100-300 pts. wt./acre Heilu soil, 150-200 pts. wt./acre organic fertilizer, 10-15 pts. wt./acre microbial inoculants, 40-90 pts. wt./acre slaked lime, 80-90 pts. wt./acre coconut bran, 60-110 pts. wt./acre bone charcoal and again tilling to combine with the soil.

PN CN113477278-A; CN113477278-B
TI Use of hybrid system of pyridinium quaternary ammonium salt and inorganic semiconductor for photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide, comprises reducing carbon dioxide in presence of light, and pyridine quaternary ammonium salt, inorganic semiconductor material and electronic sacrificial body.
AU REN Y
   LIU J
   WANG F
AE UNIV HUAZHONG SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYHZ-C)
GA 2021C0843R
AB    NOVELTY - Use of hybrid system of pyridinium quaternary ammonium salt and inorganic semiconductor for photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide, is claimed. The use method comprises (i) introducing pyridine quaternary ammonium salt, inorganic semiconductor material and electronic sacrificial body into solvent and placing it in a light-transmitting reaction vessel, then sealing the reaction vessel and (ii) blowing carbon dioxide into the sealed reaction vessel, carrying out reduction of carbon dioxide into carbon monoxide under the action of light and using quaternary pyridine ammonium salt as a co-catalyst, and inorganic semiconductor material as a photocatalyst.
   USE - The hybrid system of pyridinium quaternary ammonium salt and inorganic semiconductor is useful for photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The photocatalytic hybrid system has simple synthesis method of the promoter, no metal, and excellent effect of reducing carbon dioxide in the aqueous phase by the photocatalytic system constructed with inorganic semiconductor materials.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The quaternary pyridinium salt is a quaternized product of poly(4-vinylpyridine) or a quaternized product of poly(2-vinylpyridine). The quaternized product of poly(4-vinylpyridine) is compound of formula (I). The quaternized product of poly(2-vinylpyridine) is compoud of formula (II). The inorganic semiconductor materials as photocatalysts are cadmium selenide, cadmium sulfide, zinc sulfide, zinc selenide, indium phosphide, cadmium selenide/cadmium sulfid, cadmium selenide/zinc sulfide, copper indium sulfide/zinc sulfide, cadmium telluride, copper indium sulfide and/or perovskite quantum dots; the cadmium selenide/cadmium sulfide is a core-shell structure with cadmium selenide as the core and cadmium sulfide as the shell, the cadmium selenide/zinc sulfide is a core-shell structure with cadmium selenide as the core and zinc sulfide as the shell, the copper indium sulfide/zinc sulfide is a core-shell structure with copper indium sulfide as the core and zinc sulfid as the shell, preferably, the perovskite quantum dots are cesium-lead compound of formula (CsPbX3). The inorganic semiconductor material as a photocatalyst has a ligand on the surface, preferably the ligand is a sulfhydryl compound. The electronic sacrificial body is triethylamine, triethanolamine, sodium ascorbate, ascorbic acid, sodium sulfide, sodium sulfite and/or isopropanol. The solvent is water, methanol, ethanol, acetonitrile, tetrahydrofuran and/or N,N-dimethylformamide. The concentration of the quaternary pyridine ammonium salt is greater than or equal to 1x10-7 mol/l, preferably 1x10-7 to 1x10-3 mol/l; the concentration of the inorganic semiconductor is greater than or equal to 1x10-7 mol/l, preferably 1x10-7-1x10-3 mol/l; the volume ratio of the solvent to the electronic sacrificial body is 4: (0.1-1).R1 and R2 = 1-16C aliphatic carbon chain;X- = halide ion or an acid radical ion;X = halo.Preferred Method: The wavelength of the illumination is visible light greater than 380 nm.

   Solvent (BB)

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: Manufacture of carbon dioxide absorbent further involves splitting the droplets, after mixing the solution (CC) and the solution (FF). The volume of the surfactant mixture is 0.125-0.5 times the volume of hydrocarbon oil. PT P
PN US2018230372-A1; US10131834-B2
TI Producing self-suspending proppant particles by coating proppant particles with polymerizable precursor material of carbon dioxide-philic material, polymerizing precursor and converting to self-suspending particles.
AU LIANG F
   AL-MUNTASHERI G A
   LI L
   REDDY B R
   JOHN G
   ADIYALA V
AE ARAMCO SERVICES CO (SAOI-C)
   UNIV NEW YORK CITY RES FOUND (UNYC-C)
   ARAMCO SERVICES CO (SAOI-C)
GA 201863429Q
AB    NOVELTY - Method for producing self-suspending proppant particles involves coating proppant particles with a polymerizable precursor material of a carbon dioxide-philic material to form coated proppant particles and polymerizing the polymerizable precursor material to convert the coated proppant particles to self-suspending proppant particles coated with the carbon dioxide-philic material in which the carbon dioxide-philic coating is lightly crosslinked and has a physical structure that constrains carbon dioxide molecules.
   USE - The method is used for producing self-suspending proppant particles (claimed), which is used in carbon dioxide-based hydraulic fracturing fluids for treating subterranean formations. The proppant particles are used to stimulate groundwater wells, to precondition or induce rock cave-ins for mining operations, to dispose waste by injecting it deeply into rock, to measure stress in the Earth's crust, to generate electricity in enhanced geothermal systems, or to increase injection rates for geologic sequestration of carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple and economical and produces non-aqueous hydraulic fracturing fluids that adequately support and suspend proppant particles.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: The method involves coating the proppant particles by contacting the polymerizable precursor material with the proppant particles in a fluidized bed process. The method further involves coating the proppant particles with an adhesion promoter before coating the proppant particles with the polymerizable precursor material of the carbon dioxide-philic material. The method further involves roughening the proppant particles before coating and producing the proppant particles having a rough surface with an arithmetic average roughness (R0) of greater than or equal to 1 nm. The method involves adding the self-suspending proppant particles to a water-based fluid, in which the carbon dioxide-philic coating does not dissociate from the proppant particles. Preferred Components: The carbon dioxide-philic material is chosen from polysaccharide acetates, polyethylene glycols, partially fluorinated oxygen-containing polymers, oxygenated polymers, crosslinked oxygen-containing polystyrenes, polyvinyl acetates and their combinations.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The activated carbon carrier is coal-based activated carbon, wood activated carbon, charcoal, shell charcoal and/or bamboo charcoal. The carbonate is selected from lithium carbonate, sodium carbonate and/or potassium carbonate. The hydroxide is selected from lithium hydroxide, sodium hydroxide and/or potassium hydroxide. The molar concentration of carbonate or hydroxide-containing Group (IA) element or Group (IIA) element is 0.15-0.25 mol/L. The molar concentration of mass transfer promoter-containing Group (IA) element is 0.15-0.3 mol/L. Preferred Properties: The specific surface area of activated carbon carrier is larger than 240 m2/g. The carbon dioxide solid adsorbent-based impregnated activated carbon is provided with specific surface area of greater than or equal to 200 m2/g and particle size of 1700-4800 mu m. PN KR2021014437-A; KR2255424-B1
TI Carbon dioxide reduction catalyst complex useful for electrochemically producing ethylene comprises copper (II) oxide nanoparticles having protruding branch structure, and conductive carbon support.
AU JUN S H
   KIM J M
   CHOI U
   SHIN D
   PARK J W
AE KOREA ADVANCED SCI & TECHNOLOGY INST (KOAD-C)
GA 202116865C
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide reduction catalyst complex comprises copper (II) oxide nanoparticles having a protruding branch structure, and conductive carbon support on which the copper (II) oxide nanoparticles are supported.
   USE - The catalyst complex is useful for electrochemically producing ethylene (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst complex: has a maximum ethylene Faraday efficiency of greater than or equal to 60% in a voltage range of -0.9 to -1.2 (V vs. RHE), and excellent selectivity of greater than or equal to 60% and durability, and ethylene Faraday efficiency of greater than or equal to 60% even during a reaction time of up to 12 hours.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) electrochemically reducing carbon dioxide using the carbon dioxide reduction catalyst complex; and
   (2) carbon dioxide reduction device comprising a working electrode which is an electrode containing the carbon dioxide reduction catalyst complex, a counter electrode of the working electrode, a separator positioned between the working electrode and the counter electrode, and a carbon dioxide-containing electrolyte.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The average size of the nanoparticles is 50-200 nm. The protruding branches are acicular carbon dioxide whose cross section is narrowed in the outer direction of the nanoparticles. The nanoparticles has a crystalline domain size of 10-25 nm. The carbon dioxide reduction catalyst complex contains 30-50 wt.% copper (II) oxide. The electrochemically active surface area (ECSA) of copper is greater than or equal to 0.25 cm2. The catalyst complex has a sum of ethylene Faraday efficiency and hydrogen Faraday efficiency of 90% in voltage range of -0.95 to -1.2 (V vs. RHE). The carbon dioxide reduction catalyst complex meets the requirement of equation 1 less than or equal to FEC2(10h)/FEC2(0.5h), where FEC2 (0.5h) is ethylene Faraday efficiency value at the time when the carbon dioxide reduction reaction was performed for 30 minutes under -1.1 V (V vs. RHE) conditions, and FEC2 (10h) is the ethylene Faraday efficiency value when the same carbon dioxide reduction reaction is performed for 10 hours. The catalyst complex increases the ethylene Faraday efficiency and decreases the hydrogen Faraday efficiency as the carbon dioxide supply rate increases. The copper (II) oxide aggregates in which primary particles form grain boundary and aggregate and a carbon support, the average radius of the primary particles is 3-10 nm. The average size of the aggregate is 50-200 nm. In the aggregate, copper (II) oxide nanoparticles of a protruding branch structure supported on a conductive carbon support are electrochemically bored.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC A41 (Monomers and Condensants These are also included in Section E.); E17 (Other aliphatics.); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)
