PT P
PN US2018318754-A1; US10843123-B2
TI Recovering ammoniacal nitrogen in e.g. ammonium carbonate form comprises e.g. introducing ammoniacal nitrogen-containing vapor into vessel and introducing carbon dioxide-containing vapor, and pumping ammoniacal nitrogen-containing solution.
AU WITARSA F
   LUPITSKYY R
   LANSING S
   MOSS A
AE MOSS A (MOSS-Individual)
   UNIV MARYLAND COLLEGE PARK (UMBA-C)
   UNIV MARYLAND COLLEGE PARK (UMBA-C)
   PLANET FOUND ENERGY DEV LLC (PLAN-Non-standard)
   MOSS A (MOSS-Individual)
GA 201887000J
AB    NOVELTY - Recovering ammoniacal nitrogen, in ammonium carbonate and/or ammonium bicarbonate form, from ammoniacal nitrogen-containing vapor comprises: introducing vapor into at least one pressurizable ammonia scrubbing vessel containing water to produce ammoniacal nitrogen-containing solution; simultaneously introducing a carbon dioxide-containing vapor into at least one pressurizable carbon dioxide scrubbing vessel containing water to produce carbon dioxide-containing solution; simultaneously pumping ammoniacal nitrogen-containing solution in pressurizable ammonia scrubbing vessel and carbon dioxide-containing solution in carbon dioxide scrubbing vessel to and from pressurizable ammonia scrubbing vessel and pressurizable carbon dioxide scrubbing vessel to create ammonium carbonate and/or ammonium bicarbonate mixture; recovering ammonium carbonate and/or ammonium bicarbonate for further use; and maintaining independent and unmixed vapor streams throughout the entire process.
   USE - The method is useful for recovering ammoniacal nitrogen, in form of ammonium carbonate and/or ammonium bicarbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method reduces costs associated with acid addition in ammoniacal-nitrogen recovery process, and allows for the simultaneous treatment of two independent vapors.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The pressurizable ammonia scrubbing vessel is at least two pressurizable ammonia scrubbing vessels that operate in series and/or in parallel. The pressurizable carbon dioxide scrubbing vessel is at least two pressurizable carbon dioxide scrubbing vessels that operate in series and/or in parallel.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The process further comprises: adding ammoniacal nitrogen-containing solution to the ammonium carbonate and/or ammonium bicarbonate containing mixture being mixed between the scrubbing vessels to increase the concentration of ammonia in the mixture; cooling the ammonium carbonate and/or ammonium bicarbonate containing mixture via mechanical and/or geothermal heat exchange, where the mixture in the scrubbing vessels is maintained at or below 30 degrees C. The ammonium carbonate and/or ammonium bicarbonate containing mixture in the pressurizable ammonia scrubbing vessel and the at least one pressurizable carbon dioxide scrubbing vessel is circulated within the pressurizable ammonia scrubbing vessel and the pressurizable carbon dioxide scrubbing vessel to produce a countercurrent flow of the ammonium carbonate and/or ammonium bicarbonate containing mixture to the incoming ammoniacal nitrogen-containing and/or carbon dioxide-containing vapor. The ammoniacal nitrogen-containing vapor is entrained in the ammonium carbonate and/or ammonium bicarbonate containing mixture prior to delivery to the pressurizable ammonia scrubbing vessel. The carbon dioxide-containing vapor is entrained in the ammonium carbonate and/or ammonium bicarbonate containing mixture prior to delivery to the pressurizable carbon dioxide scrubbing vessel. The introduction of the carbon dioxide-containing vapor into the pressurizable carbon dioxide scrubbing vessel is monitored and controlled to provide a quantity of carbon dioxide that approximates the stoichiometric ratio of accumulating ammoniacal nitrogen in the pressurizable ammonia scrubbing vessel that is necessary for the formation of ammonium carbonate and/or ammonium bicarbonate in the scrubber solution. The ammoniacal nitrogen-containing vapor is recirculated to the pressurizable ammonia scrubbing vessel to further reduce a proportion of ammoniacal nitrogen present in the ammoniacal nitrogen-containing vapor. The carbon dioxide-containing vapor is recirculated to the pressurizable carbon dioxide scrubbing vessel in order to further reduce a proportion of carbon dioxide present in the carbon dioxide-containing vapor. The recovery of a concentrated liquid ammonium carbonate and/or ammonium bicarbonate mixture and/or a solid ammonium carbonate and/or ammonium bicarbonate is accomplished via gravity settling, centrifusion, and/or filtration. Preferred Components: The pressurizable ammonia scrubbing vessel and the pressurizable carbon dioxide scrubbing vessel contains packing material(s) that are synthetic and/or natural in composition; and/or sieve plates, impingement plates, bubble plates and/or valve plates that are synthetic and/or natural in composition. The water resulting from the settling, centrifusion, and/or filtration is returned to the pressurizable ammonia scrubbing vessel and the pressurizable carbon dioxide scrubbing vessel for further use.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC E35; J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

PN WO2018078585-A1; IN201947021034-A; IN381852-B
TI Preparation of methanol from carbon dioxide using heterocyclic carbene-borane adduct as intermediate, involves reacting abnormal heterocyclic carbene or its adduct with borane derivative to obtain methoxy borane derivative, and hydrolyzing.
AU MANDAL S K
   SAU S C
AE MANDAL S K (MAND-Individual)
   SAU S C (SAUS-Individual)
   MANDAL S K (MAND-Individual)
   SAU S C (SAUS-Individual)
GA 2018341879
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of methanol from carbon dioxide involves reacting abnormal heterocyclic carbene or its adduct with a borane derivative in presence of carbon dioxide to obtain methoxy borane derivative, and hydrolyzing the methoxy borane derivative.
   USE - Preparation of methanol from carbon dioxide using abnormal heterocyclic carbene-borane adduct as intermediate (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables simple, economical and efficient preparation of methanol having high purity from carbon dioxide with enhanced yield using carbene or its adduct, under mild reaction conditions.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) preparation abnormal heterocyclic carbene-borane adduct of formula (I), which involves reacting abnormal N-heterocylic carbene of formula (II) with 9-borabicyclo(3.3.1)nonane; and
   (2) preparation of formic acid from carbon dioxide, which involves (i) reacting carbene with borane to obtain carbine-borane adduct, (ii) reacting carbine-borane adduct with carbon dioxide to obtain carbene-borane adduct formate, (iii) reacting resultant with sodium hydroxide to obtain sodium formate, and (iv) hydrolyzing sodium formate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Properties: The purity of the carbon dioxide is 50-99.999%, preferably 99.995%.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The abnormal heterocyclic carbene adduct is chosen from abnormal heterocyclic carbine-carbon dioxide adduct and abnormal heterocyclic carbine-borane adduct, preferably chosen from compounds (I) and (II), and compound of formulae (III)-(X). The borane derivative is chosen from 9-borabicyclo(3.3.1)nonane, catecholborane, pinacolborane, borane dimethyl sulfide, borane tetrahydrofuran, and/or ammonia borane, preferably 9-borabicyclo(3.3.1)nonane. The methoxy borane derivative is chosen from beta -methoxy-4,4,5,5-tetramethyl- (l,3,2)dioxaborolane, beta -methoxy-catecholborane, beta -methoxy-4,4,5,5-tetramethyl-(l,3,2)dioxaborolane, trimethyl borate-dimethyl sulfide, ammonia-trimethyl borate and trimethyl borate-tetrahydrofuran.R1-R4 = alkyl or aryl;andX = N, O, or S.Preferred Properties: The concentration of the carbene or its adduct is 0.02-5 mol%, preferably 0.1 mol%. The concentration of the borane derivative is 2-5 mmol, preferably 3.4 mmol. The methanol has purity of greater than 90%. Preferred Process: The reactions are carried out in presence of solvent(s) which are organic solvent chosen from tetrahydrofuran, toluene, benzene, bromobenzene, acetonitrile, dimethyl sulfoxide and dimethylformamide. The process further involves isolating and purifying the corresponding products in each step. The isolation and purification is carried out by addition of solvent, quenching, filtration, and/or extraction. The reaction mixture of reactants comprising carbene and solvent of the process is degassed before introducing carbon dioxide into the reaction mixture. The step (i) is carried out in presence of solvent selected from toluene, benzene and tetrahydrofuran. The step (ii) is carried out in solvent selected from toluene, benzene, tetrahydrofuran and water. The step (iv) is carried out in water. Preferred Conditions: The step (a) is carried out at 20-50 degrees C and 1-5 atm, preferably 1 atm of carbon dioxide for 2-10 hours. The preparation of formic acid from carbon dioxide is carried out at 20-50 degrees C for 28-42 hours.

PN CN109824635-A
TI Synthesis of dialkyl 2,5-furandicarboxylate by catalytically oxidizing furfural to furancarboxylic acid using spinel-type copper aluminate catalyst, adding tannic acid, carbonate and alcohol, sealing, purging nitrogen, and adding carbon dioxide.
AU MA Y
   WANG L
   DI N
   LI X
   CHEN Z
   JIE F
AE CHONGQING CHEM IND VOCATIONAL COLLEGE (CHON-Non-standard)
GA 201950048U
AB    NOVELTY - A synthesis of dialkyl 2,5-furandicarboxylate comprises catalytically oxidizing furfural to furancarboxylic acid using spinel-type copper aluminate as catalyst, adding tannic acid, carbonate and alcohol, sealing, purging nitrogen for 3 times, adding carbon dioxide at 1-4 MPa, and reacting to 200-240 degrees C for 6-24 hours.
   USE - Method for synthesis of dialkyl 2,5-furandicarboxylate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has wide source of raw materials, mild reaction conditions, 90-95% target product yield, 100% target product selectivity, and short reaction time.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The spinel-type copper aluminate catalyst is prepared by mixing copper hydroxide and pseudoboehmite, ball milling for 4-12 hours, and calcining to 700-1100 degrees C for 2-5 hours under an air atmosphere. Preferred Components: The molar ratio of copper hydroxide and pseudoboehmite is 1:2-1:6. The carbonate is sodium carbonate, potassium carbonate, rubidium carbonate and/or cesium carbonate.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The mass ratio of catalyst and furfural is 0.1:1-0.3:1. The alcohol is methanol, ethanol, propanol or butanol. The mass ratio of carbonate and tannic acid is 0.1:1-0.3:1. The molar ratio of alcohol and tannic acid is 2:1-10:1.

PT P
PN CN109704386-A; CN109704386-B
TI Biogas residue fertilization method agricultural planting, involves placing biogas residue in closed container filled with ionic liquid, and performing carbon dioxide supercritical treatment on biogas residue.
AU DAI H
   LIU K
   GONG K
   LIU L
   CHEN L
   LI X
   SUN L
AE CROP RES INST SHANDONG ACAD AGRIC SCI (SDAC-C)
GA 201942321F
AB    NOVELTY - The biogas residue fertilization method involves placing biogas residue in a closed container filled with an ionic liquid, without being in contact with the ionic liquid, and performing carbon dioxide supercritical treatment on the biogas residue.
   USE - Biogas residue fertilization method for agricultural planting (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The dissolution of the nutrient elements of the biogas residue is effectively increased, and bonding reaction of the amino acid ionic liquid and the biogas residue is reduced, thus reducing the loss of nutrient elements, effectively improving the yield increase effect of the biogas residue farmland and crop yields.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: The biogas residue comprises biogas residue formed by fermentation of livestock manure and plant straw, or biogas residue formed by sludge. Preferred Process: The biogas residue fertilization method involves pulverizing the biogas residue, performing vacuum drying before the supercritical treatment and fertilizing supercritically treated biogas residue on the farmland.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The ionic liquid is amino acid ionic liquid, and includes glycine nitrate, glutamic acid nitrate, and/or valine nitrate. Preferred Compositions: The ionic liquid is added in an amount of 0.5-1% by volume of the reaction vessel. Preferred Conditions: The temperature of the supercritical treatment is 40-60 degrees C pressure is 20-30 MPa., supercritical treatment time is 5-15 minutes.
   ACTIVITY - Fertilizer.
DC C04 (Fertilisers - including urea and phosphoric acid production. Also soil modifiers and plant growth media. Chemical aspects of compost production.); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); P11 (Soil working, planting (A01B, C).)

PT P
PN CN109089759-A
TI Increasing crop yield during continuous cropping, by placing soil on waterproof surface or substrate tank, disinfecting, taking three-layer co-extruded composite film as greenhouse film, and controlling humidity by dehumidification.
AU LIN X
AE DONGGUAN JIFENG AGRIC ECOLOGICAL PARK (DONG-Non-standard)
GA 201903246G
AB    NOVELTY - Method for increasing crop yield during continuous cropping, involves placing soil on a waterproof surface or substrate tank, when cropping, spraying with a high-pressure water gun, disinfecting soil, taking three-layer co-extruded composite film as a greenhouse film, using a fermentation tank in a greenhouse to produce carbon dioxide as a supplement to greenhouse carbon dioxide, and controlling humidity by artificial dehumidification.
   USE - The method is useful for increasing crop yield during continuous cropping.
   ADVANTAGE - The method reduces plant diseases and insect pests.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - AGRICULTURE - Preferred Method: The soil is disinfected using solar energy or air energy or steam to perform high temperature sterilization. The soil disinfection further involves applying nutrient material to soil after disinfection to provide nutrients to the soil. The nutrient material comprises 80-90 pts. wt. coated expanded perlite, 30-40 pts. wt. seaweed mud, 20-30 pts. wt. fly ash, 5-10 pts. wt. distiller's grains, 10-20 pts. wt. peat, 1-3 pts. wt. trace elements and 100-120 pts. wt. water. The dehumidification is performed by covering soil layer of greenhouse with covering composed of organic waste to thickness of 1-3 cm. The organic waste comprises 30-60 pts. wt. straw, 30-60 pts. wt. wheat bran, 1-5 pts. wt. biomass charcoal, 5-10 pts. wt. wood residue, and 1-5 pts. wt. waste newspapers. The organic waste is taken as a raw material for fermentation in fermentation tank after dehumidification.
DC P11 (Soil working, planting (A01B, C).); P13 (Plant culture, dairy products (A01G, H, J).)
MC P11-B01; P13-A04

PN CN108102788-A; CN108102788-B
TI Extraction of essential oil from lemon leaves includes collecting fresh lemon leaves, washing, drying, crushing, sieving, adding methanol, leaching, subjecting to supercritical carbon dioxide extraction and distilling.
AU DAI L
   ZHANG B
   MA L
   LONG L
AE CHONGQING HUIDA LEMON TECHNOLOGY GROUP (CHON-Non-standard)
   CHONGQING HUIDA BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (CHON-Non-standard)
GA 201844983W
AB    NOVELTY - Extraction of essential oil from lemon leaves comprises collecting fresh lemon leaves, washing, drying, crushing, sieving to obtain 20-40 mesh powder, adding methanol, leaching for 4-6 hours at 18-25 degrees C, drying, subjecting evaporated residue to supercritical carbon dioxide extraction under 20-30 MPa at 45-60 degrees C for 1-3 hours and distilling under 0.1-0.3 MPa at 140-160 degrees C.
   USE - Method of extracting essential oil from lemon leaves.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves extraction to more than 0.4%.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The supercritical carbon dioxide extraction pressure is 20 MPa. The supercritical carbon dioxide extraction temperature is 60 degrees C. The supercritical carbon dioxide extraction time is 3 hours. The distillation pressure is 0.3 MPa. The distillation temperature is 160 degrees C.

A heat exchange device (105) is configured to exchange heat between a stream of ambient temperature air (106) and the carbon dioxide at delivery pressure, thus, producing a vaporized carbon dioxide stream. A backpressure regulator (109) is configured to maintain the vaporized carbon dioxide above a minimum delivery pressure. PN CN113845499-A
TI Preparing 2,5-furandicarboxylic acid using carbon dioxide by nitrogen-heterocyclic carbene catalysis, comprises e.g. reacting furoate ester, nitrogen-heterocyclic carbene (NHC) and metal compound catalyst by passing carbon dioxide, reacting obtained product with alkali, and acidifying with acid.
AU GUO K
   SHEN L
   LI Y
   HE W
   LIU F
   ZHANG K
AE NANJING ADVANCED BIOMATERIAL & PROCESS (NANJ-Non-standard)
GA 202214656A
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing 2,5-furandicarboxylic acid (IV) using carbon dioxide by nitrogen-heterocyclic carbene catalysis, comprises (1) dissolving furoate ester (I), nitrogen-heterocyclic carbene (NHC) and metal compound catalyst in first solvent, and passing carbon dioxide to react to obtain 2,5-furandicarboxylic acid monoester (II), (2) dissolving (II) and alkali in second solvent, and reacting to obtain 2,5-furandicarboxylate salt (III), and (3) dissolving (III) in deionized water, and acidifying with acid to obtain final product.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing 2,5-furandicarboxylic acid.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has simple reaction system, and high product yield; has no intermediate oxidation product accumulation; is suitable for industrial production; utilizes cheap and easily available carbon dioxide, ensures efficient conversion of carbon dioxide into products with high economic added value, and thus reduces carbon dioxide emission.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing 2,5-furandicarboxylic acid of formula (IV) using carbon dioxide by nitrogen-heterocyclic carbene catalysis, comprises (1) dissolving furoate ester of formula (I), nitrogen-heterocyclic carbene (NHC) and metal compound catalyst in first solvent, and passing carbon dioxide to react to obtain 2,5-furandicarboxylic acid monoester of formula (II), (2) dissolving (II) and alkali in second solvent, and reacting to obtain 2,5-furandicarboxylate salt of formula (III), and (3) dissolving (III) in deionized water, and acidifying with acid to obtain final product.
   R = 1-5C alkyl.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In step (1): the reaction is carried out in carbon dioxide atmosphere for 36-48 hours at 130-150 degreesC. In step (1), the solvent is removed from the reaction mixture under vacuum after the reaction is completed, and then (II) is obtained as white solid by column chromatography. In step (2), the reaction temperature is 60-75 degrees Celsius, and the reaction time is 12-16 hours. In step (3), the crude 2,5-furandicarboxylate salt is obtained after acidification, the solvent is removed under vacuum, deionized water is added, then extracted with dichloromethane, the organic layer is acidified with concentrated hydrochloric acid until pH is 2, the heterogeneous solution is extracted with ethyl acetate, the organic layer is dried over anhydrous sodium sulfate, and the solvent is removed under vacuum to obtain (IV).Preferred Components: The nitrogen-heterocyclic carbene is imidazole-based nitrogen-heterocyclic carbene, preferably 7,9-bis(2,6-diisopropylphenyl)-7H-acenaphtho(1,2-d)imidazol-9-ium bromide. In step (1): (I) is methyl furoate, ethyl furoate or propyl furoate; the metal compound catalyst is rhodium chloride, rhodium acetate, rhodium nitrate or rhodium octanoate; the first solvent is N,N-dimethylformamide; and the molar ratio between (I), nitrogen-heterocyclic carbene, metal compound catalyst and first solvent is 1:0.008-0.013:0.015-0.025:100. In step (2): alkali is sodium hydroxide or potassium hydroxide; the second solvent is methanol or ethanol; the molar ratio of (II) to alkali is 1:1-1.1; and the amount of the second solvent is 8-10 times the total mass of (II) and alkali. In step (3), the acid is non-oxidizing strong acid, preferably concentrated hydrochloric acid.

MC E11-F02B; E07-A01; E11-W; N02-E02; N07-D02B

AB    NOVELTY - The measuring method involves leading the primary gas of the waste incineration exhaust gas into a primary processing chamber, and processing by using sodium hydroxide solution in the primary treatment chamber. The sulfur oxide is neutralized in primary gas, and obtained the secondary gas containing mixed gas of carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide. The second secondary gas is introduced into the secondary treatment chamber, and used the heat generated in the wastewater incineration process to heat the second treatment room. The carbon monoxides in the secondary air are converted into carbon dioxide to obtain three-stage gas, and then introduced the third-stage air into the detection room. The content of carbon dioxide in the third stage gas is detected in the detection chamber.
   USE - Method for measuring and calculating waste incineration processing carbon dioxide emission in a medical industry.
   ADVANTAGE - The waste incineration processing carbon dioxide emission measuring method can effectively obtain the emission of carbon dioxide, and has accurate calculation, simple process, and can realize better economical efficiency. By setting the filtering device, the solid particles under the driving of the heat flow into the first stage processing chamber, the influence of the processing effect can be avoided. The device is also capable of recycling the sulphur oxide generated by the waste incinerating, at the same time of avoiding the air pollution.

PT P
PN CN111253780-A
TI Preparing transparent nano calcium carbonate useful for high-grade ink, comprises introducing carbon dioxide gas into calcium chloride slurry, carbonizing, adding coating agent into nano calcium carbonate slurry, drying and pulverizing.
AU ZHANG H
   ZHANG Z
   LI J
   LEI T
   HU J
   LI Y
AE JIANGXI SHITONG CALCIUM IND CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 2020536599
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing transparent nano calcium carbonate comprises (i) introducing carbon dioxide gas into calcium chloride slurry for carbonization, adding crystal regulator to continue carbonization, adding surface charge and steric hindrance modifier, and continuously carbonizing to pH 6.5-7.0 to generate nano calcium carbonate slurry; (ii) adding a coating agent into nano calcium carbonate slurry to perform wet coating; and (iii) de-watering, drying and pulverizing the nano calcium carbonate slurry.
   USE - The transparent nano calcium carbonate is useful for high-grade ink.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has high concentration of calcium ion in the slurry. The nanometer calcium carbonate particles are uniform and the particle size is small, has good gloss, transparency and fluidity in high-grade inks.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIM are also included for:
   (1) transparent nano-calcium carbonate; and
   (2) device for preparing transparent nano calcium carbonate, comprising a carbonization device including a carbonization tower, a carbon dioxide filter, a carbon dioxide compressor, a carbon dioxide water vapor separator and a carbon dioxide gas distributor connected in sequence. The carbonization tower has a calcium chloride slurry inlet and multiple carbon dioxide inlets. An atomizing device for atomizing the calcium chloride slurry is provided at the entrance of the calcium chloride slurry. Each carbon dioxide inlet is provided with a nozzle is connected with the inside of the carbonization tower. The air inlets of the nozzles are all connected to the carbon dioxide water vapor separator.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (i), carbonizing to pH 8.0-9.0 after adding crystal form modifier, where the crystal form modifier is sodium hexametaphosphate, sodium citrate, maltose, tartaric acid and/or lactic acid and the addition amount of the crystal form modifier is 0.01-0.5% of the weight of the nano calcium carbonate. In the step (ii), configuring the coating agent as an aqueous solution with a mass fraction of 8-12%, heating to 70-90 degrees C to prepare the coating liquid, adding a coating liquid into the nano calcium carbonate slurry to perform wet coating, where the coating time is 0.5-1.5 hours. The step (i) further includes adding hydrochloric acid into the calcium hydroxide slurry to generate calcium chloride slurry, where the particle size of calcium hydroxide in the calcium hydroxide slurry is 1-3 mu m, and the concentration of hydrochloric acid is 4-8 mol/l. Preferred Components: The surface charge and steric hindrance modifier is aluminum sulfate, sodium dodecylbenzenesulfonate, magnesium chloride, magnesium sulfate, and/or sodium polyacrylate and the amount of surface charge and steric hindrance regulator added is 0.01-0.1% of the weight of nano calcium carbonate. The coating agent is resin acids and its salts, and the amount of the coating agent added is 2.5-3.5% by weight of the nano calcium carbonate. The average particle size of the nano calcium carbonate is 15-40 nm, the shape is spherical or spherical, with a specific surface area greater than 35 m2/g, and the particle size distribution GSD is 1.5-1.6.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The digestion device includes a digestion tank, a calcium hydroxide slurry pump, a sand mill and a calcium chloride slurry tank connected in sequence. The digestion tank has a calcium oxide inlet, a hot water inlet and a slurry outlet. The digestion tank is provided with a stirring blade and a sieve plate. The top of the digestion tank is provided with a motor for driving the rotation of the stirring blade. The slurry outlet on the digestion tank is connected to the sand mill through a calcium hydroxide slurry pump. The calcium chloride slurry tank has a hydrochloric acid inlet, a slurry inlet for connecting with the sand mill, and a calcium chloride slurry outlet for connecting with the carbonization device. The calcium chloride slurry tank is equipped with stirring blades. The top of the calcium chloride slurry tank is provided with a motor for driving the rotation of the stirring blade.

TI Method for increasing production of coalbed methane (CBM) by injecting carbon dioxide into exhausted coalbed methane group, involves driving methane gas into CBM well, and closing well immediately when carbon dioxide gas is detected.
AU LI D
   LI B
   LIU L
   ZHANG J
   YANG Y
   ZHANG Y

   QIN Y

   CUI Y
AE SHANXI JINCHENG ANTHRACITE MINING IND GR (JCAM-C)
GA 202032268B
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves selecting the gas production block of the depleted CBM well. A CBM well that does not produce water or gas in the middle of the CBM well group is adopted as a carbon dioxide pressurized injection well. The carbon dioxide is directly injected into the coal reservoir of this well. The pressurized carbon dioxide is set to diffuse along the range of the pressure drop funnel and gradually flown into the surrounding coalbed methane wells to displace the methane gas in the coal reservoir. The methane gas is driven into the adjacent CBM well, and the well closed immediately when carbon dioxide gas is detected in the well group until the wellhead pressure of the CBM well injected with carbon dioxide rises to the point where the coal seam fracture pressure in the well casing fracturing, production stops and the production increase ends.
   USE - Method for increasing production of coalbed methane (CBM) by injecting carbon dioxide into exhausted coalbed methane group.
   ADVANTAGE - The coal bed methane recovery rate is maximized in the coal reservoir, and is especially suitable for CBM production wells that entered the depletion stage without water. The production of coalbed methane in coal seams is accelerated, and the economic value of depleted coalbed methane wells is increased. The continuous production increase among coalbed methane well groups are realized.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view illustrating the method for increasing production of CBM by injecting carbon dioxide into exhausted coalbed methane group.

The gas supply flow path has a                first opening provided on one end side, a second                opening provided on the other end side, and an                auxiliary flow path provided to a portion of a flow                path between the first and second opening, and                configured to make at least the rinse material flow                through it.
   USE - Carbon dioxide electrolytic device.
   ADVANTAGE - By making the rinse material flow from the                second opening toward the first opening of the                carbon dioxide (CO2) gas flow path, it is possible                to efficiently dissolve the salt. Specifically, the                precipitation amount of salt is small on the second                opening side, so that in a state where a                concentration of the salt component in the                introduced rinse material such as the rinse                solution is suppressed, it is possible to send the                solution to the first opening side to cause elution                of the salt, resulting in that the elution of salt                can be caused efficiently by a small amount of the                rinse material. Therefore, by mixing the CO2gas and                the rinse material and making the mixture flow                through the CO2gas flow path, the rinse operation                can be performed while the reaction is                continued.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Carbon dioxide electrolytic device comprises a                first electrolysis cell including a cathode to                reduce carbon dioxide to produce a carbon compound,                an anode to oxidize water to produce oxygen, a gas                supply flow path to supply carbon dioxide to the                cathode, a solution supply flow path to supply an                electrolytic solution containing water to the                anode, and a separator to separate the anode from                the cathode, a carbon dioxide supply unit to supply                the carbon dioxide to the gas supply flow path, an                electrolytic solution supply unit to supply the                electrolytic solution to the solution supply flow                path, and a rinse material supply unit to supply a                rinse material to the gas supply flow path. The gas                supply flow path has a first opening provided on                one end side, a second opening provided on the                other end side, and an auxiliary flow path provided                to a portion of a flow path between the first                opening and the second opening, and configured to                make at least the rinse material flow through it,                and a switching mechanism configured to switch a                flow direction of the rinse material in the                auxiliary flow path is connected to the gas supply                flow path.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                of electrolyzing carbon dioxide, which involves:                (a) supplying carbon dioxide from a carbon dioxide                supply unit to a gas supply flow path of a first                electrolysis cell, and supplying an electrolytic                solution from an electrolytic solution supply unit                to a solution supply flow path of the first                electrolysis cell, the first electrolysis cell                including a cathode, an anode, the gas supply flow                path to supply carbon dioxide to the cathode, the                solution supply flow path to supply the                electrolytic solution to the anode, and a separator                configured to separate the anode from the cathode;                (b) making a rinse material flow through an                auxiliary flow path provided to a part of a flow                path between a first opening and a second opening                of the gas supply flow path, simultaneously with                the supply of the carbon dioxide; (c) applying a                voltage from a power supply connected to the anode                and the cathode, reducing carbon dioxide in the                vicinity of the cathode of the first electrolysis                cell to produce a carbon compound, and oxidizing                water in the vicinity of the anode to produce                oxygen; and (cdswitching a flow direction of the                rinse material in the auxiliary flow path to rinse                the inside of the gas supply flow path with the                rinse material that exists in the auxiliary flow                path.

gel, (ii) placing the drying kettle drying aerogel                in advance before each batch is dried, fully                immersing in solvent, and the supercritical carbon                dioxide entering the kettle is fully mixing with                aerogel felt and replacing after many cycles and                fully occupying all voids in aerogel, and then                dissolving the supercritical carbon dioxide fluid                in which the solvent flows out of the extraction                kettle, after vacuum extracting to 4-7 MPa through                throttle valve, separating the carbon from the                supercritical state and converting into gaseous                state, passing the liquid solvent enters the                separation kettle as mixture, (iii) rapidly                dropping the solubility of solvent in gaseous                carbon dioxide, quickly resolving from gaseous                carbon dioxide and stripped down, and finally                discharging from bottom of the separation kettle,                escaping gaseous carbon dioxide from the solvent                and from top of the separation kettle, (iv)                separating the gaseous carbon dioxide from                separation kettle escaping from top of the                separation kettle, passing first-stage condenser to                make carbon dioxide, fully condensing the solvent                in solution, and passing the liquid phase outlet of                separation kettle from bottom of the condenser, and                escaping gas phase escapes from top of condenser,                passing secondary condenser, condensing into liquid                phase, and returning into intermediate storage                tank, (v) separating liquid solvent using                separation kettle flows out from bottom of the                separation kettle, mixing with solvent condensed                from the primary condenser, decompresses through                the primary decompression valve, passing primary                flash tank, and is separating after flashing, after                passing the carbon dioxide gas into the first stage                compressor and compressing to 4.8-6.5 MPa,                returning into inlet of second stage condenser,                after confluent with gas at top of separation                kettle, i returning to intermediate storage tank of                carbon dioxide to the second stage pressure                reducing valve to reduce pressure, (vi) vacuum                extracting the liquid in bottom of the first-stage                flash tank by second-stage pressure reducing valve,                and then passing the second-stage flash tank, and                separating the carbon dioxide gas, flashing enters                the second-stage compressor to be compressed to                first-stage flash pressure, the compressed carbon                dioxide gas returning to inlet of the primary                compressor, the liquid phase of the secondary flash                tank flows out from the bottom, reducing pressure                to three-stage pressure reducing valve, (vii)                vacuum extracting liquid in the bottom of the                secondary flash tank by tertiary pressure reducing                valve and then passing into tertiary flash tank,                separating the carbon dioxide gas after flashing                passing into tertiary compressor and compressing                into the secondary flash pressure, returning the                carbon dioxide gas into inlet of the second stage                compressor, the three-stage flash liquid phase                flows out from the bottom to the rectification                operation unit, and (viii) flowing the liquid phase                from the bottom of the three-stage flash tank                passing the rectification tower, determining the                rectification pressure to difference between the                boiling points of the solvent and heavy components,                and distilling the high-purity solvent from the top                of the rectification tower for recovery and                storage, reusing, and the top of the tower,                returning the non-condensable carbon dioxide to                inlet of the three-stage compressor and recovering                as drying medium, discharging bottom of tower                discharges high-boiling materials containing heavy                solvents and other impurities as waste water                efflux.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In step (iv), adding                  first-stage condenser into gas outlet of the                  separation kettle, setting temperature fully                  consider the premise of carbon dioxide not                  condense with solvent and cause excessive loss of                  drying medium, ensuring the solvent recovered as                  much as possible by condensation, the temperature                  of first-level condenser is 24-40 degrees C and                  the temperature of secondary condenser is 5-23                  degrees C. In step (v), the flash pressure of                  first-level flash tank is 3.2-4.8 MPa. In step                  (vi), flash pressure of secondary flash tank is                  1.2-2.8 MPa. In step (vii), upper limit of the                  three-stage flash pressure consider whether the                  separation effect of carbon dioxide gas and                  solvent is ideal, and the lower limit should                  consider the selection and equipment cost of                  three-stage compressor in pressurized at 0.05-1.1                  MPa. In step (viii), the pressure of                  rectification column ensuring the purity of                  distillate solvent is guarantee for recycling,                  necessary to maintain the temperature of tower                  kettle not too high and loss of heat source,                  preferably 0.1-0.4 MPa. The recovery device                  comprises in the recovery method, adding primary                  condenser at gas phase outlet of separation                  kettle, condensing and separating the solvent                  dissolved in the gas phase carbon dioxide in                  advance, merge with the liquid phase at the                  bottom of the separation kettle, and entering                  into downstream to recover the solvent,                  separating the kettle liquid, designing                  multi-stage flash tank downstream of the phase,                  reducing pressure step by step, and compressing                  the recovered carbon dioxide gas through                  different stages of compressors to recover the                  solvent, the last stage of flash compression,                  adding distillation unit to recover most of the                  high-purity solvents only very small part                  discharging with high boiling point of                  rectification heavy components.

TI Promoting absorption of carbon dioxide by magnetic            metal organic framework material immobilized carbonic            anhydrase involves dispersing magnetic metal organic            framework material immobilized carbonic anhydrase, and            introducing carbon dioxide.
AU ZHANG S
   YING Q
   CHEN J
   SHAO P
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG TECHNOLOGY (UYZT-C)
GA 202131433E
AB    NOVELTY - Promoting absorption of carbon dioxide by                using magnetic metal organic framework material                immobilized carbonic anhydrase comprises uniformly                dispersing magnetic metal organic framework                material immobilized carbonic anhydrase in                potassium carbonate solution to obtain carbon                dioxide absorption liquid, and introducing carbon                dioxide into the carbon dioxide absorption liquid                while continuously stirring.
   USE - The method is useful for promoting absorption                of carbon dioxide by using magnetic metal organic                framework material immobilized carbonic                anhydrase.
   ADVANTAGE - The method uses adsorption method to fix                carbonic anhydrase to obtain immobilized enzyme                particles with magnetic property, which is good for                efficient recycling of immobilized enzyme.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Promoting absorption of carbon dioxide by                using magnetic metal organic framework material                immobilized carbonic anhydrase comprises uniformly                dispersing magnetic metal organic framework                material immobilized carbonic anhydrase in                potassium carbonate solution to obtain carbon                dioxide absorption liquid, and introducing carbon                dioxide into the carbon dioxide absorption liquid                while continuously stirring, where the preparation                of magnetic metal organic framework material                immobilized carbonic anhydrase comprising (i)                mixing ferric chloride hexahydrate, ethylene glycol                and sodium acetate, then placing in a reaction                kettle, reacting at 160-200 degrees C for 8-16                hours in the reaction kettle, collecting the                prepared ferroferric oxide magnetic microsphere by                a magnet, using absolute ethyl alcohol to wash and                drying, (ii) dispersing the ferroferric oxide                magnetic microsphere prepared in the step (i) in                the sodium polystyrene sulfonate aqueous solution,                ultrasonically treating for 15-25 minutes,                recycling sample by magnet, and washing with                de-ionized water, (iii) dispersing the material                prepared in the step (ii) in methanol, then adding                2-methyl imidazole and zinc nitrate hexahydrate,                stirring and reacting for 2-3 hours at 50-60                degrees C, separating by magnet, washing with                absolute ethyl alcohol, vacuum drying at 55-65                degrees C to obtain the magnetic metal organic                framework material, (iv) uniformly dispersing the                magnetic metal organic framework material obtained                in the step (iii) in de-ionized water to obtain                mixed solution, then adding carbonic anhydrase                aqueous solution into the obtained mixed solution,                stirring at 25-30 degrees C, reacting for 0.5-1.5                hours, collecting by magnet, using de-ionized                water, washing to obtain the magnetic metal organic                framework material immobilized carbonic anhydrase.                INDEPENDENT CLAIMs are also included for:
   (1) carbon dioxide absorbing liquid comprising                potassium carbonate, water and magnetic metal                organic framework material immobilized carbonic                anhydrase; and
   (2) use of carbonic anhydrase immobilized on                magnetic metal-organic framework materials in                promoting the absorption of carbon dioxide in                potassium carbonate solution.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method comprises                  controlling the temperature of the carbon dioxide                  absorption reaction at 35-45 degrees C. The                  method comprises ensuring the rotating speed of                  the continuous stirring at a rate of 100-150                  revolutions/minute. Preferred Components: The                  mass ratio of the magnetic metal organic                  framework material to the carbonic anhydrase in                  the carbonic anhydrase aqueous solution is 1:0.1.                  The mass concentration of carbonic anhydrase in                  the aqueous carbonic anhydrase solution is 10                  mg/ml. The mass concentration of the magnetic                  metal organic framework material in the mixed                  solution is 2.5 mg/ml. The concentration of the                  potassium carbonate in the potassium carbonate                  solution is 0.1mol/L, the 10mg/35mL;                  concentration of the immobilized carbonic                  anhydrase of the magnetic metal organic framework                  material in the carbon dioxide absorption liquid                  is relative to 35mL of potassium carbonate                  solution, the carbon dioxide is 150-200mL/min.                  The concentration of potassium carbonate in the                  potassium carbonate solution is 0.1 mol/l. The                  mass concentration of the carbonic anhydrase                  immobilized on the magnetic metal-organic                  framework material in the carbon dioxide                  absorption solution is 10 mg/35 ml relative to 35                  ml of potassium carbonate solution. The carbon                  dioxide feed rate is 150-200 ml/minute. The ratio                  of the ferric chloride hexahydrate, ethylene                  glycol and sodium acetate in the step (i) is 2.7                  g:50 ml: 5.75 g. The ratio of the ferroferric                  oxide magnetic microspheres to the aqueous                  solution of sodium polystyrene sulfonate in the                  step (ii) is 0.2 g:600 ml. The amount of sodium                  polystyrene sulfonate in the aqueous solution of                  sodium polystyrene sulfonate is 0.3 wt.%. The                  amount of methanol in the step (iii) is 200 ml.                  The amount of 2-methylimidazole is 3.29 g, and                  the amount of zinc nitrate hexahydrate is 1.48                  g.

PN CN110844942-A; CN110844942-B
TI Synthesizing carbonyl iron complex by introducing carbon dioxide gas into high-pressure reaction kettle, reducing iron-containing raw material, discharging, performing gas-liquid separation on carbonyl iron complex, and synthesizing.
AU ZHAO B
   TENG R
   LIU L
   HOU C
   WANG X
   FAN H
   YUAN H
   WANG M
AE SHANXI JINCHI TECHNOLOGY DEV CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2020200981
AB    NOVELTY - Process for synthesizing carbonyl iron complex involves (i) continuously introducing carbon dioxide gas into high-pressure reaction kettle, and reducing carbon dioxide gas and iron-containing active raw material by hydrogen in the high-pressure reaction kettle synthesize carbonyl iron complex, where the pressure is 15-18 MPaG and temperature is 150-180 degrees C, (ii) discharging unreacted carbon dioxide gas and carbonyl iron complex gas formed by the reaction from the high-pressure reaction kettle, using condenser and multi-stage gas-liquid separator to realize condensation and liquefaction of the carbonyl iron complex, and gas-liquid separation from the carbon dioxide gas, and subjecting carbonyl iron complex liquid to enter carbonyl iron storage tank, and (iii) circulating carbon dioxide gas in gas-liquid separation through compressor and passing to high-pressure reaction kettle for synthesis reaction.
   USE - The process is useful for synthesizing carbonyl iron complex.
   ADVANTAGE - The process is conducive to the smooth progress of the synthesis reaction under condition of less pressure and temperature fluctuation, reduces the reaction time by 8-10 hours, and increases the synthesis rate and product yield.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Process for synthesizing carbonyl iron complex involves (i) continuously introducing carbon dioxide gas into high-pressure reaction kettle, and reducing carbon dioxide gas and iron-containing active raw material by hydrogen in the high-pressure reaction kettle synthesize carbonyl iron complex, where the pressure is 15-18 MPaG and temperature is 150-180 degrees C, (ii) discharging unreacted carbon dioxide gas and carbonyl iron complex gas formed by the reaction from the high-pressure reaction kettle, using condenser and multi-stage gas-liquid separator to realize condensation and liquefaction of the carbonyl iron complex, and gas-liquid separation from the carbon dioxide gas, and subjecting carbonyl iron complex liquid to enter carbonyl iron storage tank, and (iii) circulating carbon dioxide gas in gas-liquid separation through compressor and passing to high-pressure reaction kettle for synthesis reaction, where the total number of cycles of the circulating carbon dioxide gas and newly introduced carbon dioxide gas in primary, climax, intermediate and final phases of the synthesis reaction are 3-5 times/hour, 8-10 times/hour, 5-8 times/hour and 3-5 times/hour respectively, and the durations of the primary, climax, intermediate, and final stages of the synthesis reaction account for 25 plus minus 5%, 50 plus minus 5%, 15 plus minus 5% and 10 plus minus 5% of the total duration of the synthesis cycle, respectively.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The iron-containing active material reduced by hydrogen is sponge iron. Preferred Device: The multi-stage gas-liquid separator in the gas-liquid separation step comprises high-pressure gas-liquid separator, medium-pressure gas-liquid separator, low-pressure gas-liquid separator and cryogenic gas-liquid separator provided in order along the carbon dioxide gas flow direction, where the pressure of the high-pressure gas-liquid separator, the intermediate-pressure gas-liquid separator, the low-pressure gas-liquid separator, and the cryogenic gas-liquid separator are sequentially reduced. The high-pressure reaction kettle is provided with a cooling water jacket at the bottom. Preferred Conditions: The pressures of the high-pressure gas-liquid separator, the intermediate-pressure gas-liquid separator, and the low-pressure gas-liquid separator are sequentially 3.0 MPa, 1.5 MPa and 0.1-0.3 MPa, respectively. The temperature of the cooling medium of the cryogenic gas-liquid separator is -12 to 0 degrees C. In the synthetic reaction step, carbon dioxide gas enters from the upper portion of the high-pressure reaction kettle and is discharged from the bottom. In the synthetic reaction step, the synthetic reaction is carried out for 52-60 hours. In the carbon dioxide cycle step, the carbon dioxide inlet pressure of the circulation compressor is controlled at 0.1-0.3 MPa and the carbon dioxide outlet pressure is controlled at 0.3-0.5 MPa higher than the pressure in the high-pressure reactor, the temperature of the mixed gas entering the circulating compressor is 0-5 degrees C, and the volume percentage of the carbonyl iron complex in the mixed gas is less than 1%.

PN CN110779261-A; CN211451522-U
TI Freezing system for circulating carbon dioxide spray, has exhaust gas recovery device sucks expanded and vaporized carbon dioxide gas after refrigeration, compress and cool that again through specific compressor so as to realize cycle spray.
AU ZHANG X
   ZHU Y
   ZHENG Q
AE UNIV PEKING (UYPK-C)
GA 202015642V
AB    NOVELTY - The freezing system has a carbon dioxide supplemental gas tank (1) is connected to a carbon dioxide cooling circulation system through a pipeline, and an electronic expansion valve (2) and a pressure sensor are provided on a pipeline connecting the carbon dioxide gas supplemental tank to the carbon dioxide cooling circulation system. An infusion pump is connected to uniformly distributed single-fluid combined nozzles (13) through a pipeline. A pressure regulating valve (19) is arranged on side wall of a freezing chamber (18). A conveyor belt (14) is set in the freezing chamber, and frozen objects are evenly placed on the conveyor belt. An exhaust gas recovery device (17) sucks the expanded and vaporized carbon dioxide gas after refrigeration, compress and cool that again through first compressor (41), and transports that back to the carbon dioxide cooling cycle system, so as to realize the cycle spray.
   USE - Freezing system for circulating carbon dioxide spray used in storage and preservation of fresh and perishable frozen object.
   ADVANTAGE - The recycling and reuse of the refrigerant in the spray quick-freezing process is realized, the loss of the refrigerant is greatly reduced, and the cost of the spray quick-freezing is saved. The freezing process is carried out in a low-oxygen environment to prevent food from being oxidized and improve the fresh-keeping quality of the quick-freezing process. The single-fluid combined nozzle is proposed, which overcomes the problem that single-fluid nozzles cannot take into account the droplet size and spray uniformity, and strengthens the effect of spray quick freezing. The spray liquid is mainly composed of low-pressure condensate, and the high-pressure condensate mainly plays auxiliary functions such as pre-cooling, pressurization, and mixed flow, which enhances the safety of the system. The refrigerant in different states in the cycle is reasonably selected, the cascade utilization of energy is realized, the refrigeration capacity of carbon dioxide in the cycle is fully utilized, and the operating energy required for the cycle is saved.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a freezing method for circulating carbon dioxide spray.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the freezing system for circulating carbon dioxide spray.
   Carbon dioxide supplemental gas tank (1)
   Electronic expansion valve (2)
   Single-fluid combined nozzle (13)
   Conveyor belt (14)
   Exhaust gas recovery device (17)
   Freezing chamber (18)
   Pressure regulating valve (19)
   First compressor (41)

PN CN112898349-A
TI New metal manganese complex with 4,4'-diamino-2,2'-pyridine, used for photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide into formic acid.
AU XU Q
   LI N
AE UNIV YUNNAN (UNYN-C)
GA 2021669239
AB    NOVELTY - A metal manganese complex (I) with 4,4'-diamino-2,2'-pyridine, is new.
   USE - New metal manganese complex is used for photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide into formic acid (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The new complex can be prepared with high purity and yield by a simple method.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A metal manganese complex of formula (I) (C20H20Br2MnN8) with 4,4'-diamino-2,2'-pyridine, is new. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for synthesis of compound (I), which involves carrying out coordination reaction with 4,4'-diamino-2,2'-pyridine and pentacarbonyl manganese bromide under solvothermal conditions to obtain yellow block crystals.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Properties: Compound (I) is a solid crystal belonging to monoclinic system and space group P21/n, and having cell parameters a of 8.4049(9) Angstrom , b of 26.2400(19) Angstrom , c of 10.8238(9) Angstrom , alpha and gamma of 90 degrees , beta of 110.633(10), and V of 2234.0(4) Angstrom 3. Preferred Method: The synthesis of compound (I) preferably involves dissolving 4,4'-diamino-2,2'-pyridine and pentacarbonyl manganese bromide in a molar ratio of 1:1 in a solvent, preferably acetonitrile, to obtain a transparent mixed solution, sealing the mixed solution, solvothermally reacting at 120-130 degrees C for 72-96 hours, slowly cooling to room temperature at 5 degrees C/hour to obtain yellow blocky crystals, and washing the crystals using distilled water and ether in turns to obtain a single crystal sample of compound (I).

PT P
PN CN112759397-A; CN112759397-B
TI Preparing mixed gas of hydrogen, oxygen and            hydrocarbon by reacting rare earth diboron-dicarbon            material with water to prepare mixed gas comprising            hydrogen, oxygen, methane, ethane, ethylene, propane,            propylene, butene and carbon dioxide.
AU CHEN J
   LI Z
AE INST METAL RES CHINESE ACAD SCI (CAMA-C)
GA 2021540684
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing mixed gas of hydrogen,                oxygen and hydrocarbon by reacting rare earth                diboron-dicarbon material with water, involves                reacting rare earth diboron-dicarbon material with                water to prepare mixed gas at room temperature,                where the main components of the mixed gas comprise                hydrogen, oxygen, and further comprises methane,                ethane, ethylene, acetylene, propane, propylene,                butene, carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing mixed gas                of hydrogen, oxygen and hydrocarbon for energy,                chemical and machinery industries.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The rare earth                  diboron-dicarbon material is prepared by (i)                  taking raw material comprising rare earth oxide                  powder, boron carbide powder and graphite powder                  in a molar ratio of                  (0.7-1.1):(0.8-1.1):(5.5-6.5), and (ii) weighing                  rare earth oxide powder, boron carbide powder and                  graphite powder, and mixing for 6-12 hours by                  physical and mechanical method, sintering in a                  sintering furnace filled with high-purity argon                  at a heating rate of 5-20 degrees C/minute at                  1800-2200 degrees C for 1-8 hours to obtain                  synthesized material which is a single-phase rare                  earth diboron-dicarbon material powder. The                  method involves passing the prepared rare earth                  diboron-dicarbon material powder through a 500                  mesh sieve, and reacting with water at room                  temperature to prepare a mixed gas of hydrogen,                  oxygen and hydrocarbons. Preferred Components:                  The rare earth material comprises scandium,                  yttrium, lanthanum, cerium, praseodymium,                  neodymium, promethium, samarium, europium,                  gadolinium, terbium, dysprosium, holmium, erbium,                  thulium, ytterbium, and/or lutetium. The water                  comprises deionized water. Preferred Conditions:                  The environment where rare earth diboron dicarbon                  material reacts with water is room temperature.                  Preferred Composition: The mixed gas comprises                  50-80 vol.% hydrogen, 10-40 vol.% oxygen, 2-15                  vol.% methane, and 0.01-0.5 vol.% ethane,                  ethylene, acetylene, propane, propylene, butene,                  carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide.

AB    NOVELTY - A colorimetric sensor comprises an indicator                and a surfactant.
   USE - Colorimetric sensor used in colorimetric                sensor array for measuring analyte e.g. pH, carbon                dioxide, carbon monoxide, sulfur dioxide, amine,                ethylene, sulfur dioxide, glucose and sugar of                solution (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The sensor prevents smoothing and                irreversibility phenomena, reduces the surface                tension, and allows the pH measurement of turbid                samples.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) manufacture of colorimetric sensor;
   (2) colorimetric sensor array;
   (3) manufacture of array; and
   (4) measuring an analyte of a solution.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The colorimetric                  sensor further comprises a dye or two or more                  dyes, and one or more additives. The indicator is                  chosen from cresol red, metacresol purple, thymol                  blue, Carmine (RTM: aluminum lake of the red                  anthraquinone dye), tetrabromophenol blue, methyl                  orange, 2-4-dinitrophenol, bromophenol blue,                  Congo red, ethyl orange, Alizarin violet N,                  Bromocresol Green (RTM: triarylmethane dyes),                  methyl red, propyl red, chlorophenol red, basic                  green 4, bromophenol red, nitrazine yellow,                  purple pyrocatecol violet, bright yellow, nile                  red, resazurin, aurin, bromothymol blue, phenol                  red, orange ii, phenolphthalein, xylenol blue,                  nile blue, and thiazole yellow. The surfactant is                  a cationic surfactant comprising                  hexadecyltrimethylammonium p-toluenesulfonate                  (CTApTs). The precursors of sensor are chosen                  from tetraethyl orthosilicate,                  methyl-trimethoxysilane, ethyl-triethoxysilane,                  phenyltrimethoxysilane, octyltriethoxysilane,                  dodecyl-triethoxysilane,                  hexadecyl-trimethoxysilane, zirconium                  tetrapropoxide,                  3-methacryloxypropyl-trimethoxysilane, titanium                  tetrapropoxide, and titanium isopropoxide (TTIP).                  Preferred Composition: The ratio of surfactant                  and precursor is 0.11-0.38.

PN CN110963880-A
TI Production of ethylene by ethane oxidation by providing raw material gas comprising ethane, oxygen and carbon dioxide, and catalytically oxidizing on mixed metal oxide catalyst.
AU YANG W
   WANG H
   CHU W
   LI Q
   LI X
   LIU Y
AE CAS DALIAN CHEM & PHYSICAL INST (CACP-C)
GA 2020289544
AB    NOVELTY - Production of ethylene by ethane oxidation comprises providing raw material gas comprising ethane, oxygen and carbon dioxide, and catalytically oxidizing on mixed metal oxide catalyst to produce ethylene.
   USE - The method is for production of ethylene by ethane oxidation.
   ADVANTAGE - Compared with traditional ethane steam cracking process, which has energy consumption of 18.5 MJ/kg-ethylene (C2H4), the method has greatly reduced energy consumption of only 11.0 MJ/kg-C2H4, which is a reduction by 40%. The method has low raw material consumption, low process energy consumption, low investment, and less waste and pollutant discharge.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The carbon dioxide is used as diluent for the reaction gasses of ethane and oxygen. The mixed metal oxide catalyst is molybdenum-vanadium-tellurium-niobium oxide catalyst. The content of oxygen is 1-30 mol.%, preferably 5-15 mol.%. The content of carbon dioxide is 10-97 mol.%, preferably 55-85 mol.%.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The content of ethane is 2-60 mol.%, preferably 10-30 mol.%.

PT P
PN CN110586067-A; CN110586067-B
TI Preparing bismuth/bismuth silicate photo-catalyst            for photo-driven reduction of carbon dioxide to carbon            monoxide, involves utilizing bismuth trinitrate            pentahydrate and sodium metasilicate nonahydrate as            bismuth source and silicon source.
AU ZHANG X
   GUAN X
   REN G
   ZHANG C
   LI R
   WANG Y
   FAN C
AE UNIV TAIYUAN TECHNOLOGY (UYTL-C)
GA 2019A7700B
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing bismuth/bismuth silicate                photo-catalyst involves utilizing bismuth                trinitrate pentahydrate and sodium metasilicate                nonahydrate as bismuth source and silicon source,                ethylene glycol and deionized water as solvents,                and sodium hydroxide solution as a pH regulator.                High-purity bismuth/bismuth silicate photocatalysts                can be obtained by using one-step hydrothermal                method and adjusting the pH of the reaction                solution by adjusting the concentration of sodium                hydroxide solution to 9-14.
   USE - Method for preparing bismuth/bismuth silicate                photo-catalyst for photo-driven reduction of carbon                dioxide to carbon monoxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The method involves                  dissolving bismuth trinitrate pentahydrate and                  sodium metasilicate nonahydrate in equal volumes                  of ethylene glycol and deionized water,                  respectively, and stirred for 0.5 to 2 hours to                  form solutions A and B, taking equal volumes of                  solutions A and B and mixing and stirring for 1                  to 3 hours. A white suspension C is formed. The                  suspension C is adjusted to a solution pH of 9 to                  14 by dripping the suspension C with a                  concentration of 1 mol / l sodium hydroxide                  solution, and the reaction solution is                  transferred to a 100-500 ml reaction kettle. The                  reaction is performed at 200 degrees C for 6-12                  hours. The reaction kettle is taken out and                  cooled to room temperature, and centrifuged. The                  precipitate is washed three times with deionized                  water and absolute ethanol, and then the                  precipitate is collected and placed in an oven at                  80 degrees C. After drying for 12h, a                  bismuth/bismuth silicate photo-catalyst is                  prepared. Preferred Conditions: The volumes of                  the ethylene glycol and deionized water are both                  50 to 200 ml.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); E32 (Compounds of Ti, Zr, Hf, Cu, Ag, Au, Zn, Cd, Hg, Ga, In, Te, Ge, Sn, Pb, As, Sb, Bi.); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The refrigerant                  composition contains 10-25 mass%, preferably 25                  mass% carbon dioxide, 60-83 mass%, preferably 66                  mass% HFO selected from R1234yf and/or                  R1234ze(E); and 4-12 mass%, preferably 9 mass%                  R227ea. TI Preparing coffee essential oil comprises subjecting coffee powder to supercritical carbon dioxide extraction and collecting coffee essential oil.
AU HU R
   DONG W
   LONG Y
   TAN L
   ZHAO J
   ZONG Y
   CHU Z
AE SPICE & BEVERAGE RES INST CHINA TOPIC AG (CTAG-C)
GA 201935793V
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing coffee essential oil comprises subjecting coffee powder to supercritical carbon dioxide extraction and collecting coffee essential oil, where the extracting pressure is 25-35 MPa, extracting temperature is 50-70 degrees C, extracting time is 30-60 minutes, and the conditions for collecting coffee essential oil at 35-40 MPa for 8-15 minutes.
   USE - Used as preparing coffee essential oil.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has simple process and strong operability; and significantly increases extraction rate; and high content of active ingredients.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) coffee essential oil obtained by above method.
   (2) food, medicinal, and/or cosmetic comprising coffee essential oil.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Components: The coffee powder has particle size of 20-40 mesh. The coffee powder has color difference of 55-70, the coffee beans on which the coffee powder is prepared have surface color difference of 45-65. Preferred Method: The method comprises extracting pressure is 28 MPa, extracting temperature is 62 degrees C, extracting time is 45 minutes, and the conditions for collecting coffee oils at 38 MPa for 12 minutes. The rate of pressure increase after extraction is 2-5 MPa/minute. The collection valve for collecting coffee oils has temperature of 90-130 degrees C.

TI Preparation of cyclic carbonate using vanadium-based catalyst involves reacting carbon dioxide and epoxide in presence of conjugated vanadium porphyrin bipyridyl/conjugated vanadium porphyrin bipyrrole microporous polymer material.
AU ZHAO J

   LV M
   CAO Y
   XU Z
AE HENAN INST ENG (HEAN-C)
GA 201824341Q
AB    NOVELTY - The preparation method of cyclic carbonate using vanadium-based catalyst involves reacting carbon dioxide and epoxide in the presence of conjugated vanadium porphyrin bipyridyl/conjugated vanadium porphyrin bipyrrole microporous polymer material as heterogeneous catalyst and tetra-n-butylammonium bromide as co-catalyst at normal temperature and normal pressure for 12-48 hours without solvent.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing cyclic carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables simple, economical, rapid and safe preparation of cyclic carbonate with excellent catalytic activity and stability under mild reaction condition, and is suitable for large-scale industrial production.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The conjugated vanadium porphyrin bipyridyl microporous polymer material is prepared by carrying out Scholl coupling reaction of vanadium tetraphenylporphyrin of formula (I) and 2,2-bipyridyl as monomer in the presence of anhydrous aluminum chloride catalyst. The conjugated vanadium porphyrin bipyrrole microporous polymer material is prepared by carrying out Scholl coupling reaction of vanadium tetraphenylporphyrin of formula (II) and pyrrole as monomer in the presence of anhydrous aluminum chloride catalyst. Preferred Components: The epoxide is propylene oxide, butylene oxide, epichlorohydrin, epibromohydrin, styrene oxide, phenyl glycidyl ether or cyclohexene oxide. The heterogeneous catalyst is used in an amount of 0.2-0.5 mol%. The co-catalyst is used in an amount of 3.6-7.2 mol%.

PN WO2021074688-A1
TI System for desalination of raw water, comprises source of carbon dioxide gas, gas transmission pipeline, and desalination unit having feed water unit, mixing unit, pretreatment unit, permeate water unit, and brine water unit.
AU ALTMANN T
   DAS R
AE INT CO WATER & POWER PROJECTS (ITWA-Non-standard)
GA 2021420673
AB    NOVELTY - System for desalination of raw water, comprises a source of carbon dioxide gas (20); a gas transmission pipeline (22) coupled to the source of carbon dioxide gas; and a desalination unit (24), where the desalination unit includes: a feed water unit (26) connected to a source of raw water; a mixing unit (28) coupled to the feed water unit, and including a mixing unit output (30); a pretreatment unit (32) including a pretreatment unit output (34); a reverse osmosis membrane (36,38,40) coupled to the pretreatment unit output, where the reverse osmosis membrane includes a first output and a second output; a permeate water unit (42) coupled to the first output of the reverse osmosis membrane, where the permeate water unit provides permeate water at a permeate water unit output (44); and a brine water unit (46) coupled to the second output of the reverse osmosis membrane. The brine water unit provides brine water at the brine water unit output (48).
   USE - The system is useful for desalination of raw water (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The improved desalination systems and methods reduce or eliminate the need for industrial acids (e.g. sulfuric acid (H2SO4), HCl, or other inorganic acids) and/or antisealant additives, while simultaneously improving the efficiency of the coagulation process and maintaining or increasing the recovery ratio, by mixing or injecting CO2 gas into the raw water, such as seawater or brackish water, prior to the pretreatment and reverse osmosis membrane separation processes. The systems and methods reduce the pH of raw water below its natural pH before pretreatment, allowing for more efficient coagulation of colloidal matter and maintaining or increasing recovery ratios, while reducing the need for industrial acids and/or antisealant additives.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) desalination of raw water which involves mixing carbon dioxide gas with raw water to produce acidified raw water; then providing the acidified raw water to a pretreatment unit to produce treated raw water; then passing the treated raw water through a reverse osmosis membrane; then separating the output of the reverse osmosis membrane into a permeate water stream and a product water stream;
   (2) a reverse osmosis water desalination plant comprising at least one reverse osmosis membrane unit and a carbon dioxide (CO2) mixing unit upstream of the at least one reverse osmosis membrane unit;
   (3) a split partial second pass reverse osmosis water desalination plant comprising at least two reverse osmosis membrane units, where each membrane unit comprises one or more elements, and CO2 mixing unit upstream of each of at least two reverse osmosis membrane units; and
   (4) a forward osmosis water desalination plant comprising at least one front osmosis membrane unit and CO2 mixing unit upstream of the forward osmosis membrane unit.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the system for desalination of raw water.
   Source of carbon dioxide gas (20)
   Gas transmission pipeline (22)
   Desalination unit (24)
   Feed water unit (26)
   Mixing unit (28)
   Mixing unit output (30)
   Pretreatment unit (32)
   Pretreatment unit output (34)
   Reverse osmosis membrane (36,38,40)
   Permeate water unit (42)
   Permeate water unit output (44)
   Brine water unit (46)
   Brine water unit output (48)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The raw water is seawater or brackish water. The source of carbon dioxide gas is at least one from emissions from an industrial power plant, a gas storage tank and a commercial provider of carbon dioxide gas. No industrial acid is added to the raw water in the pretreatment unit. No antisealant additive is added to the raw water in the pretreatment unit. Preferred Composition: Preferably, less than or equal to 50 mg/L of an industrial acid or less than or equal to 2 mg/L of antisealant additive is added to the raw water in the pretreatment unit. Further, less than or equal to 50 mg/L of food-grade carbon dioxide gas is added to the permeate water in the post-treatment unit. Preferred Method: Mixing carbon dioxide gas with raw water is conducted in a section of a pipe, a chamber, a tank, a static mixer, or a high pressure injection unit. The permeate water stream is provided to a post-treatment unit, where the post-treatment unit produces product water.
DC D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).)
