PT P
PN DE102020002543-A1
TI In-situ fabrication of carbon materials and components for buildings produced on construction site by molecular steam coating, comprises obtaining raw material carbon from carbon dioxide in ambient air of construction site.
AU BERDELLE N
AE BERDELLE N (BERD-Individual)
GA 202082624L
AB    NOVELTY - In-situ fabrication of carbon materials (CNMs) and CNM components for buildings produced on the construction site by molecular steam coating, comprises obtaining raw material carbon from carbon dioxide in the ambient air of the construction site, obtaining optionally methane from a reactor on the construction site and utilizing biogenic waste from the environment of the construction site. The sequestration of greenhouse gases takes place in the simplest case on an electro-catalytic surface. The carbon dioxide molecules are attracted and split to create carbon molecules on surface. This reaction is triggered by a voltage to provide the component with a thin layer of CNMs from top to bottom. Then the process starts over for the deposition of next layer. The two different catalytic surfaces are used, after first catalyst surface is saturated with CNMs, electrical voltage is changed such that molecules separate again from surface and diffuse on final surface of the desired component.
   USE - The method is useful for in-situ fabrication of carbon materials (CNMs) and CNM components for buildings produced on the construction site by molecular steam coating.
   ADVANTAGE - The method releases oxygen into the environment and improves the ambient air.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - In-situ fabrication of carbon materials (CNMs) and CNM components for buildings produced on the construction site by molecular steam coating, comprises obtaining raw material carbon from carbon dioxide in the ambient air of the construction site, obtaining optionally methane from a reactor on the construction site and utilizing biogenic waste from the environment of the construction site. The sequestration of greenhouse gases takes place in the simplest case on an electro-catalytic surface. The carbon dioxide molecules are attracted and split to create carbon molecules on the surface. This reaction is triggered by a voltage to provide the component with a thin layer of CNMs from top to bottom. Then the process starts over for the deposition of the next layer. The two different catalytic surfaces are used, after the first catalyst surface is saturated with CNMs, the electrical voltage is changed such that the molecules separate again from the surface and diffuse onto the final surface of the desired component. Then the voltage can be applied again and the next layer of CNMs can be synthesized from carbon dioxide. The molecular vapor coating takes place on another catalytic surface, which represents a permanent formwork for the finished components. The carbon molecules meet an existing layer of carbon molecules and combine. The mixture of carbon nanotubes and graphene can be completely unstructured. All types of CNTs and graphene are available, without orientation, or secondary structural specifications such as fibers or fiber bundles. A massive carbon layer is created, possibly of poor structural quality, but which can quickly bind large amounts of greenhouse gases. The lost formwork can be a film that is pulled vertically down from a roll under the roof of the construction site and is thus placed in the final position of the building part. The component can be a complete wall, floor, column, or part of it.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The gas is sprayed into the space between the formwork and the catalytic surface and a structural layer of carbon is deposited on the two insides of the formwork. Then the remaining space is filled with carbon molecules. The gap is filled with another material for the wall. This can be a load-bearing material such as concrete, or an insulating material such as foam, and forms a force-fit bond with the two surfaces to produce sandwich components. The space in between is filled with other carbon materials as fillers such as waste paper flakes, graphite powder or biochar powder, in combination with the molecular carbon molecules as an adhesive. The aggregate can be further compressed with CNMs in a second pass. The redensification can be done by blowing in additional greenhouse gases. The greenhouse gas splits on the catalytic surface of the aggregate and precipitates into CNMs. The two sides of the lost formwork are connected with non-positive carbon structures such that they represent a stable box. This can be done by electrical voltage between the catalytic surfaces. The remaining space can be filled with filler material that has no load-bearing properties and does not connect the two sides with a force fit, e.g. blown insulation made from paper flakes or other carbon-based waste products from rapidly renewable raw materials.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PT P
PN CN111185239-A; CN111185239-B
TI Preparing fixed carbon dioxide epoxidation catalyst useful for e.g. promoting the reaction of carbon dioxide with epoxy compound comprises e.g. adding zinc tungstate and sodium tungstate to deionized aqueous solution, and stirring.
AU XIAO G
   WU Y
   CHEN Y
   ZHANG Z
   YUAN H
   FAN J
   GAO L
   WEI R
   ZHANG J
AE UNIV SOUTHEAST (UYSE-C)
GA 202046603F
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing fixed carbon dioxide epoxidation catalyst comprises e.g. (i) adding zinc tungstate and sodium tungstate to deionized aqueous solution, and stirring, adding zinc salt with sodium tungstate, stirring, transferring to hydrothermal reactor, reacting, cooling, filtering obtained precipitate and washing, drying, and calcining to obtain zinc tungsten oxide, (ii) in-situ growth method introduces a layered metal organic structure, adding zinc tungsten oxide prepared into a hydrothermal reactor, and adding an organic solvent to the hydrothermal reactor and stirring, (a) adding equimolar amount of 2methylimidazole to zinc tungsten oxide to the hydrothermal reaction kettle, stirring, sealing, statically reacting, cooling, filtering the precipitate in the hydrothermal kettle, washing, and dissolving in organic solvent, (b) adding equal molar amount of zinc salt to 2methylimidazole to the hydrothermal reaction kettle, stirring, sealing, reacting, cooling, filtering and washing.
   USE - The catalyst material is useful for promoting the reaction of carbon dioxide with epoxy compound preferably propylene oxide, epichlorohydrin, butylene oxide, styrene oxide, epoxycyclohexane, and/or allyl glycidyl ether (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalytic material: has excellent microstructure, Lewis acid-base active sites.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing fixed carbon dioxide epoxidation catalyst comprises (i) adding zinc tungstate and sodium tungstate to deionized aqueous solution containing 0.01-10 g/l of surfactant dissolved at a solid-liquid ratio of 0.01-10 mmol/l, and stirring at a temperature of 20-70 degrees C degrees C for 1-12 hours, adding equimolar amount of zinc salt with sodium tungstate, continue to stirring at 20-70 degrees C for 1-12 hours, transferring to hydrothermal reactor, statically reacting at 120-180 degrees C for 12-48 hours, cooling to room temperature, filtering obtained precipitate and washing 3 times, drying at 80 degrees C for 24 hours, and calcining at 500-900 degrees C for 3-10 hours to obtain zinc tungsten oxide, (ii) in-situ growth method introduces a layered metal organic structure, adding zinc tungsten oxide prepared into a hydrothermal reactor, and adding an organic solvent with a solid-liquid ratio of 0.01-10 mmol/l to the hydrothermal reactor and stirring for 0.1-2 hours at 60 degrees C, (a) adding equimolar amount of 2methylimidazole to zinc tungsten oxide to the hydrothermal reaction kettle, stirring at 20-60 degrees C for 1-6 hours, sealing, statically reacting at 120-180 degrees C for 12-48 hours, cooling at room temperature, filtering the precipitate in the hydrothermal kettle, washing 3 times, and dissolving in organic solvent, (b) adding equal molar amount of zinc salt to 2methylimidazole to the hydrothermal reaction kettle, stirring at 20-60 degrees C for 0.1-2 hours, and sealing, statically reacting at 120-180 degrees C for 12-48 hours, cooling to room temperature, filtering and washing the precipitating in the hydrothermal kettle 3 times, by regulating the process (a), (b) the number of repetitions, the number of control layers, the synthesis of zinc tungsten oxide as the core, the layered structure carbon dioxide epoxidation fixed catalyst, where 2 methylimidazole and zinc ions alternately appear.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the surfactants comprises dodecyltrimethylammonium bromide, cetyltrimethylammonium bromide, hexamethylenetetramine, tetraethylammonium bromide, tetrapropyl bromide ammonium and/or tetrabutylammonium bromide. The zinc salt comprises zinc nitrate, zinc chloride, zinc acetate, zinc perchlorate, zinc bromide, and/or zinc sulfate. The solvent used for washing comprises deionized water, methanol, ethanol, and/or nitroxyl formamide. In the step (ii), the organic solvent comprises methanol, ethanol, nitroxyl formamide, nitroazide diethylformamide and/or tetrahydrofuran. The washing solution for washing 2 methylimidazole comprises methanol, ethanol, nitroxyl formamide, nitroazodiethyl formamide, methylene chloride, chloroform, tetrachloromethane and/or tetrahydrofuran. The washing solution for washing the zinc salt comprises deionized water, methanol, ethanol, and/or nitrogen-nitroformamide. The zinc salt comprises zinc nitrate, zinc chloride, zinc acetate, zinc perchlorate, zinc bromide and/or zinc sulfate. Preferred Method: In the step (i), The calcination process comprises using a programmed temperature increase rate of 5 degrees C/minutes to the target temperature under an empty atmosphere. In the step (ii), after one cycle of (a) and (b), a layer of metal-organic structure is formed on the surface of ZnWO4, and its layer thickness is equal to the distance between two zinc ions and different nitrogen atoms in 2methylimidazole to form a stable structure.
DC E13 (Heterocyclics.); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

PT P
PN US2020156002-A1; US11202989-B2
TI Converting carbonaceous feedstock to syngas-fermentation product, involves e.g. introducing carbonaceous feedstock and oxidant to gasifier, feeding portion of conditioned syngas stream to fermentor and capturing tail gas.
AU WINTER J D
   HOHMAN J
AE ALDOUS D C (ALDO-Individual)
   LANZATECH INC (LANZ-C)
GA 2020434963
AB    NOVELTY - Converting carbonaceous feedstock to syngas-fermentation product, involves (a) introducing carbonaceous feedstock and oxidant to gasifier, under suitable gasification conditions to produce raw syngas stream comprising carbon monoxide, hydrogen and carbon dioxide, (b) optionally feeding at least a portion of the raw syngas stream to a syngas-cleanup unit, to produce an intermediate syngas stream, (c) feeding at least a portion of the raw syngas stream and/or at least a portion of the intermediate syngas stream, if present, to acid-gas removal unit, to remove at least some of the carbon dioxide and produce a conditioned syngas stream, (d) feeding at least a portion of the conditioned syngas stream to fermentor, under suitable fermentation conditions and in presence of suitable microorganisms and nutrients, (e) capturing tail gas, (f) recycling a first amount of the tail gas to the fermentor and (g) recycling a second amount of tail gas to acid-gas removal unit.
   USE - The method is useful for converting a carbonaceous feedstock to a syngas-fermentation product, where the syngas-fermentation product comprises ethanol, butanol, butyric acid and/or acetic acid (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: converts carbon monoxide and hydrogen is at least 90%, where the total conversion of carbon oxide and hydrogen is at least 5% points, preferably at least 10% points higher than total conversion of carbon oxide and hydrogen that is attained in a comparable method with R1 and R2 is 0 (claimed); increases syngas conversion to ethanol; improves process efficiency and better overall biorefinery economics for conversion of biomass to ethanol.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Converting a carbonaceous feedstock to a syngas-fermentation product, comprises (a) introducing a carbonaceous feedstock and an oxidant to a gasifier, under suitable gasification conditions to produce a raw syngas stream comprising carbon monoxide, hydrogen and carbon dioxide, where the carbonaceous feedstock comprises biomass, cellulose, hydrocarbons, carbohydrates, coal, petroleum coke, charcoal, lignite, carbon-rich waste materials and/or natural gas, and the oxidant comprises at least one of air, oxygen, and steam, (b) optionally feeding at least a portion of the raw syngas stream to a syngas-cleanup unit, to produce an intermediate syngas stream, (c) feeding at least a portion of the raw syngas stream and/or at least a portion of the intermediate syngas stream, if present, to an acid-gas removal unit, to remove at least some of the carbon dioxide and produce a conditioned syngas stream, (d) feeding at least a portion of the conditioned syngas stream to a fermentor, under suitable fermentation conditions and in the presence of suitable microorganisms and nutrients to biologically convert at least one of carbon monoxide, hydrogen and carbon dioxide to a syngas-fermentation product, (e) capturing a tail gas from an exit of the fermentor, where the tail gas comprises at least carbon dioxide and unconverted carbon monoxide or hydrogen, (f) recycling a first amount of the tail gas to the fermentor (R1), the volumetric ratio of the first amount to the tail gas, where R1 is 1 or 1 and (g) recycling a second amount of the tail gas to the acid-gas removal unit (R2), the volumetric ratio of the second amount to the tail gas, where R2 is 0-1, where R1+R2 is 0-1.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The gasifier is a fluidized-bed gasifier. The acid-gas removal unit additionally removes at least some hydrogen sulfide, if present. The second amount of the tail gas is compressed before being recycled to the acid-gas removal unit.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The first amount of the tail gas is compressed before being recycled to the fermentor. The first amount and the second amount of the tail gas are separately compressed before being recycled to the fermentor and the acid-gas removal unit, respectively. The sum R1 and R2 is 0.001-0.8. The carbon dioxide content in a feed to the fermentor is controlled to a level comprising 5-50 vol.% by adjusting R1 and/or R2. The acid gas molar ratio, (carbon monoxide and hydrogen)/(carbon dioxide and hydrogen sulfide), in a feed to the fermentor is controlled to 2-20. The R1 and/or R2 are adjusted in response to: at least one upstream parameters comprising feedstock type, oxidant profile, syngas-generation design or performance, syngas-cleanup design or performance, and acid-gas removal design or performance; at least one fermentor parameters comprising temperature, pressure, residence time, pH, redox potential, nutrient concentration, cell viability or cell vitality; at least one fermentor variable comprising carbon monoxide conversion, hydrogen conversion, carbon dioxide conversion, ethanol selectivity, ethanol productivity, ethanol titer or acetic acid selectivity. The compressed tail gas recycle increases the pressure within the fermentor, thus allowing more syngas to enter the liquid phase for bioconversion. The method further comprises: recycling cells from the fermentor back to the gasifier; and recovering the syngas-fermentation product from the fermentor. Preferred Components: The tail gas contains 2-10% syngas contained in the raw syngas stream.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); E17 (Other aliphatics.); H09 (Fuel products not of petroleum origin - excluding coal handling, preparation or mining, but including coking, briquetting, peat processing synthesis, gas production, coal gasification. PT P
PN US2021379575-A1
TI Yolk-shell catalyst in dry reforming of methane,            comprises multiple particles, where each particle            comprises nickel, platinum and third material comprises            metal or metalloid and porous shell that encapsulates            multiple particles.
AU KIM S
   LAUTERBACH J
   SASMAZ E
AE UNIV SOUTH CAROLINA (USCL-C)
GA 2021E1376S
AB    NOVELTY - Yolk-shell catalyst comprises multiple                particles, where each particle comprises nickel,                platinum and third material comprises a metal or a                metalloid and a porous shell that encapsulates                multiple particles.
   USE - Yolk-shell catalyst used in the dry reforming                of methane.
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst demonstrates long-term stability                that would be helpful in industrial applications                such as mitigating fossil fuel plant                emissions.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for reforming methane, involving: contacting                reactants including methane and carbon dioxide with                the catalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The third material                  comprises cerium (Ce), palladium (Pd), or iron                  (Fe). The porous shell comprises silica. The                  particles comprise 5-25 wt.% catalyst. The catalyst comprises the platinum                  and the nickel in an atomic ratio of                  platinum-to-nickel of about 0.01. The                  third material is selected from cerium (Ce),                  lithium (Li), sodium (Na), cesium (Cs), magnesium                  (Mg), calcium (Ca), titanium (Ti), zirconium                  (Zr), vanadium (V), yttrium (Y), manganese (Mn),                  rhenium (Re), gallium (Ga), germanium (Ge), tin                  (Sn), indium (In), cobalt (Co), gold (Au), silver                  (Ag), copper (Cu), ruthenium (Ru), rhodium (Rh),                  iridium (Ir), osmium (Os), palladium (Pd) and                  iron (Fe). The                  catalyst has a surface area density of from about                  30-600 m2/g.

AU GUAN G
   HE Y
   DING J
   YUAN D
   WAN H
AE UNIV NANJING TECHNOLOGY (UNTY-C)
GA 2021724865
AB    NOVELTY - A metal-coordinated triazine polyionic compound (I), is new.
   USE - New metal-coordinated triazine polyionic compound used as liquid catalyst for synthesizing dimethyl carbonate (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The compound (I) can catalyze carbon dioxide, epoxy compounds and methanol to synthesize dimethyl carbonate, and provides catalyst having excellent catalytic activity under mild reaction conditions, and reusability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A metal-coordinated triazine polyionic compound of formula (I), is new.
   R = hydrocarbyl;and
   M = transition metal.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) preparation of the metal-coordinated triazine polyionic compound (I); and
   (2) use of the metal-coordinated triazine polyionic compound in the catalytic synthesis of dimethyl carbonate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation (claimed): The compound (I) is prepared by adding cyanuric chloride to the reaction solvent and stirring until it is completely dissolved, adding 1-vinylimidazole and stirring at 60-80 degrees C for 12-24 hours, filtering to remove the reaction solvent, and drying in vacuum to obtain a brown-yellow triazine ionic liquid monomer of formula (II), (ii) adding the obtained monomer and an equimolar amount of maleate to the ethanol aqueous solution, adding a photoinitiator under stirring, and reacting at 60-80 degrees C for 24-36 hours under the protection of nitrogen, filtering and vacuum drying to obtain triazine polyionic liquid compound of formula (III), and (iii) mixing and dispersing the triazine polyionic liquid compound and metal carboxylate in a solvent, and stirring at 70-120 degrees C for 24-48 hours, filtering the product, washing, and drying in vacuum. Preferred Components: The reaction solvent in step (i) is at least one of dimethyl sulfoxide, acetonitrile, tetrahydrofuran or chloroform. The maleate is at least one of sodium maleate, potassium maleate or lithium maleate. The initiator is at least one of benzoyl peroxide, azobisisobutylamidine hydrochloride or azobisisobutyronitrile. The metal carboxylate is at least one of manganese acetate, cobalt acetate, copper propionate or zinc benzoate. The solvent is at least one of acetone, toluene or N,N-dimethylformamide. The epoxy compound is at least one of propylene oxide, epichlorohydrin, epibromohydrin, or styrene oxide. Preferred Composition: The molar ratio of cyanuric chloride to 1-vinylimidazole is 1:(3-5). The initiator is at least one of benzoyl peroxide, azobisisobutylamidine hydrochloride or azobisisobutyronitrile. The mass ratio of triazine polyionic liquid to carboxylic acid metal salt is 1:(1-3). The mass ratio of epoxy compound to methanol is 1:3-1:6. The method is carried out using 5-15 %mass catalyst. Preferred Process: The synthesis of dimethyl carbonate involves adding catalyst, epoxy compound and methanol in the reaction kettle, and starting the reaction after introducing the carbon dioxide, and filtering and separating the catalyst.
   Preferred Definitions:
   R = methyl, ethyl, or phenyl;and
   M = Mn, Co, Cu, or Zn.

PN CN109956846-A
TI Producing styrene comprises e.g. taking ethylbenzene as reaction raw material, dehydrogenating ethylbenzene in contact with the catalyst under the conditions of a reaction pressure.
AU FAN D
   WANG W
   LIU K
   WANG H
AE ANHUI HAOYUAN CHEM GROUP CO LTD (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 201959876S
AB    NOVELTY - Producing styrene comprises taking ethylbenzene as reaction raw material, dehydrogenating ethylbenzene in contact with the catalyst under the conditions of a reaction pressure of 0-20 Kpa to obtain final product, where the reaction temperature is 480-600 degrees C, the ethylbenzene having space velocity 0.4-2.5 hours -1, the molar ratio between carbon dioxide, water and ethylbenzene is 3:7:1-7:3:1, and the catalyst is vanadium(V) oxide- chromium(III) oxide-aluminum oxide and a catalyst containing iron(III) oxide-potassium oxide.
   USE - The method is useful for producing styrene.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The reaction material first passes through vanadium(V) oxide- chromium(III) oxide-aluminum oxide catalyst and it is passed through a catalyst containing iron(III) oxide-potassium oxide, and the method comprises (i) adding 100-120 pts. wt. pure water into the reaction kettle, stirring, adding 0.05-0.1 pts. wt. active calcium phosphate, 0.0001-0.0003 pts. wt. trisodium phosphate, 0.01-0.02 pts. wt. sodium dodecylbenzene sulfonate and 100 pts. wt. styrene into the reaction vessel, and then adding 0.03-0.06 pts. wt. tert-butyl peroxybenzoate, 0.01-0.03 pts. wt. dioctyl phthalate, 0.005-0.015 pts. wt. dicumyl peroxide, 0.15-0.3 pts. wt. toluene, 0.2-0.4 pts. wt. xylene and 0.1-0.2 pts. wt. nucleating agent, and then stirring uniformly, (ii) introducing steam into the jacket of the reactor equipment, and slowly heating, (iii) adding 0.4-0.6 pts. wt. benzoyl peroxide at 55-65 degrees C, (iv) raising the temperature to 88-92 degrees C, granulating the temperature, and the process time is 4-6 hours, (v) adding 0.1-0.15 pts. wt. dispersing agent calcium phosphate and 0.1-0.2 pts. wt. ash calcium powder, (vi) adding 5-6 pts. wt. pentane, and slowly heating up the steam, heating to 95-98 degrees C, and reacting for 1.5-2.5 hours, (vii) raising the temperature to 115-118 degrees C, controlling the temperature for 3-3.5 hours, and controlling the temperature at 125-130 degrees C, and controlling the pressure at 0.8-0.95 Mpa, and (viii) after the reaction is completed, lowering the temperature to 45-50 degrees C, and discharging the material.

AU SHEN J
   WU X
   ZHANG Z
AE UNIV TONGJI (UYTJ-C)
GA 2018861378
AB    NOVELTY - A nanoporous carbon material is prepared by dissolving template agent in aqueous solution, stirring, obtaining template aqueous solution, adding phloroglucinol and melamine, stirring, adding formaldehyde, dissolving, adding sodium bicarbonate solution, stirring to form sol-gel, carrying out heat treatment to form gel, cooling to obtain organic wet gel, repeatedly washing with sorbitan oleate deionized aqueous solution, drying, obtaining organic gel block, pumping nitrogen, carbonizing, and carrying out carbon dioxide activation.
   USE - Method for preparing nanoporous carbon material.
   ADVANTAGE - The product is low-cost, has adjustable density, high specific surface area and suitable for electrochemical field.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The nanoporous carbon material is prepared by dissolving template agent in aqueous solution, stirring, obtaining template aqueous solution, adding phloroglucinol and melamine, stirring, adding formaldehyde, dissolving, adding 0.04-0.06 mol/L sodium bicarbonate solution, stirring to form sol-gel, carrying out heat treatment to form gel, cooling to obtain organic wet gel, repeatedly washing 4-8 times with 5-20 g/L sorbitan oleate deionized aqueous solution, drying at 80-90 degrees C for 3-5 days, obtaining organic gel block, pumping nitrogen at rate of 200-400 mL/minute, carbonizing at 900-1100 degrees C for 2.5-3.5 hours, and carrying out carbon dioxide activation at 900-1100 degrees Cfor 2.5-3.5 hours at carbon dioxide rate of 10-50 mL/minute to obtain product with density of 200-800 mg/cm3 and specific surface area of 200-1200 m2/g. Preferred Components: The mol ratio of melamine and formaldehyde is 0.25-4:1:10, melamine, phloroglucinol to melamine to sodium hydrogencarbonate is 50-300:1.

PT P
PN CN114921257-A; CN114921257-B
TI Improving the quality of oil shale pyrolysis oil            by deep pyrolysis, comprises crushing and sieving oil            shale sample and placing primary pyrolysis unit,            introducing water dissolving, placing heating, nitrate            of alkali metal potassium and supercritical carbon            dioxide, reacting, cooling and collecting.
AU ZHAO J
   KANG Z
   HUANG D
   YANG D
   LU Y
   CAO R
AE UNIV TAIYUAN TECHNOLOGY (UYTL-C)
GA 2022B0827N
AB    NOVELTY - Improving the quality of oil shale pyrolysis                oil by deep pyrolysis, comprises (i) crushing and                sieving the oil shale sample and placing in the                primary pyrolysis unit, (ii) continuously                introducing supercritical water dissolving with                double-metal catalyst into the primary pyrolysis                unit, performing pyrolysis reaction for 1-8 hours,                placing the obtained pyrolysis volatiles into the                secondary pyrolysis unit pre-placing catalyst                through the pipeline with heating sleeve, the                double-metal catalyst is sulfate, carbonate,                acetate and nitrate of alkali metal potassium or                sodium, and (iii) continuously introducing                supercritical carbon dioxide to the secondary                pyrolysis unit, performing secondary pyrolysis with                the primary pyrolysis volatile and pre-placing                catalyst, reacting for 0.2-1.2 hours, cooling and                collecting to obtained secondary pyrolysis product                pyrolysis oil gas.
   USE - The method is useful for improving quality of                oil shale pyrolysis oil by deep pyrolysis.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has enhancing the hydrogenation                effect of oil shale pyrolysis, generating                synergistic effect and performing secondary                pyrolysis by supercritical carbon dioxide and                catalyst, realizing deep pyrolysis of oil shale                pyrolysis oil.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (ii), the                  mass ratio of two metal catalysts in the                  double-metal catalyst dissolved in supercritical                  water is 1: (1-5). The mass ratio of double-metal                  catalyst and oil shale sample in the primary                  pyrolysis unit is 1: (6-15). The mass of                  double-metal catalyst dissolved in the                  supercritical water and the volume ratio of the                  supercritical water is (1: 40 g/ml)-(1: 100                  g/ml). The catalyst in the secondary pyrolysis                  unit is active metal barium, iron, cerium                  sulfate, carbonate, acetate and/or nitrate. The                  mass ratio of the catalyst and oil shale sample                  in the secondary pyrolysis unit is 1: (9-20). In                  the step (iii), the volume ratio of the oil shale                  sample mass and the supercritical carbon dioxide                  is (1: 60g/ml)-(1: 100g/ml).Preferred Method: In the step (ii), the                  supercritical water temperature is controlled at                  374-500&#8451; and the pressure is controlled                  at 23-30Mpa. The pipeline temperature with                  heating sleeve is controlled at                  200-300&#8451;. In the step (iii), the                  supercritical carbon dioxide temperature is                  controlled at 410-500&#8451;, and the                  pressure is controlled at 8-12Mpa.

Combustion improvement additives for coal, peat and other nonhydrocarbon based fuels are included in this Section together with coal liquefaction and desulphurisation.)

   HU J
   QU J
AE UNIV SUZHOU SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYSO-C)
GA 202290912F
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of CC surface-modified MOF-derived                nickel cobaltite nanosheet array composite                material, involves: soaking CC in a mixed solution                of nitric acid and hydrochloric acid, cleaning,                placing in a buffer solution, adding dopamine                hydrochloride, and reacting to obtain CC-doped                polydopamine; immersing in a soluble cobalt salt                solution, adding an imidazole ligand solution, and                reacting to obtain CC-doped cobalt-MOF; adding a                soluble nickel salt solution, and calcining to                obtain CC-doped nickel cobaltite material; and                adding CC-doped nickel cobaltite material to a                sodium borohydride solution, and reacting.

   HE W
   ZHANG H
   GUO K
AE UNIV NANJING TECHNOLOGY (UNTY-C)
GA 202244181A
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of cyclic carbamate (3) involves                reacting mixed homogeneous solution containing                epoxy compound (1), amine compound (2), electrolyte                and organic solvent in continuous flow                micro-channel reactor provided with an electrode,                in a carbon dioxide atmosphere to obtain a reaction                solution containing cyclic carbamate (3).
   USE - Preparation of cyclic carbamate used for                synthesizing organic chemical.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has simple operation, high safety,                economic and environment-friendly, green and                practical. It can effectively solve the traditional                reaction process complex, long reaction time,                excessive strong oxidant, reaction temperature and                low atomic efficiency and so on, and can improve                the reaction efficiency, and is suitable for                industrial production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of cyclic carbamate of formula (3)                involves reacting mixed homogeneous solution                containing epoxy compound of formula (1), amine                compound of formula (2), electrolyte and organic                solvent in a continuous flow micro-channel reactor                provided with an electrode, in a carbon dioxide                atmosphere to obtain a reaction solution containing                cyclic carbamate (3).
   R1= phenyl, 4-ethylbenzyl, 4-chlorobenzyl,                4-methoxybenzyl, 4-nitrobenzyl or a naphthyl; and                R2= H, methyl, ethyl, n-propyl, isopropyl,                4-chlorophenyl, 4-bromophenyl, phenyl or                cyclopentyl.
                                                                                                                     
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - R1Preferred Composition: The electrolyte is                  tetrabutylammonium hexafluorophosphate,                  tetrabutylammonium tetrafluoroborate,                  tetrabutylammonium bromide, tetrabutylammonium                  iodide and/or sodium iodide. The organic solvent                  is acetonitrile, methanol and/or dichloromethane.                  The concentration of epoxy compound (1) is                  0.01-0.05 mmol/mL organic solvent, and amine                  compound (2) is 0.01-0.03 mmol/mL organic                  solvent, and electrolyte is 0.01-0.10 mmol/mL                  organic solvent.Preferred Process: The reaction solution                  containing cyclic carbamate (3) is diluted with                  ethyl acetate, washed with water, dried,                  filtered, washed and separated with a mixed                  solvent of ethyl acetate and petroleum ether, to                  obtain the cyclic carbamate (3).

PN CN113549341-A
TI Preparing core-shell silica-coated nano-calcium            carbonate latex coating, coating, layered fabric paper,            involves putting unslaked and refining quicklime into            calcium hydroxide slurry, and passing cellar gas            containing carbon dioxide.
AU YONG J
   CHEN S
AE UNIV NANJING TECHNOLOGY (UNTY-C)
GA 2021C4853W
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing core-shell silica-coated                nano-calcium carbonate involves putting unslaked                and refining quicklime into calcium hydroxide                slurry, passing cellar gas containing carbon                dioxide under control of reaction temperature,                refining to obtain a nano-calcium carbonate                suspension, adding silicate to the system, stirring                the reaction, performing with the progress of the                carbonization reaction, when the pH value of the                carbonization reaction system is 6.5-7, generating                core-shell silica coated nano-calcium carbonate,                heating to 40-70degrees Celsius, adding coating                agent to performing coating treatment, performing                carbonization reaction continuously for 2-3 hours                to end, stopping passing in the cellar gas                containing carbon dioxide, curing the reaction                system, filtering the obtained slurry, washing, and                drying to obtain core-shell silica-coated                nano-calcium carbonate.
   USE - Method for preparing core-shell silica-coated                nano-calcium carbonate used in latex coating,                coating, layered fabric paper.
   ADVANTAGE - The prepared core-shell silica-coated                nano-calcium carbonate has good acid resistance and                dispersibility, and significantly improves the                physical and chemical property of the product. The                method has simple operation and easy industrial                production, and is applied to the field of latex                coating, coating, layered fabric paper.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The organic acid is                  selected from sodium oleate, stearic acid or                  resin acid rosin.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The silicate is                  selected from water glass, sodium metasilicate or                  potassium silicate. The coupling agent is                  selected from titanate coupling agent,                  aqueous-soluble borate coupling agent or organic                  silicon coupling agent. Preferred Compositions:                  The added weight of silicate is 2.0-5.0% based on                  the weight of calcium carbonate. The added amount                  of the coating agent is to control the solute                  added weight to be 2.0-5.0% based on the weight                  of calcium carbonate. Preferred Conditions: The                  unslaked and refining quicklime is putting into                  calcium hydroxide slurry, passing cellar gas                  containing carbon dioxide under control of                  reaction temperature at 15-35degrees Celsius. The                  weight solid content of the nano calcium                  carbonate suspension is 5.0-10.0%. The cellar gas                  comprises 15-30 vol.% carbon dioxide, the flow                  rate of the cellar gas is 1-5 meter/seconds. The                  coating agent is an aqueous solution with a mass                  concentration of 4-15% prepared by a coupling                  agent or an organic acid. The curing treatment is                  performed at temperature of 40-50degrees Celsius,                  and the time is 1.5-2.5 hours.

PT P
PN CN112679351-A
TI Producing dimethyl carbonate involves adding methanol and porous catalyst to reaction vessel, passing carbon dioxide to purge air and pressurize vessel to set pressure, heating, cooling, releasing reactant, and separating.
AU XU K
   YIN X
   ZHANG X
   SHUI Q
   LU J
   LIU S
AE SHANDONG DEPU CHEM TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2021457872
AB    NOVELTY - Production process of dimethyl carbonate involves (i) adding methanol and porous catalyst to reaction vessel, closing vessel, passing carbon dioxide to purge air in vessel, continuing to add carbon dioxide to pressurize vessel to set pressure, and heating the vessel, and (ii) cooling the temperature of the reaction vessel to room temperature after the reaction, releasing reactant after the gas in vessel is discharged, and separating dimethyl carbonate in the reactant, where the porous catalyst is prepared by (a) dissolving copper hydroxide powder and nickel hydroxide powder in aqueous ammonia and uniformly stirring to form a bimetallic complex solution, (b) completely soaking porous montmorillonite in bimetallic complex solution, evaporating liquid components in mixture in a protective atmosphere and continuing to heat and decompose to obtain catalyst precursor, and (c) placing catalyst precursor in a reducing atmosphere to perform heating reduction reaction to obtain the product.
   USE - The process is useful for the production of dimethyl carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The process loads copper and nickel on the montmorillonite with a porous structure, which increases the content of the catalyst on the carrier with the same quality and promotes the catalytic efficiency.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In step (b), the protective atmosphere comprises nitrogen or argon. In step (c), the reducing atmosphere comprises hydrogen or carbon monoxide.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: In step (1), the pressure is 1.2-4.5 MPa, the heating temperature is 105-140 degrees C, and the reaction time is maintained above 3 hours. In step (b), the heating temperature is 80-95 degrees C, and the heating time is after the liquid components are evaporated and the temperature is maintained for more than 0.5 hour. In step (c), the heating is carried out at 450-550 degrees C, preferably not less than 480 degrees C for 0.5-1.5 hours. In step (ii), the dimethyl carbonate product in the reaction product is separated by a rectification method. Preferred Composition: In step (1), the ratio of the methanol and the catalyst is (90-130) ml:(0.9-1.8) g. In step (a), the mass ratio of the copper hydroxide powder and the nickel hydroxide powder is 1:(0.5-0.9). In step (a), the concentration of the aqueous ammonia is 25-28 %mass. In step (b), the total mass ratio of the porous montmorillonite to copper hydroxide powder and nickel hydroxide powder is (65-85):(15-35).
DC E17 (Other aliphatics.); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

TI Vehicle children asphyxia preventing safety device, has opening air conditioner device in mode by alarming module, and detector for dividing concentration in vehicle to transmit signal to owner of vehicle to direct alarm to help children.
AU YIN C
   ZHANG S
   YU H
   ZHANG B
AE UNIV ANHUI SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYLG-C)
GA 2020B2753X
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a multi-system combining device for preventing suffocation of children in a vehicle by detecting whether the children is present in the vehicle by a human body infrared sensor and voiceprint identification. A carbon dioxide concentration detector and a temperature sensor detect whether the vehicle is safe. An air conditioner device is opened in a window-lowering mode by an alarming module. The carbon dioxide concentration detector divides carbon dioxide concentration in the vehicle into three danger levels, mild level and moderate level to transmit a signal to an owner of the vehicle to direct an alarm to help the children when the children is present in the vehicle according to voiceprint.
   USE - Vehicle children asphyxia preventing safety device.
   ADVANTAGE - The device prevents locking of the children or pet in the vehicle by opening the air conditioner device in the window-lowering mode by the alarming module in an effective manner so as to avoid tragedy of suffocation death.

PT P
PN CN111841533-A; CN111841533-B
TI Preparing supported metal diatomic catalyst for            catalyzing hydrogenation of carbon dioxide, by            dissolving chloride of platinum group metal in water,            dispersing oxide carrier, adding metal precursor            solution, filtering, drying and calcining.
AU LOU Y
   JIANG F
   YAO T
   WANG L
   ZHU Y
   LIU X
AE UNIV JIANGNAN (UYJN-C)
GA 2020A96254
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing supported metal diatomic                catalyst, involves dissolving chloride or nitrate                of platinum group metal in water or alcohol solvent                to obtain a metal precursor solution, controlling                the temperature of the metal precursor solution to                20-40 degrees C, adjusting the isoelectric point of                the solution to 1-8 by adding an acid solution, so                that the platinum group metals in the precursor                solution form a binary coordination configuration,                dispersing oxide carrier in water or alcohol                solvent to prepare a carrier solution, and                uniformly dispersing the oxide carrier in the                carrier solution by ultrasonic vibration, where the                oxide carrier comprises cerium oxide, gallium                oxide, indium oxide and zinc oxide, adding the                metal precursor solution to the carrier solution,                so that the metal precursor is uniformly dispersed                on the surface of the oxide carrier, filtering,                drying, and calcining in air or oxygen.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing supported                metal diatomic catalyst for catalyzing                hydrogenation of carbon dioxide to produce ethanol                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The supported metal diatomic catalyst has high                activity and high selectivity for the catalytic                hydrogenation of carbon dioxide to produce                ethanol.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The platinum group                  metal is palladium, platinum, rhodium or iridium.                  The acid solution is hydrochloric acid. Preferred                  Composition: The concentration of the metal                  precursor solution is 0.15-0.5 mmol/l. Preferred                  Method: The method for synthesizing the oxide                  carrier involves reacting the nitrate or acetate                  of the carrier metal with sodium carbonate as a                  precipitant to form a basic carbonate, drying the                  basic carbonate, and calcining under the                  atmosphere of nitrogen and oxygen at 350-450                  degrees C to obtain the oxide carrier. Preferred                  Conditions: The metal precursor solution is added                  to the carrier solution at a sample rate of                  0.5-1.5 ml/minute. The calcination is carried out                  at 200-400 degrees C for 4 hours.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The alcohol solvent                  comprises ethanol and ethylene glycol.
DC E17 (Other aliphatics.); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

PN CN111825796-A; CN111825796-B
TI Polyacrylate composite material used for carbon dioxide adsorption, is prepared by aminating and grafting porous polyacrylate, and performing end-capping treatment on epoxy resin surface.
AU YE J
   GUO S
   LIU H
   HUANG X
AE WUHAN HUITAN TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (WUHA-Non-standard)
GA 2020A8793D
AB    NOVELTY - Polyacrylate composite material is prepared by aminating and grafting porous polyacrylate, and performing end-capping treatment on the epoxy resin surface to obtain the product.
   USE - The composite material is used for carbon dioxide adsorption.
   ADVANTAGE - The composite material has simple synthesis process, is cost-effective, has three-level interconnection via structure, meets the needs of rapid carbon dioxide adsorption/desorption, and does not cause the water vapor in the flue gas to block the pores, where the particle size distribution is uniform and the size of the ball is controllable, which meets the needs of the fluidized bed.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing the composite material, involving (i) preparing the acrylate monomer, crosslinking agent, surfactant, porogen, initiator and water into an emulsion, (ii) preparing water and dispersant into a dispersed phase, (iii) adding the emulsion obtained to the dispersed phase, dispersing into balls, adding reducing agent after stabilization, separating and drying after curing to prepare spherical porous polyacrylate pellets, and (iv) immersing the porous polyacrylate pellets in the solution containing the amine-based modifier, heating for amination graft modification, and blocking the surface with epoxy resin to obtain the product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The porous polyacrylate is a polyacrylate pellet formed by crosslinking three monomers of methyl methacrylate, n-butyl acrylate and glycidyl methacrylate. The amine-based modifier comprises diethanolamine, ethylenediamine, diethylenetriamine, tetraethylene pentamine, pentaethylene hexaamine, tetraethylene pentamine acrylonitrile, melamine, urea, and/or polyethyleneimine. The polyacrylate monomer comprises methyl methacrylate, n-butyl acrylate and glycidyl methacrylate. The crosslinking agent is an acrylate crosslinking agent. The crosslinking agent is trimethylolpropane triacrylate. The initiator is benzoyl peroxide. The porogen is toluene. The reducing agent is N,N,N',N'-tetramethylethylenediamine. Preferred Condition: The heating step (iv) is carried out at 60-80 degrees C.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: The amination and grafting, and the solution containing the amine-based modifier comprises a mixed solution comprising an amine-based modifier, and glycidyl methacrylate, epichlorohydrin or butylene oxide. The surfactant is a polyoxypropylene polyoxyethylene copolymer.

PN CN111701618-A
TI Ionic liquid catalyst useful in preparation of ethylene carbonate, comprises N-alkylimidazole, halogenated alkyl alcohol, and halogenated zinc salt.
AU WU L
   ZHANG H
   LI B
   ZHAO Y
   PENG Y
AE JIANGSU SIPAI NEW ENERGY TECHNOLOGY CO (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 202097553M
AB    NOVELTY - Ionic liquid catalyst comprises N-alkylimidazole, halogenated alkyl alcohol, and halogenated zinc salt in a molar ratio of 1:(1-1.5):(1.5-2).
   USE - The ionic liquid catalyst is useful in preparation of ethylene carbonate (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The ionic liquid catalyst dissolves the carbon dioxide and epoxy compounds to promote the carbonylation reaction.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a method for preparing the catalyst, involving injecting N-alkylimidazole to reactor, heating at 80-100 degrees C, slowly injecting halogenated alkyl alcohol to reactor under stirring condition, controlling the temperature to 50-70 degrees C, maintaining the constant temperature for 4-5 hours under temperature condition, vacuuming with vacuum pump, and evaporating unreacted halogenated alkyl alcohol, adding halogenated zinc salt to reactor, performing constant-temperature mixing at 70-80 degrees C for 2-3 hours, and taking out to obtain ionic liquid catalyst; and
   (2) a use of ionic liquid catalyst in the preparation of ethylene carbonate, involving adding ethylene oxide and ionic liquid catalyst to reaction kettle, introducing carbon dioxide to ethylene oxide and carbon dioxide in a molar ratio of 1:(1-2), reacting at 100-150 degrees C under pressure of 0.5-6 MPa for 2-3 hours, and performing distillation under reduced pressure to obtain ethylene carbonate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The halogenated zinc salt comprises zinc chloride, zinc bromide and/or zinc iodide.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The N-alkylimidazole comprises N-methylimidazole, N-ethylimidazole and/or N-propylimidazole. The halogenated alkyl alcohol comprises 4-chlorobutanol, 4-bromobutanol and/or 4-iodobutanol.

AE AIXIER SHENZHEN PHARM RES & DEV CO LTD (AIXI-Non-standard)
GA 2020314431
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing canagliflozin impurities (I) involves dissolving 2-(4-fluorophenyl)-5-((5-iodo-2-methylphenyl) methyl) thiophene in a solvent under the protection of inert gas, dripping n-butyl lithium solution at the reaction temperature, introducing carbon dioxide gas into the reaction system, heating at room temperature for 2 hours, adding saturated ammonium chloride solution, extracting, drying, concentrating and purifying the resulted product to obtain the product, where the reaction temperature does not exceed 40 degrees C.

   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for preparing canagliflozin impurities of formula (I) involves dissolving 2-(4-fluorophenyl)-5-((5-iodo-2-methylphenyl) methyl) thiophene in a solvent under the protection of inert gas, dripping n-butyl lithium solution at the reaction temperature, introducing carbon dioxide gas into the reaction system, heating at room temperature for 2 hours, adding saturated ammonium chloride solution, extracting, drying, concentrating and purifying the resulted product to obtain the product, where the reaction temperature does not exceed -40 degrees C.

Preferred Components: The n-butyl lithium solution is a n-hexane solution or a toluene solution with a n-butyl lithium concentration of 1-2.5 M. PN CN110560121-A; CN110560121-B
TI Preparing carbon dioxide-assisted porous carbon nitride material useful for catalytic photo-degradation of rhodamine B, comprises e. g. adding measured melamine into a reaction kettle, adding measured deionized water and stirring.
AU CAI K
   ZHOU Y
   WANG X
   WANG Y
   WANG P
   XU L
AE UNIV XUZHOU TECHNOLOGY (XUZH-C)
   UNIV XUZHOU TECHNOLOGY (XUZH-C)
GA 2019A6743L
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing carbon dioxide-assisted porous carbon nitride material comprises (i) adding measured melamine into a reaction kettle, adding measured deionized water, magnetically stirring, passing carbon dioxide into the deionized water, reacting at 50-100 degrees C, keeping the pressure in the reactor at 0.01-0.1 MPa for 2-6 hours, naturally cooling the mixture and suction filtering to obtain intermediate, where the mass ratio of the melamine and the deionized water is 1:20-100; and (ii) transferring the intermediate obtained in step (i) into a crucible with a lid, placing together in a muffle furnace, calcining the muffle furnace at 2-6 degrees C/minute to 540-580 degrees C in the furnace cavity for 2-5 hours, naturally cooling the temperature in the furnace cavity to room temperature, and taking out the product.
   USE - The material is useful for catalytic photo-degradation of rhodamine B (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method has rich raw material source, and is simple, low cost and environmentally-friendly and ensures high product yield. The material has high specific surface area.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a porous carbon nitride material is prepared by above mentioned method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the mass ratio of the melamine and deionized water is 1:50. Preferred Method: In the step (i), the reaction temperature is 80 degrees C, the pressure in the reactor is 0.05 MPa, and the reaction time is 4 hours. In the step (ii), the muffle furnace is heated at rate of 4 degrees C/minute to 550 degrees C in the furnace cavity for 3 hours.

TI Mixed matrix film for use in carbon dioxide gas separation, comprises 2-methyl-isoindole-1,3-dione compound.
AU LIU H
   BEI P
   YAO H
   WANG Y
   GAO Y
   ZHANG Y
AE UNIV SHENYANG TECHNOLOGY (UYTE-C)
GA 201994373G
AB    NOVELTY - A mixed matrix film comprises 2-methyl-isoindole-1,3-dione compound.
   USE - Mixed matrix film for use in carbon dioxide gas separation (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The mixed matrix film has interaction of the electrostatic force to improve the agglomeration phenomenon and dispersion degree of the graphene oxide in the film casting solution and enhanced selectivity of carbon dioxide gas.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A mixed matrix film comprises 2-Methyl-isoindole-1,3-dione compound of formula (I).
   R1 = benzene moiety of formula (a) or biphenyl moiety of formulae (b-f); and
   n = 100-200.
   . An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a method for preparing a mixed matrix film, which involves adding an aminated functional ionic liquid to a casting film liquid formed of polyimide, heating in a water bath and stirring, passing the aminated functional ionic liquid through the characteristic structure of Schiff base and is grafted to the polyimide main chain in the form of side groups to obtain a cast film solution of grafted polyimide, adding graphene oxide to the casting film solution of the graft-type polyimide, heating and stirring in a water bath to obtain the finished product by a solvent evaporation method, drying the film to solidify, and peeling off the film after freezing to obtain a grafted ionic liquid polyimide/graphene oxide mixed matrix film.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The graphene oxide is prepared by mixing graphite and sodium chloride at a mass ratio of 1:60-80, and transferring to beaker after grinding, adding deionized water to fully dissolve, suction filter under reduced pressure, drying filter cake in a vacuum drying box and passing the dried graphite through a 200 mesh standard sieve, adding a concentrated sulfuric acid to a three-necked flask containing graphite powder to control the mass ratio of graphite to concentrated sulfuric acid is 1:55-65, and slowly adding a potassium permanganate, graphite and permanganate and stirring, heating the mixed solution in the low temperature stage at 35-45 degrees C and stirring for 0.5-1.5 hours, slowly adding the deionized water to the three-necked flask and continue to stirring for 0.5-1 hour, further adding a hydrogen peroxide solution to control the mass ratio of hydrogen peroxide to potassium permanganate to 1.4-1.8:1, pouring 500-800 mL deionized water, diluting the mixed solution in the three-necked flask, suction filter, washing with dilute hydrochloric acid, and wash the filter cake with deionized water to neutrality and sonicating for 1-2 hours, drying in a vacuum drying oven for 48-60 hours, and controlling the temperature of the vacuum drying oven to 45-65 degrees C to obtain the finished product. Preferred Components: The mixed matrix membrane is used to separate a mixed gas system of carbon dioxide/nitrogen, carbon dioxide/methane, and carbon dioxide/oxygen.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The aminated functional ionic liquid is prepared by heating N-butyl imidazole with bromoethylamine hydrobromide, refluxing an ethanol, and drying to obtain an intermediate, and adding a potassium hydroxide to remove potassium bromide, performing an ion exchange reaction with potassium hexafluorophosphate, after the ethanol is dissolved, heating the solution in a water bath to remove the ethanol to obtain the finished product.

GA 201932708U
AB    NOVELTY - A catalyst comprises carrier comprising 55-85                wt.% aluminum oxide, and 1-20 wt.% carbon                nanotubes, active component comprising 10-25 wt.%                nickel(II) oxide, and 0-5 wt.% additive comprising                lanthanum oxide, and cerium dioxide. The additive                is chosen from one or more of lanthanum oxide, and                cerium dioxide.
   USE - Catalyst used in the low-temperature                methanation of carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst having high strength, and                excellent carbon dioxide low-temperature                methanation activity, and catalyst stability even                at high temperature, is manufactured efficiently by                simple method.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for                preparation of catalyst, which involves adding                aluminum oxide, and carbon nanotubes powder to the                aluminum nitrate solution, stirring uniformly,                adding potassium carbonate solution in drops to                obtain mixed solution, carrying out precipitation                reaction to obtain precipitate, washing, filtering,                drying, calcining to obtain alumina-carbon nanotube                carrier powder, adding water to the alumina-carbon                nanotube carrier powder, uniformly mixing,                press-molding, drying to obtain alumina-carbon                nanotube carrier, preparing hydrated nitrate                solution of nickel nitrate, and auxiliary agent,                adding water to obtain impregnation solution,                impregnating the carrier in the impregnation                solution, drying the impregnated carrier, and                calcining.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Properties: The aluminum oxide                  powder, and carbon nanotube is sieved through                  100-400 mesh. Preferred Conditions: The                  precipitation reaction is performed at 50-90                  degrees C for 30-200 minutes. The pH of the mixed                  solution is 7-8.5. The calcining is performed at                  300-600 degrees C for 4-12 hours. The carrier is                  impregnated in the impregnating solution at 50-90                  degrees C for 0.5-2 hours. The impregnated                  carrier is dried at 300-600 degrees C for 4-12                  hours. Preferred Process: The press forming                  process involves sieving the alumina-carbon                  nanotube carrier powder to obtain fine powder,                  and uniformly mixing the fine powder, and water.                  Preferred Composition: The concentration of                  hydrated nickel nitrate solution in the                  impregnation solution is 1.62-1.7 g/mL. The                  concentration of additive in the hydrated nickel                  nitrate solution is 0.16-0.18 g/mL.

   KOMODA Y
   HIDEMA R
AE DAIKIN KOGYO KK (DAIK-C)
   DAIKIN APPLIED SYSTEMS CO LTD (DAIK-C)
   UNIV KOBE (UYBK-C)
GA 2018073654
AB    NOVELTY - A thermal medium comprises an aqueous solution of host substance, a guest substance (a1) capable of forming a hydrate having an empty cage clathrated in the aqueous solution of host substance, and a guest substance (a2) containing carbon dioxide occupying the empty cage of the hydrate formed by the guest substance (a1).
   USE - Thermal medium is used as refrigerant for thermal storage material of thermal storage-type cooling device and refrigerant circulation-type cooling device used in cold storage warehouse.
   ADVANTAGE - The thermal medium has excellent latent heat-raising property, when compared with thermal medium capable of forming single hydrate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for formation of double hydrate, which involves cooling a solution in which the guest substance (a1) is dissolved in the aqueous solution of host substance to a temperature (t1) lower than the phase transition point, forming a hydrate of guest substance (a1), obtaining a solution by heating and dissolving the hydrate, cooling the obtained solution to a temperature (t2) higher than the temperature (t1) for carrying out reformation of the hydrate by a memory effect, and supplying carbon dioxide of guest substance (a2) to the solution for occupying the empty cage of the hydrate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The thermal medium further contains brine containing ethyl alcohol, ethylene glycol, or propylene glycol. The content of brine in the thermal medium is 1-99 %mass. The guest substance (a1) contains quaternary ammonium salt, quaternary phosphate, or halide containing four n-butyl groups, preferably tetra-n-butylammonium bromide. The mass ratio of guest substance (a1) with respect to aqueous solution of host substance is 1/99-99/1. Preferred Process: The hydrate is dissolved at a temperature at which the temperature difference from the phase transition point is less than 5 degrees C. Formation of double hydrate further involves supplying the carbon dioxide to the solution by bubbling under atmospheric pressure.

PN CN111285373-A
TI Device for producing dry ice, has reaction tower that provides liquid carbon dioxide through liquid carbon dioxide inlet flange to enter finned heat exchanger and exchange with low-temperature gas carbon dioxide to become cold.
AU SHI W
   ZENG Y
   LIU F
AE HUIZHOU HUADATONG PETROCHEMICAL CO LTD (HUIZ-Non-standard)
GA 2020564976
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a reaction tower (1), a dry ice storage tower (2) and a dry ice compressor (3). The reaction tower is provided with a finned heat exchanger (4). The upper portion of the first pipe of the finned heat exchanger is connected to a liquid carbon dioxide inlet flange (5). The second pipe inlet of the finned heat exchanger is connected to the cavity of the reaction tower, and second pipe outlet is connected to an exhaust flange (8). The liquid carbon dioxide comes out of the reaction tower through the liquid carbon dioxide inlet flange, enters the finned heat exchanger and exchanges with the low-temperature gas carbon dioxide to become cold. The liquid carbon dioxide enters the dry ice storage tower and becomes dry ice powder after reducing pressure to normal pressure through the branch pipe and a solenoid valve (7). The dry ice powder falls to the outlet by gravity and enters into the dry ice compressor to be compressed into block.
   USE - Device for producing dry ice.
   ADVANTAGE - The device greatly improves the exchange efficiency of the cooling capacity and conversion rate of dry ice. The exhaust gas scientifically controls the emission. The large amount of gaseous carbon dioxide is completely separated from dry ice to avoid a large amount of aerosol on site to pollute the environment. The photoelectric sensor controls the flow of carbon dioxide through the solenoid valve to avoid the pressure of carbon dioxide too large or too small and to allow even flow through the pipeline. The sensor and weight counter control the dry ice of a set weight to enter the dry ice compressor to be mold to compress into blocks, so as to avoid the disorderly accumulation of dry ice powder and block the dry ice compressor inlet. The device has high-efficiency and is energy-saving.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for producing dry ice.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of the structure of the device for producing dry ice.
   Reaction tower (1)
   Dry ice storage tower (2)
   Dry ice compressor (3)
   Finned heat exchanger (4)
   Liquid carbon dioxide inlet flange (5)
   Solenoid valve (7)
   Exhaust flange (8)

The snow horn is                accommodated within an upwardly closed casing tube                (3) which projects downwards in relation to the                outlet opening of the snow horn and has an outlet                funnel (13) which tapers towards a bottom outlet                opening (16) of the casing tube.
   USE - Device for metering carbon dioxide snow, for                freezing and/or crusting product such as food                product, pharmaceutical product or medical or                biological sample.
   ADVANTAGE - The device for metering carbon dioxide snow                which can deliver an exact and constant amount of                carbon dioxide snow, is simple in construction and                in handling and is flexibly adapted to different                requirements. The casing tube serves to thermally                insulate the snow horn, since the cold carbon                dioxide gas penetrating briefly into the casing                chamber reliably shields ambient heat from the wall                of the expansion chamber. The small amounts of                carbon dioxide snow are generated in the snow horn                without the risk of the carbon dioxide snow                subliming for the most part on the path to the                outlet opening of the casing tube. The bell mouth                of the casing tube projecting downwardly from the                mouth opening of the snow horn prevents ambient                moisture ingress during operation of the device.                The penetration of ambient moisture into the casing                tube during running operation is reliably avoided.                The accurate detection of the quantity of snow                produced is enabled. The gas phase contained in the                liquid carbon dioxide is largely separated off. The                impairment of the functionality of the device by                baking carbon dioxide snow is reliably avoided due                to the design.

PN WO2020035528-A1; SE201850984-A1; SE542646-C2; CN112638858-A; EP3837236-A1; US2021324301-A1; EP3837236-B1
TI Use of water and carbon dioxide to make edible            organic substances as food or feed by e.g.            electrolyzing water, capture or recovery of carbon            dioxide, converting carbon dioxide to carbon monoxide            and subjecting hydrogen and carbon monoxide.
AU LOREN A
   VESTERGREN M
AE RISE RES INST SWEDEN AB (RISE-Non-standard)
   RISE RES INST SWEDEN AB (RISE-Non-standard)
   RISE RES INST SWEDEN AB (RISE-Non-standard)
   GREEN-ON AB (GREE-Non-standard)
GA 202015065R
AB    NOVELTY - Use of water and carbon dioxide for producing                edible organic substances by (a) electrolysis of                water to produce hydrogen and oxygen, (b) capture                or recovery of carbon dioxide, (c) conversion of                the carbon dioxide to carbon monoxide, (d)                subjecting the hydrogen and carbon monoxide to a                Fischer-Tropsch synthesis to produce a mixture of                olefins, (e) optionally increasing the proportion                of ethylene by coupling of methane to form                ethylene, and/or converting higher olefins to form                ethylene, (f) isolating ethylene, (g) synthesizing                alpha -olefins or fatty acid alcohols from the                ethylene, and (h) oxidizing the alpha -olefins or                fatty acid alcohols to free fatty acids, is                claimed, where the net energy input is in the form                of electricity.
   USE - The water and carbon dioxide is useful for                producing edible organic substances, which is                useful for producing food or feed product (all                claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The water and carbon dioxide provides the food                or feed product to minimize the need for                agriculture and animal farming, and may                significantly reduce and even reverse the                environmental impact of the earth's growing                population.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) producing edible organic substances                comprising the steps (a)-(h) as per se; and
   (2) a food or feed product produced by the                method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: The net energy input                  is in the form of electricity. The free fatty                  acids as such, or after esterification to                  triglycerides, are formulated into food for human                  or feed for animal consumption. The free fatty                  acids as such, or after esterification to                  triglycerides, are added to culture media for the                  growing of cells, the cells comprising yeast                  cells, bacterial cells, fungal cells, plant cells                  or non human animal cells. The triglycerides or                  lipids are used as feed for insects, and the                  insects are harvested for the production of food,                  or feed for animals, reducing the need for                  plant-based feed when raising animals.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The process further                  comprises adding glycerol and esterifying the                  free fatty acids to triglycerides. The oxygen                  produced in the electrolysis step is led into a                  combustion unit for the combustion of organic                  matter, and carbon dioxide is captured/recovered                  from flue gases from the combustion. The                  electrolysis step is a high-temperature                  electrolysis step, and waste heat from the                  Fischer-Tropsch synthesis or the exothermic                  methanol synthesis is used to generate steam for                  the high-temperature electrolysis step.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

AB    NOVELTY - Device comprises low temperature plasma                source, front end of the low temperature plasma                source has nozzle and back end of the                low-temperature plasma source is connected to a                carbon dioxide container through a connecting pipe.                The carbon dioxide container is provided with                pressure gauge.
   USE - Device for strengthening weathered fossil                artifacts by low-temperature plasma-activated                calcium hydroxide in carbon dioxide                atmosphere.
   ADVANTAGE - The device is non-destructive, has no side                effect and high efficiency.

   (C) repeating 10-15 times.

PN CN109485773-A
TI Oil-absorbing resin is formed by copolymerizing styrene monomer and carbon dioxide stimuli-responsive monomer containing carbon-carbon double bond and dimethylamino group, or carbon-carbon double bond and diethylamino group.
AU CAI S
   GUO S
AE UNIV JIANGHAN (UYJJ-C)
GA 201927157W
AB    NOVELTY - An oil-absorbing resin is formed by copolymerizing a carbon dioxide stimuli-responsive monomer and a styrene monomer in the presence of an initiator. The carbon dioxide stimuli-responsive monomer contains a carbon-carbon double bond and a dimethylamino group, or a carbon-carbon double bond and a diethylamino group, preferably carbon-carbon double bond and diethylamino group.
   USE - Oil-absorbing resin.
   ADVANTAGE - The oil-absorbing resin introduces carbon dioxide in styrene monomer to stimulate reaction monomer, and utilizes adsorption capacity of olefinic segment in styrene monomer to absorb oil phase. The amine group in carbon dioxide stimuli-response monomer completes lipophilic-hydrophilic transition under the action of carbon dioxide to achieve desorption of adsorbed oil phase. The recovery and reuse of adsorbed oil phase is completed by carbon dioxide stimulation, by simple and environmentally-friendly method using inexpensive and simple device. The adsorption and desorption does not affect the durability of oil-absorbing resin itself.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of the oil-absorbing resin, which involves dissolving and mixing carbon dioxide stimuli-responsive monomer, styrene monomer, and initiator, adding the obtained product to a container filled with deionized water, copolymerizing at 60-90 degrees C for 4-12 hours, suction-filtering the polymerized product, and drying.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - The carbon dioxide stimuli-responsive monomer is 1,4-bis(diethylamino)-3-((2-methylprop-2-enoyl)oxy)but-2-yl 2-methylprop-2-enoate of formula (I) or 2-(dimethylamino)ethyl 2-methylprop-2-enoate of formula (II). The initiator is azobisisobutyronitrile, azobisisoheptonitrile, or dibenzoyl peroxide. Preferred Composition: The molar ratio of carbon dioxide stimuli-responsive monomer and styrene monomer is 5:1-1:5. The molar ratio of initiator and total content of carbon dioxide stimuli-responsive monomer and styrene monomer is 1:10-1:1000. The solvent used for copolymerization is 1-2 mol/L toluene solution. The volume ratio of deionized water and toluene is 6:1-20:1.

PN JP2018080071-A
TI Carbon dioxide gas removal in exhaust gas used in incinerator involves reacting combustion apparatus exhaust gas with caustic soda aqueous solution to form aqueous solution of sodium carbonate and reacting with catalyst in heated reactor.
AU ISHIKAWA Y
   OKUYAMA M
AE TI KK (TITI-Non-standard)
GA 201841165T
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide gas is removed from exhaust gas by reacting exhaust gas from combustion apparatus with caustic soda aqueous solution to form aqueous solution of sodium carbonate, and supplying aqueous solution of sodium carbonate into heated reactor (8) and reacting with stainless steel catalyst containing sodium in stainless steel container to generate hydrogen and to remove carbon dioxide gas in exhaust gas.
   USE - Carbon dioxide gas removal in exhaust gas used in thermal power plant and incinerator.
   ADVANTAGE - The method and apparatus can efficiently process large amount of carbon dioxide gas in exhaust gas. When hydrogen is used as fuel of combustion furnace, discharge of carbon dioxide gas in exhaust gas can be reduced.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for apparatus (M) for removing carbon dioxide gas in exhaust gas in which sodium of stainless steel catalyst is supplied from aqueous solution of sodium carbonate produced in tank (3), and sodium is combined with reactor inner wall, stainless steel powder, stainless steel mass and/or stainless steel plate to form stainless steel catalyst.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic block diagram of an apparatus for removing carbon dioxide gas in exhaust gas.
   Combustion furnace (1)
   Caustic soda tank (3)
   Bubbling piping (5)
   Reactor (8)
   Apparatus (M)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The stainless steel catalyst is a combination of a stainless steel component and a sodium component. Sodium component is supplied from sodium carbonate and/or sodium hydroxide. Preferred Devices: Reactor is installed on combustion apparatus so that control of heating temperature is possible. Preferred Processes: Sodium of stainless steel catalyst is supplied from caustic soda previously supplied to container of stainless steel, and is also supplied from aqueous solution of sodium carbonate after operation is started.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Components: Reactor consists of austenitic stainless steel material containing nickel. Reactor comprises SUS304 stainless steel.
