PN CN113731157-A
TI Recycling ammonia nitrogen and carbon dioxide synchronously, comprises e.g. utilizing waste heat resources to decompose ammonia nitrogen and carbon dioxide synchronous absorption liquid, and ionizing calcium ions and sulfate ions in water, and reducing concentration of calcium ions.
AU ZHU W
   WANG N
   FENG Y
   DIAO X
   HE W
AE UNIV TIANJIN (UTIJ-C)
GA 2021E6223N
AB    NOVELTY - Recycling ammonia nitrogen and carbon dioxide synchronously, comprises (i) utilizing waste heat resources to decompose ammonia nitrogen and carbon dioxide synchronous absorption liquid into ammonia gas and carbon dioxide mixed gas under the action of waste heat, (ii) passing the mixed gas into the desulfurized gypsum suspension to obtain ammonium sulfate liquid and calcium carbonate solid, and (iii) ionizing a small amount of calcium ions and sulfate ions in water, when calcium ions meet carbonate ions, combining to form calcium carbonate that is insoluble in water, and reducing the concentration of calcium ions.
   USE - The method is useful for recycling ammonia nitrogen and carbon dioxide synchronously.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is harmless and recycles waste heat.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method comprises after passing through the waste heat recovery device, the absorbing liquid containing high-concentration ammonia nitrogen and inorganic carbon changes from normal temperature to high temperature. The high-temperature absorption liquid undergoes thermal decomposition reaction to produce a mixed gas with ammonia and carbon dioxide as the main body, where the ammonia nitrogen and carbon dioxide synchronous absorption liquid is rich in ammonia, carbonate, bicarbonate, and ammonium bicarbonate decomposes under the action of high temperature to produce ammonia gas and carbon dioxide gas. The high-temperature absorption liquid and mixed gas obtained by the waste heat recovery device are separated inside the insulation device, and the mixed gas is directly transported to the subsequent carbon and nitrogen absorption device through the corrosion-resistant pipeline, and the remaining high-temperature absorbing liquid can continue to undergo decomposition reaction in the device to generate mixed gas. The calcium sulfate suspension and the mixed gas undergo metathesis reaction to generate ammonium sulfate and calcium carbonate. The flow mode of gas-liquid two-phase includes countercurrent and cocurrent; the contact mode of gas-liquid two-phase includes: (i) directly injecting the mixed gas into the liquid to react, e.g. direct aeration, (ii) dispersing the mixed gas in the liquid in the form of bubbles to react, and (iii) the reaction between gas and droplet being trapped by the collision of liquid film. During the operation of the calcium sulfate waste residue carbon and nitrogen resource utilization system, The optimal treatment effect is achieved by adjusting various parameters: by adjusting the heating temperature of the heat exchanger, the heating time of the absorption liquid, the pH value of the absorption liquid, the concentration of the absorption liquid, the stirring speed, the flow rate of the absorption liquid, and the reaction time to control the thermal decomposition rate.

   GE Q
   YAO R
   WEI J
AE CAS DALIAN CHEM & PHYSICAL INST (CACP-C)
GA 202270748K
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing olefin by carbon dioxide                hydrogenation involves reacting raw material gas                contains hydrogen (H2) and carbon-dioxide (CO2)                which passes through reactor contains catalyst to                obtain olefin, where catalyst comprises active                component and auxiliary agent; loading auxiliary                agent on active component, where active component                is iron oxide (Fe3O4) nanometer micro-sphere; and                grain diameter of Fe3O4nanometer micro-sphere is                100 to 1000 nanometer (nm).
   USE - Method for preparing olefin by carbon dioxide                hydrogenation.
   ADVANTAGE - The method for preparing olefin by carbon                dioxide hydrogenation enables to provide uniform                and controllable appearance, good monodispersity,                high reaction activity, single conversion rate                reach more than 30%, selectivity of methane and CO                in product are less than 15%, selectivity of olefin                of more than 70%, good stability, high catalytic                activity after 500 hour of reaction, and has good                industrial application prospect.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The auxiliary agent is                  selected from alkali metal and alkaline earth                  metal, or cobalt (Co), copper (Cu), zinc (Zn),                  zirconium (Zr), silicon (Si), aluminum (Al),                  cerium (Ce) oxide. The alkali metal is selected                  from at least one of lithium (Li), sodium (Na),                  potassium (K), rubidium (Rb), cesium (Cs). The                  alkaline earth metal is at least one selected                  from magnesium (Mg), calcium (Ca), barium (Ba)                  and strontium (Sr). The auxiliary salt is at                  least one of chloride, nitrate and acetate of                  alkali metal or alkaline earth metal or Co, Cu,                  Zn, Zr, Si, Al and Ce. The iron salt is selected                  from iron salts; the iron salt is selected from                  at least one of iron chloride, iron sulfate, iron                  acetate and iron nitrate.Preferred Composition: The catalyst                  comprises 0.1-30 wt.% auxiliary agent, preferably                  0.1-10 wt.% auxiliary agent, where content of                  auxiliary agent calculated by quality of metal                  element. The reducing atmosphere is selected from                  H2 and carbon-monoxide (CO). The reactor is fixed                  bed reactor.Preferred Conditions: The mol ratio of H2                  and CO2 is 1-8:1, preferably 2-4:1; reaction                  temperature is 240-400&#8451;, preferably                  280-350&#8451;; reaction pressure is 0.1-10                  Megapascal (MPa), preferably 1-4 MPa; space                  velocity is 500-30000 mL/hour * gcat, preferably                  2000 to 8000 mL/hour * gcat. The molar ratio of                  iron salt to alkaline precipitate agent is 1:                  2-8; the solid-to-liquid ratio of iron salt to                  solvent is 1 g: 10-50 mL; the content of surface                  active agent in mixed solution is 1-6%.Preferred Method: The method for preparing                  olefin involves preparing Fe3O4 nanometer                  micro-sphere by solvothermal method involves                  reacting mixed solution containing iron salt,                  alkaline precipitate agent, solvent and                  surfactant for 2-24 hours at 150-220&#8451;                  in sealed container to obtain Fe3O4 nanometer                  microspheres; soaking Fe3O4 nano-microspheres in                  solution contains auxiliary agent salt, drying                  and roasting to obtain Fe3O4 contains auxiliary                  agent nano-microspheres, where catalyst further                  comprises reduction step before using, preferably                  reducing in situ for 2-24 hours at                  300-500&#8451; in reducing atmosphere.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The solvent is                  selected from ethylene glycol; the alkaline                  precipitate agent is selected from at least one                  of sodium acetate, sodium oxalate and sodium                  citrate; the surface active agent is at least one                  selected from polyethylene glycol,                  polyethyleneimine and polyvinylpyrrolidone.

TI Preparing bismuth-copper bimetallic catalyst useful electrochemical reduction of carbon dioxide comprises synthesizing bismuth salt and copper salt by solution chemical reduction method.
AU GU D
   WANG M
   ZHANG C
   YAN X
   SONG Z
   KANG H

   MAO Y
   ZHANG X
AE UNIV HENAN URBAN CONSTR (UHUC-C)
GA 2021E49423
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing bismuth-copper bimetallic catalyst, comprises synthesizing bismuth salt and copper salt by solution chemical reduction method.
   USE - The catalyst is useful for electrochemical reduction of carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst: improves the selectivity of carbon dioxide reduction and energy efficiency; reduces the overpotential of carbondioxide reduction; ensures competitive hydrogen evolution side reaction in the carbon dioxide reduction process is effectively suppressed; and improves utilization rate and conversion rate of carbondioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for electrochemical reduction of carbon dioxide using catalyst comprising loading working electrode in carbon dioxide electrochemical reduction with bismuth-copper bimetallic catalyst, and its preparation method comprises (i) dispersing the bismuth-copper bimetallic catalyst into isopropanol to obtain a catalyst slurry, adding Nafion solution and mixing to obtain a mixed solution; and (ii) coating mixed solution on the working electrode body and drying.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The catalyst is prepared by (a) dissolving bismuth salt and copper salt in an organic solvent, adding polyvinylpyrrolidone, and mixing to obtain a catalyst precursor solution, where the ratio of the amount of polyvinylpyrrolidone and sum of the amount of metal ions in bismuth salt and copper salt is (1-5):(1-10); and (ii) dissolving sodium borohydride solution in an organic solvent to obtain a reduction solution; (iii) adding reduction solution into the catalyst precursor solution for chemical reaction, centrifuging and seperating obtained suspension, washing solid, centrifuging, and drying in vacuum to obtain a bismuth-copper bimetallic catalyst, where ratio of the amount of sodium borohydride in the sodium borohydride solution and sum of the amount of metal ions in the bismuth salt and the copper salt is (3-8 ):1. In the step (iii), the chemical reaction control conditions, the reaction temperature is 0-25degrees Celsius, and the reaction time is 5-120 minutes. The obtained solid is washed successively with acetone, absolute ethanol and deionized water, where concentration of sodium borohydride solution is 1M. The method comprises vacuum drying at 50 -70degrees Celsius, for 6-8 hours.Preferred Components: The molar ratio of bismuth salt and copper salt is (1-3): (0-1). The organic solvent is N,N-dimethylformamide, the bismuth salt is bismuth nitrate pentahydrate, and the copper salt is copper nitrate trihydrate. The working electrode main body is carbon paper.

PT P
PN CN109665549-A
TI Preparing calcium-aluminum hydrotalcite by carbon dioxide by reacting aluminum hydroxide and calcium hydroxide in aqueous solution and introducing carbon dioxide to calcium aluminate hydrate slurry and carrying out solid-liquid separation.
AU ZENG W
AE HUNAN HENGGUANG CHEM CO LTD (HUNA-Non-standard)
GA 2019399775
AB    NOVELTY - Process for preparing calcium-aluminum hydrotalcite by carbon dioxide involves (a) reacting aluminum hydroxide and calcium hydroxide in an aqueous solution system to obtain calcium aluminate hydrate slurry and (b) introducing carbon dioxide to calcium aluminate hydrate slurry, reacting to obtain calcium-aluminum hydrotalcite precipitates, carrying out solid-liquid separation and drying.
   USE - The process is useful for preparing calcium-aluminum hydrotalcite by carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The process has no waste water discharge and is economical and suitable for industrial production.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step (a) involves reacting at 80-100 degrees C for 6-12 hours. The step (b) involves cooling below 40 degrees C before introducing carbon dioxide to the calcium aluminate hydrate slurry. The reaction in step (b) is carried out by introducing carbon dioxide to the calcium aluminate hydrate slurry until the pH of the slurry system drops to less than 11.5. Preferred Composition: The slurry has solid content of 5-40%. The molar ratio of calcium hydroxide and aluminum hydroxide is (1.5-3):1, preferably 2:1.

PT P
PN CN113896197-A
TI Preparation of carbon monoxide by reforming hydrocarbon carbon dioxide includes mixing hydrocarbon raw material gas with carbon dioxide gas for compression, and preheating compressed mixed gas using high-temperature reforming gas.
AU GE D
   LIAO J
   CHENG M
   ZHAO P
   LIU G
   NI W
   YANG J
   HUANG X
   XIAO Y
   LIU Y
   ZHANG X
   WANG T
   ZHOU J
   MA L
   JIAN S
AE SOUTHWEST RES & DESIGIN CHEM IND INST (CNCC-C)
GA 2022188512
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of carbon monoxide by reforming hydrocarbon carbon dioxide includes mixing hydrocarbon raw material gas with carbon dioxide gas for compression, preheating the compressed mixed gas in a preheater by using high-temperature reforming gas at the outlet of reforming reaction, purifying the preheated mixed gas in a desulfurizing device, passing the mixed gas after purifying through subsequent carbon dioxide (CO2) compressor and hydrogen (H2) compressor, pressurizing and mixing, where part of the high-temperature reformed gas at the outlet of the reforming device is guided to the inlet of the reforming device by utilizing the power energy of the gas, the part of the high-temperature reformed gas comes from the outlet of the reforming reactor or the outlet of the steam generator, and oxygen is introduced into the reforming device to carry out hydrocarbon carbon dioxide reforming reaction with the inlet gas of the reforming device.
   USE - The method is for preparation of carbon monoxide by reforming hydrocarbon carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method uses hydrocarbon carbon dioxide reforming process without using steam, saving steam consumption and obtaining high concentration carbon monoxide reforming gas, and fully recovers the carbon dioxide generated by the system and externally supplements carbon dioxide, thus realizing carbon emission reduction. The high temperature circulating gas is mixed with the raw material gas, reaching the reforming reaction requirement temperature. The system does not need external heating source, saving a lot of energy consumption. The method greatly reduces hydrocarbon consumption by adjusting the ratio of carbon dioxide in the feeding hydrocarbon and by circulating hydrogen, where compared with the prior art, the hydrocarbon consumption is reduced by 20%. The method cancels hydrocarbon steam conversion reactor, saves a lot of investment, saves the heating fuel needed by steam conversion reaction, realizes effect 3 negative carbon emission target, and adds carbon dioxide in the raw material gas, thus, reducing the consumption of hydrocarbon and improving the carbon monoxide yield of the product.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of carbon monoxide by reforming hydrocarbon carbon dioxide comprises mixing hydrocarbon raw material gas with carbon dioxide gas for compression, preheating the compressed mixed gas in a preheater by using high-temperature reforming gas at the outlet of reforming reaction, purifying the preheated mixed gas in a desulfurizing device, passing the mixed gas after purifying through subsequent carbon dioxide (CO2) compressor and hydrogen (H2) compressor, pressurizing and mixing, where part of the high-temperature reformed gas at the outlet of the reforming device is guided to the inlet of the reforming device by utilizing the power energy of the gas, the part of the high-temperature reformed gas comes from the outlet of the reforming reactor or the outlet of the steam generator, and oxygen is introduced into the reforming device to carry out hydrocarbon carbon dioxide reforming reaction with the inlet gas of the reforming device; using an injector drainage, returning the high temperature reforming gas part of the reforming reactor outlet to the first step as the circulating gas, preheating the raw material gas through preheater I and preheating the carbon dioxide and hydrogen mixed gas through preheater II, recycling low heat by decarburizing device I and decarburizing device II, cooling, and steam-water separating to obtain low temperature reforming gas; passing the high temperature reforming gas of the reforming reactor outlet through steam generator to produce by-product steam, draining the high-temperature reforming gas by an ejector and returning to the first step (1), and using the rest high-temperature reforming gas the same as the second step; entering the low temperature reforming gas obtained in second or third step in the decarburizing device I, mixing the carbon dioxide recovered in the low temperature reforming gas with the carbon dioxide recycled by the post-process decarburization device II and entering the CO2 compressor, where the decarbonization device preferably performs N-methyldiethanolamine (MDEA) decarburization, and the reboiler heat source in the decarburizing device is from the reformed gas low-position heat, and entering the decarburized reformed gas into pressure swing adsorption (PSA) purifying carbon monoxide device to obtain high-purity carbon monoxide product; purifying the reformed gas after carbon monoxide enters into the conversion device, carrying out conversion reaction for the carbon monoxide not separated by the PSA extraction carbon monoxide device in the fourth step, entering the converted gas in the decarburization device II, recycling the carbon dioxide and carbon dioxide mixed in the decarburization device I, entering carbon dioxide-removed synthesis gas into the PSA hydrogen extracting device, extracting residual synthesis gas hydrogen, and entering hydrogen into H2 compressor; and entering the carbon dioxide pressurized by CO2 compressor and hydrogen pressurized by H2 compressor into the preheater II, using high temperature reforming gas to exchange heat for the mixed gas, mixing with the raw material gas after desulfurization, and returning mixed gas to the injector, and recycling part of the high temperature circulating reforming gas to the inlet of the reforming device by the mixed aerodynamic force.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The hydrocarbon raw material gas is gaseous hydrocarbon or gaseous liquid hydrocarbon after heating at room temperature. The volume ratio of the hydrocarbon raw material gas and external carbon dioxide is 5:1. The hydrocarbon raw material gas is gaseous mixed hydrocarbon gas after naphtha is heated or ocean exploitation natural gas.

TI High-efficiency circulatory sugar dipping system for preserved fruit comprises raw liquid tank conveying sugar liquid to each of a sugar soaking tank and sugar liquid after recycling and processing by mixing area conveyed back to sugar dipping processing area through second transfusion pipeline.
AU LIN B
AE ANHUI XUEYU YAN ORCHARD FOOD CO LTD (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 2021C4786R
AB    NOVELTY - High-efficiency circulatory sugar dipping system for preserved fruit comprises a raw liquid tank, a sugar dipping processing area and a blending area. The sugar dipping processing area comprises multiple sugars soaking tank. The raw liquid tank (3) conveying the sugar liquid to each of a sugar soaking tank (1) through a first liquid infusion pipe (4). The sugar liquid after completing soaking in the sugar soaking tank is transported to a mixing area through a recovery pipe (5). The blending area comprises an enzymatic hydrolysis tank (6), a decolorization tank and a filter press which is connected in sequence. The sugar liquid after recycling and processing by the mixing area is conveyed back to the sugar dipping processing area through a second transfusion pipeline. The second transfusion pipeline is further provided with a sugar liquid tempering tank and each of the sugar soaking tank is externally connected with a vacuum pump and a carbon dioxide gas flushing system
   USE - Used as high-efficiency circulatory sugar dipping system for preserved fruit.
   ADVANTAGE - The system improves production efficiency; promotes the penetration of sugar solution; ensure that the preserved fruit is soaked in sugar evenly inside and outside and ensure that the pressure and carbon dioxide work together to reduce the browning rate and protects the color.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for efficient cyclic sugar dipping method for preserved fruit comprising (a) placing the cleaned preserved fruit in the sugar soaking tank, conveying the sugar liquid in the original liquid tank to the sugar soaking tank through a infusion circulating pump, controlling entire sugar dipping process at 25-55degrees Celsius through a temperature control system, (b) soaking the sugar for 0.5-1.5 hours under normal pressure, discharging the air in the sugar soaking tank by a vacuum pump, maintaining the vacuum environment in the sugar dipping tank at a vacuum degree of - 0.12 MPa to -0.09 MPa, (c) filling carbon dioxide gas into the sugar soaking tank by carbon dioxide punching system until the pressure in the tank is increased to 5-10 Mpa, maintaining the pressure for 0.3-1 hours, quickly releasing the pressure, releasing the carbon dioxide, soaking in sugar at normal pressure for 2-6 hours, filling carbon dioxide gas again, maintaining pressure for 0.5-1 hours, soaking the sugar for 2-6 hour at normal pressure after releasing pressure, finishing the sugar soaking treatment and (d) using the transfusion circulating pump to convey the sugar liquid to the blending area through the recycling pipeline after completing soaking, taking the preserved fruit soaked in sugar, sending to a drying system, recycling the sugar liquid through the blending area and sending the sugar liquid back to the sugar dipping processing area through a second transfusion pipeline.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the high-efficiency circulatory sugar dipping system for preserved fruit.
   Sugar soaking tank (1)
   Raw liquid tank (3)
   First liquid infusion pipe (4)
   Recovery pipe (5)

PN CN112226319-A
TI Preparing sparkling bayberry wine comprises selecting fully mature bayberry, removing rot, pests, and immature fruits and branches and leaves, and then cleaning through cleaning equipment to obtain raw bayberry.
AU WU S
AE WU S (WUSS-Individual)
GA 2021092330
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing sparkling bayberry wine comprises selecting fully mature bayberry, removing rot, pests, and immature fruits and branches and leaves, and then cleaning through cleaning equipment to obtain raw bayberry. The bayberry is put into the stainless steel tank, and slowly carbon dioxide gas is filled from the bottom of the tank, and after the air in the tank is exhausted and the carbon dioxide gas reaches saturation, adding of carbon dioxide gas and impregnating it for 10-15 days is stopped. The fruit is pulped, and potassium metabisulfite is added to obtain fruit pulp. The crushed and pitted fruit pulp is subjected to pressure filtration treatment, and 0.3 wt.% pectinase is added to obtain the original juice. The high temperature instantaneous sterilization at 95 degrees C for 30-60s is used, white granulated sugar is added to the original juice and the sugar content is adjusted to 18-20 wt.% to obtain the juice.
   USE - Method for preparing sparkling bayberry wine.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare sparkling bayberry wine, which is rich in a variety of amino acids and vitamins. The color is golden yellow, sweet and sour and refreshing, is mellow and elegant, the foam is white and delicate, and the liquid surface and foam continue to rise.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing sparkling bayberry wine comprises selecting fully mature bayberry, removing rot, pests, and immature fruits and branches and leaves, and then cleaning through cleaning equipment to obtain raw bayberry. The bayberry is put into the stainless steel tank, and slowly carbon dioxide gas is filled from the bottom of the tank, and after the air in the tank is exhausted and the carbon dioxide gas reaches saturation, adding of carbon dioxide gas and impregnating it for 10-15 days is stopped. The fruit is pulped, and potassium metabisulfite is added to obtain fruit pulp. The crushed and pitted fruit pulp is subjected to pressure filtration treatment, and 0.3 wt.% pectinase is added to obtain the original juice. The high temperature instantaneous sterilization at 95 degrees C for 30-60s is used, white granulated sugar is added to the original juice and the sugar content is adjusted to 18-20 wt.% to obtain the juice. The juice is pumped into the cleaned and disinfected fermentation tank, and the fruit wine yeast is added, the temperature is controlled at 16-18 degrees C, and the fermentation is 10-15 days to obtain the original juice wine. The original wine is put into a clean sparkling bottle, and then the pre-treated cork is pressed into the bottle mouth, and after the cork is pressed into the bottle mouth. The wine in the foaming bottle is pumped into the cleaned and disinfected fermentation tank, and the fruit wine yeast is added, the fermentation temperature is 15-16 degrees C, and the fermentation time is 30-45 days, and after the second fermentation is completed, bottling, and then seasoning is performed according to the type of wine and preset parameters. The cork is immediately pressed into the cork, and hooked, and then the wine bottle is placed in a flat and cool place for 15 days, and then quality inspection is carried out to obtain the finished sparkling bayberry wine. The sparkling bayberry wine is coded, labeled and boxed.

TI Carbon di-oxide and waste heat recovery system of proton exchange membrane fuel cell comprises hydrogen production system, separation system, power generation system, CO2 recovery system, and waste heat recovery system.
AU YANG Y
   ZHANG Y
   CHI J
AE SHANDONG YANHUANG IND DESIGN CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2020B53478
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon di-oxide and waste heat recovery system of proton exchange membrane fuel cell comprises a hydrogen production system, a separation system, a power generation system, a CO2 recovery system, and a waste heat recovery system. The hydrogen production system is connected to a separation system and a waste heat recovery system through pipelines, and the separation system is connected to a CO2 recovery system, a power generation system, and a waste heat recovery system through pipelines. The hydrogen production system consists of a burner (BUNNER1), an autothermal reformer (ATR), a high and low temperature CO shift reactor (HTS:LTS), a CO priority oxidation reactor (PROX), and a heat exchanger (EX1, EX2, EX3) composition. The purpose is to obtain hydrogen-rich gas from domestic liquefied petroleum gas through a series of reactions. The burner is connected with domestic liquefied petroleum gas (LPG) and air (AIR1) through the air inlet pipe.
   USE - Carbon di-oxide and waste heat recovery system of proton exchange membrane fuel cell.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon di-oxide and waste heat recovery system of proton exchange membrane fuel cell is used to increase the heat storage area of the water and phase change material in the water tank, and enhance the heat exchange effect, recovery of CO2 generated in the hydrogen production process and efficient use of waste heat generated in the PEMFC power generation process, improves energy efficiency of the fuel cell system by adopting CO2 recovery to prepare dry ice equipment. The CO2 generated in the hydrogen production process is effectively recovered and reused, and prepared dry ice that can be used for home and sale, and realize effective use of resources.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a carbon di-oxide recovery and waste heat recovery method for a proton exchange membrane fuel cell, which involves:
   (A) passing liquefied petroleum gas (LPG) and air into the burner (BUNNER) for combustion to obtain combustion gas;
   (B) passing the combustion gas and water obtained from S100 into an autothermal reformer (ATR) to convert LPG into hydrogen-rich synthesis gas;
   (C) passing hydrogen-rich synthesis gas through the high and low temperature CO shift reactor (HTS; LTS) and the CO priority oxidation reactor (PROX) to reduce the CO concentration to below 10 ppm;
   (D) producing mixed gas by the reaction and passing into the separation device to obtain dry hydrogen and dry carbon dioxide gas;
   (E) passing hydrogen into the PEMFC power generation device, where carbon dioxide gas is passed into the compressor (COMPRL1) and the heat exchanger ( EX6), and then pass the compressor (COMPRL2), heat exchanger (EX5), de-dryer (CLEANER), and dryer (DRYER) to obtain dry and pure carbon dioxide gas with a certain pressure;
   (F) passing the dry and pure carbon dioxide gas with a certain pressure into the heat exchanger (EX6) to obtain liquid carbon dioxide;
   (G) feeding liquid carbon dioxide into a dry ice machine (DIM) for decompression and temperature reduction; and
   (H) entering heated water in heat exchanger (EX7) through the phase change heat storage pipeline (PIPE).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The separation device is composed of a module containing NaOH solution, a concentrated sulfuric acid module for drying hydrogen, a module containing dilute hydrochloric acid, and a concentrated sulfuric acid module for drying carbon dioxide gas. The phase change heat storage pipeline (PIPE) in the waste heat recovery system adopts a phase change temperature of 50-60 degrees C paraffin material. Preferred Process: The phase change hot water storage tank in the waste heat recovery system adopts paraffin material with a phase change temperature of 50-60 degrees C. The paraffin material is encapsulated with a slender spiral thin-walled copper tube and placed horizontally in the heat preservation water tank, which has a built-in spring snap mechanism.

PN CN111333095-A
TI Preparing ultrafine calcium carbonate from            industrial alkali residue white mud involves preparing            first filter residue from alkali residue white mud,            industrial waste acid and electrolyte solution, and            performing supercritical treatment.
AU LI B
   CHEN Z
AE GUANGDONG YUFENG POWDER MATERIAL CO LTD (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 2020611679
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing ultrafine calcium carbonate from                industrial alkali residue white mud involves                controlling the water content of alkali residue                white mud at 30-40 wt.%, adding industrial waste                acid, adjusting pH to 8.5-9.0, adding the                electrolyte solution, stirring the contents                completely. The obtained product is allowed to                stand, separated, the filtrate is collected to                obtain the first filter residue. The filtrate is                placed into the autoclave, in the first                supercritical state, stirred at 32-35 degrees C for                2-3 hour. After introducing carbon dioxide, the                pressure is maintained in the kettle at 7.5-7.8                megaPascal for 2-3 hour. In the second                supercritical state, stirring is performed at 40-45                degrees C for 1 hour, carbon dioxide is passed to                maintain the pressure in the kettle at 8-10                megaPascal for 1-2 hour. In the third supercritical                state, heated and stirred at 45-50 degrees C for 1                hour, and carbon dioxide is passed to maintain the                pressure in the kettle at 10-11 megaPascal for                0.5-1 hour.
   USE - Method for preparing ultrafine calcium                carbonate from industrial alkali residue white                mud.
   ADVANTAGE - The method can effectively remove elements                such as chloride ions, sodium ions and the like in                the alkali residue white mud, improves the purity                of the calcium carbonate produced, that results in                preparing ultrafine calcium carbonate meeting                industry standards.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing ultrafine calcium carbonate from                industrial alkali residue white mud involves                controlling the water content of alkali residue                white mud at 30-40 wt.%, adding industrial waste                acid, adjusting pH to 8.5-9.0, adding the                electrolyte solution, stirring the contents                completely. The obtained product is allowed to                stand, separated, the filtrate is collected to                obtain the first filter residue. The filtrate is                placed into the autoclave, in the first                supercritical state, stirred at 32-35 degrees C for                2-3 hour. After introducing carbon dioxide, the                pressure is maintained in the kettle at 7.5-7.8                megaPascal for 2-3 hour. In the second                supercritical state, stirring is performed at 40-45                degrees C for 1 hour, carbon dioxide is passed to                maintain the pressure in the kettle at 8-10                megaPascal for 1-2 hour. In the third supercritical                state, heated and stirred at 45-50 degrees C for 1                hour, and carbon dioxide is passed to maintain the                pressure in the kettle at 10-11 megaPascal for                0.5-1 hour. Flocculant is added to the obtained                product, stirred, allowed to stand for separation,                the filtrate is collected to obtain the second                filter residue. The first filter residue and the                second filter residue are combined, 10 wt.% water,                dispersant and brightener are added, heated up to                50-60 degrees C for heat preservation, filtered,                and 5 wt.% water is added for pulping. The obtained                product is filtered, and the pulping operation is                performed 3-5 times to obtain pulping filter                residue. The filter residue is pulped, dried to                obtain powder. The powder is superfinely                pulverized, barreled, and packaged to obtain                superfine calcium carbonate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The waste acid is                  selected from waste hydrochloric acid, waste                  nitric acid or waste sulfuric acid. The                  dispersant is sodium polyacrylate or disodium                  ethylenediaminetetraacetic acid. The whitening                  agent is stilbene. The flocculant is                  polyacrylamide. Preferred Compositions: The                  electrolyte solution is a 10 wt.% calcium acetate                  solution. The added amount of the calcium acetate                  solution is 0.01-0.1 wt.% of the weight of the                  alkali residue white mud. Preferred Conditions:                  The drying is carried out, and the moisture                  content is less than or equal to 0.5%. The                  average particle diameter of ultrafine calcium                  carbonate is 0.03 mu m not less than d greater                  than or equal to 0.7 mu m. The heat preservation                  time is 2-3 hour.

   GASCONTEC GMBH (GASC-Non-standard)
GA 201840123K
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves feeding a                carbon-containing energy carrier stream (3) and an                oxygen stream (4) to a synthesis gas reactor                arrangement (6) for obtaining a synthesis gas                stream (7) with hydrogen and carbon oxides. A                hydrogen-containing stream (10) is obtained and fed                to an ammonia reactor arrangement (11) and is                partially converted into ammonia (2). The molar                fraction of hydrogen of the synthesis gas stream                obtained from the synthesis gas reactor arrangement                is increased compared to the molar fraction of                carbon oxides before being fed to a methanol                reactor arrangement (8).
   USE - Method for combined production of methanol and                ammonia.
   ADVANTAGE - The very complex and expensive steam reforming                step required to provide hydrogen in the synthesis                gas stream for methanol synthesis and ammonia                synthesis is eliminated. The consumption of                hydrogen in the synthesis of methanol is reduced by                carbon dioxide. The need for a recycle compressor                in the synthesis of methanol is eliminated. The                economy of methanol synthesis can be                optimized.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The carbon dioxide is partially removed from                the synthesis gas stream. The synthesis gas stream                supplied to the methanol reactor arrangement is                provided with a molar fraction of carbon dioxide of                up to 3%. A partial conversion stream (13) of the                synthesis gas stream of a shift conversion (14) is                supplied, in which carbon monoxide at least                partially by reaction with water vapor to hydrogen                and carbon dioxide is preferably. The shift                conversion supplied partial stream is fed to a                carbon dioxide scrubbing (12). The high-temperature                water gas shift reaction is carried out by the                shift conversion. The molar fraction of the                particular conversion stream supplied to the shift                conversion at the synthesis gas stream for                adjusting the ratio of a methanol (1) obtained by                conversion and the ammonia is obtained by                conversion. The supplied synthesis gas stream is                completely converted into methanol, so that a molar                proportion of carbon monoxide in a residual gas                stream (9) is 4%. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included                for a plant for combined production of methanol and                ammonia has an oxygen recovery arrangement (5) that                is provided for the oxygen stream. The synthesis                gas reactor arrangement is provided for obtaining                the synthesis gas stream with hydrogen and carbon                oxides from a carbon-containing energy carrier                stream and the oxygen stream. The methanol reactor                arrangement is provided to obtain a residual gas                stream. The molar fraction of hydrogen of the                synthesis gas stream obtained from the synthesis                gas reactor arrangement is increased in relation to                the molar proportion of carbon oxides in an                enrichment arrangement (30), before fed to the                methanol reactor arrangement.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow diagram illustrating                the method for combined production of methanol and                ammonia.

PT P
PN JP2018003275-A; JP6694632-B2
TI Geothermal utilization system has aeration tank            which is configured to aerate geothermal water flowing            from aeration water separation tank through check valve            to degas carbon dioxide in geothermal water.
AU MORITA M
AE UNIV TOKYO (UYTY-C)
GA 201803202Y
AB    NOVELTY - The system (1) separates the geothermal fluid                flowing out from the outlet (11a) of the geothermal                fluid output pipe (11) is separated into geothermal                water and geothermal steam. A steam-water                separating tank (2) is configured to maintain the                carbon dioxide partial pressure of the geothermal                steam above the degassing suppression partial                pressure value. An aeration tank (3) is configured                to aerate the geothermal water flowing from the                aeration water separation tank through the check                valve to degas carbon dioxide in the geothermal                water.
   USE - Geothermal utilization system.
   ADVANTAGE - The geothermal water is made acidic by keeping                the carbon dioxide partial pressure of the                geothermal steam above the degassing suppression                partial pressure value in the steam/water                separating tank and the precipitation of calcium                carbonate and magnesium silicate is suppressed.                Thus, the scale is prevented from adhering to the                geothermal fluid takeout pipe. The geothermal water                is made aerobic to deaerate carbon dioxide, thus                the geothermal water is made alkaline and calcium                carbonate and magnesium silicate are forcibly                precipitated. The precipitation of scale of calcium                carbonate and magnesium silicate in external                equipment such as hot water transport pipe and heat                exchanger is suppressed. The maintenance cycle of                piping is greatly prolonged.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A hot water transporting pipe (12) is                configured to transport the geothermal water stored                in the aeration tank to an external facility. The                temperature of the geothermal water flowing in the                hot water transporting pipe is maintained at a                temperature at which silicon oxide is not                precipitated. A check valve is configured to                prevent reverse flow of the geothermal steam                exhausted from the steam/water separating tank and                maintain the carbon dioxide partial pressure of the                geothermal steam in the steam/water separating tank                at a constant value. A steam exhaust pipe (14) is                provided with the check valve and is configured to                exhaust geothermal steam of the steam/water                separation tank. The outflow port of the geothermal                fluid takeoff pipe is arranged in a gas phase of                the steam-water separation tank. A rapid                depressurization prevention unit (25) for                preventing decrease in the pressure of the                geothermal fluid is provided at the outflow port of                the geothermal fluid takeoff pipe. The outflow port                of the geothermal fluid output pipe is placed in a                liquid phase of the steam-water separation tank.                The gas-water separation tank and the aeration tank                are connected to each other through connecting                pipes (13) so as to connect the liquid phases                through a connecting pipe provided with the check                valve. The calcium ions dissolved in the geothermal                water are precipitated as insoluble calcium                carbonate and magnesium ions dissolved in the                geothermal water are precipitated as insoluble                magnesium silicate in the aeration tank.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                geothermal utilization system. (Drawing includes                non-English language text)
   Geothermal utilization system (1)
   Steam-water separating tank (2)
   Aeration tank (3)
   Geothermal fluid output pipe (11)
   Outlet of geothermal fluid output pipe                (11a)
   Hot water transporting pipe (12)
   Connecting pipe (13)
   Steam exhaust pipe (14)
   Rapid depressurization prevention unit                (25)
DC D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); E33 (Compounds of Be, Mg, Ca, Sr, Ba, Ra, Sc, Y, La, Ac, Al, lanthanides (Rare-earths), Th.); Q49 (Mining (E21)); X15 (Non-Fossil Fuel Power Generating Systems)

PT P
PN CN113622993-A; CN113622993-B
TI Environmentally-friendly protective coal mining            method for mineralization and utilization of carbon            dioxide waste gas involves constructing airtight walls            at both ends of mining space to seal off mining area,            and e.g. pouring carbon dioxide mineralized filling            material into closed area.
AU WANG Y
   DI J
   XU Y
   WU Y
   MA L
AE UNIV CHINA MINING & TECHNOLOGY BEIJING (UYMB-C)
GA 2021D0178H
AB    NOVELTY - Environmentally-friendly protective coal                mining method for mineralization and utilization of                carbon dioxide waste gas involves constructing                airtight walls at both ends of the mining space to                seal off the mining area as work progresses,                pouring carbon dioxide mineralized filling material                (CFBF) into closed area, mining the goaf and                filling, using the critical key parameters of the                strength of the filling component required to                ensure the stability, obtaining fly ash and coal                gangue produced in the mining process on the spot                during deployment, crushing gangue to get gangue                powder, mixing and stirring gangue powder, fly ash                and cement to obtain a solid mixture, using                alkaline silicon-based additive and water to                prepare an aqueous solution of the alkaline                silicon-based additive, feeding the aqueous                solution into the solid mixture and the carbon                dioxide recovered from the industrial waste gas,                feeding carbon dioxide, and obtaining carbon                dioxide mineralized filling material CFBF.
   USE - Environmentally-friendly protective coal                mining method for mineralization and utilization of                carbon dioxide waste gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple and reliable results, and                improves the recovery rate of coal resources on the                premise of realizing carbon dioxide mineralization                and solid waste material treatment. The CFBF is                prepared from mining gangue, fly ash and mine                sewage, supplemented by cement and alkaline                silicon-based additives, which ensures the                formation of the seepage isolation zone of the                mining overlying rock, can carry out continuous                CFBF filling, and realizes the replacement of                carbon dioxide and coal.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Environmentally-friendly protective coal                mining method for mineralization and utilization of                carbon dioxide waste gas involves constructing                airtight walls at both ends of the mining space to                seal off the mining area as work progresses,                pouring CFBF into closed area, mining the goaf and                filling, using the critical key parameters of the                strength of the filling component required to                ensure the stability of the overburden seepage                isolation zone in the CFBF filling stope to ensure                the support of the goaf after the filling of CFBF                solidifies, obtaining fly ash and coal gangue                produced in the mining process on the spot during                deployment, crushing gangue to get gangue powder,                mixing and stirring gangue powder, fly ash and                cement with particle size less than 5 mm to obtain                a solid mixture, using alkaline silicon-based                additive and water to prepare an aqueous solution                of the alkaline silicon-based additive, feeding the                aqueous solution into the solid mixture and the                carbon dioxide recovered from the industrial waste                gas through while stirring, feeding carbon dioxide                into the mixture of aqueous solution and solid per                cubic meter with 5000-50000 L/hour for 60 minutes,                and obtaining carbon dioxide mineralized filling                material CFBF. In the working face, CFBF is used                for continuous mining and continuous filling until                all the mining and filling of the working face are                completed. The carbon dioxide mineralization                filling material CFBF comprises 36-48 %mass fly                ash, 12-24 %mass coal gangue, 5-10 %mass cement,                3-5 %mass alkaline silicon-based additives, and                13-44 %mass mine water.

PN CN113604273-A
TI Supercritical carbon dioxide segmented extraction process of Zanthoxylum bungeanum oleoresin, comprises extracting Zanthoxylum bungeanum powder, using peel, realizing separation, realizing solid-liquid separation, and centrifuging.
AU YANG J
   YANG H
   HUANG T
   MI T
   LU L
   WANG Q
AE GUIZHOU XUANDE BIO-TECHNOLOGY CORP LTD (GUIZ-Non-standard)
GA 2021C9962D
AB    NOVELTY - Supercritical carbon dioxide segmented extraction process of Zanthoxylum bungeanum oleoresin, comprises (i) extracting the Zanthoxylum bungeanum powder, using peel and stems of Zanthoxylum bungeanum, and crushing and liquefaction, (ii) introducing zanthoxylum bungeanum powder into a supercritical extraction tank, and passing carbon dioxide, (iii) introdcing pre-extracted liquid the separation tank, and heating, (iv) introduicng preliminary separation separated in the early separation in the extraction tank again, (vi) realizing separation and purification of Zanthoxylum oleoresin again, using membrane-molecular integrated distillation equipment, (vii) contacting and fusing separation liquid with carboxymethyl cellulose, realizing solid-liquid separation again, removing the peculiar smell, 9(viii) centrifuging the separated liquid to remove water to obtain finished product of Zanthoxylum bungeanum oleoresin is obtained.
   USE - Used as supercritical carbon dioxide segmented extraction process of Zanthoxylum bungeanum oleoresin.
   ADVANTAGE - The supercritical carbon dioxide segmented extraction combined with film-molecular distillation integrated separating and purifying new technique of the invention can improve the pepper oil bitter taste, bad organoleptic and improving the quality of pepper oil.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Supercritical carbon dioxide segmented extraction process of Zanthoxylum bungeanum oleoresin, comprises (i) extracting the Zanthoxylum bungeanum powder, using peel and stems of Zanthoxylum bungeanum, and crushing to 70-90 mesh, preparing gaseous crbon dioxide in carbon dioxide, carbon dioxide storage tank for refrigeration and liquefaction, (ii) introducing zanthoxylum bungeanum powder into a supercritical extraction tank, and passing carbon dioxide for supercritical extraction, controlling pressure to 18 Mpa, extraction temperature is set to 50degreesC to obtain the preliminary extraction liquid, (iii) introdcing pre-extracted liquid the separation tank, heating to 55degrees Celsius, and the pressure drops less than 6.5 Mpa, where carbon dioxide flow rate is greater than or equal to 140 m3/hours, and process product is the pepper volatile oil, preliminary separation and gaseous CO2, the volatile oil of Zanthoxylum bungeanum was centrifuged to remove water to obtain the finished product of Zanthoxylum bungeanum volatile oil. The gaseous carbon dioxide, passing through the cold heat exchanger to become a liquid cycle, (iv) ntroduicng preliminary separation separated in the early separation in the extraction tank again, increase the pressure to 25 Mpa, dropping temperature to 50degrees Celsius, and passing carbon dioxide for supercritical extraction. After the slag is discharged, the latter extraction is obtained, (v) late extract enters the separation tank, the pressure drop is less than 6.5Mpa, temperature rises to 55degrees Celsius, the carbon dioxide flow rate is greater than or equal to 140 m3/hours, and the separated liquid and gaseous carbon dioxide are obtained after separation, where gaseous carbon dioxide is recycled into a liquid state through a cold heat exchanger, (vi) realizing separation and purification of Zanthoxylum oleoresin again, using membrane-molecular integrated distillation equipment, which includes membrane processing system and molecular distillation system, carrying out oil-water separation through the molecular distillation system, (vii) contacting and fusing separation liquid with carboxymethyl cellulose, realizing solid-liquid separation again, removing the peculiar smell, 9(viii) centrifuging the separated liquid to remove water to obtain finished product of Zanthoxylum bungeanum oleoresin is obtained.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The pre-extraction time is 30 minutes, and later extraction time is 60 minutes. The pre-separating time is 30 minutes, the later separating time is 60 minutes, and reseparating time of the purifying core technology is 60 minutes. The separating liquid and carboxymethyl cellulose contact the fusion time is 5 hours. The molecular distillation system uses a rotary scraper film evaporator; the separating liquid flows through the heating pipe wall in film shape, to perform heat transfer and evaporation of water vapor.

PN CN110591781-A
TI Coke oven gas adjustable decarbonization system for producing liquefied natural gas has filter, carbon removing system, heating furnace, methanation reaction tower, cooling towers, rectifying tower, and liquefied natural gas storage tank.
AU LI D
   HU X
   GAO Z
   XU G
   REN J
   LI X
AE INNER MONGOLIA HENGKUN CHEM CO LTD (SNJT-C)
GA 2019A75679
AB    NOVELTY - A coke oven gas adjustable decarbonization system comprises filter (1), carbon removing system (2), heating furnace (3), methanation reaction tower (4), 1st cooling tower (5), rectifying tower (6), 2nd cooling tower (7), and liquefied natural gas (LNG) storage tank (8). Carbon removing system comprises coke oven gas absorption tower for absorbing carbon dioxide flowing direction, flash evaporation tower for liquid coarse separation, gas heat exchanger to separate waste gas and coke oven gas, analyzing tower for cooling carbon dioxide gas of carbon dioxide cooling tower, carbon dioxide separator for separating carbon dioxide gas, suction pump for coke oven gas, and desulfurizing tower for removing sulfide, where desulfurizing tower is connected with heating furnace.
   USE - A coke oven gas adjustable decarbonization system for producing liquefied natural gas (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - Carbon dioxide content in the raw gas of the coke oven gas can be controlled, improving the production quality of the liquefied natural gas, and also avoids the blockage of the cold box caused by the condensation of the carbon dioxide gas, ensuring the safe operation of the production system.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for producing LNG by adjusting the decarburized coke oven gas which involves passing raw material gas from coke oven through a filter to remove solid impurities, entering filtered raw material gas into heating furnace for preheating, entering preheated raw material gas through methanation reaction tower for methanation reaction to transform raw gas into mixed gas with methane as main component, pre-cooling mixed gas by 1st cooling tower, entering mixed gas into rectification column for distillation to distill out nitrogen and hydrogen, passing through top of rectification column, discharging and obtaining liquid methane at the bottom of the tower, re-cooling and decompressing liquid methane in 2nd cooling tower, storing in a normal-pressure LNG storage tank to complete the production of liquefied natural gas, adjusting or reducing carbon dioxide content, opening 2 bypass stop valves of decarbonization system and closing process tube between filter and heating furnace, entering filtered raw gas into absorption tower, discharging remaining waste gas from top of absorption tower, entering effective mixed gas into flash tower for flash evaporation, so that effective gas is separated out, entering into heat exchanger, entering heat-exchanged gas into analysis tower, discharging effective gas of raw gas analyzed by analysis tower from the top of analysis tower and entering carbon dioxide cooling tower for cooling, where carbon dioxide absorbed by absorption tower becomes a liquid and enters carbon dioxide separator together with raw material gas for separation, depositing liquid carbon dioxide at the bottom of the carbon dioxide separator and entering underground storage tank, entering raw material gas for carbon dioxide separation again, entering desulfurization tower through suction pump to remove sulfide, where raw material gas becomes a qualified raw material gas for removing carbon dioxide gas, and entering into heating furnace to produce LNG.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a process flow diagram of the coke oven gas adjustable decarbonization system for producing liquefied natural gas.
   Filter (1)
   Carbon removing system (2)
   Heating furnace (3)
   Methanation reaction tower (4)
   1st cooling tower (5)
   Rectifying tower (6)
   2nd cooling tower (7)
   LNG storage tank (8)

   USE - Generating near-zero carbon emission                integrated coal gasification combined power is                useful for peak shaving (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Generating near-zero carbon emission                integrated coal gasification combined power                comprises allowing 0.4-0.8 MPa pressurized air to                enter air separation device for air separation to                obtain liquid oxygen and liquid nitrogen and                pumping pressurized liquid oxygen as coolant for                heat exchange vaporization and power generation by                using steam for power generation, storing liquid                nitrogen and part of liquid oxygen at night to use                for peak period during daytime, allowing coal                powder and high-pressure oxygen transported by                pressure to enter coal gasifier to generate                gasification reaction, metals to                desulfurization, dechlorination and degreasing,                placing in power generation subsystem, generating                electricity by high temperature supercritical                carbon dioxide using turbine, allowing                high-pressure purification gas, high-pressure                oxygen and high-pressure circulating carbon dioxide                to enter gas turbine combustion expansion to push                compressor to compress air to 0.4-0.8 MPa and                generator to rotate at high speed to generate                electricity, subjecting high-temperature combustion                flue gas supercritical carbon dioxide power                generated by supercritical carbon dioxide waste                heat boiler heat exchange, cooling using                pressurized liquid oxygen or liquid nitrogen as                coolant, circulating supercritical carbon dioxide                is through carbon dioxide compressor, exchanging                flue gas heat with pressurized liquid nitrogen,                making liquid nitrogen vaporization to drive                nitrogen turbine generators to generate                electricity, dehydrating staged cooled flue gas,                separating and recovering liquid flue gas by                distillation tower to recover carbon dioxide, of supercritical carbon dioxide power                generation as partially sold as liquid carbon                dioxide product, pumping and pressurizing liquid                nitrogen stored at night and separated during day                during peak period of grid power consumption during                day, generating electricity using heat exchange and                generating peaking TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The composition is a                  powder composition. The composition comprises all                  components (a) to (h) are in solid form,                  preferably powder form. The carbon                  dioxide-generating compound is carbonate or                  bicarbonate salts of alkali or alkaline-earth                  metals, preferably sodium, potassium carbonate,                  magnesium carbonate, calcium carbonate, sodium                  bicarbonate, potassium bicarbonate, magnesium                  bicarbonate, and/or calcium bicarbonate.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The organic acids are                  carboxylic acids, preferably 1-8C atoms,                  preferably propanoic acid, butanoic acid, acetic                  acid, lactic acid, citric acid, oxalic acid,                  malonic acid, succinic acid, fumaric acid,                  glutaric acid, tartaric acid, adipic acid, maleic                  acid, glycolic acid, salicylic acid, malic acid,                  and mineral acids including hydrochloric acid,                  sulfuric acid, nitric acid and acid phosphoric,                  preferably hydrochloric acid and phosphoric acid.                  The chemical oxidizing agent comprises (i)                  peroxygenated salts, preferably persulfates,                  perborates, peracids, and their precursors,                  alkali or alkaline earth metal percarbonates,                  e.g. sodium carbonate peroxide, also called                  sodium percarbonate; (ii) alkali metal bromates                  or ferricyanides, (iii) solid chemical oxidizing                  agents which generate hydrogen peroxide, e.g.                  urea peroxide and polymer complexes which can                  release hydrogen peroxide, preferably                  polyvinylpyrrolidone/hydrogen peroxide (H2O2);                  (iv) oxidases producing hydrogen peroxide in the                  presence of a suitable substrate. The oxidizing                  dyeing agent comprises oxidation bases and                  couplers, preferably oxidation bases comprising                  para-phenylenediamines, bis(phenyl)                  alkylenediamines, para-aminophenols,                  orthoaminophenols, heterocyclic bases, their                  organic or mineral acid salts, their optical or                  geometric isomers, tautomers and/or solvates e.g.                  hydrates, and couplers including                  meta-aminophenols, meta-phenylenediamines, and                  meta-diphenols, naphthols, mono- or                  polyhydroxylated naphthalene derivatives, and                  heterocyclic coupler their organic acid salts or                  inorganic, their optical or geometric isomers,                  tautomers and solvates e.g. hydrates, preferably                  oxidation bases including p-phenylenediamines,                  p-aminophenols, and heterocyclic bases, couplers                  including meta-phenylenediamines, meta-diphenols,                  and meta-aminophenols, their organic acid salts                  or inorganic compounds, their optical or                  geometric isomers, their tautomers, and their                  solvates e.g. hydrates. The composition comprises                  more than one basifying agent(s) including                  silicates and metasilicates, e.g. alkali metal                  metasilicates, and/or ammonium salts. The                  composition is anhydrous.
   EXAMPLE - Typical composition comprised of 30 pts. wt.                sodium percarbonate, 20 pts. wt. ammonium chloride,                10 pts. wt. cellulose gum, 5 pts. wt. sodium lauryl                sulfate, 2 pts. wt. dimethicone, 9.5 pts. wt.                sodium bicarbonate, 21 pts. wt. citric acid, 1 pts.                wt. para phenylenediamine, 1.3 pts. wt. para-amino                ortho cresol, and 0.2 pts. wt. para                aminophenol.

PT P
PN WO2022018594-A1
TI Preparing dichloro(methoxy(oxetanyl)methyl)benzoic acid used to prepare dihydroisoquinolinones, comprises treating bromodichlorophenyl(methoxy)methyloxetane with bromine-magnesium exchange reagent and reacting resultant with carbon dioxide.
AU COLLINS M R
   CRITCHER D J
   DESROSIERS J
   KARMILOWICZ M J
   MARTINEZ C A
   RAGAN J A
   SUTTON S C
AE PFIZER INC (PFIZ-C)
GA 202216430R
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing (R)-2,5-dichloro-3-(methoxy(oxetan-3-yl)methyl)benzoic acid (A1) comprises treating (R)-3-((3-bromo-2,5-dichlorophenyl)(methoxy)methyl)oxetane (H1) with a bromine-magnesium exchange reagent and reacting the resulting arylmagnesium species with carbon dioxide.
   USE - The process is useful for preparing (A1) (claimed), which is used to prepare (R)-5,8-dichloro-7-(methoxy(oxetan-3-yl)methyl)-2-((4-methoxy-6-methyl-2-oxo-1,2-dihydropyridin-3-yl)methyl)-3,4-dihydroisoquinolin-1(2H)-one which are EZH2 inhibitors.
   ADVANTAGE - The process is simple and provides the compounds with higher enantiomeric excess.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing (R)-2,5-dichloro-3-(methoxy(oxetan-3-yl)methyl)benzoic acid of formula (A1) comprises treating (R)-3-((3-bromo-2,5-dichlorophenyl)(methoxy)methyl)oxetane of formula (H1) with a bromine-magnesium exchange reagent and reacting the resulting arylmagnesium species with carbon dioxide. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a compound comprising ((3-bromo-2,5-dichlorophenyl)-oxetan-3-yl) methyl ketone, (R)-(3-bromo-2,5-dichlorophenyl)(oxetan-3-yl)methanol, (R)-3-((3-bromo-2,5-dichlorophenyl)(methoxy)methyl)oxetane, or (R)-2,5-dichloro-3-(methoxy(oxetan-3-yl)methyl) benzoic acid or a its salt.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The base is potassium tert-butoxide, or potassium carbonate. The solvent is dimethyl sulfoxide. The alkylating agent is 3-iodooxetane.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The process further comprises: preparing (H1) by treating (R)-(3-bromo-2,5-dichlorophenyl)(oxetan-3-yl)methanol (G1) with a base and a methylating agent; preparing (G1) by treating (3-bromo-2,5-dichlorophenyl)(oxetan-3-yl)methanone (F1) with an enantioselective ketoreductase enzyme; preparing (F1) by treating 2-(3-bromo-2,5-dichlorophenyl)-2-(oxetan-3-yl)acetonitrile (E1) with oxygen gas in the presence of a base in a polar, aprotic solvent; preparing (E1) by treating 2-(3-bromo-2,5-dichlorophenyl)acetonitrile (D1) with an alkylating agent and a base in a polar, aprotic solvent; preparing (D1) by treating tert-butyl 2-(3-bromo-2,5-dichlorophenyl)-2-cyanoacetate (C1) with water and a polar, aprotic solvent at a temperature above 50 degrees C i.e. decarboxylation occurs; preparing (C1) by treating 1-bromo-2,5-dichloro-3-fluorobenzene (B1) with a malononitrile reagent and a base in a polar, aprotic solvent; preparing (F1) by (a) treating diethyl ((3-bromo-2,5-dichlorophenyl)(1-ethoxyethoxy)methyl)phosphonate (L1) with a base and then 3-oxetanone to provide 3-((3-bromo-2,5-dichlorophenyl)(1-ethoxyethoxy)methylene)oxetane (M1) and (b) treating (M1) with an acid; preparing (L1) by treating diethyl ((3-bromo-2,5-dichlorophenyl)(hydroxy)methyl)phosphonate (K1) with ethoxy vinyl ether and an acid; preparing (K1) by treating 3-bromo-2,5-dichlorobenzaldehyde (J1) with diethyl phosphite; and preparing (J1) by treating 1,3-dibromo-2,5-dichlorobenzene (I1) with a bromine-magnesium exchange reagent and reacting the resulting arylmagnesium species with dimethylformamide. The temperature is 85-90 degrees C. Preferred Components: The bromine-magnesium exchange reagent is an isopropyl magnesium chloride-lithium chloride complex. The methylating agent is dimethyl sulfate. The solvent comprises dioxane, dimethyl sulfoxide or sulfolane. The malononitrile reagent is tert-butyl malononitrile. The base comprises 1,8-diazabicyclo(5.4.0)undec-7-ene, cesium carbonate, or potassium carbonate. The solvent is dimethyl sulfoxide or sulfolane. The base is lithium hexamethyldisilazide, lithium diisopropylamide or potassium tert-butoxide. The acid is acetic acid, or pyridinium 4-toluenesulfonate. The compound (R)-2,5-dichloro-3-(methoxy(oxetan-3-yl)methyl) benzoic acid has a 99% enantiomeric excess (ee).
   EXAMPLE - 1.5 g (R)-2,5-Dichloro-3-(methoxy(oxetan-3-yl)methyl) benzoic acid was combined with 23 ml tetrahydrofuran and cooled to -15 to -20 degrees C. A solution of 4.5 ml isopropylmagnesium chloride-lithium chloride complex was added over 10-15 minutes, at which addition rate, the reaction temperature remained below -10 degrees C. The resulting mixture was stirred at -15 to -20 degrees C. Carbon dioxide gas was then bubbled through the mixture for 2-5 minutes, during which time the temperature rose from -5 to 0 degrees C. The mixture was then warmed to ambient temperature and then held for 30 minutes. The reaction mixture was then sparged with nitrogen for 10-15 minutes, followed by addition of 10 ml 0.4 N aqueous sodium hydroxide. The pH of the mixture was adjusted from an initial value of 8 by addition of 1 N aqueous sodium hydroxide, to a pH of 10. The mixture was washed with 7.5 ml toluene. The organic layer was discarded and the aqueous layer was acidified to a pH of 2 by addition of cone. The resulting organic phase was solvent swapped to methanol by distillation to a final volume of 4.5 ml. While maintaining this solution at 50 degrees C, 4.5 ml water was slowly added, and the mixture was seeded. The resulting slurry was cooled to ambient temperature over 60 minutes. An additional portion of 3 ml water was added, and the solids were collected by filtration, rinsing the filter cake with 4.5 ml water to obtain 1.19 g (R)-2,5-dichloro-3-(methoxy(oxetan-3-yl)methyl)benzoic acid.
DC B02 (Fused ring heterocyclics.); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.)
MC B04-E99; B04-L03D; B07-A03; B11-A02A4; D05-A02A; D05-C; D05-H99
IP C07D-305/06

PN CN112827493-A; CN112827493-B
TI Preparing monoatomic metal catalyst used in e.g. carbon dioxide photocatalytic reduction, by mixing alkoxysilane and metal salt to perform hydrolysis reaction to prepare hydrolyzed gel and subjecting to solid-phase redox reaction.
AU YANG Z
   CHEN H
   XIONG Y
   SU C
AE UNIV SUN YAT-SEN (UYSY-C)
GA 202159747M
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing monoatomic metal catalyst involves (i) uniformly mixing the alkoxysilane and metal salt solution to perform a hydrolysis reaction to prepare hydrolyzed gel with dispersed metal ions and (ii) subjecting the hydrolyzed gel to solid-phase redox reaction to prepare a monoatomic metal catalyst.
   USE - Method for preparing monoatomic metal catalyst is used in carbon dioxide photocatalytic reduction, photocatalytic degradation, photocatalytic hydrogen production, conversion of carbon dioxide into multi-carbon hydrocarbons and organic catalytic reactions (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method realizes the process of co-crystal growth of target metal atoms and substrate materials from co-crystal growth to thermal cracking to crystal re-growth through fine solid chemical synthesis and regulation, does not only occur on the surface of the catalyst, provides an effective reaction place, has a simple synthesis method, high yield and stability, no additional reagents and auxiliary materials need to be added, and has huge application potential in the single-atom catalysis.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a monoatomic metal catalyst prepared using above-mentioned method, where the its morphology is spherical, rod-shaped, plate-shaped, dish-shaped or cone-shaped, and its particle size is 1 nm-100 mu m.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method involves performing the step (i) under a protective gas atmosphere and in response to external stimuli, where the protective gas comprises nitrogen, argon, hydrogen, oxygen and/or carbon dioxide. The step (ii) involves performing the reaction in presence of external stimuli comprising light stimulation, temperature stimulation, acid-base stimulation, chemical or catalytic stimulation, electric field stimulation, magnetic field stimulation, ultrasound stimulation, and biological functional molecule stimulation, where the external stimulus response is light stimulation, the wavelength of the light source used for the light stimulation is 350-650 nm, and the power is 1-100 W, when the external stimulus response is a temperature stimulus, the temperature of the temperature stimulus is 25-900 degrees C, when the external stimulus is acid-base stimulation, the pH of the acid-base stimulation is 0.1-4 or 10-13, and when the external stimulus is chemical initiation or catalytic stimulus, the initiator or catalyst comprises azobisisoheptonitrile, azobisisobutyronitrile, benzoyl peroxide, cyclohexanone peroxide, tert-butyl hydroperoxide, palladium and/or rhodium. The step (i) involves mixing alkoxysilane, metal salt solution, water and alcohol, stirring, and preparing hydrolyzed gel with uniformly dispersed metal ions through a hydrolysis reaction. Preferred Components: In step (ii), the reducing agent used in the solid-phase reduction reaction comprises hydrogen or metal. The metals in the metal salt solution comprises iron, cobalt, nickel, copper, gold, silver, tin, lead, germanium, platinum and/or molybdenum. Preferred Composition: The volume ratio of silane compound, alcohol and water is (1-30):(1-30):(1-30), and the concentration of metal salt solution is 0.1-2 mg/ml.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The alkoxysilane comprises tetramethoxysilane, tetraethoxysilane, trimethoxysilane, triethoxysilane, methyltrimethoxysilane and/or ethyltrimethoxysilane.

PN CN112675811-A; CN112675811-B
TI Silver-exchanged molecular sieve adsorbent for separating mixture of nitrous oxide and carbon dioxide, is obtained by exchanging molecular sieve having preset silicon-aluminum ratio to obtain sieve having preset silver ion exchange degree.
AU YANG J
   WANG L
   LIU J
   LI J
AE UNIV TAIYUAN TECHNOLOGY (UYTL-C)
GA 202145617H
AB    NOVELTY - A silver-exchanged molecular sieve adsorbent is obtained by exchanging molecular sieve having silicon-aluminum ratio not more than 1.2 with silver to obtain a silver-exchanged molecular sieve having silver ion (Ag+) exchange degree of not less than 66%.
   USE - Silver-exchanged molecular sieve adsorbent for separating mixture of nitrous oxide and carbon dioxide in nitric acid or adipic acid tail gas (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The adsorbent efficiently separates nitrous oxide and carbon dioxide, and has excellent nitrous oxide preferential adsorption capacity, high selectivity and adsorption capacity since balance cation is changed from alkali metal or alkaline earth metal to silver ion.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of the adsorbent, which involves adding the molecular sieve having silicon-aluminum ratio of not more than 1.2 into an aqueous silver nitrate solution, heating and stirring, performing silver exchange, washing by deionized water, filtering, drying to obtain silver-exchanged molecular sieve having silver ion exchange degree of not less than 66%, and vacuumizing and activating to 150-200 degrees C. The molecular sieve is 13X molecular sieve or 5A molecular sieve.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Properties: The 13X molecular sieve has a silicon-aluminum ratio of 1.15, and obtains a silver-exchanged 13X molecular sieve adsorbent having a silver ion exchange degree of 84% or more through silver exchange. The 5A molecular sieve has a silicon-aluminum ratio of 1, and obtains a silver-exchanged having silver ion exchange degree of not less than 66%. Preferred Composition: The mass volume ratio of 13X molecular sieve and silver nitrate solution is 1 g/(50-100) mL. The mass volume ratio of 5A molecular sieve and silver nitrate solution is 1 g/(50-100)mL. The concentration of aqueous silver nitrate solution is 0.05-0.4 mol/L. Preferred Conditions: The heating and stirring step is performed at 60-80 degrees C for 1-3 hours. The drying step is carried out at 80-120 degrees C for 24 hours. The vacuumizing and activating time is 5-10 hours.

The desalination of seawater comprises extracting sodium chloride through fresh water extraction by reverse osmosis and ion exchange with respect to the removed supernatant after step (c).
