PN CN111714540-A
TI Preparing micro-nano drug delivery system, by mixing porous/mesoporous micro/nano carrier and volatile oil substances, passing carbon dioxide, releasing reaction system by cooling and depressurizing and collecting micro-nano particles.
AU JIA J
   ZHOU X
   GE F
AE UNIV SUN YAT-SEN (UYSY-C)
GA 202098416D
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing a micro-nano drug delivery system of volatile oil substance, involves (i) uniformly mixing porous/mesoporous micro/nano carrier and volatile oil substance in supercritical state infiltration stage, introducing carbon dioxide and heating to start the process of penetration and loading of volatile oil substance in supercritical state, (ii) in the volatile oil loading stage, after the volatile oil substance penetration and loading in the supercritical state in step (i) reach equilibrium, releasing the reaction system from the supercritical state by cooling and depressurizing, and loading the volatile oil substance in the porous/mesoporous micro/nano carrier, and (iii) repeating the steps (i) and (ii) in the cycle drug loading stage, and collecting micro-nano particles loaded with volatile oil substances.
   USE - The method is used for preparing a micro-nano drug delivery system of volatile oil substance for preparing volatile oil functionalized nano drug (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method utilizes environmentally-friendly and super permeability supercritical carbon dioxide, and prepares the high drug-loading volatile oil substance micro-nano drug powder as a solid powder with a drug-loading of more than 30 wt.%.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Components: The porous/mesoporous micro/nano carrier is a particle with a porous or mesoporous structure with a size below 100 microns. The volatile oil substance in step (i) comprise volatile substances extracted from flowers, leaves, stems, roots or fruits of plants. The porous/mesoporous micro/nano carrier is mesoporous silicon, chitosan modified mesoporous silicon or silk protein nano aerosol particles. The volatile oil comprises at least one of patchouli oil, cinnamon oil and turmeric oil. Preferred Conditions: In step (i), the input of carbon dioxide meets the requirement that the pressure in the supercritical penetration stage is 8-30 MPa, and the temperature after heating is 35-50 degrees C. In step (i), the mass-volume ratio (mg:ml) of the porous/mesoporous micro/nano carrier to the volatile oil substances is (160-250):2. The temperature after cooling in step (ii) is below 25 degrees C.

AU SHAO X

AE SHANGHAI INST TECHNOLOGY (SHGH-C)

AB    NOVELTY - Copper-zinc/UiO-66 catalyst comprises copper                and zinc supported on UiO-66, where the mass of                copper and zinc is 5-55% of the total catalyst                mass, the ratio of copper and zinc is                (1:1)-(4:1).
   USE - The catalyst is useful for synthesizing                methanol by hydrogenation of carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method prevents aggregation of copper                particles, and improves stability of catalyst,                selectivity of methanol and conversion rate of                carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for preparing the catalyst involves (a) preparing                copper and zinc saline solution with a                concentration of 0.1-0.5 mol/l, adding UiO-66 to                obtain a mixed solution to co-precipitate at 60-90                degrees C, where the concentration of UiO-66 in the                mixed solution is 0.1-0.2 g/ml, preparing 0.5-1                mol/l sodium carbonate aqueous solution, dripping                to the above mixed solution, controlling to a pH of                7-8, aging to precipitate for 2-4 hours and                filtering to obtain a precipitation precursor, and                (b) drying the precipitation precursor at 80-110                degrees C for 10-16 hours, calcining at 250-400                degrees C for 2-4 hours, and screening to 40-80                mesh particles to obtain the product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The UiO-66 is prepared by                  adding zirconium tetrachloride and terephthalic                  acid to a mixed solution of N,N-dimethylformamide                  and hydrochloric acid and dissolving under                  ultrasonic treatment, heating the mixed solution                  at 80-110 degrees C, maintaining heat for 24                  hours, cooling to room temperature, washing for 6                  times with terephthalic acid and methanol, drying                  the obtained solid powder at room temperature and                  drying the dried solid powder in an oven at                  80-110 degrees C for 12-16 hours.

The processing temperature of the extrusion die is 100-160 degrees C, preferably 110-140 degrees C under pressure of 10-25 MPa, preferably 12-20 MPa.

PT P
PN CN110878070-A; CN110878070-B
TI Preparation of asymmetric urea compound involves reacting primary amine compound and secondary amine compound with carbon dioxide in presence of base and hydrosilane, and performing carbonylation coupling reaction.
AU YU B
   LIU Z
   HAN B
   ZHANG H
   ZHAO Y
AE CHINESE ACAD SCI CHEM INST (CSCI-C)
GA 2020222082
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of asymmetric urea compound (I) or (II) involves reacting a primary amine compound (III) and a secondary amine compound (IV) or (V) with carbon dioxide in presence of a base and a hydrosilane, and performing carbonylation coupling reaction.
   USE - Preparation of asymmetric urea compound.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables economical, efficient, and environmentally-friendly industrial preparation of asymmetric urea compound without using toxic reagent, expensive dehydrating agent and precious metal, and suppressed generation of intermediate. The product can be separated by simple filtration.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of asymmetric urea compound of formula (I) or (II) involves reacting a primary amine compound of formula (III) and a secondary amine compound of formula (IV) or (V) with carbon dioxide in presence of a base and a hydrosilane, and performing carbonylation coupling reaction.
   R1-R3 = aryl optionally substituted by A, or alkyl or cycloalkyl (all optionally substituted by alkoxy, halo, hydroxy, nitro, nitrilo, oxo, alkynyl, heterocyclyl, cycloalkyl, or aryl optionally substituted by A);
   aryl = phenyl, naphthyl, pyridyl, or thiazolyl;
   A = alkyl, alkoxy, halo, nitro, nitrilo, oxo, phenoxy, hydroxy, alkynyl, aryloxy, or aryl;and
   ring E = group formed by removing H from group of formula (i), preferably optionally substituted morpholinyl, piperidinyl, pyrrolidinyl, indolinolinyl, isoindolinolinyl, tetrahydroquinolinyl, or piperazinyl.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The hydrosilane is alkylhydrosilane, phenylhydrosilane and/or polymethylhydrosiloxane having an average molecular weight of 200-10000. The base is a Lewis base. The alkylhydrosilane is chosen from triethylsilane, diethylsilane, and methyldiethoxysilane. The phenylhydrosilane is chosen from phenylsilane, methyldiphenylsilane, and dimethyl(phenyl)silane. The Lewis base is chosen from cesium acetate, tetra-n-butylammonium fluoride, potassium carbonate, cesium fluoride, potassium fluoride, potassium tert-butoxide, and sodium tert-butoxide. The reaction is carried out in an organic solvent chosen from dimethyl sulfoxide, dimethylformamide, tetrahydrofuran, acetonitrile, and gamma -valerolactone diglyme. Preferred Composition: The molar ratio of primary amine compound and secondary amine compound is 1:1-1:3. The molar ratio of amine group of primary amine compound and base is 1:1-1:5. The molar ratio of amine group of primary amine compound and hydrosilane is 1:1-1:10. Preferred Condition: The coupling reaction is performed at carbon dioxide gas pressure of 0.1-10 MPa and 30-150 degrees C for 1-36 hours.

PN CN109007740-A
TI Preparing natural lotus leaf fragrance, comprises pretreating of raw materials using dry lotus leaves, pulverizing dry lotus leaves, extracting carbon dioxide, placing pulverized lotus leaf raw material into supercritical extraction kettle.
AU WENG S
   CHEN J
   LI M
   CHEN C
AE XUMEI KAIFENG BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (XUME-Non-standard)
   SHANGHAI XUMEI FOOD TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2018A4551H
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing a natural lotus leaf fragrance, comprises pretreating of raw materials using dry lotus leaves, pulverizing dry lotus leaves; extracting supercritical carbon dioxide, placing the pulverized lotus leaf raw material into a supercritical extraction kettle, and carbon dioxide is introduced for extraction, where the obtained extract is a lotus leaf extract; dissolving and purifying separation, heating the lotus leaf extract and stirring and dissolving by edible alcohol. The heating temperature is 40-90 degrees C, and the mixture is stirred and dissolved for 10 to 120 minutes, and then the suspended matter and the precipitate are removed by filtration, and the obtained filtrate is passed through a frozen high-speed tube. The centrifuge is processed, the temperature of the centrifugation is set to -20 to 10 degrees C, and the supernatant obtained by centrifugation is a natural lotus leaf fragrance.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing a natural lotus leaf fragrance.
   ADVANTAGE - The method utilizes natural lotus leaf fragrance which has a vivid, pleasant fresh lotus leaf fragrance and sweet taste, natural coordination, high aroma intensity, and can be used in various beverages and other foods which need to highlight the lotus leaf aroma, and has high marketing value.

TI Fresh air system quality warning method, involves obtaining air purification efficiency and heat exchange efficiency of ventilation system according to environment data, and calculating preset purifying efficiency threshold value.
AU ZHAO T
   GUO H
AE AIGO ELECTRONIC TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (BJHQ-C)
GA 2018A0371L
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves obtaining environment data of a ventilation system. Air purification efficiency and heat exchange efficiency of the ventilation system are obtained according to the environment data. A preset purifying efficiency threshold value is calculated. Warning instruction is transmitted to the ventilation system when the air purification efficiency is less than a purification rate threshold value or heat exchange efficiency is less than a heat-exchange efficiency threshold value. The environment data is provided with carbon dioxide concentration. Judgment is made to check whether the environment data satisfies preset carbon dioxide concentration condition. A carbon dioxide concentration value is calculated.
   USE - Fresh air system quality warning method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables generating pre-warning information so as to improve fresh air system working efficiency and realize purifying filter core monitoring and pre-warning prompting operation so as to replace a filtering core of air purification, and improving air quality of a fresh air system.

PN CN108503851-A
TI High stability metal organic framework material            preparation for high-efficiency acetylene or carbon            dioxide selective separation and adsorption, involves            dissolving organic ligand amino hydroxybenzoic acid and            zinc nitrate hexahydrate.
AU QIAN G
   ZHANG L
   JIANG K
   LI B
   YANG Y
   CUI Y
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG (UYZH-C)
GA 2018723746
AB    NOVELTY - High stability metal organic framework                material preparation involves dissolving organic                ligand 3-amino-4-hydroxybenzoic acid and zinc                nitrate hexahydrate in a mixed solution of                dimethylformamide, acetonitrile, ethanol and                deionized water at 80-90 degrees C for 1-3 days to                obtain a homogeneous crystalline material. The                obtained homogeneous crystal material is exchanged                multiple times in anhydrous acetone or anhydrous                dichloromethane by solvent exchange for half an                hour to remove pores from the material. The                coordinated solvent molecules are placed at 273                degrees K for 1 day and then placed at room                temperature for 1 day to obtain highly stable metal                organic framework material.
   USE - Method for preparing high stability metal                organic framework material for high-efficiency                acetylene or carbon dioxide selective separation                and adsorption (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method prepares high stability metal                organic framework material in a simple,                cost-effective and eco-friendly manner with                excellent chemical stability, thermal stability and                cycle stability, and wide potential application                prospects, and effective prevention of the carbon                dioxide storage.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic X-ray                diffraction pattern of the prepared high stability                metal organic framework material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The organic ligand                  3-amino-4-hydroxybenzoic acid and zinc nitrate                  hexahydrate are provided in a mass ratio of 1:2.                  The solvent mixture includes dimethylformamide,                  acetonitrile, ethanol and deionized water in a                  volume ratio of 20:3:3:1.

PN CN109666187-A
TI Preparing light calcium carbonate based on slaked lime, comprises e.g. e.g. dissolving slaked lime powder in water without impurities to prepare calcium hydroxide slurry, introducing carbon dioxide gas, heating and adding coating agent.
AU LIAO J
   LIAO W
AE XIAMEN GONGWEI INFORMATION TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (XIAM-Non-standard)
GA 201940846C
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing light calcium carbonate based on slaked lime, comprises (i) dissolving slaked lime powder in water without impurities to prepare a calcium hydroxide slurry, (ii) introducing the prepared calcium hydroxide slurry into a first reaction chamber through a first water inlet, turning off a second discharge port of the first reaction chamber, introducing a high pressure carbon dioxide gas through the first air inlet to open a first agitator in the first reaction chamber, (iii) detecting pH value of the calcium hydroxide slurry in the first reaction chamber, if the pH value meets a preset pH value, closing first air inlet and the first agitator, opening the second discharge port, discharging the solution in the first reaction chamber to obtain a calcium carbonate suspension, (iv) heating the obtained calcium carbonate suspension to 45-95 degrees C, and then adding a coating agent and coating for 0.5-3.5 hours, and (v) filtering, washing, drying and crushing the suspension.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing light calcium carbonate based on slaked lime.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves production efficiency and quality of calcium carbonate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing light calcium carbonate based on slaked lime, comprises (i) dissolving slaked lime powder in water without impurities to prepare a calcium hydroxide slurry, (ii) introducing the prepared calcium hydroxide slurry into a first reaction chamber through a first water inlet, turning off a second discharge port of the first reaction chamber, introducing a high pressure carbon dioxide gas through the first air inlet to open a first agitator in the first reaction chamber, where the pressure of the high pressure carbon dioxide gas is 0.5-5 Mpa, the first reaction chamber includes the first water inlet, the first air inlet, and the second discharge port, and the first agitator is disposed in the first reaction chamber for stirring the calcium hydroxide slurry to dissolve the high pressure carbon dioxide, (iii) detecting pH value of the calcium hydroxide slurry in the first reaction chamber, if the pH value meets a preset pH value, closing the first air inlet and the first agitator, opening the second discharge port, discharging the solution in the first reaction chamber to obtain a calcium carbonate suspension, (iv) heating the obtained calcium carbonate suspension to 45-95 degrees C, and then adding 0.5-3.5 wt.% a coating agent and coating for 0.5-3.5 hours, and (v) filtering, washing, drying and crushing the obtained calcium carbonate suspension to obtain light calcium carbonate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The drying in the step (v) is performed by first dehydrating the washed calcium carbonate through a centrifuge, and then placing in a vacuum forging box to dry. Preferred Components: The concentration of the calcium hydroxide slurry in the step (i) is 15-30%. The concentration of the carbon dioxide gas in the step (ii) is 50-70%. The preset pH in the step (iii) is 6.5-7.5. The coating agent in the step (iv) is a fatty acid and/or a water-soluble titanate coupling agent.

PN CN108998380-A
TI Cultivating selenium-enriched whitefly Cordyceps militaris comprises inoculating Cordyceps militaris strain and yellow Cordyceps militaris strain on activation medium, activating and carrying out inoculation onto liquid fermentation medium.
AU ZHANG Y
   WANG Y
   YE L
   JIANG X
   HAN B
AE ANKANG AGRIC SCI INST (ANKA-Non-standard)
GA 2018A3554U
AB    NOVELTY - Cultivating selenium-enriched whitefly Cordyceps militaris comprises (i) inoculating Cordyceps militaris strain and yellow Cordyceps militaris strain on the activation medium, activating albino Cordyceps militaris and activated yellow Cordyceps militaris; (ii) carrying out inoculation of activated whitening Cordyceps militaris and activated yellow Cordyceps militaris of step (i) onto the same liquid fermentation medium, culturing, rotating and diluting with distilled water to obtain bacterial seed solution and inoculating the culture solution into the cultivation medium; (iii) placing the inoculated culture medium of (ii) under carbon dioxide, then at air relative humidity of 70-80% under carbon dioxide and the light intensity is 200-500 lx, and the differentiation time is 5-7 days and under the condition of relative humidity of air of 80-90% and illumination time of 8 hours/day and sub-seat grows for 40-50 days.
   USE - The method is useful for cultivating selenium-enriched whitefly Cordyceps militaris.
   ADVANTAGE - The method increases the yield of Cordyceps militaris and high content of selenium in cultivated Cordyceps militaris, improves the nutritional and health value of Cordyceps militaris.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Cultivating selenium-enriched whitefly Cordyceps militaris comprises (i) inoculating the Cordyceps militaris strain and yellow Cordyceps militaris strain on the activation medium, respectively at a temperature of 21-23 degrees C with air relative humidity of 60-65% under carbon dioxide volume fraction of less than 0.05%, culturing for 5-7 days, respectively activating the albino Cordyceps militaris and activated yellow Cordyceps militaris; (ii) carrying out inoculation of activated whitening Cordyceps militaris and activated yellow Cordyceps militaris of step (i) onto the same liquid fermentation medium, culturing for 7-10 days at 19-23 degrees C, rotating at 150 rotations/minute and diluting with 6-10 times distilled water to obtain bacterial seed solution and inoculating the culture solution into the cultivation medium at 50-70 ml/serving; (iii) placing the inoculated culture medium of (ii) at 21-23 degrees C with air relative humidity of 60 -70% under carbon dioxide volume fraction of less than 0.05% for 7-10 days, then at air relative humidity of 70-80% under carbon dioxide volume fraction of less than 0.1% and the light intensity is 200-500 lx, and the differentiation time is 5-7 days and under the condition of relative humidity of air of 80-90% and illumination time of 8 hours/day and sub-seat grows for 40-50 days.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: In step (ii) the inoculation amount of activated albino Cordyceps militaris strain and activated yellow Cordyceps militaris strain is 3 pieces of cake/100 ml liquid fermentation medium and the diameter of the cake is 5 mm. Preferred Method: In step (i) the activation medium is prepared by taking 200 g potato, 5 g glucose, 10 g sucrose, 2 g peptone, 1 g potassium dihydrogen phosphate, 0.5 g magnesium sulfate, 12 g agar, adding into 1000 ml water, boiling, mixing, sterilizing and pouring. In step (ii) the liquid fermentation medium is prepared by adding 10 g glucose, 5 g sucrose, 10 g peptone, 2 g yeast extract, 1 g potassium dihydrogen phosphate, 0.5 g magnesium sulfate into water 1000 ml, mixing and sterilizing. The cultivation medium is prepared by (a) adding 5-12 g glucose, 8-15 g sucrose, 2-5 g peptone, 0.5-1 g potassium dihydrogen phosphate and 0.25-0.75 g magnesium sulfate into 1000 ml water and uniformly mixing to obtain basic nutrient solution; (b) adding sodium selenite into the basic nutrient solution to obtain selenium-enriched culture solution, where the amount of the sodium selenite is greater than 0 and less than or equal to 150 mg/l; and (c) preparing the cultivation medium in portions, where each cultivation medium is obtained by mixing 350 g wheat and selenium-enriched culture liquid equivalent to 1.4-1.6 times the weight of wheat and autoclaving.
DC D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).)

   LI H

   LIN L
AE UNIV TIANJIN POLYTECHNIC (UNTP-C)
   UNIV TIANJIN POLYTECHNIC (UNTP-C)
GA 201827171R
AB    NOVELTY - A polyimide-zinc organic framework-loaded                graphene oxide hybrid film comprises a polyimide                and zinc organic skeleton-loaded graphene oxide                composite nanosheets, and has thickness of 70-80 mu                m. The mass ratio of polyimide and zinc organic                framework-loaded graphene oxide nanosheets is                (0.7-0.95):(0.3-0.05).
   USE - Polyimide-zinc organic framework-loaded                graphene oxide hybrid film used for separating                carbon dioxide and nitrogen (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The polyimide-zinc organic framework-loaded                graphene oxide hybrid film has excellent                comprehensive performance, carbon dioxide                permeability of 238 barrer or less, and carbon                dioxide/nitrogen selectivity of 65. The carbon                dioxide/nitrogen permeability of the polyimide-zinc                organic framework hybrid membrane is increased by                155% and 134%, respectively compared to pure                polyimide film which is increased by 180% and 48%,                respectively.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for                manufacture of polyimide-zinc organic                framework-loaded graphene oxide hybrid film, which                involves adding 98 %mass concentrated sulfuric acid                to a reactor, placing the reactor in an ice bath,                vigorously stirring, adding graphite flakes and                sodium nitrate, vigorously stirring, adding                potassium permanganate, continuously stirring at                273-283 K for 1-5 hours, placing the mixture in                water bath at 20-60 degrees C, stirring for 2-8                hours, adding water to the mixture, heating the                mixture to 70-120 degrees C for 20-60 minutes,                diluting the mixture with water, adding 30%                hydrogen peroxide to remove the residual potassium                permanganate and manganese dioxide, filtering,                washing with 5% hydrochloric acid and distilled                water until pH is neutral to obtain graphene oxide,                mixing graphene oxide in a methanol-water solution                in mass ratio of (1-4):1 to obtain graphene oxide                solution (a), ultrasonically dispersing for 1-10                hours, dissolving zinc nitrate hexahydrate and                2-methylimidazole in methanol, stirring for 5-30                minutes to obtain solution (b), mixing the solution                (a) and the solution (b), stirring at room                temperature for 1-5 hours, filtering, washing with                methanol, vacuum drying to obtain zinc organic                skeleton-loaded graphene oxide composite                nanosheets, adding polyimide to                N,N-dimethylacetamide to prepare a polyimide                solution having concentration of 5-10%, adding zinc                organic skeleton-loaded graphene oxide composite                nanosheets, ultrasonically treating for 1-6 hours,                adding 5-10% polyimide solution, stirring for 2-20                hours, filtering, and drying at 50 degrees C for 12                hours and 80 degrees C for 12 hours. The mass ratio                of graphite flakes to sodium nitrate is (1-5):1.                The mass ratio of graphite flakes to potassium                permanganate is 1:(1-10).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Properties: The polyimide-zinc                  organic framework-loaded graphene oxide hybrid                  film has selectivity of 33-65 and permeability of                  6-238 barrer.

PN CN114437841-A
TI Co-producing natural gas, LPG and naphtha using            Fischer-Tropsch synthesis tail gas, comprises adjusting            molar percentage of carbon monoxide, separating raw            material gas, carrying out methanation treatment of            methanation raw material and flashing absorption rich            liquid.
AU LI Y
   YANG Y
   DONG G
   HAO X
   GAO J
   HE S
AE SYNFUELS CHINA TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SYNF-Non-standard)
GA 202266236A
AB    NOVELTY - Co-producing natural gas, LPG and naphtha                using Fischer-Tropsch synthesis tail gas, comprises                (i) adjusting the molar percentage of carbon                monoxide in the Fischer-Tropsch synthesis tail gas                to 8-10% to obtain a raw material gas; (ii)                separating raw material gas at low temperature to                obtain absorption dry gas and absorption rich                liquid, and preparing methanation raw material by                exchanging heat between the absorption dry gas and                the raw material gas, where the methanation raw                material is rich in hydrogen , carbon monoxide,                carbon dioxide, nitrogen and C3-hydrocarbons; (iii)                carrying out the methanation treatment of the                methanation raw material to obtain synthetic                natural gas SNG and condensate; (iv) flashing the                absorption rich liquid to obtain a flash gas and a                flash liquid, and extracting and separating the                flash liquid to obtain a mixture rich in carbon                monoxide and carbon hydrocarbons.
   USE - The method is useful for Co-producing natural                gas, LPG and naphtha using Fischer-Tropsch                synthesis tail gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method can realize efficient utilization                of various useful components inFischer-Tropsch                synthesis tail gas, and improves the economic                benefit of coal oil production enterprise.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Co-producing natural gas, LPG and naphtha                using Fischer-Tropsch synthesis tail gas, comprises                (i) adjusting the molar percentage of carbon                monoxide in the Fischer-Tropsch synthesis tail gas                to 8-10% to obtain a raw material gas; (ii)                separating raw material gas at low temperature to                obtain absorption dry gas and absorption rich                liquid, and preparing methanation raw material by                exchanging heat between the absorption dry gas and                the raw material gas, where the methanation raw                material is rich in hydrogen, carbon monoxide,                carbon dioxide, nitrogen and C3-hydrocarbons; (iii)                carrying out the methanation treatment of the                methanation raw material to obtain synthetic                natural gas SNG and condensate; (iv) flashing the                absorption rich liquid to obtain a flash gas and a                flash liquid, and extracting and separating the                flash liquid to obtain a mixture rich in carbon                monoxide and carbon hydrocarbons, where mixture                rich in carbon dioxide and C3+ hydrocarbons is                subjected to low-temperature oil washing separation                to obtain oil-washed dry gas, LPG, and naphtha, and                the oil-washed dry gas is returned and mixed with                the absorption dry gas to obtain the methanation                feedstock.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                device for implementing the method comprising                low-temperature separation unit, the                low-temperature separation unit comprises a first                heat exchanger connected in fluid communication                manner, a quenching device, an absorption tower, a                flash tank and an extraction separation tower,                methanation unit, connected to the first heat                exchanger in a fluid communication manner; and the                methanation unit comprises a multi-stage                methanation reactor connected in fluid                communication manner, a second heat exchanger, a                cooler and a separator; a low temperature oil                washing unit, the low temperature oil washing unit                is connected to the absorption tower by means of                fluid communication and the low temperature oil                washing unit comprises an absorption tower, a                stabilizing towerand a regeneration tower, which                are connected in fluid communication way
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow chart of                Co-producing natural gas, LPG and naphtha using                Fischer-Tropsch synthesis tail gas.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (i), the molar                  percentage of carbon dioxide in the                  Fischer-Tropsch synthesis tail gas is adjusted by                  a part or all of the carbon dioxide is removed                  from a part of the Fischer-Tropsch synthesis tail                  gas to obtain a decarbonized tail gas, and the                  decarbonized tail gas is combined with The other                  part of the Fischer-Tropsch synthesis tail gas is                  mixed relative to the total amount of carbon                  dioxide in the Fischer-Tropsch synthesis tail                  gas, more than 98 vol% of carbon dioxide is                  removed from the 20-25 vol.% of the                  Fischer-Tropsch synthesis tail gas to obtain                  decarbonized tail gas. In the step (ii), the raw                  material gas and the absorption dry gas are                  firstly subjected to heat exchange to be cooled                  to 0 to 10&#8451; and then cryogenically                  cooled to -60 to 0&#8451;, and then cooling                  to -60-0&#8451; in a cold absorbent methanol                  and the low-temperature separation is carried out                  under the action of the absorbing and desorbing                  tower and in the step (ii), the low-temperature                  separation is carried out on the raw material gas                  in an absorption and desorption tower, and the                  theoretical plate number of the absorption and                  desorption tower is 5-50, the temperature at the                  top of the tower is -60 to -50&#8451;, the                  temperature in the tower still is -50 to                  -50&#8451;, and the operating pressure is                  0-6MPa. In the step (iii), the methanation                  treatment is carried out after heating the                  methanation raw material to 150-300&#8451;,                  where the methanation raw material is subjected                  to the methanation treatment under a one-stage                  reaction is performed at 150-300&#176; C                  under 0-8.0 MPa, performing the reaction at                  150-350&#8451; under 0-8.0 MPa, two-stage                  reaction, carrying out three-stage reaction at                  150-300&#8451; and 0-8.0MPa, and carrying out                  four-stage reaction at 150-300&#8451; and                  0-8.0MPa, where one-stage reaction is carried out                  after the methanation raw material is mixed with                  the one-stage return gas; preferably, the steam                  generated by the four-stage reaction is cooled to                  30-5&#8451; to obtain the synthetic natural                  gas SNG. In the step (iv), the flash gas is                  compressed by a compressor and returned and mixed                  with the Fischer-Tropsch synthesis tail gas as                  the raw material gas, where the flashing is                  performed under the following conditions Steaming                  and temperature is -40 to -50&#8451;, and the                  pressure is 0-2 MPa. In the step (iv), water is                  used as an extractant, and the flashing liquid is                  subjected to the extraction and separation, so                  that methanol in the flashing liquid is recovered                  to obtain an aqueous methanol solution, and the                  carbon dioxide and C3+ hydrocarbons are separated                  out mixture; preferably, the extraction                  separation is carried out under the following                  conditions temperature is -40 to -50&#8451;,                  the pressure is 0-2MPa, and the number of trays                  is 2-15. The recovered methanol aqueous solution                  is desorbed and separated to obtain the                  extractant water and the cold absorbent methanol,                  where the reclaimed methanol aqueous solution is                  desorbed and separated in an absorbent recovery                  tower, the theoretical plate number of the                  absorbent recovery tower is 5-100, the                  temperature at the top of the tower is 0-100                  &#176;C, the temperature of the tower still                  is 50-150 &#176;C, and the operating pressure                  is 0-5MPa and part or all of the cold absorbent                  obtained by desorption and separation is returned                  to the absorption and desorption tower;                  preferably, the extractant obtained by desorption                  and separation is returned for recycling. In step                  (iv), the low-temperature oil-washing separation                  includes the following operations: using naphtha                  as an absorbent, treating the mixture rich in                  carbon dioxide and C3+ hydrocarbons in an                  absorption tower to obtain top dry gas and                  absorption Tata kettle liquid, where liquid from                  the column-receiving tower still is processed                  into the stabilization tower to obtain LPG and                  the liquid from the still of the stable tower;                  the liquid from the still of the stabilizing                  tower is entered into the regeneration tower for                  processing to obtain oil-washed naphtha and                  absorbent naphtha, the top dry gas is recycled                  back and mixed with the absorption dry gas and                  oil-washed dry gas as a raw material for                  methanation, the PN CN113842795-A
TI Preparing ZIF-boron nitride/polyvinyl alcohol membrane used for screening mixed gas hydrogen/carbon dioxide, involves preparing ZIF-boron nitride and polyvinyl alcohol solutions, making membrane, adding polyvinyl alcohol solution, filtering, adding ZIF-boron nitride solution, filtering, and drying.
AU LIU J
   XUE Y
   ZENG X
   LI X
   GUO M
AE NANYANG INST TECHNOLOGY (UNYI-C)
GA 2022096558
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing ZIF-boron nitride/polyvinyl alcohol screening membrane involves: (1) preparing ZIF-boron nitride sheet by using hydroxylated boron nitride, methanol-deionized water mixture, zinc nitrate hexahydrate, methanol solution, and 2-methylimidazole; and (2) dispersing the ZIF-boron nitride in an ethanol-deionized water mixture, ultrasonically treating to obtain a uniformly dispersed ZIF-boron nitride solution, dissolving polyvinyl alcohol in ethanol-deionized water mixture by mechanical stirring to form a polyvinyl alcohol solution, making a membrane with a nylon membrane as a carrier by vacuum-assisted filtration operation method, adding the polyvinyl alcohol solution, completely suction filtering, then adding ZIF-boron nitride solution, completely suction filtering, repeating the above operation 7-10 times, finally adding polyvinyl alcohol solution to complete suction filtration, and drying the obtained membrane in a vacuum oven at 40degrees Celsius for 24 hours.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing ZIF-boron nitride/polyvinyl alcohol membrane used for screening mixed gas hydrogen/carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides the ZIF-boron nitride/polyvinyl alcohol screening membrane material, which has excellent hydrogen/carbon dioxide screening ability and mechanical strength, compact and regular structure, prepares ZIF-boron nitride, which has excellent compatibility than spherical ZIF, and avoids the disorder phenomenon of the internal structure of the screening membrane prepared by melt blending method.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing ZIF-boron nitride/polyvinyl alcohol screening membrane involves: (1) dispersing hydroxylated boron nitride in methanol-deionized water mixture to obtain a hydroxylated boron nitride solution, dissolving zinc nitrate hexahydrate in methanol solution to obtain zinc nitrate hexahydrate solution, immersing the hydroxylated boron nitride solution in the zinc nitrate hexahydrate solution to make the divalent zinc ion fully contact with the hydroxylated boron nitride sheet to obtain a mixed solution, dissolving 2-methylimidazole in methanol, then mixing with the mixed solution, so that the molar ratio of zinc nitrate hexahydrate to 2-methylimidazole is 1:8, and stirring at room temperature to obtain a pale yellow solution, filtering the pale yellow solution, washing several times with methanol, and collecting the filter cake to obtain an initial product ZIF-boron nitride sheet, and dispersing the initial product ZIF-boron nitride in methanol again, and ultrasonically treating in an ice-water bath to remove the ZIF generated at the edge of the hydroxylated boron nitride sheet and non-porous structure area from hydroxylated boron nitride, centrifuging with a centrifuge, and drying the precipitate to obtain a final product ZIF-boron nitride sheet; and (2) dispersing the final product ZIF-boron nitride in an ethanol-deionized water mixture, ultrasonically treating at 0degrees Celsius to obtain a uniformly dispersed ZIF-boron nitride solution, dissolving polyvinyl alcohol in ethanol-deionized water mixture by mechanical stirring at 70degrees Celsius to form a polyvinyl alcohol solution, making a membrane with a nylon membrane with a pore size of 200 nm as a carrier by vacuum-assisted filtration operation method, adding the polyvinyl alcohol solution, completely suction filtering, then adding ZIF-boron nitride solution, completely suction filtering, repeating the above operation 7-10 times, finally adding polyvinyl alcohol solution to complete suction filtration, and drying the obtained membrane in a vacuum oven at 40degrees Celsius for 24 hours.
DC A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

PN WO2021177807-A1
TI Manufacturing animal leather substitute based on fungal mycelium substitute product for animal leather, involves selecting one species of fungi with potential to develop material with suitable properties to act as animal leather substitute.
AU DIAZ TAZZER HERRERIAS D
   MARTIN BARRIENTOS R
   MUTTIO LIMAS R
   DUARTE GONZALEZ A
AE PAPEL LAB IDEAS SA DE CV (PAPE-Non-standard)
GA 2021A3890L
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacturing animal leather substitute based on fungal mycelium involves selecting one species of fungi with the potential to develop a material with suitable properties to act as animal leather substitute (SCA), selected from families of polyporal order including Ganodermataceae, Polyporaceae and Fomitopsidaceae; isolating the selected microorganism (by living tissue, spore, mycelium) by subjecting it to a purification process; eliminating pollutants and external agents to the organism to be cultivated, evaluating nutrition, and through nutritious diet and growth stimuli (light, agitation, temperature, humidity, carbon dioxide/oxygen (CO2/O2) balance, time) and resistance; and obtaining culture medium based on technical selection of nutrients to carry out selective growth of vegetative body of the fungi called mycelium with nutrients assimilable by the body, and are limited to carbon, nitrogen, phosphorus, potassium, magnesium, sulfur obtained from different sources.
   USE - Method for manufacturing animal leather substitute based on fungal mycelium substitute product for animal leather.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Manufacturing animal leather substitute based on fungal mycelium involves selecting one species of fungi with the potential to develop a material with suitable properties to act as animal leather substitute (SCA), selected from families of polyporal order including Ganodermataceae, Polyporaceae and Fomitopsidaceae; isolating the selected microorganism (by living tissue, spore, mycelium) by subjecting it to a purification process; eliminating pollutants and external agents to the organism to be cultivated, evaluating nutrition, and through nutritious diet and growth stimuli (light, agitation, temperature, humidity, carbon dioxide/oxygen (CO2/O2) balance, time) and resistance; and obtaining culture medium based on technical selection of nutrients to carry out selective growth of vegetative body of the fungi called mycelium with nutrients assimilable by the body, and are limited to carbon, nitrogen, phosphorus, potassium, magnesium, sulfur obtained from different sources. The method further involves inoculating the culture at a rate of 10-30 vol.% of standard culture medium (SCM) in a culture reactor; incubating in a culture reactor with control of parameters such as lighting under a photoperiod of 12/12 hours, under a wavelength of 380-480 nm; temperature with a general range of 22-27 degrees C, relative humidity between 75-95%, agitation with different phases of agitation, with balance between oxygen and carbon dioxide in the range greater than 20,000 ppm CO2 and a retention time of culture between 5-45 days; harvesting a cover of plant mycelium on the surface of the reactor approximately 3-15 cm thick, removing a separation membrane, for easy harvesting without the need to completely remove the culture medium; post-harvest treatment of the mycelium vegetable mat, subjecting it to soaking in 10-40 vol.% solution of ptastifying agent in clean water and then washing with clean water with a soaking time of between 12-48 hours and a drying in a range of 40-65 degrees C until it loses 80% of its humidity; and give a finish by submitting one or more treatment such as staining, waxing, waterproofing, chemical finishing and pressing to obtain a finished product with the same or better characteristics than animal leather. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a substitute product for animal leather, is made from fungal mycelium obtained by method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The culture medium comprises 5-10 wt.% carbon source, 2-4 wt.% nitrogen source, 0.5-1 wt.% phosphorus source, 1-2 wt.% potassium source, 1-2 wt.% magnesium source, and 0.5-1 wt.% sulfur source. Preferred Components: The carbon source is selected from molasses or cane stillage. The nitrogen source is selected from nitrate of sodium or urea, or source of phosphorus is potassium phosphate. The potassium source is potassium phosphate. The magnesium source is magnesium sulfate. The sulfur source is selected from magnesium sulfate or copper sulfate. The post-harvest treatment is carried out using plasticizing agent selected from glycerol, ethylene glycol, surfactants or polyols. The chemical finishing stage it is carried out with aldehydes, catechols or tannins. Preferred Conditions: The culture reactor floating or fixed membrane is incorporated to the reactor floor, which allows a superficial separation between the medium and the culture to be developed (fungal organism) and guarantees a vertical and underground growth. The floating membrane of an autoclavable polymer such as polypropylene with a pore size not greater than 10mm. The stirring comprises a phase, which begins agitation is constant at 100 revolutions/minute (RPM) for period of 5 days to favor the initial growth of the mycelium, increasing its mass and reproductive capacity. The growth phase where the agitation is periodic for 1 hour every 20 hours at a speed of 25 RPM in a period of approximately 10 days to favor the growth of the surface layer and the oxygenation of the organism. A thickening phase with constant agitation to promote the homogeneous growth of the surface portion of the reactor. The dyeing is carried out with lipid-based pigments of vegetable origin. The finishing stage the waxing is carried out with natural fats of cocoa, coconut, beeswax. The soft polymers are used in the waterproofing finishing stage. The finishing stage in the chemical treatments, waxing and waterproofing are made in a proportion of between 5-15 wt.% of the dry weight of the sample through a 12 hour immersion treatment with subsequent drying, obtaining the material in its finished product presentation.
DC D18 (Skins, hides, pelts, leather and chemical treatment of tobacco.); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); P13 (Plant culture, dairy products (A01G, H, J).)

PN CN112831359-A; CN112831359-B
TI Synthesis of liquefied natural gas by introducing purified mixed gas into methanation reactor, and reacting mixed gas with carbon dioxide, carbon monoxide and hydrogen in mixed gas under the action of catalyst set on catalyst bed layer.
AU LIU Y
AE HEGANG JIARUN ENERGY CO LTD (HEGA-Non-standard)
GA 202160715K
AB    NOVELTY - Synthesis of liquefied natural gas involves introducing purified mixed gas into methanation reactor using first air inlet, and reacting mixed gas with carbon dioxide, carbon monoxide and hydrogen in mixed gas under the action of catalyst set on catalyst bed layer for generating methane. The methanation reactor is provided with temperature detecting device, air outlet, catalyst bed layer, catalyst inlet and humidity detector. The air outlet is used to discharge methane from air outlet to storage tank. The humidity detector is used to detect real-time humidity W in methanation reactor. The catalyst inlet is used to add catalyst from catalyst inlet to catalyst bed layer. The catalyst inlet is provided with electromagnetic valve to control speed of catalyst entering methanation reactor. A central control unit is set outside methanation reactor and respectively connected with humidity detector and electromagnetic valve.
   USE - The method is used for synthesizing liquefied natural gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method adjusts opening and closing degree of electromagnetic valve according to detected humidity value to realize control of methane synthesis rate and realize precise control of synthesis process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Synthesis of liquefied natural gas involves introducing purified mixed gas into methanation reactor through first air inlet, and reacting mixed gas with carbon dioxide, carbon monoxide and hydrogen in mixed gas under the action of catalyst set on catalyst bed layer to generate methane. The methanation reactor is provided with catalyst bed layer, air outlet, humidity detector, catalyst inlet and temperature detecting device. The air outlet is used for discharging methane from air outlet to storage tank. The humidity detector is used for detecting real-time humidity W in methanation reactor. The catalyst inlet is used for adding catalyst from catalyst inlet to catalyst bed layer. The catalyst inlet is provided with electromagnetic valve for controlling speed of catalyst entering methanation reactor. A central control unit is set outside methanation reactor and respectively connected with humidity detector and electromagnetic valve. The central control unit adjusts opening degree of electromagnetic valve according to real-time humidity detected by humidity detector to control quality of catalyst to meet speed of generating methane by mixed gas. The central control unit is provided with humidity matrix W (W1, W2, W3, W4) and electromagnetic valve opening and closing degree matrix K (K1, K2, K3, K4,K5), and adjusts electromagnetic valve opening degree through real-time humidity information. The temperature detecting device is used for detecting temperature T of methanation reactor. The central control unit is further provided with standard reaction temperature T0. A temperature detection meter is wirelessly connected with central control unit for transmitting temperature of temperature detection meter to central control unit. The central control unit is further provided with forward compensation coefficient A1 and reverse compensation coefficient A2. The forward compensation coefficient A1 and reverse compensation coefficient A2 are used to compensate parameters in humidity matrix W (W1, W2, W3, W4) according to comparison result of temperature in methanation reactor and standard reaction temperature T0. The central control unit compensates parameter in electromagnetic valve opening degree matrix K (K1, K2, K3, K4, K5) according to quality of catalyst in reaction process.
DC H06 (Gaseous and liquid fuels - including pollution control. Chemical aspects of catalytic exhaust systems for cars are included as well as liquid or gaseous fuels of non-petroleum origin e.g. methanol or ethanol-based fuels. Combustion improvement additives for liquid fuels are included (C10L).)

PN CN112640728-A
TI Performing industrial bottle cultivation method of seafood mushroom involves paving non-woven fabric at bottle opening to control culture temperature and humidity, and controlling carbon dioxide concentration for certain days.
AU HUANG Z
   CHEN G
   XIAO S
   KE L
AE ZHANGZHOU ECONOMY CROPS STATION (ZHAN-Non-standard)
GA 202148704E
AB    NOVELTY - Performing industrial bottle cultivation method of seafood mushroom involves preparing mother culture medium and original culture medium, where the mother culture medium is potato dextrose agar (PDA) culture medium and original culture medium is dry saw dust, rice bran and bran, preparing raw culture medium and mother culture medium, controlling the water content in the original culture medium at 60-65% and pH between 7.2-7.5, mixing the dry sawdust, rice bran and bran in the mother culture medium with sorghum, cotton seed hull material and original culture medium, standing, waiting for full absorbtion of water, putting into stirrer to stir, stirring to obtain the culture material, putting into bottle cultivation for sterilizing the culture material, and cooling, sending the culture material into the sterile room for sterile operation by automatic mechanical device.
   USE - Method for performing industrial bottle cultivation method of seafood mushroom.
   ADVANTAGE - The method saves transportation cost and purchase cost, reduces production cost of seafood mushroom and improves comprehensive economic value of seafood mushroom industrial production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Performing industrial bottle cultivation method of seafood mushroom involves preparing mother culture medium and original culture medium, where the mother culture medium is potato dextrose agar (PDA) culture medium and original culture medium is dry saw dust, rice bran and bran, preparing raw culture medium and mother culture medium, controlling the water content in the original culture medium at 60-65% and pH between 7.2-7.5, mixing the dry sawdust, rice bran and bran in the mother culture medium with sorghum, cotton seed hull material and original culture medium, standing, waiting for full absorbtion of water, putting into stirrer to stir, stirring to obtain the culture material, putting into bottle cultivation for sterilizing the culture material, and cooling, sending the culture material into the sterile room for sterile operation by automatic mechanical device, stirring, cultivating and culturing the fresh mushroom, firstly feeding the bottled culture material to the automatic inoculating machine, inoculating the strain, after inoculating, sending the culture material into the culture chamber by conveying mechanism, controlling the proper temperature, performing rational culture, maintaining proper temperature, humidity and carbon dioxide concentration, culturing for days after which the hypha reaches the bottom of the bottle, ripening culture for number of days, where hypha is thick white, forms coarse mycelium and the material surface appears white granular, and secretes light yellow pigment to reach physiological maturation, fruiting and harvesting strains of seafood mushrooms enters the fruiting management stage after the bottle is full, opening the bottle and the scratching measures are conventionally operated, injecting 10-15 ml water into the bottle through the industrial water injection mechanism, and paving non-woven fabric at the bottle opening culture bottle controlling the culture temperature and humidity, removing the covered compound bar when the mushroom bud appears, controlling the culture temperature, and watering mechanism, spraying atomized water to the culture chamber, keeping the indoor humidity, and performing normal ventilation treatment, controlling the carbon dioxide concentration, waiting for certain days, picking the seafood mushroom by the picking device.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Conditions: The selection of the culture bottle can be between 500-1000 ml. The component ratio of the cotton seed shell material is higher than that of the sorghum.

PN CN112552200-A
TI Preparing 4-(1-aminoethyl)benzoate or its derivatives comprises e.g. preparing N-(1-(4-bromophenyl)ethyl)acetamide, reacting with solvent, adding lithium reagent, passing carbon dioxide, adding acid and carrying out esterification.
AU NAN F
   ZHANG Y
   YIN J
   LI P
   WEI P
AE YANTAI MATERIA MEDICA INST (YANT-Non-standard)
GA 2021343560
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing 4-(1-aminoethyl)benzoate or its derivatives (I) or their free amine comprises (i) (a) reacting 1-phenylethanamine (II), first solvent and protective agent in reaction vessel, (b) adding first acid and bromination reagent, reacting, adding first alkali, drying by suction to obtain N-(1-(4-bromophenyl)ethyl)acetamide (III), (ii) (c) reacting (III) and second solvent in reaction vessel, replacing with molecular nitrogen, adding lithium reagent and reacting, (d) passing carbon dioxide gas, adding second acid to quench the reaction, purifying and drying to obtain 4-(1-acetamidoethyl)benzoic acid (IV), (iii) (e) reacting esterification reagent and (IV) in reaction vessel, concentrating, adding first alcohol, adding methyl tertiary ether to precipitate and drying by suction to obtain (I) or (f) reacting esterification reagent and (IV) in reaction vessel, concentrating, adding second alkali, extracting and drying to obtain free amine of (I).
   USE - The method is useful for preparing compound i.e. 4-(1-aminoethyl)benzoate derivative (I) or their free amine.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has low cost, high optical purity, simple operation and easy industrial production; and is environmentally friendly.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing 4-(1-aminoethyl)benzoate or its derivative of formula (I) or their free amine comprises (i) (a) introducing 1-phenylethanamine of formula (II) and the first solvent in a reaction vessel, adding the protective agent dropwise at -10-20 degrees C, reacting until the compound (II) has reacted completely and (b) adding the first acid into the product obtained in step (a), then adding bromination reagent at -10-20 degrees C, reacting for 8-24 hours, adding the first alkali at 5-20 degrees C until the pH of the reaction solution is 7-9, suction filtering the obtained product and drying to obtain N-(1-(4-bromophenyl)ethyl)acetamide of formula (III), (ii) (c) placing the compound (III) obtained in step (i) and the second solvent in a reaction vessel, replacing with molecular nitrogen, adding a lithium reagent dropwise at -40 to -70 degrees C and reacting until the reaction of the compound of formula (III) is complete, (d) controlling the temperature below -40 degrees C, passing carbon dioxide gas until the temperature of the reaction solution is constant, then adding a second acid to quench the reaction, purifying and drying to obtain 4-(1-acetamidoethyl)benzoic acid of formula (IV), (iii) (e) controlling the temperature below 25 degrees C, placing the esterification reagent and the compound of formula (IV) obtained in step (ii) in a reaction vessel, reacting at 50-65 degrees C for 8-24 hours and concentrating the reaction solution to no fractions, then adding the first alcohol, dissolving the solid obtained from the reaction at 40-45 degrees C, add methyl tertiary ether to precipitate the solid and drying by suction to obtain the compound of formula (I) or (f) controlling the temperature below 25 degrees C, placing the esterification reagent and the compound of formula (IV) obtained in step (ii) in a reaction vessel, reacting at 50-65 degrees C for 8-24 hours, then concentrating the reaction solution to no fraction, then adding second base, extracting and drying to obtain the free amine of the compound of formula (I).
   R1 = 1-8C alkyl group.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The configuration of the compound (II) is R configuration, S configuration or racemate. The protective agent is trifluoroacetic anhydride (TFAA) and/or trifluoromethanesulfonic anhydride. In step (a), the molar ratio of the protective agent to the compound (II) is greater than 1. The first acid is MsOH (methanesulfonic acid), trifluoromethanesulfonic acid, benzenesulfonic acid and/or trifluoroacetic acid; the brominating reagent is dibromohydantoin, N-bromosuccinimide (NBS) and/or bromine. In step (i), the molar ratio of the first acid to the compound (II) is 2.9-3.1; the molar ratio of the bromination reagent to the compound (II) is 0.45-0.65. The lithium reagent is N-butyl lithium, sec-butyl lithium, methyl lithium and/or tert-butyl lithium; the second acid is hydrochloric acid, sulfuric acid, trifluoroacetic acid and/or methanesulfonic acid. In step (c), the molar ratio of the lithium reagent to the compound (III) is 2-2.4. The esterification reagent is a mixture of thionyl chloride and 1-8C alkyl alcohol. The molar ratio of the thionyl chloride to the compound (IV) in the esterification reagent is 2.8-3.2.
DC E14 (Aromatics - i.e. containing at least one benzene ring.)

PT P
PN CN111286430-A
TI Producing cold-resistant wine grapes involves            de-stalking the stems of mature cold-resistant wine            grapes and crushing to obtain a mixture of grape skin            and juice, filling the bottom tank with CO2 before            filling, adding carbon dioxide gas.
AU HOU M
AE SHANGRI QINHUANGDAO WINE CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 202056474N
AB    NOVELTY - Producing cold-resistant wine grapes involves                de-stalking the stems of mature cold-resistant wine                grapes and crushing to obtain a mixture of grape                skin and juice. The bottom of the tank fill with                CO2 before filling, after filling, add carbon                dioxide (CO2) gas to the surface of the mixture of                grape skin and juice. The surface layer of the                mixture of grape skin and juice is put under a CO2                gas protective layer with a thickness of 7-13 cm.                The mixture of grape skin and juice is immersed at                10&#176; C for 24 hours and then heated to                15&#176; C. The fermentation strain is added to                the mixture of grape skin and juice and circulate                uniformly to obtain fermentation broth. The                fermentation temperature control between                22-26&#176; C after the alcohol fermentation                period begins. The malic acid content is not higher                than 0.05 g/L, filter when the specific gravity of                the fermentation broth drops to between 990-1000                g/L and no longer changes.
   USE - Method for producing cold-resistant wine                grapes.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to produce cold-resistant                wine grapes is clear and transparent, elegant wine                aroma, rich fruit wood aroma, thick taste, long                aftertaste, and excellent style.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Producing cold-resistant wine grapes involves                de-stalking the stems of mature cold-resistant wine                grapes and crushing to obtain a mixture of grape                skin and juice. The bottom of the tank fill with                CO2 before filling, after filling, add carbon                dioxide (CO2) gas to the surface of the mixture of                grape skin and juice. The surface layer of the                mixture of grape skin and juice is put under a CO2                gas protective layer with a thickness of 7-13 cm.                The mixture of grape skin and juice is immersed at                10&#176; C for 24 hours and then heated to                15&#176; C. The fermentation strain is added to                the mixture of grape skin and juice and circulate                uniformly to obtain fermentation broth. The                fermentation temperature control between                22-26&#176; C after the alcohol fermentation                period begins. The malic acid content is not higher                than 0.05 g/L, filter when the specific gravity of                the fermentation broth drops to between 990-1000                g/L and no longer changes. The grape skin and                original wine is Separately obtain, and press the                grape skin to obtain pressed wine. The press wine                is incorporated into the original wine and the free                SO2 of the original wine is adjusted to 30-40 mg/L.                The upper parts of the original wine is separate                and continue to store after storing the original                wine for 60 days. The upper clear original wine was                separated again after 120 days. The                color-protecting tannin is added to the original                wine that has been separated twice, and after                circular treatment, let stand for 24 hours. The                gumming treatment is carry out, after 15 days of                gumming treatment, filter to remove the                precipitate, and then get the clarified original                wine. The potassium potassium tartrate crystals are                added at a ratio of 500 mg/L to the clarified raw                wine obtained in the above step, stir and freeze to                -6-15&#176; C and incubate for 7 days, filter                and fill.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Component: The cold-resistant wine                  grapes are selected from Oshan hybrid grapes. The                  fermentation strain is ES488 yeast, and the ES488                  yeast addition concentration is 150-200 g/T.                  Preferred Composition: The sugar content of the                  cold-resistant wine grape is 220-230 g/L, and the                  total acid is 5.5-6.0 g/L. The sulfur dioxide                  (SO2), pectinase, and color-protecting tannin are                  alternately added in the process of removing                  stems until the final concentration of SO2 in the                  mixture is 60-80 mg/L, the final concentration of                  pectinase is 20-30 mg/L, and the final                  concentration of color protection tannin is                  80-100 mg/L. Preferred Conditions: A closed cycle                  is carried out every 6 hours, the malic acid                  concentration is monitored once every 24 hours,                  and the specific gravity is monitored once every                  4 hours in the fermentation process. The method                  includes when the specific gravity of the                  fermentation broth is reduced to 1080 g/L and                  1050 g/L, after adding color protection tannin                  according to the ratio of 80-100 mg/L, each of                  them will be subjected to an open cycle. The                  method includes when the alcohol fermentation                  period starts 12 hours, add lactic acid bacteria                  to the fermentation broth at a ratio of 5-10 g/T                  and do a closed cycle. The method includes in the                  gumming process, egg white powder is used and                  added to the original wine at a ratio of 60-80                  mg/L. The filtration method of the clarified raw                  wine is diatomite cross-flow filtration.
DC D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.)

PN CN111003722-A; CN111003722-B
TI Preparing active calcium carbonate comprises e.g.            calcining limestone, digesting the quicklime and water,            reacting, transferring resultant lime slurry into            carbonation reaction tower, adjusting temperature,            adding sucrose and recovering gas.
AU LIANG L
   ZHU Y
   YANG A
   FANG G
   WEN Q
   HUANG H
   FAN Y
AE ANHUI XUANCHENG HUANA NEW MATERIAL TECHN (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 202032314A
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing active calcium carbonate comprises                e.g. (i) calcining limestone, digesting the                quicklime and water and reacting to obtain lime                slurry, (ii) transferring lime slurry into                carbonation reaction tower, adjusting the                temperature, adding sucrose, recovering the gas in                lime kiln to start the carbonation reaction and                terminating carbonation reaction to obtain calcium                carbonate suspension, (iii) transferring calcium                carbonate suspension to the carbonation reaction                tank containing thermal insulation jacket, adding                sodium salt and saccharide complex morphology                modifying agent, heating, stirring and passing low                concentration carbon dioxide gas and adding lime                slurry to obtain large cube-type calcium carbonate                suspension and (iv) transferring calcium carbonate                suspension obtained in step (iii) to treatment                tank, heating, adding sodium stearate treatment                agent for surface treatment modification and                stirring.
   USE - The active calcium carbonate is useful for                high-filling two-component silicone adhesive                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: combines heating and aging with                low-speed carbonation which exhibits synergistic                effect and thus shortens the aging time, improves                production efficiency; recovers carbonated tail                gas; reduces carbon dioxide emissions and reduces                energy consumption; and has low production                cost.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing active calcium carbonate comprises                (i) calcining the limestone at 1100-1300 degrees C                for 300-500 minutes to obtain quicklime, and then                digesting the quicklime and water and reacting to                obtain lime slurry, passing lime slurry through a                100-mesh sieve to remove the waste residue,                adjusting the solid content of the lime slurry to                10-13 wt.% and allowing to stand, (ii) transferring                resulting lime slurry into the carbonation reaction                tower, start the refrigeration cycle, adjusting the                temperature of the lime slurry to 25-30 degrees C,                adding 0.01 wt.% sucrose (based on the mass of lime                slurry) and recovering the gas in the lime kiln to                start the carbonation reaction, when the pH of the                reaction system to 7.5, then stop aeration and                terminating the carbonation reaction to obtain                calcium carbonate suspension, (iii) transferring                the calcium carbonate suspension to the carbonation                reaction tank containing the thermal insulation                jacket, adding the sodium salt and saccharide                complex morphology modifying agent, then heating                the suspension at 55-70 degrees C, then turn on the                low speed stirring and while passing low                concentration carbon dioxide gas within 30-90                minutes and adding 5-10 wt.% lime slurry with the                mass of calcium carbonate suspension to obtain                large cube-type calcium carbonate suspension with                particle size and morphology, where the addition                amount of the saccharide complex morphology                modifying agent is 0.5-2% mass of calcium                carbonate, and (iv) transferring the calcium                carbonate suspension obtained in step (iii) to the                treatment tank, heating slurry at 55-70 degrees C,                adding1.5-3 wt.% sodium stearate treatment agent                (based on the mass of calcium carbonate) for                surface treatment modification, then continuously                stirring, finishing the surface treatment for 30-60                minutes; dehydratingthe surface-treated calcium                carbonate slurry, drying and crushing to obtain                activated calcium carbonate having a specific                surface area of 7-12 m2/g.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The digestion reaction                  quicklime and water are digested according to the                  quicklime/water mass ratio of 1:8-12. In step                  (ii): the volume concentration of carbon dioxide                  in the recovery gas of the lime kiln is 30-35                  wt.%, and the specific surface area of the                  calcium carbonate suspension is 20-25 m2/g. In                  step (iii): the sodium includes sodium phosphate,                  sodium acetate and/or sodium oxalates, and the                  saccharide complex includes glucose and/or                  sucrose. The mass ratio between sodium salt and                  saccharide complex is 3-1:1. In step (iii): the                  low-concentration carbon dioxide gas is tail gas                  recovered from the carbonation reaction tower,                  and its volume concentration is 5-15%. Preferred                  Method: The stirring speed of steps (ii) and (iv)                  is 1000-3000 revolutions/minute, and the stirring                  speed of step (iii) is 100-150                  revolutions/minute. The drying is carried out at                  a temperature of 110-125 degrees C for 24-48                  hours.
