PN CN111000017-A
TI Flavored dark chocolate contains cocoa butter,            cocoa powder, vegetable oil, fruit pulp, lactose,            honey, probiotics, chitosan, tea polyphenols, nut            granules, oat, lecithin, beta-sitosterol preparation            and gamma-oryzanol preparation.
AU XU M
   LONG Z
AE SIXIAN GANZILUO FOOD CO LTD (SIXI-Non-standard)
GA 2020323983
AB    NOVELTY - Flavored dark chocolate and its preparation                method, are claimed. The flavored dark chocolate                comprises 500 pts. wt. cocoa butter, 100-200 pts.                wt. cocoa powder, 5-15 pts. wt. vegetable oil, 3-8                pts. wt. immobilized lipase, 10-50 pts. wt. fruit                pulp, 5-25 pts. wt. petals, 10-55 pts. wt. lactose,                15-60 pts. wt. honey, 1-10 pts. wt. compressed                carbon dioxide, 3-8 pts. wt. probiotics, 1-4 pts.                wt. chitosan, 3-12 pts. wt. tea polyphenols, 10-30                pts. wt. nut granules, 5-15 pts. wt. oat, 2-6 pts.                wt. lecithin, 0.1-0.6 pts. wt. beta -sitosterol                preparation and 0.1-0.5 pts. wt. gamma -oryzanol                preparation.
   USE - As flavored dark chocolate.
   ADVANTAGE - The flavored dark chocolate has abundant                nutritional content, a crisp and refreshing taste                before entrance and smooth and soft waxy taste                after entrance, where the inclusion of pulp,                flowers and nuts in the dark chocolate gives the                chocolate a crisp taste.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Flavored dark chocolate and its preparation                method, are claimed. The flavored dark chocolate                comprises 500 pts. wt. cocoa butter, 100-200 pts.                wt. cocoa powder, 5-15 pts. wt. vegetable oil, 3-8                pts. wt. immobilized lipase, 10-50 pts. wt. fruit                pulp, 5-25 pts. wt. petals, 10-55 pts. wt. lactose,                15-60 pts. wt. honey, 1-10 pts. wt. compressed                carbon dioxide, 3-8 pts. wt. probiotics, 1-4 pts.                wt. chitosan, 3-12 pts. wt. tea polyphenols, 10-30                pts. wt. nut granules, 5-15 pts. wt. oat, 2-6 pts.                wt. lecithin, 0.1-0.6 pts. wt. beta -sitosterol                preparation and 0.1-0.5 pts. wt. gamma -oryzanol                preparation. The flavored dark chocolate is                prepared by (a) cleaning the fruit, then naturally                drying, then removing the fruit peel and pulp,                cutting, separating pulp, drying until the moisture                content reaches 15-40% under vacuum condition and                obtaining baked pulp, (b) after cleaning the                leaves, drying until the moisture content reaches                5-15%, crushing, drying in oven and obtaining petal                powder, (c) uniformly mixing the cocoa butter,                vegetable oil and immobilized lipase, stirring at                50-75 degrees C for 2-3 hours, then filtering to                remove the immobilized lipase, then adding chitosan                and tea polyphenol, and stirring at 40-60 degrees C                for 0.5-2 hours to obtain mixture 1, (d) then                mixing and stirring the obtained mixture 1 with                cocoa powder, lactose and honey at 55-65 degrees C                for 3-5 hours, then adding the baked pulp, petal                powder, probiotics, nut grnaules, oat, lecithin,                beta -sitosterol preparation and gamma -oryzanol                preparation, continuously stirring for 1-3 hours,                then cooling to 40-45 degrees C, adding compressed                carbon dioxide and stirring at 150-250                revolutions/minute (rpm) for 5-15 minutes to obtain                chocolate paste, and (e) pouring the chocolate                paste on a marble plate, using scraper to shovel                the material, reducing the temperature to 30-35                degrees C, then maintaining in a water bath,                heating at 30-35 degrees C for 10-15 minutes,                hardening at 5-8 degrees C after casting and                vibrating the mold to obtain product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Composition: The flavored dark                  chocolate comprises 500 pts. wt. cocoa butter,                  120-180 pts. wt. cocoa powder, 8-12 pts. wt.                  vegetable oil, 5-6 pts. wt. immobilized lipase,                  15-45 pts. wt. fruit pulp, 8-20 pts. wt. petals,                  15-40 pts. wt. lactose, 20-45 pts. wt. honey, 3-8                  pts. wt. compressed carbon dioxide, 4.2-6.8 pts.                  wt. probiotics, 2-3 pts. wt. chitosan, 5-10 pts.                  wt. tea polyphenols, 15-25 pts. wt. nut granules,                  8-12 pts. wt. oat, 2-5 pts. wt. lecithin, 0.2-0.5                  pts. wt. beta -sitosterol preparation and 0.2-0.5                  pts. wt. gamma -oryzanol preparation. Preferred                  Components: The vegetable oil is chosen from                  coconut oil, walnut oil, olive oil, tea seed oil,                  linseed oil, sunflower oil, hemp oil and castor                  oil. The fruit pulp is chosen from apple pulp,                  peach pulp, dragon fruit pulp, grapefruit pulp,                  banana pulp, hawthorn pulp, plum fruit pulp, plum                  fruit, strawberry pulp, pineapple pulp, grape                  pulp, mango pulp, Chinese goosebeery pulp,                  mangosteen pulp, avocado pulp and cherry fruit                  pulp. The petals are chosen from Osmanthus flower                  petals, peony flower petals, Magnolia flower                  petals, pear flower petals, petals of rose,                  jasmine flower petals, peach blossom petals,                  Chrysanthemum flower petals, Wisteria flower                  petals, tea flower petals, Begonia flower petals,                  orchid flower petals, chicken's crown flower                  petals, plum blossom petals, honeysuckle flower                  petals and Gardenia flower petals. The probiotic                  is chosen from Bacillus, Clostridium butyricum,                  Lactobacillus, Bifidobacterium, Actinomyces and                  yeast. The nut granules are chosen from crushed                  walnut, crushed hazelnut, crushed pistachio,                  crushed sunflower seed, crushed thunder fruit,                  crushed chestnut, crushed almond, crushed cashew                  nut, crushed pine nut, crushed peanut and crushed                  Macadamia nut. Preferred Method: The step (a)                  involves drying at 40-75 degrees C under vacuum                  degree of -20-5 Pa for 1-3 hours. The step (b)                  involves drying at 40-75 degrees C for 1-3                  hours.

PN CN110771503-A; CN110771503-B
TI Sugar-free cultivation and transplanting method of            sweet potato virus-free seedling by detoxifying tissue            culture of sweet potato, cutting root, transplanting to            box with vermiculite, adding nutrient solution,            incubating and spraying water.
AU XIE H
   WANG L
   XIE X
   LI J
   WU Y
   WANG M
   ZHANG H
   WU Z
AE SHANXI ACAD AGRIC SCI COTTON RES INST (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2020167966
AB    NOVELTY - Sugar-free cultivation and transplanting                method of sweet potato virus-free seedling,                involves (i) detoxifying tissue culture of sweet                potato in test tube, multiplying virus-free                seedling, selecting virus-free seedling, cutting                old root, cutting stem into section, transplanting                to sugar-free culture box containing vermiculite,                pouring nutrient solution, transplanting, covering                box cover and closing with parafilm, (ii) placing                on culture rack in closed room, subjecting carbon                dioxide to culture box to open the ventilation                holes of the culture box, (iii) allowing light                intensity of 80 mu mol/m2/second from 3rd day and                allowing light intensity of 160 mu mol/m2/second                from 8th day, (iv) supplement nutrient solution in                time according to the growth status of virus-free                vaccines, (v) after 25 days incubation in the                culture box, opening the lid and carrying out                refining transition under natural condition,                spraying water and moving to greenhouse, and (vi)                taking out seedling and transplanting.
   USE - Sugar-free cultivation and transplanting                method of sweet potato virus-free seedling.
   ADVANTAGE - The method satisfies the nutrition requirement                for the growth of tissue culture seedling,                simplifies the procedure, reduces production cost,                maintains the permeability of the matrix which                facilitates nutrient absorption and respiration of                the root of tissue culture seedlings, and provides                excellent the growth and rooting of tissue culture                seedling.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Sugar-free cultivation and transplanting                method of sweet potato virus-free seedling,                involves (i) detoxifying tissue culture of sweet                potato in test tube, multiplying virus-free                seedling, selecting virus-free seedling to grow for                45-55 days, cutting off old root, cutting stem into                4 cm sections, transplanting to sugar-free culture                box containing 670 g vermiculite in each box,                pouring 800 ml nutrient solution, transplanting 70                seedlings per box, covering the box cover and                closing the gap between box cover and box body with                a parafilm, where the nutrient solution comprises 2                g/l calcium ammonium nitrate, 170 mg/l                monopotassium phosphate and remaining of deionized                water, (ii) after transplanting, placing on culture                rack in a closed room, subjecting carbon dioxide to                the culture box to open the ventilation holes of                the culture box, and gradually increasing the                amount of carbon dioxide with the growth of the                culture period, (iii) allowing light intensity of                80 mu mol/m2/second from 3rd day, allowing light                intensity of 160 mu mol/m2/second from 8th day and                providing the light for 12 hours every day, where                no light is used for the first 2 days of the                culture period, (iv) supplement nutrient solution                in time according to the growth status of                virus-free vaccines, (v) after 25 days incubation                in the culture box, opening the lid and carrying                out refining transition under natural condition                with light intensity of 80 mu mol/m2/second,                spraying water based on wetness and dryness of the                vermiculite on the 3rd day, and moving to                greenhouse on 4th day, and (vi) taking out the                tissue culture seedling from the culture box and                transplanting to greenhouse soil.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Composition: In step (2), the                  highest value of the carbon dioxide concentration                  in the closed room is 1500 parts per million, and                  the lowest value is 1000 parts per million. In                  step (iv), the soil humidity in the greenhouse is                  13-14%, and the environmental humidity is more                  than 75%. Preferred Conditions: In step (ii), the                  carbon dioxide gas supply is not performed in the                  first 4 days culture period, the carbon dioxide                  gas supply amount is 300-600 ml/minute on the 5th                  day, the carbon dioxide gas supply amount is 2000                  ml/minute from 10th day, and the carbon dioxide                  gas supply time is same as the time of giving                  light. In step (vi), after the tissue culture                  seedlings are transplanted to the soil of the                  greenhouse, setting up a small plastic arch shed                  and when the temperature inside the greenhouse is                  higher than 32 degrees C, covering 50% shade                  nets. In the method, after transplanting to the                  soil in the greenhouse on the 5th to 7th day,                  punching holes in the small plastic arch shed to                  make 3 round holes with a diameter of 1 cm/m2,                  and gradually increasing the round holes.
DC C06 (Biotechnology - including plant genetics and veterinary vaccines.); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); P13 (Plant culture, dairy products (A01G, H, J).)

      CN110199827-A   GUIZHOU ZHENAN PHARM CO LTD (GUIZ-Non-standard)   LI H
      CN110622863-A   UNIV FUJIAN AGRIC & FORESTRY (UYFN)   CHEN X, CHEN G, NUERLA N, HUANG X, BI C, YANG Z, LIN S
   CN110771503-B
      CN108967192-A   SHANGQIU ACAD AGRIC & FORESTRY SCI (SHAN-Non-standard)   WANG W, HU Q, CHU F, LIU Y, YANG A
      CN110199827-A   GUIZHOU ZHENAN PHARM CO LTD (GUIZ-Non-standard)   LI H

PN CN110656343-A; CN110656343-B
TI Preparation of gypsum involves placing cation            exchange membrane in electrolytic cell, dividing into            anode and cathode region, adding weakly acidic sodium            salt solution, adding cathode and anode            electrocatalyst, oxidizing and crystallizing.
AU LU Z
AE CHENGDU ZICHEN ENERGY STORAGE CHEM EQUIP CO              LTD (CHEN-Non-standard)
   WANG Y (WANG-Individual)
GA 202007551U
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of gypsum using Glauber's salt and                limestone by proton-coupled electron transfer                (PCET) reaction involves placing cation exchange                membrane in electrolytic cell, dividing                electrolytic cell into anode and cathode region,                adding weakly acidic sodium salt solution, sodium                carbonate or sodium hydroxide, adding compound (Q)                as cathode electrocatalyst, adding compound (QH) as                anode electrocatalyst, applying direct current                power source between anode and cathode, where when                producing soda ash, carbon dioxide passed in                cathode region is ionized as hydrogen ion and                carbonate ion under action of current to form                compound (QH), and when producing caustic soda,                compound (Q) in catholyte combines with hydrogen                ion ionized by water to form compound (QH),                oxidizing compound (QH), charging weakly acidic                sodium salt solution, passing sodium ions through                cation exchange membrane, evaporating,                crystallizing to obtain solid caustic soda or soda                ash and producing high-purity gypsum using                anolyte.
   USE - Preparation of gypsum.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of gypsum using Glauber's salt and                limestone by proton-coupled electron transfer                (PCET) reaction involves placing cation exchange                membrane in electrolytic cell, dividing                electrolytic cell into anode region and cathode                region, adding weakly acidic sodium salt solution                as anolyte in anode region, adding sodium carbonate                or sodium hydroxide as catholyte, adding compound                (Q) with PCET reaction performance as cathode                electrocatalyst in cathode region, adding compound                (QH) with PCET reaction performance as anode                electrocatalyst in anode region, applying direct                current power source between anode and cathode,                where when producing soda ash, carbon dioxide                passed in cathode region is ionized as hydrogen ion                and carbonate ion under action of current, hydrogen                ion is combined with compound (Q) at cathode to                form compound (QH), and solution rich in carbonate                is formed in catholyte and when producing caustic                soda, compound (Q) in catholyte combines with                hydrogen ion ionized by water to form compound (QH)                and solution rich in hydroxide ion is formed in                catholyte, oxidizing compound (QH) in anode region                to compound (Q) on anode to release hydrogen ion,                where anolyte forms acid solution rich in hydrogen                ion, charging weakly acidic sodium salt solution                into anode region, passing sodium ions through                cation exchange membrane to reach cathode region                and combine with hydroxide ions or carbonate ions                to form sodium hydroxide solution or sodium                carbonate solution, when large amount of weak acid                solution is generated, evaporating, crystallizing                to obtain solid caustic soda or soda ash and                producing high-purity gypsum using anolyte while                regenerating weakly acidic sodium salt solution by                dissolution and precipitation processes. The                dissolution process involves reacting anolyte                containing large amount of weak acid with                limestone, where divalent calcium is generated                while hydrogen ion is being consumed continuously,                and thereby achieving rapid dissolution of calcium                carbonate. The precipitation process involves                combining divalent calcium generated with sulfate                ion after Glauber's salt to form high-purity                calcium sulfate precipitate and combining sodium                ion in Glauber's salt with weak acid ions in                anolyte to regenerate weakly acidic sodium salt to                anode region, where when product is soda ash,                carbon dioxide released during dissolution process                is recycled to cathode region for production of                soda ash.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The Glauber's salt is                  passed through the sodium sulfate solution to                  participate in the reaction. The method                  preferably involves passing limestone and the                  weak acid solution generated by electrolysis into                  a dissolving stirred tank to carry out the acid                  dissolution reaction of the limestone. Carbon                  dioxide in the dissolved solution is used for the                  production of soda ash or other uses after                  washing and compression processes. The method                  preferably involves generating calcium-rich                  solution by acid-soluble reaction between                  limestone and the sodium sulfate solution after                  the Glauber's salt is passed into a precipitation                  reaction kettle, generating calcium sulfate solid                  in the precipitation reaction kettle, performing                  solid-liquid separation of the generated calcium                  sulfate precipitate and the weakly acidic sodium                  salt solution, purifying regenerated weakly                  acidic sodium salt solution by brine, passing                  into the electrolytic cell to continue the                  reaction to obtain calcium sulfate precipitate as                  a by-product, drying and removing water. The                  sodium carbonate solution generated in the                  cathode region is further converted into a heavy                  sodium carbonate product by evaporation and                  crystallization, calcination of monohydrate                  alkali, and converting cold alkali. The sodium                  hydroxide solution generated in the cathode                  region can be directly used as a liquid alkali                  product, or it can be further transformed into a                  solid alkali product by evaporation.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The weakly acidic                  sodium salt solution is one of sodium acetate,                  sodium formate, sodium oxalate, and sodium                  citrate. The PCET reaction preferably involves                  dissociating compound (QH) to compound (Q),                  hydrogen ions and an electron or associating                  compound (QH) to compound (Q), hydrogen ions and                  an electron to form compound (QH), where the                  compound (QH) and the compound (Q) are compounds                  capable of undergoing a PCET reaction, compound                  (Q) is an oxidation state, and compound (QH) is a                  reduced state. When the oxidized compound (QH) in                  the anode region reacts with the reduced compound                  (Q), extraction-back extraction is carried out                  through dichloromethane, chloroform, carbon                  tetrachloride, ethyl acetate, kerosene, ionic                  liquid methylimidazolium hexafluorophosphate,                  trioctylphosphine oxide or dialysis membrane and                  nanofiltration membrane retention treatment is                  performed to exchange cathode and anode                  electrocatalyst and maintain continuous and                  stable electrochemical reaction. The method                  preferably involves washing and drying calcium                  sulfate precipitate obtained in the anode region                  at 50-150 degrees C to obtain a high-purity                  gypsum product. Preferred Component: The anode                  and the cathode are both a carbon material                  electrode or a porous electrode or a                  three-dimensional structure electrode made of a                  carbon material. The carbon material electrode is                  one of graphite felt, carbon felt, carbon paper,                  and carbon cloth. Preferred Composition: The                  molar ratio of limestone, calcium carbonate and                  weak acid in the anolyte is 1:(2-1):0.5.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The compound (Q) is                  chosen from polycylic compounds of formulae                  (a)-(t).R = H, methyl, ethyl, hydroxyl, sulfonato,                  carboxy, polyethylene glycol, imidazolyl, amino,                  chloro or bromo.
DC A85 (Electrical applications.); A25 (Polyurethanes; polyethers.); J03 (Electrochemical processes and electrophoresis - including ozone production, brine electrolysis, water electrolysis, production of chemical compounds and non-metallic elements, but excluding batteries or other means of producing power and the treatment of metals (C25B).)

PN US2019263734-A1; US10538465-B2
TI Oxidative dehydrogenation of lower alkane into corresponding alkene comprises e.g. contacting, in oxidative dehydrogenation reactor, passing the product stream through quench tower and passing the product stream through amine wash.
AU SERHAL K
   SIMANZHENKOV V
   GOODARZNIA S
   GENT D
   WILLIAMSON M
AE NOVA CHEM INT SA (NVCH-C)
GA 201974784U
AB    NOVELTY - Oxidative dehydrogenation of lower alkane into corresponding alkene comprises: e.g. (i) contacting, in oxidative dehydrogenation reactor, optionally in presence of inert diluent, oxygen containing gas and lower alkane containing gas, with oxidative dehydrogenation catalyst to produce product stream comprising corresponding alkene, and possibly (a) unreacted lower alkane, (b) oxygen, (c) inert diluent, (d) carbon oxides, (e) oxygenates, including acetic acid, acrylic acid and maleic acid, and (f) water; (ii) passing the product stream from (i) through quench tower to remove oxygenates and water from the product stream; (iii) passing the product stream from (ii) through amine wash to remove carbon dioxide from the product stream; (iv) passing the product stream from (iii) through dryer to remove water; and (v) passing the product stream from (iv) through distillation tower to separate overhead stream comprising C1 hydrocarbons and any other compounds lighter than C2/C2+ hydrocarbons.
   USE - The process is useful for oxidative dehydrogenation of a lower alkane into a corresponding alkene (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The process is cost-effective, safety concerns and reduces emission of greenhouse gases.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Oxidative dehydrogenation of a lower alkane into a corresponding alkene, comprises: (i) contacting, in at least one oxidative dehydrogenation reactor, optionally in the presence of an inert diluent, an oxygen containing gas and a lower alkane containing gas, with an oxidative dehydrogenation catalyst to produce a product stream comprising the corresponding alkene, and possibly at least one of (a) unreacted lower alkane, (b) oxygen, (c) inert diluent, (d) carbon oxides, including carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide, (e) oxygenates, including but not limited to, at least one of acetic acid, acrylic acid and maleic acid, and (f) water; (ii) passing the product stream from (i) through a quench tower to remove oxygenates and water from the product stream; (iii) passing the product stream from (ii) through an amine wash to remove carbon dioxide from the product stream; (iv) passing the product stream from (iii) through a dryer to remove water; (v) passing the product stream from (iv) through a distillation tower to separate an overhead stream comprising C1 hydrocarbons and any other compounds lighter than C2/C2+ hydrocarbons; (vi) passing the overhead stream from (v) and optionally a combustible fuel into one or both of a retentate side and a permeate side of an oxygen separation module comprising an oxygen transport membrane; (vii) introducing atmospheric air into the retentate side allowing for generation of heat by combustion of the overhead stream and the optional fuel within the retentate side to increase the temperature of the oxygen transport membrane to at least 850 degrees C permitting unreacted oxygen from the atmospheric air to cross the oxygen transport membrane into the permeate side producing an oxygen enriched gas on the permeate side and leaving an oxygen depleted air containing combustion products on the retentate side; (viii) expelling the oxygen depleted air from the retentate side through an exhaust on the oxygen separation module; and (x) removing the oxygen enriched gas through an oxygen rich outlet on the oxygen separation module, optionally, the oxygen enriched gas is directed back to (i) as, or a portion of, the oxygen containing gas introduced into the oxidative dehydrogenation reactor.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The process further comprises a caustic wash tower immediately downstream of the amine wash. Preferred Components: The oxygen enriched gas comprises at least 20% oxygen, preferably at least 80% oxygen. The lower alkane is ethane. The ratio of lower alkane containing gas to oxygen containing gas is above the upper explosive limit. The lower alkane containing gas and the oxygen containing gas are mixed in a flooded gas mixer before entry into the oxidative dehydrogenation reactor. The oxidative dehydrogenation catalyst comprises a mixed metal oxide comprising e.g. (i) catalysts of formula (MoaVbTecNbdPdeOf) (I), and (ii) catalysts of formula (NigA1hB1iD1jOf) (II), and (v) catalysts of formula (MoaVrX1sY1tZ1uM1vOf) (III).a-f = relative atomic amounts of the elements Mo, V, Te, Nb, Pd or O;a = 1;b = 0.01-1;c = 0.01-1;d = 0.01-1;e = 0.00-0.1;f = a number to satisfy the valence state of the catalyst;g = 0.1-0.9, preferably 0.6-0.8;h = 0.04-0.9;i = 0-0.5;j = 0-0.5;A1 = Ti, Ta, V, Nb, Hf, W, Y, Zn, Zr, Si and/or Al;B1 = La, Ce, Pr, Nd, Sm, Sb, Sn, Bi, Pb, Tl, In, Te, Cr, Mn, Mo, Fe, Co, Cu, Ru, Rh, Pd, Pt, Ag, Cd, Os, Ir, Au and/or Hg;D1 = Ca, K, Mg, Li, Na, Sr, Ba, Cs and/or Rb;X1 = at least one of Nb or Ta;Y1 = at least one of Sb or Ni;Z1 = at least one of Te, Ga, Pd, W, Bi or Al;M1 = at least one of Fe, Co, Cu, Cr, Ti, Ce, Zr, Mn, Pb, Mg, Sn, Pt, Si, La, K, Ag or In;r = 0.05-1; = 0.001-1;t = 0.001-1;u = 0.001-0.5; andv = 0.001-0.3.The oxidative dehydrogenation reactor comprises at least one reactor of a fixed bed type or a fluidized bed type. The oxidative dehydrogenation reactor comprises at least one swing bed type reactor arrangement. The oxidative dehydrogenation reactor comprises more than one oxidative dehydrogenation reactor, each comprising the same or different oxidative dehydrogenation catalyst, connected in series, and the product stream from each oxidative dehydrogenation reactor except the last oxidative dehydrogenation reactor in the series is fed into a downstream oxidative dehydrogenation reactor, where at least one of the oxidative dehydrogenation reactors comprises a fixed bed type reactor, a fluidized bed type reactor or a swing bed type reactor arrangement. The oxidative dehydrogenation reactor comprises more than one oxidative dehydrogenation reactor connected in parallel and each comprising the same or different dehydrogenation catalyst, where at least one of the oxidative dehydrogenation reactors comprises a fixed bed type reactor, a fluidized bed type reactor or a swing bed type reactor arrangement. The product stream from the oxidative dehydrogenation reactor is passed through at least one heat exchanger immediately upstream of the quench tower to reduce temperature of the product stream.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

TI Processing rapid quantitative senescent cell            detection comprises detecting, culturing, incubating,            heating, centrifuging, and pipetting.
AU WU C
   LIU G
   HE H
   LI J
   YUAN Z
   LI A
AE ANHUI GUJING TRIBUTE WINE CO LTD (AHGJ-C)
   ANHUI RUISIKEER TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (ANHU-Non-standard)
   ANHUI GUJING DISTILLERY CO LTD (AHGJ-C)
GA 201947417Q
AB    NOVELTY - Processing rapid quantitative senescent cell                detection comprises (i) drawing standard curve to                establish a standard curve between the number of                cell senescence and corresponding absorbance at the                maximum absorption wavelength, (ii) detecting of                the analyte and culturing, adding senescent cell                staining solution, incubating without carbon                dioxide, discarding the supernatant, adding                dimethyl sulfoxide, vortexing or heating,                centrifuging, and pipetting.
   USE - The method is useful for processing rapid                quantitative senescent cell detection.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has high reproducibility, high                reliability, strong objectivity, less influence                factors and high speed, and realizes rapid                quantification of aging cells of animals and human                bodies.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Processing rapid quantitative senescent cell                detection comprises (ia), culturing two bottles of                the same density with two identical 25 cm2 cell                culture flasks, respectively, as I and II, fusing                completely, taking I from the incubator for cell                counting, where the result is x, washing the cells                with phosphate-buffered saline buffer for 21-2                times without fixing, (ib) adding 5-8 ml senescent                cell staining solution to the cell II, and                incubating in a 37 degrees C incubator without                carbon dioxide for 48-96 hours, (ic) centrifuging                at 10000-14000 g for 2-10 minutes to obtain a blue                product, discarding the supernatant, adding 300-500                mu l dimethyl sulfoxide to dissolve the blue                product, dissoluting, centrifuging to 10000-14000 g                for 2-10 minutes to ensure the blue product,                dissolving and removing impurities e.g. cell                debris, (id) pipetting 200 mu l the supernatant in                a 96-well plate, measuring the absorbance of the                sample at a wavelength of 200-1000 nm and                recording, where the number of cells corresponding                to the absorbance is y, and (ie) diluting 200 mu l                supernatant measured by doubling with dimethyl                sulfoxide, mixing, utilizing 200 mu l mixture to                determine the absorbance of the sample in the range                of 200-1000 nm, where the number of cells                corresponding to the absorbance is y/2, diluting                and counting, drawing a standard curve according to                the number of cells and absorbing, (iia) detecting                of the analyte, culturing vessel culture cells are                fused to more than 90%, taking out cells tested                from the incubator, washing the cells 2-3 times                with phosphate buffered saline buffer without                fixing, (iib) adding senescent cell staining                solution to the (iia) product, adding senescence                cell staining solution in the same volume as the                medium added when the cells are cultured in the                container, and incubating in a 37 degrees C                incubator without carbon dioxide for 48-96 hours,                (iic) centrifuging 10000-14000 g (iib) product for                2-10 minutes to obtain a blue product, discarding                the supernatant, adding dimethyl sulfoxide to                dissolve the blue product, vortexing or heating to                accelerate the dissolution rate, dissolving,                centrifuging for 2-10 minutes, ensuring the blue                product dissolves sufficiently while removing                impurities, e.g. cell debris and (iid) pipetting                200 mu l the supernatant into a 96-well plate and                scan to obtain an absorption spectrum curve in the                wavelength range of 200-1000 nm, where the                absorbance is linearly related to the number of                senescent cells, and the number of senescent cells                is obtained by comparison with a standard                curve.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The senescent cell                  staining solution is a pharyngal cell senescence                  beta -galactosidase staining solution. Preferred                  Method: The method comprises dissolving blue                  product completely and measuring within 72 hours                  to ensure the accuracy of the measurement. When                  the absorbance was measured, the detected                  wavelength was 640 nm.

TI Welding of thick plate butt comprises opening a single V-shaped groove with a single side, completing the remaining welding of the groove and removing the ceramic liner and removing the arc runner plate.
AU LIU X
   LI L
   WANG Q
   YUAN J
   ZHOU F
AE XIANGTAN ELECTRIC MFG GROUP CO LTD (XEMC-C)
GA 201940772N
AB    NOVELTY - Welding of thick plate butt comprises (i) opening a single V-shaped groove with a single side of more than 8 degrees on the butt plate I and the butt plate II; (ii) providing the arcing plate I and the arcing plate II at the welding starting end of the butting plate I and the butting plate II, providing welding end of the butt plate I and the butt plate II with an arc-receiving plate I and an arc-receiving plate II and connecting the arc striking plate I and the arc striking plate II together through the arc striking connecting plate at the bottom of the groove at the starting end of the welding; (iii) adhering the ceramic liner on the reverse side of the bottom of the V-shaped groove; (iv) completing the remaining welding of the groove; and (v) removing the ceramic liner and removing the arc runner plate I, the arc runner plate II, the arc trap plate I, the arc trap plate II, the arc striking connection plate, the arc connecting plate, and smooth the end portion.
   USE - The method is useful for welding of thick plate butt.
   ADVANTAGE - The method ensures the welding quality of the thick plate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Welding of thick plate butt comprises (i) opening a single V-shaped groove with a single side of more than 8 degrees on the butt plate I and the butt plate II, where the bottom gap between the butt plate I and the butt plate II during the welding is 6-10 mm and the opening angle is more than 16 degrees ; (ii) providing the arcing plate I and the arcing plate II at the welding starting end of the butting plate I and the butting plate II, providing welding end of the butt plate I and the butt plate II with an arc-receiving plate I and an arc-receiving plate II, connecting the arc striking plate I and the arc striking plate II together through the arc striking connecting plate at the bottom of the groove at the starting end of the welding and connecting arc-receiving plate I and the arc-receiving plate II together by the arc-receiving connecting plate at the bottom of the groove at the end of the welding; (iii) adhering the ceramic liner on the reverse side of the bottom of the V-shaped groove, where the first layer at the bottom of the groove is made of carbon dioxide gas shielded welding, the carbon dioxide gas shielded welding torch is a flat type welding torch and the carbon dioxide gas shielded welding speed can maintains the state and arc welding; (iv) completing the remaining welding of the groove; and (v) removing the ceramic liner and removing the arc runner plate I, the arc runner plate II, the arc trap plate I, the arc trap plate II, the arc striking connection plate, the arc connecting plate, and smooth the end portion.

PN CN109077149-A; CN109077149-B
TI Preparing pure instant tea comprises using supercritical carbon dioxide band, ethanol and acetone, extracting pulverized tea leaves to extract tea essential oil, taking water:ethanol:acetone, using composite solvent and extracting the tea.
AU LV H
   ZHANG Q
   LIU J
   LV Y
   ZHANG W
   REN Y
   LI S
   ZHU Y
AE UNIV JIANGXI NORMAL (UYJX-C)
GA 201901694F
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing pure instant tea comprises (i) using supercritical carbon dioxide band, ethanol and acetone, extracting the pulverized tea leaves under pressure and temperature to extract tea essential oil; (ii) taking water:ethanol:acetone, using composite solvent as extracting agent and extracting the tea; (iii) subjecting the material obtained in step (ii) to pressure filtration through dense filter cloth and the filtration method is intermittent filtration or continuous filtration; (iv) centrifuging the filtrate obtained by the step (iii); (v) concentrating the clear solution in two stages by evaporation under reduced pressure of the three-effect film, and adding ethanol to precipitate to obtain molecular impurities; and (vi) mixing the tea essential oil extracted in step (i) with the concentrated liquid obtained in step (v), using spray drying the nozzle diameter is 0.8-1.2 mm, the inlet air temperature is 170 plus minus 5 degrees C and the outlet air temperature is 80 plus minus 5 degrees C.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing pure instant tea.
   ADVANTAGE - The method retains the aroma component of tea and the tea has strong aroma, improves the cold solubility of instant tea, eliminates the problem of secondary precipitation after cold and low temperature preservation, overcomes the taste of instant tea products, the problem of deeper color and improved instant tea yield.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing pure instant tea comprises (i) using supercritical carbon dioxide band 5% ethanol and 5% acetone as extracting agent, extracting the pulverized tea leaves of 10-20 meshes under pressure of 25-30 MPa and temperature 45-50 degrees C for 2-3 hours to extract tea essential oil and the flow rate of the extracting agent is 5-15 l/h/kg tea leaves; (ii) taking water:ethanol:acetone in a volume ratio of 8:1:1, using composite solvent as extracting agent, and extracting the tea treated at a temperature of 70-80 degrees C and pH of 5.5-7.5; (iii) subjecting the material obtained in step (ii) to pressure filtration through dense filter cloth with the operation pressure of 0.05-1 MPa and the filtration method is intermittent filtration or continuous filtration; (iv) centrifuging the filtrate obtained by the step (iii) at a centrifugal force of 3000-15000 g; (v) concentrating the clear solution in two stages by evaporation under reduced pressure of the three-effect film and the first stage is concentrated to 12-15 degrees B, and adding 20-25% ethanol to precipitate to remove the large molecular weight of more than 50000 to obtain molecular impurities and the second stage is concentrated to 22-28 degrees B; and (vi) mixing the tea essential oil extracted in step (i) with the concentrated liquid obtained in step (v), using spray drying the nozzle diameter is 0.8-1.2 mm, the inlet air temperature is 170 plus minus 5 degrees C and the outlet air temperature is 80 plus minus 5 degrees C. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for pure instant tea prepared by the method as mentioned above.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Components: The tea leaves are 30% spring tea with 70% summer tea. The tea leaves are spring tea and autumn tea in a mass ratio of 1:1. Preferred Method: In step (ii) the composite extraction is a secondary cross-flow extraction, the first extraction is to add 10 times the amount of the exacting agent to the tea leaves and the second extraction is to add 5 times the amount of the exacting agent to the tea leaves and the extraction time is 25 minutes each time. The composite extraction is three-stage countercurrent extraction. The ratio of the material to the exacting agent is 1:10 and the extraction time is 25 minutes/grade. In step (iv) the centrifugal treatment is carried out by centrifugation using disc centrifuge to control the centrifugal force to 3000-10000 g. In step (iv) the centrifugal treatment is carried out by centrifugation using tube centrifuge to control the centrifugal force to 3000-10000 g. In step (ii) method comprises when the hot-melt instant tea is prepared the extraction temperature is 75-80 degrees C the pH is 6.5-7.5, when preparing the cold soluble instant tea, when the extraction temperature is 70-75 degrees C the pH is 5.5-6.5. In step (v) the concentration temperature is 55-65 degrees C, when the cold-soluble instant tea is prepared the concentration temperature is 45-50 degrees C.

PN CN108788133-A
TI Preparing testing silver-graphene (sic) comprises e.g. adding graphene into deionized water, ultrasonic treating, adding methanol, ethanol, aqueous sodium hydroxide solution, aqueous ammonia, polar solvent, water and enzyme and reacting.
AU WANG L
AE SHENZHEN ZHONGKE RUIJIN PRECIOUS MATERIAL TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SHEN-Non-standard)
GA 2018933456
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing testing silver-graphene(sic) comprises e.g. adding graphene into deionized water, ultrasonic treating, pouring methanol and ethanol into a homogenizer with a temperature control system, adding aqueous sodium hydroxide solution and aspirate aqueous ammonia, adding polar solvent having dielectric constant of 15-125, water and silver ammonia solution C and ball milling, pouring the suspended portion of the upper layer of the turbid liquid D, filtering out the precipitate, and filtering with a sand filter, introducing the supercritical carbon dioxide, and removing impurities from the solution, mixing tartaric acid and glucose in water, pouring into a mixer with a temperature control system to stirring and dissolving, adding enzyme and allowing to stand, and pouring the mixture E into the washing machine, and washing the mixture B and mixture F alternately during the period to obtain powdery silver-graphene.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing testing silver-graphene(sic).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: utilizes cheap and easily available reaction raw materials; has mild reaction conditions, and high efficiency, stable product performance, conducive to expanding production, and less surface functional groups; does not require high temperature; and is safety, and short in time.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing testing silver-graphene(sic)comprises (i) adding graphene into deionized water, and ultrasonic treating for 1-5 hours to obtain dispersion, where the ratio between graphene and water is 3:0.5 (g:l) to obtain dispersion A, (ii) pouring methanol and ethanol into a homogenizer with a temperature control system, controlling the temperature of the homogenizer at 150-180 degrees C and stirring for 25-40 minutes to obtain mixture B, (iii) adding aqueous sodium hydroxide solution into the aqueous silver nitrate solution, adding aspirate aqueous ammonia while stirring until the solution is clear to obtain silver ammonia solution C, (iv) adding the dispersion A into the ball mill tank, then adding polar solvent having dielectric constant of 15-125, water and silver ammonia solution C and ball milling to obtain turbid liquid D, (v) pouring the suspended portion of the upper layer of the turbid liquid D, filtering out the precipitate, and filtering with a sand filter, introducing the supercritical carbon dioxide into the sand filter for 5 minutes every 20-40 minutes during the filtration period, and removing impurities from the solution to obtain mixture E, (vi) mixing tartaric acid and glucose in water, pouring into a mixer with a temperature control system to stirring and dissolving, controlling the stirring temperature at 40-60 degrees C, and the stirring is rapid for 3-5 minutes, then adding enzyme and allowing to stand for 3-5 days to obtain mixed solution F, and (vi) pouring the mixture E into the washing machine, and washing the mixture B and mixture F alternately during the period to obtain powdery silver-graphene, where the testing silver-graphene(sic) comprises 10-18 pts. wt. enzyme, 50-150 pts. wt. graphene, 5-35 pts. wt. sodium hydroxide, 1-8 pts. wt. silver nitrate, 3-15 pts. wt. aqueous ammonia, 2-40 pts. wt. tartaric acid, 1-38 pts. wt. glucose, 5-40 pts. wt. ethanol, 1-9 pts. wt. carbon dioxide, 3-18 pts. wt. methanol and 2-9 pts. wt. deionized water.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The testing silver-graphene(sic) comprises 15 pts. wt. enzyme, 100 pts. wt. graphene, 20 pts. wt. sodium hydroxide, 5 pts. wt. silver nitrate, 10 pts. wt. aqueous ammonia, 20 pts. wt. tartaric acid, 20 pts. wt. glucose, 20 pts. wt. ethanol, 5 pts. wt. carbon dioxide, 10 pts. wt. methanol and 6 pts. wt. deionized water. Preferred Components: The mass ratio between silver nitrate and sodium hydroxide is 1:0.4-1, the concentration of the silver nitrate aqueous solution is 1-10 wt.%, the concentration of the aqueous sodium hydroxide solution is 1-5 wt.%, and the concentration of the aqueous ammonia is 1-5 wt.%. The weight ratio between glucose, ethanol and carbon dioxide is 1-38:5-40:5-40. The weight ratio between silver nitrate, aqueous ammonia and tartaric acid is 1-8:3-15, 2-40. Preferred Method: In the (vi), the stirring temperature is controlled at 55 degrees C, and the mixture is rapidly stirred for 4 minutes to obtain mixed solution F. In the (ii), the temperature of the homogenizer is controlled at 165 degrees C and stirred for 35 minutes.

PN CN108545968-A
TI Production of lime and aluminum hydroxide from waste involves reacting generated carbon dioxide with sodium tetrahydroxyaluminate solution, and subjecting precipitated aluminum hydroxide to modified drying treatment.
AU LI S
   PENG Q
   WEI S
   LIN B
   XIAO J
   ZHENG F
   LEE J
   LIN J
AE FUJIAN DINGSHENGYUAN ENVIRONMENTAL TECHN (FUJI-Non-standard)
GA 2018766186
AB    NOVELTY - Production of lime and aluminum hydroxide from waste involves calcining white mud or recovered calcium carbonate to obtain quicklime; mixing aluminum ash, waste alkali liquor containing 20% alkali or recovered alkali to obtain mixture; subjecting mixture to solid-liquid separation to obtain sodium tetrahydroxyaluminate solution; transporting sodium tetrahydroxyaluminate solution in carbon separation tank; reacting carbon dioxide generated in rotary kiln calcination with sodium tetrahydroxyaluminate solution to obtain 20% sodium carbonate solution and precipitated aluminum hydroxide; taking out precipitated aluminum hydroxide for modified drying treatment to obtain active aluminum hydroxide; transferring sodium carbonate solution to second reaction kettle and mixing with quicklime; sending mixture to second filter press for solid-liquid separation to obtain precipitated calcium carbonate and 20% sodium hydroxide solution.
   USE - Production of lime and aluminum hydroxide from waste.
   ADVANTAGE - The method utilizes wastes including white mud, waste alkali and aluminum ash to generate quicklime and aluminum hydroxide, thus converting waste into valuable product to control pollution and improve environment, while creating beautiful environment for people and promoting ecological and harmonious environment between man and nature.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Production of lime and aluminum hydroxide from waste comprises taking 0.9-1.1 pts. wt. white mud or recovered calcium carbonate and putting it into rotary kiln; calcining to obtain 0.52-0.60 pts. wt. quicklime; taking 0.9-1.1 pts. wt. aluminum ash, 6.5-7.5 pts. wt. waste alkali liquor containing 20% alkali or recovered alkali and transporting into first reaction kettle; mixing and stirring; sending mixture in first reactor to first filter press for solid-liquid separation to obtain 0.35-0.42 pts. wt. sodium tetrahydroxyaluminate solution; transporting sodium tetrahydroxyaluminate solution in carbon separation tank; discharging 0.41-0.47 pts. wt. carbon dioxide generated in rotary kiln calcination into carbon separation tank to react with sodium tetrahydroxyaluminate solution to obtain 5.8-7.2 pts. wt. of 20% sodium carbonate solution and 0.05-0.08 pts. wt. precipitated aluminum hydroxide; taking out precipitated aluminum hydroxide for modified drying treatment to obtain active aluminum hydroxide; taking out sodium carbonate solution and transferring it to second reaction kettle; adding quicklime to second reaction vessel; and mixing and stirring to obtain mixture; sending mixture to second filter press for solid-liquid separation to obtain 1.20-1.40 pts. wt. precipitated calcium carbonate and 4.5-5.5 pts. wt. of 20% sodium hydroxide solution, in which precipitated calcium carbonate can be used as recovered calcium carbonate and sodium hydroxide solution can be used as recovered alkali. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for production line utilizing waste to produce lime and aluminum hydroxide comprising quick lime production equipment including rotary kiln and quicklime silo; aluminum hydroxide generating device comprising first reaction vessel that is acid and alkali resistant reaction vessel, first filter press, and carbon separation tank; and recycled material generating apparatus comprising second reaction kettle that is acid and alkali resistant reaction vessel, second filter press, and sodium hydroxide solution silo.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of a process for producing lime and aluminum hydroxide from waste (Drawing includes non-English language text).

PN CN108455627-A
TI Preparing Sc/ZSM-12 molecular sieve material comprises adding ionized water and sodium hydroxide solution into silica sol, magnetic stirring, adding e.g. sodium aluminate and manganese sulfate solution, aging, crystallizing and combusting.
AU 
AE HUIAN CANXIN NEW MATERIAL TECHNOLOGY CO (HUIA-Non-standard)
GA 2018689973
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing Sc/ZSM-12 molecular sieve material comprises ultrasonically processing silica sol and sodium aluminate in an oil bath, processing tetraethylammonium bromide (TEABr), lanthanum nitrate and sodium hydroxide solution in an ultrasonic water bath, adding silica sol into a flask, adding ionized water and sodium hydroxide solution, magnetically stirring, adding sodium aluminate and TEABr template, magnetically stirring, dissolving manganese sulfate in ionic water, adding manganese sulfate solution to the above solution, heating the mixture in a magnetic stirring oil bath to prepare a gel liquid, aging, pouring into a reactor having PTFE lining, placing the reactor in a microwave reactor, heating, crystallizing under nitrogen atmosphere, centrifuging, drying, first time pre-combusting in a muffle furnace, second time pre-combusting, under nitrogen atmosphere at a negative pressure and the third time pre-combusting under argon, carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide atmosphere.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing Sc/ZSM-12 molecular sieve material.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing Sc/ZSM-12 molecular sieve material comprises (a) ultrasonically processing 30 g silica sol and 10 g sodium aluminate in an oil bath at 100 degrees C for 3 hours under ultrasonic vibration, (b) processing 10 ml tetraethylammonium bromide (TEABr), 10 g lanthanum nitrate and 5 ml sodium hydroxide solution (2 mol/l) in an ultrasonic water bath at 100 degrees C for 2 hours to remove impurities, (c) adding silica sol into a flask, then adding 100 ml ionized water and sodium hydroxide solution in a drop wise manner at a rate of 20 drops/minute, magnetically stirring in an oil bath at 100 degrees C for 3 hours, (d) adding sodium aluminate to the above solution, adding tetraethylammonium bromide (TEABr) template in a dropwise manner at a rate of 30 drops per minute, magnetically stirring in an oil bath at 100 degrees C for 2 hours while adding drop wise, (e) dissolving manganese sulfate in 100 ml ionic water, then adding a solution of manganese sulfate to the above solution, heating the mixture in a magnetic stirring oil bath at 100 degrees C for 3 hours to prepare a gel liquid, (f) aging the gel liquid in air for 24 hours, (g) pouring the gel solution into a reactor having PTFE lining through a drainer, placing the reactor in a microwave reactor, boosting to 300 kPa at a rate of 20 kap/minute, heating to 250 degrees C at a rate of 20 degrees C/minute and crystallizing under nitrogen atmosphere for 12 hours, (h) centrifuging in a centrifugal separator, drying, first time pre-combusting in a muffle furnace under 30% carbon dioxide and 70% carbon monoxide atmosphere for 30 minutes at 500 degrees C, second time pre-combusting for 60 minutes, under nitrogen atmosphere at 700 degrees C at a negative pressure of 0.05 MPa, the third time pre-combusting for 50 minutes at 600 degrees C under at 0.05 MPa, at 35% argon, 35% carbon dioxide and 30% carbon monoxide atmosphere.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the Sc/ZSM-12 molecular sieve material.

PN CN108144420-A
TI Recycling device useful for supercritical water oxidation of excess oxygen, comprises energy recycling module connected to the reaction product outlet of the supercritical water oxidation reactor and exhaust port of energy recovery module.
AU CHEN H
   CHEN J
   KANG T
   SHEN J
AE UNIV SHAANXI SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYSK-C)
GA 201847425S
AB    NOVELTY - Recycling device comprises energy recycling module (6) connected to the reaction product outlet of the supercritical water oxidation reactor (3). The exhaust port of the energy recovery module is connected with oxygen molecule and carbon-di-oxide separation modules. The oxygen molecule and carbon-di-oxide separation modules include a regeneration tower (9). A heat exchange device is provided in the regeneration tower, and the inlet of the heat exchange device is connected to the exhaust port of the energy recovery module. A first gas-liquid separator is connected to the outlet of the heat exchange device. An absorption tower is connected to the gas phase outlet of the first gas-liquid separator.
   USE - The recycling device is useful for supercritical water oxidation of excess oxygen (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The device is economical; ensures high purity carbon dioxide product; has simple structure; improves system oxygen recovery rate; saves oxidant consumed.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Recycling device comprises energy recycling module (6) connected to the reaction product outlet of the supercritical water oxidation reactor (3). The exhaust port of the energy recovery module is connected with oxygen molecule and carbon-di-oxide separation modules. The oxygen molecule and carbon-di-oxide separation modules include a regeneration tower (9). A heat exchange device is provided in the regeneration tower, and the inlet of the heat exchange device is connected to the exhaust port of the energy recovery module. A first gas-liquid separator is connected to the outlet of the heat exchange device. An absorption tower is connected to the gas phase outlet of the first gas-liquid separator. A second gas-liquid separator is connected to the liquid-phase outlet of the first gas-liquid separator. A second mixer is connected between the gas phase outlet of the second gas liquid separator and the gas phase outlet of the regeneration tower and is connected to the gas inlet of the second mixer. The liquid phase outlet of the regeneration tower is connected to the feed inlet of the absorption tower via the first solution pump and the heat exchanger. A heat exchanger is connected between the liquid phase outlet of the absorption tower and the nozzle of the regeneration tower. A first mixer is connected to the gas phase outlet of the absorption tower. A first compressor, a first dryer, a membrane separator and an oxygen buffer tank are connected to the exhaust port of the first mixer in sequence. The carbon dioxide outlet of the membrane separator is connected to the inlet of the second mixer. A second compressor, a dryer, a condenser and a rectifying tower are connected to the exhaust port of the second mixer in this order. The gas phase outlet of the rectification column is connected to the oxygen buffer tank, and the liquid phase outlet of the rectification column is connected to the third back pressure valve through the second heat exchanger. A carbon dioxide tank is connected to the outlet of the heat exchanger.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the recycling device for supercritical water oxidation of excess oxygen.
   Supercritical water oxidation reactor (3)
   Energy recycling module (6)
   Regeneration tower (9)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: A first back pressure valve is provided between the energy recovery module and the first gas-liquid separator. A second back pressure valve is connected between the first gas-liquid separator and the second gas-liquid separator. A third back pressure valve is connected between the bottom liquid phase outlet of the rectification column and the second end of the heat exchanger. A waste liquid supply module is connected to the waste liquid inlet of the supercritical water oxidation reactor. An auxiliary heating device is provided in the supercritical water oxidation reactor, and an oxidant supply module is connected to the oxidant inlet of the supercritical water oxidation reactor. A gas-solid separation module is provided between the supercritical water oxidation reactor and the energy recovery module. The solid inorganic salt produced by the supercritical water oxidation reactor is removed by the gas-solid separation module, and the mixed gas from the gas-solid separation module enters the energy recovery module. The gas-solid separation module is provided with a high pressure hydrocyclone. The energy recovery module is provided with a turbine power generation device for recovering pressure energy. In addition, a heat exchanger for recovering heat energy recovers the heat energy of the reaction product through a heat exchanger to preheat the raw material and the oxidant. The first gas-liquid separator is a high-pressure gas-liquid separator. The separator pressure is 4-8 MPa, the temperature is 30-100 degrees C. The second gas-liquid separator is an atmospheric gas-liquid separator. The absorption tower is provided with an ethanolamine solution for absorbing carbon dioxide in the supercritical water oxidation reaction product. The oxidant supply module is provided with an oxidant pressure pump, and the oxidant pressure pump is a high pressure plunger pump or compressor. The auxiliary heating device is an electric heater or a gas stove. The liquid phase outlet of the second gas-liquid separator is connected to the water storage tank. The supercritical water oxidation reactor has a reaction temperature of 375-650 degrees C and a pressure of 23-30 MPa.

PN CN108149131-A
TI Preparing tungsten carbide-silicon carbide particles reinforced heat-resistant gray cast iron comprises e.g. blowing precursor mixed powder of tungsten carbide-silicon carbide particles blown into gray cast iron melt by mixed gas.
AU XU S
   SUN X
   QI Z
AE UNIV SHANDONG JIANZHU (SDSJ-C)
GA 2018473287
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing tungsten carbide-silicon carbide particles reinforced heat-resistant gray cast iron comprises e.g. blowing precursor mixed powder of tungsten carbide-silicon carbide particles blown into gray cast iron melt by mixed gas of carbon dioxide, methane, argon and oxygen and generating self-propagating combustion synthesis reaction during the blowing process, generating ultrafine tungsten carbide-silicon carbide reinforced particles by reaction, adding silver powder, copper powder and tungsten carbide-silicon carbide particle reinforced heat-resistant gray cast iron melt and smelting, mixing mechanically, performing modification treatment, refining, casting ingot by high-pressure torsion and severe plastic deformation, chemical composition of gray cast iron base material comprises carbon, nickel, chromium, phosphorus, sulfur and tin, preparing precursor mixing of tungsten carbide-silicon carbide particles comprises adding tungsten powder and silica powder to absolute ethanol.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing tungsten carbide-silicon carbide particles reinforced heat-resistant gray cast iron.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves strength, elastic modulus, hardness and wear resistance of gray cast iron, and is economical and tungsten carbide-silicon carbide particles reinforced heat-resistant gray cast iron has anti-bacterial property.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing tungsten carbide-silicon carbide particles reinforced heat-resistant gray cast iron comprises blowing precursor mixed powder of tungsten carbide-silicon carbide particles blown into gray cast iron melt by mixed gas of carbon dioxide, methane, argon and oxygen and generating self-propagating combustion synthesis reaction during the blowing process, generating ultrafine tungsten carbide-silicon carbide reinforced particles by reaction, adding silver powder, copper powder and tungsten carbide-silicon carbide particle reinforced heat-resistant gray cast iron melt and smelting at a ratio of 1:99, mixing mechanically and agitating, performing modification treatment, refining, casting ingot by high-pressure torsion and severe plastic deformation; (a) chemical composition of gray cast iron base material comprises 2.6-3% carbon, 1-1.5% nickel, 0.2-0.4% chromium, 0.01-0.05% phosphorus, 0.03-0.15% sulfur and 0.01-0.1% tin; (b) ratio of each component in mixed gas is methane (8-45), argon (8-35), oxygen (8-35) and carbon dioxide (8-70), pressure of mixed gas is 0.05-0.3 MPa and gas flow rate is 0.02-0.15 m3/minutes; (c) preparing precursor mixing of tungsten carbide-silicon carbide particles comprises adding tungsten powder and silica powder to absolute ethanol, stirring uniformly and ultrasonically treating for 5-10 minutes, adding nano-sized activated carbon powder, performing ultrasonication for 10-50 minutes and transferring to crucible, calcining in a vacuum furnace at a high temperature of 1550-1800 degrees C for 3 hours, cooling with furnace and grinding to obtain precursor powder of ultrafine tungsten powder, tungsten carbide powder, silica powder and silicon carbide mixed powder particles having an average particle size of 40-100 nm; (d) adding precursor powder of ultrafine tungsten powder, tungsten carbide powder, silica powder and silicon carbide mixed powder in 5 wt.% of the alloy melt; and (e) adding silver powder and copper powder to hot gray cast iron melt having an average particle size of 40-100 nm obtained by mechanized ball milling for 24 hours.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The gray cast iron base material melt comprises 2.6-3 wt.% carbon, 0.8-1.3 wt.% manganese (Mn), 1-1.5 wt.% Mn element and temperature of gray cast iron melt is 1480-1560 degrees C. Preferred Method: The reaction produces a reinforcing particle for 15-60 minutes, and higher the particle content requirement, longer is the reaction that produces reinforcing particles. The method comprises adding silver powder and copper powder to smelting tungsten carbide-silicon carbide particle reinforced hot gray cast iron material melt, where addition ratio of silver powder and copper powder is 50:50, content of silver powder and copper powder in tungsten carbide-silicon carbide particle reinforced heat-resistant gray cast iron material is 1%, adding silver powder, copper powder and tungsten carbide-silicon carbide particle reinforced heat-resistant gray cast iron material melt and smelting at a ratio of 1:99, mixing mechanically, agitating, refining and casting, and reinforcing tungsten carbide-silicon carbide particles to enhance hot gray cast iron material ingot. The method comprises performing high-pressure torsional deformation at 400-500 degrees C to obtain ingot, where high-pressure torsion processing parameters include pressure of indenter at 500 rotations/minute and downforce is 1 GPa, where test shows that the obtained tungsten carbide-silicon carbide particles reinforced high heat resistant gray cast iron has high strength and toughness.

PN CN107759255-A
TI Processing waste comprises e.g. quickly decomposing mainly protein, simple carbohydrates, starch and easily decomposed substance, releasing carbon dioxide, ammonia, and lot of heat and gradually increasing thermophilic microorganisms.
AU CHEN F
AE CHEN F (CHEN-Individual)
GA 2018199853
AB    NOVELTY - Processing waste comprises e.g. (i) composting at stack temperature of 50 degrees C in heating stage, quickly decomposing mainly protein, simple carbohydrates, starch and easily decomposed substance, releasing carbon dioxide, ammonia, and lot of heat, and gradually increasing thermophilic microorganisms, (ii) when the temperature in reactor is higher than 50 degrees C, entering high temperature stage, gradually replacing mesophile microorganisms in heap by thermophilic microorganisms as temperature increases, however, when stack temperature exceeds 70 degrees C, thermophilic microorganisms don't fit.
   USE - The method is useful for processing waste.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: enables waste to quickly produce fertile compost when composting; solves existing waste; and does not requires special process for processing.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Processing waste comprises (i) composting at stack temperature of 50 degrees C in heating stage, quickly decomposing mainly protein, simple carbohydrates, starch and easily decomposed substance, releasing carbon dioxide, ammonia, and lot of heat, and gradually increasing thermophilic microorganisms, (ii) when the temperature in the reactor is higher than 50 degrees C, entering the high temperature stage, gradually replacing mesophile microorganisms in the heap by the thermophilic microorganisms as the temperature increases, however, when the stack temperature exceeds 70 degrees C, thermophilic microorganisms don't fit, most of them go to sleep, apart from continuing to decompose the easily decomposable organic substances in compost, main decomposition of hemicellulose, cellulose and other complex organic substances, at the same time, begin the humification process, humus appeared, when the stack temperature drops below 70 degrees C, the dormant, thermophilic microorganisms have started their activities again, continue to break down cellulose, so that the high temperature lasts for a while, after that, weakening decomposition of organic matter, reducing heat production and gradually decreasing temperature of the reactor, (iii) when the stack temperature drops below 50 degrees C, medium-temperature microbes (also aerobic microbes) also predominate, at this time, decomposition of organic matter continues, further decomposing compost, increasing humus, characteristics of this stage are as follows, most of the organic matter is decomposed, remaining part is a difficult to decompose, microbiological activities gradually weakened, reduced heat production, temperature of the reactor gradually decreased, organic matter that has not been decomposed during the high temperature stage continues to decompose and increased accumulation of humus, and (iv) when the temperature in the reactor falls slightly above the temperature, entering the maturity phase, at this point, there is a large amount of organic matter, various available nutrients of humic substances, especially nitrogen, have increased significantly, however, the number of actinomycetes that decompose organic matter increased, anaerobic fiber breaks down bacteria, there are also more anaerobic bacteria and antibacterial bacteria, appearing of white hair in the surface of the fertilizer pile comprising fungal mycelium, at this time, if no fertilizer measures are taken, can cause strong decomposition of humus with ammonia, at the same time nitrate formed by nitrification, it will denitrify and lose nitrogen as water flows into the reactor, therefore, compost enters the cooling phase, compost should be compacted by distasteful conditions, it is beneficial to the formation of humus and the preservation of nitrogen.

Chemical aspects of compost production.); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.)

PN KR2145974-B1
TI Neutralization system for neutralizing tunnel excavation wastewater, has partition wall formed in shape inclined indirection facing rotation direction of carbon dioxide gas and tunnel excavation wastewater.
AU LEE W P
   HONG A B
   PARK J Y
AE ECHOENTEC CO LTD (ECHO-Non-standard)
GA 202079986T
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a gas regulator installed at one end of the liquefied carbon dioxide gas injection pipe to vaporize the liquefied carbon dioxide gas and adjust the pressure of the vaporized carbon dioxide gas. A wastewater injection line is formed in the form of a pipe through which the alkaline tunnel excavation wastewater in the flow control tank is transferred. Multiple mixing blades (51) are fixedly formed to be spaced apart from each other at regular intervals on the fixing bar. A fluid flow passage (53) is formed between multiple wing portions to rotate the fluid passing through. A partition wall (54) reduces the rotational speed of the tunnel excavation wastewater and carbon dioxide gas at the upper end of the wing of the mixing blade, and is formed in a shape inclined in a direction facing the rotation direction of carbon dioxide gas and tunnel excavation wastewater.
   USE - Neutralization system for neutralizing tunnel excavation wastewater generated during tunnel construction.
   ADVANTAGE - The pressure of the fluid mixed with the tunnel excavation wastewater and carbon dioxide gas is increased to dissolve a greater amount of carbon dioxide in the tunnel excavation wastewater as the rotation speed is reduced by preventing the rotation of tunnel excavation wastewater and carbon dioxide gas, thus increasing the neutralization efficiency. The effect of reducing the rotational speed of the tunnel excavation wastewater and the carbon dioxide gas and increasing the pressure is increased since the partition wall is formed in a shape inclined by a predetermined angle in a direction facing the rotation direction of the tunnel excavation wastewater and carbon dioxide gas. The facility is prevented from being corroded, is handled safely, and is more efficiently neutralize alkaline wastewater. The system increases the pressure only when necessary by dividing the discharge pressure of the tunnel excavation wastewater into a number of sequential pressure values, thus reducing the load of the driving device unnecessary.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view showing the shape of a mixing blade included in the tunnel excavation wastewater neutralization device.

TI Sea water softening by adding buffer solution to            sea water to elevate its alkalinity, mixing flue gas            with sucked non-condensable gases from thermal            desalination unit, bubbling in reactor to produce            carbonates and sulfates by precipitation, washing            precipitate, filtering and drying precipitate.
AU ABOUKHLEWA A A
   ABOTALEB A
   SINOPOLI A
AE QATAR FOUND EDUCATION SCI & COMMUNITY (QATA-Non-standard)
GA 2022C5319S
AB    NOVELTY - Method of sea water softening, comprises                adding buffer solution (sodium hydroxide) to sea                water to elevate its alkalinity up to pH 10, mixing                flue gas with sucked non-condensable gases from                thermal desalination unit, bubbling in a reactor to                produce carbonates and sulfates by precipitation,                washing the precipitate, and filtering and drying                the precipitate to yield valuable mineral product.                The processed filtered saline water, free from most                of divalent ions, is directed to a thermal                desalination unit for producing fresh water and                brine, where rejected brine from the desalination                unit is partially recycled as feedstock, and at                least a portion is blow down a portion is utilized                in brine crystallizer to achieve zero liquid                discharge.
   USE - The method is used for sea water softening to                yield valuable mineral product, such as                calcium/magnesium carbonates and barium sulfate                which is used in building rocks, concrete, cement,                paints, and plastic.
   ADVANTAGE - The method solves the problems of: scale                formation in the thermal desalination (multistage                flash (MSF)/multi-effect distillation                (MED)/membrane distillation (MD)) process which                affecting plant performance and elevated                operational cost; scale formation in membrane                desalination technologies (both MD and reverse                osmosis (RO)); carbon dioxide negative effect on                the environment, when released into the atmosphere;                environmentally impact of dispose the reject brine                from the water desalination plants. The method                provides an economical process for carbon dioxide                capture, sequestration, and utilization. The method                is scale-adapted and can be designed to fit small,                mid and large- scale plants. The method can be                adapted for different gas stream, for any brine                source stream, and can be easily adapted to a                current and new co-generation plant, where carbon                dioxide source is available from (flue gas) and                fresh sea water/reject brine stream is coming from                desalination plants. The method ensures that the                salinity of recirculation brine is reduced,                compared to the feed dilution, thus the scale                formation and fouling factor are also reduced;                overall heat transfer coefficient is increased and                specific heat transfer area is reduced (i.e.                decreases capital expenditures CAPEX). The method                also reduces the frequency of ball cleaning by                reducing the scale, which helps to decrease the                operating expenses (OPEX) and the solution reduces                the fouling effect. The method allows for the                decrease in friction for improvement in the pumping                performance during operation, which allows for                maintenance of the same salinity within the                recovery section and hence, reduction of the                make-up rate, nanofiltration (NF) pretreatment cost                and evaporator CAPEX and reduction of the brine                recirculation rate, which improves both pump                capital cost and power requirements.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Components: The crystallizer is a                  mechanical vapor compression system.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The first step is                  precipitating the sulfates through chemical                  precipitation. The feed water should be filtered                  first to remove sediments, marine life, and                  solids. The method is performed at a top brine                  temperature at or above 65&#176; C. In the                  method, a reduction of specific energy                  consumption is &#8805; 27 %. Preferred                  Components: The carbonates produced are at least                  one of calcium carbonate, magnesium carbonate,                  sodium carbonate and barium carbonate. The                  crystallizer is barium chloride. The sulfate                  precipitated is barium sulfate. The flue gas                  originates from at least one of exhaust of diesel                  engines, industrial plants, and desalination                  plants.
