TI Method for preparing blocking type tobacco            humectant for use in cigarettes, involves subjecting            tobacco to supercritical carbon dioxide, heating and            dissolving the extract with a set amount of absolute            ethyl alcohol and fully mixing the solution under the            stirring condition, standing for layering.
AU WAN S
   CUI T
   DU J
   WANG E
   WANG Q
   CAI L
   XI G
   LIU Q
   CHEN Z
   XU K
AE CHINA TOBACCO HENAN IND CO LTD (CNTA-C)
GA 2022A93541
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing blocking type tobacco                humectant involves i. subjecting tobacco to                supercritical carbon dioxide, heating and                dissolving the extract with a set amount of                absolute ethyl alcohol; ii. fully mixing the                solution obtained in the step i. under the stirring                condition, standing for layering, and separating a                lower-layer oil layer; and iii. slowly cooling the                oil layer obtained in the step 2) to separate out                solid insoluble substances, where the obtained                solid insoluble substances are the blocking type                tobacco humectant. The blocking tobacco humectant                comprises nicotine, neophytadiene, hexadecanoic                acid, linoleic acid, linolenic acid, octadecanoic                acid, n-pentacosane, heptacosane, 3-methyl                heptacosane, octacosane, trans-squalene, 2-methyl                octacosane, nonacosane, 2-methyl nonacosane,                3-methyl nonacosane, triacontane, 2-methyl                triacontane, 3-methyl triacontane, hentriacontane,                vitamin E, 2-methyl hentriacontane and 3-methyl                hentriacontane.
   USE - Method for preparing blocking type tobacco                humectant for use in cigarettes (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The blocking type tobacco humectant remarkably                reduce the water loss of the tobacco shreds, keep                the tobacco shreds moist and improve the smoking                quality of the tobacco.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for preparing blocking type tobacco                humectant involves i. subjecting tobacco to                supercritical carbon dioxide, heating and                dissolving the extract with a set amount of                absolute ethyl alcohol; ii. fully mixing the                solution obtained in the step i. under the stirring                condition, standing for layering, and separating a                lower-layer oil layer; and iii. slowly cooling the                oil layer obtained in the step 2) to separate out                solid insoluble substances, where the obtained                solid insoluble substances are the blocking type                tobacco humectant. The blocking tobacco humectant                comprises nicotine, neophytadiene, hexadecanoic                acid, linoleic acid, linolenic acid, octadecanoic                acid, n-pentacosane, heptacosane, 3-methyl                heptacosane, octacosane, trans-squalene, 2-methyl                octacosane, nonacosane, 2-methyl nonacosane,                3-methyl nonacosane, triacontane, 2-methyl                triacontane, 3-methyl triacontane, hentriacontane,                vitamin E, 2-methyl hentriacontane, 3-methyl                hentriacontane, dotriacontane, 2-methyl                dotriacontane, 3-methyl dotriacontane,                stigmasterol, dotriacontane and 3-methyl                dotriacontane.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   Composition of blocking type tobacco                moisturizing agent, which comprises nicotine,                neophytadiene, hexadecanoic acid, linoleic acid,                linolenic acid, octadecanoic acid, n-pentacosane,                heptacosane, 3-methyl heptacosane, octacosane,                trans-squalene, 2-methyl octacosane, nonacosane,                2-methyl nonacosane, 3-methyl nonacosane,                triacontane, 2-methyl triacontane, 3-methyl                triacontane, hentriacontane, vitamin E, 2-methyl                hentriacontane, 3-methyl hentriacontane,                dotriacontane, 2-methyl dotriacontane, 3-methyl                dotriacontane, stigmasterol, dotriacontane and                3-methyl dotriacontane. The weight percentage of                each component in the formula composition of the                blocking type tobacco moisturizing agent is                0.2-10.0 wt.%; and
   an application of blocking type tobacco                moisturizing agent in cigarettes.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The preparation method of                  tobacco supercritical CO2 extraction extract in                  step i. uses sweet and fragrant tobacco leaves,                  tobacco powder or tobacco leaf fragments as raw                  materials, and the supercritical extraction                  conditions are: extraction pressure 30 MPa,                  extraction temperature 45 &#176;C, extraction                  time 2 hours, the separation pressure is 8Mpa.                  The heating and dissolving temperature in step i.                  is 65-75 &#176;C, and the cooling rate in                  step iii. is 5-15 &#176;C per hour. Preferred                  Composition: The amount of absolute ethanol added                  is 5-10 times the quality of the extract of                  tobacco supercritical CO2 extraction. The                  application of blocking type tobacco moisturizing                  agent involves heating a set amount of blocking                  type tobacco moisturizing agent and melting, then                  spraying on the surface of cut tobacco or tobacco                  leaves, mixing evenly and cooled to room                  temperature, then, it is rolled into cigarettes                  according to the conventional cigarette                  processing technology, heating and melting                  temperature is 45-60 &#176;C, and the sample                  is a light yellow or brownish-yellow fluid liquid                  after melting. The application amount of blocking                  type tobacco moisturizing agent is to apply 5-25                  grams of blocking type tobacco moisturizing agent                  per kilogram of cut tobacco or tobacco leaf                  products agent.

TI Intelligent method for preventing, treating and            controlling air pollution in indoor and outdoor space,            involves intelligently selecting and controlling            whether outdoor air is introduced into indoor space or            not through air exchanger such that air pollution in            indoor space is exchanged.
AU GU Y
   HUANG Q
   HAN Y
   WU J
   LIN J
   MO H
AE MICROJET TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (MIJT-C)
GA 202285271P
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves detecting and transmitting                outdoor air detection data for outdoor air                pollution. An outdoor air detector is provided for                detecting and transmitting the outdoor air                detection data. Indoor air detection data of the                air pollution of the indoor space is transmitted.                An indoor air detector is provided for detecting                and transmitting the indoor air detection data. An                indoor air exchange system is utilized to implement                clean processing under the indoor space                environment. Outdoor air is introduced into the                indoor space for ventilation. An air exchanger is                utilized for intelligently comparing the outdoor                air detection data with the indoor air detection                data, providing intelligent selection control for                the implementation of air pollution ventilation in                the indoor space, comparing the air exchanger                through intelligent operation, and intelligently                selecting and controlling whether the outdoor air                is introduced into the indoor space or not through                the air exchanger such that air pollution in the                indoor space is implemented and exchanged.
   USE - Intelligent method for preventing, treating                and controlling air pollution such as suspended                PM1, PM2.5 and PM10-type Particulate matters,                Carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide, Ozone, Sulfur                dioxide, Nitrogen dioxide, Lead, Total Volatile                Organic Compounds, formaldehyde, bacteria, fungi                and viruses, or combinations (all claimed) in an                indoor and outdoor space.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables controlling whether the                outdoor air is introduced into the indoor space or                not through the air exchanger such that air                pollution in the indoor space is implemented and                exchanged, so that the indoor air detection data of                the air pollution in the indoor space is reduced to                a safe detection value, and the indoor air is                rapidly exchanged to form a clean and safe                breathing air state in the indoor space.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The air pollution refers to suspended PM1,                PM2.5 and PM10-type Particulate matters, carbon                monoxide (CO), carbon dioxide (CO2), ozone (O3),                Sulfur dioxide (SO2), nitrogen dioxide (NO2), lead                (Pb), total volatile organic matter (TVOC),                formaldehyde (HCHO), bacteria, fungus, virus, or a                combination. An air sensor is a bacteria sensor for                detecting bacteria and fungus information. A                particle sensor is used for detecting the suspended                particles information. The air sensor is a volatile                organic matter sensor for detecting carbon dioxide                or total volatile organic matter air information, a                formaldehyde sensor for detecting the air                information formaldehyde, a bacteria sensor for                detecting bacteria and fungus information and a                virus sensor for detecting the virus air                information.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow diagram of an                intelligent method for preventing, treating and                controlling air pollution. (Drawing includes                non-English language text).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INDUSTRIAL STANDARDS - The method enables performing wireless                  transmission by a wireless-fidelity (Wi-Fi)                  module, a Bluetooth module and a wireless                  radio-frequency identification module and near                  field communication modules.

TI Preparing copper-iron-zinc-based catalyst,            comprises e.g. dissolving copper(II) nitrate            trihydrate, iron(III) nitrate nonahydrate and zinc            nitrate hexahydrate in deionized water, dissolving            sodium hydroxide and 2,6-pyridinedicarboxylic acid in            deionized water and post-processing.
AU ZHANG W
   HUANG W
   WANG B
   YANG C
   WEN Y
AE UNIV TAIYUAN TECHNOLOGY (UYTL-C)
GA 202283339V
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing copper-iron-zinc-based catalyst,                comprises (i) dissolving soluble copper salt, iron                salt and zinc salt in 100 ml deionized water to                obtain solution A, (ii) dissolving sodium hydroxide                and 2,6-pyridinedicarboxylic acid in 200 ml                deionized water to obtain solution B, (iii)                simultaneously dropping solution A and B into 100                ml deionized water placed in water bath having                peristaltic pump under vigorous stirring,                controlling the pH value, and (iv) continue                stirring, aging, suction filtering, washing to                neutrality, drying and calcining to obtain final                product. The copper salt, iron salt and zinc salt                are copper(II) nitrate trihydrate, iron(III)                nitrate nonahydrate and zinc nitrate hexahydrate;                the molar ratio of copper-iron-zinc-based ternary                metal and 2,6-pyridinedicarboxylic acid is                0.5:1:2:0-1.2. The calcining atmosphere includes                air or nitrogen.
   USE - The copper-iron-zinc-based catalyst is useful                for producing C2+ alcohol by hydrogenation of                carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method has simple operation process, low                production cost and high catalytic                performance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing copper-iron-zinc-based catalyst,                comprises (i) dissolving soluble copper salt, iron                salt and zinc salt in 100 ml deionized water to                obtain solution A, (ii) dissolving sodium hydroxide                and 2,6-pyridinedicarboxylic acid in 200 ml                deionized water to obtain solution B, (iii)                simultaneously dropping solution A and B into 100                ml deionized water placed in water bath having                peristaltic pump under vigorous stirring,                controlling the pH value and temperature of the                water bath is at 20-35 &#8451;, (iv) stirring,                aging, suction filtering, washing to neutrality,                drying and calcining to obtain the                copper-iron-zinc-based catalyst. In step (i): the                copper salt, iron salt and zinc salt are copper(II)                nitrate trihydrate, iron(III) nitrate nonahydrate                and zinc nitrate hexahydrate respectively; the                molar ratio of copper-iron-zinc-based ternary metal                and 2,6-pyridinedicarboxylic acid is 0.5:1:2:0-1.2.                In step (ii): the concentration of sodium hydroxide                solution is 0.5-2 mol/l. In step (iii): controlling                pH to 7-9. In step (iv): the stirring time is 0.5-2                hours, the aging is at 60-80 &#8451; for 8-15                hours, the drying is at 50-80 &#8451; for 8-24                hours, the roasting is at 300-350 &#8451; for                2-6 hours and the calcining atmosphere includes air                or nitrogen.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for use                method of copper-iron-zinc-based catalyst for                producing C2+ alcohol by hydrogenation of carbon                dioxide, comprising hydrogenating carbon dioxide to                C2+ alcohol in a pressurized fixed-bed continuous                flow reactor.

TI Preparing laser-induced            electrocatalyst-semiconductor-three-dimensional porous            graphene ternary composite photoelectrode for detecting            glucose concentration in alkaline glucose sample            solution by dissolving metal ion.
AU UNANNOUNCED I
AE UNIV QINGDAO AGRIC (UNQA-C)
GA 202155095T
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing the laser-induced                electrocatalyst-semiconductor-three-dimensional                porous graphene ternary composite photoelectrode                involves adopting laser-induced synchronous                preparation method, dissolving the metal ion                precursor required for the preparation of                laser-induced electrocatalysts and the metal ion                precursor required for the preparation of                laser-induced semiconductors into                N,N-dimethylformamide (DMF), adding the polysulfone                polymer and magnetically stirring until dissolves,                spinning the resulting viscous solution onto the                surface of the indium tin oxide conductive glass,                and heating in a vacuum, forming metal                ion-polysulfone polymer composite film on the                surface of indium tin oxide conductive glass, using                carbon dioxide infrared laser (l equal to 10.64 mm)                engraving machine to engrave the pre-designed                photoelectrode pattern onto the surface of the                metal ion-polyethersulfone composite                membrane.
   USE - The photoelectrode used for detecting glucose                concentration in alkaline glucose sample solution                (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for preparing the                electrocatalyst-semiconductor-three-dimensional                porous graphene ternary composite photoelectrode                involving (i) adding 0.12-0.18 g metal ion                precursor required for the preparation of                laser-induced electrocatalysts and 0.09-0.13 g                metal ion precursor required for the preparation of                laser-induced semiconductors into 20-30 ml                dimethylformamide solution, performing magnetic                force stirring to dissolve completely, (ii) adding                2-3 g polysulfone polymer to the solution obtained                in the step (i) in 3-5 times (time interval is                20-30 minutes), and continuously stirring to form a                metal ion-containing polysulfone polymer with                certain viscosity substance solution, (iii)                spin-coating the metal ion-containing polysulfone                polymer solution synthesized in the step (ii) onto                the cleaned indium tin oxide conductive glass                surface to form a uniform metal ion-containing                polysulfone polymer solution film on the surface of                the indium tin oxide conductive glass, where the                spin coating speed is 2000-3000rpm, and the spin                coating time is 80-100 seconds, (iv)                vacuum-preserving the indium tin oxide conductive                glass modified by the metal ion-containing                polysulfone polymer solution film modified for 1-2                hour at 75-100 degrees C to make the solvent                volatilize and preparing indium tin oxide                conductive glass metal ion-doped polysulfone                polymer membrane on the surface, and (v) cooling                the metal ion-doped polysulfone polymer film                obtained in the step (iv) to normal temperature,                placing on the working platform of the carbon                dioxide laser engraving machine, and using the                carbon dioxide laser to scan the surface of the                metal ion-doped polysulfone polymer film to remove                the indium oxide and synchronously transforming it                into an                electrocatalyst-semiconductor-three-dimensional                porous graphene ternary composite                photoelectrode.

Preferred Conditions: The                  laser power is 4.0-4.8 W, the laser engraving                  speed is 166-250 mm/s, the laser engraving                  resolution is 600-1200, and the laser defocusing                  distance is 0.2-0.4 cm.

PT P
PN EA37398-B1
TI Gasification method of coal for production of heat and chemicals involves performing separation of carbon dioxide from composition of process gas and then separating remaining gas into separate pure gasses on semipermeable membranes.
AU SHARIFOV A
   GAJBULLOEVA ZUMRAT K
   KHAMROEV FARIDUN B
   SHODIEV GOLIBDZHON G
   SUBKHONOV DALERZHON K
AE SHARIFOV A (SHAR-Individual)
   GAJBULLOEVA Z K (GAJB-Individual)
   KHAMROEV F B (KHAM-Individual)
   SHODIEV G G (SHOD-Individual)
   SUBKHONOV D K (SUBK-Individual)
GA 2021421586
AB    NOVELTY - The gasification method involves preheating coal in the reactor by heating through the walls, separating volatile matters, cooling in heat exchanger to convert into liquid state, and collecting in the collector. A separator separates resinous substances before performing gasification of carbonic matter substance in the gas generator, heating the thermal reactor by the heat of the process gas, separation of carbon dioxide from the composition of the process gas by absorption with alkali solutions, separating remaining gas into separate pure gasses on semipermeable membranes. A portion of heat is removed from the process gas before entering the heat reactor in the first heat-recovery boiler to produce water steam. The process gas is fed into the second heat-recovery boiler and passed through the heat exchanger-cooler to remove the residual heat and decrease the temperature.
   USE - Gasification method of coal for production of heat and chemicals.
   ADVANTAGE - Increases efficiency of using gas heat from coal gasification and ensures high degree of separation of gas mixture into separate pure components. Allows use of heat of process gas, volatile substances, and ash to obtain steam or hot water used in the internal cycle of the coal gasification process, and to obtain chemically pure substances and individual gases, which are widely used in the chemical industry. Performs environmental friendly gasification process since it does not have waste that pollutes the production of heat and materials.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The gasification method involves preheating coal in the reactor by heating through the walls, separating volatile matters, cooling in heat exchanger to convert into liquid state, and collecting in the collector. A separator separates resinous substances before performing gasification of carbonic matter substance in the gas generator, heating the thermal reactor by the heat of the process gas, separation of carbon dioxide from the composition of the process gas by absorption with alkali solutions, separating remaining gas into separate pure gasses on semipermeable membranes. A portion of heat is removed from the process gas before entering the heat reactor in the first heat-recovery boiler to produce water steam. The process gas is fed into the second heat-recovery boiler and passed through the heat exchanger-cooler to remove the residual heat and decrease the temperature. The resulting coal ash from the gas generator is fed to the heat exchanger-cooler to remove heat and reduce the temperature. The resulting hot water in the heat exchanger is fed to the heat recovery boiler to produce steam, and the steam from heat recovery boilers serves for gasification of carbonaceous material to the gas generator. The isolated resinous substances from the composition of coal are used to obtain construction pitch.

   (B) using bi-directional pneumatic cylinder to drive pressure plate to press against mold for pressure treatment to bend glass in the mold.

PT P
PN CN110669723-A
TI Extracting exosomes based on differential            centrifugation, comprises taking HEK-293 cells and            culturing them in DMEM medium, adding horse serum            before using the medium, and then culturing the cells            in an incubator containing carbon dioxide.
AU ZHAO K
AE ZHAO K (ZHAO-Individual)
GA 202006789P
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting exosomes based on differential                centrifugation, comprises taking HEK-293 cells and                culturing them in DMEM medium, adding 10% horse                serum before using the medium, and then culturing                the cells in an incubator containing 5% carbon                dioxide, and passaging every 4-6 days. Observe the                growth of HEK-293 cells. When the cell fusion                reaches about 90%, discard the original medium.                After washing three times with PBS buffer, add DMEM                medium and place it at 37 degrees C. Incubate for                24-36 hours. Collect the supernatant after                incubation. Take the supernatant from 100mL and                centrifuge at 3000 x g for 15-20 min at 4 degrees C                to remove dead cells. Transfer the remaining                supernatant to a centrifugal ultrafilter, and spin                the rotor at 4000 degrees C at 4 degrees C g                centrifugally concentrated to about 1mL. Centrifuge                the supernatant after treatment at 10,000 x g for                30-40min to remove impurities such as cell debris                and nuclear mitochondria of dead cells, and leave                the supernatant.
   USE - Method for extracting exosomes based on                differential centrifugation.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to extract exosomes based                on differential centrifugation, which improve                purity of exosomes in the extract to a certain                extent, and the extraction effect is better.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Extracting exosomes based on differential                centrifugation, comprises taking HEK-293 cells and                culturing them in DMEM medium, adding 10% horse                serum before using the medium, and then culturing                the cells in an incubator containing 5% carbon                dioxide, passaging every 4-6 days. Observe the                growth of HEK-293 cells. When the cell fusion                reaches about 90%, discard the original medium.                After washing three times with PBS buffer, add DMEM                medium and place it at 37 degrees C. Incubate for                24-36 hours. Collect the supernatant after                incubation. Take the supernatant from 100mL and                centrifuge at 3000 x g for 15-20 min at 4 degrees C                to remove dead cells. Transfer the remaining                supernatant to a centrifugal ultrafilter, and spin                the rotor at 4000 degrees C at 4 degrees C g                centrifugally concentrated to about 1mL. Centrifuge                the supernatant after treatment at 10,000 x g for                30-40min to remove impurities such as cell debris                and nuclear mitochondria of dead cells, and leave                the supernatant. Centrifuge the supernatant by                100,000-120000 x g for 70-80min, remove the                supernatant and leave a precipitate. Add 1 mL of 1                x PBS buffer to the pellet, rinse, resuspend, and                centrifuge at 100,000-120000 x g for 70-80 min.                Discard the supernatant after centrifugation. The                resulting pellet is exosomes. The exosomes are                stored at 4 degrees C and -80 degrees C for                short-term and long-term storage.

PN CN110433813-A; CN110433813-B
TI Preparing copper-indium alloy catalyst for            synthesizing methanol through carbon dioxide            hydrogenation, by mixing copper nitrate, indium nitrate            and zirconium nitrate, dripping citric acid,            magnetically stirring, drying and roasting            mixture.
AU XU Q
   GAO J
   SONG F
   CHENG W
   LI Y
AE YANCHENG INST TECHNOLOGY (YANH-C)
GA 2019969264
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing a copper-indium alloy                catalyst, involves (i) mixing copper nitrate,                indium nitrate and zirconium nitrate in deionized                water, and dripping a citric acid aqueous solution                under magnetic stirring to obtain a uniformly mixed                sol solution, (ii) magnetically stirring the sol                solution to obtain a glass-shaped amorphous                product, (iii) drying the amorphous product in a                vacuum drying oven to obtain a gel-like catalyst                precursor, (iv) roasting the precursor in a tubular                furnace at 350-500 degrees C at a programmed                temperature of 5-10 degrees C/minute for 3-5 hours,                cooling, grinding, tableting and molding through a                40-80 mesh sieve to obtain a catalyst matrix, and                (v) transferring the catalyst matrix into a tubular                furnace having a V(hydrogen):V(nitrogen) ratio of                1:9 atmosphere with a flow rate of 50 ml/minute,                roasting at 350-500 degrees C at a programmed                temperature of 5-10 degrees C/minute for 3-5 hours,                cooling and grinding the roasted product.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing                copper-indium alloy catalyst, which is useful for                synthesizing methanol through carbon dioxide                hydrogenation (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst is prepared by sol-gel method,                and has high carbon dioxide conversion rate of                9.2-12.2% and high methanol selectivity of                47.1-71.8% at a higher reaction temperature of 270                degrees C.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for preparing a copper-indium alloy                catalyst, involves (i) mixing copper nitrate,                indium nitrate and zirconium nitrate in deionized                water, and dripping a citric acid aqueous solution                under magnetic stirring to obtain a uniformly mixed                sol solution, where the molar ratio of copper ions,                indium ions and zirconium ions in the copper                nitrate, indium nitrate and zirconium nitrate is                1:(0-2):4, and the molar ratio of the copper ions,                the indium ions and the zirconium ions to the                citric acid is 1:(1-1.5), (ii) magnetically                stirring the sol solution to obtain a glass-shaped                amorphous product, (iii) drying the amorphous                product in a vacuum drying oven to obtain a                gel-like catalyst precursor, (iv) roasting the                precursor in a tubular furnace at 350-500 degrees C                at a programmed temperature of 5-10 degrees                C/minute for 3-5 hours, cooling, grinding,                tableting and molding through a 40-80 mesh sieve to                obtain a catalyst matrix, and (v) transferring the                catalyst matrix into a tubular furnace having a                V(hydrogen):V(nitrogen) ratio of 1:9 atmosphere                with a flow rate of 50 ml/minute, roasting at                350-500 degrees C at a programmed temperature of                5-10 degrees C/minute for 3-5 hours, cooling and                grinding the roasted product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: in step (i), the molar                  ratio of copper ions, indium ions and zirconium                  ions in the copper nitrate, indium nitrate and                  zirconium nitrate is 1:2:4, the magnetic stirring                  is carried out at 75-95 degrees C at a stirring                  speed of 400-600 rpm. In step (ii), the magnetic                  stirring is carried out at 75-95 degrees C at                  stirring speed of 400-600 rpm. In step (iii), the                  drying treatment is carried out for 24 hours at                  80-120 degrees C. In step (iv), the roasting is                  carried out at 350 degrees C at a programmed                  temperature of 5 degrees C/minute for 4 hours. In                  step (v), the roasting is carried out at 350                  degrees C at a programmed temperature of 5                  degrees C/minute for 3 hours.

PN CN109022358-A
TI Promoting proliferation of human adipose stem            cells, involves washing human adipose tissue, cutting,            digesting, filtering, centrifuging, resuspending cell,            culturing, digesting, culturing, and replacing with            drug-containing complete medium.
AU ZHAO L
   CHEN M
AE HUAIAN KANGYILAI STEM CELLS BIOTECHNOLOG (HUAI-Non-standard)
GA 2018A55032
AB    NOVELTY - Method for promoting proliferation of human                adipose stem cells, involves taking fresh human                adipose tissue, washing, removing the adipose                tissue membrane and blood vessels, cutting into                portions, digesting type I collagenase and trypsin,                oscillating, adding complete medium, filtering,                centrifuging, discarding supernatant, resuspending                the cell pellet in complete medium, adding red                blood cell lysate, mixing, disintegrating,                centrifuging, removing supernatant, resuspending                the cell pellet in complete medium, inoculating                into a culture flask, culturing, changing the                culture medium to remove the non-adherent cells,                digesting the cells, passaging cells, taking the                passage cells, digesting, inoculating into a                culture flask, culturing in a complete medium at 37                degrees C in a 5% carbon dioxide for 24 hours, and                replacing with a drug-containing complete medium to                continue the culture, where the drug-containing                complete medium contains an effective concentration                of thujopsene.
   USE - The method is useful for promoting                proliferation of human adipose stem cells. The                thujopsene is useful in preparation of culture                medium for promoting the proliferation of human                adipose derived stem cells (all claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for promoting proliferation of human                adipose stem cells, involves taking fresh human                adipose tissue, washing with phosphate-buffered                saline (PBS) buffer, removing the adipose tissue                membrane and blood vessels, cutting into portions,                digesting 0.1% type I collagenase and 0.25% trypsin                at 37 degrees C, repeatedly oscillating, after the                end of digestion, adding complete medium, using                200-mesh filter to remove undigested fibrous                connective tissue, centrifuging at 1200x g for 5                minutes, discarding supernatant to remove suspended                fat cells and lipid droplets, resuspending the cell                pellet in complete medium, adding 6 times volume of                red blood cell lysate, mixing, disintegrating at                room temperature for 6 minutes, centrifuging at                1200x g for 5 minutes, removing supernatant,                resuspending the cell pellet in complete medium,                inoculating into a culture flask, culturing at 37                degrees C in a 5% carbon dioxide incubator, after                24 hours, changing the culture medium to remove the                non-adherent cells, then changing the medium once                in every 3 days, after the cells are fused,                digesting the cells with 0.25% trypsin and 1 mmol/l                EDTA at 37 degrees C for 3-5 minutes, completely                digesting the medium completely, passaging cells,                taking the passage cells, digesting to prepare a                cell suspension, inoculating into a culture flask,                culturing in a complete medium at 37 degrees C in a                5% carbon dioxide for 24 hours, and replacing with                a drug-containing complete medium to continue the                culture, where the drug-containing complete medium                contains an effective concentration of thujopsene.                An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for use of                thujopsene, in preparation of culture medium for                promoting the proliferation of human adipose                derived stem cells.

PN CN108424857-A
TI Wastewater flue gas treatment by carrying out microfiltration and autoclaving wastewater, inoculating wastewater in cultured Scenedesmus obliquus in Bold's Basal Medium with carbon dioxide synthesis gas, filtering, diluting, and culturing.
AU SONG C
   QIU Y
   ZHANG E
   SUN Y
   XIE M
   FAN Z
AE UNIV TIANJIN (UTIJ-C)
GA 201866715M
AB    NOVELTY - Wastewater flue gas treatment comprises inoculating Scenedesmus obliquus in a container, adding Bold's Basal Medium (BBM) culture medium, placing in a light incubator to culture for 10-15 days at 23-27 degrees C with 110-135 mu mol.m2/second white fluorescent lighting filtration sterilization, and passing 0.5-1 L/minute algae suspension grid absorbance adjustment in the base culture medium; carrying out microfiltration of wastewater, and autoclaving at 100-121 degrees C, where the wastewater is secondary urban sewage and food wastewater; culturing S. obliquus for 6-10 days in BBM with 5% carbon dioxide (CO2) synthesis gas at 23-27 degrees C with 110-135 mu mol.m2/second white fluorescent lighting, and inoculating 300 mL secondary urban sewage, filtering, diluting secondary urban sewage and food wastewater using CO2, and preparing sewage water solution including secondary urban sewage and food wastewater at a volume ratio of 98:2; and culturing at 27 degrees C water bath for 6 days.
   USE - Method for wastewater flue gas treatment.
   ADVANTAGE - The method uses Scenedesmus, thus additional nutrient substances are not required and biomass is produced.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Wastewater flue gas treatment comprises inoculating Scenedesmus obliquus in a container, adding Bold's Basal Medium (BBM) culture medium, where inoculation volume is 10% (Volume of inoculum/Volume of medium), placing in a light incubator to culture for 10-15 days at 23-27 degrees C with 110-135 mu mol.m2/second white fluorescent lighting filtration sterilization, and passing 0.5-1 L/minute algae suspension grid absorbance adjustment in the base culture medium under air condition of 0.5-1; carrying out microfiltration of the wastewater using 0.2 mu m nylon to remove microorganisms and suspended solid particles from the wastewater, and autoclaving at 100-121 degrees C, where the wastewater is secondary urban sewage and food wastewater; culturing S. obliquus for 6-10 days in BBM with 5% carbon dioxide (CO2) synthesis gas at 23-27 degrees C with 110-135 mu mol.m2/second white fluorescent lighting, and adding 300 mL secondary urban sewage in a sealed aluminum pressure serum bottle, inoculating at 3-5 vol.% (Volume inoculum/Volume wastewater), filtering the secondary urban sewage, diluting 0.5 vol.%, 1.2 vol.%, and 3 vol.% secondary urban sewage and food wastewater using 5 vol.%, 10 vol.%, and 14.1 vol.% CO2, and preparing sewage water solution including secondary urban sewage and food wastewater at a volume ratio of 100:0, 99.5:0.5, 99:1, and 98:2; and placing wastewater solution in an inoculation bottle, culturing at 150 revolutions/minute (rpm) and at 27 degrees C water bath with 120 mu mol.m2/second white fluorescent lighting strength for 6 days.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The CO2 synthesis gas comprises 5% CO2, and 95% nitrogen gas.

PN CN108201773-A; CN108201773-B
TI Absorbing volatile organic compounds in gas by using a hydrophilic switchable reversible solvent comprises e.g. passing a gas containing volatile organic compounds into an absorption device, and absorbing it with a reversible solvent.
AU LI Y
   LIU X
   CHANG H
   TIAN S
   NING P
   HUANG J
   HU X
AE UNIV KUNMING SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UKST-C)
   UNIV KUNMING SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UKST-C)
GA 2018515617
AB    NOVELTY - Absorbing volatile organic compounds in gas by using a hydrophilic switchable reversible solvent comprises e.g. (1) passing a gas containing volatile organic compounds into an absorption device, and absorbing it with a reversible solvent at a temperature of -20 degrees C to 60 degrees C and a pressure of 1-50 atm for 20-50 minutes to obtain absorption system containing volatile organic compounds; where the reversible solvent is a hydrophilic switchable reversible solvent; (2) adding water to the volatile organic compound-containing absorption system obtained in the step (1), and uniformly mixing to obtain a mixed system, and at a temperature of -10 degrees C to 50 degrees C, introducing carbon dioxide into the mixed system, and reacting for 50 minutes, then allowing to stand to obtain the upper organic phase and lower aqueous phase; and (3) at a temperature of 0-80 degrees C, passing air or inert gas into the lower aqueous phase, and then allowing to stand to obtain an upper reversible solvent and lower aqueous layer.
   USE - The method is useful for absorbing volatile organic compounds in a gas by using a hydrophilic switchable reversible solvent.
   ADVANTAGE - The method can solve the problem that the traditional solvent difficult regeneration after absorbing volatile organic matter, and is easy to cause secondary pollution.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Absorbing volatile organic compounds in gas by using a hydrophilic switchable reversible solvent comprises (1) passing a gas containing volatile organic compounds into an absorption device, and absorbing it with a reversible solvent at a temperature of -20 degrees C to 60 degrees C and a pressure of 1-50 atm for 20-50 minutes to obtain absorption system containing volatile organic compounds; where the reversible solvent is a hydrophilic switchable reversible solvent; (2) adding water to the volatile organic compound-containing absorption system obtained in the step (1), and uniformly mixing to obtain a mixed system, and at a temperature of -10 degrees C to 50 degrees C, introducing carbon dioxide into the mixed system, and reacting for 50 minutes, then allowing to stand to obtain the upper organic phase and the lower aqueous phase; where the volume of the water is 10-70% of the volume of the saturated absorption system, and the volume of the carbon dioxide is not less than 5% of the volume of the mixed system; and (3) at a temperature of 0-80 degrees C, passing air or inert gas into the lower aqueous phase obtained in step (2) for 30-120 minutes, and then allowing to stand to obtain an upper reversible solvent and a lower layer of water, where the volume of air or inert gas is not less than 50% of the volume of the lower aqueous phase.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In step (i), the hydrophilic reversible solvent comprises amidine compound of formula (I), guanidine compound of formula (II) and/or amine compound of formula (N-Rx) (III). In the step (3), the inert gas is at least one of nitrogen, argon gas, and helium gas.R1, R3 = H or alkyl (preferably methyl, ethyl, propyl, butyl, pentyl, hexyl, heptyl, octyl or its isomers);R2, R4 = methyl, ethyl, propyl, butyl, pentyl, hexyl, heptyl, octyl or its isomers;x = 1-3; andR = phenyl or alkyl B (preferably methyl, ethyl, propyl, butyl, pentyl, hexyl, heptyl, octyl or its isomers).

TI Method for preparing sodium carbonate and ammonium            sulfate by using sodium sulfate, involves mixing sodium            sulfate, ammonium bicarbonate recovery liquid, ammonium            bicarbonate solid or mixed gas of ammonia gas and            carbon dioxide, carrying out double decomposition            reaction, separating the liquor.
AU CHEN P
   ZHENG S
   SHEN C
   ZHANG H
   FAN B
   ZHANG Y
AE BEIJING JINGCHENG TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (BEIJ-Non-standard)
   INST PROCESS ENG CHINESE ACAD SCI (CAGC-C)
GA 202291792L
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing sodium carbonate and                ammonium sulfate by using sodium sulfate, involves                i. mixing sodium sulfate, ammonium bicarbonate                recovery liquid, ammonium bicarbonate solid or                mixed gas of ammonia gas and carbon dioxide,                carrying out double decomposition reaction at 20-50                &#176;C, and carrying out solid-liquid                separation to obtain sodium bicarbonate and double                decomposition mother liquor; ii. calcining the                obtained sodium bicarbonate to obtain sodium                carbonate; separating the double decomposition                mother liquor; iii. mixing double salt, evaporation                mother liquor and the sulfate concentrated solution                obtained in the step (ii), heating for                crystallization, and carrying out solid-liquid                separation to obtain sodium sulfate and double salt                washing liquor; the obtained sodium sulfate is                reused in the step (i); iv. cooling and                crystallizing the double-salt washing liquor; iv.                cooling and crystallizing the double-salt washing                liquor obtained in the step (iii).
   USE - Method for preparing sodium carbonate and                ammonium sulfate by using sodium sulfate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method relieves the problem of excessive                sodium sulfate, realizes high-value utilization of                sodium sulfate, reduces energy consumption,                simplifies the process flow, and gives                consideration to environmental protection and                economy.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for preparing sodium carbonate and                co-producing ammonium sulfate by using sodium                sulfate involves i. mixing sodium sulfate, ammonium                bicarbonate recovery liquid, ammonium bicarbonate                solid or mixed gas of ammonia gas and carbon                dioxide, carrying out double decomposition reaction                at 20-50 &#176;C, and carrying out solid-liquid                separation to obtain sodium bicarbonate and double                decomposition mother liquor; ii. calcining the                obtained sodium bicarbonate to obtain sodium                carbonate; separating the double decomposition                mother liquor obtained in the step (i) by a                nanofiltration membrane to obtain an ammonium                bicarbonate recovery solution and a sulfate                concentrated solution; recycling the obtained                ammonium bicarbonate recovery liquid in the step                (i); iii. mixing double salt, evaporation mother                liquor and the sulfate concentrated solution                obtained in the step (ii), heating for                crystallization, and carrying out solid-liquid                separation to obtain sodium sulfate and double salt                washing liquor; the obtained sodium sulfate is                reused in the step (i); iv. cooling and                crystallizing the double-salt washing liquor                obtained in the step (iii), and performing                solid-liquid separation to obtain double salt and a                rich ammonium sulfate solution; recycling the                obtained double salt in the step (iii); and v.                evaporating and crystallizing the rich ammonium                sulfate solution obtained in the step (iv), and                performing solid-liquid separation to obtain                ammonium sulfate and evaporation mother liquor; the                resulting evaporation mother liquor was recycled to                step (iii).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The molar ratio of                  total ammonium to total sodium in the metathesis                  reaction of step (i) is (0.9-1.4): 1. The molar                  ratio of total ammonium to total sodium in the                  metathesis reaction of step (i) is (1-1.1): 1.                  Preferred Method: The method involves carrying                  out metathesis reaction of step (i) for a period                  of time ranging from 0.5-3 hours, nanofiltration                  membrane separation in the step (ii) is 10-60                  &#176;C, temperature-rising crystallization                  in the step (iii) is 70-100 &#176;C, cooling                  crystallization in the step (iv) is 0-40                  &#176;C, evaporative crystallization of step                  (5) is 80-110 &#176;C. The solid-liquid                  separation comprises hydrocyclone separation,                  centrifugal separation, filtration separation                  and/or sedimentation separation.

TI Preparing immune vaccine against swine epidemic diarrhea virus variant strain shxx1902 involves adding vero cells to culture medium containing fetal bovine serum, and culturing in carbon dioxide incubator.
AU GE J
   LIU J
   JU H
   WANG J
   LI X
   YANG D
   SHEN L
   YU H
   SHEN H
   GE F
AE SHANGHAI ANIMAL EPIDEMIC DISEASES PREVEN (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2022847161
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing immune vaccine against swine epidemic diarrhea virus variant strain shxx1902 involves adding vero cells to culture medium containing 10 wt.% fetal bovine serum, culturing in carbon dioxide (CO2) incubator containing 5 vol.% at 37degrees Celsius, and digesting and subculturing to cells shake flask, diluting with serum-free medium to cell density of 6x106 cells/mL. When vero cells are cultured to cell density of 6x106 cells/mL, 0.1 L of porcine epidemic diarrhea virus mutant strain shxx1902 cell culture solution is added, diluted and passaged, and inoculated with MOI=0.1, pH is 7.4plus minus0.1, shaker speed is set to 120 revolutions/minute (rpm), culture temperature is 37degrees Celsius, cell density after passage is 3x106 cells/mL, and cells are cultured for 72 hours.
   USE - Method for preparing immune vaccine against swine epidemic diarrhea virus variant strain shxx1902
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to generate antibody similar to a commercial vaccine level can be generated after mice are immunized.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing immune vaccine against swine epidemic diarrhea virus variant strain shxx1902 involves adding vero cells to culture medium containing 10 wt.% fetal bovine serum, culturing in carbon dioxide (CO2) incubator containing 5 vol.% at 37degrees Celsius, and digesting and subculturing to cells shake flask, diluting with serum-free medium to cell density of 6x106 cells/mL. When vero cells are cultured to cell density of 6x106 cells/mL, 0.1 L of porcine epidemic diarrhea virus mutant strain shxx1902 cell culture solution is added, diluted and passaged, and inoculated with MOI=0.1, pH is 7.4plus minus0.1, shaker speed is set to 120 revolutions/minute (rpm), culture temperature is 37degrees Celsius, cell density after passage is 3x106 cells/mL, and cells are cultured for 72 hours. The virus solution of variant strain of porcine epidemic diarrhea virus shxx1902 with titer of 2x106 TCID50/mL is added to formaldehyde with final mass percentage concentration of 0.2%. The variant strain of porcine epidemic diarrhea virus shxx1902 is deposited at China General Microbiological Culture Collection (CGMCC NO: 22380), inactivated virus at 37degrees Celsius for 28 hours, added to 96-well plate covered with vero cells, 50 muL/well, 37degrees Celsius, incubated for 2 days in CO2 incubator with concentration of 5% by volume, harvested cell supernatant, fixed cells with cold acetone, added harvested supernatant to 96-well plate with vero cells, and continued to culture for 2 days. The supernatant is discarded, fixed cells with cold acetone, and added aluminum gel adjuvant after inactivation is qualified, mixed according to volume ratio of antigen and adjuvant at 1:1.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a variant strain of porcine epidemic diarrhea virus, which is deposited at China General Microbiological Culture Collection (CGMCC NO: 22380).

PN CN113559145-A
TI Composition used for extracting walnut green peel polyphenols, comprises choline chloride as hydrogen acceptor, compound as hydrogen donor, microencapsulated enzyme complex agent, complexing agent and ethanol.
AU GE Q
   FAN Y
   XU Z
   HUI J
   WU X
   MENG L
AE UNIV HUNAN CHINESE MEDICINE FIRST AFFILI (UHCM-C)
GA 2021C6929F
AB    NOVELTY - Walnut green peel polyphenol extraction composition comprises 20-40 pts. wt. choline chloride as hydrogen acceptor, 10-15 pts. wt. compound (A) as hydrogen donor, 15-20 pts. wt. microencapsulated enzyme complex agent, 6-12 pts. wt. complexing agent and 100-500 pts. wt. ethanol.
   USE - The composition is used for extracting walnut green peel polyphenols (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: firstly uses the self-prepared microcapsule-embedded enzyme complex for enzymatic hydrolysis, then centrifuges the supernatant obtained by centrifugation to perform supercritical carbon dioxide extraction to purify the polyphenols in the enzymatic hydrolysis products; and then uses an ethanol mixed solution with a hydrogen acceptor, a hydrogen donor and a complexing agent to further decompose the walnut green peel lignin, hemilignin and other plant cell walls in the solid residue obtained by centrifugation, which improves the further extraction of polyphenols in the residue of enzymatic hydrolysis products, and effectively improves the purification efficiency of polyphenols.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a method for extracting the walnut green peel polyphenols using the composition, involving (a) picking green peel walnuts, taking the green peels, washing the green peels on the surface under clean water, drying at 50-60degrees Celsius, and pulverizing to a walnut green peel powder, (b) dissolving the microencapsulated complex agent in 300-500 ml distilled water to form an aqueous solution of microencapsulated complex agent, placing the obtained walnut green peel powder in the microencapsulated complex agent aqueous solution, leaving still at 30-40degrees Celsius for 1 -1.5 hours, and centrifuging at room temperature, (c) centrifuging the supernatant and performing supercritical carbon dioxide extraction of polyphenols in the supernatant at a temperature of 45-55degrees Celsius, a pressure of 25-30 MPa, and a carbon dioxide flow rate of 25-35 kg/hour, where the entrainer dosage is 4-5 ml/g and the ethanol fraction used is 75 vol.%, (d) mixing the solid obtained by centrifugation in step (b) with mixed solution of choline chloride as hydrogen acceptor, compound (A) as hydrogen donor, complexing agent and 500-1000 ml alkali solution and ethanol, and extracting at a temperature of 75-85degrees Celsius according to a solid-to-liquid ratio of 20:1-30:1 for 45 minutes to 1 hour, quickly cooling to room temperature, vacuum filtering at 0.08-1.5 MPa, and collecting the filtrate to a constant volume, which is the polyphenol obtained by the second extraction of centrifugal solid, and (e) collecting polyphenols obtained in steps (c) and (d), establishing a standard solution curve of gallic acid, and calculating the polyphenol extraction amount in the walnut green peel.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Properties: The particle size of the walnut green peel powder after pulverizing in step (a) is 30-70 mesh.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Components: The raw materials for the preparation of microencapsulated enzyme complex agent comprises 50-60 pts. wt. thermosensitive polymer, 30-35 pts. wt. nano-silica particles with a particle size of 50-60 mm, 15-20 pts. wt. silane modifier, 30-40 pts. wt. enzyme complex, 10-15 pts. wt. hyperbranched polyethoxysilane and 20-25 pts. wt. acetone. The enzyme complex in the microencapsulated enzyme complex agent is a complex formed by mixing cellulase, pectinase and xylanase in a mass ratio of (0.3-0.6):(0.2-0.5):(0.1-0.4).Preferred Process: The microencapsulated enzyme complex agent is prepared by (a) dissolving the thermosensitive polymer in acetone to form a thermosensitive polymer oil phase, (b) uniformly mixing the nano-silica particles with the silane modifier having a particle size of 20-50 nm, and uniformly stirring at 30-40degrees Celsius for 30-45 minutes to obtain silane-modified nano-silica solution, (c) uniformly mixing the thermosensitive polymer oil phase with the silane-modified nano-silica solution and the enzyme complex, adding hyperbranched polyethoxysilane for crosslinking, adding a mixed solution of water and ethanol in a volume ratio of 1:3, and cleaning for 3-5 times to obtain nano-silica/thermosensitive polymer microcapsule with embedded enzyme complex, and (d) drying at 25-28degrees Celsius to obtain the product.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The thermosensitive polymer is poly(N-isopropylacrylamide), polyvinyl alcohol methyl ether, and/or poly(isopropylacrylamide-co-acrylic acid).
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The complexing agent is EDTA, ethanolamine, triethylamine, oleic acid, and/or sodium citrate. The hydrogen donor compound is urea, citric acid, and/or lactic acid. The silane modifier is dichlorodimethylsilane, octyltrimethoxysilane, and/or methyltrimethoxysilane.

PT P
PN CN111944160-A; CN111944160-B
TI Oxalic acid functionalized microporous coordination polymer material for gas adsorption, is represented by linear structure, and has one-dimensional spiral pores with periodic shrinkage-expansion changes in pore size.
AU CUI X
   YU C
   DING Q
   XING H
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG (UYZH-C)
   UNIV ZHEJIANG (UYZH-C)
GA 2020B74157
AB    NOVELTY - Oxalic acid functionalized microporous coordination polymer material is represented by linear formula, and space group of the oxalic acid functionalized microporous coordination polymer material is P41212, and has one-dimensional spiral pores with periodic shrinkage-expansion changes in pore size, and organic oxalate anions, triazole rings and methyl groups are distributed on the surface of the pores.
   USE - Oxalic acid functionalized microporous coordination polymer material used in the field of gas adsorption, and to adsorb carbon dioxide and 2-3 carbons hydrocarbon gases, and for the adsorption and separation of carbon dioxide/methane, carbon dioxide/nitrogen, acetylene/ethylene, propyne/propylene, ethylene/ethane, and propylene/propane (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The oxalic acid functionalized microporous coordination polymer material has pocket-like one-dimensional spiral pores that periodically shrink and expand in cross-section, and organic oxalate anions, triazole ring and high-density methyl group have suitable adsorption sites, which not only has excellent gas separation ability, but also has excellent stability, and can still maintain excellent performance after air, water, acid or alkali treatment adsorption performance, and preparation method is a solvothermal method assisted by a homogeneous reactor.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Oxalic acid functionalized microporous coordination polymer material is represented by linear formula of C8H8N6O4Zn2 and the chemical formula is (M2L2A) infinity, where M is the metal cation is zinc ion (Zn2+), L is the organic ligand 3-methyl-1H-1,2,4-triazole, A is the organic oxalate anion, infinity is that the oxalic acid functionalized microporous coordination polymer material is composed of various structural units composed of M2L2A arranged regularly. The space group of the oxalic acid functionalized microporous coordination polymer material is P41212, and has one-dimensional spiral pores with periodic shrinkage-expansion changes in pore size, and organic oxalate anions, triazole rings and methyl groups are distributed on the surface of the pores. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a preparation method of oxalic acid functionalized microporous coordination polymer material, which involves:
   (A) adding 3Zn(OH)2.2ZnCO3, oxalic acid and 3-methyl-1H-1,2,4-triazole to the mixed solution of water and organic solvent at a mass ratio of 1:1:4;
   (B) stirring at room temperature for 5-120 minutes, mixing uniformly, placing in a homogeneous reactor and solvothermally reacting at 120-200 degrees C, 10-50 revolutions/minute for 12-60 hours, and filtering to obtain a solid precipitation product, and organic solvent is at least one of methanol or n-butanol; and
   (C) washing the obtained solid precipitated product with water and methanol, and then vacuum degassing and activating at 50-120 degrees C for 10-30 hours to obtain the oxalic acid functionalized microporous coordination polymer material.

PT P
PN CN111396017-A; CN111396017-B
TI Fracturing dense oil reservoir with low-porosity, low-penetrant rate and high mud content by injecting pre-liquid supercritical carbon dioxide to target reservoir, and injecting pre-acid and slick water fracturing fluid mixed with ceramsite.
AU LIU Y
   WANG Y
   LV X
   LIU W
   BAI J
   LIU C
   WANG X
   WANG Q
   LU W
   HUANG Q
   XIE T
AE CNPC BOHAI DRILLING ENG CO LTD (CNPC-C)
   CHINA NAT PETROLEUM CORP (CNPC-C)
   CNPC BOHAI DRILLING ENG CO LTD (CNPC-C)
GA 202066643L
AB    NOVELTY - Fracturing dense oil reservoir with low-porosity, low-penetrant rate and high mud content by injecting pre-liquid supercritical carbon dioxide (CO2) to target reservoir, injecting pre-acid into target reservoir, injecting high-viscosity slick water fracturing fluid mixed with ceramsite into target reservoir, injecting low-viscosity slick water fracturing fluid mixed with quartz sand into target reservoir, injecting medium viscosity slick water fracturing fluid mixed with another quartz sand and medium viscosity slick water fracturing fluid mixed with another ceramsite sequentially into target reservoir, injecting another high viscosity slick water fracturing fluid mixed with additional ceramsite into target reservoir, orderly pouring medium-viscosity slick water fracturing fluid into target reservoir for displacement construction, and injecting clean water into target reservoir for displacement construction.
   USE - The method is used for fracturing dense oil reservoir with low-porosity, low-penetrant rate and high mud content.
   ADVANTAGE - The method uses liquid supercritical CO2 of pre-fluid to penetrate fractures, increases extension and swept area of crack, uses pre-acid to remove mud pollution in near-well zone while reducing fracture pressure and stress concentration of rocks in near-well zone, adopts rapid increase of displacement/large displacement + reverse mixing fracturing and sanding process, and improves fracture longitudinal transformation degree, commissure and stream guidance capability of near well zone.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Fracturing dense oil reservoir with low-porosity, low-penetrant rate and high mud content involves injecting pre-liquid supercritical carbon dioxide (CO2) to target reservoir, injecting pre-acid into target reservoir, injecting high-viscosity slick water fracturing fluid mixed with 40/70 mesh ceramsite into target reservoir, injecting low-viscosity slick water fracturing fluid mixed with 70/140 mesh quartz sand into target reservoir, injecting medium viscosity slick water fracturing fluid mixed with 70/140 mesh another quartz sand and medium viscosity slick water fracturing fluid mixed with 40/70 mesh another ceramsite sequentially into target reservoir, injecting another high viscosity slick water fracturing fluid mixed with 40/70 mesh additional ceramsite into target reservoir, orderly pouring medium-viscosity slick water fracturing fluid with viscosity of 15-20 mPa.second into target reservoir for displacement construction, and injecting clean water into target reservoir for displacement construction. The viscosity of high-viscosity slick water fracturing fluid, low-viscosity slick water fracturing fluid, medium viscosity slick water fracturing fluid, and another high-viscosity slick water fracturing fluid is 50-70 mPa.second, 2-4 mPa.second, 15-20 mPa.second and 50-70 mPa.second, respectively. The sand ratio of ceramsite, quartz sand, another quartz sand, another ceramsite and additional ceramsite is 3-5%, 6-15%, 10-16%, 8-13% and 13-23%, respectively.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The CO2 has injecting liquid amount of 200-300 m3 and injection volume of 2.5-3.5 m3/minute. The pre-acid comprises 100 pts. wt. clean water, 15 pts. wt. industrial hydrochloric acid, 2 pts. wt. corrosion inhibitor, 2 pts. wt. iron ion stabilizer, 2 pts. wt. clay stabilizer and 2 pts. wt. emulsion-breaking discharge aiding agent. The corrosion inhibitor is imidazoline corrosion inhibitor. The iron ion stabilizer is citric acid, trisodium triacetic acid or tetrasodium EDTA. The clay stabilizer is tetramethylammonium chloride and swelling agent at weight ratio of 7:3. The pre-acid has pouring liquid amount of 15-20 m3 and injection volume of 1.5-2.0 m3/minute. The high-viscosity slick water fracturing fluid comprises 0.4-0.6 wt.% anionic polyacrylamide with molecular weight of 1000-1500, 0.1-0.15 wt.% oil cleaning agent, 0.3-0.5 wt.% cleanup additive, 0.5-1 wt.% anti-swelling agent and balance water. The medium-viscosity slick water fracturing fluid comprises 0.2-0.3 wt.% anionic polyacrylamide with molecular weight of 1000-1500, 0.1-0.15 wt.% oil cleaning agent, 0.3-0.5 wt.% cleanup additive, 0.5-1 wt.% anti-swelling agent and balance water. The low-viscosity slick water fracturing fluid comprises 0.1 wt.% anionic polyacrylamide with molecular weight of 1000-1500, 0.1-0.15 wt.% oil cleaning agent, 0.3-0.5 wt.% cleanup additive, 0.5-1 wt.% anti-swelling agent and balance water. The volume ratio of ceramsite is 4-10:47-58:38-48. The volume ratio of quartz sand is 57-67:32-40.
DC H01 (Obtaining crude oil and natural gas - including exploration, drilling, well completion, production and treatment. General off-shore platform and drilling technology is included together with the treatment of tar sands and oil shales (C10G, E21B).); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); Q49 (Mining (E21))

PT P
PN CN110152646-A
TI Preparation of immobilized cerium oxide-activated carbon sphere photocatalyst used for photoreduction of carbon dioxide, involves using phenol, aldehyde compound, ethylene glycol, cerium salt, ammonium metavanadate and sodium lauryl sulfate.
AU ZHANG X
   REN G
   ZHANG C
   LI R
   LIU J
   WANG Y
   FAN C
AE UNIV TAIYUAN TECHNOLOGY (UYTL-C)
GA 2019773217
AB    NOVELTY - The preparation of immobilized cerium oxide-activated carbon sphere photocatalyst, involves (1) adding phenol, aldehyde compound and ethylene glycol to three-necked flask containing water, and stirring, adding ammonium metavanadate, controlling temperature of oil bath at 60-120 degrees C, stirring to obtain mixed solution, (2) adding cerium salt and sodium lauryl sulfate to mixed solution, maintaining the temperature at 60-120 degrees C, reacting, adding hexamethylenetetramine to above solution, reacting, suction filtering and washing with dilute alkali solution, drying to obtain rare earth element cerium-phenolic resin ball, (3) placing rare earth element cerium-phenolic resin ball in tube furnace and carbonizing under protection of nitrogen at 500-1000 degrees C, switching the water vapor for activating, cooling to room temperature after the reaction is finished, washing 3 times with dilute acid, washing with distilled water until neutral, and drying at room temperature.
   USE - Preparation of immobilized cerium oxide-activated carbon sphere photocatalyst used for photoreduction of carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparation of immobilized cerium oxide-activated carbon sphere photocatalyst with high stability, high adsorption capacity, high physical and chemical properties, well-developed pore structure, uniform particle size and uniform dispersion of cerium oxide, excellent photoreduction of carbon dioxide activity and stability, under mild condition, by simple economical, easy, stable and industrial process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The preparation of immobilized cerium oxide-activated carbon sphere photocatalyst, involves (1) adding 30-60 pts. wt. phenol, 40-80 pts. wt. aldehyde compound and 15-35 pts. wt. ethylene glycol to a three-necked flask containing 200-300 pts. wt. water, and stirring for 0.5-1.5 hours, adding 2-5 pts. wt. ammonium metavanadate, controlling temperature of oil bath at 60-120 degrees C, stirring for 0.5-2 hours to obtain mixed solution, (2) adding 5-10 pts. wt. cerium salt and 1-9 pts. wt. sodium lauryl sulfate to mixed solution, maintaining the temperature at 60-120 degrees C, reacting for 0.5-2 hours, adding 1-3 pts. wt. hexamethylenetetramine to above solution, reacting for 4-8 hours, suction filtering and washing with dilute alkali solution, drying at 60-100 degrees C for 10-24 hours to obtain rare earth element cerium-phenolic resin ball, step (3) placing rare earth element cerium-phenolic resin ball in tube furnace and carbonizing under protection of nitrogen at 500-1000 degrees C, switching the water vapor to temperature of 700-1000 degrees C and heating rate of 1.5-5 degrees C/minute for activating for 1-3 hours, cooling to room temperature after the reaction is finished, washing 3 times with dilute acid, washing with distilled water until neutral, and drying at room temperature.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The dilute alkali solution is potassium hydroxide, sodium hydroxide and/or aqueous ammonia. The cerium salt is cerium nitrate, cerium acetate and/or cerium chloride. The dilute acid is dilute nitric acid, sulfuric acid and/or hydrochloric acid.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The phenol is phenol, m-cresol, p-phenylphenol and/or 6-tert-butyl m-cresol. The aldehyde compound is formaldehyde or furfural.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.)

PT P
PN CN109868267-A; CN109868267-B
TI Improving transfection efficiency of EGFP plasmid involves resuscitating cells into T75 placing bottle, using nutrient solution containing 0.8-2g three antibiotics and 0.5-1.5g bactericide, after culturing for 3 generations.
AU LI H
   LI Z
   DING Z
   ZHANG R
   WANG T
AE GUANGZHOU PACKGENE BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 201954505J
AB    NOVELTY - Improving transfection efficiency of EGFP plasmid involves resuscitating the cells into the T75 placing bottle, using a nutrient solution containing 0.8-2 g of a three antibiotics and 0.5-1.5g of a bactericide, after culturing for 3 generations, harvesting 293T suspension cells having a good cell state, inoculating them into 3 shake flasks for culture, and after reaching the specified cell culture parameters, putting them into the incubator and setting the culture parameters of the carbon dioxide constant temperature shaking incubator, culturing for 3 generations until the cells reach specific conditions to obtain a suspension containing suspension cells.
   USE - Method for improving transfection efficiency of EGFP plasmid.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to improve transfection efficiency of EGFP plasmid, which improves the transfection efficiency of the EGFP plasmid, and the transfection rate of the EGFP plasmid can reach 80% or more, has a simple operation process, and greatly saves the experiment cost.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Improving transfection efficiency of EGFP plasmid involves resuscitating the cells into the T75 placing bottle, using a nutrient solution containing 0.8-2 g of a three antibiotics and 0.5-1.5g of a bactericide, after culturing for 3 generations, harvesting 293T suspension cells having a good cell state, inoculating them into 3 shake flasks for culture, and after reaching the specified cell culture parameters, putting them into the incubator and setting the culture parameters of the carbon dioxide constant temperature shaking incubator, culturing for 3 generations until the cells reach specific conditions to obtain a suspension containing suspension cells. The suspension is centrifuged containing the suspension cells at 1000-1500 revolutions for 1-10 minutes, and removing the supernatant, adding the transfection mixture containing the EGFP plasmid, and gently shaking and uniformly mixing to obtain a transfection suspension. The incubation and culture of 293T cells includes placing the transfection suspension in a carbon dioxide constant temperature shaking incubator, and setting the incubation parameters, performing static or dynamic incubation for 1-6 hours, after the incubation, adding half of the original volume containing 1-20% bovine serum and the three antibiotics to the culture medium, setting the culture parameters after the incubation, and carrying out the culture for 20-28 hours after the incubation to obtain a mixed solution containing the EGFP plasmid, centrifuging the mixture containing the EGFP plasmid at 1000-1500 revolutions per minute for 1-10 minutes, and discarding all the supernatant, adding the culture medium containing no serum, and setting the culture parameters after the liquid exchange, culturing for 48-72 hours, and taking out the cells and observing under a fluorescence microscope.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Conditions: The mass ratio of ethylhexyl glycerine and alcohol sorbate is 7:4. The inoculating density is 1.0x 105-30.0x 105/mL, the cell survival rate is 90-100%, the culture parameter of the carbon dioxide constant temperature shaking culture box is 35-38 degrees C, the rotating speed is 30-100 revolutions per minute, the carbon dioxide concentration is 3-7%, the specific condition is the cell density reaches 1-9x 106/ml, cell survival rate is stabilized at 90-100%. The amount of EGFP plasmid in transfection mixed liquid containing EGFP plasmid is 100-1800 mu g. The volume of transfection mixed solution containing EGFP plasmid is 10-90mL, pH value is 6.0-7.4, transfecting the EGFP in plasmid-containing mixed solution contains a mass concentration of 2.5mg/mL polyetherimide solution, and the adding amount of polyetherimide solution is 0.5-4.5mL. The third antibody consists of penicillin, tetracycline and aminoglycoside in a mass ratio of 5:5:2. The incubation culture parameter includes a temperature of 25-37 degrees C, a rotation speed of 0-50 revolutions per minute and the culture after the incubation parameters are temperature 35-38 degrees C, rotation speed 50-100 revolutions per minute, and carbon dioxide concentration is 3-7%. The culture parameter after the liquid exchange is a temperature of 35-38 degrees C, a rotation speed of 10-80 revolutions per minute, and a carbon dioxide concentration of 3-7%.

PT P
PN CN109731555-A; CN109731555-B
TI Preparation of zeolitic imidazolate framework/grapefruit peel composite used for carbon dioxide gas adsorption by pouring functionalized grapefruit peel powder in zinc nitrate methanol solution into 2-methylimidazole methanol solution.
AU GE F
   ZHU D
   AO Y
   ZHAO Z
   GAI F
AE UNIV CHANGCHUN TECHNOLOGY (UYGL-C)
GA 2019452516
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of zeolitic imidazolate framework (ZIF-8)/grapefruit peel composite involves cutting grapefruit peel into cubes; drying at 50-70 degrees C for 12 hours; grinding into powder; putting 10-15 g ground powder in 400 ml of 1 mol/l sodium hydroxide solution; heating at 80 degrees C for 3 hours; filtering and washing to pH of 7; freeze-drying; putting 10-15 g grapefruit peel powder in 400 ml of 1mol/l nitric acid solution; heating at 80 degrees C for 2 hours; filtering and washing to pH of 7; freeze-drying; putting functionalized grapefruit peel powder in solution of 0.29376 g zinc nitrate in 10 ml methanol; ultrasonically dispersing for 30 minutes; soaking for 12 hours to obtain precursor; pouring precursor into solution containing 0.32424 g 2-methylimidazole in 10 ml of methanol; putting in oven at 60 degrees C to react for 12 hours; filtering; and freeze-drying to obtain metal organic framework (MOF)-loaded grapefruit membrane of MOF-loaded grapefruit peel powder.
   USE - The method is for preparation of zeolitic imidazolate framework (ZIF-8)/grapefruit peel composite used for carbon dioxide gas adsorption (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method uses raw material waste with low utilization cost and wide sources. It has simple operation, is non-toxic, harmless and environment-friendly, and produces ZIF-8/grapefruit peel composite with good adsorption performance, preferably has high adsorption capacity and high adsorption rate for carbon dioxide gas.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of zeolitic imidazolate framework (ZIF-8)/grapefruit peel composite comprises peeling off outer peel of grapefruit to obtain white sponge in peel; cutting peel into cubes; drying in oven at 50-70 degrees C for 12 hours; grinding into powder; putting 10-15 g ground powder in 400 ml of 1 mol/l sodium hydroxide solution; heating at 80 degrees C for 3 hours; filtering and washing to pH of 7; freeze-drying to obtain grapefruit peel powder; putting 10-15 g grapefruit peel powder in 400 ml of 1mol/l nitric acid solution; heating at 80 degrees C for 2 hours; filtering and washing to pH of 7; freeze-drying to obtain functionalized grapefruit peel powder; putting functionalized grapefruit peel powder in solution of 0.29376 g zinc nitrate in 10 ml methanol; ultrasonically dispersing for 30 minutes; soaking for 12 hours to obtain precursor; pouring precursor into solution containing 0.32424 g 2-methylimidazole in 10 ml of methanol; putting in oven at 60 degrees C to react for 12 hours; filtering; and freeze-drying to obtain metal organic framework (MOF)-loaded grapefruit membrane of MOF-loaded grapefruit peel powder.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The reaction method of zinc ions (Zn2+) and functionalized grapefruit peel assembly is carried out via electrostatic self-assembly method. The reaction method for forming topological structure of zinc ions (Zn2+) and dimethylimidazole is carried out via in-situ synthesis.
