PT P
PN CN109506419-A
TI Method for preparing purified liquid argon and/or argon product, involves obtaining pretreated gas mixture and catalytically oxidized gas mixture, contacting oxidized gas mixture with deoxygenation unit, and determining flow rate.
AU XIU G
AE XIU G (XIUG-Individual)
GA 2019285883
AB    NOVELTY - A purified liquid argon and/or argon product preparing method involves subjecting the gas mixture to a pretreatment to remove oil mist, dust and other particulate matter in it to obtain a pretreated gas mixture. The pretreated gas mixture is contacted with a catalytic oxidation unit such that the reducing gas in the gas mixture is oxidized to obtain a catalytically oxidized gas mixture. The catalytically oxidized gas mixture is contacted with a deoxygenation unit and a suitable proportion of hydrogen is introduced into the deoxygenation unit followed by contacting the hydrodeoxygenated gas mixture with an adsorption unit to remove water, carbon dioxide and/or hydrocarbons in the gas mixture to obtain a gas mixture to be rectified, determining the flow rate of the gas mixture to be subjected to the rectification treatment and adjusting the flow rate of the gas mixture and subjecting the gas mixture to be rectified to a cryogenic rectification treatment to remove nitrogen and hydrogen.
   USE - Method for preparing purified liquid argon and/or argon product.
   ADVANTAGE - The system can efficiently remove air components such as oxygen and nitrogen in a gas mixture to be treated, and other impurities such as carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, water, oil mist, to obtain high purity argon and/or liquid argon products.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A purified liquid argon and/or argon product preparing method involves subjecting the gas mixture to be treated to a pretreatment to remove oil mist, dust and other particulate matter in it to obtain a pretreated gas mixture. The pretreated gas mixture is contacted with a catalytic oxidation unit such that the reducing gas in the gas mixture is catalytically oxidized to obtain a catalytically oxidized gas mixture. The catalytically oxidized gas mixture is contacted with a deoxygenation unit and a suitable proportion of hydrogen is introduced into the deoxygenation unit to reduce the oxygen content to less than 2 ppm, to obtain hydrodeoxygenated gas mixture followed by contacting the hydrodeoxygenated gas mixture with an adsorption unit to remove water, carbon dioxide and/or hydrocarbons in the gas mixture to obtain a gas mixture to be rectified, determining the flow rate of the gas mixture to be subjected to the rectification treatment and adjusting the flow rate of the gas mixture to be rectified in the rectification treatment flow elastic range by adjusting with the rectified qualified buffer gas and subjecting the gas mixture to be rectified to a cryogenic rectification treatment to remove nitrogen and hydrogen to obtain a purified liquid argon and/or argon product. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a system.

PN WO2018162939-A1; AU2017402747-A1; CA3054479-A1; CN110392665-A; KR2019125351-A; IN201937034898-A; US2019383312-A1; EP3592690-A1; VN67441-A; JP2020509975-W; BR112019018400-A2; HK40016667-A0; RU2736252-C1; MX2019010297-A1; ZA201905686-A; JP2022046778-A; KR2401625-B1; JP7161484-B2
TI Pressure regulator valve for maintaining pressure            connected to container for beverage, has piston engages            capsule force pad when pressure in headspace falls            below predetermined level to move capsule force pad to            open capsule valve unit.
AU RADFORD M P
   MARK P R
AE CAN LAUNCH LTD (CANL-Non-standard)
   JOSEPH INT CO INC (JOSE-Non-standard)
   JOSEPH INT CO INC (JOSE-Non-standard)
   JOSEPH CO INT INC (JOSE-Non-standard)
   JOSEPH CO INT INC (JOSE-Non-standard)
   JOSEPH INT CO INC (JOSE-Non-standard)
   JOSEPH INT CO INC (JOSE-Non-standard)
   CAN LAUNCH LTD (CANL-Non-standard)
GA 201871519B
AB    NOVELTY - The valve has a multi-liter capacity for a                carbonated beverage for automatically maintaining                the pressure in the headspace (11) at a                predetermined level to assure dispensing of the                beverage. A housing (16) contains a piston (26)                divides the housing into first and second chambers                (32,34). A capsule (66) contains a pressurized gas                secured to first chamber. A valve unit (60)                releases the pressurized gas into first chamber                when the pressure within headspace falls below                predetermined level. A capsule force pad (86) is                moved between first and second positions, the                capsule valve unit is closed in first position and                the capsule valve is open in second position and                gas under pressure is directed through first                chamber of housing into headspace. A first face                (28) of piston engages capsule force pad to force                generating portion urging piston toward capsule,                when the pressure in headspace falls below                predetermined level to move capsule force pad to                second position to open capsule valve unit.
   USE - Pressure regulator valve adapted for                maintaining pressure connected to container for                carbonated beverage such as soft drink, water, and                beer.
   ADVANTAGE - Allows the use of a smaller capsule which has                a very low vent rate to prevent the liquid carbon                dioxide from entering the beverage. The multi liter                capacity for a carbonated beverage is provided for                automatically maintaining the pressure in the                headspace.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a cross-sectional view of                the structure of a lid for a container for a                beverage.
   Headspace (11)
   Housing (16)
   Piston (26)
   First face (28)
   First and second chambers (32,34)
   Valve unit (60)
   Capsule (66)
   Capsule force pad (86)
EA    (ZA201905686-A)   NOVELTY - The regulator valve includes a housing that is                provided with a piston having one surface exposed                continuously to the pressure in the headspace and                the other surface being engaged by a spring. The                source of pressurized gas such as carbon dioxide is                secured to the housing. The valve portion is                activated by the piston which moves responsive to a                differential between the spring force and the                headspace pressure to open the valve when the                headspace pressure falls below a predetermined                level to insert the carbon dioxide into the                headspace to maintain the headspace pressure                sufficient to dispense the beverage.
   USE - Pressure regulator valve adapted to be secured                within container housing for beverage to be                dispensed through aid of pressure within                headspace.

TI Brewing healthcare barley wine comprises heating            highland barley, adding highland barley root, accessing            buckle bag laminating spore yeast liquid, saccharifying            under closed condition and inoculating Saccharomyces            cerevisiae.
AU XUE D
   CENG X
   ZENG X
AE UNIV HUBEI TECHNOLOGY (UYHI-C)
   UNIV HUBEI TECHNOLOGY (UYHI-C)
GA 201808579C
AB    NOVELTY - Brewing healthcare barley wine comprises                heating highland barley, adding highland barley                root, accessing buckle bag laminating spore yeast                liquid for saccharifying liquid culture under                closed condition after saccharifying, then                inoculating Saccharomyces cerevisiae, fermenting to                obtain solid fermenting; adding water into the                solid fermenting, fermenting, carrying highland                solid zymohydrolysis, adding water, and then adding                saccharifying enzyme, placing into stainless steel                tank, filling carbon dioxide and sealing the jars,                maintaining pressure and carrying heat                preservation.
   USE - The method is useful for brewing healthcare                barley wine.
   ADVANTAGE - The wine is rich in beta -glucan and has                combination of flavors.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Brewing healthcare barley wine comprises                heating highland barley, adding 7-9% highland                barley root, accessing buckle bag laminating spore                yeast liquid for saccharifying liquid culture under                closed condition after saccharifying, then                inoculating Saccharomyces cerevisiae, fermenting to                obtain solid fermenting; (ii) adding 3-4 times                water into the solid fermenting, fermenting at                10-11 degrees C for 6 days to obtain liquid                fermented wine, (iii) carrying highland solid                zymohydrolysis, adding 1-1.2 times of volume of                water, and then adding saccharifying enzyme,                placing into the stainless steel tank, filling                carbon dioxide and sealing the jars, maintaining                the pressure at 0.05-0.055 MPa, carrying heat                preservation at 30-32 degrees C for 8-12 hours;                opening the tank, cooling, adding highland barley                quality 1-1.5 times of 95% edible alcohol, filling                carbon dioxide and sealing the tank, maintaining                the pressure at 0.05-0.055 MPa at 20-22 degrees C                for 5-6 days to obtain the soaked highland barley                powder solution, (iv) adding fermentation liquid,                and fermented mash soaked highland barley powder                solution into the 3-4 times of the mass of water,                fermenting for 1-2 days at 20-23 degrees C; then                fermenting at 10-12 degrees C for 8-10 days to                obtain mature mash; (v) re-fed low temperature                fermentation into the mature mash solution adding                2-3% of highland barley powder, then adding                fermenting highland barley saccharified liquid at                30-32 degrees C 10-12 hours, fermenting at 10-12                degrees C for 2-3 days; and then adding highland                barley, the barley of the mass concentration is                10-14 g/l, continuing to ferment at 10-12 degrees C                for 4-6 days; and then adding steamed mixture                highland barley highland barley root, flower,                steamed mixture having mass concentration of 2-3                g/l to obtain the final mash; and (vi) filtering                the final mash liquid by plate-and-frame filter                press, then adopting diatomite filter for                filtering, and then finally filtering the                liquid.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Method: The saccharification in                  step (i) is a airtight saccharification carried                  for 3-3.5 days at 28-30 degrees C. The                  fermentation in step (i) is carried at 20-24                  degrees C for 8-9 days. The step (i) comprises                  accessing buckle bag laminating spore yeast                  saccharization liquid into the highland barley,                  stirring at 100-120 rotations per minute for                  20-22 hours at 26-28 degrees C to obtain the                  buckle bag laminating spore yeast liquor. The                  preparation of steamed mixture in step (v)                  comprises heating the mixture of highland barley                  root at 180-200 degrees C for 6-8 hours, adding                  heated burnt highland barley root mass 27-30% of                  highland barley roasting coke at 140-150 degrees                  C for 2 hours, and crushing to obtain highland                  barley root. The preparation of steamed barley                  comprises soaking the barley in 3-4 times of                  water at 70-75 degrees C for 4-6 hours, draining                  the water and steeping at 100 degrees C for 0.5-1                  hours in a steamer. The preparation of highland                  barley saccharified liquid comprises grinding                  steamed highland barley, adding water and adding                  saccharifying enzyme. Preferred Components: The                  dosage of the maltogenic amylase in step (iii) is                  1-1.2 % of highland barley quality. The volume of                  the highland barley saccharifying liquid in step                  (v) is 2-3% of the volume of the highland barley                  powder.

PN CN113912062-A
TI Carbon dioxide process for sugar recovery and purification of saturated tail gas carbon dioxide, involves passing sugar-rich mixed gas to juice catcher, performing cooling and tail gas desugaring process, passing mixed buffer tail gas to gas-liquid separator, drying and purifying.
AU NI J
   LU Z
   ZHONG Y
   ZHANG J
   ZENG L
   YANG S
AE GUANGZHOU HUATANG FOOD CO LTD (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 202215176X
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide process for sugar recovery and purification of saturated tail gas carbon dioxide, involves (a) passing gas generated after syrup filling in syrup filling tank to foam partition device, (b) passing sugar-rich mixed gas to first juice catcher, and performing primary cooling and tail gas desugaring process, inputting primary sugar-containing condensate to sugar-liquid collecting device for temporary storage, passing primary desugaring gas to second juice catcher for secondary cooling and tail gas desugarization to obtain sugar-free mixed gas and secondary sugar-containing condensate, (c) after passing mixed buffer tail gas to gas-liquid separator to obtain dry mixed gas and process water, recycling and reusing process water, sequentially drying the dry mixed gas and cooling to obtain dry and cold mixed gas, and passing to dry gas buffer to obtain dry buffer tail gas, and (d) purifying dry buffer tail gas by carbon dioxide gas.
   USE - Carbon dioxide process for sugar recovery and purification of saturated tail gas carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method effectively solves the technical problem of efficient recovery and purification of carbon dioxide in the saturated tail gas of carbonic acid production, reduces carbon emissions, and saves energy.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Carbon dioxide process for sugar recovery and purification of saturated tail gas carbon dioxide, involves (a) passing gas generated after syrup filling in syrup filling tank to foam partition device, where the gas generated in the saturated tank is high-concentration carbon dioxide tail gas, regularly spraying hot water to foam partition device for eliminating foam mixed in high-concentration carbon dioxide exhaust gas to obtain sugar-rich mixed gas and sugar-containing liquid after foam is partitioned, passing sugar-containing liquid to sugar liquid collection device for temporary storage, (b) passing sugar-rich mixed gas to first juice catcher, and performing primary cooling and tail gas desugaring process to obtain primary desugaring gas and a primary sugar-containing condensate, inputting primary sugar-containing condensate to sugar-liquid collecting device for temporary storage, passing primary desugaring gas to second juice catcher for secondary cooling and tail gas desugarization to obtain sugar-free mixed gas and secondary sugar-containing condensate, inputting secondary sugar-containing condensate to sugar liquid collection device for temporary storage, and passing sugar-free mixed gas to tail gas buffer to obtain mixed buffer tail gas, (c) after passing mixed buffer tail gas to gas-liquid separator to obtain dry mixed gas and process water, recycling and reusing process water, sequentially drying the dry mixed gas and cooling to obtain dry and cold mixed gas, and passing to dry gas buffer to obtain dry buffer tail gas, and (d) purifying dry buffer tail gas by carbon dioxide gas to obtain high-purity carbon dioxide gas.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step (d) involves sequentially passing dried buffer exhaust gas through nitrogen molecular sieve membrane and oxygen molecular sieve membrane for purifying carbon dioxide gas to obtain high-purity carbon dioxide gas. The step (b) involves opening front-end one-way valve by sugar-free mixed gas under pressure for passing to tail gas buffer, when the sugar-free mixed gas pressure in the tail gas buffer reaches the set pressure, and passing back-end one-way valve opened under pressure to gas-liquid separator. The step (c) involves storing dry and cold mixed gas in dry gas buffer by input check valve opened under pressure, when the pressure in dry gas buffer reaches the output pressure, opening output check valve under pressure for carbon dioxide gas purification. The method involves extracting gas flow by tail gas recovery pump, and connecting extraction end of exhaust gas recovery pump to exhaust gas buffer. The step (c) involves extracting gas flow by dry gas collection pump, and connecting suction end of dry gas collection pump to dry gas buffer.Preferred Conditions: The volume content of carbon dioxide gas in the high-concentration carbon dioxide tail gas in step (a) is 5-7%, the temperature is 78-82degrees Celsius, the temperature of sugar-rich mixed gas is 78-82degrees Celsius. In step (b), the temperature of primary desugaring gas is 73-77degrees Celsius, the temperature of sugar-free mixed gas is 58-62degrees Celsius, the temperature of mixed buffer tail gas is 53-57degrees Celsius. In step (c), the temperature of dry-cooled mixed gas is 33-37degrees Celsius, the humidity is 8-12%, the volume content of carbon dioxide gas in high-purity carbon dioxide gas in step (d) is 30-40%.

   SHI Y
AE UNIV DALIAN TECHNOLOGY (UYDA-C)
GA 2022685923
AB    NOVELTY - Method comprises (1) mixing dicyandiamide, ammonium chloride, carbon nano-tube in a mass ratio of 10:10:1, dispersing in the deionized water, ultrasonically processing to disperse uniformly to obtain the mixed solution, where the mass concentration of dicyandiamide is 3 g/l, (2) stirring and heating the mixed solution obtained in the step (1) at 60-80degreesC water bath, evaporating the water in the mixed solution, obtaining the grey black solid of the carbon nano-tube surface with recrystallizing dicyandiamide and ammonium chloride, (3) ball-milling the obtained grey black solid into uniform grey black powder, (3) transferring the carbon nanotube composite material to uniformly mixed grey black powder obtained in the step (1) into a tubular furnace, introducing inert gas for 1 hour, then calcining for 1-2 hours at 550degreesC, cooling to room temperature, and grinding the black solid obtained by calcining.

TI Device for phase control of friction welding, has            control gate installed on shell, limiting hole opened            in control gate, rotating rod installed at corner in            limiting hole, limiting assembly located in limiting            hole, and fire extinguishing having pressure box and            inflammable rod.
AU WU J
   KUANG L
   ZHANG X
AE HUNAN KUNDING CNC TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (HUNA-Non-standard)
GA 202219007M
AB    NOVELTY - The device for phase control of friction                welding, has a control gate installed on the shell.                Rotationally connected gate handles are installed                on the control gate. A limiting hole is opened in                the control gate. A rotating rod for rotational                connection is installed at the corner in the                limiting hole. A limiting assembly (4) is located                in the limiting hole, and has a limiting rod (41),                a limiting groove (42), a telescopic spring (43), a                clamping hole (44), a pressing rod (45) and a                telescopic rod (46). The limiting rod is installed                in the limiting hole. The telescopic spring that is                fixedly connected is installed in the limiting                groove. The top part of both ends of the limiting                rod are provided with clamping holes. The slidingly                connected pressing rod is installed in the clamping                hole. A fire extinguishing assembly is installed                inside the shell and has a pressure box and an                inflammable rod.
   USE - Device for controlling phase of friction                welding.
   ADVANTAGE - When the device for friction welding phase                control is used, if the temperature is too high,                the inside of the device is burnt, the combustible                rod is ignited by fire. When the combustible rod is                burnt, the opening at the bottom of the pressure                box leaks, the liquid carbon dioxide in the                pressure box flows out. When the liquid carbon                dioxide contacts the pressure outside the pressure                box, the liquid carbon dioxide is quickly vaporized                into a gas state and extinguishes the flame in the                device, so that the guarantee of the device on the                working safety is effectively improved. When the                device is used, the two fans can be turned on, the                two fans can rotate in different directions, so                that the internal air and the external air of the                device can be circulated, inert gases can be                eliminated. After carbon dioxide extinguishes                flame, the fans can be turned on to quickly purify                the air in the control device. The inert gases such                as carbon dioxide are prevented from damaging the                inside of the device, and the protection of the                device on the device is effectively improved.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                interior diagram of the limiting rod.

AE SIEMENS AG (SIEI-C)

The gaseous impurities are removed from a gas space (36) of electrochemical cell (30) by setting a volume flow of a gas mixture comprising CO2 and water is higher than a regulating volume flow to the gas space. The electrochemically active cell is cooled by supplying an electrolyte from a reservoir (56) into an electrolyte space (38) by a pump device (18) and the pump device is switched off for supplying an electrolyte.
   USE - Method for controlling device for carbon dioxide electrolysis.
   ADVANTAGE - The energy consumption of the device is reduced. The damage to the cathode due to mechanical stress can be avoided. The electrochemical cell is cooled more quickly, which speeds up the shutdown of the device.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The predetermined differential pressure is set up by setting the volume flow of the gas mixture to 1% by volume to 15% by volume of the regulating volume flow. The volume flow of the gas mixture and the temperature in the electrode space and the differential pressure in the electrode space are detected by the sensor element. The concentration of the gaseous impurity and the current density applied to the gas diffusion electrode is detected by the sensor element. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a device for carbon dioxide electrolysis and carbon monoxide electrolysis, has pump device with fluid is transported between a reservoir in the electrochemical cell and is connected to a heat exchanger. A control device controls the volume flow of the gas mixture and the temperature in the electrode space and the differential pressure in the electrode space and the concentration of the gaseous impurity and the indirectly controllable current density applied to the gas diffusion electrode. The electrochemical cell has a sensor element in the cathode compartment and in the anode compartment.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawings show the sectional view of a device for carbon dioxide electrolysis and carbon monoxide electrolysis and flowchart illustrating the method for controlling device for carbon dioxide electrolysis.

   Gas space (36)
   Electrolyte space (38)
   Gas diffusion electrode (40)

PN CN113080225-A
TI Preparing ultra-micro black tea powder with high anthocyanin content for pastry comprises mixing purple juan tea and water, using organic weak acid to adjust the pH, soaking, freezing and pressing, thawing to obtain crushed aggregates.
AU WANG J
   ZHANG C
AE WANG J (WANG-Individual)
GA 202180024F
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing ultra-micro black tea powder with high anthocyanin content comprises mixing the purple juan tea and water according to mass ratio of 1:1-1:5, and using organic weak acid to adjust the pH to 4.0-5.0; soaking, freezing and pressing, and thawing to obtain the crushed aggregates; crushing frozen crushed aggregates, polyethylene glycol-b-poly (N-amidino) dodecyl acrylamide, cyclodextrin, dopamine, carbomer, propylene glycol, glycerol and water; crushing the crushed material, polyethylene glycol-b-poly (N-amidino) dodecyl acrylamide, dopamine, propylene glycol, glycerol and water, and mixing, introducing carbon dioxide gas with 10-20 wt.% of the thawed crushed material, after ending the carbon dioxide gas, filtering, removing the filter residue to obtain primary mixed filtrate; adding cyclodextrin and carbomer into the primary mixed filter liquid, stirring and mixing, freezing and drying wet gel, and crushing.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing ultra-micro black tea powder for pastry.
   ADVANTAGE - The method can effectively avoid oxidation of anthocyanin in preparation and storage process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing ultra-micro black tea powder with high anthocyanin content comprises mixing the purple juan tea and water according to mass ratio of 1:1-1:5, and using organic weak acid to adjust the pH to 4.0-5.0; soaking, freezing and pressing, and thawing to obtain the crushed aggregates; taking 100-200 pts. wt. frozen crushed aggregates, 10-15 pts. wt. polyethylene glycol-b-poly (N-amidino) dodecyl acrylamide, 8-10 pts. wt. cyclodextrin, 0.8-1.5 pts. wt. dopamine, 4-6 pts. wt. carbomer, 8-20 pts. wt. propylene glycol, 2-4 pts. wt. glycerol and 60-80 pts. wt. water; crushing the crushed material, polyethylene glycol-b-poly (N-amidino) dodecyl acrylamide, dopamine, propylene glycol, glycerol and water, stirring and mixing, slowly introducing carbon dioxide gas with 10-20 wt.% of the thawed crushed material, after ending the carbon dioxide gas, filtering, removing the filter residue to obtain primary mixed filtrate; adding cyclodextrin and carbomer into the primary mixed filter liquid, continuously stirring and mixing, stopping stirring, introducing argon gas pre-cooled to below room temperature, argon gas inlet amount and carbon dioxide amount, ending the argon gas inlet, refrigerating and allowing to stand to obtain wet gel; and soaking the wet gel with vegetable oil for 3-5 times, freezing and drying the wet gel, crushing to obtain the high anthocyanin content ultra-red tea powder for pastry.

TI Extracting osmanthus oil using supercritical            carbon dioxide fluid extraction comprises freeze-drying            osmanthus, crushing osmanthus fragrant, sieving,            sealing, pouring grape seed oil, stirring, extracting            and freezing grape seed oil.
AU YI F
   LV C
   HAO L
   CUI X
   ZHANG S
AE SHANGHAI INST TECHNOLOGY (SHGH-C)
GA 2020536542
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting Osmanthus oil using supercritical                carbon dioxide fluid extraction comprises (a)                freeze-drying the fresh Osmanthus in the freeze                dryer to obtain dried Osmanthus, collecting the                Osmanthus cell liquid into the refrigerator for                storage, (b) crushing the dried Osmanthus fragrant,                sieving to obtain dried osmanthus fragrants with                uniform particles, sealing, storing in a dry and                clean ziplock bag, (c) pouring grape seed oil into                dried Osmanthus, stirring well, ultra sonically                processing, (d) loading Osmanthus fragrants                obtained in step (c) into an extraction kettle for                extraction to obtain grape seed oil containing                Osmanthus fragrants extract and (e) freezing the                grape seed oil containing the Osmanthus fragrants                extract at 5-8 degrees C and performing suction                filter for 3-5 times, until the filtrate does not                appear flocculent substance after centrifugation to                obtain Osmanthus oil.
   USE - The method is useful for extracting Osmanthus                oil using supercritical carbon dioxide fluid                extraction.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves the extraction rate of                essential oil in supercritical fluid extraction                plants, especially flowers; attaches importance to                the use of by-products in the extraction process;                improves the commercial added value of Osmanthus                fragrant and promotes the commercialization of                products containing Osmanthus fragrant extract                .
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for: (i)                Osmanthus lip balm comprising 6.00-12.00 wt.%.                Osmanthus oil, 54.18-60.18 wt.%. olive oil, 0.75                wt.%. vitamin E, 9.00 wt.%. cocoa butter and native                beeswax (remaining amount); and (ii) Osmanthus                protecting hand cream comprising Phase A comprises                4.5 wt.%. emulsifier, 10.0 wt.%. emollient, 3.00                wt.%. emollient II, 2.50 wt.%. humectants one and                2.00 wt.%. emollient three. The Osmanthus oil                extracted by the supercritical carbon dioxide fluid                extraction process is 1.00-3.00 wt.%. The phase B                including 5.00 wt.%. humectants two and 0.15 wt.%.                gelling agent. The Osmanthus cell fluid collected                in step (a) of the supercritical carbon dioxide                fluid extraction process of Osmanthus oil is                0.00-13.00 wt.%. and the solvent is 56.15-71.15                wt.%. The phase C including 0.70 wt.%.                preservatives. The emulsifiers comprises cetyl                alcohol, glyceryl stearate, polyethylene glycol-75                stearate, cetyl alcohol-20 and stearyl-20. The                emollient one comprises 10-18carbon fatty acid                triglyceride. The emollient two comprises                cyclopentadimethylsiloxane and cyclohexylsiloxane.                The first humectant is cetearyl alcohol. The                emollient is octyldodecanol myristate. The second                humectant is glycerin. The gelling agent is xanthan                gum. The solvent is deionized water and the                preservative comprises phenoxyethanol and                ethylhexylglycerol
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (b) the                  ratio of dried Osmanthus to grape seed oil is                  2g:3ml. Preferred Method: In the step (c) the                  ultrasonic process is carried out for 2-3 hours                  for 10-15 times and each time for 10 minutes. In                  the step (d) the device used for extraction                  including an extraction kettle connected to a                  carbon dioxide gas source. The carbon dioxide gas                  source and the extraction kettle are also                  connected through a purifier and a refrigeration                  system. The upper part of the extraction kettle                  is connected to the inlet of a separation kettle                  one through a parallel flat pressure valve and a                  booster valve. The upper part of the separation                  kettle one is connected to a feeding port of a                  separation kettle two. The bottom of the                  extraction kettle, separation one and separation                  kettle two are all equipped with discharging                  ports. The temperature of the extraction kettle                  is 40 degrees C and the pressure is 35 MPa. The                  flow rate of carbon dioxide flow source is 30                  l/hour. The temperature of the separation kettle                  one is 42 degrees C and the pressure is 6 MPa.                  The temperature of separation kettle two is 34                  degrees C, and the pressure is 5MPa.The material                  is added to the extraction kettle for sealing,                  and continuous extraction is performed for 2.5                  hours after the product obtained by the                  separation kettle two is added anhydrous sodium                  sulfate to absorb water, it is mixed with the                  product obtained by the separation kettle I to                  obtain Osmanthus fragrant orange-yellow oily                  liquid namely grape seed oil containing Osmanthus                  extract. In the step (e) the standing time is 12                  hours, the suction filtration uses medium-speed                  quantitative filter paper and the centrifugation                  time is 15 minutes. The preparation of Osmanthus                  lip balm comprises (1) mixing the soil beeswax,                  Osmanthus oil, olive oil and cocoa butter,                  heating in a water bath until the soil beeswax                  and cocoa butter are completely melted, uniformly                  stirring until no bubbles appears, (2) adding                  vitamin E after stirring, cooling the oily liquid                  obtained in step (1) at 60 degrees C, stirring                  and evenly mixing the mixture, (3) carrying out                  filling work in grinding tools after the oily                  liquid obtained in step (c) naturally cooled to                  the wall hanging phenomenon and (4) scraping the                  top to shape and installing the cover after                  standing the grinding tool for 2-3 hours. In the                  step (1) the rotation speed of the stirring is                  150revolution/minute. The preparation of                  Osmanthus protecting hand cream comprises mixing                  the raw materials of Phase A, heating to 80                  degrees C to fully dissolve, dispersing xanthan                  gum in glycerin, stirring evenly, dispersing in                  solvent to obtain a solution, adding phase B into                  the solution, dissolving, heating to 80 degrees                  C, adding phase A to phase B when both phases are                  at 80 degrees C, homogenizing, emulsifying for                  4minutes, stirring at 180revolutions/minute,                  completely stirring evenly, reducing below 50                  degrees C, adding phase C and continuously                  stirring until uniform.

The second mixing is carried out at critical                pressure below supercritical carbon dioxide                fluid.
   USE - The method is used for dry mixing battery                electrode material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves dispersion uniformity of                powder while avoiding use of organic solvent,                avoids problem of uneven distribution of components                in powder, controls pressure of mixing, and                continuously converts carbon dioxide between                supercritical fluid gas to improve dispersing                effect of component.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a                method for preparing battery electrode sheet                comprising preparing mixed powder according to dry                mixing method, electrostatic spraying mixed powder                on surface of current collector and hot-pressing                current collector sprayed with mixed powder.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: Supercritical carbon                  dioxide fluid mixing is done at 30-40degrees                  Celsius at supercritical carbon dioxide fluid                  filling speed of 50-100 sccm. First mixing is                  done for 5-30 minutes at pressure of 7.9-8.1 MPa.                  Second mixing is done for 2-10 minutes at                  pressure of 6-7 MPa. The electrostatic spraying                  is done at voltage of 30-40 kV, main gas flow                  speed of 7-13 psi and atomizing gas flow speed of                  7-13 psi. Hot pressing is done at 180- 220degrees                  Celsius for 1-10 seconds at pressure of 10-20                  MPa.Preferred Components: The mass ratio of                  anode active material to conductive agent to                  adhesive is 50-95:2-25:3-25. The anode active                  material is lithium iron phosphate, lithium                  cobaltate, lithium manganate, lithium titanate                  and/or ternary material.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The conductive agent                  is carbon black, carbon nanotube, graphene,                  graphite and/or carbon fiber.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The adhesive is PTFE,                  polyvinylidene fluoride, polyacrylic acid,                  chitosan, guar gum and/or carboxymethyl                  cellulose.

TI Hydrogen production device comprises dry reforming reaction unit which is used for directly reacting methane and carbon dioxide in bio gas to produce synthesis gas containing hydrogen and gas shift unit is used for reacting carbon monoxide.
AU HAM H C
   KWON B W
   LEE C H
   OH J
   JANG S C
   CHOI S
   PARK H S
   YOON C W
   HAN J
   YOON S P
   NAM S W
   LEE K B
   CHOI S H
AE KOREA INST SCI & TECHNOLOGY (KSCT-C)
GA 2019138178
AB    NOVELTY - Hydrogen production device comprises a dry reforming reaction unit which is used for directly reacting methane and carbon dioxide in bio gas to produce a synthesis gas containing hydrogen and a gas shift unit is used for reacting carbon monoxide in the synthesis gas which is produced in the dry reforming reaction unit with water vapor to produce carbon dioxide and hydrogen for capturing the produced carbon dioxide.
   USE - Hydrogen production device.
   ADVANTAGE - The hydrogen production device is useful as a small-scale hydrogen production device for polymer electrolyte fuel cells, reduces energy consumption and cost-effective, and simple to process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for producing hydrogen by using hydrogen production device, which involves:
   (A) dry reforming reaction unit and a gas shift unit, and directly reacting methane and carbon dioxide in biogas to produce a synthesis gas; and
   (B) reacting carbon monoxide in the synthesis gas which is produced in the dry reforming reaction unit with water vapor to produce carbon dioxide and hydrogen, and capturing the produced carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The carbon dioxide is captured in the gas shift unit which is supplied to the dry reforming reaction unit that is recycled in the reaction with methane in biogas. The gas shift unit comprises a hydrogen production catalyst and an adsorbent for capturing carbon dioxide. The hydrogen production catalyst comprises one transition metal which is selected from group consisting of copper, nickel, and iron. The adsorbent for capturing carbon dioxide comprises an alkali metal double salt-based adsorbent or a hydrotalcite-based adsorbent. The alkali metal double salt-based adsorbent is an adsorbent prepared by coprecipitation or impregnation process of an alkaline earth metal carbonate, and the hydrotalcite-based adsorbent is an adsorbent which is prepared by mixing a chloride which is represented by group of linear formulae (1 x)M(OH)2, (1 x)M(NO3)2, xL(OH)3, xL(NO3)3, (x/2)A2CO3, where x is a number of 0.17-0.4, M is selected from the group consisting of magnesium, zinc, and nickel, L is selected from the group consisting of aluminum, gallium, iron and manganese, and A is selected from group consisting of sodium, potassium, rubidium, cesium and francium. The carbon dioxide is captured from the reaction of carbon monoxide and water vapor is recycled in the reaction with methane in biogas in the dry reforming reaction unit. The gas shift unit comprises a hydrogen production catalyst and an adsorbent for capturing carbon dioxide. The dry reforming reaction unit comprises a catalyst comprises compound which is represented by linear formula Sr1-yYyTiRuxO3- delta , where x is 0-1, y is 0-0.1, and delta is 0-1. Preferred Compositions: The weight ratio of hydrogen production catalyst and adsorbent for capturing carbon dioxide is 1:9-9:1. Preferred Conditions: The methane conversion rate of catalyst comprising 80 wt.% or more during an operation period of 60-120 hours at a temperature of 700-900 degrees C. The preheating unit is operated by using the waste heat of the dry reforming reaction unit as a heat source.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN CN113398622-A
TI Preparing silver wormwood primary pulp useful in            preparation of daily chemical product, comprises            extracting wormwood coarse powder in compressed            carbondioxide supercritical extraction kettle and            molecular-distilling and scraping suspension.
AU WANG Y
   REN Z
   GUO Y
   MA J
   WANG Q
   LIAO X
AE UNIV JINAN (UJIN-C)
GA 2021B3075P
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing silver wormwood primary pulp                comprises e.g. (i) taking the ground part of the                dried wormwood, cutting it, crushing into 5-30                meshes of wormwood coarse powder; (ii) adding the                wormwood coarse powder into a supercritical                extraction kettle, introducing the compressed                carbondioxide, setting pressure of the analyzing                kettle to 2-20 MPa and the temperature is 30-70                degrees C to obtain the wormwood crude extract;                (iii) placing the crude wormwood extract at 40-80                degrees C, stirring and heating to remove residual                carbondioxide to obtain the wormwood suspension;                and (iv) transferring the wormwood suspension into                molecular distillation film-scraping device feeding                port, dividing into single-stage or multi-stage                setting, controlling the feeding speed, where the                feeding speed of each stage is 0.5-2 ml/min, the                distillation pressure is 10-100 Pa, the scraping                speed is 30-300 revolutions/minute and the                distillation temperature is 20-80 degrees C.
   USE - The silver wormwood primary pulp is useful in                the preparation of daily chemical products,                preferably facial mask liquid, lotion, cream, skin                care lotion, facial cleanser, shower gel and                shampoo with moisturizing, repairing and                antiinflammatory effects (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: can adopt a supercritical carbon                dioxide extraction process, optimize process                parameters; is efficient and solvent-free; and uses                the physical characteristics of carbon dioxide                super critically in a green and environmentally                friendly manner which directly extracts and                collects the plant cell fluid and oil in the                harvested wormwood; adopts molecular distillation                to separate supercritical carbon dioxide from the                crude wormwood extract to obtain clear and                transparent wormwood pulp with monoterpenoid                volatile components, flavonoids, and                polysaccharides with antioxidant and                antiinflammatory effects and good water solubility,                moisturizing properties and special physical and                chemical properties (e.g. pH value) which is widely                used in daily care products and wash care products                e.g. lotions, emulsions and creams, etc.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing silver wormwood primary pulp                comprises (i) taking the ground part of the dried                wormwood, cutting it, crushing into 5-30 meshes of                wormwood coarse powder; (ii) adding the wormwood                coarse powder into a supercritical extraction                kettle, introducing the compressed carbondioxide,                setting the extraction pressure at 10-60 MPa, the                extraction temperature at 25-80 degrees C and the                extraction time is 0.5-6 hours, the carbondioxide                flow rate is 30-80 liter/h, setting the pressure of                the analyzing kettle to 2-20 MPa and the                temperature is 30-70 degrees C to obtain the                wormwood crude extract; (iii) placing the crude                wormwood extract at 40-80 degrees C, stirring and                heating to remove residual carbondioxide to obtain                the wormwood suspension; and (iv) transferring the                wormwood suspension into molecular distillation                film-scraping device feeding port, dividing into                single-stage or multi-stage setting, controlling                the feeding speed, where the feeding speed of each                stage is 0.5-2 ml/min, the distillation pressure is                10-100 Pa, the scraping speed is 30-300                revolutions/minute and the distillation temperature                is 20-80 degrees C and the wormwood in the step (i)                Artemisia argyi, Artemisia argyi var gracilis,                folium artemisiae argyi, Artemisia argyi, Ambrosia                artemisiifolia, big leaf wormwood, Artemisia,                Blumea balsamifera, mugwort, fragrant wormwood,                Artemisia indica, yellow grass, Artemisia                lavandulifolia, white Artemisia wormwood, wormwood,                red wormwood, heat wormwood, Artemisia stolonifera,                Artemisia gilvescens, Artemisia lavandulaefolia,                Artemisia verlotiorum, Artemisia leucophylla,                Artemisia mongolica, Artemisia rubripes, Artemisia                princeps and/or Artemisia igniaria. INDEPENDENT                CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) silver wormwood primary pulp, prepared as                mentioned above; and
   (2) application of the silver wormwood primary                pulp in the preparation of daily chemical products                with moisturizing, repairing and antiinflammatory                effects.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Method: The step (ii) comprises                  the extraction pressure in the supercritical                  extraction kettle is 10-50 MPa, the extraction                  temperature is 30-70 degrees C, the extraction                  time is 0.5-6 hours, and the carbon dioxide flow                  rate is 30-90 liter/h or it can be extracted in                  two stages comprises setting the extraction                  pressure in the first-level supercritical                  extraction kettle to be 10-50 MPa and the                  extraction temperature is 30-70 degrees C and the                  extraction pressure in the secondary                  supercritical extraction kettle is set to 20-40                  MPa, the extraction temperature is 30-70 degrees                  C, the extraction time is 0.5-6 hours and the                  carbon dioxide flow rate is 30-90 liter/h. The                  step (ii) comprises adding an extraction                  cosolvent comprising petroleum ether, diethyl                  ether, ethyl acetate, n-butanol, 30-90% ethanol,                  and/or absolute ethanol. The pressure of the                  analyzing kettle in the step (ii) is 2-20 MPa,                  the temperature is 30-70 degrees C, or the                  dividing analysis into two stages, setting the                  analysis pressure in the first-stage                  supercritical analysis kettle to 10-20MPa, and                  the extraction temperature is 50-70 degrees C and                  setting the extraction pressure in the secondary                  supercritical extraction kettle to be 2-10 MPa,                  and the extraction temperature to be 30-50                  degrees C and the total amount of extraction                  cosolvent in the step S2 is 0.2 to 3 times the                  amount of medicinal materials, adding once in                  every 20-60 minutes, and the amount of each input                  is 0-0.8 times the amount of medicinal materials.                  The first-stage molecular distillation in step                  (iv) the distillation pressure is 10-80 Pa, the                  film scraping speed is 30-260 revolutions/minute                  and the distillation temperature is 40-80 degrees                  C and two-stage molecular distillation, the                  distillation pressure is 10-75Pa, the scraping                  speed is 30-120 revolutions/minute and the                  distillation temperature is 60-120 degrees C.                  Preferred Components: The total amount of                  extraction cosolvent in the step (ii) is 0.2 to 3                  times the amount of medicinal materials, adding                  once in every 20-60 minutes, and the amount of                  each input is 0 to 0.8 times the amount of                  medicinal materials. The daily chemical products                  comprising facial mask liquid, lotion, cream,                  skin care lotion, facial cleanser, shower gel and                  shampoo. The facial mask liquid comprises 2-3                  wt.% glycerol, 3-5 wt.% propylene glycol, 1-5                  wt.% silver wormwood primary pulp, 0.1-0.5 wt.%                  honeysuckle extract, 0.1-0.3 wt.% xanthan gum,                  0.1-0.5 wt.% seaweed polysaccharide, 0.5-1 wt.%                  preservative and 84.7-93.2 wt.% water.
   ACTIVITY - Antiinflammatory.

PN CN113058404-A
TI High phosphorus-containing, chlorine-containing, sulfur-containing waste gas/waste liquid processing system has incinerator used for heating waste gas/waste liquid, oxidizing, decomposing into flue gas containing water, carbon dioxide.
AU HOU G
   LI N
   LI X
AE TIANJIN CHENCHUANG ENVIRONMENT ENG SCI (TIAN-Non-standard)
GA 202178465J
AB    NOVELTY - High phosphorus-containing, chlorine-containing, sulfur-containing waste gas/waste liquid processing system has incinerator used for heating waste gas/waste liquid, oxidizing, decomposing into flue gas containing water (H2O), carbon dioxide (CO2), hydrochloric acid (HCl), tricyclo(3.3.1.13,7)tetraphosphoxane 1,3,5,7-tetraoxide (P4O10), sulfur dioxide (SO2) and NOx within the range of 1100-1200 degrees C, a quenching tank used for absorbing the hydrochloric acid, tricyclo(3.3.1.13,7)tetraphosphoxane 1,3,5,7-tetraoxide, sulfur dioxide in the flue gas, a demisting device used for separating the fog drop gas processed by the quenching tank. The acid washing tower is connected with the demisting device for absorbing the HCl. The desulfurizing tower is connected with the acid washing tower for absorbing SO2 in the flue gas and producing ammonium sulfate.
   USE - High phosphorus-containing, chlorine-containing, sulfur-containing waste gas/waste liquid processing system.
   ADVANTAGE - The system has not only processing high phosphorus, chlorine, and sulfur waste gas/waste liquid also discharging to the standard, without generating unprocessable waste residues during the treatment process, at the same time, it can by-produce high-concentration phosphoric acid, hydrochloric acid and ammonium sulfate, thus avoiding the waste of resources and having better application promotion value.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - High phosphorus-containing, chlorine-containing, sulfur-containing waste gas/waste liquid processing system has incinerator used for heating waste gas/waste liquid, oxidizing, decomposing into flue gas containing water (H2O), carbon dioxide (CO2), hydrochloric acid (HCl), tricyclo(3.3.1.13,7)tetraphosphoxane 1,3,5,7-tetraoxide (P4O10), sulfur dioxide (SO2) and NOx within the range of 1100-1200 degrees C, a quenching tank used for absorbing the hydrochloric acid, tricyclo(3.3.1.13,7)tetraphosphoxane 1,3,5,7-tetraoxide, sulfur dioxide in the flue gas, a demisting device used for separating the fog drop gas processed by the quenching tank. The acid washing tower is connected with the demisting device for absorbing the HCl. The desulfurizing tower is connected with the acid washing tower for absorbing SO2 in the flue gas and producing ammonium sulfate. The SCR reactor is connected with the desulfurizing tower for absorbing NOx in the flue gas. The quenching tank is connected with a phosphoric acid concentrating system. The desulfurizing tower is connected with an ammonium sulfate evaporating and crystallizing device. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for processing waste gas/waste liquid containing high phosphorus, chlorine and sulfur, which involves:
   (A) heating exhaust gas/waste liquid by the incinerator;
   (B) oxidizing completely;
   (C) decomposing into flue gas containing H2O, CO2, HCl, P4O10, SO2 and NOx in the range of 1100-1200 degrees C;
   (D) introducing flue gas into quench tank for absorbing HCL, P4O10 and a small amount of SO2 in the flue gas;
   (E) recycling the phosphoric acid in the quenching tank is concentrated by phosphoric acid system to obtain high-concentration phosphoric acid;
   (F) processing the flue gas by the quench tank is passed through a demister to separate the mist droplets in the flue gas;
   (G) introducing the flue gas into the acid washing tower;
   (H) absorbing the HCl;
   (I) treating the flue gas by the pickling tower enters the desulfurization tower;
   (J) absorbing SO2 in the flue gas;
   (K) producing a saturated ammonium sulfate slurry;
   (L) preparing saturated ammonium sulfate slurry by the ammonium sulfate evaporation crystallization device to obtain finished ammonium sulfate;
   (M) heating the flue gas treated by the desulfurization tower,;
   (N) entering the SCR reactor; and
   (O) absorbing the NOx in the flue gas.

PN CN112806194-A
TI Preparing heat stack by fermenting straw block comprises e.g. following two ends of the greenhouse along the mountain wall, uniformly mixing straw block, and adding straw block mixed with fermentation bacteria material.
AU GUAN F
AE JILIN ACAD AGRIC SCI (JLAA-C)
GA 202158502N
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing heat stack by fermenting straw block comprises e.g. following two ends of the greenhouse along the mountain wall, uniformly mixing straw block after pressing block by the straw briquetting machine, adding straw block mixed with fermentation bacteria material into the plastic woven bag, adding into grid box body, uniformly sprinkling water on the plastic woven bag filled with straw block on each layer, until the water content of the straw block is about 45-55%, filling the plastic woven bag filled with the straw block with the grid box, and finishing the PVC hard water supply pipe laying, after the fermentation bacteria material and watering, covering the whole grid box body by plastic film.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing heat stack by fermenting straw block.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: realizes fermentation of large quantities of straw blocks at low temperatures to solve the problem of winter greenhouses in high-cold areas; heating problem of breeding houses and other facilities also promotes the generation of large amounts of carbon dioxide during the decomposition process of straw blocks, to meet the needs of carbon dioxide for plant photosynthesis in a confined environment.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing heat stack by fermenting straw block comprises (i) following two ends of the greenhouse along the mountain wall to determine the width of not greater than 3 m, the length is 4.5-6 m of strip-shaped space, and the space along the perimeter every one inserting soil is 30 cm, the horizontal height is not greater than 2.5 m of the upright post, the upright post is a cement column, wooden column or iron column, the upright post is cylindrical and the diameter is greater than or equal to 10 cm, upright post vertical direction every 0.5 m pull a 10 iron wire, and fixing the upright post, finally forming a grid box body composed of an upright post and iron wire, (ii) uniformly mixing the straw block after pressing block by the straw briquetting machine, calculating according to each ton of straw block, the proportion of the fermentation bacteria material is 1 kg low temperature resistant microbial inoculum, 20 kg rice bran, 10 kg wheat bran, 1 kg brown sugar, 5 kg urea, 0.1 kg unslaked lime, the components are fully mixed to obtain the fermented bacteria material, (iii) adding the straw block mixed with fermentation bacteria material into the plastic woven bag of 50 cmx 80 cm, then paving the plastic woven bag filled with straw block one by one, adding into grid box body, each swinging a layer of plastic woven bag filled with straw block, along the direction of the mountain wall in the middle position of the plastic woven bag layer filled with straw block, placing one outer diameter of 40 mm, the wall thickness is not less than 10 mm of the PVC hard water supply pipe, two sides of the water supply pipe are extended out of the grid box body 10 cm, and the PVC hard water supply pipe is every 20 cm, any angle is prickles one hole with diameter of 2 cm, for ventilating and carbon dioxide gas dispersing out, (iv) uniformly sprinkling water on the plastic woven bag filled with straw block on each layer, until the water content of the straw block is about 45-55%, (v) filling the plastic woven bag filled with the straw block with the grid box, and finishing the PVC hard water supply pipe laying, after the fermentation bacteria material and watering, covering the whole grid box body by plastic film.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (ii), the components of the used fermentation bacteria material are 1 kg low temperature resistant microbial inoculum (the low temperature resistant microbial inoculum mainly contains Bacillus polymyxa (accession number is CGMCC NO: 20494) 5x 108 colony forming units/g, Trichoderma viride (3x 107 colony forming units/g), Azotobacter sphaeroides (3x 106 colony forming units/g), Aspergillus oryzae (3x 105 colony forming units/g), Lactococcus (2x 106 colony forming units/g), Bacillus circulans (2x 107 colony forming units/g), white rot fungus (2x 107 colony forming units/g), 20 kg rice bran, 10 kg wheat bran, 1 kg brown sugar, 5 kg urea, 0.1 kg unslaked lime, the mixed fermentation bacteria material can ferment dry weight 1 ton of straw block. Preferred Method: In the step (iii), the straw block mixed with the fermentation bacteria material is filled in the plastic woven bag, and one is placed in the grid box body next to one ground layer, there is a gap between the plastic woven bag, so it can contain the straw block capable of absorbing and fermenting and expanding, avoiding the expanded straw block to expand the grid box body, so as to cause the grid box body. In the step (iv), after stacking a plastic woven bag filled with straw blocks with a height of about 100-160 cm, the middle position of the plastic woven bag layer filled with straw blocks along the direction of the mountain wall is stacked, placing 1 plastic polyethylene water pipe with outer diameter of 50 mm and wall thickness not less than 20 mm, two ends of the water pipe are connected with radiating fin and hot water pipeline circulating pump with radiating device in the room, forming a closed loop for taking away the heat of the heat pile and supplying heat to the room.

PN CN111960444-A
TI Preparing lithium carbonate by using manganese-containing wastewater and waste lithium-rich lithium battery solution comprises e.g. adding carbonate into manganese-containing ammonia nitrogen wastewater, and separating solid and liquid.
AU DONG X
   PENG T
   YANG H
   LI J
   SU X
   CHEN X
   YAO J
AE GUIZHOU DACHENG HUICHENG NEW MATERIAL CO (GUIZ-Non-standard)
   HUNAN XINDA NEW MATERIAL CO LTD (HUNA-Non-standard)
GA 2020B7934M
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing lithium carbonate by using manganese-containing wastewater and waste lithium-rich lithium battery solution comprises e.g. adding carbonate into manganese-containing ammonia nitrogen wastewater, and separating solid and liquid to obtain manganese carbonate precipitation and primary filtrate, and calcining manganese carbonate to obtain manganese dioxide and carbon dioxide, adding calcium hydroxide to the primary filtrate to form hydroxides and calcium sulfate precipitation that are hardly soluble in water, simultaneously, introducing air in for aeration and stripping to separate part of the free ammonia, collecting the ammonia-containing gas, and separating the solid-liquid to obtain secondary filtrate, adding the composition of sodium carbonate and ammonium carbonate into the second filtrate, and removing the remaining calcium ions in the filtrate.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing lithium carbonate by using manganese-containing wastewater and waste lithium-rich lithium battery solution.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: produces product with more than 99.6 wt.% purity, which contains less than 0.006 wt.% magnesium, less than 0.004 wt.% calcium, less than 0.001 wt.% iron, and less than 0.009 wt.% fluorine; and produces deamminated wastewater at the bottom of the vacuum deamination tower, which contains less than or equal to 1 mg/l manganese, less than or equal to 10 mg/l ammonia nitrogen, and having total hardness of less than or equal to 50 mg/l, which is low compared with GB 31573-2015 Pollutant discharge standard for inorganic chemical industry. The treated wastewater can be discharged up to the standard or returned to the production system for reuse.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing lithium carbonate by using manganese-containing wastewater and waste lithium-rich lithium battery solution comprises adding carbonate into manganese-containing ammonia nitrogen wastewater, and separating solid and liquid to obtain manganese carbonate precipitation and primary filtrate, and calcining manganese carbonate to obtain manganese dioxide and carbon dioxide, adding calcium hydroxide to the primary filtrate to form hydroxides and calcium sulfate precipitation that are hardly soluble in water, simultaneously, introducing air in for aeration and stripping to separate part of the free ammonia, collecting the ammonia-containing gas, and separating the solid-liquid to obtain secondary filtrate, adding the composition of sodium carbonate and ammonium carbonate into the second filtrate, removing the remaining calcium ions in the filtrate, separating the solid and liquid to obtain third filtrate, adding pH adjuster into the third filtrate, placing the filtrate in a top of the vacuum ammonia removal tower after preheating, flowing the ammonia-containing wastewater in the vacuum ammonia removal tower to downwards, contacting the high-temperature steam directly into the bottom of the tower in countercurrent contact, where the ammonia wastewater that meets the discharge standards is obtained at the bottom of the vacuum ammonia removal tower, escaping ammonia-containing gas from the top of the vacuum ammonia removal tower, and combining with the ammonia-containing gas obtained after aeration stripping for use, introducing the lithium-rich solution in a filter device equipped with manganese dioxide to convert iron(II) into iron(III), and converting it into hydroxide precipitation, evaporating and concentrating, and solid-liquid separating to obtain concentrated lithium-rich solution, adding sodium carbonate into the concentrated lithium-rich solution to remove of the magnesium and calcium ions, adding mixture of sodium hydroxide and sodium carbonate to further remove the remaining magnesium and calcium ions to obtain de-impurized lithium-rich solution, performing electrodialysis of the lithium-rich solution after removal of impurities using a monovalent ion selective permeable ion exchange membrane to obtain a purified lithium-rich solution, introducing ammonia and carbon dioxide into the purified lithium-rich solution, precipitating lithium carbonate crystals, solid-liquid separating, and flash drying to obtain lithium carbonate product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The concentrated lithium-rich solution is subjected to fluorine removal treatment, and the existing fluorine removal methods are used, including extraction method to remove fluoride, precipitation method to remove fluoride, and then to remove magnesium and calcium. The sodium carbonate is added Based on to the amount of magnesium and calcium ions in the lithium-rich solution, and the precipitate is filtered after full reaction to remove of the magnesium and calcium ions. In the method, mixture of sodium hydroxide and sodium carbonate is added to adjust the pH of the lithium-rich solution to 13, the mass concentration of sodium hydroxide is 10%-20%, and the mass concentration of sodium carbonate is 40%-60%. In the method, ammonia and carbon dioxide are introduced into the purified lithium-rich solution, and the molar ratio of ammonia/lithium is 1-1.6; and the molar ratio of carbon dioxide/lithium is 0.8-1.2. Preferred Components: The carbonate is sodium carbonate or potassium carbonate. The molar ratio between carbonate and manganese ions is 1-3:1. The carbonate and manganese ions in the waste water are used to react to produce manganese carbonate precipitation that is insoluble in water. The manganese dioxide and carbon dioxide generated after pyrolysis are recycled. The amount of calcium hydroxide solution is 10-30 wt.%, the molar ratio between calcium hydroxide and sulfate is 1-3:1, and the molar ratio is greater than 1:1. The molar ratio between sodium carbonate and ammonium carbonate is 0.1-10:1. The pH adjusting agent is sodium hydroxide, potassium hydroxide or calcium hydroxide, and adjusts the pH value of the filtrate to greater than or equal to 10.8. The lithium concentration in the purified lithium-rich solution is 10-200 g/l.

TI Generating hydrogen or hydrocarbon fuel from            feedstock via centrifuge reactor involves introducing a            flow of feedstock to a centrifuge reactor, where            centrifuge reactor included central rotational axis X,            housing having a sidewall.

GA 202079861S
AB    NOVELTY - Generating hydrogen or hydrocarbon fuel from                feedstock via centrifuge reactor involves                introducing a flow of feedstock to a centrifuge                reactor, where the centrifuge reactor included a                central rotational axis X, housing having a                sidewall, a first housing end and a second housing                end that define a cavity, the first housing end                defining a first housing opening and the second                housing end defining a second housing opening,                centrifuge assembly that is rotationally located                within the cavity of the housing and configured to                rotate about the central rotational axis X. The                centrifuge assembly comprises reaction chamber with                a first reaction chamber end that is radially                closer to the central rotational axis X than a                second reaction chamber end.
   USE - Method for generating hydrogen or hydrocarbon                fuel from feedstock via centrifuge reactor.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to generate hydrogen or                hydrocarbon fuel from feedstock via centrifuge                reactor is cost-effective.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Generating hydrogen or hydrocarbon fuel from                feedstock via centrifuge reactor involves                introducing a flow of feedstock to a centrifuge                reactor, where the centrifuge reactor included a                central rotational axis X, housing having a                sidewall, a first housing end and a second housing                end that define a cavity, the first housing end                defining a first housing opening and the second                housing end defining a second housing opening,                centrifuge assembly that is rotationally located                within the cavity of the housing and configured to                rotate about the central rotational axis X. The                centrifuge assembly comprises reaction chamber with                a first reaction chamber end that is radially                closer to the central rotational axis X than a                second reaction chamber end, a first entry channel                that allows the feedstock to enter the reaction                chamber, a first fraction channel, a second                fraction channel, a separation unit, and a mixing                unit, rotating the centrifuge assembly about the                central rotational axis X at a tip speed of                100-1000 m/s to generate an acceleration from the                central rotational axis X and from the first                reaction chamber end to the second reaction chamber                end, generating reaction conditions in the reaction                chamber, including pressure of 5-500 megaPascal and                temperature within a range of 200-1000 degrees C.                The reaction conditions and acceleration causes                separation of products from a reaction of the                feedstock within the reaction chamber, removed                first portion of the separation of products from                the reaction chamber includes water and dissolved                salts, via the first fraction channel, removed a                second portion of the separation of products from                the reaction chamber comprises hydrocarbons, via                the second fraction channel, removed a third                portion of the separation products from the                reaction chamber comprises carbon dioxide, via the                separation unit, separated at least a portion of                the carbon dioxide from the third portion of the                separation products via the separation unit to                generated separated carbon dioxide and separation                remainders, returned the separation remainders to                the reaction chamber via the separation unit,                removed a fourth portion of the separation products                at the reaction chamber first end of the reaction                chamber via the mixing unit, introduced at least                portion of the fourth portion of the separation                products back into the reaction chamber at the                reaction chamber second via one or more nozzles at                the reaction chamber second end, where the                centrifuge reactor operates in a continuous flow                manner with a continuous flow of the feedstock                introduced into the reaction chamber and continuous                flows of the first and second portions of the                separation of products leaving the centrifuge                reactor.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The hydrocarbons                  comprises at least one of a gasoline, diesel,                  crude oil, or jet-fuel equivalent liquid fuel.                  The first fraction channel and the second                  fraction channel and the first portion of the                  separation of products and the second portion of                  the separation of products exit the housing at                  the first or second housing end and proximate to                  the central rotational axis X. The feedstock                  comprises coal or biomass included one of                  agricultural waste, forestry waste, and municipal                  waste. Preferred Conditionsa: The feedstock                  enters the housing at the first housing end and                  proximate to the central rotational axis X. The                  method involves removing a first portion of the                  separation of products from the reaction chamber                  comprises water and dissolved salts, via a first                  fraction channel. The method involves removing a                  second portion of the separation of products from                  the reaction chamber comprises hydrocarbons, via                  a second fraction channel. The method involves                  removing a third portion of the separation                  products from the reaction chamber, comprises                  carbon dioxide, via a separation unit, separating                  at least a portion of the carbon dioxide from the                  third portion of the separation products via the                  separation unit to generated separated carbon                  dioxide and separation remainders, returning the                  separation remainders to the reaction chamber via                  the separation unit and combusting the separation                  remainders to heat the reaction chamber. The                  method involves removing a fourth portion of the                  separation products at the first end of the                  reaction chamber via a mixing unit, introducing                  at least portion of the fourth portion of the                  separation products back into the reaction                  chamber at the reaction chamber second to                  generate mixing at the reaction chamber second                  end.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
