PN CN111518747-A
TI Separating and extracting amniotic membrane stem cells from placenta comprises spreading meat emulsion on plastic petri dish, culturing, adding trypsin substitute solution, covering the cells, centrifuging, and removing supernatant.
AU YU K
   LIU P
   LIU R
   LI X
   HE Q
   WANG J
AE CHENGDU RONGYIHUI BIOTECHNOLOGY RES INST (CHEN-Non-standard)
GA 202081117Q
AB    NOVELTY - Separating and extracting amniotic membrane stem cells from placenta comprises placing the pre-processed amniotic membrane into plastic petri dish with culture medium, spreading the meat emulsion on the plastic petri dish, and placing the plastic petri dish in 5% carbon dioxide incubator for culturing, removing the culture supernatant and amniotic membrane block from the plastic petri dish, adding the trypsin substitute solution to the plastic petri dish, and then placing the plastic petri dish in an incubator, dividing the cell suspension into multiple culture flasks with medium, oscillating the cells uniformly, adding trypsin substitute solution to the culture flask after rinsing the cells, uniformly covering the surface of the cells, then placing the culture flask in an incubator, tapping the culture flask for making the cells fall off the flask, centrifuging the cell suspension, and removing the cell supernatant to obtain amniotic membrane stem cells.
   USE - The method is useful for separating and extracting amniotic membrane stem cells from the placenta.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is simple, easy to operate; promotes more amniotic membrane stem cells to be exposed; increases the contact area between amniotic membrane stem cells and culture medium; promotes the in vitro expansion of amniotic membrane stem cells; and increases the amount of amniotic membrane stem cells extracted from amniotic membrane.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Separating and extracting amniotic membrane stem cells from the placenta comprises (a) placing the pre-processed amniotic membrane into plastic petri dish with culture medium, then cutting into 2-3 mm3 meat emulsion, spreading the meat emulsion on the plastic petri dish, and placing the plastic petri dish in 5% carbon dioxide incubator for culturing, (b) removing the culture supernatant and amniotic membrane block from the plastic petri dish on the 7th day, and then rinsing the remaining cells in the plastic petri dish with physiological saline to obtain small amount of clean cells, (c) adding the trypsin substitute solution to the plastic petri dish, then uniformly covering the cell surface, and then placing the plastic petri dish in an incubator or room temperature for digestion, (d) observing the cells digested, then tapping the plastic petri dish when the cells become round and starts to fall off for preparing the cells fall off the plastic petri dish, and then adding culture medium to the plastic petri dish for stopping the digestion to obtain the cell suspension, (e) dividing the cell suspension into multiple culture flasks with medium, oscillating the cells uniformly, placing the culture flask in 5% carbon dioxide incubator, and culturing until the cells grow to a confluence of more than 80%, (f) removing the cell supernatant in all culture flasks from the culture flask, then collecting on 13th day, and rinsing the cells in the culture flask with normal saline to obtain large number of clean cells, (g) adding trypsin substitute solution to the culture flask after rinsing the cells, uniformly covering the surface of the cells, and then placing the culture flask in an incubator or at room temperature for digestion, (h) observing the cells digested until the cells become round, starting to falling off, tapping the culture flask for making the cells fall off the flask, adding culture medium or cell supernatant collected to the culture flask for stopping the digestion to obtain cell suspension, and (i) centrifuging the cell suspension, and removing the cell supernatant to obtain amniotic membrane stem cells.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The pretreatment of the broken amniotic membrane specifically comprises (a) taking out the placenta, placing on a sterile culture plate, washing the blood stains on the placenta surface with sterile distilled water containing penicillin, and rinsing the placenta again with normal saline, cutting a section of amniotic membrane from the rinsed placenta, and then transferring to new sterile plastic petri dish for washing the blood stains on the surface of the amniotic membrane with saline, and (b) further cutting the amniotic membrane washed into small pieces of 0.5-1 cm2, washing with normal saline, then transferring the washed small piece of amniotic membrane to a new sterile plastic petri dish, continuing rinsing with normal saline until there is no blood stains, and then cutting the rinsed small piece of amniotic membrane into pieces. The step (a) comprises incubating in 5% carbon dioxide incubator at 37 degrees C, and replacing all the liquid in the culture flask with a new medium for every 3 days during the cell culture process. The step (c) comprises placing the plastic petri dish in incubator for digestion at 37 degrees C for 30-90 seconds. The step (g) comprises placing the culture flask in the incubator for digestion at 37 degrees C for 30-90 seconds. The step (h) comprises using the culture flask containing the cell suspension for collecting the cell supernatant and cell recovery, and centrifuging the cell supernatant after washing together with cell suspension. The temperature of the centrifugal environment in the step (i) is 2-8 degrees C. The preservation of amniotic membrane stem cells specifically comprises (a) adding fresh Dulbecco's modified essential medium/F12 medium to the amniotic membrane stem cells for re-dispersing the cells, (b) adjusting the cell concentration of the cells dispersed in the Dulbecco's modified essential medium/F12 medium, and then adding the cryopreservation protection solution to obtain a cell suspension, and (c) firstly allowing to stand the cell suspension for 11-13 hours, and then transferring to liquid nitrogen for storage. Preferred Components: The physiological saline is 0.9% physiological saline. The medium is Dulbecco's modified essential medium/F12 or alpha -minimal essential medium. The concentration of penicillin in the sterile distilled water containing penicillin is 100 units/ml. The cryopreservation solution comprises 30-55% dimethyl sulfoxide + 5-25% dextran + medium, where the cell concentration in the step C2 is 5-10 million.

PN CN110643832-A
TI Extracting lithium from fly ash comprises e.g. choosing a coal pieces from the coal mine, and crushing the coal pieces into small pieces, then combusting the small pieces of coal and generating flue gas during combustion and collecting.
AU LIAO J
   WEI M
   LIANG X
   TANG S
   PAN Y
AE GUANGXI CHINA COAL GEOLOGY CO LTD (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 202004032R
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting lithium from fly ash comprises e.g. choosing a coal pieces from the coal mine, and crushing the coal pieces into small pieces, combusting small pieces of coal, generating the flue gas during the combustion and collecting, and the flue gas contains fine coal ash powder to be collected to obtain fly ash, choosing a certain amount of fly ash and adding a quantitative solution to it, so that the fly ash and water are fully mixed, after mixing, filtering the mixed solution of fly ash and water to obtain fly ash leaching solution, acidifying the leaching solution, adding potassium carbonate and sodium carbonate to the leaching solution, when adding potassium carbonate and sodium carbonate, and to ensure three addition processes, adding leachate and stirring by a stirring rod, where the second process is: adding leachate, and performing a second stirring by stirring rod, and the third process is: adding leaching solution, and performing a third stirring with a stirring rod.
   USE - The method is useful for extracting lithium from fly ash.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: avoids the waste of resources, increases the benefit of the enterprise; and is economical.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Extracting lithium from fly ash comprises (i) choosing a coal pieces from the coal mine, and crushing the coal pieces into small pieces, then combusting the small pieces of coal, generating the flue gas during the combustion and collecting, and the flue gas contains fine coal ash powder to be collected to obtain fly ash; (ii) choosing a certain amount of fly ash in step (i) and adding a quantitative solution to it, so that the fly ash and water are fully mixed, after mixing, filtering the mixed solution of fly ash and water to obtain fly ash leaching solution; (iii) acidifying the leaching solution, adding potassium carbonate and sodium carbonate to the leaching solution in step (ii), when adding potassium carbonate and sodium carbonate, and to ensure three addition processes, adding an amount of 20% of the total volume of the leachate and stirring by a stirring rod, where the second process is: adding the amount of 50% of the total volume of the leachate, and performing a second stirring by the stirring rod, and the third process is: adding 30% of the total volume of the leaching solution, and performing a third stirring with a stirring rod to finally obtain an acidic leaching solution; (iv) after the potassium carbonate and sodium carbonate are completely added in step (iii) and the reaction with the leaching solution is completed, the reaction liquid needs to be filtered, separating the solid matter from the liquid after the reaction; (v) placing the liquid filtered on a heating furnace for heating and crystallization, and the heating temperature of the heating furnace is divided into three stages, where the first stage is: heating furnace temperature is 10-45 degrees C, and the heating time is 10-12 minutes, the second stage is: the temperature range of the heating furnace is 45-60 degrees C, and the heating time is 15-25 minutes, the third stage is: the temperature of the heating furnace is 60-20 degrees C, and the heating time is 8-10 minutes; (vi) drying the crystals obtained in step (v), placing the crystals in a drying furnace for rapid drying; and (vii) adding carbon dioxide, dissolving the crystals in an aqueous solution vaporized by carbon dioxide, and heating the crystals and the aqueous solution vaporized by carbon dioxide to dissolve the crystals.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (i), the multi-stage crusher is used when crushing the coal block. In the step (i), the collecting fly ash is: passing the flue gas into a dust collector so that the flue gas, and the fly ash enters the ash bucket of the dust collector. In the step (ii), placing the fly ash and water in a beaker, placing the beaker on the shaker, allowing the fly ash in the beaker to mix well with the water. the step (iv), the obtained solid is placed in hot water for heat treatment, so that the solids are dissolved, then further adding potassium carbonate and sodium carbonate again, obtaining get solid matter, and repeating for 3-4 four times. In the step (vi), the drying temperature in the drying furnace is 100-200 degrees C, and the drying time is 2-4 hours. In the step (vi), when the crystals are dried to ensure the drying effect and speed of crystals, where the drying mechanism is set in the drying furnace, and ensure that the crystals shake in the drying furnace and improve the drying effect and drying speed of crystals. In the step (vii), the heating temperature is 30 degrees C.

PN CN109108048-A; CN109108048-B
TI Method of mechanochemical conversion and recovery            of sodium-containing and fluorine-containing compounds            in aluminum electrolytic anode carbon residue, involves            converting cyanide-containing compound into nitrogen            and processing.
AU LIU E
   WANG L
AE UNIV XIANGTAN (UNXT-C)
GA 201904033C
AB    NOVELTY - Method of mechanochemical conversion and                recovery of sodium-containing and                fluorine-containing compounds in aluminum                electrolytic anode carbon residue, involves placing                mixed powder material of fluorine-containing                minerals and carbon in an air atmosphere in                high-temperature furnace at 700-1200 degrees C for                0.5-5 hours, completely oxidizing the carbon                component, obtaining mixture of fluorine-containing                minerals as residue, concentrating or crystallizing                dilute solution of soluble mixture, obtaining                concentrated solution of soluble mixture or solid                phase soluble mixture, oxidatively converting                cyanide-containing compound into toxic nitrogen or                ammonia and carbon dioxide, analyzing and                inspecting conversion system in batches,                terminating conversion reaction until the cyanide                ion content in the system reaches the national                emission standard, subjecting ammonia, carbon                dioxide and water vapor to absorption conversion                process, drying, collecting generated reducing gas,                and directly oxidizing.
   USE - Method of mechanochemical conversion and                recovery of sodium-containing and                fluorine-containing compounds in aluminum                electrolytic anode carbon residue.
   ADVANTAGE - The recovery of sodium-containing and                fluorine-containing compounds in aluminum                electrolytic anode carbon residue, is carried out                efficiently by simple method with high productivity                without affecting the environment.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The mixed powder material of                fluorine-containing minerals and carbon, is                obtained by crushing, grinding and homogenizing                aluminum electrolytic anode carbon residue,                obtaining anode carbon residue powder having                particle size of 200 mu m or less, analyzing and                determining the mole number or mass of sodium and                fluorine in the unit mass of aluminum electrolytic                anode carbon residue powder, determining the mole                number or mass of cyanide ions in the unit mass                aluminum electrolytic anode carbon residue powder,                adding the grinding component to the conversion                mill, vacuumizing or replacing the air by nitrogen,                adding metered anode carbon residue powder to the                conversion mill, controlling the mass ratio of the                anode carbon residue powder with respect to                abrasive component as 1:0.2-10, adding metered                water to the conversion mill, controlling the                solid-liquid mass ratio of the anode carbon residue                powder to water as 1:1-10, converting                fluorine-containing compound to 1-3 times the ratio                of the corresponding product, or adding conversion                agent to the conversion mill in an amount of 5-80                %mass of the anode carbon residue powder, adding                grinding additive to the conversion mill in an                amount of 0-1 %mass with respect to content of                anode carbon residue powder, turning on the                reforming mill, controlling the rotational speed of                the reforming mill as 10-1000 rpm, carrying out                conversion process at 10-110 degrees C, carrying                out sampling analysis and inspection of the slurry                in batches until the sodium ion content in the                slurry solid phase reaches the enterprise standard,                terminating the conversion mill when the soluble                fluoride ion content reaches the national emission                standard, transferring the slurry to a stirred                reactor, carrying out gel breaking and aging                treatment at 60-150 degrees C for 0.5-5 hours,                subjecting resultant slurry to solid-liquid                separation by filtration or centrifugal separation,                repeating dispersing washing and solid-liquid                separation with water as detergent in the                solid-liquid mass ratio of 1:1-10, washing until                the content of soluble ions in the solid phase                reaches the corporate standard, combining the                separation solution, obtaining mixture of wet                fluorine-containing mineral and carbon which is in                the form of aqueous solid phase and dilute solution                of soluble mixture, drying the aqueous solid phase                at 80-300 degrees C, heat-treating for 1-10 hours,                and pulverizing. Cyanide ions in the reaction                mixture is converted to nitrogen or ammonia and                carbon dioxide in the anode carbon residue powder,                by adding cyanide conversion agent to 0.1-10 %mass                anode carbon residue powder.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The anode carbon                  residue comprises carbon, sodium, fluorine,                  aluminum, calcium, oxygen, nitrogen, lithium,                  magnesium, silicon, potassium and/or iron as main                  component. The sodium-containing compound                  includes sodium fluoride, sodium aluminum                  fluoride (Na3AlF6), sodium cyanide and/or sodium                  ferrocyanide (Na4(Fe(CN)6)). The                  fluorine-containing compound includes sodium                  fluoride, sodium aluminum fluoride, aluminum                  fluoride, lithium fluoride, calcium difluoride,                  magnesium difluoride and/or potassium fluoride.                  The conversion agent containing sodium compound                  and fluorine-containing compound includes oxide,                  hydroxide, sulfate, nitrate, carbonate, acetate                  and/or citrate of calcium, aluminum, magnesium,                  strontium, barium, lanthanum and cerium. The                  cyanide conversion agent comprises sodium                  carbonate pentahydrate, potassium carbonate                  pentahydrate, sodium peroxide, potassium                  carbonate hydrate, potassium peroxide, calcium                  oxide, strontium oxide, barium oxide, hydrogen                  peroxide, ammonium persulfate, potassium sulfate,                  potassium manganate, oxygen and/or ozone. The                  grinding additive is polymeric polyol, polymeric                  alcohol amine, triisopropanolamine, ethylene                  glycol, propylene glycol, glycerol, diethylene                  glycol, triethanolamine, amide, stearic acid,                  oleic acid, sodium hexametaphosphate, and/or                  sodium stearate.

TI Extracting silicon, aluminum and rare earth metals            using plasma activated solid waste method comprises            e.g. placing solid waste of silicon and aluminum into            the reaction kettle and activating the silicon-aluminum            solid waste.
AU CAO Y
AE SHANXI ZHONGPU ENERGY TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 201898370J
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting silicon, aluminum and rare earth                metals using plasma activated solid waste method                comprises (i) placing solid waste of silicon and                aluminum into the reaction kettle, activating the                silicon-aluminum solid waste, releasing the                multiple of rare earth metals bound in the                silicon-aluminum crystal phase and migrating to the                surface of the newly formed silicon-aluminum, (ii)                heating and transferring the product dissolved in                water to the liquid phase, (iii) obtaining two                products of high silicon-containing solid material                and high aluminum-containing aqueous solution and                separating the silicon and aluminum in the original                solid waste, (iv) obtaining the product with high                silicon content, (v) obtaining the portion released                from the original solid in a weak acid or strong                alkaline environment, (vi) obtaining more rare                earth metals and (vii) repeating the steps for the                rare earth solution produced to obtain a solid                phase product of a rare earth metal.
   USE - The method is useful for extracting silicon,                aluminum and rare earth metals using plasma                activated solid waste method.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Extracting silicon, aluminum and rare earth                metals using plasma activated solid waste method                comprises (i) placing solid waste of silicon and                aluminum into the reaction kettle, using sodium                salt or potassium salt as an auxiliary agent,                activating the silicon-aluminum solid waste first                under the action of the plasma,, so the complex                silicon-aluminum phase is separated in the solid                waste, releasing the multiple of rare rare earth                metals bound in the silicon-aluminum crystal phase,                migrating to the surface of the newly formed                silicon-aluminum, (ii) destroying the solid phase                product treated in the step (i) by mechanical force                e.g. ball milling, mixing with water, supplementing                by ultrasonic, stirring, microwave, or heating and                transferring the product dissolved in water to the                liquid phase, (iii) filtering, carrying out                pressure filtration or centrifuging the obtained                solid or water mixture to obtain two products of                high silicon-containing solid material and high                aluminum-containing aqueous solution and separating                the silicon and aluminum in the original solid                waste, (iv) mixing the high silicon-containing                solid product with water, supplementing by any one                of ultrasonic, stirring, microwave, or heating,                adjusting the pH of the solution environment to                1-5, dissociating the rare earth metal e.g.rare                earth in the remaining high silicon-containing                solid product in an acidic environment and                obtaining the product with high silicon content by                filtration, pressure filtration or centrifugation,                (v) adjusting the acidic solution containing the                rare earth metal e.g. rare earth filtered to adjust                the pH of the mixed solution to 5-14, obtaining the                portion released from the original solid in a weak                acid or strong alkaline environment, (vi) filtering                out the high aluminum-containing alkaline solution                in the above step (iv), adjusting the pH of the                mixed solution to 1-5, e.g. carbon dioxide (CO2),                or in a weakly acidic environment of supercritical                carbon dioxide, dissolving, heating to 35-250                degrees C, releasing rare earth metals e.g. rare                earths present in the high aluminum liquid,                adjusting the carbon dioxide concentration and the                reaction temperature, entering the aluminum into                the solid phase, and the rare earth is in the                liquid phase, separating the solid aluminum and the                rare earth solution by separation operations e.g.                filtration, pressure filtration or centrifugation                to obtain more rare rare earth metals e.g. rare                earths solid phase product and (vii) repeating the                steps for the rare earth solution produced to                obtain a solid phase product of a rare earth                metal
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The solid waste of                  silicon-aluminum is coal gangue, inferior coal,                  fly ash, coal char, dried coal washing plant                  liquid residue or coal tar, the solid waste in                  the iron and steel non-ferrous smelting industry                  may involve solid waste or dry liquid waste                  generated in the production process of all                  metallurgical industries and the rare earth                  concentrate or slag is the rare earth ore itself                  or the rare earth slag or the dried liquid                  residue obtained after being extracted by other                  methods. The precious rare earth metal comprises                  scandium, lanthanide series elements, rhenium,                  ruthenium, rhodium, palladium, niobium, tantalum,                  iridium, gallium, indium and germanium. In the                  step (i), the sodium salt is sodium oxide, sodium                  peroxide, sodium hydroxide, sodium carbonate,                  sodium chloride, sodium nitrate or sodium sulfate                  and the potassium salt is potassium oxide,                  potassium peroxide, potassium hydroxide,                  potassium carbonate, potassium chloride,                  potassium nitrate or potassium sulfate. Preferred                  Method: In the step (i), the reactor is provided                  with a plasma generator and the solid waste                  temperature detecting device is to control the                  solid waste temperature to 450-900 degrees C. In                  the step (iv) and the step (v), the acidic                  environment is carried out directly using an                  industrial inorganic acid, an organic acid or an                  acidic method based on electrochemical methods.                  The step-by-step reaction process can assist                  microwave, agitation, ultrasonic or heating or                  acid-enhanced extraction and enhanced mass                  transfer means or use the mechanical force of                  high-speed ball milling to enhance the reaction                  effect by means of breaking and rejoining the                  chemical bond.

TI Graphene biological battery, has battery cover formed with inserting holes, where cavity of battery cover is formed with vent holes for adding fuel and discharging carbon dioxide and oxygen.
AU HU J
   HU D

GA 201873126B
AB    (CN207852808-U)   NOVELTY - The utility model claims a graphite biological cell. the graphene bio-battery by the battery chamber, battery chamber cover, located in the cavity of the battery anode and cathode; wherein, bio-fuel, the battery cavity for containing the bottom of the battery cavity is provided with a discharging port; the outside of battery cavity is provided with a constant temperature layer; said battery chamber cover are respectively provided with one or more holes for inserting an anode and a cathode, holes for inserting an anode and a cathode are arranged in parallel on the battery cavity cover, top of the anode and cathode are respectively connected with copper alloy cap. said battery chamber cover is further provided with one to two vent holes, for adding fuel, discharging carbon dioxide and oxygen is absorbed. A new graphene bio-battery of this utility model is safe and reliable, high energy density, and environment-friendly, low cost, long service life, simple production, convenient use, only adding bio-fuel can continuously generate current.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A graphene bio-battery comprises a battery cavity, a battery cavity cover, an anode and a cathode, where the anode and the cathode are located in the battery cavity. The battery cavity is used for containing biofuels. The bottom of the battery cavity is provided with a discharge opening used to discharge old materials. The outside of the battery cavity is provided with a constant temperature layer. The battery cavity cover is connected to an upper portion of the battery cavity, and is respectively provided with holes for inserting the anode and the cathode. The upper end of the hole is covered with a copper alloy cap. The copper alloy cap is detachably connected to the battery cavity cover. The copper alloy cap is completely covered by the hole for inserting the anode and the cathode, when the battery cavity cover is connected by a fixing device, and connecting to the anode and cathode in the insertion hole. The hole for inserting the anode and the hole for inserting the cathode are arranged in parallel on the battery cavity cover. The battery cavity cover is further provided with vent holes used to add biofuels, exhaust carbon dioxide and absorb oxygen.
EA    NOVELTY - A graphene bio-battery comprises a battery cavity, a battery cavity cover, an anode and a cathode, where the anode and the cathode are located in the battery cavity. The battery cavity is used for containing biofuels. The bottom of the battery cavity is provided with a discharge opening used to discharge old materials. The battery cavity cover is connected to an upper portion of the battery cavity, and is respectively provided with holes for inserting the anode and the cathode. The upper end of the hole is covered with a copper alloy cap. The copper alloy cap is detachably connected to the battery cavity cover. The copper alloy cap is completely covered by the hole for inserting the anode and the cathode, when the battery cavity cover is connected by a fixing device, and connecting to the anode and cathode in the insertion hole. The hole for inserting the anode and the hole for inserting the cathode are arranged in parallel on the battery cavity cover.

   ADVANTAGE - The graphene bio-battery is safe and reliable, high in energy density, environmentally-friendly, low in cost, long in service life, simple in production, and convenient to use.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ELECTRICAL POWER AND ENERGY - Preferred Device: The graphene bio-battery further comprises an insulation layer controlled by an intelligent insulation system. The insulation layer comprises from inside to outside, cooling and heating modules, glass wool and a layer of metal or carbon fiber material. The temperature of the battery cavity is kept constant by controlling the temperature of the cooling and heating modules. The insulation layer maintains the temperature of the battery cavity at 10-60 degrees C. Preferred Components: The anode is composed of 40-50 pts. wt. nano bamboo charcoal powder, 20-30 pts. wt. three-dimensional graphene powder, 10-20 pts. wt. montmorillonite, 3-7 pts. wt. glucose oxidase and coenzyme, and 2-3 pts. wt. phosphate buffer, where the weight ratio of glucose oxidase to coenzyme is 3:2. The cathode is composed of 40-50 pts. wt. nano bamboo charcoal powder, 20-30 pts. wt. three-dimensional graphene powder, 10-20 pts. wt. diatomaceous earth, 5-7 pts. wt. bilirubin oxidase and 2-3 pts. wt. phosphate buffer solution. The outer wall of the battery cavity is made of plant plastic. Preferred Method: The anode is prepared by: mixing nano bamboo charcoal powder, three-dimensional graphene powder and montmorillonite according to the weight ratio, then thoroughly stirring with water, repeatedly kneading or beating mud by using smooth wood hammer, removing air in the mud material, preparing a tubular shape, a plate shape, a tube plate shape and other shapes according to needs, and then firing in a kiln to form an anode cavity tube; and adding glucose oxidase and coenzyme to phosphate buffer solution according to the weight fraction to get a mixed solution, and soaking the fired anode cavity tube in the mixed solution, where the anode tube adsorbs 5-10 pts. wt. mixed solution. The cathode is prepared by: mixing nano bamboo charcoal powder, three-dimensional graphene powder and diatomaceous earth according to the weight ratio, then thoroughly stirring with water, repeatedly kneading or beating mud by using smooth wood hammer, removing air in the mud material, preparing a tubular shape, a plate shape, a tube plate shape and other shapes according to needs and then firing in a kiln to form a cathode cavity tube; and adding bilirubin oxidase to phosphate buffer solution according to the weight fraction to get a mixed solution, and soaking the fired cathode cavity tube in the mixed solution, where the cathode tube adsorbs 5-10 pts. wt. mixed solution.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The biofuel is a 50 wt./wt.% glucose solution.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The plant plastic is polylactic acid.

PN US2018178292-A1
TI Method for production of metallic iron, involves producing hydrogen for reverse water gas shift reaction by water electrolysis, and reducing oxide minerals of iron by carbon monoxide, carbon, hydrogen, or methane to form iron product.
AU BERGGREN M
   ZUBRIN R
   RASMUSSEN J D
AE PIONEER ASTRONAUTICS (PION-Non-standard)
GA 2018506271
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves preparation and regeneration of carbon monoxide reductant from carbon dioxide through the reverse water gas shift reaction. The hydrogen for the reverse water gas shift reaction is produced by water electrolysis. The oxide minerals of iron are reduced by carbon monoxide, carbon, hydrogen, or methane to form iron product. The hydrogen for reduction is obtained by water electrolysis. The carbon monoxide for reduction is obtained by the addition of a reverse water gas shift reactor to form carbon monoxide reductant and water electrolyzer to form hydrogen for conversion of carbon dioxide resulting from iron oxide reduction to carbon monoxide in the reverse water gas shift reactor with simultaneous production of oxygen byproduct. The majority of carbon monoxide reductant is supplied through reverse water gas shift and electrolysis to make a closed-loop system in which additional carbon monoxide is required only to make up for leaks and process losses.
   USE - Method for production of metallic iron.
   ADVANTAGE - The EMP techniques have additional potential for the processing of lower-grade ores and feed stocks including other process residues and wastes. Thus, the EMP can help to reduce overall processing costs by enabling the use of feed stocks.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Methane for reduction is obtained by methanation from synthesis gas and hydrogen from water electrolysis. The carbothermal reduction of iron oxide uses products of a reverse water gas shift reaction, the Boudouard carbon deposition reaction, and water electrolysis. Iron oxide is produced as sufficiently fine particles, subjected to reduction followed by sintering at temperatures below the melting point of iron. The iron-rich product can be melted and refined to transport impurities such as phosphorus, sulfur, and silicon to a slag phase while adjusting carbon content and alloying agent compositions of the iron melt. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a method for production of silicon involves production of carbon reductant from carbon monoxide, reduction of silicon dioxide by carbon to form silicon product and recovery of carbon monoxide from silicon dioxide reduction with subsequent regeneration of carbon reductant from carbon monoxide. The iron oxide is present in the feed and iron silicide is a product. The carbon is obtained from a source including carbon dioxide in the Mars atmosphere, carbon from lunar soil, carbon imported from a remote location, carbon recovered from carbothermal reduction as carbon monoxide, from iron oxide reduction or carbon deposited through the Boudouard reaction; and
   (2) a method for production of metallic magnesium involves carbothermal reduction of oxide minerals of silicon to form metallic silicon or ferrosilicon and reduction of magnesium by silicon. Silicon or ferrosilicon produced by extraterrestrial metals processing (EMP) is used as a reductant for production of high-purity magnesium or other light metals. Silicon oxides contained in the silicothermic reduction products from magnesium oxide reduction are recycled and reacted with carbon. Carbothermal reduction of magnesium-oxide-containing feeds in vacuum, ionization of produced magnesium metal vapors, separation of ionized magnesium metal vapors from carbon monoxide gas are performed. Ionized magnesium metal vapors are directed by passing through a magnetic field downstream of the radio frequency coil. Water produced in the reverse water gas shift reactor system is fed to an electrolyzer to generate hydrogen and oxygen. The closed-loop system is provided in which magnesium oxide containing feed is converted to magnesium metal and oxygen byproduct through the use of integrated an carbothermal reactor, RWGS system, and electrolysis, resulting in very low emissions of carbon gases.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the structure for production of magnesium metal through carbothermal reduction.

PT P
PN EP3269699-A1
TI Direct carboxylation of activated alkanes/olefins for producing carboxy product, involves reacting alkanes/olefins with carbon dioxide and catalyst formed by contacting nickel salt, reducing agent and phenanthroline ligand, with solvent.
AU MARTIN ROMO R
   JULIA HERNANDEZ F
   CORNELLA J
AE FUNDACIO INST CATALA DINVESTIGACIO QUIMI (CATA-Non-standard)
   ICREA INST CATALANA RECERCA I ESTUDIS AV (ICRE-Non-standard)
GA 2018042538
AB    NOVELTY - Direct carboxylation of activated alkanes/olefins (I), involves reacting compound (I) with carbon dioxide in the presence of nickel catalyst and polar aprotic solvent. The nickel catalyst is obtained by contacting a nickel(II) salt, a reducing agent chosen from manganese and zinc or an electrochemical reducing agent, hydrogen source chosen from water and brominated compounds (IV) and/or (V), and ligand which is phenanthroline compound (III), with polar aprotic solvent.
   USE - Direct carboxylation of activated alkanes and/or olefins to yield carboxyl group-containing products (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple, and produces highly purified products, thus reducing purification efforts, and efficiently produces a single end product having a carboxyl group attached to a well-defined position even if starting materials with different structures or a mixture of such starting materials is used.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Direct carboxylation of activated alkanes/olefins of formula (I), involves reacting the compound (I) with carbon dioxide in the presence of nickel catalyst and polar aprotic solvent. The nickel catalyst is obtained by contacting a nickel(II) salt chosen from nickel(II) halide, nickel(II) acetylacetonate, nickel(II) sulfate, nickel(II) perchlorate, nickel(II) trifluoromethanesulfonate, nickel(II) hexafluoroacetylacetonate, nickel(II) sulfamate, nickel(II) carbonate, nickel(II) oxalate, nickel(II) oxalate of formula: Ni(OOCR)2 (II), nickel hexafluorosilicate or their solvates, a reducing agent chosen from manganese and zinc or an electrochemical reducer, phenanthroline compound of formula (III) as ligand, with polar aprotic solvent, and optionally hydrogen source chosen from water and brominated compounds of formulae: R7R8CHCH2Br (IV) and R7R8CBr-CHR9R10 (V).
   R=1-20C linear or branched alkyl, or 3-8C cycloalkyl optionally substituted by 1-6C linear or branched alkyl;
   R4=H, 1-20C linear or branched alkyl substituted by fluoro and/or chloro, or 1-20C linear or branched alkoxy;
   X=H, Br, I, phenylsulfonate having phenyl optionally substituted by methyl, methylsulfonate, or trifluoromethylsulfonate;
   u=0 or 1;
   p=0-15;
   R3,R3'=H and/or radical of formula: -Y'(-Z)n;
   Y'=1-20C linear or branched alkyl, 3-20C branched alkyl, or 3-8C cycloalkyl;
   n=0 to number of carbon atoms in Y';
   Z=F, Cl, aldehyde, nitro, cyano, radical of formula: -OR5, -OC(O)R5, -O(O)OR5, -NHC(O)OR5, or -OC(O)NHR5;
   R5=1-20C linear or branched alkyl, cyclyl having 1 ring or 2-5 condensed rings which is saturated, unsaturated or aromatic and has 5-6 components chosen from C, CH, CH2, O, S, N, and NR6 and is optionally substituted by radical(s) chosen from F, Cl, 1-6C alkyl, 2-6C haloalkyl, 1-6C alkoxy, 1-6C alkylcarbonyl, 1-6C alkylcarbonyloxy, 1-6C alkylcarbonylamino, and 1-6C alkylaminocarbonyl, -SO2R11;
   R11= 1-6C alkyl, 1-6C haloalkyl, phenyl optionally substituted by 1-6C alkyl, cyclyl (G) which is saturated, unsaturated or aromatic, or is optionally saturated 2-5 condensed ring having 5 or 6 components chosen from C, CH, CH2, O, S, N, and NR6, and optionally substituted by radical(s) chosen from 1-6C (halo)alkyl, 1-6C alkoxy, 1-6C alkylcarbonyl, 1-6C alkylcarbonyloxy, 1-6C alkylcarbonylamino, 1-6C alkylaminocarbonyl, and -Ra'-Rb';
   R6=H, or 1-6C alkyl;
   R3+R3'=cyclyl (G);and
   R2,R2'=H, 1-12C linear or branched alkyl, 1-12C linear or branched haloalkyl, 6-20C aryl optionally substituted by substituent(s) chosen from 1-6C linear or branched alkyl, 1-6C linear or branched alkoxy, 1-6C linear or branched haloalkyl, F, Cl, and Br,
   with specified provisos. Full definitions are given in the DEFINITIONS (Full Definitions) Field.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The method yields reaction product which is carboxyl group-containing compounds of formulae (VI), (VII), (VIII) and (IX). The compound (I) is preferably of formula (Ia), (Ib), (Ic), or (Id). The double bond in the compound (Ia) is in cis or trans configuration. The compound (I) is a mixture of 2 or more compounds of formula (I) having same total number of carbon atoms, yielding same reaction products and differing in position of the double bond with respect to groups R3 and R4. The carboxyl group in the reaction products (IV), (V), (VI) or (VII) is preferably bonded via a methylene group. The reaction is carried out in a solvent which is or which comprises N,N-dimethylformamide, N,N-diethylformamide, dimethylsulfoxide, N,N-dimethylacetamide, N-methylpyrrolidine, N,N-dibutylformamide, and/or 1-formylpyrrolidine. The compound (III) is a mixture of 2 or more compounds, which have a group X other than hydrogen, same total number of carbon atoms and yield same reaction products, and in which R3 and R4 groups are such that the 2 or more compounds differ from each other only with respect to the position of the leaving group X, or the compound (III) is a mixture of 2 or more compounds having group X representing hydrogen and same total number of carbon atoms in which the bond represented by the combination of a solid line and a dotted line is a double bond, and the groups R3 and R4 are such that the two or more compounds of formula (III) differ from each other only with respect to the position of the double bond and same reaction product is obtained for each of these compounds.R4a,R4b,R4c=R4;p,l=0-3;p+l=3 or less;R3a=radical chosen from 1-(20-l)C linear or branched alkyl optionally substituted by 6-20C aryl, 3-6C cycloalkyl, F, or Cl;R3b=radical chosen from 1-(20-l)C linear or branched alkyl optionally substituted by 6-20C aryl which is optionally substituted by 1-6C alkylcarbonyloxy, or 3-6C cycloalkyl, radical of formula: -OC(O)R5' (ix), or radical of formula: -C(O)OR5;andR5'=methyl, or phenyl which is optionally substituted by radical(s) chosen from fluoro, 1-6C alkyloxy, F, or Cl.(1) R4b is selected such that carboxy is attached to terminal aliphatic carbon atom of R4b group which is derived from primary aliphatic carbon atom in the starting material, and connected to the position of the leaving group X or double bond in the starting material via a chain of secondary and tertiary carbon atoms that is not interrupted by any quaternary carbon atoms. The reaction conditions are fulfilled when the catalyst is made of nickel(II) iodide, manganese is used as reducing agent, and when the compound (III) is used as ligand. Preferred Composition: 0.9-11 mol% nickel(II) salt is added, 0.9-0.55 mol% compound (I) is added, and/or 100-550 mol% reducing agent is added, with respect to 100 mol% starting material, to carry out the reaction. Preferred Condition: The reaction is carried out at 0-90 degrees C, preferably at 5-85 degrees C if the starting material is a compound having leaving group (X) other than hydrogen, or at 45-90 degrees C if the starting material is a compound having hydrogen as the leaving group.
   Full definitions:
   R=1-20C linear or branched alkyl, or 3-8C cycloalkyl optionally substituted by 1-6C linear or branched alkyl;
   R4=H, 1-20C linear or branched alkyl substituted by F and/or Cl, or 1-20C linear or branched alkoxy;
   X=H, Br, I, phenylsulfonate having phenyl optionally substituted by methyl, methylsulfonate, and trifluoromethylsulfonate;
   u=0 or 1;
   p=0-15;
   R3,R3'=H and/or radical of formula: -Y'(-Z)n;
   Y'=1-20C linear or branched alkyl, 3-20C branched alkyl, or 3-8C cycloalkyl;
   n=0 to number of carbon atoms in Y';
   Z=F, Cl, aldehyde, nitro, cyano, radical of formula: -OR5, -OC(O)R5, -O(O)OR5, -NHC(O)OR5, or -OC(O)NHR5;
   R5=1-20C linear or branched alkyl, cyclyl having 1 ring or 2-5 condensed rings which is saturated, unsaturated or aromatic and has 5-6 components chosen from C, CH, CH2, O, S, N, and NR6 and is optionally substituted by radical(s) chosen from fluoro, chloro, 1-6C alkyl, 2-6C haloalkyl, 1-6C alkoxy, 1-6C alkylcarbonyl, 1-6C alkylcarbonyloxy, 1-6C alkylcarbonylamino, and 1-6C alkylaminocarbonyl or -SO2R11;
   R11= 1-6C alkyl, 1-6C haloalkyl, phenyl optionally substituted by 1-6C alkyl, cyclyl (G) which is saturated, unsaturated or aromatic and optionally saturated 2-5 condensed rings having 5 or 6 components chosen from C, CH, CH2, O, S, N and NR6, and optionally substituted by radicals chosen from 1-6C alkyl, 1-6C haloalkyl, 1-6C alkoxy, 1-6C alkylcarbonyl, 1-6C alkylcarbonyloxy, 1-6C alkylcarbonylamino, 1-6C alkylaminocarbonyl, and group of formula: -Ra'-Rb';
   R6=H, or 1-6C alkyl;
   Ra'=single bond or di-radical chosen from -O-, -S-, -C(=O)-O-, -O-C(=O)-, -C(=O)-NH,-NH-C(=O)-, -O-C(=O)-NH, -NH-C(=O)-, -O-C(=O)-O-, -S(O)2-, -S(O)2-NH-, -NH-S(O)2-, -N=N-, and -NH-C(=O)-NH-;
   Rb'=6-10C aryl, 3-8C cycloalkyl optionally containing 1 or 2 double bonds, or optionally saturated heterocyclyl having 5-7 ring components chosen from C, CH, CH2, O, S, N, and NR6;
   R3+R3'=cyclyl (G);
   R2,R2'=H, 1-12C linear or branched alkyl, 1-12C linear or branched haloalkyl, 6-20C aryl having substituent(s) chosen from 1-6C linear or branched alkyl, 1-6C linear or branched alkoxy, 1-6C linear or branched haloalkyl, F, Cl, and Br;
   R1,R1'=H, halo, 1-12C linear or branched alkyl, 1-12C linear or branched haloalkyl, 6-20C aryl, and 6-20C aryl having substituent(s) chosen from 1-6C linear or branched alkyl, 1-6C linear or branched alkoxy, 1-6C linear or branched haloalkyl, and halo;
   R7,R8,R9,R10=H, 1-20C linear or branched alkyl which is optionally substituted by Radical(s) chosen from 1-20C alkoxy, and 3-8C cycloalkyl which is optionally substituted by radical chosen from hydroxy and 1-20C alkoxy, and 6-20C aryl;and
   R7+R8,R7+R9,R8+R10=ring system having 1-3 optionally saturated aryl which are isolated, fused or bridged, comprising 5-6 compounds chosen from C, CH, and CH2, and optionally substituted by radicals chosen from hydroxy, 1-6C alkyl, and 1-6C alkoxy.
   (a) When X is H, the combination of solid line and dotted line is a double bond and u is 0; (b) when X is not H, the combination of solid line and dotted line is a single bond, u is 1 and R is not H; (c) when R2 is H, R2' is not H; and (d) when R2' is H, R2 is not H. Preferred Definitions:
   R1,R1'=1-naphthyl or 2-naphthyl;and
   R2,R2'=CH3, C2H5, n-C4H9, n-C6H13, i-C3H7, or i-C4H9.

PN CN115111597-A
TI Compensation type pneumatic sealing function            featured flue gas baffle door plate, has piston whose            outer end is fixedly connected with magnet block,            between magnet block and gas injection cylinder is            connected with compression spring.
AU XU W
AE JIANGSU HANTANG ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 2022C5988L
AB    NOVELTY - The plate has a smoke baffle whose outer end                is fixedly connected with a compensation airbag                (2). The outer wall of the air guide pipe (5) is                opened with a through hole. The outside of the                connecting ball (4) is provided with a mounting                port. A movable plate (6) is rotatably connected to                the mounting port, and an extrusion rod (8)                penetrates the through hole and rotates with the                movable board (6), and a magnetic hemisphere (10)                is fixed on the outer end. The air injection                cylinder (11) is slidably connected with the piston                (12) and a magnet block (13). A compression spring                is fixed between the magnet block and the gas                injection cylinder. The inner wall of a first                waterproof ventilating film is fixed with a first                water-proof film.
   USE - Compensation type pneumatic sealing function                featured flue gas baffle door plate for use in                smoke pipeline system, large thermal power                generation industry and metallurgy industry.
   ADVANTAGE - The plate realizes the smoke blowing impact                smoke baffle door, carbon dioxide solution shaking,                the carbon dioxide gas overflows into the air guide                tube, pushing the extruding rod to push the top                movable plate to expand outwards, the magnetic                hemisphere close to the magnet block, repelling the                movement, extruding the air in the air injection                cylinder into the compensation airbag, making it                plump to the inner wall of the smoke contact,                filling the gap between the smoke baffles door and                the pipeline, making the smoke not easy to overflow                through the gap, improving the sealing property, at                the same time. The magnet block intermittently                attracts the movable ball to move under the opening                and closing state of the magnetic insulating panel,                the hot air is intermittently circulated under the                telescopic action of telescopic insulating                film.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a                compensation type pneumatic sealing function                featured flue gas baffle door plate.
   1Smoke baffle door
   2Compensation airbag
   3Liquid storage ball
   4Connecting ball
   5Air guide pipe

PT P
PN WO2022170225-A1; US2022252221-A1
TI Tank for storing and delivering e.g. solid carbon            dioxide used in e.g. fire fighting applications, has            storage region that contains multiple pieces of solid            material mixed together with cryogenic liquid.
AU ANDERSON T C
   FANG T
   SNAPP C
   GHIAASIAAN M
AE CARVER ENTERPRISES INC (CARV-Non-standard)
   CARVER LABS INC (CARV-Non-standard)
GA 2022A1549Y
AB    NOVELTY - The tank (110) has an inner wall (315)                defining a storage region for storing a set of                pieces of solid material e.g. dry ice. An outer                wall (335) surrounds the inner wall, where the                inner and outer walls together define an interior                space. A port unit (320) is formed in the walls,                and permits ingress and egress of the pieces of the                solid material. The storage region contains the                pieces mixed together with a cryogenic liquid. The                port unit defines an opening in the outer and inner                walls, and couples the walls. The pieces are                submerged within the liquid comprising liquid                nitrogen.
   USE - Tank for storing and delivering cold solids                e.g. dry ice such as solid carbon dioxide, used in                emergency applications such as fire fighting                applications, industrial applications such as blast                cleaning applications, and customer applications                such as food and beverages rapid cooling                applications.
   ADVANTAGE - The tank allows an eductor to feed the solid                material stored in the tank into a piping using a                gas or liquid propellant from the tank, thus                facilitating flow of the solid material and                propellant mixture without any additional moving                parts, thus enhancing reliability of the tank and                reduced maintenance costs. The tank allows more                reliable distribution and storage of dry ice so as                to permit dry ice to be kept available for                on-demand use for extended periods of time.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) a method for refreshing a set of pieces of                a solid material stored in a cold material storage                tank; and
   (2) a system for refreshing a set of pieces of                a solid material stored in a cold material storage                tank;
   (3) a method for creating a mixture of a set                of pieces of a solid material and a cryogenic                liquid; and
   (4) a system for creating a mixture of a set                of pieces of a solid material and a cryogenic                liquid.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a                vertically-oriented cold material storage                tank.
   Vertically-oriented cold material storage                tank (110)
   Inner wall (315)
   Port unit (320)
   Outer wall (335)
   Seal (340)

PT P
PN CN114847419-A
TI Flavored carbonated milk beverage is prepared from            predefined amounts of water, sweetening agent namely            sucralose or psicose, fruit juice, skimmed milk powder            containing predefined amount of cream content and dry            matter content, composite stabilizer, composite            buffering agent and edible essence.
AU LIN J
AE LIN J (LINJ-Individual)
GA 2022A6306J
AB    NOVELTY - A flavored carbonated milk beverage is                prepared from 80 pts. wt. water, 0.001-0.003 pts.                wt. sweetening agent, 50-80 pts. wt. fruit juice,                1-1.5 pts. wt. skimmed milk powder, 0.5-1.5 pts.                wt. composite stabilizer, 0.1-1.0 pts. wt.                composite buffering agent and 0.0002-0.001 pts. wt.                edible essence. The composite stabilizer comprises                sodium carboxymethylcellulose, propylene glycol                alginate, xanthan gum and beta-cyclodextrin, in a                weight ratio 3:2:3: 2. The composite buffer agent                contains sodium tripolyphosphate and sodium citrate                in a weight ratio (1-2):1.
   USE - As flavored soda milk beverage.
   ADVANTAGE - While preparing the flavored carbonated milk                beverage, the mixed raw material is filled with                carbon dioxide to form carbonated milk, based on                the capacitance properties of bicarbonate (H2CO3),                casein agglutination occurs when there is rise in                temperature and the pH value drops to 4.6, so that                mixed raw material contains a considerable amount                of metal ions such as calcium 2+, magnesium 2+                (casein agglutination accelerator). The addition of                complex stabilizer during the preparation of the                milk beverage, enables milk proteins to combine in                a charged form after self-crosslinking, to avoid                the aggregation of casein. The addition of                composite buffer reduces the fluctuation of pH                value and enables to combine metal ions, especially                calcium 2+ ions, in the mixed raw materials.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for the                preparation of flavored soda milk beverage which                involves: 1) weighing the raw materials; 2) mixing                water, sweetener, fruit juice, skimmed milk powder,                compound stabilizer, compound buffer and edible                essence, homogenizing, followed by cooling to form                mixed raw materials; 3) performing instantaneous                high-temperature sterilization on the mixed raw                materials; 4) passing carbon dioxide or nitrogen                into the mixed raw materials after instantaneous                high-temperature sterilization, to form a                semi-finished product; 5) filling the semi-finished                product with equal pressure, followed by performing                secondary high-temperature sterilization.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The sweetener present                  in the flavored carbonated milk beverage, is                  sucralose or psicose.Preferred Composition: The cream content of                  the skim milk powder contained in the milk                  beverage, is 0.05 wt.% or less and dry matter                  content of the milk powder, is 8.5 wt.% or                  more.Preferred Conditions: In step 2 of the                  flavored carbonated milk beverage preparation,                  homogenization is performed at 45-55&#8451;,                  under pressure 12-25 millipascals (mpa), with                  speed 500-1000 rotations/minute. In step 3 of the                  preparation process, the instantaneous                  sterilization is performed at 135&#8451; for                  2 seconds. Step 4 of the process, involves                  charging carbon dioxide or nitrogen at                  5&#8451; or less, under pressure less than                  0.05 mpa.In step 5 of the process, the isobaric                  pressure used for filling the semifinished                  product, is 0.05- 0.3 mpa. The secondary                  high-temperature sterilization is performed at                  temperature more than 150&#8451; for 10-15                  minutes.

TI Low-carbon heat recovery and trapping device,            comprises organic Rankine cycle power generation            system, carbon dioxide recovery system, liquefied            natural gas cold energy recovery system and low            pressure steam heat exchange system.
AU LEI Z
   XIE X
   LI Z
   WANG Y
   GUO D
   NIU H
   LIU L

AE HUANENG HUNAN YUEYANG POWER GENERATION (CHHN-C)

GA 202295310T
AB    NOVELTY - Low-carbon heat recovery and trapping device,                comprises an organic Rankine cycle power generation                system, carbon dioxide (CO2) recovery system,                liquefied natural gas (LNG) cold energy recovery                system and low pressure steam heat exchange system.                The organic working medium is used to perform                cyclic expansion work and cooling. The CO2 recovery                system collected and enriched the generated CO2,                and the gaseous CO2 generated liquid CO2 after heat                exchange with the organic working medium and the                LNG cold energy respectively. The LNG cold energy                introduced into the LNG cold energy recuperation                system is heat-exchanged with CO2 in the CO2                recovery system and the organic working medium                respectively, and the LNG cold energy is used as                city gas after absorbing heat. The low-pressure                steam heat exchange system enables to exchange heat                with the low-pressure steam and the organic working                medium in the organic Rankine cycle power                generation system.
   USE - Low-carbon heat recovery and trapping                device.
   ADVANTAGE - The device enables to fully recover the                low-grade heat of the low-pressure steam and the                regenerated gas to obtain a large amount of                low-grade heat through the organic working medium                and converted into electric energy, and further                perform energy cascade utilization process, which                effectively utilizes cold energy of the liquefied                natural gas, portion of low-grade heat is converted                into electric energy through the energy cascade                utilization process, and the rest of heat is                finally converted into sensible heat of the natural                gas, so that to provide domestic gas with proper                temperature and pressure for cities.

The gaseous CO2 is passed                  through the CO2 recovery system for heat recovery                  and storage. Preferred Components: The CO2                  recovery system also includes several gas-liquid                  separators. The gas-liquid separators are used to                  separate the gas CO2 after each heat exchange of                  the gas CO2.

PT P
PN WO2022128674-A1; TW202233765-A
TI Producing purified carbon black with reduced            content of polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbons by treating            carbon black having initial content of the hydrocarbons            with extraction agent in supercritical state to extract            aromatic hydrocarbons.
AU WAIDMANN G
   KRAUSS K
   DETERS D
   SCHINKEL A
   ETZOLD B
   DROCHNER A
   GLAESEL J
   WIDJAJA A
   GLASEL J
AE ORION ENGINEERED CARBONS GMBH (EVON-C)
GA 202281616H
AB    NOVELTY - Producing a purified carbon black with a                reduced content of polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbons                comprises: (a) providing a carbon black comprising                an initial content of polycyclic aromatic                hydrocarbons; (b) treating the carbon black                comprising an initial content of polycyclic                aromatic hydrocarbons with an extraction agent                comprising carbon dioxide in a supercritical state                to extract at least a portion of the polycyclic                aromatic hydrocarbons from the carbon black, and                (c) removing the extraction agent comprising the                extracted polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbons from the                carbon black to obtain a purified carbon black with                a lower content of polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbons                than the initial content of polycyclic aromatic                hydrocarbons.
   USE - The process is useful for producing purified                carbon black with reduced content of polycyclic                aromatic hydrocarbons, which is useful as pigment,                reinforcing filler or conductive agent to for                manufacture of plastic or rubber articles, paints,                inks, coatings, electrodes or energy storage                devices (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The process: utilizes supercritical carbon                dioxide for purification of carbon black,                preferably for removing polycyclic aromatic                hydrocarbons from carbon black and beneficially                combines densities similar to those of liquid with                solute diffusivities and viscosities closer to                those of a gas, which enables high mass transfer                rates and rapid and efficient extraction of PAHs                from carbon blacks; utilizes supercritical                extraction agent i.e. solute diffusivities,                viscosity and mass transfer rate and on the other                hand the solubility and vapor pressures of the                polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbons each depend on the                pressure and/or the temperature, thus, changing the                temperature, the pressure and/or the flow rate of                the extraction agent in the treatment step over                time can contribute to optimize the extraction                efficiency; is beneficially implemented in carbon                black production plant and be carried out e.g.                after carbon black production or treatment process                e.g. implemented downstream of carbon black                production, e.g. downstream of furnace reactor, and                upstream or downstream of other carbon black; and                enables effectively purifying carbon blacks and                obtaining carbon blacks with a low PAH content,                substantially without adversely affecting other                properties of the carbon black or the use of                purifying agents which are expensive, harmful or                difficult to remove from the carbon black                product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The process further                  comprises at least one step of: drying the carbon                  black comprising an initial content of polycyclic                  aromatic hydrocarbons prior to treating it with                  the extraction agent; separating at least a                  portion of the polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbons                  from the extraction agent comprising the                  extracted polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbons                  removed from the carbon black in step (c), by                  reducing the pressure, and optionally recycling                  the thus obtained extraction agent for use in                  step (b); and detecting the amount of polycyclic                  aromatic hydrocarbons extracted by the extraction                  agent, by an online measurement, where preferably                  the treatment of the carbon black with the                  extraction agent in step (b) is controlled based                  on the detected amount of extracted polycyclic                  aromatic hydrocarbons. The step of treating the                  carbon black with the extraction agent in step                  (b) is conducted at a temperature of greater than                  or equal to 75 degrees C, preferably 100-300                  degrees C, and/or at a pressure of greater than                  or equal to 75 bar, preferably 150-300 bar and/or                  conducted for a time of at least 1 minute,                  preferably up to 48 hours. The step of treating                  the carbon black with the extraction agent in                  step (b) comprises exposing the carbon black to a                  flow of the extraction agent, where the average                  flow rate of the extraction agent per mass unit                  of the amount of treated carbon black (in kg)                  corresponds to greater than or equal to 1000                  Nl/hour/kg preferably 5000-100000 NL/hour/ kg.                  The step of treating the carbon black with the                  extraction agent in step (b) comprises extracting                  at least 40 wt.%, preferably at least 95 wt.%                  initial PAH22 content from the carbon black;                  and/or extracting at least 50 wt.%, preferably at                  least 95 wt.% initial nitro-PAH8 content from the                  carbon black. The purified carbon black obtained                  in step (c) has a content of polycyclic aromatic                  hydrocarbons, which corresponds to less than or                  equal to 60 wt.%, preferably greater than or                  equal to 5 wt.% the initial PAH22 content and/or                  50 wt.% preferably less than or equal to 5 wt.%                  initial nitro-PAH8 content. The process is                  conducted as a continuous process, as a                  semi-batch or as a batch process and/or where the                  process is carried out in a pressure-resistant                  reactor with heating means. Preferred Components:                  The carbon black comprises an initial content of                  polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbons comprising a                  furnace black, a thermal black, a lamp black, a                  gas black, an acetylene black, and/or a recycled                  black and/or comprises an oxidized carbon black,                  and/or the carbon black comprising an initial                  content of polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbons                  exhibits: an ash content determined according to                  ASTM D1506-15 of less than or equal to 20 wt.%,                  preferably less than or equal to 0.1 wt.%; a                  volatile content determined by heating to 950                  degrees C according to DIN 53552:1977 of less                  than or equal to 20 wt.%, preferably less than or                  equal to 10 wt.%; and a moisture content                  determined according to ASTM D1509-18 of less                  than or equal to 15 wt.% preferably less than or                  equal to 1 wt.%, and/or a carbon content as                  determined by elemental analysis of greater than                  or equal to 80 wt.%, preferably greater than or                  equal to 98 wt.%. The content of polycyclic                  aromatic hydrocarbons is determined as the                  polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbons 22 (PAH22)                  content, the nitro-PAH8 content or the sum of the                  PAH22 content and the nitro-PAH8 content, where                  the carbon black comprising an initial content of                  polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbons may e.g. have -                  an initial PAH22 content of greater than or equal                  to 10 parts per million, preferably greater than                  or equal to 1000 parts per million and/or an                  initial nitro-PAH8 content of greater than or                  equal to 1 ppm, preferably greater than or equal                  to 500 parts per million. The extraction agent                  comprises at least 50 wt.%, preferably 99 wt.%                  carbon dioxide. The extraction agent further                  comprises at least one auxiliary agent or                  supercritical carbon dioxide. The carbon black                  has a PAH22 content of less than 1500 parts per                  million, preferably less than 10 ppm and/or a                  nitro-PAH8 content of less than 200 ppm,                  preferably less than 1 parts per million.
   EXAMPLE - 10 g Carbon black was each loaded to the                extraction chamber of the supercritical fluid                extraction setup. The sample was dried at a                temperature of 150 degrees C and a pressure of 10                bar under carbon dioxide atmosphere for 90 minutes                under a gentle flow of carbon dioxide gas.                Subsequently, the dried sample was subjected to                continuous supercritical fluid extraction using                carbon dioxide as extraction agent at a pressure of                200 bar, a temperature of 150 degrees C and an                estimated average flow rate of the extraction agent                through the extraction chamber corresponding to 55                NL/hour (average flow rate per unit volume of the                extraction chamber: 684 NL/hour/l) for a time that                varied from 2 hours to obtain purified carbon black                with a reduced content of polycyclic aromatic                hydrocarbons.

TI Data statistics device of power planning            intelligent high safety, comprises shell provided with            device main body, radiating plate provided with            multiple radiating slide ways along vertical direction            and lower ball baffle plate inclined downward from left            to right.
AU QIAN W
   ZHOU J
   HUANG Y
   WANG Y
   ZHANG P
   XU X
AE JIANGSU ZEYU INTELLIGENT POWER CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 202285452E
AB    NOVELTY - Data statistics device of power planning                intelligent high safety, comprises a shell provided                with a device main body. The back wall of the                device main body is connected with a radiating                plate through a heat conducting silica gel. The                radiating plate is provided with multiple radiating                slide ways along the vertical direction. The bottom                of the radiating slide way is provided with a                sealing valve. The bottom side wall of the                radiating slide way is provided with a reserved                groove. The lower end of a piezoelectric ceramic is                fixedly connected to the side wall of the reserved                groove through an electric push rod. PN CN114436601-A
TI Regenerated aggregate self-compact concrete used            in building material, comprises cement, modified            regenerated aggregate, sand, fly ash, mineral powder,            additive, water and plant fiber.
AU CHE X
AE SHAOXING WENJUN MORTAR CO LTD (SHAO-Non-standard)
GA 202272997H
AB    NOVELTY - The concrete comprises 290-310 pts. wt.                cement, 800-900 pts. wt. modified regenerated                aggregate, 700-800 pts. wt. sand, 75-85 pts. wt.                fly ash, 80-100 pts. wt. mineral powder, 5-9 pts.                wt. additive, 160-200 pts. wt. water, and 10-20                pts. wt. plant fiber. The modified regenerated                aggregate is prepared by (i) crushing the waste                concrete, sorting and sieving, (ii) mixing water                glass, polyvinyl alcohol, nano silicon dioxide,                phosphoric acid and water, preparing composite                liquid, putting aggregate particles from step (i)                in the composite liquid, soaking, taking out the                aggregate particles, (iii) putting the aggregate                particles in a sealed container, pumping vacuum to                - (0.1-0.2) MPa, introducing carbon dioxide gas,                keeping the pressure for 20-24 hours to prepare the                reinforced aggregate particle, (iv) soaking in a                water-based silicone resin emulsion, and drying at                160-180 &#8451; for 3-4 hours.
   USE - Regenerated aggregate self-compact concrete is                used for building material.
   ADVANTAGE - The regenerated aggregate of the concrete has                compact structure, high mechanical strength and                strong frost resistance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The concrete comprises 290-310 pts. wt.                cement, 800-900 pts. wt. modified regenerated                aggregate, 700-800 pts. wt. sand, 75-85 pts. wt.                fly ash, 80-100 pts. wt. mineral powder, 5-9 pts.                wt. additive, 160-200 pts. wt. water, and 10-20                pts. wt. plant fiber. The modified regenerated                aggregate is prepared by (i) crushing the waste                concrete, sorting and sieving, (ii) mixing water                glass, polyvinyl alcohol, nano silicon dioxide,                phosphoric acid and water, preparing composite                liquid, putting aggregate particles from step (i)                in the composite liquid, soaking for 1-2 hours at                120-140 &#8451;, taking out the aggregate                particles, (iii) putting the aggregate particles in                a sealed container, pumping vacuum to - (0.1-0.2)                MPa, keeping the pressure for 1-1.5 minutes,                introducing carbon dioxide gas, boosting to 0.1-0.4                MPa, keeping the pressure for 20-24 hours to                prepare the reinforced aggregate particle, (iv)                soaking in a water-based silicone resin emulsion                with a mass concentration of 25-50% for 30-40                minutes, and drying at 160-180 &#8451; for 3-4                hours.

The plant fiber is                  wood fiber, and/or straw fiber

A cooler (313) rapidly cools to withstand a maximum of hundred bars in a buffer tank.
   USE - Product lightweight foaming system.
   ADVANTAGE - The system has low initial investment cost because no need to modify a mass-production injection molding machine. The system simultaneously impregnates carbon dioxide and nitrogen so that physical foaming and chemical foaming can be achieved. The system realizes chemical foaming applied without a need for equipment modification so that physical foaming is that factors that increase product cost are excluded. The system minimizes gas desorption through nitrogen impregnation and precise metering in a machine hopper, shortens carbon dioxide gas impregnation to 1 to 2 hours through supercritical impregnation so that unit cost of the impregnation equipment can be reduced by 20 to 40 percentage and mass production reproducibility can be improved. The system adjusts cell size within the product from an average of 100 microns to 50 microns, so product weight savings of 15-25 percentage can be achieved. The system increases number of cavities in a mold and productivity. The system reduces holding pressure and cooling time of injection process, which improves productivity by more than 15 percent. The system improves reproducibility in the injection process to ensure dimensional accuracy and secure by constantly adjusting an amount of impregnated resin pellets.
