TI Predictive model for transient and persistent acute kidney injury in patients with severe sepsis, comprises arterial partial pressure of oxygen, in millimeters of mercury, arterial partial pressure of carbon dioxide and anion gap.
AU WANG L
   WANG H
   WU X
   WU T
   WANG M
   ZHANG N
   KANG Y
   LIU Q
   DENG Y
   YAN P
   LUO X
   DUAN S
AE UNIV CENT SOUTH XIANGYA 2ND HOSPITAL (UYCS-C)
GA 2021C4156T
AB    NOVELTY - Predictive model comprises arterial partial pressure of oxygen (PaO2), in millimeters of mercury, arterial partial pressure of carbon dioxide (PaCO2), in millimeters of mercury, anion gap, in millimoles per liter, lactate, in units of millimeters millimole per liter, international normalized ratio (INR), no unit and partial thromboplastin time (Ptt), unit is second. The categorical variables are diabetes mellitus, congestive heart failure, chronic kidney disease, mechanical ventilation requiring mechanical ventilation and RRT induction for renal replacement therapy. These five types of variables are selected according to actual situation of patient. The corresponding value is 1. When none is selected, corresponding value is 0.
   USE - Predictive model used for transient and persistent AKI in patients with severe sepsis.
   ADVANTAGE - The predictive model has simple and practical overperformance and persistent AKI risk prediction tool for clinician.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a method for constructing predictive model of transient and persistent acute kidney injury (AKI) in patients with severe sepsis, which involves:
   a. collecting clinical data of sepsis patients who have AKI within 48 hours after entering intensive care unit in intensive care database;
   b. using extreme gradient boosting Xgboost algorithm to process clinical data to obtain ranking of importance of feature variables used to distinguish transient and persistent AKI, and selecting top 20 features for further feature screening;
   c. using least absolute contraction and selection operator LASSO algorithm to further filter 20-bit features, obtaining 14 variables, namely age, arterial PaO2, arterial PaCO2, anion gap, lactate Lactate, INR, Ptt, diabetes mellitus, congestive heart failure, chronic kidney disease, chronic kidney disease, mechanical ventilation required, RRT induction for renal replacement therapy, AKI urine output staging UO stage and AKI creatinine staging SCr stage;
   d. selecting 14 variables, using logistic regression to construct prediction model of transient and persistent AKI in patients with severe sepsis;
   e. using R software to convert prediction model into prediction probability expression, and calculating transition of severe sepsis patients according to prediction probability expression and risk of persistent AKI.

AU LIU L
   WANG H
   XIA G
   SU L
   HE Y
AE UNIV CHONGQING THREE GORGES (UCQT-C)
GA 2021B5590F
AB    NOVELTY - Deformed magnesium alloy with low cost, high conductivity and high electromagnetic shielding performance comprises 1.5-2.0 wt.% zinc, 0.2-0.8 wt.% cerium, 0.4-0.6 wt.% copper and magnesium and unavoidable impurities as the rest, and the impurities have a total amount of less than 0.1 wt.%.
   USE - Deformed magnesium alloy with low cost, high conductivity and high electromagnetic shielding performance.
   ADVANTAGE - The deformed magnesium alloy with low cost, high conductivity and high electromagnetic shielding performance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing deformed Mg alloy with low cost, high conductivity and high electromagnetic shielding performance, which involves:
   (A) alloy smelting and casting;
   (B) using a semi-continuous casting method for casting, wherei an industrial pure magnesium ingot, an industrial pure zinc ingot, a magnesium-30 wt.% cerium master alloy and a magnesium-20 wt.% copper master alloy are used as raw materials under protection of a mixed gas of sulfur hexafluoride and carbon dioxide;
   (C) putting magnesium ingot into a crucible to be melted and then heated to 730-750 degrees C for slagging;
   (D) adding pure zinc ingot and the master alloys to a ratio of the alloy composition;
   (E) heatingmaster alloys at 150-200 degrees C for 20-40 minutes before heating;
   (F) pressing below when being added;
   (G) stirring at a constant speed for 3-6 minutes after melting;
   (H) stirring uniformly and standing at 730-750 degrees C for 20-40 minutes;
   (I) quickly fishing out a scum on a surface of the melt a scum on a surface of the melt under protection of a mixed gas of sulfur hexafluoride and carbon dioxide;
   (J) placing the casted casting in a heat treatment furnace at 370-410 degrees C with a holding time of 8-13 h; and
   (K) preheating the homogenized ingot and an extrusion mold at 350-390 degrees C, where an extrusion is conducted at 350-390 degrees C with an extrusion ratio of 11.7:1 and an extrusion rate of 0.5-1.1 m/min.

AE ANHUI DONGZHI GUANGXIN AGROCHEM CO LTD (ANHU-Non-standard)

TI Preparing Inonotus obliquus extract comprises crushing Inonotus obliquus, then adding to soaking agent, heating water bath, high-pressure treatment, filtering, recovering filtrates, distilling mixed filtrates, centrifuging and vacuum drying.
AU REN Y
AE XIAN VICTORY BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (XIAN-Non-standard)
GA 2021839632
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing Inonotus obliquus extract comprises crushing Inonotus obliquus to obtain Inonotus obliquus powder, adding Inonotus obliquus powder to soaking agent, where the soaking agent is a mixture of ethanol, ethyl acetate and dimethyl sulfoxide, heating the water bath, performing high-pressure treatment for 180-200 seconds, filtering, then recovering the first filtrate, washing the filter residue with ethanol for multiple times, then recovering the second filtrate and second washing residue, contacting the second washing residue with critical carbon dioxide, then adding soaking agent, soaking, increasing the pressure to 500-600 MPa under the oxygen isolation condition, performing high-pressure treatment, filtering, then recovering the third filtrate, mixing the first filtrate, second filtrate and third filtrate, distilling under vacuum, centrifuging, collecting the precipitate, and vacuum drying.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing Inonotus obliquus extract.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has Inonotus obliquus extract extraction rate of more than 6%.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing Inonotus obliquus extract comprises crushing Inonotus obliquus to obtain Inonotus obliquus powder, adding Inonotus obliquus powder to soaking agent, where the soaking agent is a mixture of ethanol, ethyl acetate and dimethyl sulfoxide in a mass ratio of 1:1:2-3, and the mass ratio of Inonotus obliquus powder to soaking agent is 1:10-20, heating the water bath at 50-70 degrees C for 1-2 hours, then increasing the pressure to 500-600 MPa under the oxygen isolation condition after soaking, performing high-pressure treatment for 180-200 seconds, filtering, then recovering the first filtrate, washing the filter residue with ethanol for multiple times, then recovering the second filtrate and second washing residue, contacting the second washing residue with critical carbon dioxide, then adding soaking agent, soaking for 1-2 hours, increasing the pressure to 500-600 MPa under the oxygen isolation condition, performing high-pressure treatment for 180-200 seconds, filtering, then recovering the third filtrate, mixing the first filtrate, second filtrate and third filtrate, distilling under vacuum, centrifuging, collecting the precipitate, and vacuum drying at 50-70 degrees C. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for Inonotus obliquus extract prepared by the above method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Components: The mass ratio of ethanol, ethyl acetate and dimethyl sulfoxide is 1:1:3. The mass ratio of Inonotus obliquus powder and soaking agent is 1:10. The concentration of ethanol in the soaking agent is 80-90%. The ethanol concentration for cleaning the filter residue is 20%. The Inonotus obliquus powder has a particle size of 100-200 mesh. Preferred Method: The method comprises increasing the pressure to 550 MPa under the oxygen isolation condition, and performing high-pressure treatment for 190 seconds. The centrifuging is carried out for 10 minutes. The vacuum drying is carried out at 65 degrees C. The water bath is heated to a temperature of 65 degrees C.

PN DE102019131369-A1; WO2021099195-A1
TI Oxygenator device for enriching blood with oxygen and depleting blood of carbon dioxide, has housing that is provided with opening such that blood is moved during operation along hollow fibers bidirectionally by displacement of membrane.
AU SCHMITZ-RODE T
   GROSS-HARDT S
   ARENS J
   STEINSEIFER U
AE RHEINISCH WESTFAELISCHE TECH HOCHSCHULE (RWTH-C)
GA 202153992G
AB    NOVELTY - The oxygenator device (1) has a housing (2) which is filled with blood and in which fabric-permeable hollow fibers (3) are arranged, in particular as a stack or roll of hollow fiber mats. A gas flows through the hollow fibers on the inside and blood flows around the hollow fibers on the outside. The housing is connected to a single cannula (8) in which blood is transferred bidirectionally between the housing and the body of a patient during operation of the device. The housing is provided with an opening (5a) covered with a flexible and/or elastic pump membrane (6). The blood is moved during operation along the hollow fibers bidirectionally by displacement of membrane.
   USE - Oxygenator device for enriching patient's blood with oxygen and depleting blood of carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The lower pressure generated in the hollow fibers prevents gas in the form of bubbles from escaping from the hollow fibers into the blood. The gas pressure is preferably reduced only during the intake phase with the device generating negative pressure so that the greater gas exchange is achieved during the ejection phase. The effectively effective suction cross-section is enlarged and thus the negative pressure during suction is reduced. The patient is subjected to treatment more easily and quickly, in emergency situations. The handling of devices is simplified with regard to the pumping function, preferably without separate blood inlet and outlet, as well as connecting tube between oxygenator and pump. Only single access to the patient's blood circulation is found, which is beneficial especially in emergency situation. More favorable hydraulic conditions such as reduced pressure losses, improved blood protection and less recirculation are opened up in the lumen of the single cannula, since the entire cannula cross-section can be used.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the oxygenator device.
   Oxygenator device (1)
   Housing (2)
   Fabric-permeable hollow fibers (3)
   Opening (5a)
   Flexible and/or elastic pump membrane (6)
   Single cannula (8)

PT P
PN CN112598986-A; CN112598986-B
TI Preparing carbonate rock reservoir micro-model by            cutting calcite crystal into crystal sheet, polishing            and flattening, cleaning and drying, dipping crystal            sheet in beeswax in molten state, forming wax coating            on surface of crystal sheet.
AU LIANG T
   ZHOU F
   SU H
   YU F
   LI Y
   LI J
   ZUO J
   YANG K
   QU H
   YAO E
   LI B
   HU X
AE UNIV CHINA PETROLEUM BEIJING (UPEB-C)
   BEIJING KEMAISHI OIL FIELD CHEM AGENT TE (BEIJ-Non-standard)
GA 202136210J
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing carbonate rock reservoir micro-model                involves cutting calcite crystal into crystal                sheet, polishing and flattening and cleaning and                drying, dipping crystal sheet in beeswax in molten                state, forming wax coating on surface of crystal                sheet, taking out crystal sheet and cooling,                polishing wax coating on one surface of crystal                sheet uniformly, taking it as front surface,                sealing back surface and other side surfaces of                crystal sheet by adhesive tape, using carbon                dioxide laser, removing wax coating on front face                of crystal sheet corresponding to target pattern                channel, finishing mapping and re-etching of target                pattern channel, immersing crystal sheet in                hydrochloric acid and ultrasonically                oscillating.
   USE - Method for preparing carbonate rock reservoir                micro-model in research of carbonate rock water                injection development (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple and fast, and the model                bearing capacity is very strong. The method can be                used for researching the influence of wettability                to carbonate rock water injection development and                improving recovery effect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing carbonate rock reservoir micro-model                involves cutting calcite crystal into crystal                sheet, polishing and flattening and cleaning and                drying, dipping crystal sheet in beeswax in molten                state, forming wax coating on surface of crystal                sheet, taking out crystal sheet and cooling,                polishing wax coating on one surface of crystal                sheet uniformly, taking it as front surface,                sealing back surface and other side surfaces of                crystal sheet by adhesive tape, using carbon                dioxide laser, removing wax coating on front face                of crystal sheet corresponding to target pattern                channel, finishing mapping and re-etching of target                pattern channel, immersing crystal sheet in                hydrochloric acid and ultrasonically oscillating to                etch channel pattern exposed by wax coating, taking                out crystal sheet and cleaning to remove residual                acid liquid, removing adhesive tape of crystal                sheet and wax coating of other parts, and drilling                etched channel connected with outside of inlet and                outlet channel, using glass cover sheet and                adhesive to cover crystal sheet front cover so that                crystal sheet is adhered, cleaning channel and                wetting and modifying channel to obtain carbonate                rock reservoir micro model.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The crystal sheet is                  placed on the aluminum foil sheet to cool. The                  surface in contact with the aluminum foil sheet                  is the front surface of the crystal sheet and                  after the crystal sheet is cooled, tear off the                  aluminum foil sheet and sand with sandpaper to                  form a uniform thickness of acid-resistant wax                  coating. The excess acetone is injected into the                  etched channel at a constant pressure of 20                  kilopascal to remove the remaining adhesive in                  the channel, and then flushing the channel with                  water at a constant pressure of 100 kilopascal.                  The wetting modifier is injected into the etched                  channel, and the water-wettable calcite mineral                  surface in the channel is modified for                  oil-wetness. The channel is flushed with water at                  a constant pressure of 20 kilopascal. The                  n-pentane is injected into the channel at a                  constant pressure of 20 kilopascal and observed                  under a microscope and when the contact angle                  between n-pentane and the calcite wall surface in                  the channel is less than 90 degrees , the                  modification is successful, otherwise, the above                  wetting modification is repeated until the                  contact angle is less than 90 degrees . The                  preparation method of the wetting modifier                  involves adding cyclohexavaleric acid to                  n-pentane, and configuring the n-pentane solution                  with a cyclohexavaleric acid mass concentration                  of 1.5% as the wetting modifier.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The ethanol and ether                  are used to rinse the crystal sheet in sequence.                  The acetone, ethanol and water are used to clean                  the stone wafer, and nitrogen gas gun is used to                  dry the front of the crystal wafer. The adhesive                  includes CA40 cyanoacrylate.

TI Preparing Chinese herbal medicine plant atomized liquid with active molecule useful as electronic atomization device products atomizer comprises mixing flavors and fragrances, food additives, and purified water, and stirring evenly.
AU LIU M
   CAI R
   SHI S
   SHI H
AE LIU M (LIUM-Individual)
GA 202075195C
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing Chinese herbal medicine plant atomized liquid with active molecule comprises e.g. mixing flavors and fragrances, food additives, and purified water, and stirring evenly to obtain the first mixed liquid, providing raw materials of medicinal plant extracts incuding Chinese herbal medicine, flower leaf extract or mint extract, where the plant extract material includes Cyperus rotundus, Vitex trifolia, fingered citron, tochi, Artemisia carvifolia, Gentiana macrophylla, mandarin, basil, mint, lavender, Notopterygium incisum, Pueraria montana, Ephedra, almonds, Platycodon, Nepeta, Aster, Stemona tuberosa, Cynanchum, red peony root, Codonopsis pilosula, spring onions, mixing liquid and the Chinese herbal medicine plant extract to obtain the Chinese herbal medicine plant atomized liquid product.
   USE - The atomized liquid is useful as electronic atomization device products atomizer, electronic ultrasonic atomizer, electronic ultrasonic machine, electronic nano atomizer, medical equipment e.g. electronic nano atomizers, vaporized vaporizers, vaporized vaporizers, and ventilators (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The atomized liquid requires less dosage, and has quick effect without side effects.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing Chinese herbal medicine plant atomized liquid with active molecule comprises (1) mixing flavors and fragrances, food additives, and purified water, and stirring evenly to obtain the first mixed liquid, (2) providing raw materials of medicinal plant extracts incuding Chinese herbal medicine, flower leaf extract or mint extract, where the plant extract material includes Cyperus rotundus, Vitex trifolia, fingered citron, tochi, Artemisia carvifolia, Gentiana macrophylla, mandarin, basil, mint, lavender, Notopterygium incisum, Pueraria montana, Ephedra, almonds, Platycodon, Nepeta, Aster, Stemona tuberosa, Cynanchum, red peony root, Codonopsis pilosula, spring onions, Nepeta cataria, Ligusticum striatum, Xanthium sibiricum, Pinellia, licorice, Phyllanthus emblica, tangerine peel, rose, tea, Magnolia, dandelions, honeysuckle, Rehmannia glutinosa, Angelica, Ganoderma, Forsythia, Gardenia, Ophiopogon, Scutellaria, siler, Angelica dahurica, Atractylodes, Cistanche deserticola, Rhodiola rosea, and Saussurea involucrata, powdering plant extract raw materials with a particle size of 60 mesh, subjecting powdered raw material to carbon dioxide supercritical extraction under carbon dioxide gas envelope at 35-40 degrees C to obtain extract having high boiling point, low volatility, and easily pyrolyzed substances at temperature far below its boiling point, and (3) mixing liquid and the Chinese herbal medicine plant extract to obtain the Chinese herbal medicine plant atomized liquid product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Components: The Chinese herbal medicinal plant atomized liquid product includes 0.1-50 g plant Chinese herbal medicine extracts, 1-50 g food additives, 1-30 g flavors and fragrances, and 10-100 g purified water. The flavors and fragrances include food flavors and fragrances e.g. clove, citrus or lemon flavor. The food additives include borneol, vitamins, amino acids, glucose, glycerol or propylene glycol and polyethylene glycol. The Chinese herbal medicinal plant atomized liquid product specifically includes 40 g herbal extracts, 5 g flavors and fragrances, 50 g purified water, and 5 g glycerol, propylene glycol, or polyethylene glycol. The plant extract raw materials include 5 g tochi, 5 g spring onion, 5 g sesame flower, 5 g mint, 5 g Xanthium sibiricum, 5 g honeysuckle, 5 g Forsythia, and 5 g Nepeta.

PN CN111333874-A
TI Preparation of polyvinyl alcohol powder involves desalinating seawater, reacting with carbonate, filtering, adding polyvinyl alcohol and alkali solution to filtrate, reacting, purifying, classifying, screening, filtering and crushing.
AU TAO Q
   CHEN J
AE NINGXIA YIXING NEW MATERIAL TECHNOLOGY (NING-Non-standard)
GA 202062106E
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of polyvinyl alcohol powder involves rapid cooling polyvinyl alcohol to -80 degrees C to -90 degrees C for 15-25 minutes, desalinating seawater, adding carbonate, reacting under stirring condition for 20-30 minutes, introducing carbon dioxide gas into mixture to convert the insoluble carbonate into a more soluble carbonate, removing insoluble impurities by filtering slurry, adding polyvinyl alcohol to filtrate, adding small amount of alkali solution, reacting under stirring condition, purifying mixture, injecting into classification kettle, classifying by internal overflow classification kettle, screening, subjecting filtered 2000 mesh product to suction filtration with vacuum pump, washing with deionized water, suction filtering, washing with absolute ethanol, further suction filtering for 2-4 times, performing reverse phase coarse crushing through adjustable preset crusher at feeding speed of 55-65 kg/hour to form 2-4 mm ultra-fine powder and screening.
   USE - Preparation of polyvinyl alcohol powder.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple, and produces product with stable quality, and high recovery rate and is suitable for industrial production. The polyvinyl alcohol has uniform particle size distribution, improved specific surface area and adsorption and solubility rate, thereby increasing reaction contact area which increases the reaction speed and saves production time.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of polyvinyl alcohol powder involves rapid cooling polyvinyl alcohol to -80 degrees C to -90 degrees C for 15-25 minutes, desalinating seawater, adding carbonate, reacting under stirring condition for 20-30 minutes, introducing carbon dioxide gas into mixture to convert the insoluble carbonate into a more soluble carbonate, removing insoluble impurities by filtering slurry, adding polyvinyl alcohol to filtrate, adding small amount of alkali solution, reacting under stirring condition, purifying mixture, injecting into classification kettle, classifying by internal overflow classification kettle with diameter of 1400, height of 5000 and sieve of 2000 mesh, 1500 mesh and 1200 mesh, screening, removing 1200 mesh and 1500 mesh filtered product and retaining 2000 mesh product, subjecting filtered 2000 mesh product to suction filtration with vacuum pump, washing with deionized water, suction filtering, washing with absolute ethanol, further suction filtering for 2-4 times, performing reverse phase coarse crushing through adjustable preset crusher at feeding speed of 55-65 kg/hour to form 2-4 mm ultra-fine powder and screening.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The carbonate comprises soluble carbonate and insoluble carbonate. The soluble carbonate is potassium carbonate or sodium carbonate. The insoluble carbonate is calcium carbonate, magnesium carbonate or lithium carbonate. The alkali solution is sodium hydroxide and potassium hydroxide. Preferred Composition: The molar ratio of soluble carbonate and seawater is 3-4:1. The molar ratio of insoluble carbonate and seawater is 2-3:1. The concentration of soluble carbonate is 3.2-3.5 mol/L and insoluble carbonate is 2.6-3.0 mol/L.

PN CN111229245-A; CN111229245-B
TI Treating agent useful for treating waste gas containing organic chlorine comprises calcium carbonate, ferrous sulfate, manganese sulfate and metatitanic acid.
AU WEI G
   HU W
   YANG J
   CUI G
   XING X
   CUI X
AE SHANDONG XUNDA CHEM IND GROUP CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 202053020J
AB    NOVELTY - Treating agent useful for treating waste gas containing organic chlorine comprises 60-80 pts. wt. calcium carbonate, 10-15 pts. wt. ferrous sulfate, 5-10 pts. wt. manganese sulfate and 5-15 pts. wt. metatitanic acid, where the raw material are water-free.
   USE - Treating agent is useful for treating waste gas containing organic chlorine.
   ADVANTAGE - Treating agent: has high organic chlorine hydrolysis activity and organic component combustion activity; achieves the dechlorination of organochlorine compounds and the catalytic combustion of by-products containing chlorine-free organic components at 300-350 degrees C, thus converts the organochlorine into calcium chloride, carbon dioxide, and water, which is easy to achieve compliance emissions; dose not affected the organic chlorine conversion ability and dechlorination capacity by the carbon dioxide contained in the exhaust gas; utilizes low cost the raw material used in the dechlorination agent, low cost dechlorination agent, it is basically non-toxic after use, and it is easy to scrap and handle.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing the treating agent comprises (i) adding 80-100 pts. wt. water into a reaction kettle, adding all the ferrous sulfate and manganese sulfate, stirring, adding metatitanic acid and pulping, gradually adding calcium carbonate to react, heating at 80-95 degrees C, taking pH of 3-4 as the control end point of the feeding reaction of calcium carbonate, maintaining the reaction temperature and pH value range and aging for 2-3 hours to obtain slurry containing calcium sulfate fiber, (ii) placing the remaining calcium carbonate into a kneading machine, add Sesbania cannabina powder and mixing well, gradually sprinkling the slurry containing calcium sulfate fiber, kneading and extruding the strip, and (iii) drying the extruded strip, baking at 420-450 degrees C under air conditions for 2-4 hours to obtain final product; and #use of treating agent for treating waste gas containing organic chlorine, where the operating condition has temperature of 300-350 degrees C and gas space velocity 100-1000/hours, the exhaust gas contains more than 5% by volume of oxygen and 3-5% by volume of water vapor.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The metatitanic acid is intermediate material in the production of titanium dioxide by the sulfuric acid method. The ferrous sulfate is ferrous sulfate heptahydrate produced from by-product titanium dioxide of the Russian-Japanese sulfuric acid process. The calcium carbonate has average particle size of 1-3 mu m and specific surface area of 3-6 m2/g. The organic chlorine includes chlorine-containing alkanes and chlorine-containing aryl hydrocarbons.

PN CN111119977-A; CN111119977-B
TI Preparing steel slag-based explosion suppression material comprises e.g. dissolving steel slag particles, mixing persulfate, stirring, heating, separating washing, steam-curing residue, packing ultra-fine filling powder and drying.
AU GONG C
   LEI J
   GAO X
   LU L
AE UNIV JINAN SHANDONG (UNJN-C)
   UNIV JINAN (UNJN-C)
GA 202042135B
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing steel slag-based explosion suppression material for inhibiting coal dust explosion comprises (i) dissolving steel slag particles in the acid solution of aluminum and iron elements in the steel slag, mixing persulfate and stirring under heating conditions, adjusting pH of the reaction solution to be alkaline; (ii) stirring flocculent turbid liquid, washing with water, adding amino-modified polydimethylsiloxane dropwise thereto for reaction, heating during the dropwise addition, separating solid after the reaction, drying and grinding the solid product to obtain ultra-fine filling powder; (iii) washing and then steam-curing residue under pressurized heating conditions, completing, carbonizing residue under a carbon dioxide atmosphere and heating conditions to obtain porous residues for use; and (iv) packing ultra-fine filling powder into pores of porous residue by vacuum suction filtration, adding ettringite, cement and water to mix and drying the obtained particles.
   USE - The material is useful for inhibiting coal dust explosion in the mining field.
   ADVANTAGE - The material has good explosion suppression effect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing steel slag-based explosion suppression material for inhibiting coal dust explosion comprises (i) dissolving steel slag particles in the acid solution of aluminum and iron elements in the steel slag, mixing persulfate and stirring under heating conditions, adjusting pH of the reaction solution to be alkaline, allowing to stand, where the upper layer is flocculent turbid liquid and lower residue and separating flocculent turbid liquid and residue and setting aside; (ii) stirring flocculent turbid liquid, washing with water, adding amino-modified polydimethylsiloxane dropwise thereto for reaction, heating during the dropwise addition, separating solid after the reaction, drying and grinding the solid product to obtain ultra-fine filling powder; (iii) washing and then steam-curing residue under pressurized heating conditions, completing, carbonizing residue under a carbon dioxide atmosphere and heating conditions to obtain porous residues for use; and (iv) packing ultra-fine filling powder into the pores of the porous residue by vacuum suction filtration, then adding ettringite, cement and water to mix and drying the obtained particles.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (i), the addition ratio of the steel slag particles and persulfate is 40-70 pts. wt. and 0.5-1 pts. wt., the acid solution is a hydrochloric acid solution, and its mass concentration is 20-40% and the persulfate salt includes ammonium persulfate, sodium persulfate and potassium persulfate. In the step (i), the alkalinity is pH of 8.5-9.5. Preferably, ammonia water, sodium hydroxide or potassium hydroxide is used to adjust the pH value. In the step (i), the heating comprises stirring at 50-60 degrees C for 1-1.5 hours, in the step (ii), the heating temperature is 30-55 degrees C, and the reaction time is 7-9 hours and in the step (ii), the particle size of the ultra-fine filling powder is above 300 mesh. In the step (ii), the conditions for carbonization under a carbon dioxide atmosphere and heating conditions comprise carbonization under 2-3 MPa, a carbon dioxide concentration of not less than 80% at 30-55 degrees C for 2.5-4 hours. In the step (iv), the drying method comprises adding the obtained granules on the sieve, and then introducing hot air from below to dry at 45-65 degrees C. In the step (i), the obtained residue is extracted multiple times, and the method is the same as the treatment process of steel slag particles and extracted 3-5 times. Preferred Components: In the step (ii), the addition amount of the amino-modified polydimethylsiloxane, steel slag particles and amino-modified polydimethylsiloxane is 40-70:0.5-2 pts. wt. in the step (iii), the conditions of steaming under the condition of pressure heating comprises steaming at 150-200 degrees C under 1.2-1.6 MPa for 9-12 hours or in the step (iv), the added amounts of ettringite, cement and water are 100-120:10-15:3-10:40 -70 pts. wt. The structure of the steel slag-based anti-explosion material is a wrap layer composed of ettringite and cement hardened body wraps pore residue particles filled with ultrafine filler powder and forming a core-shell with a double-shell structure type steel slag-based explosion suppression material.

PN CN110787123-A
TI Preparing medical-grade cosmetics useful for removing freckles and wrinkles, comprises e.g. taking rosewood, crushing, sieving, drying, combining rosewood extraction liquid and mixed extracting solutions, vacuum extracting and concentrating.
AU HUANG X
AE SHENZHEN WEIXIN BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SHEN-Non-standard)
GA 202015501K
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing medical-grade cosmetics comprises (i) taking rosewood, crushing and sieving to obtain mahogany wood chips, performing supercritical carbon dioxide extraction to obtain rosewood extraction liquid; (ii) taking Centella asiatica, jiulongworm, Angelica, rhizoma ligustici wallichii, liquorice, persimmon leaf, ginkgo leaf, folium artemisiae argyi, cortex mori radicis, Angelica dahurica, cortex periplocae and Panax notoginseng, drying, crushing and sieving into crude powder, soaking using ethanol, heating, refluxing and extracting for 2 times, and combining the extracting solution of 2 times to obtain mixed extracting liquid; (iii) combining rosewood extraction liquid and mixed extracting solutions, vacuum extracting and concentrating, filtering to obtain traditional Chinese medicine extracting solution; and (iv) mixing traditional Chinese medicine extracting solution, hyaluronic acid and de-ionized water.
   USE - The cosmetics is useful for removing freckles and wrinkles.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple, stable, convenient, safe, economical and environmentally friendly and has controllable conditions and no side effects e.g. for allergic skin. The cosmetic is economical, environmentally friendly and has moisturizing and whitening, freckle, and resisting beauty effect of aging effect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing medical-grade cosmetics comprises (i) taking rosewood, crushing and sieving to obtain mahogany wood chips, performing supercritical carbon dioxide extraction to obtain the rosewood extraction liquid; (ii) taking Centella asiatica, jiulongworm, Angelica, safflower, rhizoma ligustici wallichii, liquorice, persimmon leaf, ginkgo leaf, folium artemisiae argyi, cortex mori radicis, Angelica dahurica, cortex periplocae and Panax notoginseng, drying, crushing and sieving into crude powder, soaking using ethanol, heating, refluxing and extracting for 2 times, and combining the extracting solution of two times to obtain mixed extracting liquid; (iii) combining rosewood extraction liquid and mixed extracting solutions, vacuum extracting and concentrating, filtering to obtain the traditional Chinese medicine extracting solution; and (iv) mixing traditional Chinese medicine extracting solution, hyaluronic acid and de-ionized water, to obtain the raw liquid, and uniformly stirring, adding glycerol, butanediol and plant essential oil, and uniformly stirring. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for freckle-reducing and wrinkle-free medical-grade cosmetics prepared by as mentioned above.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Method: The specific method of supercritical carbon dioxide extraction in step (i) is: mixing redwood sawdust and cosolvent into a mass ratio of 1:1 to obtain a mixture, charging and the mixture into a supercritical carbon dioxide extraction kettle, controlling the extraction pressure in the supercritical carbon dioxide extraction kettle to 30-35 MPa at 45-60 degrees C for 1.8-2.5 hours, adjusting the carbon dioxide in the storage tank to the temperature during the extraction process, and controlling the flow rate to 18-22 l/hour, transporting the extraction liquid to the separation kettle 1, controlling the pressure of the separation kettle 1 to 8-12 MPa, the separation temperature to 40-50 degrees C and transporting the crude extract obtained after the separation in the separation kettle 1 to the separation kettle 2 and further separating, controlling the pressure of the separation kettle 2-6 MPa, and the separation temperature to 40-50 degrees C to obtain rosewood extraction liquid. The specific method of soaking and heating under reflux in step (ii) is: placing the coarse powder and 4-5 times the weight of the ethanol solution (75%) in the extraction tank, mixing, allowing to stand for 1-3 hours, allowing the coarse powder to fully soak, and heating and refluxing for 60-80 minutes, and filtering to obtain an extracting solution. In the step (iii), vacuum extracting and concentrating temperature is 45-60 degrees C, and the pressure is 4-8x 104 Pa. Preferred Composition: In the step (ii) the addition amount of each component is: 12-18 pts. wt. Centella asiatica, 15-18 pts. wt. jiulongworm, 10-15 pts. wt. Angelica, 11-16 pts. wt. safflower, 8-12 pts. wt. rhizoma ligustici wallichii, 18-25 pts. wt. liquorice, 6-9 pts. wt. persimmon leaf, 8-10 pts. wt. ginkgo leaf, 8-12 pts. wt. folium artemisiae argyi, 6-9 pts. wt. cortex mori radicis, 5-10 pts. wt. Angelica dahurica, 6-8 pts. wt. cortex acanthopanacis and 7-12 pts. wt. Panax notoginseng. In the step (iv) the addition amount of each component is: 12-15 pts. wt. traditional Chinese medicine extracting liquid, 3-5 pts. wt. hyaluronic acid, 1-3 pts. wt. quality acid, 18-25 pts. wt. deionized water, 12-18 pts. wt. glycerol, 5-6 pts. wt. butanediol oil and 0.1-0.2 pts. wt. plant essential oil Preferred Components: In the step (iii), the weight ratio of the redwood extraction liquid to the mixed extracting solutions is 0.2-0.8:8-15. The plant essential oil is tea tree essential oil, chamomile essential oil, lily essential oil or orange essential oil.

TI Aluminum shared metal-zeolite bifunctional catalyst used for carbon dioxide catalytic hydrogenation to prepare methanol and dimethyl ether, comprises copper-aluminum oxide nanoparticle compound and nano-ZSM-5 zeolite.
AU DU T
   FANG X
   JIA H
   LI B
   WANG Y
   CHE S
   SONG Y
AE UNIV NORTHEASTERN (UNEN-C)
   UNIV NORTHEASTERN (UNEN-C)
GA 2019A6742S
AB    NOVELTY - An aluminum shared metal-zeolite bifunctional catalyst comprises copper-aluminum oxide nanoparticle compound (I) and nano-ZSM-5 zeolite. Copper is in the form of copper metal and/or copper oxide. The nano-ZSM-5 zeolite grows in situ on the surface of the compound (I), and shares copper atoms with compound (I).
   USE - Aluminum shared metal-zeolite bifunctional catalyst is useful for carbon dioxide catalytic hydrogenation to prepare methanol and dimethyl ether (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The aluminum shared metal-zeolite bifunctional catalyst has excellent synergistic effect and catalytic property.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An aluminum shared metal-zeolite bifunctional catalyst comprises copper-aluminum oxide nanoparticle compound of formula: Cu-MOx-Al2O3 (I) and nano-ZSM-5 zeolite. Copper is in the form of copper metal and/or copper oxide. The nano-ZSM-5 zeolite grows in situ on the surface of the compound (I), and shares copper atoms with compound (I).
   M = metal element;and
   x = natural number.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of aluminum shared metal-zeolite bifunctional catalyst, which involves (S1) preparing mixed metal salt solution including copper ion, metal element ion and aluminum ion, (S2) adding the metal salt solution to precipitant solution, continuously stirring, adjusting pH to 7-8 to obtain mixed solution, (S3) aging the mixed solution, filtering to obtain filter cake, washing, drying, calcining at high temperature to obtain hydrogenation catalyst powder, (S4) uniformly dispersing the hydrogenation catalyst powder in deionized water, adding template agent and sodium chloride, continuously stirring at room temperature, (S5) adding ZSM-5 zeolite, taking aluminum in the hydrogenation catalyst powder as aluminum source, adding silicon source to the solution, stirring at room temperature for 0.5-5 hours, heating, leaving, aging at room temperature, (S6) transferring the mixed solution to a high pressure reaction kettle, hydrothermally reacting for 2-4 days, heating, cooling to obtain catalyst precursor, filtering, washing, drying and (S7) calcining the catalyst precursor at high temperature to remove template agent, placing in ammonium salt solution for 3-5 times, washing, filtering, drying and calcining.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The expression of the bifunctional catalyst is (CuxM1yAlzO0.5-m-y+1.5-z).(Hn(AlnSi96-nO192)). Preferred Components: The ZSM-5 zeolite is bound on the surface of the compound (I). Silicon source comprises tetraethyl orthosilicate, monodispersed silicon dioxide, silica sol and white carbon black. Ammonium salt is ammonium chloride, ammonium nitrate and/or ammonia. The template agent is tetrapropylammonium hydroxide, tetrapropylammonium bromide, tetraethylammonium hydroxide, ethylenediamine, ammonia and/or pyridine. Preferred Property: The average particle diameter of the compound (I) is less than 100 nm. The average particle size of ZSM-5 zeolite is less than 200 nm. Preferred Conditions: The continuously stirring is step (S2) is carried out at 45-70 degrees C. The aging step is carried out at 45-70 degrees C. The calcining in step (S3) is carried out at 350-500 degrees C for 4-6 hours under air, nitrogen or argon atmosphere. The calcining in step (S7) is carried out at 450-600 degrees C for 4-6 hours under air, nitrogen or argon atmosphere. The calcining in step (S7) is carried out at 250-500 degrees C for 4-6 hours under air, nitrogen or argon atmosphere.M1 = Zn, Zr, Mn, Ce or Co;x-z = natural number;n = less than 27;andm = valence state of the metal ion M.

PN CN110029225-A; CN110029225-B
TI Recovering valuable metals in a cathode material            of a waste ternary lithium ion battery, comprises            placing the waste ternary lithium ion battery cathode            material in a tube furnace, calcining, and carrying out            pressurized reduction leaching.
AU XIE Z
   PAN J
   MA Y
   YANG L
   WEI Q
   WANG H
AE ZHEJIANG METALLURGICAL RES INST CO LTD (HZIS-C)
GA 201965116F
AB    NOVELTY - Recovering valuable metals in a cathode                material of a waste ternary lithium ion battery,                comprises (i) placing the waste ternary lithium ion                battery cathode material in a tube furnace at                60-300 ml/minute, and calcining for 15-75 minutes,                where the carbon in the cathode material of the                waste ternary lithium ion battery enters the                subsequent absorption bottle in the form of carbon                dioxide, completing the reaction, cooling, turning                off oxygen to obtain a calcined product; and (ii)                adding calcined product into a mixed solution of                ammonia-ammonium sulfate and hydrazine hydrate,                where the concentration of ammonia in the mixed                solution is 0.36-1.80 mol/l, the volume ratio of                hydrazine hydrate in the mixed solution is                1.6-6.4%, and carrying out pressurized reduction                leaching, filtering and washing to obtain leaching                slag and a leaching solution containing lithium,                nickel, cobalt, manganese, iron, aluminum and                copper.
   USE - The method is useful for recovering valuable                metals in a cathode material of a waste ternary                lithium ion battery.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Recovering valuable metals in a cathode                material of a waste ternary lithium ion battery,                comprises (i) placing the waste ternary lithium ion                battery cathode material in a tube furnace at                60-300 ml/minute, increasing the speed of tube                furnace for 10-30 minutes, heating the furnace to                400-800 degrees C, and calcining for 15-75 minutes,                where the carbon in the cathode material of the                waste ternary lithium ion battery enters the                subsequent absorption bottle in the form of carbon                dioxide, completing the reaction, cooling to normal                temperature, turning off oxygen to obtain a                calcined product; and (ii) adding calcined product                into a mixed solution of ammonia-ammonium sulfate                and hydrazine hydrate, where the concentration of                ammonia in the mixed solution is 0.36-1.80 mol/l,                the volume ratio of hydrazine hydrate in the mixed                solution is 1.6-6.4%, and carrying out pressurized                reduction leaching at 100-160 degrees C for 30-150                minutes, filtering and washing to obtain leaching                slag and a leaching solution containing lithium,                nickel, cobalt, manganese, iron, aluminum and                copper.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: Oxygen, ammonium                  sulfate, aqueous ammonia and hydrazine hydrate                  are all industrial grade reagents. The cathode                  material of the waste ternary lithium ion battery                  includes 5-10 wt.% lithium, 25-35 wt.% nickel,                  10-20 wt.% cobalt, 10-20 wt.% manganese, 0.1-1                  wt.% iron, 0.1-1 wt.% aluminum, 0.01-1 wt.%                  copper, and 1-5 wt.% carbon.

PN KR2019046562-A; KR2069259-B1
TI Method for constructing eco-friendly rainwater storage plant cultivation environmental structure in e.g. building, involves stabilizing soil amendment such that plants are grown, and charging filler within rainwater storage well to form gap.
AU KIM D G
AE UNIV IACF SUNCHON NAT (UYIA-Non-standard)
GA 2019414420
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves stabilizing (S100) a soil amendment such that multiple plants are grown. The plants are implanted (S200) in the soil amendment. A rainwater storage well is formed (S300) on the soil amendment such that the plants are planted. A sand prevention part is arranged (S400) in a cheesecloth or nonwoven fabric after performing inflow of earth and sand prevention within the rainwater storage well. A filler is charged (S500) within the rainwater storage well to form an air gap. The rainwater storage well is arranged in a lower part of an artificial ground.
   USE - Method for constructing eco-friendly rainwater storage plant cultivation environmental structure in a building and underground car park area. Uses include but are not limited to a landscape plant structure, a garden plant structure, a fruit tree plant structure and a hard-boiled food plant structure.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preventing that the rainwater is provisionally concentrated, so that flood discharge can be reduced, distribution of the soil atmosphere smoothly performed and the amount of oxygen is increased in root absorbing, thus easily ejecting carbon dioxide generated in process of inhaling the carbon dioxide by the root, and performing construction in the soil using material including rubble of field in an easy manner. PN CN109502842-A; CN109502842-B
TI Treating coal gasifier wastewater and resource            utilization process involves pretreating coal            gasification ash water to produce water and sewage,            after pretreatment, where adsorption device is returned            to original water tank for recycling.
AU JI B
   JI K
AE DALIAN DOWELLONE MEMBRANE TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (DALI-Non-standard)
   DALIAN DOWELLONE MEMBRANE TECHNOLOGY CO (DALI-Non-standard)
GA 201929049J
AB    NOVELTY - Treating coal gasifier wastewater and resource                utilization process involves pretreating coal                gasification ash water to produce water and sewage,                after pretreatment. The adsorption device is                returned to the original water tank for recycling,                analyzed the adsorbent by the analytical device,                recovered the adsorbent, and generated the                analytical waste liquid. The inorganic waste liquid                is placed to the salt tank, and the organic waste                liquid to the pyrolysis device. The desalination                device is used to recovered the salt in the gray                water, and the water is removed from the                desalination device, after the process water is                rinsed. The water is returned to the original water                tank, and the concentrated liquid of the                desalination device is returned to the analysis                device.
   USE - Method for treating coal gasifier wastewater                and resource utilization process.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to treat coal gasifier                wastewater and resource utilization process which                is helps to reduces hardness of gray water,                realizes recycling of coal gasification gray water,                free of chemicals, reduces environmental pollution,                and saves a lot of water resources.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The brine is used to analyze into the salt                tank and entered in resource flow through the salt                tank. The magnesium removal reactor is first                introduced, and sodium hydroxide is introduced to                react to precipitate, and the water is discharged                through the ceramic membrane to intercept the                precipitation of magnesium hydroxide, and the                produced water enters the next-stage calcium                removal device. The calcium removal device                generates calcium carbonate precipitation due to                the introduction of sodium hydroxide and carbon                dioxide, and the liquid phase enters the next stage                after being intercepted by the ceramic membrane.                The sodium chloride solution is subjected to                nanofiltration, a section of the solution is used                as a resolving agent in the analytical device, and                the other section is further processed to the next                stage. The sodium chloride reacts with the added                ammonia water and carbon dioxide to form ammonium                chloride and sodium hydrogencarbonate, and the                ammonia chloride which is deoxidized to generate                ammonia gas for recycling. The sodium bicarbonate                is reacted with calcium hydroxide to produce sodium                hydroxide that is supplied to the upstream                magnesium removal and calcium removal                process.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ENVIRONMENT - Preferred Conditions: The pretreated                  effluent turbidity is not more than 5 NTU. the                  adsorption device adsorbs the hardness of the                  gray water by the adsorbent at 30-120 degrees C.                  The hardness of the effluent of the adsorption                  device is less than 50 milligram per liter. The                  0-100 wt.% water produced by the adsorption                  device is entered in the original water tank for                  reuse or 0-100 wt.% entered in the desalination                  device.

TI Synthesizing battery anode material lithium manganese phosphate comprises e.g. preparing manganese acetate, preparing lithium dihydrogen phosphate, synthesizing lithium manganese phosphate, and packing.
AU LI C
   LV M
AE HEFEI ASIALON CHEM CO LTD (HEFE-Non-standard)
GA 2018955329
AB    NOVELTY - Synthesizing battery anode material lithium manganese phosphate comprises (a) preparing manganese acetate by adding electrolytic manganese to glass-lined reactor and inert gas e.g. nitrogen, argon or carbon dioxide, adding acetic acid solution, and releasing hydrogen, (b) preparing lithium dihydrogen phosphate by heating aqueous phosphoric acid solution and slowly adding lithium carbonate to carry out complexation reaction, (c) synthesizing lithium manganese phosphate by heating the resulting lithium dihydrogen phosphate solution to a reaction kettle in step (a), (d) separating and drying the resultant by spray-drying or vacuum-drying resultant to recover acetic acid, and (e) packing of lithium manganese phosphate by drying, calcining the resultant under the protection of nitrogen, cooling, grinding and pulverizing, and packaging in a warehouse.
   USE - The method is useful for synthesizing battery anode material lithium manganese phosphate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is simple and economical; requires accurate measurement; does not produce waste and large quantity of refractory wastewater; and is environmentally friendly.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Synthesizing battery anode material lithium manganese phosphate comprises (a) preparation of manganese acetate by adding electrolytic manganese to a glass-lined reactor and an inert gas e.g. nitrogen, argon or carbon dioxide and replacing the air in the reaction vessel to-be lower than the hydrogen explosion limit and adding acetic acid solution, carrying out sealing reaction, and after the reaction is finished, releasing hydrogen to obtain manganese acetate, (b) preparing lithium dihydrogen phosphate by heating an aqueous phosphoric acid solution to 40-90 degrees C and slowly adding lithium carbonate to carry out complexation reaction while stirring to obtain lithium dihydrogen phosphate solution, (c) synthesizing lithium manganese phosphate by heating and adding lithium dihydrogen phosphate solution prepared in the step (b) to the reaction kettle in the step (a) to form lithium manganese phosphate, (d) separating and drying lithium manganese phosphate by spray-drying or vacuum-drying lithium manganese phosphate solution formed in the step (c) to recover acetic acid, and (e) packing lithium manganese phosphate after drying, calcining lithium manganese phosphate under the protection of nitrogen, cooling, grinding and pulverizing, and packaging in a warehouse.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The electrolytic manganese according to step (a) is obtained by acid leaching of manganese ore to obtain manganese salt and then conveying to an electrolytic cell to electrically analyze the elemental metal, and the appearance of the elemental metal is silvery white to brown. The electrolytic manganese according to the step (a) has purity of 99%. The glass-lined reactor according to the step (a) is used for lining a glass containing high silica in a steel container, where inner surface of a composite product that is firmly adhered to a metal surface by high temperature burning. The calcination temperature in the step (e) is 500-1000 degrees C. Preferred Components: The hydrogen explosion limit in the step (a) is less than 4.1%. The mass fraction of acetic acid solution in the step (a) is 99%. The mass fraction of phosphoric acid aqueous solution in the step (b) is 85%. The lithium carbonate in the step (b) is battery grade lithium carbonate.

TI Preparation of discharge tube ceramic slurry involves grinding/mixing e.g. alumina powder, quartz sand, soda ash, feldspar, cobalt oxide, manganese oxide and titanium oxide, melting mixed powder, cooling, drying, and wet-ball-milling.
AU CHEN X
   LI J
   LI M
   WANG S
AE UNIV GUIZHOU (UYGZ-C)
GA 2018927560
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of discharge tube ceramic slurry involves mixing and grinding phosphogypsum, fly ash, additive and modifier, calcining in a kiln, obtaining clinker, dissolving the clinker, performing solid-liquid separation, adding carbon dioxide to the separated solution, filtering generated white precipitate, washing, drying, calcining, pulverizing, obtaining alumina powder, separately grinding and mixing alumina powder, quartz sand, soda ash, feldspar, cobalt oxide, manganese oxide, magnesium oxide, titanium oxide, potassium nitrate, sodium fluorosilicate, silicon carbide, calcium carbonate, cerium oxide and phosphorus pentoxide, melting the mixed powder, cooling, drying, and wet-ball-milling. The residue obtained from solid-liquid separation, is subjected to flotation, and sulfide is separated. The sulfide is placed in oxygen-rich environment, and calcined. The flue gas generated by calcination is catalyzed, and absorbed using concentrated sulfuric acid, to produce sulfuric acid.
   USE - Preparation of discharge tube ceramic slurry.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables economical preparation of discharge tube ceramic slurry with high dielectric constant and waste utilization rate. Simultaneously, acid is prepared by simple process. The ceramic slurry improves durability of ozone discharge tube.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of discharge tube ceramic slurry involves mixing and grinding phosphogypsum, fly ash, additive and modifier, calcining in a kiln, obtaining clinker, dissolving the clinker, performing solid-liquid separation, adding carbon dioxide to the separated solution, filtering generated white precipitate, washing, drying, calcining, pulverizing, obtaining alumina powder, separately grinding and mixing alumina powder, quartz sand, soda ash, feldspar, cobalt oxide, manganese oxide, magnesium oxide, titanium oxide, potassium nitrate, sodium fluorosilicate, silicon carbide, calcium carbonate, cerium oxide and phosphorus pentoxide, melting the mixed powder in a kiln, cooling, drying, and wet-ball-milling. The residue obtained from solid-liquid separation, is subjected to flotation, and sulfide is separated. The sulfide is placed in 30-50% oxygen-rich environment, and calcined at 800-1200 degrees C for 3-5 hours. The flue gas generated by calcination is catalyzed by vanadium pentoxide, and absorbed using concentrated sulfuric acid, to produce sulfuric acid.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CERAMICS AND GLASS - Preferred Composition: The discharge tube ceramic slurry comprises 8-12 pts. wt. alumina powder, 30-35 pts. wt. quartz sand, 5-8 pts. wt. soda ash, 10-15 pts. wt. feldspar, 2-3 pts. wt. cobalt oxide, 2-4 pts. wt. manganese oxide, 5-8 pts. wt. magnesium oxide, 6-9 pts. wt. titanium oxide, 4-7 pts. wt. potassium nitrate, 7-9 pts. wt. sodium fluorosilicate, 2-3 pts. wt. silicon carbide, 6-8 pts. wt. calcium carbonate, 1-2 pts. wt. cerium oxide and 1-2 pts. wt. phosphorus pentoxide.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The additive is sodium carbonate, sodium sulfate or caustic soda. The modifier is anthracite, carbon or coal gangue. The weight ratio of phosphogypsum and fly ash is 1:0.8-1.8. The addition amount of modifier is 10-25 wt.% with respect to the total content of raw material. Preferred Property: The mixed powder has particle size of 300-500 mesh.

PN CN108617820-A
TI Processing high gamma-aminobutyric acid and low            caffeine black tea powder involves preparing black by            supercritical carbon dioxide decaffeination, and            extracting lyophilization, filling black tea into            extraction kettle, and pressurizing.
AU LIN H
   WU Q
   HONG R
   LI G
   ZHANG X
AE DAMIN FOODSTUFF ZHANGZHOU CO LTD (DAMI-Non-standard)
GA 2018800505
AB    NOVELTY - Processing high gamma-aminobutyric acid (GABA)                and low caffeine black tea powder involves                preparing black tea by supercritical carbon dioxide                (CO2) decaffeination, and extracting                lyophilization, filling black tea into the                extraction kettle, pressurizing and preheating to                raise the pressure of the extraction kettle to                26-30 Mega Pascals in 50-60 minutes, and increasing                temperature of the extraction kettle to 40-50                degrees C, cycling CO2 data reaction after reaching                the supercritical state, keeping pressure of the                extraction tank 26-30 Mega Pascals, the temperature                of the extraction kettle 40-50 degrees C, adjust                the pressure of a separation tank to 8-12 Mega                Pascals, the pressure of the two separators to 5-9                Mega Pascals, and keeping two separators in a                semi-closed state, opening separation kettle every                15-25 minutes. The temperature reduction and                decompression are started in the extraction kettle                when the CO2 is circulated for 3-5 hours.
   USE - Method for processing high gamma-aminobutyric                acid and low caffeine black tea powder.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The extraction kettle is opened, and the black                tea broken tea is taken out when the pressure in                the extraction kettle drops to 0 Mega Pascals, and                the temperature in the extraction kettle drops to                room temperature. The black tea ground tea after                supercritical CO2 decaffeination treatment is mixed                with water for countercurrent extraction, the                extraction temperature is 80-85 degrees C, and the                extraction time is 30-40 minutes, extract is                collected. The extract is filtered, the filtrate is                collected and cooled. The cooled filtrate is                allowed to stand, and then the filtrate is                centrifuged to collect the centrate. The centrate                liquid is concentrated with a reverse osmosis                membrane to a concentrate having a solid content of                8-12%. The concentrated liquid is ultra-high                temperature sterilized. The sterilized concentrated                liquid is filtered. The sterilized and filtered                concentrate is paved in 10-20 minutes and vacuum                freeze-dried, and the vacuum freeze-dried powder                and pulverized material are sieved to obtain the                high GABA and low caffeine black tea powder.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Conditions: The loading amount of                  the black tea crushed tea is 50-60% of the                  capacity of the extraction kettle. The                  decompression amplitude is less than or equal to                  10 Mega Pascals every 10 minutes. The hardness of                  the water is less than or equal to 1 German                  degree. The liquid temperature of the                  concentration process is less than or equal to 45                  degrees C. The sterilization temperature is                  120-130 degrees C, and the sterilization time is                  4-15. The temperature of the concentrated liquid                  after sterilization is controlled at 40-50                  degrees C. The maximum temperature of the vacuum                  freeze-drying process do not exceed 100 degrees                  C. The black tea broken tea is prepared by                  soaking fresh leaves of black tea with 1-2%                  glutamic acid aqueous solution for 3-6 hours,                  washing fresh leaves of the soaked black tea with                  water, and then dehydrating to remove the surface                  moisture of the fresh leaves of the black tea.                  The fresh leaves of the dehydrated black tea are                  naturally withered to a tea leaf having a water                  content of 55-65%. The tea leaves are crushed to                  a cell damage rate of more than 80%, and more                  than 90% of the leaves are strips. The tea leaves                  in the fermentation chamber is fermented for 3-6                  hours, the fermentation temperature is 20-25                  degrees C, the fermentation humidity is 85-95%,                  and the tea paving thickness is 6-8 cm. The                  fermented tea leaves are dried. The dried tea                  leaves are pulverized to 8-16 mesh black tea                  broken tea. The natural withering ambient                  temperature is 25-30 degrees C, and the ambient                  humidity is 60-80%. The brightness of the tea                  drying step is 80-100 degrees C, The time is                  10-30 minutes; the foot fire temperature is 60-80                  degrees C, and the time is 80-100 minutes.
DC D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).)

PT P
PN CN108550816-A
TI Preparing lead-acid battery cathode material additive coated with mesoporous material comprises e.g. dissolving sodium hypophosphite in deionized water, filtering, extracting, washing, drying, and heat treating.
AU CHEN Q
   LIAO J
AE CHENGDU NEW KELI CHEM SCI CO LTD (XKLI-C)
GA 201875759Y
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing lead-acid battery cathode material additive coated with mesoporous material comprises (i) taking sodium hypophosphite, sodium phosphate, and polyvinylpyrrolidone, dissolving in deionized water, and adding nitric acid to adjust the pH value to obtain a solution-A, (ii) mixing erbium nitrate and lead nitrate, and adding deionized water to obtain solution-B, (iii) slowly dropping the solution-B into solution-A, and filtering to obtain a bismuth lead sol material, (iv) adding the bismuth lead sol material into aqueous solution containing hexadecyltrimethylammonium bromide, ethyl orthosilicate and sodium hydroxide, ultrasonically stirring, repeatedly extracting phenol, chloroform and isoamyl alcohol to remove hexadecyltrimethylammonium bromide and polyvinylpyrrolidone to obtain a precipitate, and (v) washing and drying the precipitate by anhydrous ethanol, subjecting to high-temperature heat treatment under an atmosphere of oxygen and carbon dioxide.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing lead-acid battery cathode material additive coated with mesoporous material.
   ADVANTAGE - The material improves the stability and cycle efficiency of the battery.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing lead-acid battery cathode material additive coated with mesoporous material comprises (i) taking 0.1-0.5 mol/l sodium hypophosphite, 0.3 mol/l sodium phosphate, and 0.4-1 mol/l polyvinylpyrrolidone, dissolving in deionized water, and adding nitric acid to adjust the pH value 4-5 to obtain a solution-A, (ii) mixing erbium nitrate and lead nitrate in a ratio of 1:1, and adding deionized water to obtain solution-B, (iii) slowly dropping the solution-B into solution-A, and filtering to separate the sol and the solution to obtain a bismuth lead sol material, (iv) adding the bismuth lead sol material into 100 ml aqueous solution containing hexadecyltrimethylammonium bromide, ethyl orthosilicate and sodium hydroxide, ultrasonically stirring at 80-85 degrees C for 3 hours, repeatedly extracting phenol, chloroform and isoamyl alcohol in a volume ratio of 25:24:1 to remove hexadecyltrimethylammonium bromide and polyvinylpyrrolidone to obtain a precipitate, and (v) washing and drying the precipitate by anhydrous ethanol, subjecting to high-temperature heat treatment under an atmosphere of oxygen and carbon dioxide. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included lead-acid battery cathode material additive coated with mesoporous material is prepared by above method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The lead ion concentration in the solution-B is 0.5-2 mol/l. In the step (iii), the solution-B droplet acceleration is 3-15 ml/minute. The concentration of the hexadecyltrimethylammonium bromide, ethyl orthosilicate and sodium hydroxide is 0.01-0.03 mol/l, 1.3 mol/l and 1-3 mol/l, respectively. The volume ratio of oxygen to carbon dioxide is 4:2. Preferred Method: The temperature of solution-A needs to be maintained between 40-45 degrees C during the preparation of solution-A. In the step (iv), the specific method of washing and drying is immersing the precipitate in absolute ethanol, performing ultrasonication for 2-10 hours, filtering, placing the filtered the solid product in an oven at 60-80 degrees C for 1-2 hours. The high temperature treatment process is sintering at a high temperature of 600-900 degrees C for 3-8 hours.

TI Indoor culture soil respiration measuring system, has detection analyzer connected with control valve, and main control machine connected with first control valve, second control valve and measurement analyzer signal connection.
AU GUO D
   LI X
   NIU D
   FU H
   SHI J
AE UNIV LANZHOU (ULAN-C)
GA 201867108R
AB    (CN207752004-U)   NOVELTY - The utility model relates to a soil respiration measuring technology field, especially claims an indoor soil cultivation and soil respiration measuring system. the indoor soil culture and soil respiration measuring system comprising an air inlet system, a first control valve, CO2 device, soil culture system, the second control valve, measuring analyzer and a main control machine; the air inlet system through the first control valve and the soil culture system connection, the CO2 filtering device is connected with the first control valve connection; the soil culture system connected with said detection analyzer through a second control valve, the main control machine is respectively connected with the first control valve and the second control valve and the detection analyzer signal. The utility model can realize automatic detection continuous to indoor soil microorganism respiratory release of CO2 in real time to meet present indoor soil cultivation test research requirement.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a front view of an indoor soil cultivation and soil respiration measuring system.
EA    NOVELTY - The system has an air inlet system connected with a soil culture system through a first control valve. A carbon dioxide filtering device is connected with the first control valve. The soil culture system is connected with a detection analyzer through a second control valve. A main control machine is connected with the first control valve. The second control valve is connected with the detection analyzer. The air inlet system is arranged with an air inlet pipe and an air pump. The air pump is connected with the air inlet pipe, a filter core assembly, a temperature and humidity control device, a flowmeter and a divider.
   USE - Indoor soil cultivation and soil respiration measuring system.
   ADVANTAGE - The system can realize automatic detection of continuous release of carbon dioxide breathing rate of microorganism in real time to meet present indoor soil cultivation test research requirement.

PN CN108424610-A
TI Preparation of flexible pressure-resistant phenolic foam plastic involves mixing preparing modified bacterial cellulose and additive, mixing phenolic resin and surfactant, adding e.g. modified bacterial cellulose, and solidifying.
AU SHI L
   WAN Z
AE SHI L (SHIL-Individual)
GA 201866720J
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of flexible pressure-resistant phenolic foam plastic comprises mixing Acetobacter xylinum and culturing liquid, adding talc powder, paraffin wax, and surfactant, mixing, incubating, filtering, washing, lyophilizing, and pulverizing to obtain modified bacterial cellulose; mixing Pluronic P123 (RTM: poly(ethylene oxide)-poly(propylene oxide)-poly(ethylene oxide) triblock copolymer) and hydrochloric acid, mixing with trimethylbenzene, adding ethyl orthosilicate and processing agent, stirring, introducing carbon dioxide, aging, filtering, washing, and calcining to obtain additive; and weighing phenolic resin, surfactant, pentane, curing agent, polypropylene, silane coupling agent KH-550, additive, and modified bacterial cellulose, mixing phenolic resin and surfactant, adding pentane, additive, silane coupling agent KH-550, and polypropylene, stirring, mixing with modified bacterial cellulose and curing agent, stirring, solidifying, and demolding.
   USE - Method for preparing flexible pressure-resistant phenolic foam plastic.
   ADVANTAGE - The foam plastic has excellent compression strength and good flexibility.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of flexible pressure-resistant phenolic foam plastic comprises: (A) mixing Acetobacter xylinum and culturing liquid at mass ratio of 1:100-1:150, adding 0.05-0.15 times talc powder, 0.01-0.02 times paraffin wax, and 0.01-0.02 times surfactant, mixing, incubating at 32-35 degrees C for 5-8 days, filtering, washing, lyophilizing, and pulverizing to obtain modified bacterial cellulose; (B) mixing Pluronic P123 (RTM: poly(ethylene oxide)-poly(propylene oxide)-poly(ethylene oxide) triblock copolymer) and hydrochloric acid at mass ratio of 1:45-1:55, mixing with trimethylbenzene at mass ratio of 140:1-160:1 for 50-80 minutes, adding 0.03-0.10 times ethyl orthosilicate and 0.1-0.2 times processing agent, stirring, introducing carbon dioxide, aging, filtering, washing until washing liquid is colorless, and calcining to obtain additive; and (C) weighing 85-100 pts. wt. phenolic resin, 4-8 pts. wt. surfactant, 8-12 pts. wt. pentane, 4-6 pts. wt. curing agent, 12-20 pts. wt. polypropylene, 5-8 pts. wt. silane coupling agent KH-550, 8-12 pts. wt. additive, and 5-10 pts. wt. modified bacterial cellulose, mixing phenolic resin and surfactant, adding pentane, additive, silane coupling agent KH-550, and polypropylene, stirring, mixing with modified bacterial cellulose and curing agent, stirring, solidifying, and demolding.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The culture liquid is prepared by mixing water and glucose at mass ratio of 50:1-55:1, adding peptone with 0.0005-0.0010 times water, yeast powder with 0.0005-0.0010 times water, sodium hydrogen phosphate and with 0.0005-0.0010 times water, and citric acid with 0.0001-0.0005 times water, and stirring. Preferred Components: The curing agent is ammonium chloride, isocyanate, or p-formaldehyde
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The surfactant is Tween -80 (RTM: polyoxyethylene (20) sorbitan monooleate), Tween-60 (RTM: polyoxyethylene (20) sorbitan monostearate), or sodium dodecyl benzene sulfonate. Preferred Method: The processing agent is prepared by mixing (N-fluorenyl)dodecyl acrylamide and polyethylene glycol-p-toluenesulfonate at mass ratio of 2:-1:1, adding 0.1-0.2 times p-dichlorobenzene and 0.08-0.12 times ferrocene, and stirring.

PN CN108191886-A; CN108191886-B
TI Ring-fused pentacyclic unit containing thioxanthene derivative as phosphorescent host material in preparing organic light-emitting diode luminous layer, is prepared by adding thioxanthene unit of boric acid or boric acid ester.
AU YING L
   PENG F
   HUANG F
   CAO Y
AE SOUTH CHINA SYNERGY INNOVATION RES INST (SCHS-Non-standard)
GA 201854086H
AB    NOVELTY - Ring-fused pentacyclic unit containing thioxanthene derivative is represented by (I-IV).
   USE - Ring-fused pentacyclic unit containing thioxanthene derivative as phosphorescent host material in preparing organic light-emitting diode luminous layer (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The ring-fused pentacyclic unit containing thioxanthene derivative has stronger molecular rigidity, and improves performance of the device and thermal stability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Ring-fused pentacyclic unit containing thioxanthene derivative is represented by formulae (I-IV).
   R1=1-4C alkyl, 6-60C aromatic hydroxyl, 3-60C aromatic heterocyclic group;
   X=(CR2)2, Si(R2)NR2, Oxygen, Sulfur, sulfur dioxide or carbon dioxide; and
   R2=alkyl with hydrogen atom and 1-4C, 6-60C aromatic hydrocarbon group, 3-60C aromatic heterocyclic group.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing ring-fused pentacyclic unit containing thioxanthene derivative, which involves:
   (A) adding thioxanthene unit of boric acid or boric acid ester functional derivatives (VI) and o-methyl or omethyl iodine to Suzuki coupling reaction to obtain cyclization precursor intermediate;
   (B) subjecting cyclization precursor intermediate product in acid condition or presence of reducing agent derivatives of five membered ring unit and condition to obtain derivative of a pentacene-containing five-membered ring unit;
   (C) reacting the derivative of the pentacene-containing five-membered ring unit into carbon dioxide and R2H five-membered ring unit under the action of methanesulfonic acid and carbon tetrachloride to obtain stilbene-containing quinone ring derivative;
   (D) subjecting derivative of thioxan unit containing a boronic acid or borate functional group is subjected to a Suzuki coupling reaction with o-nitrobromobenzene or o-nitroiodobenzene to obtain intermediate product of ring-closing precursor;
   (E) subjecting cyclization precursor intermediate product in acid condition or presence of reducing agent under the condition of generating cyclization reaction to obtain derivative of a pentacene-containing five-membered ring unit; and
   (F) subjecting thioxanthene-containing quinone ring-containing unit with R2Br or R2I five-membered ring unit to undergo Suzuki coupling reaction to obtain ring-fused pentacyclic unit containing thioxanthene derivative.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The catalyst for Suzuki coupling reaction is tetra-triphenyl phosphine palladium or palladium acetate/tri-tert-butylphosphine. The acid is sulfuric acid, acetic acid, hydrochloric acid. The reducing agent is triethyl phosphate or triphenyl phosphine. Preferred Condition: The temperature of the Suzuki coupling reaction is 30-100 degrees C. The time of the Suzuki coupling reaction is 6-48 hours. The ratio of the thioxene unit containing a boric acid or borate functional group and methyl ortho-bromobenzoate or methyl ortho-iodobenzoate is 1:1-1:5. The ratio of the thioxanthene group containing a boric acid or borate functional group to o-nitrobromobenzene or o-nitroiodobenzene is 1:1-1:5. The concentration of the sulfuric acid is 98 wt.%. The concentration of the hydrochloric acid is 38 wt.%. The reaction condition is 20-120 degrees C for 6-48 hours. The Suzuki coupling reaction temperature is 30-100 degrees C. The Suzuki coupling reaction time is 6-48 hours.
   Preferred Definitions:
   R1 and R2=formula (V); and
   X=C(R2)2.
   .
