PN CN112755154-A
TI Traditional Chinese medicine used for providing anticancer effect, contains Ganoderma lucidum, Ganoderma lucidum spore powder, radix astragali, semen litchi, Gynostemma pentaphyllum, rhizoma smilacis glabrae and Oldenlandia diffusa.
AU TENG S
   WEN S
AE WEN S (WENS-Individual)
GA 202154233U
AB    NOVELTY - Traditional Chinese medicine comprises Ganoderma lucidum, G.lucidum spore powder, radix astragali, semen litchi, Gynostemma pentaphyllum, rhizoma smilacis glabrae, Oldenlandia diffusa, rhizoma zedoariae, radix zanthoxyli, honeysuckle, Clinacanthus nutans, dandelion root, Schisandra berry, radix sophorae flavescentis, Inonotus obliquus, green tea, Coix seed, lily, buckwheat, and hairyvein agrimony.
   USE - The traditional Chinese medicinal composition is useful in granule for providing anticancer effect (claimed) for treating various cancers mainly solid tumors.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) Chinese medicinal granule comprising the above-mentioned traditional Chinese medicine; and
   (2) method for preparing the Chinese medicinal granules involves subjecting the raw materials to carbon dioxide supercritical extraction or hot water extraction, where the radix sophorae flavescentis and rhizoma smilacis glabrae are extracted by hot water.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Product: The traditional Chinese medicine comprises 27-33 g, preferably 30 g G.lucidum, 2-4 g, preferably 3 g G.lucidum spore powder, 27-33 g, preferably 30 g radix astragali, 27-33 g, preferably 30 g semen litchi, 27-33 g, preferably 30 g G.pentaphyllum, 27-33 g, preferably 30 g rhizoma smilacis glabrae, 27-33 g, preferably 30 g O.diffusa, 18-22 g, preferably 20 g rhizoma zedoariae, 18-22 g, preferably 20 g radix zanthoxyli, 18-22 g, preferably 20 g honeysuckle, 18-22 g, preferably 20 g C.nutans, 18-22 g, preferably 20 g dandelion root, 13-17 g, preferably 15 g Schisandra berry, 8-12 g, preferably 10 g radix sophorae flavescentis, 27-33 g, preferably 30 g I.obliquus, 27-33 g, preferably 30 g green tea, 27-33 g, preferably 30 g Coix seed, 27-33 g, preferably 30 g lily, 27-33 g, preferably 30 g buckwheat, and 27-33 g, preferably 30 g hairyvein agrimony. The Chinese medicine granule comprises the above-mentioned raw materials. Preferred Method: The daily dosage is divided into two equal parts, packed into the granule packing sachets, and one bag is taken orally after breakfast and dinner. The method involves performing carbon dioxide supercritical extraction of rhizoma zedoariae, Coix seed, Schisandra berry and G.lucidum spore powder respectively, performing primary hot-water extraction of I.obliquus, C.nutans, green tea and lily, performing secondary hot-water extraction of radix sophorae flavescentis, rhizoma smilacis glabrae, and buckwheat, mixing G.lucidum, G.pentaphyllum, radix astragali, radix zanthoxyli, O.diffusa, hairyvein agrimony, semen litchi, honeysuckle, and dandelion root, and performing secondary hot-water extraction. The method involves performing the above-mentioned processes, collecting the Chinese medicine liquid, adding water to the material for the second time, extracting with hot water, taking out the traditional Chinese medicine liquid, mixing the two traditional Chinese medicine liquids, spray-drying, and preparing fine powder, mixing G.lucidum, G.pentaphyllum, radix astragali, radix zanthoxyli, O.diffusa, hairyvein agrimony, semen litchi, honeysuckle, and dandelion root, extracting with hot water, taking out the Chinese medicinal liquid, adding water to the material, extracting with hot water, and taking the Chinese medicinal extract, mixing the two traditional Chinese medicine liquids, spray-drying, preparing fine powder, preparing granules by mixing all the prepared finished products to prepare granular granules. The method involves (a) extracting rhizoma zedoariae to extract beta -elemene, extracting Coix seed to extract Coix seed oil, extracting Schisandra berry to extract schisandrin B, and extracting G.lucidum spore powder to extract spore oil, (b) mixing I.obliquus, C.nutans, green tea and lily, adding 10 times water, extracting at 90-95 degrees C for 60 minutes, and spray-drying the prepared aqueous solution of traditional Chinese medicine to obtain refined powder, (c) performing secondary hot water extraction by (i) mixing radix sophorae flavescentis, rhizoma smilacis glabrae, and buckwheat, adding 10 times water, extracting at 90-95 degrees C for 60 minutes, taking out the Chinese medicinal solution, adding 5 times water, extracting at 90-95 degrees C for 60 minutes, and then taking out the Chinese medicine liquid, mixing the water extract of traditional Chinese medicine twice, spray-drying, and preparing the refined powder, and (ii) mixing G.lucidum, G.pentaphyllum, radix astragali, radix zanthoxyli, O.diffusa, hairyvein agrimony, semen litchi, honeysuckle, and dandelion root, adding 10 times water, extracting at 90-95 degrees C for 60 minutes, taking out the Chinese medicinal liquid, adding 5 times water, extracting at a temperature of 90-95 degrees C for 60 minutes, taking out the Chinese medicine liquid, mixing the extracts, spray-drying, and preparing the refined powder, and (d) preparing granules by mixing all the prepared finished products.
   ACTIVITY - Cytostatic.

PT P
PN CN112499635-A
TI Preparing aerogel slurry comprises adding silicon source into ethanol and water, adding acid to adjust the pH value, stirring, hydrolysing, adding catalyst, aging, drying and stirring uniformly.
AU LIU C
   YE T
   GUO J
AE HUBEI GUIJINNING ENERGY SAVING TECHNOLOG (HUBE-Non-standard)
GA 202128687D
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing aerogel slurry comprises (S1) adding silicon source into ethanol and water, adding acid to adjust the pH value, stirring and hydrolysing for 30-60 minutes at room temperature, adding catalyst, allowing to stand for 1-2 hours to form wet gel, aging for 48 hours at normal temperature and normal pressure, adding hydrophilic agent for processing, where the wet gel processed by hydrophilic agent is cleaned by absolute ethyl alcohol; drying by supercritical carbon-dioxide; and (S2) placing the prepared aerogel into water containing stabilizing agent, and stirring uniformly.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing aerogel slurry.
   ADVANTAGE - The packing box is convenient to transport; and avoids dust pollution when adding powder.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (S1), the silicon source is ethyl orthosilicate or methyltrimethoxysilane. In the step (S1), the acid for adjusting the pH value is hydrochloric acid, sulfuric acid, or acetic acid. In the step (S1), the catalyst is ammonia water or aqueous sodium hydroxide. In the step (S1), the hydrophilic agent is the silane coupling agent HK550, which is treated once every 12 hours for a total of 3 times. Preferred Method: In the step (S1), the pH value is 3-6. In the step (S2), the components comprise 6-25 pts. wt. aerogel, 1-3 pts. wt. stabilizer, and 72-93 pts. wt. water. In the step (S2), the stirring speed is 50-2000 revolutions/minute, and the mixing time is 30 minutes-2 hours.

PN CN112023943-A; CN112023943-B
TI Preparing flower-shaped porous indium(III)            sulfide/indium(III) oxide composite catalyst comprises            e.g. taking flower-shaped indium(III) sulfide as            precursor to directly grow indium(III) oxide on            indium(III) sulfide surface.
AU LI P
   LIU Y
   QU P
   YANG S
   GONG H
   YANG C
   FENG X
AE UNIV SHANGQIU NORMAL (USQN-C)
GA 2020C43384
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing flower-shaped porous indium(III)                sulfide/indium(III) oxide composite catalyst                comprises taking flower-shaped indium(III) sulfide                as precursor using in-situ oxidation method to                directly grow indium(III) oxide on the indium(III)                sulfide surface to obtain porous indium(III)                sulfide/indium(III) oxide composite catalyst.
   USE - The flower-shaped porous indium(III)                sulfide/indium(III) oxide composite catalyst is                useful for photolysis of water and photocatalytic                reduction of carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The composite catalyst inhibits recombination                of photo-generated carriers and improves separation                efficiency; introduces indium(III) oxide also                produces porous structure, which increases exposed                catalytic reaction active sites, and improves                catalytic activity of composite system.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The in-situ oxidation                  method is carried out by heating flower-shaped                  indium(III) sulfide precursor in oxygen-rich                  atmosphere to obtain porous indium(III)                  sulfide/indium(III) oxide composite catalyst. The                  flower-shaped indium(III) sulfide precursor is a                  flower-shaped indium(III) sulfide precursor                  prepared by a solvothermal method using                  indium(III) chloride as indium source,                  thioacetamide as the sulfur source, and mixed                  solution of ethylene glycol and water as reaction                  medium. The preparation method of flower-shaped                  indium(III) sulfide precursor comprises                  dissolving indium(III) chloride tetrahydrate and                  thioacetamide in a mixed solution of ethylene                  glycol and water, maintaining it at 150 degrees C                  for 10-20 hours, preferably 12 hours, and                  naturally cooling down to room temperature,                  centrifugally washing the resulting precipitate,                  and placing it in a 65 degrees C vacuum drying                  oven to dry. The flower-shaped indium(III)                  sulfide precursor is treated in an air atmosphere                  at 450-550 degrees C for 1-4 hours to obtain                  porous indium(III) sulfide/indium(III) oxide                  composite catalyst. Preferred Components: The                  molar ratio between indium(III) chloride                  tetrahydrate and thioacetamide is 1:4, and the                  volume ratio of ethylene glycol to water is                  1:5.

TI Fractional production of metal carbonate e.g. magnesium carbonate, involves adding pH adjusting agent to industrial wastewater, precipitating and recovering magnesium hydroxide, and reacting with aqueous solution of amine absorbent.

   WOO K
   YOO Y

AB    NOVELTY - Fractional production of metal carbonate using industrial wastewater involves adding (i) a pH adjusting agent to industrial wastewater, precipitating and recovering magnesium hydroxide, adding (ii) a pH adjusting agent to industrial wastewater from which magnesium hydroxide is recovered, precipitating and recovering calcium hydroxide, and reacting (iii) the recovered calcium hydroxide and magnesium hydroxide with an aqueous solution of an amine absorbent absorbing carbon dioxide to produce calcium carbonate and magnesium carbonate.
   USE - Fractional production of metal carbonate e.g. magnesium carbonate and calcium carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables economical, efficient and environmentally-friendly fractional production of metal carbonate from industrial wastewater in which various metal ions exist.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The pH adjusting agent is sodium hydroxide, potassium hydroxide and/or lithium hydroxide. Preferred Properties: The pH of industrial wastewater after adding the pH adjusting agent in the process (i) is 7-10. The pH of industrial wastewater after adding the pH adjusting agent in the process (ii) is 11-13. Preferred Process: The process further involves reacting (iv) industrial wastewater from which calcium hydroxide and magnesium hydroxide is recovered with aqueous solution of amine absorbent absorbing carbon dioxide to produce sodium bicarbonate.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The amine absorbent is monoethanolamine, diethanolamine, methyldiethanolamine and/or triethanolamine. The amine absorbent is monoethanolamine, and the usage amount of monoethanolamine in the aqueous solution of amine absorbent in the process (iii) and process (iv) is 20-40 wt.%. The aqueous solution of amine absorbent absorbing carbon dioxide is saturated with carbon dioxide. The aqueous solution of amine absorbent absorbing carbon dioxide in the process (iv) comprises 3 M or more sodium ions.

AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing epidermal growth factor in artificial nest device for mixed cell and simulated cell culture, involves preparing mixed cell culture solution and cell nutritional solution, setting parameters of the artificial nest device, loading the mixed cell culture solution into the simulated artificial nest, starting the circulation device for cell culture, and using enzyme-linked immunosorbent assay (ELISA) kit for cytokine detection from the collected cell supernatant.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing epidermal growth factor in artificial nest device for mixed cell and simulated cell culture (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method establishes human body temperature, nutrition, acid-base balance, oxygen balance, carbon dioxide balance and metabolite discharge simulation condition of human body when produce epidermal growth factor through artificial nest.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Components: The mixed cell culture solution comprises 60-80% human fibroblasts, 1% mast cells, macrophages, dendritic cells, Langerhans cells and pigment cells, 0.5% epidermal stem cells, 0.005% cluster of differentiation (CD)4+ T cells and 0.01% CD8+ T cells, where the cell density is 1x 107/ml, and the insufficient portion is supplemented with cell nutritional solution. The cell nutritional solution comprises 50 ng/l angiotensin, 98 mu g/l aldosterol, 66 pg/l type B brain natriuretic peptide, 2.26 nmol/l digoxin, 55 mu g/l hyaluronic acid, 28 mu g/l laminin, 56 mu g/l type IV collagen, 75 mu g/l type III procollagen peptide, 10.2 nmol/l folic acid, 396 pmol/l vitamin B12, 3 mmol/l glucose, 136 mmol/l sodium ion, 5 mmol/l potassium ion, 1 mmol/l magnesium ion and 2.2 mmol/l calcium ion. The cell nutritional solution further comprises 10 ng/l Salvia and 5 ng/l quercetin. The parameters of the artificial nest device are: pressure of 90-220 mmHg, pH of 7.35-7.45, the temperature of 36.2-37.5 degrees C, the oxygen content of 15-50 ml/100 ml culture medium, and the carbon dioxide content is 30-80 ml/100 ml culture medium. Before the device is running, the spleen area has no nutritional solution, and the first control valve of the liver area is opened so that the nutritional solution is actively transported from the stomach area to the spleen area and reaches lowest set value of the liquid level in the spleen area driven by the power of the heart area, the nutritional solution reaches the heart area and enters the lung area. The nutritional solution receives from the lung area, the gas permeates the oxygen and carbon dioxide of the membrane and enters the artificial nest to provide nutrition and oxygen for the cells wrapped in the simulated extracellular matrix. The gas exchange is performed by the second peristaltic pump and the second control valve synergy provides power, oxygen and carbon dioxide are provided by sterile air. A nutritional solution flows out from the artificial nest through the third control valve and enters the spleen area. The control platform obtains relevant instructions from the cloud data center to control cell culture, and upload cell culture data to the cloud data center. The control platform is also responsible for providing future applications for cell growth patient's life field of cells. The culture temperature of the system is maintained by a constant temperature system, and the system state is monitored by a sensor, and is connected to a control platform through a data interface. The method involves obtaining ELISA plate, adding 200 mu l standard substance in sequence, incubating the sample in the microwell at 25 degrees C for 1 hour, after washing the plate three times, adding 200 mu l substrate to each well, incubating with backlight at 25 degrees C for 20 minutes at average of 3 replicates of a sample.
